background: A FIRRM purification workflow is in progress after lysis, and the current segment covers the paired affinity enrichments used locally in the protocol. The sample is first clarified and handled with amylose beads for capture, washing, and maltose release, then the recovered material is subjected to Ni-NTA binding, washing, and imidazole elution under cold conditions.
goal: Carry the FIRRM sample through the two consecutive affinity purification stages to produce the final cold Ni-NTA eluate.
constraints: Maintain 4°C for cold spins, binding incubations, washes, and elution handling.

Include a clarification spin at 17947 x g for 30 min, repeated amylose bead wash spins at 10000 x g for 1 min, amylose bead recovery at 5000 x g for 4 min, and repeated wash spins at 3000 x g for 2 min.
Use an amylose binding incubation of 4 h with gentle mixing equivalent to 10 rpm, then a short maltose elution of 7 min using the higher first-elution condition normalized as 15 mM maltose.

Feed the amylose eluate into the Ni-NTA stage with a 3 h cold binding step under 10 mM imidazole and gentle mixing at 10 rpm, followed by cold washing and a final 10 min elution corresponding to 300 mM imidazole with vortex-based mixing at 1100 rpm.

available_inputs.name: cell_lysate
available_inputs.description: Crude FIRRM lysate prepared from expression cells before final clarification.

available_inputs.name: amylose_beads
available_inputs.description: Amylose affinity beads for the first capture step.

available_inputs.name: t300_buffer
available_inputs.description: High-salt purification buffer used for bead washing and handling.

available_inputs.name: maltose_buffer
available_inputs.description: T300-based elution buffer supplemented with maltose.

available_inputs.name: ninta_resin
available_inputs.description: Ni-NTA agarose resin for the second affinity purification step.

available_inputs.name: imidazole_wash_buffer
available_inputs.description: Wash buffer containing low imidazole for Ni-NTA handling.

available_inputs.name: imidazole_elution_buffer
available_inputs.description: T150-based elution buffer supplemented with high imidazole.

gold_program:
clarified_lysate = centrifuge_sample(sample=cell_lysate, speed_xg=17947, duration_min=30.0, temperature_c=4.0)
washed_amylose_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=amylose_beads, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=4.0)
washed_amylose_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_amylose_1, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=4.0)
amylose_binding = incubate_sample(sample=clarified_lysate, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=240.0, condition="gentle mixing with amylose beads at 10 rpm")
amylose_bead_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=amylose_binding, speed_xg=5000, duration_min=4.0, temperature_c=4.0)
amylose_wash = centrifuge_sample(sample=amylose_bead_pellet, speed_xg=3000, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=4.0)
amylose_eluate = incubate_sample(sample=amylose_wash, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=7.0, condition="elute with maltose buffer at 15 mM using rotary mixing at 15 rpm")
ninta_binding = incubate_sample(sample=amylose_eluate, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=180.0, condition="bind to Ni-NTA resin with 10 mM imidazole and gentle mixing at 10 rpm")
ninta_wash = centrifuge_sample(sample=ninta_binding, speed_xg=3000, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=4.0)
firmm_final_eluate = vortex_mix(sample=ninta_wash, duration_s=600.0, speed_rpm=1100, temperature_c=4.0)
protocol_title: Reconstitution of the antagonistic effect between C1orf112/FIRRM-FIGNL1 and BRCA2 on RAD51 filament stabilization

source_steps.description: Centrifuge the cell lysate at 13000 rpm (17947 × g ) for 30 min at 4°C and transfer the supernatant into a pre-cooled 100 mL beaker. Then add T buffer to adjust the salt concentration to 300 mM.

Affinity purification with Amylose beads. Pre-wash 400 μL of Amylose beads with 1 mL of T300 and then centrifuge at 10000 × g for 1 min at 4°C. Carefully discard the supernatant, ensure the beads pellet is not disturbed. Repeat this step twice.
Add the washed Amylose beads to the cell lysate and incubate at 4°C for 4 h on a rotary mixer at 10 rpm.
Centrifuge the cell lysate at 5000 × g for 4 min at 4°C to collect the beads. Transfer the beads to a 2 mL centrifuge tube.
Wash the beads with T300 buffer four times. Centrifuge at 3000 × g for 2 min at 4°C and then transfer the beads to a 1.5 mL centrifuge tube.

Elute proteins with 250 μL T300 buffer containing 10 mM maltose at 4°C for 7 min on a rotary mixer at 15 rpm. Note: Considering the residual buffer in the beads pellet, when performing protein elution for the first time, add 1.5 times of maltose (15 mM).
Repeat the elution step three additional times. Keep 5 μL of each eluate for SDS-PAGE and Coomassie blue staining.
Affinity purification with Ni-NTA agarose resin. Pre-wash 140 μL of Ni-NTA agarose resin with 1 mL of T300 as described above.

Mix resin with the eluate (∼3.8 mL) from three times of above affinity purification (∼20 g cell pellet in total), and add imidazole to the final concentration of 10 mM. Then incubate at 4°C for 3 h on a rotary mixer at 10 rpm.
Centrifuge at 3000 × g for 2 min at 4°C to collect the resin and transfer the resin to a 1.5 mL centrifuge tube.
Prepare T300 and T150 buffers containing 10 mM imidazole; wash the resin three times with each buffer. Centrifuge at 3000 × g for 2 min at 4°C.

Elute proteins with 120 μL T150 buffer containing 200 mM imidazole at 4°C for 10 min on a vortex mixer at 1100 rpm. Note: Considering the residual buffer in the resin, when performing protein elution for the first time, add 1.5 times of imidazole (300 mM).

background: A recombinant FIRRM sample is in the middle of a two-step affinity purification workflow. The relevant local segment begins after resin preparation and focuses on cold amylose capture from lysate, recovery of bead-bound material, repeated washing, maltose elution, and transfer of the recovered material into a second Ni-NTA cleanup step with imidazole-dependent binding, washing, and final elution.
goal: Plan the local purification segment that carries FIRRM from amylose binding through Ni-NTA elution, using only the available high-level actions and preserving the dependency of later operations on material produced earlier.
constraints: Keep the purification workflow at 4 degrees Celsius for binding, wash, and elution handling.
Represent amylose binding as a 4 hour gentle mixing step at 10 rpm, followed by bead collection at 5000 x g for 4 minutes.

Condense amylose washing into a repeated wash stage consistent with four washes collected at 3000 x g for 2 minutes, then perform amylose elution as a 7 minute mixing step at 15 rpm that stands for the serial elutions, with the first elution effectively stronger because of residual buffer.
Feed the amylose-derived eluate into Ni-NTA binding with 10 mM imidazole and incubate for 3 hours at 10 rpm, then collect resin at 3000 x g for 2 minutes.
Condense the Ni-NTA wash phase so that both T300 plus 10 mM imidazole and T150 plus 10 mM imidazole are represented before final elution.
Represent the Ni-NTA elution phase as a 10 minute 1100 rpm vortex-style elution step standing in for the serial eluctions, with the first event effectively stronger because of residual buffer.

available_inputs.description: Pre-washed amylose beads equilibrated in T300 buffer.

available_inputs.name: firmm_lysate
available_inputs.description: Cold FIRRM cell lysate ready for amylose affinity capture.

available_inputs.name: amylose_maltose_buffer
available_inputs.description: T300-based elution buffer containing maltose for amylose elution.

available_inputs.name: ninta_resin
available_inputs.description: Pre-washed Ni-NTA agarose resin for the second affinity step.

available_inputs.name: t300_imidazole_buffer
available_inputs.description: T300 buffer supplemented with 10 mM imidazole for Ni-NTA washing.

available_inputs.name: t150_imidazole_buffer
available_inputs.description: T150 buffer supplemented with 10 mM imidazole for Ni-NTA washing.

available_inputs.name: ninta_elution_buffer
available_inputs.description: T150-based buffer containing high imidazole for Ni-NTA elution.

gold_program:
amylose_binding_input = mix_sample(sample="firmm_lysate_with_amylose_beads", method="pipetting", duration_s=20.0, speed="gentle")
amylose_binding = set_thermal_mixer(sample=amylose_binding_input, temperature_c=4.0, speed_rpm=10, duration_min=240.0)
amylose_collected = centrifuge_sample(sample=amylose_binding, speed_xg=5000, duration_min=4.0, temperature_c=4.0)
amylose_washed = mix_sample(sample=amylose_collected, method="pipetting", duration_s=60.0, speed="gentle")
amylose_wash_collected = centrifuge_sample(sample=amylose_washed, speed_xg=3000, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=4.0)
amylose_eluate = set_thermal_mixer(sample=amylose_wash_collected, temperature_c=4.0, speed_rpm=15, duration_min=7.0)
ninta_binding_input = mix_sample(sample=amylose_eluate, method="pipetting", duration_s=20.0, speed="gentle")
ninta_binding = set_thermal_mixer(sample=ninta_binding_input, temperature_c=4.0, speed_rpm=10, duration_min=180.0)
ninta_collected = centrifuge_sample(sample=ninta_binding, speed_xg=3000, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=4.0)
ninta_washed = mix_sample(sample=ninta_collected, method="pipetting", duration_s=30.0, speed="gentle")
ninta_wash_collected = centrifuge_sample(sample=ninta_washed, speed_xg=3000, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=4.0)
final_firmm_elution = vortex_mix(sample=ninta_wash_collected, duration_s=600.0, speed_rpm=1100, temperature_c=4.0)
protocol_title: Reconstitution of the antagonistic effect between C1orf112/FIRRM-FIGNL1 and BRCA2 on RAD51 filament stabilization

background: In the BL-HiChIP workflow, immunoprecipitated chromatin has already been captured on magnetic beads and washed. The current stage focuses on releasing DNA-protein complexes from the beads, reversing crosslinks with Proteinase K, extracting and precipitating DNA, and finally resuspending and quantifying the recovered BL-HiChIP DNA.
goal: Process the washed immunoprecipitation bead sample through elution, reverse crosslinking, organic extraction, DNA precipitation, pellet cleanup, final resuspension, and concentration measurement to obtain purified BL-HiChIP DNA ready for downstream QC.
constraints: Use the washed immunoprecipitation bead material as the starting sample and maintain a single dependency chain through purification.
Perform bead elution under thermal mixing conditions corresponding to 65 degrees Celsius, 900 rpm, and 30 minutes.
Represent the repeated elution outcome as a combined eluate totaling 600 microliters before reverse crosslinking.
Reverse crosslinking must include Proteinase K digestion at 55 degrees Celsius overnight; normalize overnight as 16 hours.
The phase-separation tube preparation requires a high-speed spin at 16000 x g for 0.5 minutes at 25 degrees Celsius before extraction.
After adding phenol-chloroform-isoamyl alcohol, vigorously mix and then centrifuge at 16000 x g for 5 minutes at 25 degrees Celsius.
DNA precipitation must reflect isopropanol and sodium acetate conditions followed by storage at minus 20 degrees Celsius overnight; normalize overnight as 16 hours.
Pellet recovery requires centrifugation at 16000 x g for 30 minutes at 4 degrees Celsius.
After ethanol washes, include a drying step of 5 minutes at 25 degrees Celsius before final dissolution.
Final DNA dissolution must occur in 10 mM Tris-HCl at 37 degrees Celsius for 60 minutes, followed by a concentration readout with a Qubit-style fluorometric assay and a recorded concentration metric.

available_inputs.name: washed_ip_beads
available_inputs.description: Washed BL-HiChIP immunoprecipitation beads after the final wash series

available_inputs.name: dna_elution_buffer
available_inputs.description: DNA elution buffer used to release protein-chromatin DNA complexes from beads

available_inputs.name: proteinase_k_digest_mix
available_inputs.description: Proteinase K reagent already combined conceptually with the eluate for reverse crosslinking

available_inputs.name: maxtract_tube
available_inputs.description: MaXtract high-density tube prepared for phenol-chloroform extraction

available_inputs.name: organic_extraction_mix
available_inputs.description: Phenol-chloroform-isoamyl alcohol extraction mixture

available_inputs.name: dna_precipitation_mix
available_inputs.description: Isopropanol, sodium acetate, and GlycoBlue precipitation setup

available_inputs.name: tris_resuspension_buffer
available_inputs.description: 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0 used for final DNA resuspension

gold_program:
eluted_beads = set_thermal_mixer(sample=washed_ip_beads, temperature_c=65.0, speed_rpm=900, duration_min=30.0)
combined_eluate = mix_sample(sample=eluted_beads, method="pipetting", duration_s=20.0, speed="medium")
reverse_crosslinked = incubate_sample(sample=proteinase_k_digest_mix, temperature_c=55.0, duration_min=960.0, condition="static")
prepared_maxtract = centrifuge_sample(sample=maxtract_tube, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=25.0)
extraction_mixed = vortex_mix(sample=organic_extraction_mix, duration_s=10.0, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=25.0)
phase_separated = centrifuge_sample(sample=extraction_mixed, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=25.0)
precipitation_mixed = mix_sample(sample=dna_precipitation_mix, method="inversion", duration_s=15.0, speed="gentle")
precipitated_dna = store_sample(sample=precipitation_mixed, temperature_c=-20.0, duration_h=16.0, container="1.5 mL tube")
dna_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=precipitated_dna, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=30.0, temperature_c=4.0)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=dna_pellet, method="air dry", temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=5.0)
resuspended_dna = incubate_sample(sample=tris_resuspension_buffer, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=60.0, condition="static")
qubit_readout = measure_signal(sample=resuspended_dna, assay="Qubit fluorometer", wavelength_nm=0, replicate_n=1)
recorded_concentration = record_measurement(sample=qubit_readout, metric="DNA concentration", unit="ng/uL", note="BL-HiChIP DNA after purification and resuspension")

source_steps.description: Wash 150 μL of resuspend protein G magnetic beads with 1 mL of 1 × PBST buffer three times and split equally into three 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes (50 μL/tube). Add the supernatant of sonicated chromatin to the tube (1 mL/tube) to resuspend the beads. Pre-clearing of chromatin with rotation at 4°C for 1 h to remove nonspecific binding of chromatin to beads. Note: After pre-clearing, take 10 μL of precleared chromatin solution as input material for quality check of sonication. Place the solution at -20°C until use.

After pre-clearing, wash the antibody-coated magnetic beads (from step 13) three times with 1 mL of 1 × PBST buffer and split equally into three 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes. Next, resuspend the antibody-coated magnetic beads with precleared chromatin solution from step 17e (1 mL/tube). Rotate the tube at 4°C overnight (more than 8 h).

Reverse crosslinking and DNA purification. Troubleshooting 5. The next day, combine the immunoprecipitation beads of three tubes into one tube, then wash the beads three times with ChIP wash buffer 1, twice with ChIP wash buffer 2, twice with ChIP wash buffer 3 and twice with ChIP wash buffer 4, separately.

Place the tube on the magnetic rack for 1 min. Discard the supernatant and resuspend the beads in 200 μL of DNA elution buffer. Place the tube in an Eppendorf MixMate with shaking (900 rpm) at 65°C for 30 min.
Place the tube on the magnetic rack for 1 min and transfer the 200 μL of DNA elution buffer (contains protein-chromatin DNA complex) to a new 1.5 mL tube.
Repeat step b and c twice and combine the DNA elution buffer, totally 600 μL.

Add 10 μL of Proteinase K to 600 μL of protein-chromatin DNA complex solution, mix and incubate at 55°C overnight for reverse crosslinking. Note: All the samples for quality check (digestion, ligation, and input) should be digested with Proteinase K in DNA elution buffer. Then followed by the next steps.
Prepare four MaXtract high-density tubes by centrifuging it at 16000 × g for 30 s at 25°C.

Add the solution digested with proteinase K and an equal volume of phenol-chloroform-isoamyl alcohol into centrifuged MaXtract high-density tube. Mix vigorously and centrifuge at 16000 × g for 5 min at 25°C.

Transfer the supernatant (above the gel matrix) to a new 1.5 mL tube. Then add 600 μL of isopropanol, 60 μL of 3 M sodium acetate, and 1 μL of GlycoBlue into the tube for precipitating DNA. Mix the solution by inverting the tube and incubate at -20°C overnight.
Centrifuge at 16000 × g for 30 min at 4°C to pellet DNA. Wash the pellet twice with 1 mL fresh ice-cold 75% ethanol. Remove the ethanol and dry the pellet for 5 min at 25°C.

Resuspend the pellet with 20 μL of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH = 8.0) and dissolve it at 37°C for 1 h. Measure DNA concentration using Qubit 4.0 fluorometer and take 1 μL of BL-HiChIP DNA to assay DNA size distribution ( Figure 2 C). Pause point: The BL-HiChIP DNA can be stored at -20°C for up to several months.

background: A yeast sphingolipid extract in tube_2 has already been prepared from earlier extraction work. The present segment covers optional mild base hydrolysis to distinguish complex sphingolipids from glycerophospholipids, then drying and a short purification stage that uses desalting and repeated butanol partitioning before collecting the enriched lipid fraction in a fresh tube.
goal: Advance the existing extract through optional NaOH treatment, cleanup, and butanol-based enrichment so the purified complex sphingolipid-containing fraction is ready in the final collection tube.
constraints: Start with tube_2 and include the optional hydrolysis branch with a 120.0 min incubation at 30.0 C.
Use centrifugation at 20600 x g with both 1.0 min and 3.0 min durations, treating room temperature as 25.0 C.
Include a neutralization-associated clarification and then a complete drying step at 30.0 C before the purification stage.
The latter part should reflect repeated extraction-style mixing and phase-separation behavior, ending with recovery into a final tube after a wash/recovery sequence.

gold_program:
resuspended_extract = vortex_mix(sample=tube_2, duration_s=5.0, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=25.0)
clarified_extract = centrifuge_sample(sample=resuspended_extract, speed_xg=20600, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
base_treated_extract = vortex_mix(sample=clarified_extract, duration_s=5.0, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=25.0)
base_incubated_extract = incubate_sample(sample=base_treated_extract, temperature_c=30.0, duration_min=120.0, condition="static")
post_base_extract = centrifuge_sample(sample=base_incubated_extract, speed_xg=20600, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
neutralized_extract = vortex_mix(sample=post_base_extract, duration_s=5.0, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=25.0)
dried_extract = dry_sample(sample=neutralized_extract, method="nitrogen gas", temperature_c=30.0, duration_min=30.0)
desalted_extract = vortex_mix(sample=dried_extract, duration_s=5.0, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=25.0)
washed_extract = centrifuge_sample(sample=desalted_extract, speed_xg=20600, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
butanol_extract_1 = vortex_mix(sample=washed_extract, duration_s=5.0, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=25.0)
butanol_phase_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=butanol_extract_1, speed_xg=20600, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=25.0)
final_recovered_fraction = centrifuge_sample(sample=butanol_phase_1, speed_xg=20600, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=25.0)

source_steps.description: Add a 100 μL volume of CMW to the tube #1, vortex vigorously, centrifuge at 20,600 g for 3 min at RT, and transfer the supernatant to the tube #2.

Dry the combined supernatants in the tube #2 completely with N 2 gas using pressure gas blowing concentrator at 30°C. CRITICAL: Drying the CMW in the samples with N 2 gas must be done in a draft chamber, to prevent the radioactive-labeled lipid contained in the organic solvents from being aerosolized and contaminating the laboratory space. CRITICAL: Confirm with facility staff that maintains the building infrastructure that the draft chamber is equipped with an appropriate engineering control (i.e., trap) that limits radioactivity exposure to ensure that radioactive contamination of N 2 or potential exhaust lines for the building does not occur. Pause point: Samples can be stored at –20°C (for about 1 month).

NaOH treatment (Optional) To differentiate complex sphingolipids from glycerophospholipids, lipid extracts can be subjected to mild base hydrolysis with NaOH, which selectively deacylates glycerophospholipids ( Puoti et al., 1991 ). Add 100 μL of CMW to the tube #2, vortex well and centrifuge at 20,600 g for 1 min at RT.
Add 16 μL of 0.6 N NaOH (in methanol), vortex well and incubate for 2 h at 30°C.
Centrifuge at 20,600 g for 1 min at RT.
Add 16 μL of 0.6 N AcOH (in methanol), vortex well and centrifuge at 20,600 g for 1 min at RT.

Dry the samples in the tube #2 completely with N 2 gas using pressure gas blowing concentrator at 30°C. CRITICAL: Drying the CMW in the samples with N 2 gas must be done in a draft chamber. Pause point: Samples can be stored at –20°C (for about 1 month).
Butanol purification ( Figure 1 ) To desalt, add 100 μL of water to the tube #2, vortex well and spin down at 20,600 g for 1 min at RT.

Add 100 μL of water-saturated 1-butanol to the tube #2, vortex well, centrifuge at 20,600 g for 3 min at RT and transfer the butanol (upper) phase containing complex sphingolipids to a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf microcentrifuge tube (tube #3). CRITICAL: Butanol is a highly flammable and corrosive solvent that causes skin, eye, and respiratory tract irritation. When handling this solvent, we recommend using the appropriate PPE such as goggles, gloves (low breakthrough), a mask, a lab coat, and a draft chamber.

In order to collect completely the remaining radiolabeled lipids in the water phase in the tube #2, add 100 μL of water-saturated 1-butanol to the tube #2, vortex well, centrifuge at 20,600 g for 3 min at RT and transfer the butanol phase to the tube #3. Repeat this step two more times. Note: To avoid the loss of the sample attached to the pipette tip, use the same tip repeatedly for each sample.
Add 100 μL of water to the tube #3, vortex well, centrifuge at 20,600 g for 3 min at RT and transfer the butanol phase to a new 1.5 mL tube (tube #4).

In order to collect the remaining radiolabeled lipids in the tube #3, add 200 μL of butanol to the tube #3, vortex well, centrifuge at 20,600 g for 3 min at RT and transfer the butanol phase to tube #4.

background: A yeast lipid extract prepared for IPC analysis has reached the optional mild-base and desalting segment. The current material in tube 2 must be hydrolyzed under mild alkaline conditions to remove glycerophospholipid interference, neutralized, dried, and then carried through a condensed butanol cleanup so the complex sphingolipid-enriched fraction is recovered in a final tube for later separation.
goal: Process the current extract through mild base treatment and a condensed butanol purification workflow to obtain a fully dried sphingolipid-enriched sample ready for downstream separation.
constraints: Start from tube_2 with an initial remix and a brief room-temperature centrifugation, then perform the mild-base incubation for 120 min at 30C using the NaOH treatment stage represented in the protocol.

After incubation, include a 20600 x g, 1 min, room-temperature clarification spin, then represent the neutralization stage and follow it with another 20600 x g, 1 min, room-temperature spin before purification.
A complete drying step at 30C must occur after neutralization and before the desalting or butanol-cleanup portion begins.

The cleanup portion must include repeated extraction-style remix and centrifugation operations using the same recurring action families, with at least one pair of related spins differing mainly by duration such as 1 min versus 3 min while keeping 20600 x g and room temperature fixed.
Include a water-wash style remix/spin on the collected butanol-enriched state before the final solvent removal.
Finish with complete drying of the final enriched sample at 30C, and keep the program as a single dependency chain using only the provided action-pool functions.

source_steps.description: NaOH treatment (Optional) To differentiate complex sphingolipids from glycerophospholipids, lipid extracts can be subjected to mild base hydrolysis with NaOH, which selectively deacylates glycerophospholipids ( Puoti et al., 1991 ). Add 100 μL of CMW to the tube #2, vortex well and centrifuge at 20,600 g for 1 min at RT.
Add 16 μL of 0.6 N NaOH (in methanol), vortex well and incubate for 2 h at 30°C.
Centrifuge at 20,600 g for 1 min at RT.
Add 16 μL of 0.6 N AcOH (in methanol), vortex well and centrifuge at 20,600 g for 1 min at RT.

Dry the samples in the tube #2 completely with N 2 gas using pressure gas blowing concentrator at 30°C. CRITICAL: Drying the CMW in the samples with N 2 gas must be done in a draft chamber. Pause point: Samples can be stored at –20°C (for about 1 month).
Butanol purification ( Figure 1 ) To desalt, add 100 μL of water to the tube #2, vortex well and spin down at 20,600 g for 1 min at RT.

Add 100 μL of water-saturated 1-butanol to the tube #2, vortex well, centrifuge at 20,600 g for 3 min at RT and transfer the butanol (upper) phase containing complex sphingolipids to a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf microcentrifuge tube (tube #3). CRITICAL: Butanol is a highly flammable and corrosive solvent that causes skin, eye, and respiratory tract irritation. When handling this solvent, we recommend using the appropriate PPE such as goggles, gloves (low breakthrough), a mask, a lab coat, and a draft chamber.

In order to collect completely the remaining radiolabeled lipids in the water phase in the tube #2, add 100 μL of water-saturated 1-butanol to the tube #2, vortex well, centrifuge at 20,600 g for 3 min at RT and transfer the butanol phase to the tube #3. Repeat this step two more times. Note: To avoid the loss of the sample attached to the pipette tip, use the same tip repeatedly for each sample.
Add 100 μL of water to the tube #3, vortex well, centrifuge at 20,600 g for 3 min at RT and transfer the butanol phase to a new 1.5 mL tube (tube #4).

In order to collect the remaining radiolabeled lipids in the tube #3, add 200 μL of butanol to the tube #3, vortex well, centrifuge at 20,600 g for 3 min at RT and transfer the butanol phase to tube #4.

Dry the combined supernatants in the tube #4 completely with N 2 gas using pressure gas blowing concentrator at 30°C. CRITICAL: Drying the butanol in the samples with N 2 gas must be done in a draft chamber, to prevent the radioactive-labeled lipid contained in the organic solvents from being aerosolized and contaminating the laboratory space. Pause point: Samples can be stored at –20°C (for about 1 month).

Lipid separation ( Figure 2 ) Note: Prepare a developing solvent I (chloroform-methanol-0.25% KCl (55/45/10, v / v / v) solvent mixture) ( Puoti et al., 1991 ) in a medium bottle by the day before, and stir for 16–18 hours with a stirrer and a stirrer chip. Note: Add the solvent to the tank (lined with filter papers) till about 1 cm from the bottom 2 h before TLC development, and fill the tank with the volatilized solvent. CRITICAL: The use of chloroform solvent is a health hazard. When handling this solvent, we recommend using the appropriate PPE. Add 25 μL of CMW to the tube #4 and vortex well. Note: Rotate the tube #4 to dissolve the lipids on the upper wall of the tube.

background: Mouse embryonic stem cell chromatin samples have already undergone antibody binding and are now at the bead-capture and wash stage of a ChIP workflow. The next work segment focuses on collecting the immunocomplexes, performing cold wash cycles, and carrying out two room-temperature elutions to recover DNA-containing material for downstream analysis.
goal: Process the antibody-bound chromatin through bead pelleting, cold washes, and sequential elution so that the final recovered eluate is ready in a microcentrifuge tube.
constraints: Begin from a sample that has already completed the long antibody incubation and then perform a brief collection spin at 2000 x g for 10 s at room temperature; normalize 10 s as 0.17 min.
Protein A/G bead binding must occur at 4 degrees C with rotational mixing equivalent to 70 rpm for a duration within 5-8 h; use a consistent single duration in that allowed range.
After bead capture, pellet beads using 2500 x g for 0.5 min at 4 degrees C before wash handling.
Three distinct cold wash buffers are used in sequence, each with 1 mL buffer and a 5 min incubation at 4 degrees C with rotation-like mixing before the next pelleting step.
The first two wash pelleting steps should both use 2500 x g for 0.5 min at 4 degrees C.

Elution is performed twice from the washed bead pellet using ChIP elution buffer, with each elution represented by room-temperature mixing for 15 min and followed by a stronger 8000 x g spin for 1 min.

The same sample lineage should be maintained across the chain so that later centrifugation and incubation steps consume outputs from the prior wash or elution state rather than restarting from the original material.
Use only action-pool abstractions; do not model liquid-transfer mechanics directly, but preserve the dependence on prepared bead solution, cold conditions, and sequential elution recovery.

available_inputs.description: Cold ChIP wash buffer I prepared for the first wash.

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer_II_tube
available_inputs.description: Cold ChIP wash buffer II prepared for the second wash.

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer_III_tube

gold_program:
spin_collected = centrifuge_sample(sample=chip_tube, speed_xg=2000, duration_min=0.17, temperature_c=23.0)
bead_bound = set_thermal_mixer(sample=spin_collected, temperature_c=4.0, speed_rpm=70, duration_min=360.0)
pellet_after_binding = centrifuge_sample(sample=bead_bound, speed_xg=2500, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=4.0)
wash1 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=pellet_after_binding, temperature_c=4.0, speed_rpm=70, duration_min=5.0)
pellet_after_wash1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash1, speed_xg=2500, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=4.0)
wash2 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=pellet_after_wash1, temperature_c=4.0, speed_rpm=70, duration_min=5.0)
pellet_after_wash2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash2, speed_xg=2500, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=4.0)
wash3 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=pellet_after_wash2, temperature_c=4.0, speed_rpm=70, duration_min=5.0)
first_elution = set_thermal_mixer(sample=wash3, temperature_c=23.0, speed_rpm=70, duration_min=15.0)
first_elution_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=first_elution, speed_xg=8000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=23.0)
second_elution = set_thermal_mixer(sample=first_elution_spin, temperature_c=23.0, speed_rpm=70, duration_min=15.0)
final_eluate = centrifuge_sample(sample=second_elution, speed_xg=8000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=23.0)
protocol_title: Protocol for chromatin immunoprecipitation and hematovascular function assays with differentiated mouse embryonic stem cells

source_steps.description: Incubate the tube for 14–18 h at 4°C on a rotator (70 rpm).
After incubation, spin down the tube at 2,000 × g for 10 s at 22°C–25°C to collect any sample from the lid.

Add 50 μL of the prepared protein A/G PLUS bead solution (from step 6 in the before you begin section) to each tube and incubate at 4°C on a rotator (70 rpm) for 5–8 h. Note: Avoid over-incubation at this step, as extended bead binding time may increase non-specific interactions and negatively affect assay specificity. Keep incubation time consistent between experiments.
Release DNA from the antibody-captured protein-DNA complex. Centrifuge the tube at 2,500 × g for 30 s at 4°C and carefully discard the supernatant.
Add 1 mL of cold ChIP wash buffer I to each tube. Incubate the tube on a rotator at 4°C (70 rpm) for 5 min.
Centrifuge the tube at 2,500 × g for 30 s at 4°C, discard the supernatant, and wash with 1 mL of cold ChIP wash buffer II as step 14b.
Centrifuge the tube at 2,500 × g for 30 s at 4°C, discard the supernatant, and wash with 1 mL of cold ChIP wash buffer III as step 14b.
After the final wash, carefully remove all the supernatant. Add 150 μL of ChIP elution buffer to the bead pellet.
Vortex the tube at maximum speed for 5 s, then incubate at 22°C–25°C for 15 min on a rotator (70 rpm).
Centrifuge the tube at 8,000 × g for 1 min and transfer the supernatant to a 1.7 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Add another 150 μL of ChIP elution buffer to the tube containing the beads, vortex again at maximum speed for 5 s, and incubate at 22°C–25°C for 15 min on a rotator (70 rpm).
Centrifuge at 8,000 × g for 1 min and combine the supernatant with the initial eluate from step 14g.

background: This item covers the late BL-HiChIP recovery stage after chromatin has been captured on antibody-coated magnetic beads and the wash series is complete. The remaining work is to release the chromatin-DNA complex from beads, carry out reverse crosslinking with proteinase K, perform organic extraction using MaXtract tubes, precipitate DNA at low temperature, pellet it by high-speed centrifugation, and finish with ethanol cleanup and brief drying.
goal: Convert washed immunoprecipitation bead material into a dried DNA pellet suitable for later resuspension by executing the required elution, digestion, extraction, precipitation, and cleanup operations.
constraints: Start from already washed immunoprecipitation magnetic beads and keep the workflow within this local recovery segment.
Represent repeated elution from beads using a thermal mixer at 65 C and 900 rpm for 30 min, followed by 1 min magnetic separation to retain supernatant.
Include an overnight reverse-crosslinking digestion at 55 C, a MaXtract preparation spin at 16000 x g for 0.5 min at 25 C, and an extraction spin at 16000 x g for 5 min at 25 C.
Model DNA precipitation as an overnight hold at -20 C followed by pelleting at 16000 x g for 30 min at 4 C.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keyword arguments only, exactly 8-12 top-level assignments, and reuse at least two action names more than once.

available_inputs.description: Combined antibody-bound magnetic beads after the completed BL-HiChIP wash sequence.

available_inputs.description: DNA elution buffer used to release chromatin-associated material from the beads.

available_inputs.name: proteinase_k_mix
available_inputs.description: Proteinase K added to the combined eluate for reverse crosslinking and digestion.

available_inputs.name: maxtract_tubes
available_inputs.description: MaXtract high-density tubes for phenol-chloroform phase separation.

available_inputs.name: phenol_chloroform_isoamyl
available_inputs.description: Phenol-chloroform-isoamyl alcohol used during organic extraction.

available_inputs.name: precipitation_reagents
available_inputs.description: Isopropanol, sodium acetate, and GlycoBlue used for DNA precipitation.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_75
available_inputs.description: Fresh ice-cold 75 percent ethanol for DNA pellet washing.

gold_program:
elution_1 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=washed_ip_beads, temperature_c=65, speed_rpm=900, duration_min=30)
sep_1 = magnetic_separate(sample=elution_1, rack="magnetic rack", duration_min=1, collect_phase="supernatant")
elution_2 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=sep_1, temperature_c=65, speed_rpm=900, duration_min=30)
sep_2 = magnetic_separate(sample=elution_2, rack="magnetic rack", duration_min=1, collect_phase="supernatant")
digested_complex = incubate_sample(sample=proteinase_k_mix, temperature_c=55, duration_min=960, condition="static")
prepared_maxtract = centrifuge_sample(sample=maxtract_tubes, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=25)
extracted_sample = centrifuge_sample(sample=phenol_chloroform_isoamyl, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=25)
precipitated_dna = cool_sample(sample=precipitation_reagents, temperature_c=-20, duration_min=960, device="freezer")
pelleted_dna = centrifuge_sample(sample=precipitated_dna, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=30, temperature_c=4)
ethanol_wash = centrifuge_sample(sample=ethanol_75, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=4)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=ethanol_wash, method="air dry", temperature_c=25, duration_min=5)

source_steps.description: Centrifuge the sonicated chromatin at 16000 × g for 15 min at 4°C.

Wash 150 μL of resuspend protein G magnetic beads with 1 mL of 1 × PBST buffer three times and split equally into three 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes (50 μL/tube). Add the supernatant of sonicated chromatin to the tube (1 mL/tube) to resuspend the beads. Pre-clearing of chromatin with rotation at 4°C for 1 h to remove nonspecific binding of chromatin to beads. Note: After pre-clearing, take 10 μL of precleared chromatin solution as input material for quality check of sonication. Place the solution at -20°C until use.

After pre-clearing, wash the antibody-coated magnetic beads (from step 13) three times with 1 mL of 1 × PBST buffer and split equally into three 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes. Next, resuspend the antibody-coated magnetic beads with precleared chromatin solution from step 17e (1 mL/tube). Rotate the tube at 4°C overnight (more than 8 h).

Reverse crosslinking and DNA purification. Troubleshooting 5. The next day, combine the immunoprecipitation beads of three tubes into one tube, then wash the beads three times with ChIP wash buffer 1, twice with ChIP wash buffer 2, twice with ChIP wash buffer 3 and twice with ChIP wash buffer 4, separately.

Place the tube on the magnetic rack for 1 min. Discard the supernatant and resuspend the beads in 200 μL of DNA elution buffer. Place the tube in an Eppendorf MixMate with shaking (900 rpm) at 65°C for 30 min.
Place the tube on the magnetic rack for 1 min and transfer the 200 μL of DNA elution buffer (contains protein-chromatin DNA complex) to a new 1.5 mL tube.
Repeat step b and c twice and combine the DNA elution buffer, totally 600 μL.

Add 10 μL of Proteinase K to 600 μL of protein-chromatin DNA complex solution, mix and incubate at 55°C overnight for reverse crosslinking. Note: All the samples for quality check (digestion, ligation, and input) should be digested with Proteinase K in DNA elution buffer. Then followed by the next steps.
Prepare four MaXtract high-density tubes by centrifuging it at 16000 × g for 30 s at 25°C.

Add the solution digested with proteinase K and an equal volume of phenol-chloroform-isoamyl alcohol into centrifuged MaXtract high-density tube. Mix vigorously and centrifuge at 16000 × g for 5 min at 25°C.

Transfer the supernatant (above the gel matrix) to a new 1.5 mL tube. Then add 600 μL of isopropanol, 60 μL of 3 M sodium acetate, and 1 μL of GlycoBlue into the tube for precipitating DNA. Mix the solution by inverting the tube and incubate at -20°C overnight.
Centrifuge at 16000 × g for 30 min at 4°C to pellet DNA. Wash the pellet twice with 1 mL fresh ice-cold 75% ethanol. Remove the ethanol and dry the pellet for 5 min at 25°C.

background: Mouse embryonic stem cell-derived chromatin immunoprecipitation samples have already undergone antibody capture and are entering the bead-binding and wash/elution stage. The immediate task is to collect condensation, bind immune complexes to protein A/G beads at cold temperature with rotation, perform a sequence of cold washes, elute the captured material twice at room temperature, and finish by preparing the pooled eluate for reverse-crosslinking cleanup with salt, Proteinase K, and RNase A.
goal: Process the antibody-bound ChIP sample through bead capture, cold wash and double-elution handling, ending with a pooled eluate that has received the required cleanup reagent additions and is ready for downstream release of DNA.
constraints: Begin by collecting liquid from the tube lid with a brief spin at 2000 x g for 10 s at room temperature, normalized as 0.17 min at 22-25 C.
Protein A/G bead binding must use a cold incubation at 4 C with rotation-like mixing for 5-8 h; keep the condition consistent and do not extend beyond this window.
Wash-related pellet collections use centrifugation at 2500 x g for 0.5 min at 4 C and should be reused across the successive cold wash stages.
Represent the three wash exposures as separate cold incubations with wash buffers I, II, and III, each using 1 mL buffer and 5 min rotation-like mixing at 4 C.
After the final wash, the beads are eluted twice with 150 uL elution buffer each time, with vigorous resuspension represented by mixing before a 15 min room-temperature rotating incubation.
Each elution recovery uses centrifugation at 8000 x g for 1 min before combining eluates into a 300 uL pooled fraction.
The final pooled eluate must reflect addition of 12 uL 5 M NaCl, 9 uL of 10 mg/mL Proteinase K, and 1.2 uL of 20 mg/mL RNase A; model this as a final processing step on the pooled eluate.
Use only the provided action functions, keep all calls in keyword form, and maintain a dependency chain where later outputs consume earlier ones.

spin_lid_collected = centrifuge_sample(sample=chip_tube, speed_xg=2000, duration_min=0.17, temperature_c=23.0)
bead_binding = set_thermal_mixer(sample=spin_lid_collected, temperature_c=4.0, speed_rpm=70, duration_min=360.0)
pellet_after_binding = centrifuge_sample(sample=bead_binding, speed_xg=2500, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=4.0)
wash_I = set_thermal_mixer(sample=pellet_after_binding, temperature_c=4.0, speed_rpm=70, duration_min=5.0)
pellet_after_wash_I = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_I, speed_xg=2500, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=4.0)
wash_II = set_thermal_mixer(sample=pellet_after_wash_I, temperature_c=4.0, speed_rpm=70, duration_min=5.0)
pellet_after_wash_II = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_II, speed_xg=2500, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=4.0)
wash_III = set_thermal_mixer(sample=pellet_after_wash_II, temperature_c=4.0, speed_rpm=70, duration_min=5.0)
first_elution = set_thermal_mixer(sample=wash_III, temperature_c=23.0, speed_rpm=70, duration_min=15.0)
first_eluate = centrifuge_sample(sample=first_elution, speed_xg=8000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=23.0)
second_elution = set_thermal_mixer(sample=first_eluate, temperature_c=23.0, speed_rpm=70, duration_min=15.0)
pooled_eluate_ready = heat_sample(sample=second_elution, temperature_c=23.0, duration_min=1.0, device="cleanup_after_add_12uL_5M_NaCl_9uL_ProteinaseK_1p2uL_RNaseA")

source_steps.description: After incubation, spin down the tube at 2,000 × g for 10 s at 22°C–25°C to collect any sample from the lid.

Add 50 μL of the prepared protein A/G PLUS bead solution (from step 6 in the before you begin section) to each tube and incubate at 4°C on a rotator (70 rpm) for 5–8 h. Note: Avoid over-incubation at this step, as extended bead binding time may increase non-specific interactions and negatively affect assay specificity. Keep incubation time consistent between experiments.
Release DNA from the antibody-captured protein-DNA complex. Centrifuge the tube at 2,500 × g for 30 s at 4°C and carefully discard the supernatant.
Add 1 mL of cold ChIP wash buffer I to each tube. Incubate the tube on a rotator at 4°C (70 rpm) for 5 min.
Centrifuge the tube at 2,500 × g for 30 s at 4°C, discard the supernatant, and wash with 1 mL of cold ChIP wash buffer II as step 14b.
Centrifuge the tube at 2,500 × g for 30 s at 4°C, discard the supernatant, and wash with 1 mL of cold ChIP wash buffer III as step 14b.
After the final wash, carefully remove all the supernatant. Add 150 μL of ChIP elution buffer to the bead pellet.
Vortex the tube at maximum speed for 5 s, then incubate at 22°C–25°C for 15 min on a rotator (70 rpm).
Centrifuge the tube at 8,000 × g for 1 min and transfer the supernatant to a 1.7 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Add another 150 μL of ChIP elution buffer to the tube containing the beads, vortex again at maximum speed for 5 s, and incubate at 22°C–25°C for 15 min on a rotator (70 rpm).
Centrifuge at 8,000 × g for 1 min and combine the supernatant with the initial eluate from step 14g.

To each pooled eluate (300 μL), add 12 μL of 5 M NaCl (final concentration: 200 mM), 9 μL of 10 mg/mL Proteinase K (final concentration: 300 μg/mL), and 1.2 μL of 20 mg/mL RNase A (final concentration: 80 μg/mL).

background: A zebrafish CRISPR workflow has reached sgRNA preparation. A PCR product encoding the guide template is already available, and the immediate work is to recover that DNA, run T7 in vitro transcription, remove remaining DNA, purify the RNA through extraction and precipitation, and finish with a UV-based quantification of the recovered sgRNA.
goal: Complete the local sgRNA synthesis and cleanup segment starting from the PCR-derived DNA template and ending with a quantified RNA preparation in RNase-free water.
constraints: Represent both precipitation phases with cold holding at -20 C for at least 2 h, and represent both pellet recovery spins at 12000 x g for 10 min at 4 C.

The DNA template must be dried after its ethanol wash and then used as the transcription input; the transcription phase must be modeled as a 37 C incubation for 4.5 h followed by a DNase treatment at 37 C for 40 min.
Model organic cleanup as a phenol-chloroform extraction followed by a second chloroform extraction, with each extraction including a mixing step and a centrifugation at 10000 x g for 10 min at 4 C.
After the second precipitation, include ethanol wash, air drying, and final dissolution in 10 uL RNase-free water before measurement.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keep the program to 6-12 top-level calls, and make at least two action names recur on dependent intermediate samples.
End with a UV spectrophotometry readout of the final RNA sample at 260 nm rather than gel imaging.

available_inputs.name: phenol_chloroform_mix
available_inputs.description: Acidic phenol plus chloroform extraction reagent

available_inputs.name: chloroform
available_inputs.description: Chloroform for the second extraction

available_inputs.name: rnase_free_water
available_inputs.description: RNase-free water used for final RNA dissolution

gold_program:
template_precipitated = store_sample(sample=pcr_template, temperature_c=-20.0, duration_h=2.0, container='microcentrifuge_tube')
template_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=template_precipitated, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
template_dried = dry_sample(sample=template_pellet, method='air dry', temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=5.0)
transcribed_sgrna = incubate_sample(sample=t7_transcription_mix, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=270.0, condition='static')
dnase_treated_sgrna = incubate_sample(sample=transcribed_sgrna, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=40.0, condition='static')
phenol_chloroform_extracted = mix_sample(sample=dnase_treated_sgrna, method='inversion', duration_s=15.0, speed='moderate')
first_phase_separation = centrifuge_sample(sample=phenol_chloroform_extracted, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
chloroform_reextracted = mix_sample(sample=first_phase_separation, method='inversion', duration_s=15.0, speed='moderate')
rna_phase_separated = centrifuge_sample(sample=chloroform_reextracted, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
rna_precipitated = store_sample(sample=rna_phase_separated, temperature_c=-20.0, duration_h=2.0, container='microcentrifuge_tube')
final_rna_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=rna_precipitated, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
final_rna_signal = measure_signal(sample=final_rna_pellet, assay='nanodrop_uv_spectrophotometry', wavelength_nm=260, replicate_n=1)

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Perform a PCR reaction as follows to generate the double-stranded DNA template (depicted in Figure 2 B below). PCR reaction mix Reagent Amount sgRNA Oligo (100 μM) 1.25 μL Tail Oligo (100 μM) 1.25 μL DNA Polymerase 2.0 μL 10× Buffer 10 μL dNTP (2.5 mM) 8 μL ddH 2 O 77.5 μL PCR cycling conditions PCR cycling conditions Steps Temperature Time Cycles Initial Denaturation 95°C 5 min 1 Denaturation 95°C 20 s 5 Annealing 52°C 20 s Extension 72°C 20 s Final extension 72°C 5 min 1 Hold 4°C Until Removal

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: To the 100-μL annealed and extended product (depicted above), add 10 μL of 3 M sodium acetate pH 5.3 and 500 μL of absolute ethanol and incubate for at least 2 h at −20°C. Overnight is preferable.

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 12,000 g for 10 min at 4°C, wash pellet with 70% ethanol, air dry, and dissolve in 10 μL of water.

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Use the template generated above in an in vitro RNA transcription reaction to generate the sgRNA. Perform a T7 RNA polymerase-driven in vitro transcription reaction as follows (the amount of DNA template used is typically at least 1 μg). Reaction mix Reagent Amount DNA Template 3 μL (1 μg) CTP 1 μL UTP 1 μL GTP 1 μL ATP 1 μL 10× Buffer 1 μL T7 Polymerase 1 μL DTT (0.1 M) 1 μL

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Incubate the above mix at 37°C for 4.5 h (we have found this duration to work well, but it can vary between 0.5–5 h depending on the reaction efficiency).

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Add 0.5 μL of DNase (1 unit/ μL) and continue to incubate at 37°C for 40 min.

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Perform a phenol-chloroform extraction by making up the volume to 200 μL with water, adding 100 μL of acidic phenol and 100 μL of chloroform, mixing by inverting and centrifuging at 10,000 g at 4°C for 10 min. Collect the supernatant and extract with an equal volume of chloroform. Collect the supernatant and proceed to RNA precipitation.

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Precipitate the RNA by adding 10 μL of 3 M sodium acetate pH 5.3 and 500 μL of absolute ethanol and incubating at least 2 h at −20°C (alternatively, 5–10 min at −80°C can be used).

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 12,000 g for 10 min at 4°C, wash pellet with 70% ethanol, air dry, and dissolve in 10 μL of RNAse-free water.

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Quantify the RNA using a Nano drop UV spectrophotometer and verify integrity by resolving the RNA sample via 10% urea-PAGE ( Summer et al., 2009 ) as follows. Dissolve 4.2 g of urea in 2 mL of water with agitation and boiling. After cooling to room temperature, add 2 mL of 5× TBE, 3.3 mL of 30% Acrylamide / Bis-acrylamide Mix, 100 μL of 10% APS, 10 μL of TEMED and make up to 10 mL with water. Pour the gel and perform a pre-run for 20–30 min at 5 V/cm. Prepare the RNA sample as follows; take 0. 5 μL of RNA, add 4.5 μL of water and 5 μL of 2× formamide dye. Heat the 10 μL RNA sample at 95°C for 5 min and spin briefly. Flush the wells with 1× TBE to remove urea, load the RNA samples, run until the dye front migrates 3/4 th distance of the gel and stain with ethidium bromide (0.5 μg/mL) for 10 min. Visualize RNA integrity using a gel imaging system. Urea-PAGE Gel Recipe Reagent Amount Urea 4.2 g Water 2 mL 5× TBE 2 mL 30% Acrylamide/Bis-acrylamide mix 3.3 mL 10% APS 100 μL TEMED 10 μL Water To 10 mL 5× TBE Recipe Reagent Amount Tris Base 54 g Boric Acid 27.5 g 0.5 M EDTA pH 8 20 mL Water To 1 L

source_steps.stage: In vitro catalytic activity assay for assessing guide RNA efficiency
source_steps.description: Perform an in vitro catalytic activity assay with purified Cas9, sgRNA, and the target region amplicon ( Grainger et al., 2017 ). Assemble a ribonucleoprotein complex by adding the following reagents and incubating the mix at 37°C for 10 min. Reaction mix Reagent Amount sgRNA (500 ng/μL) 1 μL NEB buffer 3.1 1 μL Cas9 (250 ng/μL) 1 μL Water 8 μL

source_steps.stage: In vitro catalytic activity assay for assessing guide RNA efficiency
source_steps.description: Add 2 μL of the target-region PCR amplicon to the above mix and incubate further at 37°C for 1 h. Add 1 μL RNase A and incubate at 37°C for 30 min, and then add 1 μL Proteinase K and further incubate at 37°C for 30 min.

background: A purified single tRNA iso-decoder eluate has already been separated from capture beads and now needs post-capture processing before downstream modification analysis. At this stage, residual biotinylated probe should be digested, the RNA should be cleaned on a small-RNA-compatible spin column under high-ethanol binding conditions, the column should be washed and dried, and the tRNA should be eluted for concentration assessment.
goal: Process the tRNA eluate through probe removal, column-based RNA cleanup, elution, and final quantification record generation so the isolated tRNA iso-decoder is ready for downstream analysis.
constraints: Include an exonuclease digestion incubation at room temperature, represented as 22.0-25.0 C, for 45 min after mixing in the digestion reagents.
Represent addition and homogenization of cleanup reagents using a gentle pipetting-style mix rather than vortexing when the binding and ethanol solutions are present.
Capture the small-RNA column binding workflow with two separate high-speed spins because the full mixture exceeds a single loading volume; both spins should use the same cleanup stream.
Use max-speed centrifugation normalized as 16000 x g for all column spins, each for 1 min, and include a distinct dry-spin after the wash phase.
Model the column wash phase as two repeated wash cycles before the dry spin.
Elute with 23 uL RNase-free water and then generate a concentration measurement record from the recovered eluate.
Preserve the dependency chain so later cleanup, wash, dry, elution, and measurement actions consume outputs from earlier actions rather than restarting from the initial sample.
Do not introduce unsupported transfer, magnetic separation, or kit-specific manipulation primitives beyond the provided action pool.

mixed_exoI = mix_sample(sample=exoI_reaction, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
incubated_exoI = incubate_sample(sample=mixed_exoI, temperature_c=23.5, duration_min=45.0, condition="static")
cleanup_loaded = mix_sample(sample=ethanol_binding_mix, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
column_bind_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=cleanup_loaded, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
column_bind_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=column_bind_1, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
wash_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=column_bind_2, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
wash_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_1, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
dry_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_2, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
elution_ready = mix_sample(sample=dry_spin, method="pipetting", duration_s=5.0, speed="gentle")
eluted_tRNA = centrifuge_sample(sample=elution_ready, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
quant_read = measure_signal(sample=eluted_tRNA, assay="Qubit", wavelength_nm=0, replicate_n=1)
concentration_record = record_measurement(sample=quant_read, metric="tRNA concentration", unit="ng/uL", note="Eluted in 23 uL RNase-free water after 2-volume ethanol cleanup and two wash cycles")

source_steps.description: Centrifuge the tRNA eluates at 20,000 × g for 3 min, then apply the tube to the magnet. Transfer the supernatant to a new tube to remove any remaining beads from the eluate.

To remove any remaining Biotin DNA probe from the purified tRNAs, add 17 μL of 10X Exonuclease I buffer and 3 μL of Exonuclease I to the eluate. Mix thoroughly and perform a quick spin. Incubate at 22°C to 25°C for 45 min.

Clean up the tRNAs after the Exonuclease I treatment using the NEB Monarch Spin RNA Cleanup Kit. Note: We perform the clean-up steps using the Monarch Spin RNA Cleanup Kit, but any other RNA cleanup kit can be used following the manufacturer's protocol for small RNA. Add 2 volumes (340 μL) of NEB binding buffer and mix thoroughly by pipetting.

Add 2 volumes (1020 μL) of 100% ethanol and mix by pipetting. CRITICAL: Mix gently with care to prevent spilling of liquid, as the 1.6 mL microcentrifuge tube will be full at this step. Do not vortex. CRITICAL: According to the NEB Monarch Spin RNA Cleanup Kit protocol, adding 1 volume of ethanol in this step will enable the binding of RNA longer than 25 nt in length. However, we find that the addition of 2 volumes of ethanol leads to more efficient binding of tRNAs to the column.
Add 750 μL of the mixture to an NEB RNA cleanup column and centrifuge for 1 min at max speed (> 16000 × g). Remove liquid from the collecting tube.
Repeat step 41c to add the rest of the mixture (∼780 μL) to the same NEB RNA cleanup column and centrifuge for 1 min at max speed (> 16000 × g). Remove liquid from the collecting tube.
Add 500 μL of wash buffer and centrifuge for 1 min at max speed (> 16000 × g). Remove the liquid from the collecting tube after centrifugation. Repeat this step once again to wash the column twice.
After the last wash, remove all flow through and re-centrifuge the empty column for 1 min. Note: This dry spin is to ensure no trace of salt or ethanol is carried over to the next step.
Transfer the column to a new RNase-free microcentrifuge tube.

Add 23 μL of RNase-free water directly over the matrix in the center of the column without disturbing the matrix. Note: Make sure the water is added directly over the matrix rather than on the side of the tube. Since the tRNAs are bound to the matrix, it needs to be fully exposed to the water to ensure effective elution.
Centrifuge for 1 min at max speed (> 16000 × g) to collect the tRNA iso-decoder in the elution.
Quantify the concentration of tRNAs by Nanodrop or Qubit.

background: This item targets the MNase-ISO cleanup segment after yeast cells have become fragile from prior wall digestion. The experiment is in the nucleosome-fragment recovery phase, where cells are kept cold, chromatin is digested with MNase, the reaction is quenched and extracted, and DNA is precipitated, RNase-treated, and recovered in water for later gel-based mononucleosome selection and sequencing.
goal: Carry the fragile cell preparation through MNase digestion and DNA recovery to obtain a cleaned MNase DNA sample resuspended in water for downstream analysis.
constraints: Begin with a cold low-speed pellet step of 500 x g for 3 min at 4°C, and include two gentle sorbitol wash/resuspension events before MNase digestion.
Represent MNase digestion at 37°C for 8 min, then terminate the reaction with mixing and a 65°C treatment lasting within 5-10 min.
Include an organic extraction mix followed by a room-temperature high-speed centrifugation above 14000 x g for 7-10 min, then an ethanol-precipitation spin above 14000 x g for 5 min.
After pellet recovery, include TE/RNase handling with a 37°C incubation for 30 min, then a second ammonium acetate plus ethanol precipitation with another >14000 x g, 5 min spin.

Finish by drying the pellet and producing a water-resuspended MNase DNA output; use only provided action-pool functions, keyword arguments only, 6-12 top-level calls, and reuse at least two action names multiple times.

step1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=zymolyase_cells, speed_xg=500, duration_min=3, temperature_c=4)
step2 = mix_sample(sample=step1, method="gentle resuspension in ice-cold 1 M sorbitol wash", duration_s=20, speed="gentle")
step3 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step2, speed_xg=500, duration_min=3, temperature_c=4)
step4 = mix_sample(sample=step3, method="gentle resuspension in ice-cold 1 M sorbitol wash then cold MNase digestion buffer", duration_s=25, speed="gentle")
step5 = incubate_sample(sample=mnase_reaction, temperature_c=37, duration_min=8, condition="static")
step6 = vortex_mix(sample=step5, duration_s=5, speed_rpm=1800, temperature_c=22)
step7 = heat_sample(sample=step6, temperature_c=65, duration_min=7, device="heat block")
step8 = vortex_mix(sample=extraction_mix, duration_s=60, speed_rpm=2200, temperature_c=22)
step9 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step8, speed_xg=14000, duration_min=8, temperature_c=21)
step10 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step9, speed_xg=14000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=21)
step11 = incubate_sample(sample=te_rnase_sample, temperature_c=37, duration_min=30, condition="static")
step12 = dry_sample(sample=step11, method="air dry after second ammonium acetate ethanol precipitation and final spin; resuspend in ddH2O", temperature_c=22, duration_min=5)

source_steps.description: Centrifuge cells at 500 × g for 3 min at 4°C and carefully wash twice with 1 mL 1 M ice-cold sorbitol by gently dislodging and stirring the cell pellet with a 1000 μL pipette tip. Do not pipette up and down or vortex.
Gently resuspend cells in 200 μL of cold MNase digestion buffer with a pipette tip. CRITICAL: Cells are fragile after zymolyase digestion, so resuspend cells very gently.

Add 0.5 μL of MNase to each reaction and then incubate for 8 min in a 37°C water bath. Note: In our experience, this MNase to DNA ratio will convert >50% of chromatin into mono-nucleosomes. Due to variations in MNase activity, we recommend doing a MNase titration to find a concentration that will generate a mono-, di-, and tri- nucleosome ladder, but with most mass in the mono-nucleosome band. A mock digest without MNase will generate a genomic DNA sample (control).
Add 20 μL quench buffer and vortex to terminate the reaction. Samples are incubated at 65°C for 5–10 min. Note: The solution should become clearer after this step.
Add 0.2 mL phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol and vortex for 1 min.

Centrifuge at >14,000 × g for 7–10 min at 20°C–22°C and carefully transfer the top layers (∼150 μL) to new 1.5 mL tubes. Add 2.5 times the volume of the top layer of 100% ethanol. Vortex for a few seconds and invert to mix.
Centrifuge at >14,000 × g at 20°C–22°C for 5 min, remove supernatant, carefully aspirate all liquid. Note: You should see a clear pellet at this point.
Re-suspend the pellet in 0.4 mL TE buffer (pH8.0) and add 20 μL of 10 mg/mL RNase A. Incubate at 37°C for 30 min.
Add 6 μL of 5 M ammonium acetate plus 1 mL of 100% ethanol. Mix by inversion. Centrifuge at >14,000 × g at 20°C–22°C for 5 min. Note: Pellet size is likely to decrease but should still be visible.

Aspirate the supernatant using vacuum. Air-dry the pellet and resuspend genomic / MNase samples in 25 μL of ddH 2 O, respectively. Note: For the mock digest genomic sample, directly proceed to the next amplicon sequencing step.
Analyze MNase samples on a 1.5% TAE agarose gel electrophoresis with orange G dye (one sample per lane). Gel purify the ∼150 bp size product.

Amplicon sequencing. PCR amplify the regions containing the integrated synthetic sequences in both the MNase and genomic DNA samples. Note: Indexes and adaptors are added during two rounds of PCR ( Figure 10 ). Construction of amplicons for Illumina sequencing We amplified a 112 bp region near the TF motifs from the MNase-digested genomic DNA. Four random nucleotides (NNNN) and sequencing-primer binding sequences (yellow and orange) are added to the amplicon during the first round PCR. Sequencing flow cell binding sequences (red and green) and an index sequence (black) are added during the second round PCR. See https://www.illumina.com/ for more details about Illumina sequencing adapters The final length of the sequencing amplicon is 238 bp. PCR reaction master mix (first round) Reagent Amount MNase-digested DNA sample 200 ng Primer 1 (PF_TS_4N1st) 25 μL (10 μM) Primer 2 (PR-TS1st-2) 25 μL (10 μM) NEBNext Ultra II Q5 Master Mix 250 μL ddH 2 O To 500 μL PCR cycling conditions Steps Temperature Time Cycles Initial Denaturation 98°C 60 s 1 Denaturation 98°C 15 s 19 cycles Annealing/Extension 65°C 75 s Final extension 65°C 5 min 1 Hold 4°C ∞ Note: Four random bases are added to the end of amplicons to allow a better cluster separation in the initial calibration step of Nextseq machine ( Figure 10 ). PCR reaction master mix (second round) Reagent Amount Purified first round PCR products 200 ng Primer 1 (PF_TruSeq_2nd) 10 μL (10 μM) Primer 2 (PR_TruSeq_2nd) 10 μL (10 μM) NEBNext Ultra II Q5 Master Mix 100 μL ddH 2 O To 200 μL PCR cycling conditions Steps Temperature Time Cycles Initial Denaturation 98°C 60 s 1 Denaturation 98°C 15 s 3–6 cycles Annealing/Extension 65°C 75 s Final extension 65°C 5 min 1 Hold 4°C ∞ Note: Minimize PCR cycles to reduce bias generated during PCR. At least ∼200ng of PCR product is needed for Illumina sequencing.

background: Crosslinked mitotic cells have already been isolated and are now in the early Arima Hi-C processing phase. The sample needs sequential lysis-related handling, conditioning, stopping, several downstream reaction incubations with mild agitation, and then entry into the first SPRI bead cleanup.
goal: Advance a single aliquot of crosslinked mitotic cells through the pre-cleanup Hi-C reaction segment and end with the bead-bound DNA sample positioned for magnetic separation.
constraints: Start with a cold lysis-stage hold at 4 degrees Celsius for 20 minutes.
Include a 62 degrees Celsius incubation for 10 minutes after gentle pipette mixing, and later a 37 degrees Celsius incubation for 15 minutes after another gentle pipette mixing step.

After the stop stage, preserve one continuous sample through repeated reagent-addition style mixing events and include two mild-agitation thermal-mixer incubations, one at 350 rpm and one at 250 rpm, with the room-temperature mixer phase lasting 45 minutes in the 20 to 25 degrees Celsius range.
Include a later room-temperature hold of 15 minutes, then a final mild-agitation phase at 400 rpm after Buffer E handling before cleanup begins.
Model SPRI bead binding at room temperature for 5 minutes and then perform magnetic separation retaining the bead phase.
Use only provided action-pool functions, assign every call to a variable, and keep the program between 6 and 12 top-level steps.

available_inputs.name: crosslinked_cells_aliquot
available_inputs.description: One aliquot of fixed mitotic cells prepared for low-input Hi-C.

available_inputs.name: lysis_buffer_mix
available_inputs.description: Lysis buffer prepared for resuspension of the aliquot.

available_inputs.name: conditioning_stop_reagents
available_inputs.description: Conditioning solution and Stop Solution 2 available for sequential addition.

available_inputs.name: hic_mastermix_set
available_inputs.description: Prepared downstream Hi-C master mixes for the successive reaction stages before cleanup.

available_inputs.name: buffer_e
available_inputs.description: Buffer E for the late reaction stage before purification.

available_inputs.name: spri_beads
available_inputs.description: DNA SPRI purification beads for initiating bead-based cleanup.

gold_program:
step1 = cool_sample(sample=crosslinked_cells_aliquot, temperature_c=4, duration_min=20, device='refrigerator')
step2 = mix_sample(sample=step1, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
step3 = heat_sample(sample=step2, temperature_c=62, duration_min=10, device='thermal block')
step4 = mix_sample(sample=step3, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
step5 = incubate_sample(sample=step4, temperature_c=37, duration_min=15, condition='static')
step6 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=step5, temperature_c=37, speed_rpm=350, duration_min=15)
step7 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=step6, temperature_c=25, speed_rpm=250, duration_min=45)
step8 = incubate_sample(sample=step7, temperature_c=25, duration_min=15, condition='static')
step9 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=step8, temperature_c=25, speed_rpm=400, duration_min=5)
step10 = incubate_sample(sample=step9, temperature_c=25, duration_min=5, condition='static')
step11 = magnetic_separate(sample=step10, rack='magnetic rack', duration_min=5, collect_phase='beads')
protocol_title: Protocol for the generation of low-input Hi-C sequencing libraries of FACS-isolated mitotic cells

source_steps.description: Resuspend one aliquot of crosslinked cells in 20 μL of Lysis Buffer in a tube or a well of a PCR plate, and incubate at 4°C for 20 min.
Add 24 μL of Conditioning Solution, mix gently by pipetting, and incubate at 62°C for 10 min.
Add 20 μL of Stop Solution 2, mix gently by pipetting, and incubate at 37°C for 15 min.
Add 12 μL of a master mix containing the following reagents:
Mix gently by pipetting and incubate under mild agitation (350 rpm) as follows:
Add 16 μL of a master mix containing the following reagents:
Mix gently by pipetting, and incubate at 20°C–25°C for 45 min under mild agitation (250 rpm).
Add 82 μL of a master mix containing the following reagents:
Mix gently by pipetting, and incubate at RT for 15 min.
Add 35.5 μL of a master mix containing the following reagents:
Add 20 μL of Buffer E, mix gently by pipetting, and incubate under mild agitation (400 rpm) as follows:
DNA purification: Add 100 μL of DNA SPRI Purification Beads, mix thoroughly, and incubate at RT for 5 min.

background: A zebrafish sgRNA template has already been assembled and now needs transcription, cleanup, precipitation, and QC. At this stage, the work centers on recovering the DNA template after alcohol precipitation, carrying out T7 in vitro transcription, removing template DNA, cleaning the RNA by organic extraction and re-precipitation, and documenting final sgRNA quality for later Cas9 testing.
goal: Produce purified sgRNA from the prepared template and generate concentration and integrity QC records after the final recovery in RNase-free water.
constraints: The starting material must first pass through a sodium acetate and absolute ethanol precipitation held at -20 C for at least 120 min before template recovery.
Both the initial template pellet and the later RNA pellet require centrifugation at 12000 x g for 10 min at 4 C, followed by a 70% ethanol wash and air drying.
The transcription stage must include a 37 C incubation for 270 min and a DNase treatment at 37 C for 40 min before extraction.
Organic cleanup must include mixed acidic phenol and chloroform, then a centrifugation at 10000 x g for 10 min at 4 C before the second precipitation.

After the second precipitation, the recovered RNA must be dissolved in 10 uL rnase_free_water, and QC must include a 260 nm UV-style measurement plus a recorded RNA integrity observation tied to 10% urea-PAGE.

available_inputs.name: annealed_and_extended_product
available_inputs.description: 100 uL DNA product from sgRNA template assembly before precipitation

available_inputs.name: sodium_acetate_pH53
available_inputs.description: 3 M sodium acetate at pH 5.3 for nucleic acid precipitation

available_inputs.name: absolute_ethanol
available_inputs.description: Absolute ethanol used for nucleic acid precipitation

available_inputs.name: water
available_inputs.description: Water used during cleanup and intermediate dissolution

available_inputs.name: rnase_free_water
available_inputs.description: RNase-free water for final sgRNA recovery

available_inputs.name: transcription_reaction_mix
available_inputs.description: Prepared T7 transcription mix containing template, NTPs, buffer, polymerase, and DTT

available_inputs.name: dnase_treated_transcription_mix
available_inputs.description: Transcription reaction after DNase addition, ready for extraction

gold_program:
template_precip_mix = mix_sample(sample=annealed_and_extended_product, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
template_precip_cold = incubate_sample(sample=template_precip_mix, temperature_c=-20, duration_min=120, condition='static')
template_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=template_precip_cold, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
template_dried = dry_sample(sample=template_pellet, method='air dry', temperature_c=22, duration_min=10)
transcribed_sgrna = incubate_sample(sample=transcription_reaction_mix, temperature_c=37, duration_min=270, condition='static')
dnase_processed = incubate_sample(sample=dnase_treated_transcription_mix, temperature_c=37, duration_min=40, condition='static')
extraction_mixed = mix_sample(sample=dnase_processed, method='inversion', duration_s=15, speed='moderate')
extracted_aqueous = centrifuge_sample(sample=extraction_mixed, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
rna_precip_cold = incubate_sample(sample=extracted_aqueous, temperature_c=-20, duration_min=120, condition='static')
rna_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=rna_precip_cold, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
final_sgrna = dry_sample(sample=rna_pellet, method='air dry', temperature_c=22, duration_min=10)
uv_result = measure_signal(sample=final_sgrna, assay='UV spectrophotometry', wavelength_nm=260, replicate_n=1)
protocol_title: CRISPR-Cas9-induced gene knockout in zebrafish
