source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: To the 100-μL annealed and extended product (depicted above), add 10 μL of 3 M sodium acetate pH 5.3 and 500 μL of absolute ethanol and incubate for at least 2 h at −20°C. Overnight is preferable.

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 12,000 g for 10 min at 4°C, wash pellet with 70% ethanol, air dry, and dissolve in 10 μL of water.

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Use the template generated above in an in vitro RNA transcription reaction to generate the sgRNA. Perform a T7 RNA polymerase-driven in vitro transcription reaction as follows (the amount of DNA template used is typically at least 1 μg). Reaction mix Reagent Amount DNA Template 3 μL (1 μg) CTP 1 μL UTP 1 μL GTP 1 μL ATP 1 μL 10× Buffer 1 μL T7 Polymerase 1 μL DTT (0.1 M) 1 μL

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Incubate the above mix at 37°C for 4.5 h (we have found this duration to work well, but it can vary between 0.5–5 h depending on the reaction efficiency).

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Add 0.5 μL of DNase (1 unit/ μL) and continue to incubate at 37°C for 40 min.

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Perform a phenol-chloroform extraction by making up the volume to 200 μL with water, adding 100 μL of acidic phenol and 100 μL of chloroform, mixing by inverting and centrifuging at 10,000 g at 4°C for 10 min. Collect the supernatant and extract with an equal volume of chloroform. Collect the supernatant and proceed to RNA precipitation.

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Precipitate the RNA by adding 10 μL of 3 M sodium acetate pH 5.3 and 500 μL of absolute ethanol and incubating at least 2 h at −20°C (alternatively, 5–10 min at −80°C can be used).

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 12,000 g for 10 min at 4°C, wash pellet with 70% ethanol, air dry, and dissolve in 10 μL of RNAse-free water.

source_steps.stage: In vitro synthesis of single-guide RNA
source_steps.description: Quantify the RNA using a Nano drop UV spectrophotometer and verify integrity by resolving the RNA sample via 10% urea-PAGE ( Summer et al., 2009 ) as follows. Dissolve 4.2 g of urea in 2 mL of water with agitation and boiling. After cooling to room temperature, add 2 mL of 5× TBE, 3.3 mL of 30% Acrylamide / Bis-acrylamide Mix, 100 μL of 10% APS, 10 μL of TEMED and make up to 10 mL with water. Pour the gel and perform a pre-run for 20–30 min at 5 V/cm. Prepare the RNA sample as follows; take 0. 5 μL of RNA, add 4.5 μL of water and 5 μL of 2× formamide dye. Heat the 10 μL RNA sample at 95°C for 5 min and spin briefly. Flush the wells with 1× TBE to remove urea, load the RNA samples, run until the dye front migrates 3/4 th distance of the gel and stain with ethidium bromide (0.5 μg/mL) for 10 min. Visualize RNA integrity using a gel imaging system. Urea-PAGE Gel Recipe Reagent Amount Urea 4.2 g Water 2 mL 5× TBE 2 mL 30% Acrylamide/Bis-acrylamide mix 3.3 mL 10% APS 100 μL TEMED 10 μL Water To 10 mL 5× TBE Recipe Reagent Amount Tris Base 54 g Boric Acid 27.5 g 0.5 M EDTA pH 8 20 mL Water To 1 L

source_steps.stage: In vitro catalytic activity assay for assessing guide RNA efficiency
source_steps.description: Perform an in vitro catalytic activity assay with purified Cas9, sgRNA, and the target region amplicon ( Grainger et al., 2017 ). Assemble a ribonucleoprotein complex by adding the following reagents and incubating the mix at 37°C for 10 min. Reaction mix Reagent Amount sgRNA (500 ng/μL) 1 μL NEB buffer 3.1 1 μL Cas9 (250 ng/μL) 1 μL Water 8 μL

source_steps.stage: In vitro catalytic activity assay for assessing guide RNA efficiency
source_steps.description: Add 2 μL of the target-region PCR amplicon to the above mix and incubate further at 37°C for 1 h. Add 1 μL RNase A and incubate at 37°C for 30 min, and then add 1 μL Proteinase K and further incubate at 37°C for 30 min.

source_steps.stage: In vitro catalytic activity assay for assessing guide RNA efficiency
source_steps.description: Resolve the sample on 10% Native PAGE and visualize by DNA staining. Cleavage of the target results in cut bands and thus a different band pattern compared to the control sample (target-region PCR amplicon alone) ( Figure 6 ).

background: You are in the Hi-C library preparation phase for low-input mitotic-cell material, immediately after earlier enzymatic processing and before cleanup on SPRI beads. The sample now needs a sequence of reagent incorporation, gentle mixing, temperature-controlled holds, and a bead-based DNA purification setup leading into magnetic clarification.
goal: Process the ligated Hi-C material through the conditioning and subsequent enzymatic incubation segment, then prepare the sample for SPRI bead cleanup and magnetic clearing.
constraints: Begin with the conditioned Hi-C sample and include an initial heat treatment at 62°C for 10 min after gentle pipette-style mixing.
A second reagent addition state must be represented by another gentle mix followed by a 37°C incubation for 15 min.
Use mild agitation for the incubation that follows the 12 μL master mix addition, and later use 20°C–25°C with 250 rpm for 45 min after the 16 μL master mix addition.
After the larger 82 μL master mix addition, maintain a room-temperature incubation for 15 min before proceeding.
A later stage must include Buffer E addition followed by mild agitation at 400 rpm.
Represent reagent additions as transitions via mixing actions rather than unsupported liquid-handling primitives.
End the segment by preparing DNA purification with 100 μL SPRI beads, incubating at room temperature for 5 min, and then performing magnetic separation until the solution is clear.
Use only provided action-pool functions and keep the workflow as a single dependency chain from the starting sample.

available_inputs.name: hic_ligated_sample
available_inputs.description: Hi-C ligated material entering the late enzymatic processing segment

available_inputs.name: conditioning_solution
available_inputs.description: Conditioning reagent used before the 62°C incubation

available_inputs.name: stop_solution_2
available_inputs.description: Stopping reagent used before the 37°C incubation

available_inputs.name: mastermix_12ul
available_inputs.description: Prepared 12 μL master mix for the next enzymatic stage

available_inputs.name: mastermix_16ul
available_inputs.description: Prepared 16 μL master mix for the room-temperature stage

available_inputs.name: mastermix_82ul
available_inputs.description: Prepared 82 μL master mix for the subsequent reaction

available_inputs.name: buffer_e
available_inputs.description: Buffer E added before the final agitated incubation

available_inputs.name: spri_beads
available_inputs.description: DNA SPRI purification beads for cleanup

gold_program:
step1 = mix_sample(sample=hic_ligated_sample, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
step2 = heat_sample(sample=step1, temperature_c=62.0, duration_min=10.0, device="thermal_mixer")
step3 = mix_sample(sample=step2, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
step4 = incubate_sample(sample=step3, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=15.0, condition="static")
step5 = mix_sample(sample=step4, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
step6 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=step5, temperature_c=37.0, speed_rpm=350, duration_min=15.0)
step7 = mix_sample(sample=step6, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
step8 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=step7, temperature_c=25.0, speed_rpm=250, duration_min=45.0)
step9 = mix_sample(sample=step8, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
step10 = incubate_sample(sample=step9, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=15.0, condition="static")
step11 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=step10, temperature_c=25.0, speed_rpm=400, duration_min=15.0)
step12 = magnetic_separate(sample=step11, rack="magnetic_rack", duration_min=5.0, collect_phase="clear_solution")

        duration_min: Spin time in minutes.
        temperature_c: Centrifuge chamber temperature in Celsius.

        A handle for the centrifuged sample.

source_steps.description: Add 24 μL of Conditioning Solution, mix gently by pipetting, and incubate at 62°C for 10 min.
Add 20 μL of Stop Solution 2, mix gently by pipetting, and incubate at 37°C for 15 min.
Add 12 μL of a master mix containing the following reagents:
Mix gently by pipetting and incubate under mild agitation (350 rpm) as follows:
Add 16 μL of a master mix containing the following reagents:
Mix gently by pipetting, and incubate at 20°C–25°C for 45 min under mild agitation (250 rpm).
Add 82 μL of a master mix containing the following reagents:
Mix gently by pipetting, and incubate at RT for 15 min.
Add 35.5 μL of a master mix containing the following reagents:
Add 20 μL of Buffer E, mix gently by pipetting, and incubate under mild agitation (400 rpm) as follows:
DNA purification: Add 100 μL of DNA SPRI Purification Beads, mix thoroughly, and incubate at RT for 5 min.
Place sample against magnet and incubate until solution is clear.

background: This item covers the ChIP DNA cleanup stage after overnight reverse crosslinking has already been completed. Both the immunoprecipitated eluate and the whole-cell extract input must now undergo dilution, enzymatic cleanup, repeated organic extraction, ethanol precipitation, cold pelleting, ethanol washing, brief drying, and final dissolution for later quantitative analysis.
goal: Recover purified DNA from the reversed-crosslinked ChIP and input samples by executing the cleanup, precipitation, and reconstitution segment of the workflow.
constraints: Treat the IP eluate and WCE/input as the active materials for this stage, including equal-volume TE dilution before cleanup without enumerating manual transfer operations.
Include RNA digestion at 37 C for 2 h and protein digestion at 55 C for 2 h under static incubation conditions.
Represent two successive organic extraction rounds with vigorous vortexing for 20 s followed by centrifugation at 20000 x g for 5 min at 20-22 C.
Capture precipitation using glycogen plus at least 2x absolute ethanol, followed by overnight storage at -80 C before DNA pelleting.

Use cold recovery conditions of 20000 x g for 40 min at 4 C for pelleting, then model post-pellet cleanup with 70% ethanol wash handling, brief bench air drying, and dissolution in molecular biology grade water at 4 C for about 16 h.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keyword arguments only, assign every call to a variable, keep the program at 8-12 top-level calls, and reuse at least two action families more than once.

available_inputs.name: ip_eluate_reversed
available_inputs.description: Immunoprecipitated eluate after reverse crosslinking.

available_inputs.name: wce_reversed
available_inputs.description: Whole-cell extract input after reverse crosslinking.

available_inputs.name: te_buffer
available_inputs.description: TE buffer used to dilute samples before cleanup.

available_inputs.name: rnase_a
available_inputs.description: RNase A reagent for RNA digestion.

available_inputs.name: proteinase_k
available_inputs.description: Proteinase K reagent for protein digestion.

available_inputs.name: glycogen_ethanol_mix
available_inputs.description: Combined glycogen carrier and absolute ethanol precipitation reagents.

gold_program:
cleanup_batch = mix_sample(sample='ip_eluate_reversed+wce_reversed+te_buffer', method='pipetting', duration_s=20.0, speed='moderate')
rna_digested = incubate_sample(sample=cleanup_batch, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=120.0, condition='static')
protein_digested = incubate_sample(sample=rna_digested, temperature_c=55.0, duration_min=120.0, condition='static')
extraction_mixed_1 = vortex_mix(sample=protein_digested, duration_s=20.0, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=21.0)
phase_separated_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=extraction_mixed_1, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=21.0)
extraction_mixed_2 = vortex_mix(sample=phase_separated_1, duration_s=20.0, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=21.0)
phase_separated_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=extraction_mixed_2, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=21.0)
precipitation_mixed = mix_sample(sample='phase_separated_2+glycogen_ethanol_mix', method='inversion', duration_s=15.0, speed='thorough')
precipitated_dna = store_sample(sample=precipitation_mixed, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_h=16.0, container='microcentrifuge_tube')
dna_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=precipitated_dna, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=40.0, temperature_c=4.0)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=dna_pellet, method='air dry', temperature_c=21.0, duration_min=5.0)
reconstituted_dna = incubate_sample(sample=dried_pellet, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=960.0, condition='static')

source_steps.description: At the end of the 20-min elution, spin tubes in a microcentrifuge at full speed (20,000 × g ) for 1 min at 20°C–22°C.
Transfer 65 μL of the supernatant/eluate to a new tube and discard the beads. Incubate the eluates at 65°C for 16–18 h to reverse crosslinking.
Thaw the WCE/input tube (stored at −80°C), add 3× volume (150 μL) elution buffer, and incubate at 65°C for 16–18 h to reverse crosslinking.
ETV5-bound DNA cleaning and precipitation. Next day: add an equal volume TE; 65 μL to IP tubes and 200 μL to WCE.
RNA digestion: add RNAse A (10 mg/mL) to 200 μg/mL final concentration to all tubes and incubate at 37°C for 2 h.
Protein digestion: add proteinase K (20 mg/mL) to 200 μg/mL final concentration to all tubes and incubate at 55°C for 2 h.

DNA cleaning: add 5 M NaCl to 200 mM final concentration to all tubes. Add an equal volume of phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol and mix by vigorous vortexing for 20 s. Spin tubes at maximum speed (20,000 × g ) for 5 min at 20°C–22°C and carefully transfer the top aqueous phase to a new tube. Add an equal volume of chloroform and repeat the same procedure, then carefully transfer the top aqueous phase to a new tube.
DNA precipitation: add to each tube 35 μg of glycogen (7 μL of a 5 mg/mL solution) and ≥ 2× volume of absolute ethanol. Thoroughly mix by inverting tubes several times and store overnight at −80°C.
DNA reconstitution. Next day: pellet DNA at maximum speed (20,000 × g ) for 40 min at 4°C, carefully decant the supernatant without disturbing DNA pellet.

Add 500 μL of ice-cold 70% ethanol to DNA pellet and spin at maximum speed for 3 min at 4°C. Repeat the ethanol wash once and sediment the last of the remaining ethanol with a pulse spin, then carefully remove the extra alcohol with a thin tip.
Allow the DNA pellet to air dry for a few minutes on the bench.

Add to each tube 30 μL–50 μL of molecular biology grade H 2 O (without disturbing the DNA pellet) and allow the DNA pellets to dissolve for 16–18 h at 4°C. Note: 10 mM Tris pH 8.0 may be used in place of H 2 O to immediately dissolve DNA, with pipetting, a brief vortex, then a pulse spin.

background: This item targets the final DNA recovery phase of a ChIP workflow after crosslink reversal and enzymatic cleanup have already been completed. Both the immunoprecipitated fraction and the input control now need parallel downstream purification through extraction-associated mixing and clarification, cold ethanol precipitation, pellet cleanup, and low-temperature reconstitution before later analysis.
goal: Carry both ChIP-derived samples through the remaining DNA purification workflow so they end as dissolved DNA after extraction, precipitation, washing, and reconstitution.
constraints: Handle the immunoprecipitated sample and the input control in parallel rather than finishing one completely before starting the other.
Use two repeated extraction-style cycles, each with 20 s vigorous vortexing at about 21 C followed by centrifugation at 20000 x g for 5 min.
Represent precipitation with a mixing step before storage at -80 C for about 17 h, then pellet DNA at 20000 x g for 40 min at 4 C.
Include two repeated cold pellet-wash spin events at 20000 x g for 3 min at 4 C and then only a brief air-dry.
Finish with dissolution at 4 C for about 17 h and a final resuspension-style mixing step.

available_inputs.name: ip_digest_tube
available_inputs.description: Immunoprecipitated ChIP sample after TE adjustment and nuclease/protease digestion.

available_inputs.name: wce_digest_tube
available_inputs.description: Whole-cell extract or input control after TE adjustment and nuclease/protease digestion.

available_inputs.name: room_temp
available_inputs.description: Ambient processing condition corresponding to roughly 20-22 C.

available_inputs.name: cold_room
available_inputs.description: Cold processing condition corresponding to 4 C.

gold_program:
extract_mix1 = vortex_mix(sample="ip_digest_tube|wce_digest_tube", duration_s=20.0, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=21.0)
extract_spin1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=extract_mix1, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=21.0)
extract_mix2 = vortex_mix(sample=extract_spin1, duration_s=20.0, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=21.0)
extract_spin2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=extract_mix2, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=21.0)
precip_mix = mix_sample(sample=extract_spin2, method="inversion", duration_s=10.0, speed="thorough")
precip_store = store_sample(sample=precip_mix, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_h=17.0, container="microcentrifuge_tube")
pellet_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=precip_store, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=40.0, temperature_c=4.0)
wash_spin1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=pellet_spin, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=4.0)
wash_spin2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_spin1, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=4.0)
pellet_dry = dry_sample(sample=wash_spin2, method="air dry", temperature_c=21.0, duration_min=5.0)
dna_dissolve = incubate_sample(sample=pellet_dry, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=1020.0, condition="static")
dna_final = mix_sample(sample=dna_dissolve, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")

source_steps.description: Transfer 65 μL of the supernatant/eluate to a new tube and discard the beads. Incubate the eluates at 65°C for 16–18 h to reverse crosslinking.
Thaw the WCE/input tube (stored at −80°C), add 3× volume (150 μL) elution buffer, and incubate at 65°C for 16–18 h to reverse crosslinking.
ETV5-bound DNA cleaning and precipitation. Next day: add an equal volume TE; 65 μL to IP tubes and 200 μL to WCE.
RNA digestion: add RNAse A (10 mg/mL) to 200 μg/mL final concentration to all tubes and incubate at 37°C for 2 h.
Protein digestion: add proteinase K (20 mg/mL) to 200 μg/mL final concentration to all tubes and incubate at 55°C for 2 h.

DNA cleaning: add 5 M NaCl to 200 mM final concentration to all tubes. Add an equal volume of phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol and mix by vigorous vortexing for 20 s. Spin tubes at maximum speed (20,000 × g ) for 5 min at 20°C–22°C and carefully transfer the top aqueous phase to a new tube. Add an equal volume of chloroform and repeat the same procedure, then carefully transfer the top aqueous phase to a new tube.
DNA precipitation: add to each tube 35 μg of glycogen (7 μL of a 5 mg/mL solution) and ≥ 2× volume of absolute ethanol. Thoroughly mix by inverting tubes several times and store overnight at −80°C.
DNA reconstitution. Next day: pellet DNA at maximum speed (20,000 × g ) for 40 min at 4°C, carefully decant the supernatant without disturbing DNA pellet.

Add 500 μL of ice-cold 70% ethanol to DNA pellet and spin at maximum speed for 3 min at 4°C. Repeat the ethanol wash once and sediment the last of the remaining ethanol with a pulse spin, then carefully remove the extra alcohol with a thin tip.
Allow the DNA pellet to air dry for a few minutes on the bench.

Add to each tube 30 μL–50 μL of molecular biology grade H 2 O (without disturbing the DNA pellet) and allow the DNA pellets to dissolve for 16–18 h at 4°C. Note: 10 mM Tris pH 8.0 may be used in place of H 2 O to immediately dissolve DNA, with pipetting, a brief vortex, then a pulse spin.
Next day: mix dissolved DNA by pipetting, a brief vortex, followed by a pulse spin.

background: Conditioned cell-culture medium has already been harvested and is being processed to enrich extracellular vesicles and particles by differential centrifugation and a PBS wash. The remaining task is to clear larger contaminants, collect the EVP fraction by ultracentrifugation, wash the pellet, and place the final preparation into frozen storage while keeping the material cold throughout handling.
goal: Process the pre-cleared culture supernatant through the remaining EVP isolation workflow to obtain a washed EVP pellet resuspended in cold PBS and stored for later downstream characterization.
constraints: Maintain samples cold between processing steps; use ice-cold or 4-10°C conditions where applicable.
Include a contaminant-clearing spin at 12000 x g for 20 min at 4°C before the ultracentrifugation-based collection.
Use ultracentrifugation-based EVP collection at 100000 x g for 70 min at 10°C, and perform this collection twice with a PBS wash in between.
After the first high-speed collection, remove residual liquid rapidly and allow brief inverted drainage on the order of 1-3 min before the wash resuspension.
Wash the first pellet in cold PBS, with an initial small-volume resuspension corresponding to 500 µL before dilution into PBS for the second high-speed spin.
The final EVP preparation should be resuspended in about 100 µL cold PBS and then stored at -80°C.
Use a dependency chain where each processing stage consumes the output of the prior stage rather than restarting from the original sample.
Do not introduce assays or non-isolation characterization steps in the core workflow.

available_inputs.name: conditioned_media
available_inputs.description: Harvested cell-culture conditioned medium kept cold after initial cell removal.

available_inputs.name: cold_pbs
available_inputs.description: Pre-chilled PBS used for pellet wash and final resuspension.

available_inputs.name: ultracentrifuge_tube
available_inputs.description: Clean ultracentrifuge tube compatible with high-speed spins.

gold_program:
clarified_supernatant = centrifuge_sample(sample=conditioned_media, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=20, temperature_c=4)
chilled_supernatant = cool_sample(sample=clarified_supernatant, temperature_c=4, duration_min=5, device="ice bath")
uc_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=chilled_supernatant, speed_xg=100000, duration_min=70, temperature_c=10)
drained_uc_pellet = incubate_sample(sample=uc_pellet, temperature_c=10, duration_min=2, condition="inverted drainage")
washed_resuspension = mix_sample(sample=drained_uc_pellet, method="pipetting in 500 uL cold PBS", duration_s=30, speed="gentle")
chilled_wash = cool_sample(sample=washed_resuspension, temperature_c=4, duration_min=5, device="ice bath")
washed_uc_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=chilled_wash, speed_xg=100000, duration_min=70, temperature_c=10)
final_resuspension = mix_sample(sample=washed_uc_pellet, method="pipetting in 100 uL cold PBS", duration_s=30, speed="gentle")
frozen_evp_prep = store_sample(sample=final_resuspension, temperature_c=-80, duration_h=24, container="ultracentrifuge_tube")

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Harvest culture media and remove cells, debris, apoptotic bodies, and contaminants through serial centrifugation. Centrifuge sample at 500 × g for 10 min at 4°C to pellet cells and debris.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Collect the supernatant without disturbing the cell/debris pellet. This can be done by quickly pouring off the supernatant into a new container/tube or carefully pipetting off the supernatant. It is essential to work as quickly as possible during these steps, as the pellet will become dislodged over time. Note: For this and all subsequent steps, supernatants and re-suspended EVP pellets should be kept on ice between isolation steps. Note: A step of 3,000 × g centrifugation for 20 min at 4°C should be added for cell culture supernatants before short-term storage (<24 h) at 4°C or before freezing to remove additional cell debris contaminants. This step has to be performed if further analysis of the post-12,000 × g microvesicle sample is planned. Pause Point: Conditioned media supernatants can be stored at −80°C for long-term storage or at 4 °C for short-term (<24 h) storage before ultracentrifugation. Minor differences have been observed between EVPs isolated from fresh versus frozen samples in terms of yield and purity. However, frozen samples are often used to allow for retrospective studies, collaborative studies between different institutions and batch processing of test samples/subjects and controls.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the supernatant at 12,000 × g for 20 min at 4°C to remove apoptotic bodies and aggregates and larger vesicles that could otherwise be co-isolated with EVPs and “contaminate” the EVP preparation. Note: This step can be performed in the ultracentrifuge or tabletop centrifuge depending on volume and availability of the ultracentrifuge. We recommend performing the 12,000 × g spin right before ultracentrifugation since contaminating material could develop by freeze-thaw cycles or extended storage times.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Collect supernatant without disturbing the pellet and transfer to new/clean ultracentrifuge tube by pipetting, leaving a small volume behind to avoid contamination from pellet. CRITICAL: Wash newly purchased ultracentrifugation tubes with soap and water and rinse them extensively in ddH 2 O before the first EVP isolation, since the factory coating on the inside of the tube will prevent the EVP pellet from adhering to the tube. After use, the tubes should be washed extensively and sterilized by autoclaving if required. Cleaning of ultracentrifugation tubes was performed as per standard cleaning procedure for laboratory glassware and utensils used for molecular and cellular biology applications. We extensively wash the tubes in soap and hot water while scrubbing with a brush. The tubes are then thoroughly rinsed with tap water to remove any traces of soap, followed by additional rinses in ddH 2 O. If EVPs are isolated for in vitro or in vivo work rather than molecular biology, the tubes can be autoclaved, this however shortens the life of the tubes. No contamination between samples has ever been observed using these procedures. The tube used depends on the volume of starting material rather than the type of specimen. Note: It is important to avoid disturbing the pellet with the pipette tip in these centrifugation steps since the aim is to remove pelleted contamination; instead, leave a minimal amount of media above the pellet to avoid dissociating the pellet. Remember to work as quickly as possible during these steps as the pellet will become dislodged over time.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Isolate EVPs by ultracentrifugation Centrifuge supernatant at 100,000 × g for 70 min at 10°C in a fixed-angle rotor in a Beckman Coulter ultracentrifuge (see Materials and equipment ) to collect EVPs. CRITICAL: It is very important to accurately balance the tubes in the ultracentrifuge. An unbalanced rotor can cause spillage, contamination, run failure, or even damage the rotor/ultracentrifuge. Note: Before inserting tubes into the rotor, we recommend marking an area on the bottom of the tube where the pellet is expected to form, since it may not be easily visible. Avoid disturbing the pelleted area when discarding the supernatant and pay careful attention to it for successful retrieval of your EVP pellet ( Figure 2 ). Careful handling of the EVP pellet (A) After ultracentrifugation, carefully decant supernatant and (B and C) re-suspend the pellet in a small volume of PBS (0.5–1 mL depending on tube size) by pipetting and flushing the marked area of the lower side of the ultracentrifugation tube. Avoid generating bubbles.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant by decanting or carefully pipetting without disturbing the pellet. If pipetting off the supernatant, keep the tip of the pipet on the side of the tube opposite from the pellet. If decanting the supernatant, be sure to pour the supernatant off the side of the tube opposite the pellet and use one quick, careful motion to invert the tube. Optional: Store supernatant if interested in EVP-depleted media for measurement of other secreted factors, such as cytokines. Note: In this and subsequent steps it is important to work quickly and remove the supernatant as fast as possible after the ultracentrifuge has stopped to prevent dissociation of the pellet. After removing the supernatant, leave the pellet untouched and place the inverted tube in a rack for 1–3 min to avoid backflow of liquid. This ensures minimal carryover of contaminants and prevents dissociation of the pellet. Remove drops of liquid from tube walls by vacuum aspiration or using a Kimwipe, if necessary. Troubleshooting 1 .

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: To wash the pellet, re-suspend it in 500 μL of cold PBS by pipetting and flushing the marked area at the bottom of the ultracentrifugation tube. Ensure the pellet is re-suspended well so contaminants trapped in the pellet (free, abundant proteins) are washed away. Transfer the re-suspended pellet to a new ultracentrifuge tube and add 3 mL (tube #355645), 20 mL (tube #355631) or 44 mL (tube #355628) of cold PBS. We recommend using the same size tube throughout the protocol (i.e., depending on starting volume) since the starting volume affects the yield. Thus, small volumes, 1–3 mL should be spun in a small tube where these fewer EVPs can more readily be collected and concentrated. Troubleshooting 2 .

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the re-suspended pellet at 100,000 × g for 70 min at 10°C to collect the washed EVP pellet.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Discard the PBS supernatant by decanting and remove all liquid by gently tapping the inverted tube to avoid backflow.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Re-suspend the EVP pellet in 100 μL (see note for range below) of cold PBS and store at −80°C. Reserve 10 μL aliquots for downstream quantification and characterization, such as protein quantification, NTA, and EM, to avoid freeze-thaw cycles.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from tissue samples
source_steps.description: Collect the tumor tissue and adjacent tissue in ice-cold PBS at the time of surgery and transfer them on ice to the class II biosafety cabinet within 30 min.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from tissue samples
source_steps.description: Wash tissues in cold PBS × 3 and quickly cut the tissues into 2 mm 3 -sized pieces. Rinse tissue pieces again in PBS before plating if needed to remove debris and/or blood.

background: Purified single tRNA iso-decoder eluates have already been collected from a bead-based capture workflow. At this stage, the pooled eluate must be cleared of residual magnetic beads, treated to digest remaining biotinylated DNA probe with Exonuclease I, and then purified on a small-RNA cleanup column using binding buffer, ethanol-assisted binding, repeated column loading, washing, and final elution in RNase-free water.
goal: Process the pooled tRNA eluate through probe removal and RNA cleanup to obtain a purified single tRNA iso-decoder eluate suitable for downstream analysis.
constraints: Start from pooled tRNA eluates and retain the cleared supernatant after a 20000 x g, 3 min clarification spin followed by magnetic separation.
After Exonuclease I reagents are introduced, include thorough mixing and a 45 min incubation at room temperature, normalized to 25 C.
Represent cleanup preparation with a mixing step that captures both the 2-volume binding buffer addition and the 2-volume 100% ethanol addition without using unsupported liquid-handling primitives.
Model column purification as two sequential 1 min centrifugation-based loading events at 16000 x g, then two separate 1 min wash spins at 16000 x g.
Include a 1 min dry spin at 16000 x g after the washes to reduce carryover before elution.
Finish by modeling elution with 23 uL RNase-free water and a final 1 min centrifugation at 16000 x g, maintaining one continuous dependency chain and using only provided action-pool functions.

available_inputs.name: pooled_eluates
available_inputs.description: Combined tRNA iso-decoder eluates collected after prior capture and elution steps.

available_inputs.name: exo_buffer_mix
available_inputs.description: 10X Exonuclease I buffer available for the probe digestion reaction.

available_inputs.name: exonuclease_i
available_inputs.description: Exonuclease I enzyme used to remove remaining biotinylated DNA probe.

available_inputs.name: binding_buffer
available_inputs.description: RNA cleanup kit binding buffer used before ethanol addition and column loading.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_100
available_inputs.description: Absolute ethanol used to promote tRNA binding to the cleanup column.

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer
available_inputs.description: RNA cleanup kit wash buffer for column washing.

available_inputs.name: rnase_free_water
available_inputs.description: RNase-free water used for final elution from the cleanup column.

gold_program:
clarified_eluates = centrifuge_sample(sample=pooled_eluates, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=3, temperature_c=25)
bead_cleared_supernatant = magnetic_separate(sample=clarified_eluates, rack="magnetic_rack", duration_min=1, collect_phase="supernatant")
exo_reaction_mixed = mix_sample(sample=bead_cleared_supernatant, method="pipetting", duration_s=15, speed="thorough")
exo_treated_eluate = incubate_sample(sample=exo_reaction_mixed, temperature_c=25, duration_min=45, condition="static")
cleanup_binding_mixed = mix_sample(sample=exo_treated_eluate, method="pipetting", duration_s=20, speed="thorough with 2-volume binding buffer and 2-volume ethanol")
column_load_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=cleanup_binding_mixed, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
column_load_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=column_load_1, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
column_wash_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=column_load_2, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
column_wash_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=column_wash_1, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
dry_spun_column = centrifuge_sample(sample=column_wash_2, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
elution_mixed = mix_sample(sample=dry_spun_column, method="pipetting", duration_s=5, speed="gentle with 23 uL RNase-free water")
purified_trna_elution = centrifuge_sample(sample=elution_mixed, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
protocol_title: Protocol to detect m 2 2 G modification on single tRNA iso-decoders by immuno-northern blotting

source_steps.description: Pool all eluates together.
Centrifuge the tRNA eluates at 20,000 × g for 3 min, then apply the tube to the magnet. Transfer the supernatant to a new tube to remove any remaining beads from the eluate.

To remove any remaining Biotin DNA probe from the purified tRNAs, add 17 μL of 10X Exonuclease I buffer and 3 μL of Exonuclease I to the eluate. Mix thoroughly and perform a quick spin. Incubate at 22°C to 25°C for 45 min.

Clean up the tRNAs after the Exonuclease I treatment using the NEB Monarch Spin RNA Cleanup Kit. Note: We perform the clean-up steps using the Monarch Spin RNA Cleanup Kit, but any other RNA cleanup kit can be used following the manufacturer's protocol for small RNA. Add 2 volumes (340 μL) of NEB binding buffer and mix thoroughly by pipetting.

Add 2 volumes (1020 μL) of 100% ethanol and mix by pipetting. CRITICAL: Mix gently with care to prevent spilling of liquid, as the 1.6 mL microcentrifuge tube will be full at this step. Do not vortex. CRITICAL: According to the NEB Monarch Spin RNA Cleanup Kit protocol, adding 1 volume of ethanol in this step will enable the binding of RNA longer than 25 nt in length. However, we find that the addition of 2 volumes of ethanol leads to more efficient binding of tRNAs to the column.
Add 750 μL of the mixture to an NEB RNA cleanup column and centrifuge for 1 min at max speed (> 16000 × g). Remove liquid from the collecting tube.
Repeat step 41c to add the rest of the mixture (∼780 μL) to the same NEB RNA cleanup column and centrifuge for 1 min at max speed (> 16000 × g). Remove liquid from the collecting tube.
Add 500 μL of wash buffer and centrifuge for 1 min at max speed (> 16000 × g). Remove the liquid from the collecting tube after centrifugation. Repeat this step once again to wash the column twice.
After the last wash, remove all flow through and re-centrifuge the empty column for 1 min. Note: This dry spin is to ensure no trace of salt or ethanol is carried over to the next step.
Transfer the column to a new RNase-free microcentrifuge tube.

Add 23 μL of RNase-free water directly over the matrix in the center of the column without disturbing the matrix. Note: Make sure the water is added directly over the matrix rather than on the side of the tube. Since the tRNAs are bound to the matrix, it needs to be fully exposed to the water to ensure effective elution.
Centrifuge for 1 min at max speed (> 16000 × g) to collect the tRNA iso-decoder in the elution.

background: Autologous PBMC-derived cells are in the CyTOF staining stage after upstream preparation and sorting. This local segment covers pre-stain handling, surface staining, palladium exposure, cold fixation/permeabilization transition, and intracellular staining while preserving the small working volume needed for correct reagent ratios.
goal: Advance the cell suspension through the CyTOF staining sub-procedure so it reaches the intracellularly stained state for later acquisition.
constraints: Include an initial cRPMI wash represented with 2 mL total volume and a 400 x g, 5 min, room-temperature centrifugation before the pre-stain incubation.

Use a 30 min incubation at 37 C for pre-stain treatment, then perform a room-temperature surface staining incubation for 45 min using a 1:1 normalized ratio of 50 uL antibody mix to about 50 uL concentrated cells.
Represent CyFACS wash handling around the surface-stain portion with room-temperature 400 x g spins, while preserving the notion that the sample is concentrated to about 50 uL before stain additions.
Apply DCED palladium working solution as a 1 mL addition with a 5 min room-temperature incubation, then transition to cold conditions for later processing.
Cold processing must include fix/perm addition of 1 mL, a 45 min incubation at 4 C, and a later higher-force centrifugation at 800 x g for 5 min before intracellular staining.
Finish with intracellular antibody staining using a normalized 50 uL addition and a 45 min incubation at 4 C.

source_steps.description: Wash in 2 mL of cRPMI (centrifuge at 400× g for 5 min at RT). Critical step for accurate staining volume: Remove the supernatant by decantation, then carefully aspirate the residual hanging drop using a P1000 pipette, leaving approximately 50 μL of final volume.
Add pre-stains, mix, and incubate for 30 min at 37 °C.
Wash 3× 2 mL of CyFACS (centrifuge at 400× g for 5 min at RT).
Add surface stains, mix, and incubate for 45 min at RT (50 μL of Ab-mix to 50 μL of cells).
Wash 3× 2 mL of CyFACS (centrifuge at 400× g for 5 min at RT) and pipette the final supernatant to 50 μL.
During the final spin, prepare DCED palladium in 1 mL of CyPBS at 1:500.
Pipette mix and incubate for 5 min at RT.
Wash 2× in 5 mL of CyFACS (centrifuge at 400× g for 5 min at 4 °C) (all 4 °C from now on). Pipette the final supernatant down to 50 μL.
Add 1 mL of fix/perm buffer and incubate for 45 min at 4 °C.
Prepare Invitrogen 10× perm buffer:

Wash 2× in 5 mL of perm buffer (centrifuge at 800× g for 5 min at 4 °C). Critical step for accurate staining volume: Remove the supernatant by decantation, then carefully aspirate the residual hanging drop using a P1000 pipette, leaving approximately 50 μL of final volume.
Add the intracellular Ab cocktail, mix, and incubate for 45 min at 4 °C (50 μL of Ab-mix to 50 μL of cells).

background: Murine intestinal lamina propria cells have been dissociated and are entering the flow-cytometry staining workflow. At this stage, the cells must be collected, handled in a cold V-bottom plate workflow, exposed to Fc block and surface-staining reagents with intervening washes, then fixed, permeabilized, and stained for intracellular markers under light-protected conditions.
goal: Convert the dissociated lamina propria cell suspension into a final washed intracellularly stained cell preparation suitable for later flow-cytometry acquisition.
constraints: Include the initial collection spin at 450 x g for 10 min at 4 C before the repeated plate-based staining spins.
Use multiple cold centrifugation steps at 300 x g for 5 min during Fc-block and surface-staining handling, then switch to 400 x g for 5 min for post-fixation permeabilization washes.
Include both a 30 min Fc-block incubation at 4 C and a 30 min surface-staining incubation at 4 C in the dark.
Include fixation/permeabilization treatment at 4 C in the dark for a duration within 30 to 60 min, followed by a 60 min intracellular antibody incubation at 4 C in the dark.

Use only provided action-pool functions, keep a single dependency chain from the starting suspension to the final washed sample, and use a mixing action to represent resuspension/wash handling between spins.

available_inputs.name: culture_media
available_inputs.description: Medium used to resuspend the pelleted cells to a staining-appropriate concentration.

available_inputs.name: facs_buffer_fcblock

available_inputs.name: surface_antibody_mix
available_inputs.description: FACS Buffer-I containing viability dye plus surface-staining antibodies.

available_inputs.name: fix_perm_solution

gold_program:
collected_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=dissociated_cells, speed_xg=450, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
resuspended_cells = mix_sample(sample=collected_cells, method="pipetting", duration_s=20, speed="gentle")
plate_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=resuspended_cells, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
fc_block_mix = mix_sample(sample=plate_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=15, speed="gentle")
fc_blocked_cells = incubate_sample(sample=fc_block_mix, temperature_c=4, duration_min=30, condition="static")
washed_surface_ready = centrifuge_sample(sample=fc_blocked_cells, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
surface_stain_mix = mix_sample(sample=washed_surface_ready, method="pipetting", duration_s=15, speed="gentle")
surface_stained_cells = incubate_sample(sample=surface_stain_mix, temperature_c=4, duration_min=30, condition="protected from light")
post_surface_wash = centrifuge_sample(sample=surface_stained_cells, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
fixed_cells = incubate_sample(sample=fix_perm_solution, temperature_c=4, duration_min=30, condition="protected from light")
permeabilized_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=fixed_cells, speed_xg=400, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
intracellular_stained_cells = incubate_sample(sample=perm_buffer_with_ic_ab, temperature_c=4, duration_min=60, condition="protected from light")

source_steps.description: Centrifuge the remaining cell suspension at 450 × g for 10 min at 4°C and resuspend the pellet in the desired volume of media to get optimal cell numbers for staining or restimulation.

Once the cells are resuspended, seed them in a labeled 96-well V-bottom plate and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at 4°C. Throughout the staining process the V-bottom plate must be centrifuged at 300 × g for 5 min at 4°C unless explicitly mentioned otherwise (see troubleshooting problem 3 ).
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cells in 50 μL of FACS Buffer-I containing Fc-block (anti-mouse CD16/CD32). Incubate the cells for 30 min at 4°C.

Meanwhile, prepare the antibody cocktails for staining different immune cell populations. Above are the (8-color) panels we used to test the enzymatic efficiency of the different digestion enzymes in isolating immune cells residing in the intestinal-LP.
Add 150 μL of FACS Buffer-I to wash out the excess, unbound Fc block and centrifuge the plate 300 × g for 5 min at 4°C.
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cells in 200 μL of FACS Buffer-I to wash the cells. Centrifuge the plate at 300 × g for 5 min at 4°C and discard the supernatant.

Resuspend the cell pellet in 50 μL of FACS Buffer-I containing the antibody mix, including biotinylated antibodies, fixable viability dye, and fluorochrome-labeled antibodies against surface antigens. Incubate for 30 min at 4°C in the dark. After the incubation, add 150 μL of FACS Buffer-I per well to wash out the unbound antibodies and repeat step 27.

Prepare the fixation solution in the eBioscience FoxP3 / Transcription Factor Staining Buffer Set, by diluting 1 part of the fixation/permeabilization concentrate with 3 parts of the fixation/permeabilization diluent. For 1× permeabilization wash buffer, add 9 parts of double-distilled water to 1 part of the 10× permeabilization buffer in the kit. Mix well before use.

Resuspend the cells in 100 μL of the fixation solution and incubate for 30 min to 1 h in the dark at 4°C. Add 100 μL of the permeabilization buffer to wash off the fixation solution, centrifuge, and discard the supernatant.

Resuspend the pellet in 200 μL of permeabilization buffer to ensure permeabilization of the cells and to remove the residual fixation solution. Centrifuge at 400 × g for 5 min at 4°C and discard the supernatant.

Resuspend the cells in 50 μL of permeabilization buffer containing the intracellular antibodies and incubate in the dark for 1 h at 4°C. If there is a weak staining of intracellular antigens, refer to troubleshooting problem 4 for suggestions.

Add 150 μL of permeabilization buffer, centrifuge the cells at 400 × g for 5 min at 4°C and discard the supernatant. Repeat the wash with 200 μL of permeabilization buffer, centrifuge, and discard the supernatant.

background: Cultured cells have already completed blocking and primary antibody labeling for an in situ proximity ligation assay used to detect transcription factor occupancy on DNA in single cells. The remaining local stage covers probe binding, ligation, rolling-circle amplification, and cleanup washes under room-temperature rocking and humidified 37 C incubations, with light protection during amplification.
goal: Advance the prepared cell samples through the PLA probe, ligation, amplification, and final wash phase so they are ready for the next imaging-preparation stage.
constraints: Start from the antibody-labeled plate and keep a continuous dependency chain through probe incubation, ligation, amplification, and final washing.
Whenever Wash Buffer A is used, model the wash pattern as one 5 min gentle rocking wash at about 20 to 22 C followed by two 2 min gentle rocking washes.
Include a 37 C humidified incubation for PLA probes lasting 60 min and a separate 37 C humidified ligation incubation lasting 30 min.
Include a 37 C amplification incubation lasting 100 to 120 min, and this incubation must be protected from light.
After amplification, perform the Wash Buffer B cleanup as one 5 min wash followed by two 2 min washes, reflecting 1X buffer first and then 0.01X buffer.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keyword arguments only, assign every call to a variable, and keep the program to 6 to 12 top-level steps.

wash_a_after_primary = mix_sample(sample=antibody_labeled_plate, method='rocking', duration_s=300.0, speed='slow')
probe_incubated_plate = incubate_sample(sample=wash_a_after_primary, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=60.0, condition='humidified with PLA probes')
wash_a_after_probe = mix_sample(sample=probe_incubated_plate, method='rocking', duration_s=300.0, speed='slow')

wash_a_after_ligation = mix_sample(sample=ligated_plate, method='rocking', duration_s=300.0, speed='slow')
amplified_plate = incubate_sample(sample=wash_a_after_ligation, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=100.0, condition='humidified, protected from light, with amplification reaction mix')

wash_b_second = mix_sample(sample=wash_b_first, method='rocking', duration_s=120.0, speed='slow')
final_plate = mix_sample(sample=wash_b_second, method='rocking', duration_s=120.0, speed='slow')

source_steps.description: Remove the blocking solution from the wells and add 100 μL of the diluted primary antibodies to each well. Incubate for 12–16 h on a slow laboratory rocker at 4°C. Note: Primary antibodies could also be incubated at 20°C–22°C for 1–4 h or at 37°C for 1–2 h. However, the incubation times need to be optimized for individual antibodies. Troubleshooting problem 5 . CRITICAL: The two primary antibodies being used must be raised in two different species. Troubleshooting problem 3 .

PLA Probe incubation. Discard the primary antibodies from the wells and gently wash the cells with 150 μL of Wash Buffer A on a slow laboratory rocker for 5 min at 20°C–22°C. Repeat the washing step twice for 2 min each.

Dilute both the 5X Duolink I n Situ PLA anti-Rabbit PLUS and 5X Duolink I n Situ PLA anti-Mouse MINUS probes together in the 1X antibody diluent provided in the kit to make a 1X working probe dilution. CRITICAL: The probe mix must include a PLUS PLA probe with another MINUS PLA probe targeting two separate species corresponding to the primary antibodies (e.g., an anti-rabbit PLUS probe should be combined with an anti-mouse MINUS probe). Troubleshooting problem 3 .

Discard the Wash Buffer A from the wells using a micropipette and add 40 μL of the diluted PLA probes to each well. Note: It is important to fully cover the entire sample with the diluted probes. If needed, the total volume in each well could be increased to 50–60 μL.
Incubate the plate for 1 h at 37°C in a humidified incubator. Alternatives: A CO 2 cell culture incubator could be used.

Oligonucleotide ligation reaction. Discard the probes and wash the cells by adding 100 μL of Wash Buffer A on a slow laboratory rocker at 20°C–22°C for 5 min. Repeat the washing step twice for 2 min each.

Dilute the 5X Ligation Buffer provided with the Duolink In Situ PLA probe kits to 1X working solution using purified molecular biology grade water. Add 1 μL of the DNA Ligase per 39 μL of the 1X Ligation buffer to prepare the ligation reaction mix. Note: The 5X ligation buffer should be fully thawed and resolved on ice before dilution. It is important to store the ligase in a −20°C freezer and remove it right before adding it to the reaction mix. Troubleshooting problems 3 and 4 .
Add 40 μL of the ligation reaction mix to each well and incubate the plate at 37°C for 30 min in a humidified incubator. Alternatives: A CO 2 cell culture incubator could be used.

Rolling circle amplification reaction. Discard the ligation reaction mix from the wells and wash the cells by adding 100 μL of Wash Buffer A on a slow laboratory rocker for 5 min at 20°C–22°C. Repeat the washing step twice for 2 min each.

Dilute the 5X Amplification Buffer provided with the Duolink In Situ Detection Reagents kit to 1X working solution using purified molecular biology grade water. Add 0.5 μL of the DNA polymerase to prepare the amplification reaction mix in 39.5 μL of the 1X Amplification buffer. Note: The amplification buffer should be fully thawed and resolved before dilution. The DNA polymerase should be stored in a −20°C freezer and removed just before adding it to the reaction mix. If possible, aliquot the DNA polymerase and avoid repeated freeze/thaw cycles. Troubleshooting problems 3 and 4 .

Add 40 μL of the reaction mix to each well and incubate the plate for 100–120 min at 37°C in a humidified incubator. Alternatives: A CO 2 cell culture incubator could be used. Note: The amplification reaction is light sensitive, so the samples should be covered and protected from light during the amplification reaction. Some weaker interactions may require longer incubation durations. The incubation timings need to be optimized based on the TFs being studied. Troubleshooting problems 3 and 4 .

Nuclear staining and mounting. Discard the amplification reaction mix from the wells and wash the cells by adding 100 μL of 1X Wash Buffer B on a slow laboratory rocker for 5 min at 20°C–22°C. Wash two more times using 0.01X Wash Buffer B for 2 min each.

background: This assay stage prepares yeast chromatin for nucleosome mapping after cell collection. The pellet is converted into fragile spheroplasts, maintained under cold handling before nuclease exposure, then taken through MNase digestion, reaction quenching, organic extraction, and ethanol-based DNA recovery so the nucleosomal material can be cleaned and concentrated for later analysis.
goal: Carry out the local MNase-ISO processing segment from spheroplast formation through RNase treatment and the final ethanol re-precipitation spin.
constraints: Start from the cell pellet, include spheroplasting at 20.0-22.0 C for 6-7 min with gentle inversion, and keep samples cold after this digestion until the MNase step begins.
Include quench buffer pre-warming at 37 C, then a 500 x g centrifugation for 3 min at 4 C followed by two gentle ice-cold sorbitol wash/resuspension cycles.
Perform MNase digestion at 37 C for 8 min, then stop the reaction and include a 65 C incubation lasting within 5-10 min.

Represent organic extraction with vigorous mixing and a high-speed spin greater than 14000 x g for 7-10 min at about room temperature, followed by ethanol precipitation and another high-speed 5 min spin.
After pellet recovery, include TE resuspension with RNase treatment at 37 C for 30 min, then ammonium acetate plus ethanol re-precipitation and a final high-speed 5 min centrifugation.
Use only provided action-pool functions, assign every call to a variable, and keep the gold program to 6-12 top-level steps while reusing at least two action names.

gold_program:
step1 = incubate_sample(sample=cell_pellet, temperature_c=21.0, duration_min=6.5, condition='gentle inversion')
step2 = heat_sample(sample=quench_buffer, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=5.0, device='incubator')
step3 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step1, speed_xg=500, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step4 = mix_sample(sample=step3, method='pipetting', duration_s=20.0, speed='gentle')
step5 = mix_sample(sample=step4, method='pipetting', duration_s=20.0, speed='gentle')
step6 = incubate_sample(sample=mnase_digest_mix, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=8.0, condition='static')
step7 = heat_sample(sample=step6, temperature_c=65.0, duration_min=7.0, device='heat block')
step8 = vortex_mix(sample=organic_phase_mix, duration_s=60.0, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=22.0)
step9 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step8, speed_xg=14001, duration_min=8.0, temperature_c=22.0)
step10 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step9, speed_xg=14001, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=22.0)
step11 = incubate_sample(sample=te_rnase_sample, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=30.0, condition='static')
step12 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step11, speed_xg=14001, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=22.0)

source_steps.description: Pellet the cells again and remove as much liquid as possible. Resuspend the cells in 500 μL of cold spheroplasting solution and add 30 μL of 0.5 mg/mL zymolyase.

Incubate at 20°C–22°C for 6–7 min with gentle inversion. Note: The incubation time is influenced by the strain type and growth condition. Note: After zymolyase digestion, always keep cells cold until MNase digestion.
Pre-warm quench buffer by 37°C incubation.

Centrifuge cells at 500 × g for 3 min at 4°C and carefully wash twice with 1 mL 1 M ice-cold sorbitol by gently dislodging and stirring the cell pellet with a 1000 μL pipette tip. Do not pipette up and down or vortex.
Gently resuspend cells in 200 μL of cold MNase digestion buffer with a pipette tip. CRITICAL: Cells are fragile after zymolyase digestion, so resuspend cells very gently.

Add 0.5 μL of MNase to each reaction and then incubate for 8 min in a 37°C water bath. Note: In our experience, this MNase to DNA ratio will convert >50% of chromatin into mono-nucleosomes. Due to variations in MNase activity, we recommend doing a MNase titration to find a concentration that will generate a mono-, di-, and tri- nucleosome ladder, but with most mass in the mono-nucleosome band. A mock digest without MNase will generate a genomic DNA sample (control).
Add 20 μL quench buffer and vortex to terminate the reaction. Samples are incubated at 65°C for 5–10 min. Note: The solution should become clearer after this step.
Add 0.2 mL phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol and vortex for 1 min.

Centrifuge at >14,000 × g for 7–10 min at 20°C–22°C and carefully transfer the top layers (∼150 μL) to new 1.5 mL tubes. Add 2.5 times the volume of the top layer of 100% ethanol. Vortex for a few seconds and invert to mix.
Centrifuge at >14,000 × g at 20°C–22°C for 5 min, remove supernatant, carefully aspirate all liquid. Note: You should see a clear pellet at this point.
Re-suspend the pellet in 0.4 mL TE buffer (pH8.0) and add 20 μL of 10 mg/mL RNase A. Incubate at 37°C for 30 min.
Add 6 μL of 5 M ammonium acetate plus 1 mL of 100% ethanol. Mix by inversion. Centrifuge at >14,000 × g at 20°C–22°C for 5 min. Note: Pellet size is likely to decrease but should still be visible.

background: A conditioned cell-culture supernatant has already had larger debris removed and is being processed through the extracellular vesicle and particle isolation workflow. At this stage, the task is to clear remaining larger contaminants, collect the EVP fraction by ultracentrifugation, wash the pellet in cold PBS, and prepare the final suspension for frozen storage while maintaining cold conditions.
goal: Process the pre-cleared culture supernatant through the late isolation and wash phase to obtain a washed EVP pellet resuspended in PBS and stored for later downstream characterization.
constraints: Keep intermediates cold between isolation operations; use 4 C for standard chilling and 10 C during ultracentrifugation.
Include a contaminant-clearing centrifugation at 12000 x g for 20 min before EVP collection.
Use two ultracentrifugation spins at 100000 x g for 70 min each, with the second spin acting as the PBS wash recollection step.
After the first 100000 x g spin, represent removal of supernatant before the pellet wash and keep the pellet-associated material as the dependency for the wash.
Re-suspend the collected EVP pellet in 500 uL cold PBS for the wash phase, then dilute into wash buffer represented by a larger PBS volume before the second ultracentrifugation.
After the wash spin, remove residual PBS and re-suspend the final pellet in 100 uL cold PBS.
Store the final EVP preparation at -80 C.
Use only the provided action-pool functions and maintain a single forward dependency chain from the starting supernatant to the stored final sample.

available_inputs.name: cell_supernatant
available_inputs.description: Pre-cleared conditioned medium supernatant from cultured cells, ready for the 12000 x g clarification step.

available_inputs.name: cold_pbs
available_inputs.description: Ice-cold PBS available for pellet wash and final resuspension.

available_inputs.name: wash_pbs_volume_ml
available_inputs.description: PBS volume used to dilute the washed suspension before the second ultracentrifugation, such as 3, 20, or 44 mL depending on tube size.

gold_program:
clarified_supernatant = centrifuge_sample(sample=cell_supernatant, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=20, temperature_c=4)
chilled_supernatant = cool_sample(sample=clarified_supernatant, temperature_c=4, duration_min=1, device="ice bath")
first_evp_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=chilled_supernatant, speed_xg=100000, duration_min=70, temperature_c=10)
decanted_first_pellet = dry_sample(sample=first_evp_pellet, method="decant", temperature_c=10, duration_min=2)
washed_pellet_suspension = mix_sample(sample=decanted_first_pellet, method="pipetting with 500 uL cold PBS", duration_s=30, speed="gentle")
wash_diluted_sample = mix_sample(sample=washed_pellet_suspension, method="pipetting with additional PBS to wash_pbs_volume_ml mL", duration_s=20, speed="gentle")
second_evp_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_diluted_sample, speed_xg=100000, duration_min=70, temperature_c=10)
decanted_second_pellet = dry_sample(sample=second_evp_pellet, method="decant and tap inverted tube", temperature_c=10, duration_min=2)
final_evp_suspension = mix_sample(sample=decanted_second_pellet, method="pipetting with 100 uL cold PBS", duration_s=30, speed="gentle")
stored_evp_prep = store_sample(sample=final_evp_suspension, temperature_c=-80, duration_h=24, container="storage tube")
protocol_title: Extracellular vesicle and particle isolation from human and murine cell lines, tissues, and bodily fluids

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Collect the supernatant without disturbing the cell/debris pellet. This can be done by quickly pouring off the supernatant into a new container/tube or carefully pipetting off the supernatant. It is essential to work as quickly as possible during these steps, as the pellet will become dislodged over time. Note: For this and all subsequent steps, supernatants and re-suspended EVP pellets should be kept on ice between isolation steps. Note: A step of 3,000 × g centrifugation for 20 min at 4°C should be added for cell culture supernatants before short-term storage (<24 h) at 4°C or before freezing to remove additional cell debris contaminants. This step has to be performed if further analysis of the post-12,000 × g microvesicle sample is planned. Pause Point: Conditioned media supernatants can be stored at −80°C for long-term storage or at 4 °C for short-term (<24 h) storage before ultracentrifugation. Minor differences have been observed between EVPs isolated from fresh versus frozen samples in terms of yield and purity. However, frozen samples are often used to allow for retrospective studies, collaborative studies between different institutions and batch processing of test samples/subjects and controls.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the supernatant at 12,000 × g for 20 min at 4°C to remove apoptotic bodies and aggregates and larger vesicles that could otherwise be co-isolated with EVPs and “contaminate” the EVP preparation. Note: This step can be performed in the ultracentrifuge or tabletop centrifuge depending on volume and availability of the ultracentrifuge. We recommend performing the 12,000 × g spin right before ultracentrifugation since contaminating material could develop by freeze-thaw cycles or extended storage times.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Collect supernatant without disturbing the pellet and transfer to new/clean ultracentrifuge tube by pipetting, leaving a small volume behind to avoid contamination from pellet. CRITICAL: Wash newly purchased ultracentrifugation tubes with soap and water and rinse them extensively in ddH 2 O before the first EVP isolation, since the factory coating on the inside of the tube will prevent the EVP pellet from adhering to the tube. After use, the tubes should be washed extensively and sterilized by autoclaving if required. Cleaning of ultracentrifugation tubes was performed as per standard cleaning procedure for laboratory glassware and utensils used for molecular and cellular biology applications. We extensively wash the tubes in soap and hot water while scrubbing with a brush. The tubes are then thoroughly rinsed with tap water to remove any traces of soap, followed by additional rinses in ddH 2 O. If EVPs are isolated for in vitro or in vivo work rather than molecular biology, the tubes can be autoclaved, this however shortens the life of the tubes. No contamination between samples has ever been observed using these procedures. The tube used depends on the volume of starting material rather than the type of specimen. Note: It is important to avoid disturbing the pellet with the pipette tip in these centrifugation steps since the aim is to remove pelleted contamination; instead, leave a minimal amount of media above the pellet to avoid dissociating the pellet. Remember to work as quickly as possible during these steps as the pellet will become dislodged over time.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Isolate EVPs by ultracentrifugation Centrifuge supernatant at 100,000 × g for 70 min at 10°C in a fixed-angle rotor in a Beckman Coulter ultracentrifuge (see Materials and equipment ) to collect EVPs. CRITICAL: It is very important to accurately balance the tubes in the ultracentrifuge. An unbalanced rotor can cause spillage, contamination, run failure, or even damage the rotor/ultracentrifuge. Note: Before inserting tubes into the rotor, we recommend marking an area on the bottom of the tube where the pellet is expected to form, since it may not be easily visible. Avoid disturbing the pelleted area when discarding the supernatant and pay careful attention to it for successful retrieval of your EVP pellet ( Figure 2 ). Careful handling of the EVP pellet (A) After ultracentrifugation, carefully decant supernatant and (B and C) re-suspend the pellet in a small volume of PBS (0.5–1 mL depending on tube size) by pipetting and flushing the marked area of the lower side of the ultracentrifugation tube. Avoid generating bubbles.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant by decanting or carefully pipetting without disturbing the pellet. If pipetting off the supernatant, keep the tip of the pipet on the side of the tube opposite from the pellet. If decanting the supernatant, be sure to pour the supernatant off the side of the tube opposite the pellet and use one quick, careful motion to invert the tube. Optional: Store supernatant if interested in EVP-depleted media for measurement of other secreted factors, such as cytokines. Note: In this and subsequent steps it is important to work quickly and remove the supernatant as fast as possible after the ultracentrifuge has stopped to prevent dissociation of the pellet. After removing the supernatant, leave the pellet untouched and place the inverted tube in a rack for 1–3 min to avoid backflow of liquid. This ensures minimal carryover of contaminants and prevents dissociation of the pellet. Remove drops of liquid from tube walls by vacuum aspiration or using a Kimwipe, if necessary. Troubleshooting 1 .

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: To wash the pellet, re-suspend it in 500 μL of cold PBS by pipetting and flushing the marked area at the bottom of the ultracentrifugation tube. Ensure the pellet is re-suspended well so contaminants trapped in the pellet (free, abundant proteins) are washed away. Transfer the re-suspended pellet to a new ultracentrifuge tube and add 3 mL (tube #355645), 20 mL (tube #355631) or 44 mL (tube #355628) of cold PBS. We recommend using the same size tube throughout the protocol (i.e., depending on starting volume) since the starting volume affects the yield. Thus, small volumes, 1–3 mL should be spun in a small tube where these fewer EVPs can more readily be collected and concentrated. Troubleshooting 2 .

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the re-suspended pellet at 100,000 × g for 70 min at 10°C to collect the washed EVP pellet.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Discard the PBS supernatant by decanting and remove all liquid by gently tapping the inverted tube to avoid backflow.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from cell culture
source_steps.description: Re-suspend the EVP pellet in 100 μL (see note for range below) of cold PBS and store at −80°C. Reserve 10 μL aliquots for downstream quantification and characterization, such as protein quantification, NTA, and EM, to avoid freeze-thaw cycles.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from tissue samples
source_steps.description: Collect the tumor tissue and adjacent tissue in ice-cold PBS at the time of surgery and transfer them on ice to the class II biosafety cabinet within 30 min.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from tissue samples
source_steps.description: Wash tissues in cold PBS × 3 and quickly cut the tissues into 2 mm 3 -sized pieces. Rinse tissue pieces again in PBS before plating if needed to remove debris and/or blood.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of EVPs from tissue samples
source_steps.description: Place tissue pieces in a 6-well plate (<10 pieces) or a 10-cm dish (10–15 pieces).

background: A recombinant plant viral binary vector has been assembled and introduced into electrocompetent E. coli. The cells have recovered after transformation, candidate colonies have been grown in selective liquid culture, and the task is now to process the culture to obtain and preserve plasmid DNA corresponding to recombinant clones. This stage emphasizes bacterial pelleting, extended lysis handling, sequential loading-compatible cleanup, and appropriate cold storage of pellets or purified DNA.
goal: Process an overnight kanamycin-selected E. coli culture carrying the recombinant binary vector through harvest and plasmid-prep-related handling so that a purified plasmid sample is obtained and stored for later use.
constraints: Use the transformed E. coli culture as the starting material for harvest, not the original assembly reaction.
Include bacterial recovery/growth under vigorous shaking at 37°C within the workflow, using a duration consistent with overnight culture and allowing for slow-growing full-length viral constructs.
At least two centrifugation operations must occur at 15000 x g, with one lasting 2 min and another shorter spin of 0.5 min to remove residual medium.
After pelleting, the intermediate bacterial pellet should be compatible with either immediate plasmid processing or frozen storage; represent the pellet state explicitly before downstream handling.

Model plasmid purification at the abstraction level allowed by the action pool rather than kit-specific bind/wash chemistry, but preserve the key extended alkaline lysis clearing behavior by including a 5 min room-temperature handling period after lysis-type processing.
Use repeated action families multiple times; at least one repeated family should differ mainly by duration or temperature.
Finish with storage of the purified plasmid at -20°C for long-term preservation.
Do not introduce unsupported operations such as electroporation, plate spreading, colony picking, or liquid transfer primitives.

available_inputs.name: transformant_culture
available_inputs.description: Liquid LB culture with 50 mg/L kanamycin inoculated from a white E. coli colony carrying the candidate recombinant binary vector.

available_inputs.name: lysis_processed_sample
available_inputs.description: A symbolic sample representing the culture after addition of plasmid-prep resuspension, lysis, and neutralization solutions at double volume.

available_inputs.name: plasmid_column_load
available_inputs.description: A symbolic cleared lysate fraction intended for repeated loading onto a plasmid spin column.

gold_program:
overnight_culture = incubate_sample(sample=transformant_culture, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=960.0, condition='shaking 225 rpm')
harvested_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=overnight_culture, speed_xg=15000, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=25.0)
residual_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=harvested_cells, speed_xg=15000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=25.0)
frozen_pellet = store_sample(sample=residual_spin, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_h=24.0, container='microcentrifuge_tube')
thawed_pellet = cool_sample(sample=frozen_pellet, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=10.0, device='bench')
resuspended_pellet = mix_sample(sample=thawed_pellet, method='pipetting', duration_s=20.0, speed='gentle')
cleared_lysate = incubate_sample(sample=lysis_processed_sample, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=5.0, condition='static')
loaded_column_once = centrifuge_sample(sample=plasmid_column_load, speed_xg=15000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
loaded_column_twice = centrifuge_sample(sample=loaded_column_once, speed_xg=15000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
purified_plasmid = mix_sample(sample=loaded_column_twice, method='pipetting', duration_s=15.0, speed='gentle')
stored_plasmid = store_sample(sample=purified_plasmid, temperature_c=-20.0, duration_h=168.0, container='dna_storage_tube')
protocol_title: Heritable CRISPR-Cas9 editing of plant genomes using RNA virus vectors
