source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: In vitro homology-based assembly: Mix the gel-purified linearized vector (step 4) and a synthetic DNA fragment (step 3) in a molar ratio of 1:2 and a total volume of ∼2 μL. Note: Use a total DNA amount of 0.05–2 pmol. Note: The NEBiocalculator® Tool ( https://nebiocalculator.neb.com/ ) can be used for molarity calculations.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Add 1 volume (∼2 μL) of NEBuilder® HiFi DNA assembly master mix (New England Biolabs). Alternatives: Gibson assembly 31 can also be used for homology-based cloning; the required enzymatic mix can be in-house prepared as detailed, 32 or purchased from commercial providers (e.g., Gibson Assembly® master mix, New England Biolabs, cat. number E2611S). If using Gibson assembly, no protocol changes are required.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Incubate the mix at 50°C (1 h). Once the reaction ends, place tubes on ice.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Transformation of E. coli cells: Thaw a 40-μL aliquot of electrocompetent E. coli DH5α cells on ice. Alternatives: Other E. coli strains suitable for high-efficient transformation may be used.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Gently mix by pipetting a cell aliquot with 1 μL of the assembly reaction from step 6.b, and transfer the mixture to a pre-chilled electroporation cuvette.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Electroporate cells (1,500 V), mix them with 1 mL of room-temperature SOC (see Materials and equipment) and transfer to a 1.5-mL tube. Note: Once DNA is added to cells electroporation can be carried out immediately, without any incubation. Note: If your sample arcs when electroporating, dialyze or purify the assembly reaction to remove salt excess. Alternatives: Heat shock of chemically-competent E. coli cells for high-efficiency transformation 23 may be used.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Incubate the cells at 37°C, shaking vigorously at 200–250 rpm (1 h). Note: Warm selection plates at 37°C while cells are recovering.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Pellet bacteria by centrifuging at 15,000 × g (1 min), resuspend bacteria in 100 μL of SOC and plate the suspension onto LB agar plates supplemented with 50 mg/L kanamycin and 40 μL X-Gal (40 mg/mL). Incubate at 37°C (overnight). Note: Plate the X-Gal solution on agar plates before use for white-blue screen of recombinant clones. Note: Bacterial cells harboring binary vectors with viral genomic sequences generally display low growth rates; plate incubation up to 24 h may be necessary. Pause point: After colony appearance, plates can be stored 1–3 days at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Confirm the presence of recombinant binary vectors in E. coli transformants: Pick a white colony obtained by X-Gal selection and inoculate 5–10 mL liquid LB with 50 mg/L kanamycin in 50 mL tubes. Incubate the tubes at 37°C, shaking vigorously at 200–250 rpm (overnight). Note: Bacterial cells harboring binary vectors with full-length virus clones generally display low growth rates; extend culturing time up to 24 h if necessary. Note: It is recommended to purify plasmid DNA from 2–4 colonies per construct.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Harvest bacteria from the culture by centrifugation at 15,000 × g (2 min); discard the medium, centrifuge again (30 s) and remove medium residues by pipetting. Pause point: Harvested bacterial pellets can be frozen and stored at −80°C several days before proceeding with plasmid DNA purification.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Purify DNA plasmids from bacterial pellets using NucleoSpin® plasmid kit (Macherey Nagel) as per manufacturer’s instructions, except that: Use double volumes of resuspension, lysis and neutralization solutions supplied within the kit to improve clearing of bacterial lysates and increase the amount of purified plasmid. Note: After addition of the lysis solution incubate until the cell suspension clears (5 min).

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Transfer ∼800 μL of cleared lysate to the column, centrifuge, discard the flowthrough and load again the same column with the remainder of the lysate. Note: The volume of the cleared bacterial lysate will exceed the spin column capacity. Note: Other commercial plasmid DNA purification kits can also be used. Pause point: Purified DNA plasmids can be frozen and stored at −20°C indefinitely.

background: This experiment measures Treg suppressive function in an autologous PBMC setting using CyTOF. The current segment is a wash- and stain-intensive portion of the workflow after an earlier pre-stain, where the cells move through surface labeling, palladium treatment, cold fixation/permeabilization, intracellular staining, and final cleanup washes.
goal: Advance the partially processed cell sample through the remaining CyTOF staining stage so that it completes surface-associated handling, palladium exposure, fixation/permeabilization, intracellular staining, and terminal cold washes.
constraints: Use centrifugation-supported CyFACS washes at 400 x g for 5 min at room temperature before the cold transition, and keep later centrifugation and incubation steps at 4 C.
Include surface staining with a 50 uL antibody mix applied to a 50 uL cell suspension and incubated for 45 min at room temperature before downstream washing.
Model palladium exposure as DCED palladium prepared in 1 mL CyPBS at 1:500 with a 5 min room-temperature incubation after mixing.
After palladium treatment, perform cold CyFACS washing, then add 1 mL fix/perm buffer and incubate for 45 min at 4 C.

Use 800 x g for 5 min cold spins around the intracellular staining stage with perm buffer, add intracellular antibody cocktail at a 50 uL to 50 uL ratio, incubate 45 min at 4 C, and finish with cold perm-buffer then CyPBS washes.

available_inputs.name: prestained_cells
available_inputs.description: Cell suspension entering this CyTOF segment after completion of the earlier pre-stain incubation.

available_inputs.name: cyfacs
available_inputs.description: CyFACS buffer for wash steps.

available_inputs.name: surface_ab_mix
available_inputs.description: Prepared surface antibody mix for CyTOF staining.

available_inputs.name: fix_perm_buffer
available_inputs.description: Fixation and permeabilization buffer.

available_inputs.name: perm_buffer
available_inputs.description: Permeabilization buffer used during intracellular staining washes.

available_inputs.name: intracellular_ab_cocktail
available_inputs.description: Prepared intracellular antibody cocktail.

available_inputs.name: cypbs
available_inputs.description: CyPBS used in the palladium preparation context and final wash.

gold_program:
wash_rt = add_reagent(sample=prestained_cells, reagent=cyfacs, volume_ul=2000.0, mixing='pipette')
spin_rt = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_rt, speed_xg=400, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=22.0)
surface_stain = add_reagent(sample=spin_rt, reagent=surface_ab_mix, volume_ul=50.0, mixing='pipette')
surface_incubation = incubate_sample(sample=surface_stain, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=45.0, condition='static')
palladium_step = add_reagent(sample=surface_incubation, reagent='DCED palladium in 1 mL CyPBS at 1:500', volume_ul=1000.0, mixing='pipette')
palladium_incubation = incubate_sample(sample=palladium_step, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=5.0, condition='static')
cold_wash_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=palladium_incubation, speed_xg=400, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
fixperm_step = add_reagent(sample=cold_wash_spin, reagent=fix_perm_buffer, volume_ul=1000.0, mixing='pipette')
fixperm_incubation = incubate_sample(sample=fixperm_step, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=45.0, condition='static')
ics_step = add_reagent(sample=fixperm_incubation, reagent=intracellular_ab_cocktail, volume_ul=50.0, mixing='pipette')
ics_incubation = incubate_sample(sample=ics_step, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=45.0, condition='static')
final_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=ics_incubation, speed_xg=800, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)

source_steps.description: Add pre-stains, mix, and incubate for 30 min at 37 °C.
Wash 3× 2 mL of CyFACS (centrifuge at 400× g for 5 min at RT).
Add surface stains, mix, and incubate for 45 min at RT (50 μL of Ab-mix to 50 μL of cells).
Wash 3× 2 mL of CyFACS (centrifuge at 400× g for 5 min at RT) and pipette the final supernatant to 50 μL.
During the final spin, prepare DCED palladium in 1 mL of CyPBS at 1:500.
Pipette mix and incubate for 5 min at RT.
Wash 2× in 5 mL of CyFACS (centrifuge at 400× g for 5 min at 4 °C) (all 4 °C from now on). Pipette the final supernatant down to 50 μL.
Add 1 mL of fix/perm buffer and incubate for 45 min at 4 °C.
Prepare Invitrogen 10× perm buffer:

Wash 2× in 5 mL of perm buffer (centrifuge at 800× g for 5 min at 4 °C). Critical step for accurate staining volume: Remove the supernatant by decantation, then carefully aspirate the residual hanging drop using a P1000 pipette, leaving approximately 50 μL of final volume.
Add the intracellular Ab cocktail, mix, and incubate for 45 min at 4 °C (50 μL of Ab-mix to 50 μL of cells).
Wash twice with 5 mL of perm buffer and then once with 2 mL of CyPBS (centrifuge at 800× g for 5 min at 4 °C).

background: hiPSC-derived neural crest cells are at the flow-cytometry characterization stage after dissociation from culture wells. The remaining work is a local staining workflow in FACS tubes that includes sterile filtration, repeated wash-and-spin cleanup, cold light-protected antibody incubation, and fixation/permeabilization before intracellular staining to assess residual undifferentiated cells.
goal: Prepare the hiPSC-NCC sample for flow-cytometry analysis by carrying out the staining workflow through intracellular labeling in a FACS tube.
constraints: Start from a dissociated hiPSC-NCC suspension and pass it through a 100 um sterile filter into a 5-ml FACS tube.
Include multiple centrifugation steps at 300 x g for 3 min at room temperature.
Use a FACSB wash volume of 1000 ul before surface staining and again after the surface-stain incubation.
Surface staining must be performed with fluorescent-conjugated antibody in FACSB for 60 min at 4 C protected from light.
After the surface-stain wash, perform fixation/permeabilization with 250 ul reagent for 20 min at 4 C in the dark.
Follow fixation/permeabilization with a wash in 1x Perm/Wash buffer before intracellular antibody addition.
Complete intracellular staining with antibody in 1x Perm/Wash buffer for 20 min at 4 C protected from light.
Keep the workflow as one continuous dependency chain using only provided action-pool functions.

available_inputs.name: ncc_suspension
available_inputs.description: Dissociated hiPSC-NCC cell suspension after neutralization, ready for FACS-tube processing.

available_inputs.name: facsb
available_inputs.description: FACS buffer for wash steps and surface-staining diluent.

available_inputs.name: surface_cd271_antibody
available_inputs.description: Fluorescent-conjugated CD271 antibody prepared at working concentration in FACSB.

available_inputs.name: fixperm_solution
available_inputs.description: BD Cytofix/Cytoperm fixation and permeabilization solution.

available_inputs.name: permwash_buffer
available_inputs.description: 1x BD Perm/Wash buffer.

available_inputs.name: intracellular_sox2_antibody
available_inputs.description: Fluorescent-conjugated SOX2 antibody prepared at working concentration in 1x Perm/Wash buffer.

gold_program:
filtered_ncc = filter_sample(sample=ncc_suspension, filter_type="CellTrics sterile filter", pore_size_um=100.0, destination="5-ml FACS tube")
first_wash = add_reagent(sample=filtered_ncc, reagent=facsb, volume_ul=1000.0, mixing="resuspend")
first_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=first_wash, speed_xg=300, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=25.0)
surface_stained = add_reagent(sample=first_spin, reagent=surface_cd271_antibody, volume_ul=50.0, mixing="flick to resuspend")
surface_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=surface_stained, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=60.0, condition="protected from light")
second_wash = add_reagent(sample=surface_incubated, reagent=facsb, volume_ul=1000.0, mixing="resuspend")
second_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=second_wash, speed_xg=300, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=25.0)
fixed_perm = add_reagent(sample=second_spin, reagent=fixperm_solution, volume_ul=250.0, mixing="flick to resuspend")
fixperm_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=fixed_perm, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=20.0, condition="protected from light")
permwash_step = add_reagent(sample=fixperm_incubated, reagent=permwash_buffer, volume_ul=1000.0, mixing="resuspend")
third_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=permwash_step, speed_xg=300, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=25.0)
final_stained_sample = add_reagent(sample=third_spin, reagent=intracellular_sox2_antibody, volume_ul=50.0, mixing="flick to resuspend")
protocol_title: Three‐Dimensional Vessels‐on‐a‐Chip Based on hiPSC‐derived Vascular Endothelial and Smooth Muscle Cells

source_steps.description: Resuspend the cell pellet in cold CryoStor® CS10 cryopreservation medium to get a final concentration of 20 cm 2 /ml. Transfer 500 µl of cell suspension to each cryovial (10 cm 2 per cryovial). hiPSC‐VSMCs and CS10 cryopreservation medium should be kept on ice. Cryovials should be kept on ice during transfer of the cells.
Place all cryovials in a Mr. Frosty™ Freezing Container and leave at −80°C for 24 hr. Then transfer all cryovials into liquid nitrogen for prolonged storage.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Aspirate NC differentiation medium from hiPSC‐NCCs (P0 or P3) in 6‐well plates and add 2 ml DPBS to each well.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Aspirate DPBS and add 0.5 ml TrypLE™ to each well. Incubate 3 min at room temperature.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Add 1 ml FACSB to each well to stop the dissociation, and wash the cells by pipetting up and down once or twice.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Place a 100‐μm CellTrics sterile filter on top of a 5‐ml FACS tube. Transfer an aliquot of hiPSC‐NCC suspension into a 5‐ml FACS tube for analysis. Centrifuge the tube 3 min at 300 × g , room temperature.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Wash the cell suspension with 1 ml FACSB and centrifuge the tube 3 min at 300 × g , room temperature. Aspirate the supernatant and leave around 50 µl FACSB with the cell pellet.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Add fluorescent‐conjugated FACS antibodies to the cell suspension to the desired working concentration in FACSB. Resuspend cells by flicking the bottom of the tube. Incubate in the dark for 60 min at 4°C. CD271 antibody is used for the characterization of NCCs. Details of the antibodies are given in the Materials list.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Turn off the light of the cell culture hood. Wash the cell suspension with 1 ml FACSB and centrifuge the tube 3 min at 300 × g , room temperature. For intracellular staining, fixation and permeabilization steps are required, e.g., using the BD Cytofix/Cytoperm™ Fixation/Permeabilization Kit.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Aspirate the supernatant but leave ∼50 μl in the tube. Add 250 μl Fixation/Permeabilization solution (from BD Cytofix/Cytoperm kit). Resuspend the cells by flicking the bottom of the tube. Incubate in the dark for 20 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Wash the cells once with 1 ml 1× BD Perm/Wash™ buffer (from kit) and centrifuge the tube 3 min at 300 × g , room temperature. Aspirate the supernatant but leave ∼50 μl in the tube.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Add fluorescent‐conjugated FACS antibodies to the cell suspension to the desired working concentration in 1× BD Perm/Wash™ buffer. Resuspend the cells by flicking the bottom of the tube. Incubate in the dark for 20 min at 4°C. Intracellular staining with SOX2 antibody is used to determine whether residual undifferentiated hiPSCs are present. Details of the antibodies are listed in the Materials section.

background: hiPSC-derived neural crest cells are at the characterization stage for flow cytometry after expansion at P0 or P3. The next workup converts plated cells into a filtered single-cell suspension, labels an NCC surface marker, and then applies fixation and permeabilization so the same sample can be stained for an intracellular pluripotency-associated marker.
goal: Generate a final hiPSC-NCC suspension ready for flow-cytometric analysis by completing dissociation, cleanup, surface staining, fixation/permeabilization, intracellular staining, and final resuspension under the required temperature and light conditions.
constraints: Start from hiPSC-NCCs in a 6-well plate and include a 2 ml DPBS wash followed by 0.5 ml TrypLE treatment for 3 min at room temperature before quenching with 1 ml FACSB.
Create a filtered single-cell suspension by passing the sample through a 100-um sterile filter into a 5-ml FACS tube before downstream staining.
All centrifugation events in this segment must use 300 x g for 3 min at room temperature, and at least one FACSB wash must occur before surface antibody incubation.
Surface staining must use fluorescent antibodies in FACSB and proceed for 60 min at 4C protected from light.

After surface staining, perform fixation/permeabilization with 250 ul solution for 20 min at 4C in the dark, then carry out intracellular staining in 1x BD Perm/Wash buffer for 20 min at 4C in the dark.
Finish with the cells resuspended in 1 ml 1x BD Perm/Wash buffer for analysis.

available_inputs.name: hipsc_ncc_plate
available_inputs.description: hiPSC-NCCs at P0 or P3 in a 6-well culture plate

available_inputs.name: dpbs
available_inputs.description: DPBS wash buffer

available_inputs.name: trypLE
available_inputs.description: TrypLE dissociation reagent

available_inputs.name: facsb
available_inputs.description: FACS buffer used for quenching, washing, and surface staining

available_inputs.name: surface_facs_antibodies
available_inputs.description: Fluorescent-conjugated antibodies for surface NCC staining such as CD271

available_inputs.name: fixperm_solution
available_inputs.description: BD Cytofix/Cytoperm fixation and permeabilization solution

available_inputs.name: permwash_buffer
available_inputs.description: 1x BD Perm/Wash buffer

available_inputs.name: intracellular_facs_antibodies
available_inputs.description: Fluorescent-conjugated antibodies for intracellular staining such as SOX2

gold_program:
step1 = add_reagent(sample=hipsc_ncc_plate, reagent=dpbs, volume_ul=2000.0, mixing="gentle wash")
step2 = add_reagent(sample=step1, reagent=trypLE, volume_ul=500.0, mixing="cover cells")
step3 = incubate_sample(sample=step2, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=3.0, condition="static")
step4 = add_reagent(sample=step3, reagent=facsb, volume_ul=1000.0, mixing="pipette up and down")
step5 = filter_sample(sample=step4, filter_type="CellTrics sterile filter", pore_size_um=100.0, destination="5 ml FACS tube")
step6 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step5, speed_xg=300, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=22.0)
step7 = add_reagent(sample=step6, reagent=surface_facs_antibodies, volume_ul=50.0, mixing="resuspend pellet in residual FACSB")
step8 = incubate_sample(sample=step7, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=60.0, condition="protected from light")
step9 = add_reagent(sample=step8, reagent=fixperm_solution, volume_ul=250.0, mixing="resuspend for fixation and permeabilization")
step10 = incubate_sample(sample=step9, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=20.0, condition="protected from light")
step11 = add_reagent(sample=step10, reagent=intracellular_facs_antibodies, volume_ul=50.0, mixing="resuspend in 1x BD Perm/Wash buffer")
step12 = add_reagent(sample=step11, reagent=permwash_buffer, volume_ul=1000.0, mixing="final resuspension for analysis")
protocol_title: Three‐Dimensional Vessels‐on‐a‐Chip Based on hiPSC‐derived Vascular Endothelial and Smooth Muscle Cells

source_steps.description: Place all cryovials in a Mr. Frosty™ Freezing Container and leave at −80°C for 24 hr. Then transfer all cryovials into liquid nitrogen for prolonged storage.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Aspirate NC differentiation medium from hiPSC‐NCCs (P0 or P3) in 6‐well plates and add 2 ml DPBS to each well.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Aspirate DPBS and add 0.5 ml TrypLE™ to each well. Incubate 3 min at room temperature.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Add 1 ml FACSB to each well to stop the dissociation, and wash the cells by pipetting up and down once or twice.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Place a 100‐μm CellTrics sterile filter on top of a 5‐ml FACS tube. Transfer an aliquot of hiPSC‐NCC suspension into a 5‐ml FACS tube for analysis. Centrifuge the tube 3 min at 300 × g , room temperature.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Wash the cell suspension with 1 ml FACSB and centrifuge the tube 3 min at 300 × g , room temperature. Aspirate the supernatant and leave around 50 µl FACSB with the cell pellet.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Add fluorescent‐conjugated FACS antibodies to the cell suspension to the desired working concentration in FACSB. Resuspend cells by flicking the bottom of the tube. Incubate in the dark for 60 min at 4°C. CD271 antibody is used for the characterization of NCCs. Details of the antibodies are given in the Materials list.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Turn off the light of the cell culture hood. Wash the cell suspension with 1 ml FACSB and centrifuge the tube 3 min at 300 × g , room temperature. For intracellular staining, fixation and permeabilization steps are required, e.g., using the BD Cytofix/Cytoperm™ Fixation/Permeabilization Kit.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Aspirate the supernatant but leave ∼50 μl in the tube. Add 250 μl Fixation/Permeabilization solution (from BD Cytofix/Cytoperm kit). Resuspend the cells by flicking the bottom of the tube. Incubate in the dark for 20 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Wash the cells once with 1 ml 1× BD Perm/Wash™ buffer (from kit) and centrifuge the tube 3 min at 300 × g , room temperature. Aspirate the supernatant but leave ∼50 μl in the tube.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Add fluorescent‐conjugated FACS antibodies to the cell suspension to the desired working concentration in 1× BD Perm/Wash™ buffer. Resuspend the cells by flicking the bottom of the tube. Incubate in the dark for 20 min at 4°C. Intracellular staining with SOX2 antibody is used to determine whether residual undifferentiated hiPSCs are present. Details of the antibodies are listed in the Materials section.

source_steps.stage: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry (P0 and P3)
source_steps.description: Wash the cells once with 1 ml 1× BD Perm/Wash™ buffer and centrifuge the tube for 3 min at 300 × g , room temperature. Resuspend the cells in 1 ml 1× BD Perm/Wash™ buffer.

background: Cultured cells have already been blocked and are now at the proximity ligation assay stage used to detect transcription factor association with DNA in situ. This segment covers extended primary antibody incubation, gentle room-temperature wash series, probe binding, ligation, and rolling-circle amplification under humidified conditions, with the final amplification protected from light.
goal: Advance the prepared cell plate through the PLA reaction segment from antibody-bound cells to amplified PLA signal using the required incubation and wash conditions while preserving the continuity of the sample state.
constraints: Start from the blocked cell plate and keep a single dependency chain so each later reaction uses the output of the prior stage rather than restarting from raw material.
Represent the primary antibody step as a 4.0 C incubation for 720.0 min, corresponding to the allowed 12-16 h cold incubation window for antibodies raised in two different species.
Model gentle Wash Buffer A treatment at room temperature as repeated 22.0 C incubations on a slow rocker, with recurring washes that differ mainly by 5.0 min versus 2.0 min duration.
After the first wash series, perform PLA probe binding as a 37.0 C humidified incubation for 60.0 min using a matched PLUS/MINUS probe pair for the two primary-antibody species.

Prepare the ligation and amplification reaction mixes by brief gentle mixing immediately before use, then incubate ligation at 37.0 C for 30.0 min under humidified conditions after a wash, and incubate amplification at 37.0 C for 110.0 min under humidified, light-protected conditions after another wash.
Use only the provided action-pool functions with keyword arguments, assign every call to a variable, include 6-12 top-level calls, and reuse at least two action names multiple times.

available_inputs.name: blocked_plate
available_inputs.description: Cell culture plate after blocking, ready for PLA primary antibody incubation.

available_inputs.name: primary_antibody_mix
available_inputs.description: Primary antibody solution in antibody diluent, containing antibodies from two different species.

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer_a
available_inputs.description: Wash Buffer A for gentle room-temperature washes on a rocker.

available_inputs.name: pla_probe_mix
available_inputs.description: 1X working PLA probe solution containing matched anti-rabbit PLUS and anti-mouse MINUS probes.

available_inputs.name: ligation_reaction_mix
available_inputs.description: Fresh ligation mix made from 1X ligation buffer and DNA ligase added immediately before use.

available_inputs.name: amplification_reaction_mix
available_inputs.description: Fresh amplification mix made from 1X amplification buffer and polymerase added immediately before use.

gold_program:
primary_bound_plate = incubate_sample(sample=blocked_plate, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=720.0, condition='slow rocking with primary antibody mix')
wash1_long = incubate_sample(sample=primary_bound_plate, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=5.0, condition='slow rocking in Wash Buffer A')
wash1_short = incubate_sample(sample=wash1_long, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=2.0, condition='slow rocking in fresh Wash Buffer A')
probe_bound_plate = incubate_sample(sample=wash1_short, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=60.0, condition='humidified with PLA probe mix')
wash2_long = incubate_sample(sample=probe_bound_plate, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=5.0, condition='slow rocking in Wash Buffer A')
ligation_mix_ready = mix_sample(sample=ligation_reaction_mix, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
ligated_plate = incubate_sample(sample=wash2_long, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=30.0, condition='humidified with ligation reaction mix')
wash3_long = incubate_sample(sample=ligated_plate, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=5.0, condition='slow rocking in Wash Buffer A')
amplification_mix_ready = mix_sample(sample=amplification_reaction_mix, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
amplified_plate = incubate_sample(sample=wash3_long, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=110.0, condition='humidified and protected from light with amplification reaction mix')
protocol_title: Detection of the DNA binding of transcription factors in situ at the single-cell resolution in cultured cells by proximity ligation assay

source_steps.description: Primary antibody incubation. Dilute the primary antibodies to optimized concentrations in the antibody dilution buffer provided with the Duolink In Situ PLA probe kits. Use the same concentrations of normal rabbit and mouse IgG as the negative controls. Note: We use a 1:100 dilution of each antibody (i.e., 2 μg/mL mouse anti-dsDNA antibody, 4.29 μg/mL rabbit anti-c-Myc, or 1 μg/mL rabbit anti-HSF1 antibody).

Remove the blocking solution from the wells and add 100 μL of the diluted primary antibodies to each well. Incubate for 12–16 h on a slow laboratory rocker at 4°C. Note: Primary antibodies could also be incubated at 20°C–22°C for 1–4 h or at 37°C for 1–2 h. However, the incubation times need to be optimized for individual antibodies. Troubleshooting problem 5 . CRITICAL: The two primary antibodies being used must be raised in two different species. Troubleshooting problem 3 .

PLA Probe incubation. Discard the primary antibodies from the wells and gently wash the cells with 150 μL of Wash Buffer A on a slow laboratory rocker for 5 min at 20°C–22°C. Repeat the washing step twice for 2 min each.

Dilute both the 5X Duolink I n Situ PLA anti-Rabbit PLUS and 5X Duolink I n Situ PLA anti-Mouse MINUS probes together in the 1X antibody diluent provided in the kit to make a 1X working probe dilution. CRITICAL: The probe mix must include a PLUS PLA probe with another MINUS PLA probe targeting two separate species corresponding to the primary antibodies (e.g., an anti-rabbit PLUS probe should be combined with an anti-mouse MINUS probe). Troubleshooting problem 3 .

Discard the Wash Buffer A from the wells using a micropipette and add 40 μL of the diluted PLA probes to each well. Note: It is important to fully cover the entire sample with the diluted probes. If needed, the total volume in each well could be increased to 50–60 μL.
Incubate the plate for 1 h at 37°C in a humidified incubator. Alternatives: A CO 2 cell culture incubator could be used.

Oligonucleotide ligation reaction. Discard the probes and wash the cells by adding 100 μL of Wash Buffer A on a slow laboratory rocker at 20°C–22°C for 5 min. Repeat the washing step twice for 2 min each.

Dilute the 5X Ligation Buffer provided with the Duolink In Situ PLA probe kits to 1X working solution using purified molecular biology grade water. Add 1 μL of the DNA Ligase per 39 μL of the 1X Ligation buffer to prepare the ligation reaction mix. Note: The 5X ligation buffer should be fully thawed and resolved on ice before dilution. It is important to store the ligase in a −20°C freezer and remove it right before adding it to the reaction mix. Troubleshooting problems 3 and 4 .
Add 40 μL of the ligation reaction mix to each well and incubate the plate at 37°C for 30 min in a humidified incubator. Alternatives: A CO 2 cell culture incubator could be used.

Rolling circle amplification reaction. Discard the ligation reaction mix from the wells and wash the cells by adding 100 μL of Wash Buffer A on a slow laboratory rocker for 5 min at 20°C–22°C. Repeat the washing step twice for 2 min each.

Dilute the 5X Amplification Buffer provided with the Duolink In Situ Detection Reagents kit to 1X working solution using purified molecular biology grade water. Add 0.5 μL of the DNA polymerase to prepare the amplification reaction mix in 39.5 μL of the 1X Amplification buffer. Note: The amplification buffer should be fully thawed and resolved before dilution. The DNA polymerase should be stored in a −20°C freezer and removed just before adding it to the reaction mix. If possible, aliquot the DNA polymerase and avoid repeated freeze/thaw cycles. Troubleshooting problems 3 and 4 .

Add 40 μL of the reaction mix to each well and incubate the plate for 100–120 min at 37°C in a humidified incubator. Alternatives: A CO 2 cell culture incubator could be used. Note: The amplification reaction is light sensitive, so the samples should be covered and protected from light during the amplification reaction. Some weaker interactions may require longer incubation durations. The incubation timings need to be optimized based on the TFs being studied. Troubleshooting problems 3 and 4 .

background: A mouse genome editing workflow has reached the stage of preparing a Cas9 ribonucleoprotein mixture for embryo delivery. The sgRNA and single-stranded repair oligonucleotide have already been generated and are being handled cold before assembly in a nuclease-free microcentrifuge tube. The immediate task is to keep reagents chilled, briefly collect liquid in the tubes, allow RNP formation at 37°C, and return the assembled mix to ice for downstream use.
goal: Condition a 2× Cas9 RNP preparation from thawed sgRNA and repair template so the assembled mixture is briefly collected, incubated for complex formation, and chilled again for immediate downstream handling.
constraints: Start with both sgRNA and oligonucleotide repair template on ice, and keep the final assembled mixture on ice as the endpoint.
Use a nuclease- and pyrogen-free 0.5-ml microcentrifuge tube as the working vessel for the combined RNP mixture corresponding to a 2× preparation in 1× Cas9 RNP buffer.

Perform a brief pulse centrifugation on each thawed nucleic acid reagent before assembly-related incubation, and also pulse centrifuge the assembled mixture before and after the 37°C incubation to collect droplets.
Include a 37°C incubation for 10 min to support RNP assembly, without introducing unsupported transfer or mixing primitives.
Preserve a single dependency chain in which the final chilled output comes from the same assembled mixture that was incubated, then recollected after incubation.
Use only provided action-pool functions, assign every call to a variable, and keep the program between 6 and 12 top-level steps.
Reuse at least two action names multiple times, with one repeated action family differing mainly by small numeric arguments or sample identity.

available_inputs.name: sgRNA_tube
available_inputs.description: Tube containing thawed sgRNA kept on ice.

available_inputs.name: repair_template_tube
available_inputs.description: Tube containing thawed oligonucleotide repair template kept on ice.

available_inputs.name: rnp_mix_tube
available_inputs.description: Nuclease- and pyrogen-free 0.5-ml microcentrifuge tube containing the combined 2× Cas9 RNP assembly mixture.

available_inputs.name: ice_rack
available_inputs.description: Ice-based cooling location used to keep reagents cold before and after incubation.

gold_program:
sgRNA_cold = cool_sample(sample=sgRNA_tube, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=5.0, device=ice_rack)
repair_template_cold = cool_sample(sample=repair_template_tube, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=5.0, device=ice_rack)
sgRNA_collected = centrifuge_sample(sample=sgRNA_cold, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=4.0)
repair_template_collected = centrifuge_sample(sample=repair_template_cold, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=4.0)
rnp_mix_collected_preincubation = centrifuge_sample(sample=rnp_mix_tube, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=4.0)
rnp_mix_assembled = incubate_sample(sample=rnp_mix_collected_preincubation, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=10.0, condition="static")
rnp_mix_collected_postincubation = centrifuge_sample(sample=rnp_mix_assembled, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=4.0)
rnp_mix_on_ice = cool_sample(sample=rnp_mix_collected_postincubation, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=5.0, device=ice_rack)
protocol_title: Engineering Base Changes and Epitope‐Tagged Alleles in Mice Using Cas9 RNA‐Guided Nuclease

source_steps.description: Combine the following in a sterile PETG bottle and mix well. 2 ml of 1 M Tris, pH 7.6 (10 mM final)
Set up a disposable 0.22‐µm SFCA vacuum filter unit in the biosafety cabinet. Rinse filter with 100 ml microinjection buffer by vacuum filtration.
Transfer rinsed filter to a new collection bottle, discarding the first bottle with rinse buffer.
Filter the remaining 100 ml buffer through the rinsed filter. Store in the sterile collection bottle for the next step.
Aspirate 20 ml buffer into a γ‐radiation‐sterilized 20‐ml Luer‐lock syringe. Attach a 0.02‐µm Whatman Anotop Plus filter to the filled syringe.
Filter and discard 5 ml buffer to rinse the filter, then filter the remaining buffer into two aliquots in sterile PETG serum vials. Close with a sterile closure and store at room temperature.

To confirm that buffer is RNase‐free, add 2 µg RNA to 18 µl filtered microinjection buffer and incubate at 37°C for ≥30 min. Assess integrity of the RNA by agarose gel electrophoresis or bioanalysis compared to freshly prepared RNA. No RNA degradation should be observed.

Confirm that buffer is suitable for microinjection by injecting 40 to 60 embryos (0.5 dpc, see Basic Protocol 4 ) with 0.02‐µm filtered buffer. Transfer to pseudopregnant recipients in parallel with 40 to 60 embryos injected with previously QC'd buffer. Dissect mid‐gestation and score number of implantation sites and number of live embryos. Implantation and live embryo rates for test buffer should be within 10% of rates for previously QC'd buffer.
Thaw sgRNA and oligonucleotide repair template on ice. Mix well and pulse centrifuge to collect contents.

To prepare 2× RNPs in 1× Cas9 RNP buffer, combine the following in a 0.5‐ml nuclease‐ and pyrogen‐free microcentrifuge tube: Nuclease‐free water to a total of 5 or 11 µl (including oligonucleotide repair template)
Mix well by flicking the tube and pulse centrifuge to collect any droplets.
Incubate at 37°C for 10 min. Pulse centrifuge to collect droplets and transfer to ice.

background: A recombinant plant viral vector has already been assembled by homology-based cloning. The immediate task is to carry that assembly through bacterial recovery and clone preparation steps in E. coli, ending with a harvested pellet that can be held for later plasmid purification.
goal: Advance the assembled construct through incubation, competent-cell handling, recovery, selective outgrowth, clone expansion, and harvest so that a candidate recombinant E. coli culture is prepared as a frozen pellet for downstream plasmid isolation.
constraints: Incubate the assembly reaction at 50 C for 60 minutes, then chill it on ice before it is used with competent cells.
Keep the electrocompetent DH5alpha aliquot cold, use only a small amount of assembly reaction with it, and do not add an extra waiting period after DNA addition before immediate downstream handling.
After recovery in 1 mL SOC, incubate at 37 C for 60 minutes with vigorous shaking in the 200 to 250 rpm range.
Concentrate recovered bacteria by spinning at 15000 x g for 1 minute, then resuspend in 100 uL SOC before selective plating or equivalent plate-ready handling.
Use 37 C overnight growth for both plate outgrowth and liquid clone expansion, allowing up to 24 hours because viral binary vectors can grow slowly.
For culture harvest, use a 15000 x g spin for 2 minutes followed by a second brief 0.5 minute spin, and allow the final pellet to be stored at -80 C for several days.

available_inputs.name: assembly_mix
available_inputs.description: Completed homology-based DNA assembly reaction.

available_inputs.name: dh5alpha_cells
available_inputs.description: Electrocompetent E. coli DH5alpha cells for transformation.

available_inputs.name: soc_recovery_tube
available_inputs.description: Transformation recovery tube containing 1 mL SOC after electroporation.

available_inputs.name: plate_ready_cells
available_inputs.description: Recovered transformed cells prepared for selective plating.

available_inputs.name: white_colony_culture
available_inputs.description: Liquid LB plus kanamycin culture inoculated from a white colony.

gold_program:
heated_assembly = heat_sample(sample=assembly_mix, temperature_c=50.0, duration_min=60.0, device='heating block')
chilled_assembly = cool_sample(sample=heated_assembly, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=2.0, device='ice bath')
chilled_cells = cool_sample(sample=dh5alpha_cells, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=10.0, device='ice bath')
prepared_transformants = mix_sample(sample=chilled_cells, method='pipetting', duration_s=5.0, speed='gentle')
recovered_cells = incubate_sample(sample=soc_recovery_tube, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=60.0, condition='shaking 225 rpm')
recovery_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=recovered_cells, speed_xg=15000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
resuspended_recovery = mix_sample(sample=recovery_pellet, method='pipetting', duration_s=8.0, speed='gentle')
plate_outgrowth = incubate_sample(sample=plate_ready_cells, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=1440.0, condition='static')
expanded_clone = incubate_sample(sample=white_colony_culture, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=1440.0, condition='shaking 225 rpm')
first_harvest = centrifuge_sample(sample=expanded_clone, speed_xg=15000, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=25.0)
second_harvest = centrifuge_sample(sample=first_harvest, speed_xg=15000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=25.0)
stored_pellet = store_sample(sample=second_harvest, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_h=72.0, container='microcentrifuge tube')
protocol_title: Heritable CRISPR-Cas9 editing of plant genomes using RNA virus vectors

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Resolve by agarose gel electrophoresis 1 μL of the synthetic DNA fragments (step 3) and the gel-purified linearized vector (step 4) to check integrity, and estimate DNA amount and quality using a spectrophotometer (e.g., NanoDrop™, Thermo Fisher Scientific).

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: In vitro homology-based assembly: Mix the gel-purified linearized vector (step 4) and a synthetic DNA fragment (step 3) in a molar ratio of 1:2 and a total volume of ∼2 μL. Note: Use a total DNA amount of 0.05–2 pmol. Note: The NEBiocalculator® Tool ( https://nebiocalculator.neb.com/ ) can be used for molarity calculations.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Add 1 volume (∼2 μL) of NEBuilder® HiFi DNA assembly master mix (New England Biolabs). Alternatives: Gibson assembly 31 can also be used for homology-based cloning; the required enzymatic mix can be in-house prepared as detailed, 32 or purchased from commercial providers (e.g., Gibson Assembly® master mix, New England Biolabs, cat. number E2611S). If using Gibson assembly, no protocol changes are required.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Incubate the mix at 50°C (1 h). Once the reaction ends, place tubes on ice.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Transformation of E. coli cells: Thaw a 40-μL aliquot of electrocompetent E. coli DH5α cells on ice. Alternatives: Other E. coli strains suitable for high-efficient transformation may be used.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Gently mix by pipetting a cell aliquot with 1 μL of the assembly reaction from step 6.b, and transfer the mixture to a pre-chilled electroporation cuvette.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Electroporate cells (1,500 V), mix them with 1 mL of room-temperature SOC (see Materials and equipment) and transfer to a 1.5-mL tube. Note: Once DNA is added to cells electroporation can be carried out immediately, without any incubation. Note: If your sample arcs when electroporating, dialyze or purify the assembly reaction to remove salt excess. Alternatives: Heat shock of chemically-competent E. coli cells for high-efficiency transformation 23 may be used.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Incubate the cells at 37°C, shaking vigorously at 200–250 rpm (1 h). Note: Warm selection plates at 37°C while cells are recovering.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Pellet bacteria by centrifuging at 15,000 × g (1 min), resuspend bacteria in 100 μL of SOC and plate the suspension onto LB agar plates supplemented with 50 mg/L kanamycin and 40 μL X-Gal (40 mg/mL). Incubate at 37°C (overnight). Note: Plate the X-Gal solution on agar plates before use for white-blue screen of recombinant clones. Note: Bacterial cells harboring binary vectors with viral genomic sequences generally display low growth rates; plate incubation up to 24 h may be necessary. Pause point: After colony appearance, plates can be stored 1–3 days at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Confirm the presence of recombinant binary vectors in E. coli transformants: Pick a white colony obtained by X-Gal selection and inoculate 5–10 mL liquid LB with 50 mg/L kanamycin in 50 mL tubes. Incubate the tubes at 37°C, shaking vigorously at 200–250 rpm (overnight). Note: Bacterial cells harboring binary vectors with full-length virus clones generally display low growth rates; extend culturing time up to 24 h if necessary. Note: It is recommended to purify plasmid DNA from 2–4 colonies per construct.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Harvest bacteria from the culture by centrifugation at 15,000 × g (2 min); discard the medium, centrifuge again (30 s) and remove medium residues by pipetting. Pause point: Harvested bacterial pellets can be frozen and stored at −80°C several days before proceeding with plasmid DNA purification.

source_steps.stage: Homology-based assembly of recombinant viral vectors
source_steps.description: Purify DNA plasmids from bacterial pellets using NucleoSpin® plasmid kit (Macherey Nagel) as per manufacturer’s instructions, except that: Use double volumes of resuspension, lysis and neutralization solutions supplied within the kit to improve clearing of bacterial lysates and increase the amount of purified plasmid. Note: After addition of the lysis solution incubate until the cell suspension clears (5 min).

background: This task focuses on the bead-based cleanup and elution portion of BdLT-Seq library preparation after barcoded cDNA has been captured on magnetic beads. The sample is undergoing consecutive room-temperature washes on-bead, followed by alkaline elution of the barcode-containing cDNA in a thermomixer and magnetic recovery of the eluate for downstream neutralization and purification setup.
goal: Process the bead-bound BdLT-Seq cDNA through the remaining wash, elution, and magnetic recovery stage so that the barcode-containing cDNA is released from beads and collected as a neutralized eluate ready for subsequent purification.
constraints: Start from the bead-bound cDNA complex and keep the material bead-associated until the elution stage; use only action-pool functions and maintain a chained dependency structure.

Represent two room-temperature wash cycles using Wash Buffer 2 and Wash Buffer 3, each with 150 uL addition, gentle pipette-style mixing, 2 min incubation at about 20 degrees C, and magnetic separation that retains the bead phase.
Reuse at least two action families more than once, with one repeated family differing mainly by the wash buffer identity while keeping the same key numeric conditions.
Include alkaline elution of the bead-bound cDNA using 100 uL denaturation buffer, then process for 15 min at about 20 degrees C in a thermal mixer set to 300 rpm.
After elution, perform magnetic separation that retains the supernatant rather than beads, because the barcode-containing cDNA must be collected off the magnetic rack before stopping the reaction.
Neutralize only after eluate recovery, reflecting addition of both 10 uL of 1 M Tris pH 7.5 and 10 uL of 1 M HCl.
Use 6 to 12 top-level function calls, keyword arguments only, and assign every function call to a variable.

available_inputs.name: bead_cdna_complex
available_inputs.description: Magnetic bead-bound cDNA complex after the prior wash and separation stage.

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer_2
available_inputs.description: Room-temperature Wash Buffer 2 for the next bead wash.

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer_3
available_inputs.description: Room-temperature Wash Buffer 3 for the following bead wash.

available_inputs.name: denaturation_buffer
available_inputs.description: Alkaline denaturation buffer containing 100 mM NaOH and 0.1 mM EDTA.

available_inputs.name: tris_hcl_stop_mix
available_inputs.description: Stopping reagents corresponding to 10 uL 1 M Tris pH 7.5 plus 10 uL 1 M HCl.

gold_program:
wash2_added = mix_sample(sample=bead_cdna_complex, method="pipetting with 150 uL Wash Buffer 2", duration_s=15.0, speed="gentle")
wash2_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=wash2_added, temperature_c=20.0, duration_min=2.0, condition="static")
wash2_separated = magnetic_separate(sample=wash2_incubated, rack="magnetic rack", duration_min=2.0, collect_phase="beads")
wash3_added = mix_sample(sample=wash2_separated, method="pipetting with 150 uL Wash Buffer 3", duration_s=15.0, speed="gentle")
wash3_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=wash3_added, temperature_c=20.0, duration_min=2.0, condition="static")
wash3_separated = magnetic_separate(sample=wash3_incubated, rack="magnetic rack", duration_min=2.0, collect_phase="beads")
elution_mixed = mix_sample(sample=wash3_separated, method="pipetting with 100 uL denaturation buffer", duration_s=15.0, speed="gentle")
eluted_cdna = set_thermal_mixer(sample=elution_mixed, temperature_c=20.0, speed_rpm=300, duration_min=15.0)
eluate_supernatant = magnetic_separate(sample=eluted_cdna, rack="magnetic rack", duration_min=2.0, collect_phase="supernatant")
neutralized_eluate = mix_sample(sample=eluate_supernatant, method="pipetting with 10 uL 1 M Tris pH 7.5 and 10 uL 1 M HCl", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
protocol_title: Protocol to study the inheritance and propagation of non-genetically encoded states using barcode decay lineage tracing

background: Murine trophoblast giant cells have already been differentiated and are now at the RNA isolation and early reverse-transcription preparation stage. The task focuses on processing a TRIzol lysate through phase separation, RNA precipitation, ethanol washing, pellet recovery, RNA quality handling, and preparation of the RNA mix for downstream cDNA synthesis.
goal: Process the TGC lysate to obtain purified RNA with QC-related handling and then prepare the RNA-containing reverse transcription mix for the next enzymatic step.
constraints: Start from an RNase-free 1.5 mL tube containing 1 mL TRIzol lysate from differentiated TGCs and include a 5 min room-temperature dissociation before extraction handling.

After chloroform addition, use gentle inversion-style mixing, wait 3 min, then perform cold phase separation at 12000 x g for 15 min at 4 C; the aqueous RNA phase must be the carried-forward material.
Model RNA precipitation with isopropanol followed by a 10 min incubation at 4 C and a second spin at 12000 x g for 10 min at 4 C.
Include a 75% ethanol wash with centrifugation at 7500 x g for 5 min at 4 C, then remove residual ethanol and dry the pellet before resuspension in nuclease-free water.
After resuspension, heat the RNA at 65 C for 5 min and quick-chill on ice for 2 min before spectrophotometric QC.
Record both purity-related and quantity-related RNA assessment based on spectrophotometric handling, referencing 260/280 and concentration.
Also process the prepared reverse-transcription RNA mix through the same 65 C for 5 min and ice 2 min treatment, but do not execute the enzyme-addition step.

available_inputs.name: tgc_trizol_lysate
available_inputs.description: RNase-free 1.5 mL tube containing 1 mL TGC lysate in TRIzol

available_inputs.name: chloroform_added_lysate
available_inputs.description: The same lysate after chloroform has been added for phase separation

available_inputs.name: aqueous_phase_rna
available_inputs.description: Fresh RNase-free tube containing the transferred aqueous phase after phase separation

available_inputs.name: isopropanol_rna_mix
available_inputs.description: Aqueous RNA phase after isopropanol addition for precipitation

available_inputs.name: ethanol_washed_rna_pellet
available_inputs.description: RNA pellet after 75% ethanol wash and supernatant removal

available_inputs.name: rt_rna_mix
available_inputs.description: Individual reverse transcription RNA mix prepared from purified RNA and oligo dT primer components

gold_program:
dissociated_lysate = incubate_sample(sample=tgc_trizol_lysate, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=5.0, condition='static')
mixed_chloroform_lysate = mix_sample(sample=chloroform_added_lysate, method='inversion', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
phase_ready_lysate = incubate_sample(sample=mixed_chloroform_lysate, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=3.0, condition='static')
phase_separated_tube = centrifuge_sample(sample=phase_ready_lysate, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=15.0, temperature_c=4.0)
precipitated_rna = incubate_sample(sample=isopropanol_rna_mix, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=10.0, condition='static')
rna_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=precipitated_rna, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
washed_rna_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=ethanol_washed_rna_pellet, speed_xg=7500, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
heated_rna = heat_sample(sample=washed_rna_pellet, temperature_c=65.0, duration_min=5.0, device='heat block')
chilled_rna = cool_sample(sample=heated_rna, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=2.0, device='ice bath')
uv_readout = measure_signal(sample=chilled_rna, assay='spectrophotometry', wavelength_nm=260, replicate_n=1)
qc_entry = record_measurement(sample=uv_readout, metric='RNA_quality_and_concentration', unit='A260/280_and_concentration', note='Assess purity by 260/280 ratio and quantify RNA before cDNA synthesis; use 5 ug RNA for reverse transcription')
prepared_rt_rna_mix = heat_sample(sample=rt_rna_mix, temperature_c=65.0, duration_min=5.0, device='heat block')

source_steps.stage: Functional characterization of endoreduplicated trophoblast giant cells (TGCs)
source_steps.description: Add 1 mL 0.05% Trypsin-EDTA per T25 flask and incubate at 37°C in an incubator. Neutralize the action of trypsin with double the volume of TS complete media and transfer the cell suspension to a 15 mL centrifuge tube.

source_steps.stage: Functional characterization of endoreduplicated trophoblast giant cells (TGCs)
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 200 × g for 5 min and aspirate the trypsin containing media. Wash the cells with 2 mL sterile DPBS at 200 × g for 5 min. Discard the supernatant and dissolve the pellet in 1 mL TRIzol. Incubate at room temperature for 5 min.

source_steps.stage: Functional characterization of endoreduplicated trophoblast giant cells (TGCs)
source_steps.description: For isolating RNA from TGCs (differentiated till day 6), aspirate the media and wash the cell monolayer with sterile DPBS.

source_steps.stage: Functional characterization of endoreduplicated trophoblast giant cells (TGCs)
source_steps.description: Add 1 mL TRIzol and incubate at room temperature for 5 min. Directly scrape off the cell monolayer with a cell scraper.

source_steps.stage: Functional characterization of endoreduplicated trophoblast giant cells (TGCs)
source_steps.description: Transfer 1 mL lysed suspension into a RNase free (DEPC treated) 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube and incubate for 5 min to allow complete dissociation of the nucleoprotein complexes.

source_steps.stage: Functional characterization of endoreduplicated trophoblast giant cells (TGCs)
source_steps.description: Add 200 μL chloroform to the tube containing the lysate and mix very gently by inverting the tube in “figure-of-eight” pattern and then incubate it for 3 min.

source_steps.stage: Functional characterization of endoreduplicated trophoblast giant cells (TGCs)
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 15 min at 4°C. Collect the aqueous phase containing the RNA in a fresh RNase free tube and add 500 μL isopropanol to the tube. Incubate for 10 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Functional characterization of endoreduplicated trophoblast giant cells (TGCs)
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 10 min at 4°C. Discard the supernatant and wash the RNA pellet with 1 mL of 75% ethanol at 7,500 × g for 5 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Functional characterization of endoreduplicated trophoblast giant cells (TGCs)
source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant completely and depending on the pellet size, dissolve it in nuclease free water (volume of water depends on the size of the pellet) by gentle pipetting.

source_steps.stage: Functional characterization of endoreduplicated trophoblast giant cells (TGCs)
source_steps.description: Heat the RNA sample at 65°C for 5 min. Quick chill on ice for 2 min and measure the quality and total amount of RNA spectrophotometrically by determining the 260/280 ratio and concentration respectively. Use 5 μg RNA to reverse transcribe first-strand cDNA using oligo dT primers.

source_steps.stage: Functional characterization of endoreduplicated trophoblast giant cells (TGCs)
source_steps.description: Prepare individual reverse transcription RNA mix as described in materials and equipment . Heat the mix to 65°C for 5 min followed by a quick chilling in ice for 2 min.

source_steps.stage: Functional characterization of endoreduplicated trophoblast giant cells (TGCs)
source_steps.description: Meanwhile prepare the reverse transcription enzyme mix as described in materials and equipment . Add 7 μL of the mix to each tube and mix gently.

background: Murine trophoblast giant cells are at the RNA extraction stage after in vitro differentiation. The cells have already been disrupted in TRIzol or are ready for immediate processing, and the remaining work is to carry the lysate through phase separation, precipitation, and ethanol washing to obtain RNA for later QC and reverse transcription.
goal: Process the trophoblast giant cell TRIzol lysate through the core RNA isolation segment so that a washed RNA pellet is produced and documented for downstream resuspension.
constraints: Include a 5 min room-temperature dissociation period for the TRIzol lysate before organic extraction, then a 3 min room-temperature wait after chloroform is added.
Use gentle inversion-style mixing for chloroform incorporation rather than vortexing, and keep later operations dependent on the immediately preceding output.
Perform the first cold spin at 12000 x g for 15 min at 4 C, representing separation of the aqueous RNA phase into a fresh RNase-free tube before precipitation.
Model isopropanol precipitation with a 10 min incubation at 4 C followed by a second centrifugation at 12000 x g for 10 min at 4 C.
Include an ethanol wash of the RNA pellet using 75% ethanol and a final centrifugation at 7500 x g for 5 min at 4 C.
Use 6-12 top-level action calls total and repeat at least two action names, with one repeated family differing mainly by time or force.

available_inputs.description: Trophoblast giant cell lysate in 1 mL TRIzol in an RNase-free 1.5 mL tube

available_inputs.name: chloroform
available_inputs.description: Chloroform reagent for phase separation

available_inputs.name: isopropanol
available_inputs.description: Isopropanol for RNA precipitation

available_inputs.name: ethanol_75
available_inputs.description: 75% ethanol for washing the RNA pellet

available_inputs.description: Fresh RNase-free 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube for the aqueous phase

gold_program:
step1 = incubate_sample(sample=tgc_trizol_lysate, temperature_c=25, duration_min=5, condition='static')
step2 = mix_sample(sample=step1, method='inversion', duration_s=10, speed='gentle figure-of-eight')
step3 = incubate_sample(sample=step2, temperature_c=25, duration_min=3, condition='static')
step4 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step3, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=15, temperature_c=4)
step5 = record_measurement(sample=step4, metric='aqueous_phase_transfer', unit='qualitative', note='transfer upper aqueous RNA phase to fresh RNase-free 1.5 mL tube and add 500 uL isopropanol')
step6 = incubate_sample(sample=step5, temperature_c=4, duration_min=10, condition='static')
step7 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step6, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
step8 = record_measurement(sample=step7, metric='ethanol_wash_added', unit='qualitative', note='discard supernatant and wash RNA pellet with 1 mL 75% ethanol')
step9 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step8, speed_xg=7500, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
step10 = record_measurement(sample=step9, metric='rna_pellet_status', unit='qualitative', note='washed RNA pellet obtained and ready for supernatant removal and resuspension')

source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant completely and depending on the pellet size, dissolve it in nuclease free water (volume of water depends on the size of the pellet) by gentle pipetting.

source_steps.description: Heat the RNA sample at 65°C for 5 min. background: Primary neuronal lysates are in the protein digestion stage of a phosphoproteomics workflow. The sample has already been prepared in lysis conditions and now needs reduction, alkylation, acidification, capture on a spin filter, on-filter protease digestion, and peptide recovery before downstream phosphopeptide enrichment.
goal: Process the neuronal protein lysate through the digestion and spin-filter cleanup segment so that digested peptides are recovered from the filter after overnight proteolysis.
constraints: Include a reduction phase corresponding to DTT treatment with a 20 mM final concentration and a heated mixing period of 5 min at 56 C and 400 rpm, followed by an additional 5 min mixing period at room temperature at 400 rpm.
Include an alkylation phase corresponding to IAA treatment with a 40 mM final concentration for 30 min under light-protected conditions with shaking at 400 rpm.

Represent the acidification and binding-buffer preparation stage before filter capture; the acid target is 1.2% using 12% phosphoric acid at a 1:10 addition, and the sample is combined with binding buffer at an overall 7:1 binding-buffer-to-sample proportion.

Capture on the spin column must be represented by centrifugation at 4000 x g for 20 s-equivalent timing, and the wash stage must reflect four washes with 400 uL binding buffer using the same spin conditions.

After capture and washing, move to on-filter digestion conditions using digestion buffer with protease at a 1:50 enzyme-to-protein ratio and ensure the filter is fully soaked with no trapped air before the long incubation.
The digestion incubation must be approximately 16 h at room temperature in a humidified or wet-chamber condition with tubes closed.
Peptide elution must be represented as an 80 uL digestion-buffer addition without protease followed by centrifugation at 4000 x g for 60 s-equivalent timing.
Use only the provided action functions and keep the solution at the device-operation level rather than adding manual liquid handling details beyond what is necessary to contextualize the actions.

available_inputs.description: Primary cortical neuron protein lysate entering the digestion workflow.

available_inputs.description: Protein-binding spin filter unit used for capture, washing, and on-filter digestion.

available_inputs.name: reaction_tube
available_inputs.description: Fresh collection tube used after transfer of the spin column.

available_inputs.name: digestion_buffer
available_inputs.description: Digestion buffer used for protease digestion and later peptide elution.

available_inputs.description: Shaking heater used for reduction, room-temperature mixing, and alkylation.

gold_program:
reduced_lysate = set_thermal_mixer(sample=neuronal_lysate, temperature_c=56.0, speed_rpm=400, duration_min=5.0)
cooled_reduced_lysate = set_thermal_mixer(sample=reduced_lysate, temperature_c=22.0, speed_rpm=400, duration_min=5.0)
alkylated_lysate = incubate_sample(sample=cooled_reduced_lysate, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=30.0, condition='shaking 400 rpm protected from light after 40 mM IAA treatment')
acidified_bound_sample = incubate_sample(sample=alkylated_lysate, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=1.0, condition='mixed with 12% phosphoric acid to 1.2% final and binding buffer to 7:1 buffer-to-sample ratio for spin-column loading')
filter_captured_sample = centrifuge_sample(sample=acidified_bound_sample, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=0.33, temperature_c=22.0)
washed_filter_sample = centrifuge_sample(sample=filter_captured_sample, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=1.32, temperature_c=22.0)
transferred_filter_unit = store_sample(sample=washed_filter_sample, temperature_c=22.0, duration_h=0.01, container=reaction_tube)
primed_filter_digest = centrifuge_sample(sample=transferred_filter_unit, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=0.03, temperature_c=22.0)
overnight_digest = incubate_sample(sample=primed_filter_digest, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=960.0, condition='humidified wet chamber with closed caps; digestion buffer with protease at 1:50 enzyme-to-protein ratio and fully soaked filter')
eluted_peptides = centrifuge_sample(sample=overnight_digest, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
protocol_title: Quantifying phosphorylation dynamics in primary neuronal cultures using LC-MS/MS

source_steps.description: Add DTT dissolved in lysis buffer to reduce protein disulfide bonds. Use a final concentration of 20 mM. Heat and incubate for 5 min at 56°C shaking at 400 rpm.
Incubate for additional 5 min at room temperature shaking at 400 rpm.
Alkylate free sulfhydryl groups on cysteine residues with IAA dissolved in lysis buffer. Use a final concentration of 40 mM. Incubate 30 min in the dark shaking with 400 rpm.
Add 12% aqueous phosphoric acid at 1:10 for a final concentration of 1.2% acid to the lysate and mix it well.
Add six parts of binding buffer to one part of the acidified sample (7:1 ratio) and carefully mix it by pipetting up and down a few times.
Add the mix onto the spin column in 400 μL steps. Centrifuge at 4,000 × g for 20 s or until all solvent has passed through the filter. Discard the flow-through when necessary.
Wash the captured protein by adding 400 μL protein binding buffer and centrifuge at 4,000 × g for 20 s. Repeat this step for a total of four washes. Discard the flow-through when necessary.
Transfer the mini spin column into a new reaction tube.
Add 125 μL digestion buffer containing the protease in 1:50 enzyme-to-protein ratio to each sample.

Spin down the liquid very briefly (1–2 s) to ensure that the whole filter is soaked in digestion buffer and return any solution that passes to the top of the filter again. Ensure that there are no air bubbles on top of the filter.
Incubate the samples overnight (approx. 16 h) in a wet chamber at room temperature. Close the caps of all reaction tubes.

source_steps.stage: Peptide collection and purification prior phosphopeptide enrichment
source_steps.description: Add 80 μL of digestion buffer (without protease) to the filter unit and centrifuge at 4,000 × g for 60 s to elute generated peptides.

background: Primary neuronal lysates are in the protein digestion stage of an LC-MS/MS phosphoproteomics workflow. Reduction has already progressed, and the next operations complete cysteine alkylation, acidify and bind proteins to a filter, wash the retained material, digest on-filter overnight, and then recover peptides with sequential elutions before phosphopeptide enrichment.
goal: Process the reduced lysate through the remainder of the on-filter digestion segment and collect the peptide eluate suitable for the next cleanup and enrichment stage.
constraints: Include an initial room-temperature mixing period of 5 min at 400 rpm before the alkylation hold.
Represent alkylation as a 30 min room-temperature incubation with protection from light while maintaining agitation at 400 rpm.
Model the binding and wash phase with spin-column centrifugation at 4000 x g, using a short spin for loading and washes and a longer spin for peptide elution.
Capture that the retained protein is washed four times with the same wash condition before digestion begins.
Before overnight digestion, include a very brief spin consistent with 1–2 s to wet the filter and remove bubbles; use a normalized minute value.
Digest on-filter at room temperature for approximately 16 h under humidified conditions with the reaction vessel closed.
Peptide recovery must include one digestion-buffer elution followed by two acidic elutions using 0.2% formic acid under the same centrifugation condition.
Use later outputs as inputs to subsequent operations rather than restarting from the raw lysate.

available_inputs.description: Protein lysate that has already undergone the earlier reduction stage and is ready for the remaining digestion workflow.

available_inputs.name: spin_column
available_inputs.description: Mini spin column assembly used for filter-based protein capture and digestion.

available_inputs.name: reaction_tube
available_inputs.description: Fresh collection tube used after transfer of the spin column for digestion and peptide recovery.

available_inputs.name: digestion_buffer_sample
available_inputs.description: Filter unit after addition of digestion buffer with protease at a 1:50 enzyme-to-protein ratio.

background: During BdLT-Seq library preparation, captured barcode-bearing cDNA remains associated with magnetic beads and must be cleaned with successive room-temperature washes before alkaline release. After release, the barcode-containing cDNA is separated from the beads and neutralized so it can enter the next purification stage.
goal: Clean the bead-bound cDNA with the required wash series, elute the BdLT-Seq cDNA from the beads under the specified denaturing conditions, and neutralize the recovered eluate for downstream purification.
constraints: Maintain a single dependency chain on the same magnetic bead sample through three consecutive washes prior to elution.
For each wash, use gentle pipette-style mixing and a 2 min incubation at about 20 C, then magnetically retain the bead phase.
The three wash exchanges must correspond to Wash Buffer 1, Wash Buffer 2, and Wash Buffer 3 using 150 uL-equivalent additions on the same sample state.
Elute with denaturation buffer representing 100 mM NaOH and 0.1 mM EDTA using a thermal mixer condition of 20 C, 300 rpm, and 15 min.
After elution, magnetic separation must retain the supernatant rather than the beads.
Neutralize only after supernatant recovery, reflecting addition of both 10 uL 1 M Tris pH 7.5 and 10 uL 1 M HCl.

available_inputs.name: bead_cDNA
available_inputs.description: Magnetic bead-bound cDNA complex containing captured BdLT-Seq barcode molecules.

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer_1
available_inputs.description: First room-temperature wash buffer for bead cleanup.

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer_2
available_inputs.description: Second room-temperature wash buffer for bead cleanup.

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer_3
available_inputs.description: Third room-temperature wash buffer for bead cleanup.

available_inputs.name: denaturation_buffer
available_inputs.description: Alkaline elution buffer equivalent to 100 mM NaOH and 0.1 mM EDTA.

available_inputs.name: tris_hcl_quench
available_inputs.description: Neutralization reagents corresponding to 10 uL 1 M Tris pH 7.5 plus 10 uL 1 M HCl.

gold_program:
wash1_mixed = mix_sample(sample=bead_cDNA, method="pipetting with 150 uL wash_buffer_1", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
wash1_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=wash1_mixed, temperature_c=20.0, duration_min=2.0, condition="static")
wash1_beads = magnetic_separate(sample=wash1_incubated, rack="magnetic rack", duration_min=2.0, collect_phase="beads")
wash2_mixed = mix_sample(sample=wash1_beads, method="pipetting with 150 uL wash_buffer_2", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
wash2_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=wash2_mixed, temperature_c=20.0, duration_min=2.0, condition="static")
wash2_beads = magnetic_separate(sample=wash2_incubated, rack="magnetic rack", duration_min=2.0, collect_phase="beads")
wash3_mixed = mix_sample(sample=wash2_beads, method="pipetting with 150 uL wash_buffer_3", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
wash3_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=wash3_mixed, temperature_c=20.0, duration_min=2.0, condition="static")
wash3_beads = magnetic_separate(sample=wash3_incubated, rack="magnetic rack", duration_min=2.0, collect_phase="beads")
elution_processed = set_thermal_mixer(sample=wash3_beads, temperature_c=20.0, speed_rpm=300, duration_min=15.0)
eluted_supernatant = magnetic_separate(sample=elution_processed, rack="magnetic rack", duration_min=2.0, collect_phase="supernatant")
neutralized_cDNA = mix_sample(sample=eluted_supernatant, method="pipetting with tris_hcl_quench", duration_s=5.0, speed="gentle")
protocol_title: Protocol to study the inheritance and propagation of non-genetically encoded states using barcode decay lineage tracing

source_steps.description: Incubate for 2 min at room temperature (∼20°C), then place the tube on a magnet and once separation is complete, discard the supernatant.

source_steps.description: Add 150 μL of room temperature (∼20°C) Wash Buffer 2 to the beads and gently mix the solution by pipetting it up and down 10 times.

source_steps.stage: BdLT-seq: 10× Chromium processing, capture of BdLT-Seq barcoded mRNA and preparation of sequencing libraries
source_steps.description: Incubate for 2 min at room temperature (∼20°C), then place the tube on a magnet and once separation is complete, discard the supernatant.

source_steps.description: Add 150 μL of room temperature (∼20°C) Wash Buffer 3 to the beads and gently mix the solution by pipetting it up and down 10 times.
