source_steps.description: Incubate for 2 min at room temperature (∼20°C), then lace the tube on a magnet and once separation is complete, discard the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: BdLT-seq: 10× Chromium processing, capture of BdLT-Seq barcoded mRNA and preparation of sequencing libraries
source_steps.description: To elute H2B-GFP cDNA harboring BdLT-Seq barcodes, add 100 μL of denaturation buffer (100 mM NaOH, 0.1 mM EDTA) to the bead-cDNA complex and incubate for 15 min at room temperature (∼20°C) in a ThermoMixer C (300 rpm).

source_steps.stage: BdLT-seq: 10× Chromium processing, capture of BdLT-Seq barcoded mRNA and preparation of sequencing libraries
source_steps.description: Place the tube on a magnetic rack. Once separation is complete, transfer the supernatant into a fresh tube.

source_steps.stage: BdLT-seq: 10× Chromium processing, capture of BdLT-Seq barcoded mRNA and preparation of sequencing libraries
source_steps.description: To stop the reaction, add 10 μL of 1 M Tris buffer (pH 7.5) and 10 μL of 1 M HCl.

source_steps.stage: BdLT-seq: 10× Chromium processing, capture of BdLT-Seq barcoded mRNA and preparation of sequencing libraries
source_steps.description: Purify the H2B-GFP cDNA fraction (BdLT-Seq fraction) as well as the GEx fraction using Dynabeads MyOne Silane beads (Invitrogen, cat. #37002D). To do this, pipette 10 μL (for 2 samples) of Dynabeads MyOne Silane beads (Invitrogen, cat. #37002D) and wash the beads twice in 500 μL of RLT lysis buffer (QIAGEN, cat. #79216).

source_steps.stage: BdLT-seq: 10× Chromium processing, capture of BdLT-Seq barcoded mRNA and preparation of sequencing libraries
source_steps.description: Next, resuspend the beads in 840 μL of RLT buffer (3.5× initial sample volume) and add 420 μL to the ∼120 μL of each sample.

source_steps.stage: BdLT-seq: 10× Chromium processing, capture of BdLT-Seq barcoded mRNA and preparation of sequencing libraries
source_steps.description: Add 540 μL of absolute ethanol to each reaction (4.5× initial sample volume) and mix well by pipetting.

background: Yeast-displayed SARS-CoV-2 RBD cells have already been prepared and are entering the competitive binding stage used to detect antibody escape. At this point, cells are exposed to antibody and biotinylated ACE2, then fluorescently labeled, chilled, and washed so they are ready for downstream screening or sorting. The relevant work is a local sequence of incubation, cold handling, repeated microcentrifuge washes, and short protected-from-light labeling steps.
goal: Process the competitive binding sample through antibody and ACE2 binding, fluorescent labeling, and final cleanup so the cells are ready to be screened while preserving fluorescence and binding signal.
constraints: Begin from a cell suspension already combined with PBSF and antibody in a microcentrifuge tube, and keep the workflow focused on the competitive binding segment rather than upstream culture preparation.

Use a room-temperature incubation for the antibody binding phase and a second room-temperature incubation after adding biotinylated ACE2; each of these binding periods should be 30 min at about 20-25 C under static conditions.
After the binding reactions, perform a microcentrifuge spin at 16000 x g for 1 min and keep temperature control cold for pellet handling.
Include two PBSF wash cycles after the post-binding spin using the same pellet as the carried intermediate state, rather than restarting from raw cells.
Fluorescent labeling must occur on the washed pellet, using a cold protected-from-light incubation on ice for 10 min.
After fluorescent labeling, perform another 16000 x g 1 min spin followed by two additional PBSF wash cycles.
Maintain cold conditions for labeled cells and protect fluorophores from light during the labeling-related incubations and holding period.
End with a short on-ice storage state suitable for screening soon afterward, not long-term archiving.

available_inputs.name: binding_cells
available_inputs.description: Yeast cell suspension in a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube prepared for competitive binding.

available_inputs.name: pbsf_buffer
available_inputs.description: PBSF wash and resuspension buffer kept cold.

available_inputs.name: antibody_mix
available_inputs.description: Antibody-containing binding mixture corresponding to 10 ug/mL final antibody condition.

available_inputs.name: biotinylated_ace2
available_inputs.description: Biotinylated ACE2 reagent used for the 75 nM competitive binding condition.

available_inputs.name: fluorescent_labeling_mix
available_inputs.description: Cold fluorescent labeling mix protected from light.

gold_program:
mixed_binding = mix_sample(sample=binding_cells, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
incubated_antibody = incubate_sample(sample=mixed_binding, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=30.0, condition='static')
incubated_ace2 = incubate_sample(sample=incubated_antibody, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=30.0, condition='static')
post_binding_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=incubated_ace2, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=4.0)
wash1 = mix_sample(sample=post_binding_spin, method='pipetting', duration_s=8.0, speed='gentle')
wash1_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash1, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=4.0)
wash2 = mix_sample(sample=wash1_spin, method='pipetting', duration_s=8.0, speed='gentle')
wash2_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash2, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=4.0)
labeled_cells = mix_sample(sample=wash2_spin, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
label_incubation = incubate_sample(sample=labeled_cells, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=10.0, condition='protected from light')
post_label_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=label_incubation, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=4.0)
final_ready = store_sample(sample=post_label_spin, temperature_c=0.0, duration_h=1.0, container='microcentrifuge_tube_on_ice')
protocol_title: Identification of SARS-CoV-2 S RBD escape mutants using yeast screening and deep mutational scanning

source_steps.description: Label 5 μL cells with 44 μL of PBSF and 1 μL of anti-c-myc FITC in a 1.5mL microcentrifuge tube. Mix by pipetting up and down several times. Incubate the cells on ice for 10 min protected from light.
Centrifuge the cells 1 min at 16000 × g using a microcentrifuge. Wash twice with 200 μL ice cold PBSF and remove supernatant. Cell pellets can be stored on ice until ready to screen.
Resuspend the cells in 100μL ice cold PBSF and screen 25,000 cells. If the yeast does not display the S RBD, do not prepare the binding reactions and refer to troubleshooting 1 .

Set up three separate labeling reactions in three different 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes using the cells at OD 600 =2. Incubate 30 min at 20°C–25°C. Ensure that the biotin:protein molar ratio has been adjusted before starting the escape mutant identification, else, refer to ‘ Confirm that neutralizing antibodies selected bind and inhibit the yeast displayed glycosylated WT RBD ’. Tube 1 Tube 2 Tube 3 Library Cells 5μL 5μL - WT Cells - - 5μL Biotinylated ACE2 - 75nM - Free ACE2 - - 10μg/mL PBSF To 50μL total volume To 50μL total volume To 50μL total volume Incubate for 30 min at 20°C–25°C. Biotinylated ACE2 - - 75nM - - Incubate for 30 min at 20°C–25°C. Centrifuge 1 min at 16000 × g using a microcentrifuge. Wash twice with 200 μL PBSF and remove supernatant.
Resuspend the second and third pellet with 50 μL fluorescent labeling mix ( recipe ). The first tube must not be labeled. Keep the fluorophores on ice and protected from light at all times.
Incubate 10 min on ice and protect from light.

Centrifuge 1 min at 16000 × g using a microcentrifuge. Wash twice with 200 μL PBSF and remove supernatant. The cell pellet can be stored on ice until ready to screen. We do not recommend storing on ice for longer than 1h as fluorophores might dissociate, resulting in a lower signal.
Resuspend the cells in 100 μL PBSF and screen 25,000 cells.

In a 1.5mL microcentrifuge tube add 150 μL of cells, PBSF, and 10 μg/mL antibody to a final volume of 400 μL. Incubate 30 min at 20°C–25°C. Then, add biotinylated ACE2 to a final concentration of 75nM. Again, incubate 30 min at 20°C–25°C ( Figure 2 A). CRITICAL: If multiple samples are sorted, it is better to incubate pellets on ice before the next step. Fluorescent label right before sorting to ensure maximum fluorescence. To minimize binding loss, stagger the binding reactions to minimize the storage time so that when the reaction is completed, the sample can be sorted. Centrifuge 1 min at 16000 × g using a microcentrifuge. Wash twice with 1 mL PBSF and remove supernatant. Cell pellets can be stored on ice for ∼4 h without significant binding loss if all the supernatant is removed.

Resuspend each cell pellet with 200 μL fluorescent labeling mix ( recipe ). Keep the fluorescent labeling mix on ice and always protected from light. CRITICAL: Better results are obtained if the cells are fluorescently labelled right before sorting.
Incubate 10 min on ice and protect from light.

Centrifuge 1 min at 16000 × g using a microcentrifuge. Wash twice with 1 mL PBSF and remove supernatant. Cell pellets can be stored covered and on ice until ready to screen. Optional: While sorting, the fluorescence signal might decrease over time. If the decrease is significant, divide the sample into two 500 μL samples and spin down again, remove supernatant and spin down to make sure no liquid is left in the sample. Store each pellet on ice until ready to use.

background: Murine intestinal lamina propria single cells have already been distributed into a V-bottom plate and are entering the antibody-staining phase for flow cytometry. The remaining work in this local stage is to carry the cells through Fc receptor blocking, surface labeling, fixation and permeabilization, intracellular labeling, and final resuspension for acquisition while preserving the cold and light-protected conditions used for sensitive staining steps.
goal: Complete the staining-stage processing of the plated lamina propria cells so that the final sample is ready for flow-cytometer acquisition in the appropriate buffer.
constraints: Start from the plated cells and maintain a single dependency chain through blocking, staining, fixation/permeabilization, intracellular staining, and final preparation.
Use repeated centrifugation and incubation steps; the standard spin condition in this segment is 300 x g for 5 min at 4 C, but a later permeabilization-associated wash uses 400 x g for 5 min at 4 C.

Include a 30 min incubation at 4 C for Fc block, a 30 min surface-staining incubation at 4 C in the dark, a 30 to 60 min fixation incubation at 4 C in the dark, and a 60 min intracellular-staining incubation at 4 C in the dark.
Represent reagent addition or resuspension with available mixing actions only; do not introduce unsupported transfer primitives.
After fixation solution is used, subsequent staining and washes must proceed in permeabilization buffer before the final switch to FACS buffer-II.
The output must explicitly end as cells resuspended in FACS buffer-II, suitable for immediate acquisition.

step1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=plated_cells, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
step2 = mix_sample(sample=step1, method="pipetting", duration_s=15, speed="gentle with Fc block in FACS Buffer-I")
step3 = incubate_sample(sample=step2, temperature_c=4, duration_min=30, condition="static")
step4 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step3, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
step5 = mix_sample(sample=step4, method="pipetting", duration_s=20, speed="gentle with surface antibody mix in FACS Buffer-I")
step6 = incubate_sample(sample=step5, temperature_c=4, duration_min=30, condition="protected from light")
step7 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step6, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
step8 = mix_sample(sample=step7, method="pipetting", duration_s=20, speed="gentle with fixation permeabilization solution")
step9 = incubate_sample(sample=step8, temperature_c=4, duration_min=30, condition="protected from light")
step10 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step9, speed_xg=400, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
step11 = mix_sample(sample=step10, method="pipetting", duration_s=20, speed="gentle with intracellular antibody mix in permeabilization buffer")
step12 = incubate_sample(sample=step11, temperature_c=4, duration_min=60, condition="protected from light")

source_steps.description: Once the cells are resuspended, seed them in a labeled 96-well V-bottom plate and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at 4°C. Throughout the staining process the V-bottom plate must be centrifuged at 300 × g for 5 min at 4°C unless explicitly mentioned otherwise (see troubleshooting problem 3 ).
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cells in 50 μL of FACS Buffer-I containing Fc-block (anti-mouse CD16/CD32). Incubate the cells for 30 min at 4°C.

Meanwhile, prepare the antibody cocktails for staining different immune cell populations. Above are the (8-color) panels we used to test the enzymatic efficiency of the different digestion enzymes in isolating immune cells residing in the intestinal-LP.
Add 150 μL of FACS Buffer-I to wash out the excess, unbound Fc block and centrifuge the plate 300 × g for 5 min at 4°C.
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cells in 200 μL of FACS Buffer-I to wash the cells. Centrifuge the plate at 300 × g for 5 min at 4°C and discard the supernatant.

Resuspend the cell pellet in 50 μL of FACS Buffer-I containing the antibody mix, including biotinylated antibodies, fixable viability dye, and fluorochrome-labeled antibodies against surface antigens. Incubate for 30 min at 4°C in the dark. After the incubation, add 150 μL of FACS Buffer-I per well to wash out the unbound antibodies and repeat step 27.

Prepare the fixation solution in the eBioscience FoxP3 / Transcription Factor Staining Buffer Set, by diluting 1 part of the fixation/permeabilization concentrate with 3 parts of the fixation/permeabilization diluent. For 1× permeabilization wash buffer, add 9 parts of double-distilled water to 1 part of the 10× permeabilization buffer in the kit. Mix well before use.

Resuspend the cells in 100 μL of the fixation solution and incubate for 30 min to 1 h in the dark at 4°C. Add 100 μL of the permeabilization buffer to wash off the fixation solution, centrifuge, and discard the supernatant.

Resuspend the pellet in 200 μL of permeabilization buffer to ensure permeabilization of the cells and to remove the residual fixation solution. Centrifuge at 400 × g for 5 min at 4°C and discard the supernatant.

Resuspend the cells in 50 μL of permeabilization buffer containing the intracellular antibodies and incubate in the dark for 1 h at 4°C. If there is a weak staining of intracellular antigens, refer to troubleshooting problem 4 for suggestions.

Add 150 μL of permeabilization buffer, centrifuge the cells at 400 × g for 5 min at 4°C and discard the supernatant. Repeat the wash with 200 μL of permeabilization buffer, centrifuge, and discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the cells in 200 μL of FACS buffer-II and acquire them using a flow cytometer.

background: This experiment prepares Cas9 RNP material for mouse zygote genome editing. The guide RNA and donor oligonucleotide are already available and thawed, and the immediate task is to keep reagents cold, collect liquid from tube walls, carry out the brief warm complexing step, then chill and hold the finished mixture for later embryo work.
goal: Assemble and condition a 2× Cas9 RNP mix from thawed sgRNA and repair template, including the required brief spins, 37°C incubation, post-incubation chilling, and cold storage before use.
constraints: Keep the workflow in a nuclease-free microcentrifuge tube context and maintain cold handling with ice where specified.
Use the required numeric conditions from the protocol segment, including 37°C for 10 min, a final donor concentration of 20 µM, and a cold hold not exceeding 18 hr.
Include multiple brief collection spins to represent pulse centrifugation before and after the warm incubation and again after the final mixing step.
Use only provided action-pool functions and keep the procedure at instrument-action abstraction rather than explicit liquid-transfer robotics.
The program must contain 6-12 top-level steps, assign every call to a variable, and reuse at least two action names more than once.

available_inputs.name: sgrna_tube
available_inputs.description: Tube containing thawed sgRNA.

available_inputs.name: repair_template_tube
available_inputs.description: Tube containing thawed oligonucleotide repair template.

available_inputs.name: rnp_mix_tube
available_inputs.description: Microcentrifuge tube used for the Cas9 RNP assembly mixture.

available_inputs.name: ice_holder
available_inputs.description: Ice-based cooling location for temporary cold handling.

gold_program:
sgRNA_on_ice = cool_sample(sample=sgrna_tube, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=5.0, device=ice_holder)
repair_template_on_ice = cool_sample(sample=repair_template_tube, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=5.0, device=ice_holder)
thawed_reagents_collected = centrifuge_sample(sample=sgRNA_on_ice, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=4.0)
preincubation_mix = set_thermal_mixer(sample=rnp_mix_tube, temperature_c=4.0, speed_rpm=300, duration_min=0.2)
preincubation_collected = centrifuge_sample(sample=preincubation_mix, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=4.0)
rnp_warmed = incubate_sample(sample=preincubation_collected, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=10.0, condition="static")
postwarm_collected = centrifuge_sample(sample=rnp_warmed, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=4.0)
rnp_chilled = cool_sample(sample=postwarm_collected, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=2.0, device=ice_holder)
final_mix = set_thermal_mixer(sample=rnp_chilled, temperature_c=4.0, speed_rpm=300, duration_min=0.2)
final_collected = centrifuge_sample(sample=final_mix, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=4.0)
final_rnp_stored = store_sample(sample=final_collected, temperature_c=0.0, duration_h=18.0, container=ice_holder)
protocol_title: Engineering Base Changes and Epitope‐Tagged Alleles in Mice Using Cas9 RNA‐Guided Nuclease

source_steps.description: Transfer rinsed filter to a new collection bottle, discarding the first bottle with rinse buffer.
Filter the remaining 100 ml buffer through the rinsed filter. Store in the sterile collection bottle for the next step.
Aspirate 20 ml buffer into a γ‐radiation‐sterilized 20‐ml Luer‐lock syringe. Attach a 0.02‐µm Whatman Anotop Plus filter to the filled syringe.
Filter and discard 5 ml buffer to rinse the filter, then filter the remaining buffer into two aliquots in sterile PETG serum vials. Close with a sterile closure and store at room temperature.

To confirm that buffer is RNase‐free, add 2 µg RNA to 18 µl filtered microinjection buffer and incubate at 37°C for ≥30 min. Assess integrity of the RNA by agarose gel electrophoresis or bioanalysis compared to freshly prepared RNA. No RNA degradation should be observed.

Confirm that buffer is suitable for microinjection by injecting 40 to 60 embryos (0.5 dpc, see Basic Protocol 4 ) with 0.02‐µm filtered buffer. Transfer to pseudopregnant recipients in parallel with 40 to 60 embryos injected with previously QC'd buffer. Dissect mid‐gestation and score number of implantation sites and number of live embryos. Implantation and live embryo rates for test buffer should be within 10% of rates for previously QC'd buffer.
Thaw sgRNA and oligonucleotide repair template on ice. Mix well and pulse centrifuge to collect contents.

To prepare 2× RNPs in 1× Cas9 RNP buffer, combine the following in a 0.5‐ml nuclease‐ and pyrogen‐free microcentrifuge tube: Nuclease‐free water to a total of 5 or 11 µl (including oligonucleotide repair template)
Mix well by flicking the tube and pulse centrifuge to collect any droplets.
Incubate at 37°C for 10 min. Pulse centrifuge to collect droplets and transfer to ice.
Add oligonucleotide repair template to a final concentration of 20 µM.
Mix well by flicking the tube and pulse centrifuge to collect droplets. Place on ice until use (no more than 18 hr).

background: Yeast-displayed SARS-CoV-2 RBD libraries are in the competitive binding preparation stage for escape mutant mapping. After induction, the culture must be checked by OD600, processed into control and display-QC reactions, and then advanced into a parallel ACE2 binding setup that ends with washed pellets ready for fluorescent labeling.
goal: Process the induced yeast culture through OD documentation, unlabeled and anti-c-myc FITC quality-control reactions, and the initial three-tube ACE2 competitive binding preparation so the correct pellets are ready for downstream fluorescent screening.
constraints: Document OD600 status and capture the normalization targets used at this stage, including OD600 = 2 for controls and binding reactions; use a 600 nm optical measurement.
Represent repeated centrifugation cleanup at 16000 x g for 1 min, including an initial harvest and later post-reaction spins, with cold handling for PBSF-associated resuspensions and washes.

Include an unlabeled control incubated for 30 min at about room temperature (20-25C), followed by centrifugation and a record that it is screened as a 25,000-cell gating control after resuspension in 100 uL PBSF.

Include a separate anti-c-myc FITC display-check reaction with pipette mixing, a 10 min incubation on ice protected from light, and two sequential 200 uL PBSF washes separated by centrifugation before recording the display QC outcome.

Model a three-tube competitive binding setup using cells at OD600 = 2 in 50 uL total volume with the ACE2 conditions represented across tubes, including 75 nM biotinylated ACE2 and 10 ug/mL free ACE2, followed by a 30 min incubation at 20-25C.

After the competitive binding incubation, perform a centrifugation and two PBSF wash cycles before preparing pellets for labeling; only the second and third pellets should proceed to fluorescent labeling while the first remains unlabeled.

available_inputs.name: induced_yeast_culture
available_inputs.description: Induced yeast culture ready for competitive binding preparation.

available_inputs.name: pbsf_buffer
available_inputs.description: PBSF buffer used for cold resuspension and wash steps.

available_inputs.name: anti_cmyc_fitc
available_inputs.description: Anti-c-myc FITC reagent for checking yeast surface display.

available_inputs.name: library_cells_od2
available_inputs.description: Aliquot of library cells normalized to OD600 = 2 for control and binding reactions.

available_inputs.name: wt_cells_od2
available_inputs.description: Aliquot of WT cells normalized to OD600 = 2 for comparator ACE2 reaction.

available_inputs.name: ace2_binding_reaction_set
available_inputs.description: Three-tube reaction set containing the specified library and WT ACE2 competitive binding conditions.

available_inputs.name: fluorescent_labeling_mix
available_inputs.description: Prepared fluorescent labeling mix for the second and third pellets.

gold_program:
od_read = measure_signal(sample=induced_yeast_culture, assay='OD600 spectrophotometry', wavelength_nm=600, replicate_n=1)
od_record = record_measurement(sample=od_read, metric='OD600_targets', unit='OD600', note='Controls and binding reactions use OD600=2; sorting preparations may use OD600=10 after harvest')
harvested_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=induced_yeast_culture, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=4)
unlabeled_control = incubate_sample(sample=library_cells_od2, temperature_c=22, duration_min=30, condition='static in PBSF as unlabeled control; resuspend to 100 uL PBSF for 25000-cell gate setup after spin')
fitc_reaction = mix_sample(sample=anti_cmyc_fitc, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle for 5 uL cells plus 44 uL PBSF plus 1 uL FITC')
fitc_qc = incubate_sample(sample=fitc_reaction, temperature_c=4, duration_min=10, condition='on ice protected from light; then wash twice with 200 uL ice-cold PBSF after 16000 x g spins and assess display on 25000 cells')
ace2_binding_incubation = incubate_sample(sample=ace2_binding_reaction_set, temperature_c=22, duration_min=30, condition='three-tube competitive binding at OD600=2 in 50 uL total volume with 75 nM biotinylated ACE2 and 10 ug/mL free ACE2 conditions represented across tubes')
ace2_cleanup = centrifuge_sample(sample=ace2_binding_incubation, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=4)
ace2_wash1 = mix_sample(sample=ace2_cleanup, method='pipetting', duration_s=5, speed='gentle in 200 uL PBSF before repeat spin')
ace2_wash2 = mix_sample(sample=ace2_wash1, method='pipetting', duration_s=5, speed='gentle in 200 uL PBSF after second 16000 x g cleanup spin')
fluor_labeled_pellets = incubate_sample(sample=fluorescent_labeling_mix, temperature_c=4, duration_min=1, condition='resuspend only tube 2 and tube 3 pellets; keep fluorophores on ice and protected from light')
protocol_title: Identification of SARS-CoV-2 S RBD escape mutants using yeast screening and deep mutational scanning

source_steps.description: Transfer culture to a 14 mL culture tube for volumes lower than 1.5 mL (VWR Cat#10127-334) or a 250 mL shaker flask for large volumes (Fisher Cat# 10040F). Incubate cells at 30°C and 300rpm for 4–6 h.

Measure the culture OD 600 with a spectrophotometer. If 1.2 < OD 600 < 5, harvest the cells by centrifugation for 1 min at 16000 × g in a microcentrifuge and resuspend to OD 600 =1 in SGCAA + ( recipe ).

Transfer the appropriate amount of the culture to a new 14mL culture tube or a 250mL shaker flask as necessary and incubate 22h at 22°C and 300rpm in an incubator shaker. As previously stated, we recommend using 14 mL culture tubes for the volumes lower than 1.5 mL and 250 mL shaker flasks for larger volumes.
Transfer the culture to a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube for low volumes or a 15 mL conical tube for larger volumes and centrifuge for 1 min at 16000 × g using a centrifuge.

Measure the culture OD 600 and resuspend the cells in ice cold PBSF to an OD 600 =2 for the controls and to an OD 600 =10 for sorting. We typically perform a 10-fold dilution of culture to determine the OD 600 . The yeast culture will not necessarily increase in OD 600 during this 16–24 h induction.
Combine 5 μL cells with 45 μL of PBSF (‘unlabeled’ cells) in a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube. Incubate 30 min at 20°C–25°C.
Centrifuge 1 min at 16000 × g using a microcentrifuge and resuspend the cells in 100μL ice cold PBSF. Screen 25,000 unlabeled cells and set the FSC-A/FITC + gate as in Figure 2 B.
Label 5 μL cells with 44 μL of PBSF and 1 μL of anti-c-myc FITC in a 1.5mL microcentrifuge tube. Mix by pipetting up and down several times. Incubate the cells on ice for 10 min protected from light.
Centrifuge the cells 1 min at 16000 × g using a microcentrifuge. Wash twice with 200 μL ice cold PBSF and remove supernatant. Cell pellets can be stored on ice until ready to screen.
Resuspend the cells in 100μL ice cold PBSF and screen 25,000 cells. If the yeast does not display the S RBD, do not prepare the binding reactions and refer to troubleshooting 1 .

Set up three separate labeling reactions in three different 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes using the cells at OD 600 =2. Incubate 30 min at 20°C–25°C. Ensure that the biotin:protein molar ratio has been adjusted before starting the escape mutant identification, else, refer to ‘ Confirm that neutralizing antibodies selected bind and inhibit the yeast displayed glycosylated WT RBD ’. Tube 1 Tube 2 Tube 3 Library Cells 5μL 5μL - WT Cells - - 5μL Biotinylated ACE2 - 75nM - Free ACE2 - - 10μg/mL PBSF To 50μL total volume To 50μL total volume To 50μL total volume Incubate for 30 min at 20°C–25°C. Biotinylated ACE2 - - 75nM - - Incubate for 30 min at 20°C–25°C. Centrifuge 1 min at 16000 × g using a microcentrifuge. Wash twice with 200 μL PBSF and remove supernatant.
Resuspend the second and third pellet with 50 μL fluorescent labeling mix ( recipe ). The first tube must not be labeled. Keep the fluorophores on ice and protected from light at all times.

background: Following flow-cytometry enrichment, the GFP-negative experimental cells have been collected and need to be pelleted, preserved, and processed for genomic DNA recovery. At this stage, the task is the local genomic DNA extraction workflow for the sorted-cell fraction using a silica spin-column style cleanup before downstream sgRNA amplification and sequencing preparation.
goal: Prepare genomic DNA from the sorted GFP-negative cell fraction, including pellet handling, lysis, column-based cleanup, elution, concentration measurement, and frozen storage.
constraints: Use the sorted GFP-negative cells as the starting material and include the post-sort pelleting condition of 500 x g for 10 min.
The cell pellet must be stored at -80 C before the extraction workflow proceeds.
Model the early extraction stage as a lysis setup followed by a 10 min incubation after addition of PBS, RNase A, and Proteinase K.
After lysis buffer treatment, include a 65 C heating step for 15 min using a water-bath style device.
Before the first column-binding spin, represent thorough mixing after ethanol addition.
The column cleanup must include repeated high-speed spins at 12000 x g, with three 1 min spins associated with binding and wash handling plus one 2 min dry spin.
Include a room-temperature elution wait of 3 min before the final 12000 x g recovery spin.
Measure genomic DNA concentration with a Nanodrop-like absorbance readout and then store the purified DNA at -20 C.
Use only action-pool functions and keep the program within 7 to 13 top-level function calls.

gold_program:
pelleted_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=sorted_gfp_negative_cells, speed_xg=500, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
frozen_pellet = store_sample(sample=pelleted_cells, temperature_c=-80, duration_h=1, container="freezer")
lysed_cells = incubate_sample(sample=lysis_mix, temperature_c=25, duration_min=10, condition="static")
heated_lysate = heat_sample(sample=buffer_dl, temperature_c=65, duration_min=15, device="water bath")
binding_mix = vortex_mix(sample=ethanol_binding_state, duration_s=5, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=25)
column_bound = centrifuge_sample(sample=binding_mix, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
gw1_processed = centrifuge_sample(sample=column_bound, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
gw2_processed = centrifuge_sample(sample=gw1_processed, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
dry_column = centrifuge_sample(sample=gw2_processed, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=2, temperature_c=25)
elution_wait = incubate_sample(sample=buffer_ae, temperature_c=25, duration_min=3, condition="static")
eluted_dna = centrifuge_sample(sample=elution_wait, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
dna_signal = measure_signal(sample=eluted_dna, assay="nanodrop", wavelength_nm=260, replicate_n=1)
stored_gdna = store_sample(sample=dna_signal, temperature_c=-20, duration_h=24, container="microcentrifuge tube")

source_steps.description: Gate the GFP-negative population as a control group, collect GFP-negative cells from the experimental group, and gather over 500,000 GFP-negative cells for subsequent PCR amplification. Simultaneously, save at least 10 7 unsorted cells as input for further experiments ( Figure 4 A).
Centrifuge the sorted cells and input cells at 500 g for 10 min, discard the supernatant, and store the cells in a −80°C freezer for subsequent genomic DNA extraction.

Extract genomic DNA from cells using the HiPure Tissue DNA Mini Kit. Note: Reagent kits extract genomic DNA through cell lysis, protein removal, and purification using silica-based spin columns. The purified DNA is then eluted for use in downstream applications. Note: The starting cell number is approximately 500,000. For isolating genomic DNA from 10 7 cells for the control library, a different kit (Magen #D3122-02 HiPure Tissue DNA Midi Kit) is used. Note: Using a kit to extract genomic DNA is essential, as kits can reduce contamination by proteins, nucleases, and other impurities, ensuring the efficiency of downstream PCR processes. Conventional genomic DNA extraction kits can serve as suitable alternatives. Add 220 μL of PBS buffer, 10 μL of RNase A, and 20 μL of Proteinase K to the cells, mix gently, and incubate for 10 min.
Then add 250 μL of Buffer DL, vortex to mix thoroughly, and incubate at 65°C in a water bath for 15 min.
Add 250 μL of ethanol to the digestion solution, vortex to mix thoroughly, and transfer the mixture to the HiPure gDNA Mini Column.
Centrifuge at 12,000 g for 1 min, then discard the filtrate.
Add 500 μL of Buffer GW1, centrifuge at 12,000 g for 1 min, then discard the filtrate.
Add 650 μL of Buffer GW2, centrifuge at 12,000 g for 1 min, then discard the filtrate.
Centrifuge at 12,000 g for 2 min.
Transfer the column to a new 1.5 mL EP tube, then add 50 μL of Buffer AE. Let it sit at room temperature for 3 min to elute the DNA.
Centrifuge at 12,000 g for 1 min, then measure the DNA concentration using a Nanodrop. The genomic DNA is stored at −20°C.

Extract genomic DNA from control library cells using the HiPure Tissue Midi DNA Kit. Note: You can click on the link http://www.mgenbio.com/show-160-131-1.html to read the detailed instruction manual. Add 100 μL of PBS buffer, 2.5 mL of Buffer ATL, and 100 μL of Proteinase K to the cells.

background: Cells recovered from the CRISPR screen sorting workflow are ready for genomic DNA purification using a silica spin-column method. The local task covers pellet recovery and freezing, enzymatic and chemical lysis, column binding and cleanup by repeated centrifugation, followed by DNA elution, quantification, and storage for later PCR and sequencing.
goal: Execute the mini-column genomic DNA extraction subtask for the sorted-cell sample and finish with a documented DNA concentration and stored purified DNA.
constraints: Use the small-scale extraction path appropriate for roughly 500000 cells, not the control-library midi workflow.
Include the prespin at 500 x g for 10 min and temporary storage at -80 C before the extraction workflow proceeds.

Model the initial digestion after adding PBS, RNase A, and Proteinase K with mixing and a 10 min incubation, then include Buffer DL treatment with thorough mixing and 15 min heating at 65 C in a water bath.

After ethanol-assisted loading to the mini column, represent the column cleanup with repeated 12000 x g centrifugations, including distinct wash-related passes and an extended 2 min drying spin before elution.
Represent elution with Buffer AE as a 3 min room-temperature wait before the final recovery spin, and end with Nanodrop-style concentration measurement, recorded concentration, and storage at -20 C.

available_inputs.description: Lysis buffer used after the first digestion setup.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_mix
available_inputs.description: Ethanol-containing addition used before transfer onto the genomic DNA mini column.

gold_program:
pelleted_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=sorted_cells, speed_xg=500, duration_min=10, temperature_c=25)
frozen_pellet = store_sample(sample=pelleted_cells, temperature_c=-80, duration_h=1, container="freezer")
prepped_digest = mix_sample(sample=frozen_pellet, method="inversion", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
digested_cells = incubate_sample(sample=prepped_digest, temperature_c=25, duration_min=10, condition="static with PBS/RNase A/Proteinase K")
dl_lysate = vortex_mix(sample=digested_cells, duration_s=5, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=25)
heated_lysate = heat_sample(sample=dl_lysate, temperature_c=65, duration_min=15, device="water bath")
column_cleanup = centrifuge_sample(sample=gdna_mini_column, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
dried_column = centrifuge_sample(sample=column_cleanup, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=2, temperature_c=25)
elution_wait = incubate_sample(sample=dried_column, temperature_c=25, duration_min=3, condition="static with Buffer AE")
eluted_dna = centrifuge_sample(sample=elution_wait, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
nanodrop_reading = measure_signal(sample=eluted_dna, assay="Nanodrop DNA concentration", wavelength_nm=260, replicate_n=1)
stored_gdna = store_sample(sample=nanodrop_reading, temperature_c=-20, duration_h=24, container="1.5 mL EP tube")

background: DNA from symptomatic blueberry samples or pure culture has completed bead washing and is entering the final cleanup-to-barcoding transition. At this point, residual ethanol must be removed without overdrying the pellet, DNA is eluted and checked for concentration, and the recovered material is then taken into a native barcode ligation reaction that is incubated briefly at room temperature and stopped with EDTA.
goal: Finish the post-wash bead cleanup for one sample and carry the recovered DNA through QC and native barcode ligation stop.
constraints: Model quick spins as centrifugation at 5000 x g for 0.083 min at 25 C, and use this more than once in the workflow.
Include a brief drying step of about 0.5 min at room temperature after ethanol removal, while preserving the idea that the pellet must not be overdried.
Represent DNA elution and recovery with a short room-temperature incubation of 2.0 min before clarification and measurement.
Use a Qubit-style DNA measurement with 1 technical replicate and record that 1 ul of eluate was used for QC in the context of a 10 ul eluate.
Include cold handling of ligation reagents on ice, then a room-temperature ligation incubation of 20.0 min followed by documentation of EDTA stop amount as 2 ul or 4 ul.

Keep the program to 8-12 top-level function calls, use only the provided action-pool functions with keyword arguments, and maintain a single dependency chain from the initial bead pellet to the ligation endpoint.

available_inputs.description: Sample-specific native barcode reagents together with EDTA and short fragment buffer, thawed for setup.

gold_program:
spin_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=bead_pellet_sample, speed_xg=5000, duration_min=0.083, temperature_c=25)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=spin_pellet, method="air dry", temperature_c=25, duration_min=0.5)
eluted_sample = mix_sample(sample=dried_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
spun_elution = centrifuge_sample(sample=eluted_sample, speed_xg=5000, duration_min=0.083, temperature_c=25)
incubated_elution = incubate_sample(sample=spun_elution, temperature_c=25, duration_min=2.0, condition="static")
qubit_result = measure_signal(sample=incubated_elution, assay="Qubit dsDNA", wavelength_nm=0, replicate_n=1)
qc_record = record_measurement(sample=qubit_result, metric="qc_input", unit="ul", note="1 ul quantified from 10 ul eluate")
cooled_ligase = cool_sample(sample=ligase_master_mix, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5.0, device="ice")
cooled_barcodes = cool_sample(sample=native_barcode_reagents, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5.0, device="ice")
assembled_ligation = mix_sample(sample=incubated_elution, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
ligated_sample = incubate_sample(sample=assembled_ligation, temperature_c=25, duration_min=20.0, condition="static")
stopped_ligation = record_measurement(sample=ligated_sample, metric="EDTA_stop_added", unit="ul", note="2 ul for clear cap or 4 ul for blue cap")
protocol_title: MinION Nanopore‐Enabled Identification and Genomic Characterization of Pseudomonas syringae Complex Infecting Blueberry Using Symptomatic Plant Samples and Pathogen Pure Culture

source_steps.description: Centrifuge samples 5 s at 5000 × g , room temperature, pellet beads on a magnetic rack, and carefully remove the supernatant.
Prepare freshly made 80% ethanol in nuclease‐free water, allowing 400 µl per sample plus some excess.
Keep tubes on the magnet and wash beads twice with 200 µl of 80% ethanol. If the pellet was disturbed, wait for beads to pellet again before removing the ethanol.

Centrifuge 5 s at 5000 × g , room temperature, place on magnet, remove residual ethanol, and dry for ∼30 s (do not crack pellet). Allow it to dry for 30 s, but do not dry the pellets to the point of cracking.
Remove the tubes from magnetic rack and elute DNA in 10 µl nuclease‐free water. Centrifuge 5 s at 5000 × g , room temperature, and incubate for 2 min at room temperature.
Pellet beads until elute is clear and colorless, transfer eluate (10 µl) to clean 1.5‐ml tube. Discard the pelleted beads.

Quantify 1 µl of the eluate using Qubit. Store samples at 4°C if proceeding to barcoding later. Take forward an equimolar mass of each sample to be barcoded forward into the native barcode ligation step. However, you may store the samples at 4°C overnight.
Thaw NEB Blunt/TA ligase master mix at room temperature, spin down, mix thoroughly by pipetting, and keep on ice.

Thaw the required Native Barcodes (NB01 to NB24), EDTA, and Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) at room temperature, mix, centrifuge 5 s at 5000 × g , room temperature, and store on ice. Only use one barcode per sample.
In clean 0.2‐ml PCR tube add the reagent in the order indicated in Table 2 per well.
Mix gently, centrifuge 5 s at 5000 × g , room temperature, and incubate for 20 min at room temperature.
Add EDTA (2 µl for clear cap, 4 µl for blue cap) to each tube to stop reaction and mix thoroughly.

background: This experiment profiles low-mRNA-content immune cells from human tissues using the BD Rhapsody single-cell workflow. At this stage, random priming/extension is underway and the optional Sample Tag PCR1 branch can be prepared in parallel. The immediate work includes chilling the freshly prepared primer extension reagents, adding the extension mix to the post-incubation sample, carrying out a four-stage thermomixer incubation with changing temperatures but constant mixing, and then preserving the Sample Tag PCR1 product at the cycler hold temperature.
goal: Complete the local random priming/extension temperature program for the bead-containing reaction and process the Sample Tag PCR1 strip through amplification and hold, ending with both products in appropriate post-run states for the next downstream manipulation.
constraints: The primer extension reagent mixture must be briefly centrifuged and kept on ice before use.
The bead-containing reaction receives 13 uL of primer extension mix before the temperature series.
Use a thermomixer-style incubation sequence with four temperatures: 25 C for 10 min, 37 C for 15 min, 45 C for 10 min, and 55 C for 10 min.
Each thermomixer incubation in the temperature series must use 1200 rpm.
The Sample Tag PCR1 reaction should be amplified on a thermal cycler and then held at 4 C.
The PCR cycle count is variable and should be represented with a valid recommended value from the protocol guidance.
The thermal cycler program corresponds to Sample Tag PCR1 conditions rather than the random priming extension incubations.
Later actions should consume outputs from earlier actions instead of restarting from raw materials.

available_inputs.name: primer_extension_mix_tube
available_inputs.description: 1.5 mL LoBind tube containing the freshly prepared primer extension reagent mix.

available_inputs.name: post_incubation_bead_tube
available_inputs.description: Reaction tube that has completed the prior incubation and is ready to receive primer extension mix.

available_inputs.name: sample_tag_pcr1_strip
available_inputs.description: PCR tube strip containing the prepared Sample Tag PCR1 reactions distributed into four 50 uL aliquots.

available_inputs.name: ice_rack
available_inputs.description: Cold rack or ice position used to keep reagents chilled.

gold_program:
primer_extension_mix_spun = centrifuge_sample(sample=primer_extension_mix_tube, speed_xg=500, duration_min=0.2, temperature_c=4)
primer_extension_mix_chilled = cool_sample(sample=primer_extension_mix_spun, temperature_c=0, duration_min=2, device=ice_rack)
bead_tube_spun = centrifuge_sample(sample=post_incubation_bead_tube, speed_xg=500, duration_min=0.2, temperature_c=4)
primer_extension_added = incubate_sample(sample=bead_tube_spun, temperature_c=4, duration_min=0.1, condition="after addition of 13 uL primer extension mix")
thermomix_25 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=primer_extension_added, temperature_c=25, speed_rpm=1200, duration_min=10)
thermomix_37 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=thermomix_25, temperature_c=37, speed_rpm=1200, duration_min=15)
thermomix_45 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=thermomix_37, temperature_c=45, speed_rpm=1200, duration_min=10)
thermomix_55 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=thermomix_45, temperature_c=55, speed_rpm=1200, duration_min=10)
pcr1_run = run_thermal_cycler(plate=sample_tag_pcr1_strip, program_name="Sample Tag PCR1", cycles=11, lid_temperature_c=105)
pcr1_hold = store_sample(sample=pcr1_run, temperature_c=4, duration_h=16, container="thermal cycler hold")
protocol_title: Profiling low-mRNA content cells in complex human tissues using BD Rhapsody single-cell analysis

source_steps.description: Prepare the Primer Extension mix ( Table 4 ) by combing all reagents into one 1.5 mL LoBind tube. Pipet-mix, briefly centrifuge and place on ice. Primer Extension mix Component Amount [μl] for 1 sample incl. 20% overage Bead RT/PCR Enhancer 14.4 10 mM dNTP 9.6 WTA Extension Enzyme 7.2

source_steps.description: After incubation, centrifuge the tube and add 13 μL of Primer Extension mix.

source_steps.description: Incubate on the thermomixer. First incubation: 25°C for 10 min at 1,200 rpm.

source_steps.stage: Random priming and extension, Sample Tag PCR1 and Sample Tag PCR2
source_steps.description: Second incubation: 37°C for 15 min at 1,200 rpm.

source_steps.stage: Random priming and extension, Sample Tag PCR1 and Sample Tag PCR2
source_steps.description: Third incubation: 45°C for 10 min at 1,200 rpm.

source_steps.stage: Random priming and extension, Sample Tag PCR1 and Sample Tag PCR2
source_steps.description: Forth incubation: 55°C for 10 min at 1,200 rpm.

source_steps.stage: Random priming and extension, Sample Tag PCR1 and Sample Tag PCR2
source_steps.description: (Optional) During incubation start the Sample Tag PCR1 run. Prepare the Sample Tag PCR1 mix ( Table 5 ) in pre-amplification workspace by combing all reagents in one 1.5 mL LoBind tube. Vortex-mix, briefly centrifuge and place on ice. Sample Tag PCR1 mix Component Amount [μl] for 1 sample with no overage PCR Master Mix 100.0 Universal Oligo 20.0 Nuclease-free water 12.0 Sample Tag PCR1 Primer 1.0

source_steps.stage: Random priming and extension, Sample Tag PCR1 and Sample Tag PCR2
source_steps.description: Add 67 μL of the “Sample Tag” (step 40) to the Sample Tag PCR1 mix tube and pipet-mix 10 times. CRITICAL: Vortexing of the Sample Tag/Sample Tag PCR1 mix tube has a negative effect on the outcome of the Sample Tag PCR.

source_steps.stage: Random priming and extension, Sample Tag PCR1 and Sample Tag PCR2
source_steps.description: Distribute the Sample Tag reaction mix to four PCR tubes à 50 μL.

source_steps.stage: Random priming and extension, Sample Tag PCR1 and Sample Tag PCR2
source_steps.description: Bring PCR strip to post-PCR amplification workspace, centrifuge and start the thermal cycler according to following program ( Table 6 ). Note: The number of PCR cycles depend on the number of cells retrieved from the cartridge. We recommend 10 cycles for >20,000 cells, 11 cycles for >10,000 and 12 cycles for >5,000 cells. Pause point: Run PCR overnight at 4°C. PCR cycling conditions for Sample Tag PCR 1 Step Temperature Time Cycles Hot Start 95°C 3 min 1 Denaturation 95°C 30 s 10–15x Annealing 60°C 3 min Extension 72°C 1 min Final extension 72°C 5 min 1 Hold 4°C N/A N/A

source_steps.stage: Random priming and extension, Sample Tag PCR1 and Sample Tag PCR2
source_steps.description: After incubation continue with Random Priming and Extension and place the tube on the magnet for a maximum of 2 min and discard supernatant.

source_steps.description: Take the tube off the magnet and add 205 μL of Elution Buffer.

background: A PEM-seq workflow has reached the stage immediately after genome editing, where transfected cells have been collected and the objective is to recover genomic DNA suitable for downstream library preparation. The remaining work in this segment focuses on pelleting and washing the cells, lysing them with proteinase K, precipitating the DNA with isopropanol, rinsing the DNA pellet with 70% ethanol, drying it briefly, and dissolving it in TE under warm mixing conditions.
goal: Process the harvested cell sample through genomic DNA isolation so that a clean DNA pellet is obtained and then fully dissolved in TE buffer for later PEM-seq library construction.
constraints: Assume the cells have already been detached and transferred into a 1.5 mL tube in quenched culture medium before this task begins, and begin from that collected cell sample.
Include two low-speed cell-pelleting centrifugations at 300 x g for 5 min at 4 C, with a single PBS wash represented between them before lysis.

Represent lysis using a total 500 uL master mix equivalent to 495 uL cell lysis buffer plus 5 uL proteinase K, and perform the lysis in a thermal mixer at 56 C and 500 rpm for an overnight interval normalized within 10-16 h.
After lysis, capture genomic DNA precipitation with 500 uL isopropanol and thorough mixing, then transfer or carry the precipitated DNA into a 1 mL 70% ethanol wash with another thorough mix.
After the ethanol wash, include a high-speed spin at 13000 x g for 5 min at 4 C, then remove solvent conceptually, air-dry the DNA pellet for 2-5 min, and dissolve it in 150 uL TE buffer.

The final DNA dissolution must use warm mixing conditions of 56 C and 500 rpm for at least 4 h, and the program should reuse at least two action families multiple times while keeping later steps dependent on earlier outputs.

available_inputs.name: cell_tube
available_inputs.description: 1.5 mL tube containing harvested transfected cells in quenched medium

available_inputs.name: pbs_wash
available_inputs.description: 1x PBS prepared for washing the cell pellet

available_inputs.name: lysis_mix
available_inputs.description: Fresh cell lysis master mix equivalent to 495 uL lysis buffer plus 5 uL proteinase K per sample

available_inputs.name: isopropanol_500ul
available_inputs.description: Aliquot of 500 uL isopropanol for genomic DNA precipitation

available_inputs.name: ethanol70_1ml
available_inputs.description: 1 mL of 70% ethanol for washing the DNA pellet

available_inputs.name: te_buffer_150ul
available_inputs.description: 150 uL TE buffer for dissolving the DNA pellet

gold_program:
pelleted_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=cell_tube, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
pbs_washed_cells = mix_sample(sample=pelleted_cells, method="inversion", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
repelleted_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=pbs_washed_cells, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
lysed_cells = set_thermal_mixer(sample=repelleted_cells, temperature_c=56, speed_rpm=500, duration_min=720)
precipitated_dna = mix_sample(sample=lysed_cells, method="inversion", duration_s=20, speed="vigorous")
ethanol_washed_dna = mix_sample(sample=precipitated_dna, method="inversion", duration_s=15, speed="vigorous")
collected_dna_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=ethanol_washed_dna, speed_xg=13000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
dried_dna_pellet = dry_sample(sample=collected_dna_pellet, method="air dry", temperature_c=25, duration_min=3)
dissolved_gdna = set_thermal_mixer(sample=dried_dna_pellet, temperature_c=56, speed_rpm=500, duration_min=240)
protocol_title: PEM-seq comprehensively quantifies DNA repair outcomes during gene-editing and DSB repair

source_steps.description: Harvest transfected cells at 48–72 h post-transfection. Typically, over 0.1 million live cells are required for one PEM-seq library, and 1–3 million cells are recommended. Aspirate the cell culture medium, wash cells with 3 mL 1× PBS, and aspirate the PBS.
Add 400 μL 0.05% trypsin to cover all cells and incubate at 37°C for 2 min to allow cell detachment and separation.
Add 400 μL DMEM/10% FBS medium to quench trypsin and collect cells into a 1.5 mL tube.
Spin at 300 g, 4°C, for 5 min and remove supernatant.
Wash once with 1 mL 1× PBS, spin, and discard the supernatant.
Prepare cell lysis master mixture for each sample by mixing 495 μL of cell lysis buffer and 5 μL of proteinase K (20 mg/mL).
Loose the cell pellet with flaps, add 500 μL of cell lysis master mixture, and incubate them in a thermomixer at 56°C, 500 rpm, for overnight (10–16 h).
Add 500 μL of Isopropanol, mix immediately and thoroughly by inverting and shaking the microtube until the white pellet forms. Troubleshooting 1
Pick up the DNA pellet with a pipette tip, transfer it to a new 1.5 mL microtube containing 1 mL 70% (vol/vol) ethanol, and mix thoroughly by inverting and shaking.
Centrifuge it at 13,000 g for 5 min at 4°C.
Remove the supernatant completely, and air-dry the DNA pellet for 2–5 min.
Dissolve the DNA pellet in 150 μL TE buffer and incubate at 56°C, 500 rpm, for at least 4 h.

background: This task focuses on the late purification stage of Mycobacterium smegmatis ribosomes after an initial ribosomal pellet has already been obtained. The material is kept cold, subjected to a high-salt cleanup to remove loosely associated factors, clarified by repeated medium-speed centrifugation, pelleted again by ultracentrifugation, and then resolved on a sucrose density gradient before OD260 analysis of the collected fractions.
goal: Complete the cleanup and gradient-based separation of a ribosome preparation starting from clarified lysate, ending with collected fractions and an OD260 readout suitable for profiling rRNA-containing ribosomal species.
constraints: Keep the workflow at 4 °C for storage and centrifugation steps in this segment.
Use an initial ribosome pelleting spin of 150000 x g for 150 min, then later a second ribosome pelleting spin at the same force and duration, and finally a gradient spin of 150000 x g for 270 min.
The first stored pellet and the later stored pellet are each held in 500 µL buffer B before the next processing stage.
The high-salt cleanup must reflect addition of 19.5 mL buffer C and must be followed by two clarification spins at 13000 x g for 45 min, carrying forward the supernatant.
The sucrose gradient must represent 40%, 30%, 20%, and 10% layers in buffer B and be cold-equilibrated overnight before loading the dissolved pellet.
Collect gradient output as 750 µL fractions in microcentrifuge tubes and measure absorbance at 260 nm using buffer B as the blank context.

available_inputs.name: whole_cell_lysate_supernatant
available_inputs.description: Clarified whole cell lysate supernatant ready for ribosome pelleting in a 33 mL centrifuge tube.

available_inputs.name: buffer_B
available_inputs.description: Ribosome buffer used for pellet storage, gradient formulation, sample dissolution, and blanking during OD260 measurement.

available_inputs.name: buffer_C
available_inputs.description: High salt wash buffer used to strip weakly associated proteins from the ribosome preparation.

available_inputs.name: sucrose_gradient_tube
available_inputs.description: Fresh centrifuge tube intended to hold the layered sucrose density gradient.

available_inputs.name: fraction_tubes
available_inputs.description: Set of 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes for collecting gradient fractions.

gold_program:
ribosome_pellet_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=whole_cell_lysate_supernatant, speed_xg=150000, duration_min=150, temperature_c=4)
stored_pellet_1 = store_sample(sample=ribosome_pellet_1, temperature_c=4, duration_h=16, container="centrifuge_tube_with_500uL_buffer_B")
high_salt_treated = incubate_sample(sample=stored_pellet_1, temperature_c=4, duration_min=60, condition="resuspended in 19.5 mL buffer C with repeated mixing for high-salt wash")
clarified_once = centrifuge_sample(sample=high_salt_treated, speed_xg=13000, duration_min=45, temperature_c=4)
clarified_twice = centrifuge_sample(sample=clarified_once, speed_xg=13000, duration_min=45, temperature_c=4)
ribosome_pellet_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=clarified_twice, speed_xg=150000, duration_min=150, temperature_c=4)
stored_pellet_2 = store_sample(sample=ribosome_pellet_2, temperature_c=4, duration_h=16, container="centrifuge_tube_with_500uL_buffer_B")
cold_gradient = store_sample(sample=sucrose_gradient_tube, temperature_c=4, duration_h=16, container="sucrose_gradient_40_30_20_10_percent_in_buffer_B")
gradient_loaded_sample = incubate_sample(sample=stored_pellet_2, temperature_c=4, duration_min=5, condition="dissolved in 500 uL buffer B and overlaid on prechilled sucrose gradient")
gradient_separated = centrifuge_sample(sample=gradient_loaded_sample, speed_xg=150000, duration_min=270, temperature_c=4)
fractions_collected = collect_fraction(sample=gradient_separated, phase="bottom_to_top_gradient_fractions", destination="fraction_tubes", volume_ul=750)
od260_readout = measure_signal(sample=fractions_collected, assay="OD260_with_buffer_B_blank", wavelength_nm=260, replicate_n=1)
protocol_title: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Carefully collect the whole cell lysate and transfer to a 33 mL centrifuge tube. Subject this supernatant to ultracentrifugation at 150,000× g for 2 h and 30 min at 4 °C using a swing-out rotor.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Decant the supernatant and store the ribosomal pellet in the centrifuge tube at 4 °C by adding 500 μL of buffer B ( Figure 1A ) without resuspending. Cover the tube with parafilm to prevent contamination.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Add 19.5 mL of buffer C (high salt wash buffer) to the overnight pellet and pipette it repeatedly to ensure proper dissolving.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Transfer the mixture into a glass homogenizer and homogenize the solution for 1 h at every 5 min interval. This step is crucial for the removal of weakly interacting proteins from the ribosome.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Transfer the solution to an Oak Ridge tube and centrifuge at 13,000× g for 45 min at 4 °C. Collect the supernatant and repeat the centrifugation step using a fresh Oak Ridge tube.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Carefully collect the supernatant in a centrifuge tube and perform ultracentrifugation at 150,000× g for 2 h and 30 min at 4 °C using a swing-out rotor.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Decant the supernatant and store the pellet in the centrifuge tube at 4 °C by adding 500 μL of buffer B ( Figure 1B ). Cover the tube with parafilm to prevent contamination.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: In a fresh centrifuge tube, prepare a sucrose density gradient using buffer B. The bottom layer consists of 40% sucrose (12 mL), followed by 30% (w/v) sucrose (8 mL), 20% (w/v) sucrose (7 mL), and a top-most layer containing 10% (w/v) sucrose (6 mL). Keep this density gradient inside a 4 °C refrigerator overnight for next-day use ( Figure 1C ).

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Dissolve the overnight stored ribosomal pellet in 500 μL of buffer B by pipetting and overlay it on top of the sucrose density gradient.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Gently subject this gradient to ultracentrifugation at 150,000× g for 4 h and 30 min at 4 °C using a swing-out rotor.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: After completion of ultracentrifugation, collect 750 μL fractions from the gradient into 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes using a peristaltic pump. The peristaltic pump collects fractions from bottom to top, with the 40% layer coming out first and the 10% layer last.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Measure the OD 260 of all fractions to detect the amount of rRNA and plot in Prism (v5.00) software (GraphPad, San Diego, CA) to obtain the profile ( Figure 2A ). Buffer B is used as a blank.

background: Human iPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells have been detached from a 3D nanofiber culture and are being prepared for enrichment before co-culture with neurons. At this stage, the workflow focuses on stopping papain activity, recovering the cells by room-temperature spins, removing aggregates with a 70 μm strainer, and labeling the suspension at cold temperature with blocking reagent and O4/AN2 microbeads.
goal: Process the detached oligodendrocyte-lineage cell suspension through quenching, recovery, filtration, and cold antibody-bead labeling so it is ready for the next enrichment stage.
constraints: Include a 15 min papain incubation at 37°C under incubator conditions, then stop digestion with ovomucoid at a 1:1 papain:ovomucoid ratio followed by a 1 min room-temperature hold.
Use gentle pipette-based mixing for reagent incorporation, especially during blocking and bead-labeling; do not use vortexing for the microbead steps.
Perform room-temperature recovery spins at 400 x g for 5 min more than once in the workflow, with later operations consuming the resulting pellets or resuspended products.
Resuspend recovered cells in co-culture medium containing 10 μM ROCK inhibitor before passing them through a pre-wet 70 μm cell strainer into a 15 mL conical tube.

Cold labeling must include a 10 min incubation at 4°C in MACS buffer/FcR blocker mixture with total sample volume 87 μL, followed by addition of anti-O4/anti-AN2 microbeads totaling 13 μL and a 30 min incubation at 4°C.
Finish by washing the labeled cells with 1 mL MACS buffer using gentle mixing, yielding a washed labeled suspension for downstream enrichment.

available_inputs.name: papain_well_plate
available_inputs.description: Culture wells containing detached hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells on 3D nanofibers with activated papain added

available_inputs.name: ovomucoid_solution
available_inputs.description: Ovomucoid quenching reagent used to stop papain activity

available_inputs.name: coculture_medium_rock
available_inputs.description: Co-culture medium supplemented with 10 μM ROCK inhibitor (Y-27632)

available_inputs.name: cell_strainer_70um
available_inputs.description: Pre-wettable 70 μm cell strainer positioned over a 15 mL conical tube

available_inputs.name: macs_fcr_mix
available_inputs.description: MACS buffer mixed with FcR blocker, total labeling volume 87 μL per sample

available_inputs.name: o4_an2_microbeads
available_inputs.description: Anti-O4 and anti-AN2 microbead mixture, 13 μL per sample

available_inputs.name: macs_buffer
available_inputs.description: MACS wash buffer used for post-labeling wash

gold_program:
papain_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=papain_well_plate, temperature_c=37, duration_min=15, condition='humidified 5% CO2 static')
quenched_cells = mix_sample(sample=papain_incubated, method='pipetting with ovomucoid 1:1 to papain', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
rt_quenched_cells = incubate_sample(sample=quenched_cells, temperature_c=22, duration_min=1, condition='static')
first_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=rt_quenched_cells, speed_xg=400, duration_min=5, temperature_c=22)
resuspended_cells = mix_sample(sample=first_pellet, method='pipetting in co-culture medium with 10 uM ROCK inhibitor', duration_s=15, speed='gentle')
filtered_cells = filter_sample(sample=resuspended_cells, filter_type=cell_strainer_70um, pore_size_um=70, destination='15 mL conical tube')
second_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=filtered_cells, speed_xg=400, duration_min=5, temperature_c=22)
blocked_cells = mix_sample(sample=second_pellet, method='pipetting in MACS buffer/FcR blocker mixture 87 uL total', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
blocked_cells_cold = incubate_sample(sample=blocked_cells, temperature_c=4, duration_min=10, condition='static')
bead_labeled_cells = mix_sample(sample=blocked_cells_cold, method='pipetting with anti-O4/anti-AN2 microbeads 13 uL', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
bead_labeled_cells_cold = incubate_sample(sample=bead_labeled_cells, temperature_c=4, duration_min=30, condition='static')
washed_labeled_cells = mix_sample(sample=bead_labeled_cells_cold, method='pipetting with 1 mL MACS buffer wash', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
protocol_title: Three-dimensional co-culture platform of human induced pluripotent stem cell-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells and neurons for studying myelination

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Wash cells once with DMEM/F12.

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Add activated Papain to the cells (0.5 mL/well) and place the plate for 15 min in an incubator at 37°C and 5% CO 2 .

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Take out the plate from the incubator, add DMEM/F12 (1 mL/well) to the Papain-containing well, detach the cells mechanically with a P1000 and transfer cells into a 15 mL conical tube. Note: Cells of the same hiPSC line from different wells can be combined to reach to desired cell number. Note: It might be difficult to detach the cells from the 3D nanofibers. Apply pressure with a P1000 to detach the cells without harming the cells.

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Add Ovomucoid (ratio 1:1 Papain:Ovomucoid), mix gently by pipetting up and down a few times (P1000) and incubate for 1 min at RT (20°C–25°C).

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Centrifuge: 400 × g , 5 min, RT (20°C–25°C).

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Aspirate the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet with co-culture medium containing 10 μM ROCK inhibitor (Y-27632) (1.5 mL). Note: If you have low number of cells, you might not see a pellet.

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Filter cells through a 70 μm cell strainer. Pre-wet the strainer with co-culture medium containing 10 μM ROCK inhibitor (Y-27632) (0.5 mL).

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Filter cells through the 70 μm cell strainer over a 15 mL conical tube. Optional: If needed for future analysis (e.g., RT-qPCR), count cells and collect an aliquot of the “pre-MACSorting” condition: Take appropriate number of cells (e.g., 50.000–200.000 cells) and transfer them to a new conical tube, centrifuge (400 × g , 5 min, RT (20°C–25°C)), discard the supernatant (as much as possible) and e.g., freeze cell pellet or lyse cell pellet for future analysis.

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Centrifuge: 400 × g , 5 min, RT (20°C–25°C).

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Resuspend cells with MACS buffer/FcR blocker mixture (total volume per sample: 87 μL) and incubate for 10 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Add anti-O4/anti-AN2 microbeads mixture (total volume per sample: 13 μL), mix gently (do not vortex) and incubate for 30 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Wash by adding MACS buffer (1 mL/sample) to the mixture and mix gently by pipetting up and down a few times (P1000).

background: A crude Mycobacterium smegmatis lysate has already undergone initial clarification and ribosome pelleting. The current stage is the high-salt wash and polishing sequence used to strip weakly associated proteins from the ribosome preparation, re-pellet the cleaned ribosomes, and then set up a sucrose density gradient for final sedimentation. The work must preserve cold conditions and carry the ribosomal material forward through repeated clarification, ultracentrifugation, overnight cold holds, and gradient loading.
goal: Generate the workflow that takes the stored ribosomal pellet through high-salt dissolution, clarification, re-pelleting, overnight handling, gradient preparation, and final loading for the long ultracentrifugation run.
constraints: Maintain all centrifugation and cold-storage operations at 4 C, including the two overnight holds and the final gradient run.
Use 19.5 mL buffer C to dissolve the pellet during the high-salt wash, and include two sequential clarification spins at 13,000 x g for 45 min each with the supernatant carried forward.
After clarification, re-pellet the washed ribosome fraction at 150,000 x g for 150 min, then hold the pellet in 500 uL buffer B as an overnight intermediate before final loading.
Prepare and cold-store a 33 mL sucrose gradient in buffer B composed of 40%, 30%, 20%, and 10% layers corresponding to 12 mL, 8 mL, 7 mL, and 6 mL.
Before the final run, the stored pellet must be dissolved in 500 uL buffer B and applied onto the chilled gradient, followed by centrifugation at 150,000 x g for 270 min.

available_inputs.name: ribosome_pellet_tube
available_inputs.description: Centrifuge tube containing the initial ribosomal pellet recovered after the first ultracentrifugation.

available_inputs.name: buffer_b
available_inputs.description: Ribosome buffer B used for pellet storage, gradient preparation, and final pellet dissolution.

available_inputs.name: buffer_c
available_inputs.description: High-salt wash buffer used to remove weakly associated proteins from the ribosome pellet.

available_inputs.name: oak_ridge_tube
available_inputs.description: High-speed centrifuge tube suitable for the 13,000 x g clarification spins.

available_inputs.name: clarified_ribosome_tube
available_inputs.description: Large centrifuge tube suitable for ultracentrifugation of the washed ribosome fraction.

available_inputs.name: sucrose_gradient_tube
available_inputs.description: Fresh centrifuge tube used to build and store the layered sucrose density gradient.

gold_program:
high_salt_wash = add_reagent(sample=ribosome_pellet_tube, reagent=buffer_c, volume_ul=19500.0, mixing="pipette repeatedly to dissolve")
clarified_spin_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=high_salt_wash, speed_xg=13000, duration_min=45.0, temperature_c=4.0)
clarified_supernatant_1 = collect_fraction(sample=clarified_spin_1, phase="supernatant", destination=oak_ridge_tube, volume_ul=19500.0)
clarified_spin_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=clarified_supernatant_1, speed_xg=13000, duration_min=45.0, temperature_c=4.0)
clarified_supernatant_2 = collect_fraction(sample=clarified_spin_2, phase="supernatant", destination=clarified_ribosome_tube, volume_ul=19500.0)
washed_ribosome_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=clarified_supernatant_2, speed_xg=150000, duration_min=150.0, temperature_c=4.0)
cold_pellet_hold = add_reagent(sample=washed_ribosome_pellet, reagent=buffer_b, volume_ul=500.0, mixing="none")
chilled_pellet = store_sample(sample=cold_pellet_hold, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=16.0, container="centrifuge tube")
sucrose_gradient = add_reagent(sample=sucrose_gradient_tube, reagent="buffer B sucrose gradient 40% 12 mL + 30% 8 mL + 20% 7 mL + 10% 6 mL", volume_ul=33000.0, mixing="layer gently")
chilled_gradient = store_sample(sample=sucrose_gradient, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=16.0, container="centrifuge tube")
final_loaded_sample = add_reagent(sample=chilled_gradient, reagent="ribosomal pellet dissolved from chilled_pellet in 500 uL buffer B", volume_ul=500.0, mixing="overlay on top")
final_gradient_run = centrifuge_sample(sample=final_loaded_sample, speed_xg=150000, duration_min=270.0, temperature_c=4.0)
protocol_title: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
