source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Transfer the solution to an Oak Ridge tube and centrifuge at 13,000× g for 45 min at 4 °C.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Repeat the centrifugation step using a fresh Oak Ridge tube. These two rounds of high-speed centrifugation remove the debris formed after cell lysis.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Carefully collect the whole cell lysate and transfer to a 33 mL centrifuge tube. Subject this supernatant to ultracentrifugation at 150,000× g for 2 h and 30 min at 4 °C using a swing-out rotor.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Decant the supernatant and store the ribosomal pellet in the centrifuge tube at 4 °C by adding 500 μL of buffer B ( Figure 1A ) without resuspending. Cover the tube with parafilm to prevent contamination.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Add 19.5 mL of buffer C (high salt wash buffer) to the overnight pellet and pipette it repeatedly to ensure proper dissolving.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Transfer the mixture into a glass homogenizer and homogenize the solution for 1 h at every 5 min interval. This step is crucial for the removal of weakly interacting proteins from the ribosome.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Transfer the solution to an Oak Ridge tube and centrifuge at 13,000× g for 45 min at 4 °C. Collect the supernatant and repeat the centrifugation step using a fresh Oak Ridge tube.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Carefully collect the supernatant in a centrifuge tube and perform ultracentrifugation at 150,000× g for 2 h and 30 min at 4 °C using a swing-out rotor.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Decant the supernatant and store the pellet in the centrifuge tube at 4 °C by adding 500 μL of buffer B ( Figure 1B ). Cover the tube with parafilm to prevent contamination.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: In a fresh centrifuge tube, prepare a sucrose density gradient using buffer B. The bottom layer consists of 40% sucrose (12 mL), followed by 30% (w/v) sucrose (8 mL), 20% (w/v) sucrose (7 mL), and a top-most layer containing 10% (w/v) sucrose (6 mL). Keep this density gradient inside a 4 °C refrigerator overnight for next-day use ( Figure 1C ).

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Dissolve the overnight stored ribosomal pellet in 500 μL of buffer B by pipetting and overlay it on top of the sucrose density gradient.

source_steps.stage: Mycobacterium smegmatis Ribosome Purification, Co-sedimentation, and Subunit Association Assay
source_steps.description: Gently subject this gradient to ultracentrifugation at 150,000× g for 4 h and 30 min at 4 °C using a swing-out rotor.

background: Cells have already been transfected to induce the bait double-strand break and are now ready for recovery and genomic DNA preparation. This stage focuses on collecting and washing the cells, generating a pellet, carrying out proteinase K lysis under heated mixing, and initiating alcohol precipitation before moving the DNA pellet into 70% ethanol.
goal: Convert the harvested transfected cells into an ethanol-suspended genomic DNA pellet by performing the local recovery, wash, lysis, and precipitation segment of the workflow.
constraints: Begin from the harvested transfected cells and maintain a single continuous sample lineage through pellet recovery, wash, lysis, and precipitation handling.
Include two centrifugation steps at 300 x g for 5 min at 4 C, with a single PBS wash in between using 1 mL 1x PBS.
Represent pellet loosening and liquid mixing with available mixing actions only, and include heated lysis in a thermal mixer at 56 C and 500 rpm for an overnight duration within 10-16 h.

After lysis, model addition of 500 uL isopropanol with thorough mixing to form a visible DNA pellet, then finish by placing that pellet into a new tube containing 1 mL of 70% ethanol and mixing again.
Use only provided action-pool functions with keyword arguments, produce 6-12 top-level calls, and repeat at least two action names across the program.

available_inputs.name: harvested_cells
available_inputs.description: Transfected cells collected after trypsin quench in a 1.5 mL tube

available_inputs.name: pbs_1x
available_inputs.description: Sterile 1x PBS for washing the cell pellet

available_inputs.name: cell_lysis_master_mix
available_inputs.description: Prepared lysis solution containing 495 uL cell lysis buffer and 5 uL proteinase K per sample

available_inputs.name: isopropanol_500ul
available_inputs.description: 500 uL isopropanol for genomic DNA precipitation

available_inputs.name: ethanol70_tube
available_inputs.description: New 1.5 mL microtube preloaded with 1 mL 70% ethanol

gold_program:
pelleted_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=harvested_cells, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
washed_pellet = mix_sample(sample=pelleted_cells, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
pbs_washed_cells = mix_sample(sample=washed_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=15, speed="gentle")
re_pelleted_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=pbs_washed_cells, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
loosened_pellet = mix_sample(sample=re_pelleted_cells, method="pipetting", duration_s=8, speed="gentle")
lysis_loaded_sample = mix_sample(sample=loosened_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=15, speed="moderate")
lysed_gdna_sample = set_thermal_mixer(sample=lysis_loaded_sample, temperature_c=56, speed_rpm=500, duration_min=720)
precipitating_gdna = mix_sample(sample=lysed_gdna_sample, method="inversion", duration_s=20, speed="vigorous")
ethanol_transferred_pellet = mix_sample(sample=ethanol70_tube, method="inversion", duration_s=15, speed="moderate")
protocol_title: PEM-seq comprehensively quantifies DNA repair outcomes during gene-editing and DSB repair

source_steps.description: Add one diluted PEI (from ii) to one diluted DNA (from i), and the total volume should be less than 450 μL. Mix immediately with gentle flaps and incubate at room temperature for 15 min.
Dropwise add the DNA/PEI mixture to the cell culture by a pipette and put cells into a CO2 incubator for 8–12 h.
Remove the medium by aspiration and add 5 mL pre-warmed (37°C) DMEM/10% FBS medium.

Harvest transfected cells at 48–72 h post-transfection. Typically, over 0.1 million live cells are required for one PEM-seq library, and 1–3 million cells are recommended. Aspirate the cell culture medium, wash cells with 3 mL 1× PBS, and aspirate the PBS.
Add 400 μL 0.05% trypsin to cover all cells and incubate at 37°C for 2 min to allow cell detachment and separation.
Add 400 μL DMEM/10% FBS medium to quench trypsin and collect cells into a 1.5 mL tube.
Spin at 300 g, 4°C, for 5 min and remove supernatant.
Wash once with 1 mL 1× PBS, spin, and discard the supernatant.
Prepare cell lysis master mixture for each sample by mixing 495 μL of cell lysis buffer and 5 μL of proteinase K (20 mg/mL).
Loose the cell pellet with flaps, add 500 μL of cell lysis master mixture, and incubate them in a thermomixer at 56°C, 500 rpm, for overnight (10–16 h).
Add 500 μL of Isopropanol, mix immediately and thoroughly by inverting and shaking the microtube until the white pellet forms. Troubleshooting 1
Pick up the DNA pellet with a pipette tip, transfer it to a new 1.5 mL microtube containing 1 mL 70% (vol/vol) ethanol, and mix thoroughly by inverting and shaking.

background: This workflow is in the post-ligation cleanup and native barcoding preparation stage of MinION library preparation for Pseudomonas syringae complex samples from blueberry material. A DNA sample is already associated with cleanup beads, and the immediate tasks are to complete bead cleanup at room temperature, recover and quantify the eluate, hold it cold if needed, and then carry out the native barcode ligation setup and incubation.
goal: Complete cleanup of one bead-bound DNA sample, recover an eluted DNA fraction suitable for quantification and short-term storage, and finish the native barcode ligation incubation using that cleaned sample.
constraints: Include a room-temperature bead incubation of 5 min and a room-temperature native barcode ligation incubation of 20 min.
Use brief centrifugation steps at 5000 x g for 5 s each, represented as 0.083 min, at room temperature in more than one place in the program.
Model two repeated 80% ethanol bead washes and then a short drying step of about 30 s, normalized as 0.5 min, without implying harsh over-drying.

After elution in nuclease-free water, include a 2 min room-temperature incubation before final recovery-related handling, and later quantify the eluate with a Qubit-style measurement plus a recorded concentration QC note.
If the sample is not used immediately after quantification, store it at 4 C for an overnight-scale duration.
Keep the dependency chain local to this stage so that later barcode ligation uses the cleaned eluate-derived sample state rather than restarting from an unrelated raw input.

available_inputs.name: bead_bound_dna
available_inputs.description: DNA sample already bound to cleanup beads in a microcentrifuge tube.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_80
available_inputs.description: Freshly prepared 80% ethanol in nuclease-free water for bead washing.

available_inputs.name: nuclease_free_water
available_inputs.description: Water used for DNA elution.

available_inputs.name: barcode_reagents
available_inputs.description: Combined native barcode ligation reagents including ligase mix, barcode, EDTA, and short fragment buffer.

available_inputs.name: ligation_tube
available_inputs.description: Clean PCR tube containing the assembled barcode ligation reaction.

gold_program:
step1 = incubate_sample(sample=bead_bound_dna, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=5.0, condition="rocking")
step2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step1, speed_xg=5000, duration_min=0.083, temperature_c=22.0)
step3 = mix_sample(sample=step2, method="wash with 80% ethanol", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
step4 = mix_sample(sample=step3, method="wash with 80% ethanol", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
step5 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step4, speed_xg=5000, duration_min=0.083, temperature_c=22.0)
step6 = dry_sample(sample=step5, method="air dry", temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=0.5)
step7 = incubate_sample(sample=step6, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=2.0, condition="elute in nuclease-free water")
step8 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step7, speed_xg=5000, duration_min=0.083, temperature_c=22.0)
step9 = measure_signal(sample=step8, assay="Qubit dsDNA", wavelength_nm=0, replicate_n=1)
step10 = record_measurement(sample=step9, metric="DNA concentration", unit="ng_per_uL", note="10 uL eluate recovered after bead cleanup; suitable for equimolar barcode input")
step11 = store_sample(sample=step10, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=16.0, container="microcentrifuge_tube_1_5ml")
step12 = incubate_sample(sample=ligation_tube, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=20.0, condition="static")
protocol_title: MinION Nanopore‐Enabled Identification and Genomic Characterization of Pseudomonas syringae Complex Infecting Blueberry Using Symptomatic Plant Samples and Pathogen Pure Culture

source_steps.description: Incubate for 5 min on HulaMixer at room temperature.
Centrifuge samples 5 s at 5000 × g , room temperature, pellet beads on a magnetic rack, and carefully remove the supernatant.
Prepare freshly made 80% ethanol in nuclease‐free water, allowing 400 µl per sample plus some excess.
Keep tubes on the magnet and wash beads twice with 200 µl of 80% ethanol. If the pellet was disturbed, wait for beads to pellet again before removing the ethanol.

Centrifuge 5 s at 5000 × g , room temperature, place on magnet, remove residual ethanol, and dry for ∼30 s (do not crack pellet). Allow it to dry for 30 s, but do not dry the pellets to the point of cracking.
Remove the tubes from magnetic rack and elute DNA in 10 µl nuclease‐free water. Centrifuge 5 s at 5000 × g , room temperature, and incubate for 2 min at room temperature.
Pellet beads until elute is clear and colorless, transfer eluate (10 µl) to clean 1.5‐ml tube. Discard the pelleted beads.

Quantify 1 µl of the eluate using Qubit. Store samples at 4°C if proceeding to barcoding later. Take forward an equimolar mass of each sample to be barcoded forward into the native barcode ligation step. However, you may store the samples at 4°C overnight.
Thaw NEB Blunt/TA ligase master mix at room temperature, spin down, mix thoroughly by pipetting, and keep on ice.

Thaw the required Native Barcodes (NB01 to NB24), EDTA, and Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) at room temperature, mix, centrifuge 5 s at 5000 × g , room temperature, and store on ice. Only use one barcode per sample.
In clean 0.2‐ml PCR tube add the reagent in the order indicated in Table 2 per well.
Mix gently, centrifuge 5 s at 5000 × g , room temperature, and incubate for 20 min at room temperature.

background: This experiment quantifies membrane protein assembly by preparing a membrane-enriched pellet, exposing it to ligand or a matched solvent control, and then using DIBMA to extract membrane material while preserving native-like lipid context. The current segment begins after mechanical disruption of the pellet and focuses on cold ultracentrifugation, resuspension, treatment on ice, DIBMA-assisted solubilization, overnight cold vortexing, and a final high-speed clarification before keeping the desired end fraction for later single-molecule analysis.
goal: Carry the membrane fraction through the cold pelleting and DIBMA solubilization stage, including treatment/control handling and the final ultracentrifugation, so that the correct terminal fraction is retained for downstream use.
constraints: Use two ultracentrifugation steps at 100000 x g for 90 min each at 4 C, with later steps consuming the output of earlier ones.
After the first spin, continue with the pellet rather than the supernatant, and represent pellet resuspension to a 100-200 uL working volume before treatment-related incubation.
Include a 60 min treatment or matched solvent-control incubation at 4 C protected from light, with pipetting-style mixing represented in the program.
Model DIBMA addition as 1.5 uL of 10% DIBMA per 100 uL lysate, followed by another mixing step and then overnight vortex-based solubilization for 12-18 h at 4 C.

Before the second spin, carry forward the overnight-solubilized material as the clarified input fraction, and after that spin treat the supernatant as removed while retaining the pellet as the final stored sample.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keyword arguments only, 6-12 top-level calls total, and repeat at least two action names.

available_inputs.name: resuspended_pellet_400ul
available_inputs.description: Mechanically disrupted membrane pellet aliquot in an ultracentrifuge-compatible tube, total volume 400 uL.

available_inputs.name: resuspension_buffer
available_inputs.description: Buffer used to resuspend the membrane pellet after the first ultracentrifugation.

available_inputs.name: ligand_condition_sample
available_inputs.description: Sample branch intended for ligand exposure at the chosen concentration.

available_inputs.name: solvent_control_sample
available_inputs.description: Matched control branch receiving solvent without ligand.

available_inputs.name: dibma_reagent
available_inputs.description: 10% w/v DIBMA in 1x PBS, used at 1.5 uL per 100 uL lysate.

gold_program:
pelleted_membranes = centrifuge_sample(sample=resuspended_pellet_400ul, speed_xg=100000, duration_min=90.0, temperature_c=4.0)
resuspended_membrane_pellet = mix_sample(sample=pelleted_membranes, method='pipetting in resuspension buffer to 100-200 uL', duration_s=30.0, speed='medium')
ligand_treated_membranes = mix_sample(sample=resuspended_membrane_pellet, method='pipetting after ligand addition or matched solvent control', duration_s=45.0, speed='medium')
ligand_incubated_membranes = incubate_sample(sample=ligand_treated_membranes, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=60.0, condition='protected from light')
dibma_mixed_membranes = mix_sample(sample=ligand_incubated_membranes, method='pipetting after adding 1.5 uL 10% DIBMA per 100 uL lysate', duration_s=45.0, speed='medium')
overnight_solubilized_membranes = vortex_mix(sample=dibma_mixed_membranes, duration_s=57600.0, speed_rpm=1200, temperature_c=4.0)
clarified_solubilized_membranes = centrifuge_sample(sample=overnight_solubilized_membranes, speed_xg=100000, duration_min=90.0, temperature_c=4.0)
final_membrane_pellet = store_sample(sample=clarified_solubilized_membranes, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=1.0, container='fresh labeled 1.5 mL centrifuge tube with pellet retained')
protocol_title: SiMPull-POP: Quantification of Membrane Protein Assembly via Single Molecule Photobleaching

source_steps.stage: B. Cell lysis and membrane fractionation
source_steps.description: Resuspend pellet by pipetting a few times (recommended, 1 mL pipette tip) and then pass the sample through a 25-gauge needle 10 or more times until pellet is fragmented.

source_steps.stage: B. Cell lysis and membrane fractionation
source_steps.description: Transfer 400 μL of resuspended pellet(s) to a fresh 1.5 mL Beckman Coulter ultracentrifuge tube.

source_steps.stage: B. Cell lysis and membrane fractionation
source_steps.description: Ultracentrifuge 400 μL of resuspended pellet samples at 100,000× g (or 40,000 rpm for the TLA-55 rotor) for 1.5 h at 4 °C.

source_steps.stage: B. Cell lysis and membrane fractionation
source_steps.description: Carefully remove the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.

source_steps.stage: B. Cell lysis and membrane fractionation
source_steps.description: Resuspend pellet in 100–200 μL of resuspension buffer by passing through a 27-gauge syringe at least 10 times until there are no visible large clumps of the membrane pellet remaining.

source_steps.stage: C. Treatment and solubilization of the membrane pellet with DIBMA
source_steps.description: If testing a particular ligand influences the oligomerization of your membrane protein of interest, treat your resuspended pellet with ligand at the desired concentration. Add an equal volume of solvent (matching what the ligand is resuspended in) to samples not containing treatment with ligand (absence of ligand/treatment will serve as the control condition).

source_steps.stage: C. Treatment and solubilization of the membrane pellet with DIBMA
source_steps.description: Mix via pipetting 20–30 times. Leave on ice to incubate for 1 h protected from light.

source_steps.stage: C. Treatment and solubilization of the membrane pellet with DIBMA
source_steps.description: After the 1-h incubation, add 1.5 μL of 10% DIBMA (w/v in 1× PBS) per 100 μL of resuspended lysate. Mix by pipetting 20–30 times. An overview of the solubilization steps of the protocol is shown in Figure 3 .

source_steps.stage: C. Treatment and solubilization of the membrane pellet with DIBMA
source_steps.description: Parafilm the 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes and place them into a vortex adaptor for 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes. Vortex at medium speed overnight (12–18 h) at 4 °C.

source_steps.stage: C. Treatment and solubilization of the membrane pellet with DIBMA
source_steps.description: After overnight incubation, transfer at least 100 μL of the solution to a fresh 1.5 mL Beckman Coulter ultracentrifuge tube and keep on ice, protected from light, until ready for ultracentrifugation.

source_steps.stage: C. Treatment and solubilization of the membrane pellet with DIBMA
source_steps.description: Ultracentrifuge samples at 100,000× g (or 40,000 rpm for the TLA-55 rotor) for 1.5 h at 4 °C.

source_steps.stage: C. Treatment and solubilization of the membrane pellet with DIBMA
source_steps.description: After ultracentrifugation, remove the supernatant without disturbing the pellet, then transfer the pellet to a fresh 1.5 mL centrifuge tube. Label accordingly.

background: Adapter-ligated DNA fragments excised from a gel are being purified before control PCR. At this stage, nucleic acids must be recovered from crushed gel, clarified through a Spin-X filter, precipitated from the combined eluate, washed repeatedly with cold ethanol, dried, and resuspended to yield a clean library sample.
goal: Clean up the gel-recovered adapter-ligated library through extraction, precipitation, repeated cold-spin washing, drying, and resuspension so that purified DNA is ready for later amplification or storage.
constraints: Begin from crushed gel suspended in 0.3 M NaCl in 1X TE and heat at 70°C for 10 min to elute nucleic acids.
Use a Spin-X filter workflow with a 14000 x g centrifugation for 1 min at 25°C to clarify the slurry.
A second extraction of the remaining crushed gel is required, and the eluates should represent the combined recovery before precipitation.
Precipitation conditions include sodium acetate, glycogen, and isopropanol, followed by 30 s vortexing and incubation at -80°C for 16 h.
Pellet DNA by centrifugation at 21000 x g for 30 min at 4°C.
Wash the DNA pellet with cold 80% ethanol and centrifuge at 21000 x g for 5 min at 4°C.
The ethanol wash centrifugation must be repeated two additional times under the same conditions.
After washing, air dry the pellet for 5 min at 25°C.
Resuspend in nuclease-free water and allow 5 min at 25°C before pipette mixing to complete resuspension.
Use only action-pool operations and keep the workflow at the sample-processing level rather than instrument handling details.

available_inputs.name: crushed_gel_slurry
available_inputs.description: Initial crushed gel slurry in 0.3 M NaCl and 1X TE containing adapter-ligated nucleic acids.

available_inputs.name: remaining_crushed_gel
available_inputs.description: Residual crushed gel that undergoes a second elution pass.

available_inputs.name: precipitation_mix
available_inputs.description: Combined eluate after addition of sodium acetate, glycogen, and isopropanol.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_wash_pellet
available_inputs.description: DNA pellet resuspended or covered in cold 80% ethanol for wash spins.

available_inputs.name: nuclease_free_water
available_inputs.description: Water used for final DNA pellet resuspension.

gold_program:
heated_slurry_1 = heat_sample(sample=crushed_gel_slurry, temperature_c=70, duration_min=10, device="heat block")
clarified_eluate_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=heated_slurry_1, speed_xg=14000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
heated_slurry_2 = heat_sample(sample=remaining_crushed_gel, temperature_c=70, duration_min=10, device="heat block")
clarified_eluate_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=heated_slurry_2, speed_xg=14000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
vortexed_precipitation = vortex_mix(sample=precipitation_mix, duration_s=30, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=25)
frozen_precipitation = cool_sample(sample=vortexed_precipitation, temperature_c=-80, duration_min=960, device="freezer")
pelleted_dna = centrifuge_sample(sample=frozen_precipitation, speed_xg=21000, duration_min=30, temperature_c=4)
washed_pellet_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=ethanol_wash_pellet, speed_xg=21000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
washed_pellet_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_pellet_1, speed_xg=21000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
washed_pellet_3 = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_pellet_2, speed_xg=21000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=washed_pellet_3, method="air dry", temperature_c=25, duration_min=5)
pre_resuspension = incubate_sample(sample=dried_pellet, temperature_c=25, duration_min=5, condition="static")
final_library = mix_sample(sample=pre_resuspension, method="pipetting", duration_s=30, speed="moderate")

source_steps.stage: 3′-Adapter ligation, amplification, and gel purification of libraries
source_steps.description: Elute nucleic acids from gel by crushing gel slices as described in ( Vvedenskaya and Nickels, 2020 ), adding 350 μL of 0.3 M NaCl in 1 X TE Buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8, 0.1 mM EDTA) to the crushed gel, and incubating the slurry for 10 min at 70°C.

source_steps.stage: 3′-Adapter ligation, amplification, and gel purification of libraries
source_steps.description: Transfer the slurry to a Spin-X centrifuge tube filter, centrifuge (14,000 × g ; 1 min; 25°C) and transfer the eluate into a 1.7 mL tube.

source_steps.stage: 3′-Adapter ligation, amplification, and gel purification of libraries
source_steps.description: Remove the crushed gel from the Spin-X tube and perform steps 13.e-f one more time. Combine eluates (total volume ∼700 μL).

source_steps.stage: 3′-Adapter ligation, amplification, and gel purification of libraries
source_steps.description: Add 70 μL of 3 M NaOAc, 7.7 μL of glycogen (10 mg/mL) and 650 mL of isopropanol, mix by vortexing for 30 s, and incubate at −80°C for 16 h.

source_steps.stage: 3′-Adapter ligation, amplification, and gel purification of libraries
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the mixture to pellet DNA (21,000 × g ; 30 min; 4°C) and remove supernatant.

source_steps.stage: 3′-Adapter ligation, amplification, and gel purification of libraries
source_steps.description: Wash pellet in 1 mL of 80% EtOH kept at 4°C. Centrifuge (21,000 × g ; 5 min; 4°C) and remove supernatant.

source_steps.stage: 3′-Adapter ligation, amplification, and gel purification of libraries
source_steps.description: Perform steps 13.j two more times.

source_steps.stage: 3′-Adapter ligation, amplification, and gel purification of libraries
source_steps.description: Air dry pellets for 5 min at 25°C.

source_steps.stage: 3′-Adapter ligation, amplification, and gel purification of libraries
source_steps.description: Add 15 μL nuclease-free H 2 O to DNA pellet. Incubate at 25°C for 5 min and pipette the sample 10–15 times to ensure the full resuspension of the nucleic acids. Pause point: Purified adapter-ligated products can be stored at −80°C for ∼6 months.

source_steps.stage: 3′-Adapter ligation, amplification, and gel purification of libraries
source_steps.description: Control PCR amplification of adapter-ligated products Next, a control PCR amplification is performed to estimate the yield and quality of a library generated from the adapter-ligated products. Prepare control PCR reaction mix as indicated below: Reagent Final concentration Amount Adapter-ligated products from step 13.m n/a 2 μL 5 X HF Phusion Buffer with MgCl 2 1 X 2 μL 10 mM dNTP mix 0.2 mM 0.2 μL 2.5 μM Illumina RP1 Primer 0.25 μM 1 μL 2.5 μM Illumina Index Primer (RPI1-48) 0.25 μM 1 μL HF Phusion DNA Polymerase, 2 U/μL 0.2 U 0.1 μL Nuclease-free H 2 O n/a 3.7 μL Total n/a 10 μL

source_steps.stage: 3′-Adapter ligation, amplification, and gel purification of libraries
source_steps.description: Perform reactions using cycling conditions below: PCR cycling conditions Steps Temperature Time Cycles Initial Denaturation 98°C 30 s 1 Denaturation 98°C 10 s 12 cycles Annealing 62°C 20 s Extension 72°C 10 s Final extension 72°C 5 min 1 Hold 4°C forever

source_steps.stage: 3′-Adapter ligation, amplification, and gel purification of libraries
source_steps.description: Mix 10 μL of amplified DNA with 3 μL of 6 × Orange DNA load dye and run a non-denaturing 10% TBE gel at the conditions described at step 13.b. Load O’Gene Ruler Ultra Low Range DNA ladder as size standard. Run gel until yellow dye reaches gel bottom.

background: Human iPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells have already been detached from 3D nanofibers after papain treatment. At this point, the workflow is focused on stopping the digestion, recovering the cells by repeated spins, removing aggregates through straining, and carrying out cold microbead-labeling preparation prior to later enrichment.
goal: Prepare the detached oligodendrocyte-lineage cell suspension for downstream enrichment by completing the cleanup, filtration, and antibody-microbead labeling segment under the required temperature and centrifugation conditions.
constraints: Quench the papain-treated suspension with ovomucoid using a 1:1 papain-to-ovomucoid relationship, then hold only briefly for 1 min at room temperature.
Perform two room-temperature centrifugation steps at 400 x g for 5 min during cleanup, followed by a final centrifugation at 300 x g for 5 min after the labeling wash.
Before filtration, recover the pellet in co-culture medium containing 10 uM ROCK inhibitor using 1.5 mL total volume.
Use a 70 um cell strainer and pre-wet it with 0.5 mL co-culture medium containing 10 uM ROCK inhibitor before filtering into a 15 mL conical tube.
After the post-filtration recovery spin, expose the cells to MACS buffer with FcR blocker for 10 min at 4 C, then to anti-O4/anti-AN2 microbeads for 30 min at 4 C.
Use gentle pipetting-based mixing where mixing is needed; do not use vortexing.
Include a 1 mL MACS buffer wash after bead labeling and ensure later operations act on products of earlier ones.

available_inputs.name: papain_detached_cells
available_inputs.description: Detached oligodendrocyte lineage cells in papain-containing suspension after release from 3D nanofibers.

available_inputs.name: ovomucoid
available_inputs.description: Protease inhibitor solution used to stop papain digestion.

available_inputs.name: rock_coculture_medium
available_inputs.description: Co-culture medium supplemented with 10 uM ROCK inhibitor.

available_inputs.name: cell_strainer_70um
available_inputs.description: 70 um cell strainer for removal of large aggregates.

available_inputs.name: macs_fcr_mix
available_inputs.description: MACS buffer mixed with FcR blocker for cold pre-labeling incubation.

available_inputs.name: o4_an2_microbeads
available_inputs.description: Anti-O4 and anti-AN2 magnetic microbead mixture.

available_inputs.name: macs_buffer
available_inputs.description: MACS buffer used for washing labeled cells.

gold_program:
quenched_cells = mix_sample(sample='papain_detached_cells_with_ovomucoid_1to1', method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
quenched_hold = incubate_sample(sample=quenched_cells, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=1.0, condition='static')
cleanup_spin1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=quenched_hold, speed_xg=400, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=22.0)
rock_resuspension = mix_sample(sample=cleanup_spin1, method='pipetting', duration_s=15.0, speed='gentle')
prewet_strainer = mix_sample(sample='cell_strainer_70um_prewet_with_rock_coculture_medium_0.5mL', method='pipetting', duration_s=5.0, speed='gentle')
filtered_cells = filter_sample(sample=rock_resuspension, filter_type='cell_strainer', pore_size_um=70.0, destination='15mL_conical_tube')
cleanup_spin2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=filtered_cells, speed_xg=400, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=22.0)
fcr_blocked = incubate_sample(sample=cleanup_spin2, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=10.0, condition='static')
bead_labeled = incubate_sample(sample=fcr_blocked, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=30.0, condition='static')
label_wash = mix_sample(sample=bead_labeled, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
final_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=label_wash, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=22.0)

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Add activated Papain to the cells (0.5 mL/well) and place the plate for 15 min in an incubator at 37°C and 5% CO 2 .

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Take out the plate from the incubator, add DMEM/F12 (1 mL/well) to the Papain-containing well, detach the cells mechanically with a P1000 and transfer cells into a 15 mL conical tube. Note: Cells of the same hiPSC line from different wells can be combined to reach to desired cell number. Note: It might be difficult to detach the cells from the 3D nanofibers. Apply pressure with a P1000 to detach the cells without harming the cells.

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Add Ovomucoid (ratio 1:1 Papain:Ovomucoid), mix gently by pipetting up and down a few times (P1000) and incubate for 1 min at RT (20°C–25°C).

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Centrifuge: 400 × g , 5 min, RT (20°C–25°C).

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Aspirate the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet with co-culture medium containing 10 μM ROCK inhibitor (Y-27632) (1.5 mL). Note: If you have low number of cells, you might not see a pellet.

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Filter cells through a 70 μm cell strainer. Pre-wet the strainer with co-culture medium containing 10 μM ROCK inhibitor (Y-27632) (0.5 mL).

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Filter cells through the 70 μm cell strainer over a 15 mL conical tube. Optional: If needed for future analysis (e.g., RT-qPCR), count cells and collect an aliquot of the “pre-MACSorting” condition: Take appropriate number of cells (e.g., 50.000–200.000 cells) and transfer them to a new conical tube, centrifuge (400 × g , 5 min, RT (20°C–25°C)), discard the supernatant (as much as possible) and e.g., freeze cell pellet or lyse cell pellet for future analysis.

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Centrifuge: 400 × g , 5 min, RT (20°C–25°C).

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Resuspend cells with MACS buffer/FcR blocker mixture (total volume per sample: 87 μL) and incubate for 10 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Add anti-O4/anti-AN2 microbeads mixture (total volume per sample: 13 μL), mix gently (do not vortex) and incubate for 30 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Wash by adding MACS buffer (1 mL/sample) to the mixture and mix gently by pipetting up and down a few times (P1000).

source_steps.stage: Detachment and isolation of hiPSC-derived oligodendrocyte lineage cells from 3D nanofibers
source_steps.description: Centrifuge: 300 × g , 5 min.

background: In this stage of the BD Rhapsody workflow, a post-incubation tube undergoes random priming and extension with a sequence of thermomixer incubations, while the Sample Tag material is amplified in parallel by a first PCR. After the extension incubations, the bead-containing tube is briefly spun down, subjected to magnetic separation, and then returned to buffer for bead resuspension. The task focuses on executing the incubation series and the downstream centrifuge/elution handling, while also running the Sample Tag PCR1 program on a prepared PCR strip.
goal: Process the extension reaction through the required temperature-shaking series, run the first Sample Tag PCR on the prepared strip using the cycle count appropriate for the retrieved cell number, and then complete the immediate post-incubation cleanup through brief centrifugation, magnetic separation handling, and elution-buffer resuspension.
constraints: Begin from a tube that has already received the primer extension mix and requires a short centrifugation before the temperature series.
Use a thermomixer-style action for four incubations at 1200 rpm, with temperatures 25, 37, 45, and 55 degrees Celsius and durations 10, 15, 10, and 10 minutes respectively.
The Sample Tag PCR1 should be run on a PCR strip that has already been prepared and distributed into four 50 uL reactions, using 11 cycles for the retrieved-cell band in this item.
After the incubation series, perform a brief centrifugation of the extension tube, then represent the magnet step as a short static hold of no more than 2 minutes before the elution stage.
The downstream state must reflect addition of 205 uL elution buffer followed by bead resuspension, abstracted only with provided action-pool operations.
Use only the provided functions with keyword arguments, keep 6-12 top-level calls, and ensure at least two action names recur with later steps consuming earlier outputs.

available_inputs.name: extension_tube
available_inputs.description: Tube containing the post-incubation extension reaction after primer extension mix addition.

available_inputs.name: sample_tag_pcr_strip
available_inputs.description: PCR strip containing four aliquots of the prepared Sample Tag PCR1 reaction.

available_inputs.name: retrieved_cell_count_band
available_inputs.description: Cell-recovery category indicating the PCR cycle setting to use; here it corresponds to the >10000-cell recommendation.

available_inputs.name: thermomixer_device
available_inputs.description: Thermomixer used for the sequential temperature and shaking incubations.

available_inputs.name: thermal_cycler_device
available_inputs.description: Thermal cycler used for the Sample Tag PCR1 run.

gold_program:
extension_spun_initial = centrifuge_sample(sample=extension_tube, speed_xg=500, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=25)
extension_tm_25 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=extension_spun_initial, temperature_c=25, speed_rpm=1200, duration_min=10)
extension_tm_37 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=extension_tm_25, temperature_c=37, speed_rpm=1200, duration_min=15)
pcr1_run = run_thermal_cycler(plate=sample_tag_pcr_strip, program_name="Sample_Tag_PCR1", cycles=11, lid_temperature_c=105)
extension_tm_45 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=extension_tm_37, temperature_c=45, speed_rpm=1200, duration_min=10)
extension_tm_55 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=extension_tm_45, temperature_c=55, speed_rpm=1200, duration_min=10)
extension_spun_post = centrifuge_sample(sample=extension_tm_55, speed_xg=500, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=25)
extension_on_magnet = incubate_sample(sample=extension_spun_post, temperature_c=25, duration_min=2, condition="static on magnet")
extension_eluted = incubate_sample(sample=extension_on_magnet, temperature_c=25, duration_min=1, condition="elution buffer added to 205 uL total")
extension_resuspended = set_thermal_mixer(sample=extension_eluted, temperature_c=25, speed_rpm=1200, duration_min=1)
pcr1_held = store_sample(sample=pcr1_run, temperature_c=4, duration_h=16, container="thermal cycler hold")

background: Crosslinked His-tagged RNAP–DNA complexes have already been eluted from Ni-NTA resin and now need to be concentrated, exchanged into storage-compatible buffer, supplemented with glycerol, and then prepared for the denaturation/rebinding cleanup stage that removes non-crosslinked DNA. This stage relies on repeated centrifugal concentration at cold temperature followed by heat denaturation, cooling, binding to MagneHis Ni-particles, and magnetic recovery before a high-salt wash.
goal: Process the eluted crosslinked complex through cold concentration and buffer exchange, prepare it for glycerol storage, then carry out the denaturation and MagneHis bead-binding cleanup through the first magnetic recovery and wash incubation.
constraints: Use centrifugal concentration twice on the eluate-related sample with 100K MWCO handling implied by the context, each at 3000 x g, 4°C, and for a duration within 7–12 min.
A storage-buffer exchange must occur between the two cold concentration operations.
After the second concentration, add an equal volume of 100% glycerol and mix thoroughly before storage.
Store the glycerol-supplemented material at −80°C before the denaturation/rebinding cleanup portion.
For the cleanup portion, the complex mixture must be heat denatured at 95°C for 5 min and then cooled to 4°C for 5 min before contact with MagneHis Ni-particles.
Binding to MagneHis Ni-particles must occur at 25°C for 10 min.
A magnetic separation step must retain the bead phase and discard supernatant-equivalent material.
After magnetic recovery, perform a wash incubation at 25°C for 10 min using the bead-bound material.
Use only the provided action-pool functions and keep the workflow as a single dependency chain rather than restarting from raw material midstream.

available_inputs.name: eluate_sample
available_inputs.description: Eluted His-tagged crosslinked complexes after Ni-NTA elution.

available_inputs.name: storage_buffer_mix
available_inputs.description: 2X storage buffer added for buffer exchange prior to reconcentration.

available_inputs.name: glycerol_100
available_inputs.description: 100% glycerol used to supplement the concentrated eluate.

available_inputs.name: denaturation_mix
available_inputs.description: Prepared aliquoted complex mixture containing water, NaCl, and heparin for heat denaturation.

available_inputs.name: magnehis_particles
available_inputs.description: Equilibrated MagneHis Ni-particles ready to capture denatured crosslinked complexes.

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer
available_inputs.description: High-salt wash buffer containing Tris-Cl, NaCl, MgCl2, and heparin.

gold_program:
conc_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=eluate_sample, speed_xg=3000, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
buffer_exchanged = mix_sample(sample=conc_1, method="pipetting", duration_s=20.0, speed="moderate")
conc_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=buffer_exchanged, speed_xg=3000, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
glycerol_added = mix_sample(sample=conc_2, method="pipetting", duration_s=20.0, speed="moderate")
stored_complex = store_sample(sample=glycerol_added, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_h=1.0, container="storage_tube")
denatured = heat_sample(sample=denaturation_mix, temperature_c=95.0, duration_min=5.0, device="heat_block")
cooled = cool_sample(sample=denatured, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=5.0, device="ice_bath")
bound_beads = incubate_sample(sample=cooled, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=10.0, condition="static")
bead_phase = magnetic_separate(sample=bound_beads, rack="MagneSphere magnetic separation stand", duration_min=1.0, collect_phase="beads")
washed_beads = incubate_sample(sample=bead_phase, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=10.0, condition="static")

source_steps.stage: Isolation of crosslinked complexes from cell lysates
source_steps.description: Add 3 mL of 1 X Ni-NTA Elution Buffer containing 300 mM imidazole, incubate 1 min and elute His-tagged proteins from the resin.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of crosslinked complexes from cell lysates
source_steps.description: Concentrate the eluate to ∼0.1–0.2 mL by centrifugation (3000 × g ; 7–12 min, 4°C) using 100K MWCO Amicon Ultra-4 centrifugal filter.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of crosslinked complexes from cell lysates
source_steps.description: To perform buffer exchange, add 2 mL of 2 X Storage Buffer to the concentrated eluate. Reduce the volume of the mixture to ∼0.1–0.2 mL by centrifugation (3000 × g ; 7–12 min, 4°C).

source_steps.stage: Isolation of crosslinked complexes from cell lysates
source_steps.description: Repeat step 7.j.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of crosslinked complexes from cell lysates
source_steps.description: Add an equal volume of 100% glycerol (∼0.1–0.2 mL) to the concentrated eluate. Mix thoroughly by pipetting and store sample at −80°C.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of crosslinked complexes from cell lysates
source_steps.description: Heat denaturation of crosslinked complexes and binding to MagneHis Ni-particles The next step of the protocol involves heat denaturation of crosslinked complexes followed by binding to MagneHis Ni-particles. This step removes non-crosslinked DNA from the reaction mixture. Aliquot 20 μL MagneHis Ni-particles in two 1.5 mL tubes. Remove storage solution and equilibrate the particles in 50 μL of 1 X Taq DNA polymerase buffer using MagneSphere magnetic separation stand (Promega). Repeat equilibration step. Keep tubes on ice while preparing the crosslinked complexes for binding with MagneHis Ni-particles.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of crosslinked complexes from cell lysates
source_steps.description: Set up two tubes containing 25 μL of the eluate prepared in step 7.l. Add 25 μL of nuclease-free H 2 O, 12 μL of 5 M NaCl, and 0.5 μL of 1 mg/mL heparin to each tube.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of crosslinked complexes from cell lysates
source_steps.description: Incubate the mixtures at 95°C for 5 min to denature dsDNA. Cool to 4°C and incubate for 5 min.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of crosslinked complexes from cell lysates
source_steps.description: Add the denatured mixture to MagneHis Ni-particles from step 8.a.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of crosslinked complexes from cell lysates
source_steps.description: To bind the crosslinked complexes to beads, incubate for 10 min at 25°C. Gently flick the tube 2–3 times every 2 min to mix the sample.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of crosslinked complexes from cell lysates
source_steps.description: Collect the beads using a magnetic separation stand. Discard the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of crosslinked complexes from cell lysates
source_steps.description: Add 50 μL of wash buffer containing 10 mM Tris-Cl pH 8.0, 1.2 M NaCl, 10 mM MgCl 2 and 10 μg/mL heparin. Incubate 10 min at 25°C. Gently flick the tube 2–3 times every 2 min to mix the sample.

background: A transcriptome-wide RNA–small-molecule profiling experiment in human cells has reached the total RNA isolation stage after organic extraction. The next operations recover the RNA-containing aqueous phase, condition it for column binding, perform on-column DNA removal and washes, and finish with RNase-free elution for downstream analysis.
goal: Convert the post-extraction sample into purified total cellular RNA by carrying out the remaining extraction cleanup and elution operations.
constraints: Add 1200 uL chloroform, vortex for 15 s at about 20 C, then incubate 5 min at 20 C before the phase-separation spin.
Use a 2500 x g centrifugation for 15 min at 4 C, then continue only with the upper aqueous phase transferred into a fresh vessel.
Before column cleanup, add 1 volume of 96%–100% ethanol to the recovered aqueous fraction and mix thoroughly.
Model the cleanup using repeated column-processing steps at 10000 x g and 20 C, including DNase treatment with 80 uL digestion mix and a 15 min incubation.
Include both a short 0.5 min cleanup spin and a longer 2 min spin for complete wash-buffer removal.
Finish with two 200 uL water elutions into a 1.5 mL RNase-free tube context.

available_inputs.name: phase_sep_sample
available_inputs.description: Post-lysis extraction mixture awaiting chloroform phase separation.

available_inputs.name: chloroform
available_inputs.description: Chloroform for phase separation.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_100
available_inputs.description: 96% to 100% ethanol for RNA binding preparation.

available_inputs.name: zymo_spin_column
available_inputs.description: Zymo-Spin V-E column used for RNA purification.

available_inputs.name: rna_wash_buffer
available_inputs.description: Wash buffer for the RNA purification column.

available_inputs.name: dna_digestion_buffer
available_inputs.description: DNA digestion mix containing DNase I.

available_inputs.name: rna_prep_buffer
available_inputs.description: RNA Prep Buffer used during cleanup.

available_inputs.name: rnase_free_water
available_inputs.description: RNase-free water for RNA elution.

gold_program:
step1 = add_reagent(sample=phase_sep_sample, reagent=chloroform, volume_ul=1200.0, mixing='none')
step2 = vortex_mix(sample=step1, duration_s=15.0, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=20.0)
step3 = incubate_sample(sample=step2, temperature_c=20.0, duration_min=5.0, condition='static')
step4 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step3, speed_xg=2500, duration_min=15.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step5 = aliquot_sample(sample=step4, destination='fresh_conical_tube_aqueous_phase', volume_ul=4000.0, aliquot_count=1)
step6 = add_reagent(sample=step5, reagent=ethanol_100, volume_ul=4000.0, mixing='vortex')
step7 = aliquot_sample(sample=step6, destination=zymo_spin_column, volume_ul=8000.0, aliquot_count=1)
step8 = add_reagent(sample=step7, reagent=dna_digestion_buffer, volume_ul=80.0, mixing='none')
step9 = incubate_sample(sample=step8, temperature_c=20.0, duration_min=15.0, condition='static')
step10 = add_reagent(sample=step9, reagent=rna_prep_buffer, volume_ul=400.0, mixing='none')
step11 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step10, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=20.0)
step12 = add_reagent(sample=step11, reagent=rnase_free_water, volume_ul=400.0, mixing='none')
protocol_title: Reactivity-based RNA profiling for analyzing transcriptome interactions of small molecules in human cells

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Add 1.2 mL of chloroform and vortex for 15 s at 20°C.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Incubate the sample at 20°C for 5 min.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 2,500 × g for 15 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Transfer the aqueous layer on the top to a new 15 mL conical vial. Note: The top aqueous layer should be collected carefully using a pipette. Avoid making contact or disturbing the organic layer. It’s preferred to tilt the vial to maximize the recovery of the aqueous layer during the collection.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Add 1× volume of 96%–100% of ethanol and vortex to mix thoroughly.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Extraction of total cellular RNA. Transfer the mixture to a Zymo-Spin V-E Column (binding capacity: 1 mg of RNA) from Zymo Quick-RNA Midiprep kit (Zymo, Cat#R1056) with reservoir on a vacuum manifold. Note: The vacuum manifold should be attached to a vacuum pump that is ideally capable of producing at least 400 mm Hg pressure. This step can also be performed on a centrifuge as an alternative, but it is not recommended as it requires extensive rounds of centrifugation. Steps 8b-f are performed with a microcentrifuge at 20°C.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Digest genomic DNAs. Add 400 μL of RNA Wash Buffer to the Zymo-Spin V-E Column. Centrifuge at 10,000 × g for 30 s at 20°C.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Add 80 μL of DNA Digestion Buffer containing 5 μL of DNase I (1 U/μL) and incubate for 15 min at 20°C. Centrifuge at 10,000 × g for 30 s at 20°C.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Add 400 μL RNA Prep Buffer to the column and centrifuge. Discard the flow-through.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Add 400 μL RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge. Discard the flow-through.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Add 400 μL RNA Wash Buffer and centrifuge for 2 min for complete removal of the wash buffer.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Transfer the column to a 1.5 mL RNase-free conical tube. Elute RNA from the column twice with 200 μL of water per elution (final ∼400 μL).

background: This experiment quantifies membrane protein assembly after enriching a membrane fraction and solubilizing it with DIBMA in a near-native state. The current stage begins with a membrane pellet already recovered from ultracentrifugation. That pellet must be washed and re-pelleted, then resuspended thoroughly before setting up ligand and solvent-control conditions. After a cold light-protected incubation, DIBMA is introduced and the sample is vortexed overnight at 4 C so an aliquot can be held for the next ultracentrifugation.
goal: Carry the membrane pellet through the second wash, re-pelleting, resuspension, treatment incubation, and DIBMA solubilization sequence, ending with a cold protected aliquot ready for the next spin.
constraints: Use 400 uL fresh lysis buffer with fresh protease and phosphatase inhibitors to resuspend the starting membrane pellet, then perform a cold ultracentrifugation at 100000 x g for 90 min at 4 C.
After the second spin, resuspend the pellet in resuspension buffer to a final volume in the 100-200 uL range and mix until no large clumps remain.
Include both a ligand-treated sample and a matched solvent-only control, with 60 min incubation at 4 C under light-protected conditions before DIBMA is added.
Maintain the ratio of 1.5 uL of 10% DIBMA per 100 uL lysate and perform overnight vortex-based solubilization at 4 C for 12-18 h.
End with at least 100 uL in a fresh 1.5 mL ultracentrifuge-compatible tube kept on ice and protected from light.

available_inputs.name: membrane_pellet
available_inputs.description: Membrane pellet remaining after the previous ultracentrifugation.

available_inputs.name: fresh_lysis_buffer
available_inputs.description: Fresh lysis buffer supplemented with protease and phosphatase inhibitors.

available_inputs.name: resuspension_buffer
available_inputs.description: Buffer used for final membrane pellet resuspension.

available_inputs.name: ligand_tube
available_inputs.description: Resuspended membrane sample assigned to ligand treatment.

available_inputs.name: control_tube
available_inputs.description: Resuspended membrane sample assigned to matched solvent control.

available_inputs.name: dibma_reagent
available_inputs.description: 10% DIBMA in 1x PBS.

gold_program:
washed_pellet = mix_sample(sample=membrane_pellet, method='pipetting in 400 uL fresh lysis buffer', duration_s=30.0, speed='moderate')
fragmented_pellet = mix_sample(sample=washed_pellet, method='repeated pipetting and needle-style shearing', duration_s=60.0, speed='moderate')
repelleted_membranes = centrifuge_sample(sample=fragmented_pellet, speed_xg=100000, duration_min=90.0, temperature_c=4.0)
resuspended_membranes = mix_sample(sample=repelleted_membranes, method='needle-style resuspension in 150 uL resuspension buffer', duration_s=60.0, speed='moderate')
ligand_condition_prepared = mix_sample(sample=ligand_tube, method='pipetting with ligand added to resuspended membranes', duration_s=30.0, speed='moderate')
control_condition_prepared = mix_sample(sample=control_tube, method='pipetting with matched solvent added to resuspended membranes', duration_s=30.0, speed='moderate')
ligand_condition_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=ligand_condition_prepared, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=60.0, condition='protected from light')
control_condition_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=control_condition_prepared, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=60.0, condition='protected from light')
ligand_dibma_mixed = mix_sample(sample=ligand_condition_incubated, method='pipetting after adding 1.5 uL 10% DIBMA per 100 uL lysate', duration_s=30.0, speed='moderate')
control_dibma_mixed = mix_sample(sample=control_condition_incubated, method='pipetting after adding 1.5 uL 10% DIBMA per 100 uL lysate', duration_s=30.0, speed='moderate')
ligand_solubilized = vortex_mix(sample=ligand_dibma_mixed, duration_s=43200.0, speed_rpm=1200, temperature_c=4.0)
control_ready = store_sample(sample=ligand_solubilized, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=1.0, container='fresh 1.5 mL ultracentrifuge tube on ice protected from light, at least 100 uL')
protocol_title: SiMPull-POP: Quantification of Membrane Protein Assembly via Single Molecule Photobleaching

source_steps.stage: B. Cell lysis and membrane fractionation
source_steps.description: After the ultracentrifugation, carefully remove the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.

source_steps.stage: B. Cell lysis and membrane fractionation
source_steps.description: Add 400 μL of fresh lysis buffer (containing fresh protease and phosphatase inhibitors) to the pellet.

source_steps.stage: B. Cell lysis and membrane fractionation
source_steps.description: Resuspend pellet by pipetting a few times (recommended, 1 mL pipette tip) and then pass the sample through a 25-gauge needle 10 or more times until pellet is fragmented.

source_steps.stage: B. Cell lysis and membrane fractionation
source_steps.description: Transfer 400 μL of resuspended pellet(s) to a fresh 1.5 mL Beckman Coulter ultracentrifuge tube.

source_steps.stage: B. Cell lysis and membrane fractionation
source_steps.description: Ultracentrifuge 400 μL of resuspended pellet samples at 100,000× g (or 40,000 rpm for the TLA-55 rotor) for 1.5 h at 4 °C.

source_steps.stage: B. Cell lysis and membrane fractionation
source_steps.description: Carefully remove the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.

source_steps.stage: B. Cell lysis and membrane fractionation
source_steps.description: Resuspend pellet in 100–200 μL of resuspension buffer by passing through a 27-gauge syringe at least 10 times until there are no visible large clumps of the membrane pellet remaining.

source_steps.stage: C. Treatment and solubilization of the membrane pellet with DIBMA
source_steps.description: If testing a particular ligand influences the oligomerization of your membrane protein of interest, treat your resuspended pellet with ligand at the desired concentration. Add an equal volume of solvent (matching what the ligand is resuspended in) to samples not containing treatment with ligand (absence of ligand/treatment will serve as the control condition).

source_steps.stage: C. Treatment and solubilization of the membrane pellet with DIBMA
source_steps.description: Mix via pipetting 20–30 times. Leave on ice to incubate for 1 h protected from light.

source_steps.stage: C. Treatment and solubilization of the membrane pellet with DIBMA
source_steps.description: After the 1-h incubation, add 1.5 μL of 10% DIBMA (w/v in 1× PBS) per 100 μL of resuspended lysate. Mix by pipetting 20–30 times. An overview of the solubilization steps of the protocol is shown in Figure 3 .

source_steps.stage: C. Treatment and solubilization of the membrane pellet with DIBMA
source_steps.description: Parafilm the 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes and place them into a vortex adaptor for 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes. Vortex at medium speed overnight (12–18 h) at 4 °C.

source_steps.stage: C. Treatment and solubilization of the membrane pellet with DIBMA
source_steps.description: After overnight incubation, transfer at least 100 μL of the solution to a fresh 1.5 mL Beckman Coulter ultracentrifuge tube and keep on ice, protected from light, until ready for ultracentrifugation.

background: HeLa cells have already been harvested after infection and are ready for the fractionation stage used to assess PARP1 localization biochemically. The immediate work is to separate cytoplasmic material from the nuclear pellet under cold conditions, wash the pellet repeatedly, extract nuclear proteins, and prepare both fractions for later western blotting.
goal: Generate cytoplasmic and nuclear protein fractions from a washed HeLa cell pellet and finish with denatured samples suitable for downstream PARP1 detection.
constraints: Begin with a PBS-washed cell pellet in a 1.5 mL tube and keep fractionation steps cold or on ice.
Model Buffer A treatment as a cold extraction step lasting 30 min before centrifugation.
Use a high-speed centrifugation of 15000 x g for 6 min to separate the cytoplasmic fraction.
Include repeated wash handling of the remaining pellet with a low-speed centrifugation at 1000 x g for 5 min; represent multiple wash cycles.
Model Buffer B nuclear extraction as a 30 min cold period with intermittent vigorous vortexing.
Use three vortex events of 10-15 s each during the nuclear extraction stage.
Collect nuclear extract after centrifugation at 15000 x g for 15 min under refrigerated conditions.
Denature both prepared fractions at 95 C for 8 min.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keyword arguments only, and keep later steps dependent on earlier outputs.
The program must contain between 7 and 13 top-level function calls.

available_inputs.description: PBS-washed HeLa cell pellet collected in a 1.5 mL reaction tube

available_inputs.name: buffer_a_mix
available_inputs.description: Pre-cooled Buffer A supplemented with protease inhibitor cocktail and DTT

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer_mix
available_inputs.description: Pre-cooled wash buffer supplemented with protease inhibitor cocktail and DTT

available_inputs.name: buffer_b_mix
available_inputs.description: Pre-cooled Buffer B supplemented with protease inhibitor cocktail and DTT

available_inputs.name: cyto_loading_sample
available_inputs.description: Recovered cytoplasmic fraction already combined with 1x SDS loading buffer

available_inputs.name: nuclear_loading_sample
available_inputs.description: Recovered nuclear fraction already combined with 1x SDS loading buffer

gold_program:
buffer_a_extract = cool_sample(sample=cell_pellet, temperature_c=0, duration_min=30, device="ice bath")
cytoplasm_split = centrifuge_sample(sample=buffer_a_extract, speed_xg=15000, duration_min=6, temperature_c=4)
washed_pellet_1 = mix_sample(sample=cytoplasm_split, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
washed_pellet_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_pellet_1, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
washed_pellet_3 = mix_sample(sample=washed_pellet_2, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
washed_pellet_4 = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_pellet_3, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
nuclear_extract_cold = cool_sample(sample=washed_pellet_4, temperature_c=0, duration_min=30, device="ice bath")
vortexed_nuclear_1 = vortex_mix(sample=nuclear_extract_cold, duration_s=15, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=4)
vortexed_nuclear_2 = vortex_mix(sample=vortexed_nuclear_1, duration_s=15, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=4)
vortexed_nuclear_3 = vortex_mix(sample=vortexed_nuclear_2, duration_s=15, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=4)
nuclear_split = centrifuge_sample(sample=vortexed_nuclear_3, speed_xg=15000, duration_min=15, temperature_c=4)
denatured_cytoplasm = heat_sample(sample=cyto_loading_sample, temperature_c=95, duration_min=8, device="heat block")
denatured_nucleus = heat_sample(sample=nuclear_loading_sample, temperature_c=95, duration_min=8, device="heat block")
protocol_title: Identification of phosphorylation site on PARP1 mediating its cytosolic translocation in virus-infected HeLa cells

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Dilute the PARP1 antibody in QuickBlock Primary antibody diluent for immunostaining (Beyotime, Shanghai, China) at 1:100 and add to the dish, then incubate for 12 h at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Wash cells 3 times with 1×PBS, and incubate with 1 mL of secondary antibody (goat anti-Rabbit Alex Fluor594 antibody) (Beyotime, Shanghai, China) (diluted 1:1,000 in 1% BSA) at 25°C for 1 h in the dark. Note: Light should be avoided during the incubation of secondary antibody to prevent fluorescence quenching.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Incubate cells with 200 μL DAPI Staining Solution (Beyotime, Shanghai, China) at 25°C for 5–10 min in the dark. Note: DAPI incubation time should not be too long to avoid high fluorescent background.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: After 3 washes with 1×PBS, add 1 mL of antifade mounting medium. Collect images using a Leica TCS SP8 confocal laser microscopy system (Leica Microsystems, Buffalo Grove, IL) with 63× oil immersion. Note: It is best to observe dish with antifade mounting medium immediately. If not immediately, it is recommended to store the sample at 4°C in dark for no more than 1 week.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Determine PARP1 localization in cells from different dishes. The photos of ten different microscopic fields were taken. The DAPI signal corresponds to the nucleus. The localization of PARP can be analyzed by ImageJ software (1.8.0.172) according to signal segmentation and further data analysis can be carried out using the GraphPad Prism 8 software. Data were representative of three independent experiments with similar results can be found in Figure 1 A. Note: Avoid dish drying when washing with PBS or adding reagents in all the steps to prevent failure in experiment or collection of misleading information.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Detection of PARP1 localization by cytoplasmic and nuclear extraction. Seed HeLa cells in 6-well-plates (about 1 × 10 6 cells/well), and culture for 24 h to reach 70%–80% confluency. Collect cells from two wells as one sample for cytoplasmic and nuclear fraction. Note: In our experience, to ensure sample quantity, cells from at least two wells were needed for one group. Alternatives: If you want to improve the sample quantity, you can choose one 10 cm dish cells as a sample for cytoplasmic and nuclear extraction.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Collect cells and wash twice with 1×PBS in the 1.5 mL reaction tube;

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Centrifuge for 5 min at 120 × g and remove the supernatant completely;

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Add 40 μL Protease inhibitor cocktail (1 tablet in 2 mL PBS), 1 μL 1 M DTT to every 1 mL Buffer A (stocks can be kept at −20°C for 1–3 months) before use. Add 150–200 μL Buffer A supplemented with protease inhibitor cocktail and DTT to 20 μL cell pellet, place the tube on ice for 30 min, and centrifuge at 15,000 × g for 6 min. The supernatant is cytoplasmic extract, which is transferred to a pre-cooled 1.5 mL reaction tube. Note: Buffer A is best to be prepared freshly and pre-cooled before use. The nucleoplasm must be separated cleanly, and the residual liquid should be aspirated with a pipette.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Wash the pellets 5 times with 500 μL wash buffer (10 mM HEPES (pH 7.9), 2 mM MgCl 2 , 20 mM KCl, 100 μM EDTA (pH 8.0)) (stocks can be kept at −20°C for 1–3 months) supplemented with protease inhibitor cocktail and DTT. Avoid pipetting the pellet, centrifuge at 1,000 × g for 5 min.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Add Protease inhibitor cocktail 40 μL (1 tablet in 2 mL PBS) and 1 M DTT 1 μL for every 1 mL Buffer B (stocks can be kept at −20°C for 1–3 months) before use, add 50–80 μL Buffer B to the pellet, vortex the tube at high speed for 10–15 s every 10 min for a total of 30 min, and place it on ice during the period. Centrifuge the mixture at 15,000 × g for 15–30 min and collect the supernatant as the nuclear extract. Note: Buffer B is best prepared freshly and pre-cooled before adding.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Denature both the cytoplasmic and nuclear fractions in 1× SDS protein loading buffer (stocks can be kept at −20°C for 3 months) at 95°C for 8 min for the following western blot analysis. Note: Collected samples should be stored at −20°C, if they are not tested immediately.

background: A feeder-free primary skin cell isolation procedure has reached the stage immediately after epidermis and dermis have been separated. The epidermal fraction should be kept cold for later same-day keratinocyte isolation, while the dermal fraction has already been mechanically compressed to release endothelial-enriched material. The next operations focus on chilled holding, repeated recovery of released cells from the extrusion dish using endothelial medium, and a gentle cold centrifugation appropriate for fragile primary cells.
goal: Handle the separated tissue fractions so the epidermal strips are held cold temporarily, the endothelial-enriched suspension from the dermal extrusion dish is recovered with repeated ECGM rinses into a 50 mL conical, and the collected suspension is prepared by a cold spin.
constraints: Represent short-term storage of the epidermal strips at 4 degrees Celsius in a refrigerator or cold room, with same-day use rather than overnight storage.
The endothelial-enriched material must be associated with a sterile 50 mL conical collection tube.
Use ECGM as the rinse medium to recover remaining cells from the extrusion dish more than once.
Include repeated wash or recovery behavior and repeated gentle mixing behavior rather than a single one-shot collection.
The final centrifugation must be 7 minutes at about 250 x g and 4 degrees Celsius.
Do not imply downstream inoculation into culture flasks or later cell culture beyond the first pellet-forming spin.
Keep the procedure within this local protocol segment involving cold holding, dish rinsing, collection, and centrifugation.
Use only action-pool abstractions and preserve gentle handling suitable for primary cells.

available_inputs.name: epidermal_strips_dish
available_inputs.description: Dish containing separated epidermal strips in HBS.

available_inputs.name: dermal_extrusion_dish
available_inputs.description: Dish used for mechanical extrusion of endothelial-enriched cells from dermal strips.

available_inputs.name: ecgm
available_inputs.description: Endothelial cell growth medium used for recovering released cells from the dish.

available_inputs.name: conical_50ml
available_inputs.description: Sterile 50 mL centrifuge tube for collecting the endothelial-enriched suspension.

gold_program:
epidermis_cold_hold = store_sample(sample=epidermal_strips_dish, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=3.0, container="refrigerator")
extrusion_release = mix_sample(sample=dermal_extrusion_dish, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
rinsed_dish_1 = mix_sample(sample=dermal_extrusion_dish, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
collected_fraction_1 = mix_sample(sample=conical_50ml, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
ecgm_chilled_1 = cool_sample(sample=ecgm, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=2.0, device="ice")
rinsed_dish_2 = mix_sample(sample=rinsed_dish_1, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
collected_fraction_2 = mix_sample(sample=collected_fraction_1, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
ecgm_chilled_2 = cool_sample(sample=ecgm_chilled_1, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=2.0, device="ice")
hdmec_suspension_ready = mix_sample(sample=collected_fraction_2, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
hdmec_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=hdmec_suspension_ready, speed_xg=250, duration_min=7.0, temperature_c=4.0)
protocol_title: Isolation and feeder-free primary culture of four cell types from a single human skin sample
