source_steps.description: Prepare Collagenase: Prepare a solution containing 625 U/mL in 30 mL KGM + 5% BPE (30 mL is enough for one primary culture, in most cases). BPE is added to neutralize trypsin activity in the collagenase enzyme preparation ( Boyce, 1999 ). There can be lot-to-lot variability in collagenase activity, although it is usually >125 U/mg dry weight. We recommend storing aliquots of pre-weighed collagenase in snap-cap or screw-cap glass vials in a desiccator at 4°C for convenience. To determine the amount of collagenase (in mg) for each aliquot, use the following formula: 625 U mL × mg enzyme activity ( U ) × 30 mL = # mg collagenase per vial To prepare a working collagenase solution, combine 28.5 mL KGM + 1.5 mL BPE in a 50 mL tube, and add small amounts to glass vial with collagenase to dissolve. Bring volume to 30 mL and filter sterilize using 0.22 μm 50 mL tube-top filter (or other suitable filter) and store on ice until ready to use. The estimated volume needed is 1.5 mL collagenase/cm 2 skin.

Prepare FGM (for fibroblasts), “regular” calcium (200 μM) KGM (for keratinocytes), and ECGM (for endothelial cells). Warm to 37°C in water bath before use, protecting from light. Note: KGM-LC (“low calcium,” 0.06 mM CaCl 2 ) is used for feeding primary keratinocytes prior to passage (P0), but KGM with “regular” calcium (0.2 mM) is used during the initial isolation procedure (culture day 0) and the first overnight incubation after inoculation of flasks. After passaging (P1 and beyond), KGM with “regular” calcium (0.2 mM) will be used.

Prepare collagen-coated flasks for keratinocytes: Coat T225 flasks (∼1 per ∼10 cm 2 of skin) with collagen I using the Collagen I-Cell Culture Surface Coating Kit according to manufacturer’s instructions with minor modifications. Dilute the 100× Collagen I Solvent provided with the kit 1:100 with sterile Milli-Q water and store at 4°C. To coat one T225 flask (225 cm 2 ), dilute collagen I solution (1 mg/mL) 1:10 in 1× Collagen I Solvent and add 11.25 mL per flask (5 μg/cm 2 ). Incubate for a minimum of 1 h at 37°C. Aspirate the collagen I solution and rinse each flask 3 times with 10 mL HBS. To use immediately, add 45 mL KGM to flask and pre-equilibrate in incubator at 37°C for at least 30 min. If not used immediately, air dry the flask in the hood and store at 4°C. Note: For best results, equilibrate all flasks with media in a 37°C, 5% CO2 incubator for ≥30 min before inoculation of cells. We recommend this during the primary procedure and for all later passages.
Prepare T150 flasks for fibroblast inoculation (1 per ∼10 cm 2 of skin). Add 10 mL FGM to each flask and equilibrate in incubator ≥30 min before use.

Prepare Attachment Factor-coated flask(s) for HDMECs according to manufacturer’s instructions. Note that Attachment Factor is a 0.1% gelatin solution; a sterile solution of 0.1% gelatin in water can be used instead of the pre-made product. Coat T75 flask(s) (1 per ∼20 cm 2 of skin) with 3–4 mL Attachment Factor: rock vigorously to completely cover growth surface and incubate in sealed flask for 30 min at 37°C or at least 2 h at room temperature (20°C–22°C). Aspirate liquid (do not rinse) and store flask tightly capped in hood at room temperature (20°C–22°C) for up to 24 h. Before use, add 15 mL ECGM per flask and equilibrate in incubator for ≥30 min before inoculating cells.
Prepare 100 mL KGM + 10% FBS (trypsin/EDTA “stop” solution) and store on ice until ready to use.

Separate epidermis from dermis: Remove tissue strips from Dispase and place in 100 mm dish containing 10 mL HBS. Prepare two additional 100 mm dishes with 20 mL HBS each; label for “epidermis” and “dermis”.

Separate dermis from epidermis. To do this, grasp the dermis with fine tipped forceps, grasp the epidermis with blunt forceps, and peel off epidermis ( troubleshooting 2 ; Figure 2 C). Place epidermal strips in a fresh dish (labeled “epidermis”) with 20 mL HBS ( Figures 2 C and 2D).

Place dish with epidermal strips in a plastic zippered bag to avoid spillage, and store in refrigerator or cold room (4°C) until ready to process for keratinocyte isolation. The epidermal strips can be stored for several hours, while the dermal strips are processed for isolation of HDMEC and fibroblasts, but do not store overnight. Keratinocytes should be isolated the same day to preserve cell viability.

Isolation of HDMEC from dermis: Mechanical force will be used to extrude HDMEC from vessels in the dermis. To do this, you will compress the tissue down the length of each dermal strip by squeezing with forceps, using pressure to force the endothelial cells out from vessels (imagine flattening a tube of toothpaste from the bottom up to get out the last bits of toothpaste). Grasp each dermal strip at one end with forceps, and use blunt forceps to squeeze the dermal strip near that end. Then, run the blunt forceps down the length of the strip, squeezing gently, to extrude the endothelial cells ( Figure 2 E and Methods video S1 ). Repeat ten times for each strip, then transfer strip to fresh dish with 20 mL HBS (labeled “dermis”) for subsequent isolation of fibroblasts. Note: This step must be done carefully because too much force will result in contamination of the HDMEC with fibroblasts, but too little force will result in low yield of endothelial cells. If large amounts of fibrous material are present after the extrusion procedure, this may indicate too much force was used. If this is observed, use less pressure for remaining strips ( troubleshooting 4 ).

After scraping and moving all dermal strips to a new dish with fresh HBS, transfer the extruded cell suspension to a sterile 50 mL conical centrifuge tube. Rinse the dish used for extruding HDMEC several times with small amounts of ECGM, and add to conical tube.

Centrifuge for 7 min at 1000 rpm (∼250× g ), 4°C, without brake. Note: Although spinning without the brake may add a small amount of time to each centrifugation step, we strongly recommend this for primary cells. Using the brake at the end of the spin can disrupt or dislodge the pellet, making it more difficult to remove the supernatant without disturbing the cells, and may cause mechanical damage to fragile primary cells.

background: Native ACACA has been eluted from a StrepTactin affinity purification and now needs to be concentrated, quality-checked, and assessed for downstream characterization. The current stage focuses on reducing the eluate volume with a centrifugal concentrator under cold conditions, preparing aliquots for spectral and enzymatic readouts, and documenting the resulting activity and purity-related observations.
goal: Process the eluted ACACA through cold centrifugal concentration and then prepare the resulting material for biophysical and enzyme activity measurements, including capture of the final analytical outputs.
constraints: Use a 100 kDa molecular-weight-cutoff centrifugal concentration workflow under refrigerated conditions.

The concentration stage must be represented with repeated centrifugation behavior at 4 °C and include the source rotor setting equivalent to 2,500 rpm normalized as 2500 x g in the available action schema.
The concentration process should end at a final sample volume of 250 μL before downstream analyses are represented.
Include a circular dichroism-related measurement context using 200 μL material at 0.2 mg/mL-equivalent analytical input conditions.
Include an enzyme activity readout at 340 nm with a pre-equilibrated 37 °C incubation lasting 10 min between signal acquisitions.
Represent at least one initial and one post-incubation signal acquisition for the activity assay.
Record the reported enzyme activity outcome as 73.95 U/mL.
Maintain a dependency chain where downstream analytical steps consume the concentrated ACACA rather than restarting from a raw sample.

available_inputs.name: eluted_acaca
available_inputs.description: Eluted endogenous ACACA from the StrepTactin purification step.

available_inputs.name: amicon_100k_filter
available_inputs.description: Amicon ultra centrifugal filter device with 100 kDa molecular weight cutoff.

available_inputs.name: cd_sample
available_inputs.description: Aliquot of concentrated ACACA reserved for circular dichroism analysis.

available_inputs.name: activity_assay_mix
available_inputs.description: Prepared 1 mL enzyme activity reaction mixture containing sample and assay reagents.

gold_program:
conc_spin_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=eluted_acaca, speed_xg=2500, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
conc_spin_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=conc_spin_1, speed_xg=2500, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
conc_spin_3 = centrifuge_sample(sample=conc_spin_2, speed_xg=2500, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
conc_record = record_measurement(sample=conc_spin_3, metric="final_volume", unit="uL", note="ACACA concentrated to 250 uL with 100 kDa MW cutoff filter at 4 C")
cd_equilibrated = cool_sample(sample=cd_sample, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=5.0, device="Hirascan ACD spectrometer")
cd_readout = measure_signal(sample=cd_equilibrated, assay="circular_dichroism", wavelength_nm=0, replicate_n=1)
activity_initial = measure_signal(sample=activity_assay_mix, assay="enzyme_activity_A1", wavelength_nm=340, replicate_n=1)
activity_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=activity_initial, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=10.0, condition="static")
activity_final = measure_signal(sample=activity_incubated, assay="enzyme_activity_A2", wavelength_nm=340, replicate_n=1)
activity_record = record_measurement(sample=activity_final, metric="enzyme_activity", unit="U/mL", note="Calculated ACACA activity 73.95 U/mL")
protocol_title: Purification of Native Acetyl CoA Carboxylase From Mammalian Cells

source_steps.stage: D. Purification of ACACA protein using StrepTactin beads
source_steps.description: After loading the sample to 3 mL StrepTactin beads, wash the column with 20 times the bed volume of wash buffer.

source_steps.stage: D. Purification of ACACA protein using StrepTactin beads
source_steps.description: Elute endogenous ACACA using lysis buffer supplemented with 15 mL of 5 mM and 15 mL of 10 mM D-biotin.

source_steps.stage: D. Purification of ACACA protein using StrepTactin beads
source_steps.description: Concentrate the eluted ACACA at 2,500 rpm every 5 min at 4 °C to 250 μL using an Amicon ultra-centrifugal filter with 100 kDa MW cutoff.

source_steps.stage: D. Purification of ACACA protein using StrepTactin beads
source_steps.description: Use 5% concentrated gel and 10% separation gel to check the eluate. Add 2.5 µL of protein loading buffer to 10 µL of sample. Perform electrophoresis in the SDS-PAGE buffer at a constant voltage of 220 V for 36 min. Afterward, stain the gel with Coomassie Brilliant Blue for 15 min. To decolorize, add water and microwave the gel on high heat for 15 min.

source_steps.stage: D. Purification of ACACA protein using StrepTactin beads
source_steps.description: Load the concentrated sample into a 24 mL Superdex200 column to further purify the sample. Concentrate the sample to 250 μL. After running the size exclusion column to further purify ACACA, collect a total of four 500 μL sample fractions. In order to estimate the fraction of ACACA, besides performing the SDS-PAGE experiments, we also calculated the areas of peak1, peak2, and peak3. The area of peak1 accounts for about 91.2% of the total area, which means the amount of ACACA accounted for approximately 91.2% of the total protein. The concentration of ACACA was 0.8 mg/mL, and the total volume collected was 2 mL, yielding 2 mg of protein per liter. We used mass spectrometry to further identify that the protein is ACACA.

source_steps.stage: D. Purification of ACACA protein using StrepTactin beads
source_steps.description: Circular dichroism determination: First, equilibrate the machine (Hirascan ACD spectrometer) using eluting buffer, using 200 μL of sample with the protein concentration of 0.2 mg/mL ( Figure 2D , E).

source_steps.stage: D. Purification of ACACA protein using StrepTactin beads
source_steps.description: Enzyme activity measurement: Preheat the spectrophotometer for 30 min and set the wavelength to 340 nm. Calibrate the instrument to zero using distilled water. Add the following reagents sequentially to a 1 mL cuvette: 250 μL of reagent 1 (see Reagent no. 46), 50 μL of sample, 500 μL of working solution (see Reagent no. 46), and 200 μL of reagent 2 (see Reagent no. 46) for the measurement. For the blank, add 250 μL of reagent 1, 50 μL of distilled water, 500 μL of working solution, and 200 μL of reagent 2. First, measure the absorbance (A1) at 340 nm for 10 s. Immediately place the cuvette at 37 °C to incubate for 10 min. After incubation, measure absorbance (A2) at 340 nm after 10 s of mixing. Calculate enzyme activity as 73.95 U/mL. [Enzyme activity = (A1 M - A2 M - (A1 B - A2 B )) × 321.5; M for materials, B for blank].

source_steps.stage: E. Purification of endogenous ACACA in mouse tumors using StrepTactin beads
source_steps.description: First, lyse the tissues using sonication followed by centrifugation at 208,888× g for 60 min to remove the large debris. Then, load the supernatant onto a Strep Trap column pre-equilibrated with lysis buffer.

source_steps.stage: E. Purification of endogenous ACACA in mouse tumors using StrepTactin beads
source_steps.description: After loading the sample to 1 mL strep beads, wash the column with 20 times the bed volume of wash buffer.

source_steps.stage: E. Purification of endogenous ACACA in mouse tumors using StrepTactin beads
source_steps.description: Elute endogenous ACACA with 5 mL of lysis buffer supplemented with 10 mM D-biotin.

source_steps.stage: E. Purification of endogenous ACACA in mouse tumors using StrepTactin beads
source_steps.description: Concentrate the eluted ACACA at 1,460× g at 4 °C using an Amicon ultra-centrifugal filter with 100 kDa MW cutoff. The final concentrated volume is 250 μL.

source_steps.stage: E. Purification of endogenous ACACA in mouse tumors using StrepTactin beads
source_steps.description: Perform western blot analysis with a 5% concentrated gel and 8% separation gel. Run the gel at a constant voltage of 80 V for 120 min. Transfer the proteins at 300 mA for 2 h to a PVDF membrane (activated with 100% methanol for 2 min). Block the membrane with 7% milk powder for 1 h. Wash the membrane three times with 5 mL of TBST for 10 min each. Incubate the membrane with the primary antibody (see Reagent no. 39) overnight at 4 °C. Wash the membrane three times with 5 mL of TBST for 10 min each. Incubate with the secondary antibody (see Reagent no. 40) at room temperature for 1 h. Wash the membrane three times with 5 mL of TBST for 10 min each. Add 1 mL of chemiluminescent substrate to visualize the results.

background: Human embryonic stem cell samples are at the lipid-oriented metabolite extraction stage for LC-MS preparation. After cold solvent extraction in glass vials, the mixture contains separated aqueous and organic components with a protein layer at the interface. The current task is to finish cold processing of the extracted material, recover the lipid-containing portion for analysis, and optionally clean the remaining protein pellet for later mass determination.
goal: Complete the local extraction-processing segment that prepares the hydrophobic metabolite fraction for LC-MS and, if desired, cleans the associated protein pellet for downstream quantification.
constraints: Maintain cold handling after extraction, using ice-level cooling where appropriate and 4°C for centrifugation steps.
Before each spin in this segment, vortex the sample; each centrifugation here must be 18000 x g for 5 min at 4°C.
Advance only the hydrophobic phase for lipid analysis and avoid disturbing the interphase protein material.
Dry the recovered hydrophobic fraction completely under nitrogen-style evaporation, then reconstitute it in 100 uL of 2:1 chloroform/methanol for LC-MS readiness.
For the optional protein branch, perform a methanol wash on the retained pellet, followed by another vortex and the same cold centrifugation conditions.
After discarding the wash supernatant from the protein branch, dry the pellet for 5 min before considering it ready for later protein mass measurement.

available_inputs.description: Extracted sample in a labeled glass vial containing the post-extraction phase mixture from hESCs.

available_inputs.name: ice_bath
available_inputs.description: Cold holding setup used to keep samples on ice during processing.

available_inputs.name: nitrogen_stream
available_inputs.description: Nitrogen-based drying setup for evaporating solvent from the hydrophobic fraction.

available_inputs.name: lipid_reconstitution_solution
available_inputs.description: Prepared 2:1 chloroform/methanol solution used to dissolve the dried lipid residue.

available_inputs.name: protein_pellet_fraction

gold_program:
vortexed_extract = vortex_mix(sample=glass_vial_extract, duration_s=10.0, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=4.0)
centrifuged_extract = centrifuge_sample(sample=vortexed_extract, speed_xg=18000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
chilled_hydrophobic_phase = cool_sample(sample=centrifuged_extract, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=1.0, device=ice_bath)
dried_lipid_phase = dry_sample(sample=chilled_hydrophobic_phase, method=nitrogen_stream, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=15.0)
reconstituted_lipid_fraction = mix_sample(sample=dried_lipid_phase, method=lipid_reconstitution_solution, duration_s=20.0, speed="gentle")
chilled_lipid_fraction = cool_sample(sample=reconstituted_lipid_fraction, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=1.0, device=ice_bath)
methanol_washed_pellet = mix_sample(sample=protein_pellet_fraction, method="methanol wash", duration_s=15.0, speed="vigorous")
vortexed_pellet = vortex_mix(sample=methanol_washed_pellet, duration_s=10.0, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=4.0)
centrifuged_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=vortexed_pellet, speed_xg=18000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
dried_protein_pellet = dry_sample(sample=centrifuged_pellet, method="air dry", temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=5.0)
protein_pellet_record = record_measurement(sample=dried_protein_pellet, metric="protein_pellet_ready", unit="state", note="methanol-washed pellet dried for optional protein mass determination")

source_steps.description: Wash the well with 100 μL cell extraction solution and combine it into Tube 1. Pause point: Sample in Tube 1 can be stored in −80°C fridge for 24 h (see Note after step 12d).

Vortex each sample. Centrifuge at 18,000 × g for 5 min at 4°C. Transfer the supernatant into a new 1.5-mL tube (Tube 2). Put tubes on ice. Note: The pellet could be preserved to measure protein concentration as described below (step 14). Note: After step 12d, Tube 2 could be preserved at −80°C for 24 h. This 24 h preservation was evaluated in Figure 2 . Peak intensities of most metabolites were comparable with or without 24 h-storage. Because of the risk of losing metabolites caused by storage, we recommend immediate LC-MS analysis after extraction. Long term storage of extracted metabolites, even in −80°C refrigerator, should be avoided.
Vortex Tube 2, put tube on ice, and then transfer 200 μL extract into a sample vial for LC-MS analysis.

Method 2 (for lipid determination): CRITICAL: To avoid plastic contamination, glass vials are recommended. Polypropylene pipette tip is conditionally resistant to chloroform for a minimized contact time. A fresh tip must be used each time chloroform is dispensed. Put Milli-Q water and labeled glass vials on ice. Make sure methanol and chloroform containing 1 μg/mL heptadecanoate and 1 μg/mL coprostan-3-ol (see Materials and equipment) are pre-chilled at −80°C. Note: Heptadecanoate is used as internal control for fatty acids. coprostan-3-ol is used as internal control for cholesterol.
Rinse cells with 1 mL/well ice-cold 0.9% saline twice.
Aspirate the saline. Add 250 μL/well −80°C methanol and then add 100 μL/well ice-cold Milli-Q water.
Scrape off cells and transfer the suspension into glass vials. Put vials on ice.
Add 250 μL/vial −80°C chloroform containing 1 μg/mL heptadecanoate and 1 μg/mL coprostan-3-ol. Vortex and then centrifuge at 18,000 × g for 5 min at 4°C.

Remove the upper hydrophilic phase. Protein pellet at the interface is carefully pushed aside on the wall of the vial. A fresh tip is used to reach the bottom of the vial to collect the hydrophobic phase. Stop collecting before reaching the interface.
Transfer the hydrophobic phase into a new glass vial. Evaporate under nitrogen gas till dry. Re-dissolve the residue in 100 μL 2:1 chloroform/methanol (v/v) for LC-MS analysis.
Determine protein mass (optional) Decant supernatant and add 200 μL methanol to the protein pellet. Vigorously vortex and then centrifuge at 18,000 × g for 5 min at 4°C.
Decant the supernatant into waste. Let the protein pellet dry by putting the tube upside down on a Kimwipe in a biological safety cabinet for 5 min.

background: HeLa cells have already been prepared for analysis of PARP1 localization after viral infection. The current task is the local fractionation stage, where harvested cells are processed under cold conditions to separate cytoplasmic and nuclear extracts using sequential centrifugation, buffer-based extraction, repeated washing, and final clarification spins.
goal: Generate cytoplasmic and nuclear fractions from harvested HeLa cells for downstream PARP1 localization analysis while preserving the intended separation between the two compartments.
constraints: Begin from a harvested HeLa cell sample representing two wells combined in one 1.5 mL tube after PBS washing, and include an initial centrifugation at 120 x g for 5 min under cold conditions to form the starting pellet.

For cytoplasmic extraction, use Buffer A conditions corresponding to 150–200 uL buffer per about 20 uL pellet, keep the sample on ice for 30 min, then clarify at 15000 x g for 6 min at 4C; the supernatant is the cytoplasmic fraction.

The post-cytoplasmic pellet must be washed in repeated low-speed cycles matching the protocol logic: use wash-buffer conditions equivalent to 500 uL per wash and perform 1000 x g spins for 5 min under cold conditions; this wash/centrifuge family should recur multiple times.

For nuclear extraction, keep the sample cold, use Buffer B conditions equivalent to 50–80 uL, and incorporate vigorous vortexing for about 10–15 s at 10 min intervals across a total extraction time of 30 min.
Finish with a high-speed clarification of the nuclear extract at 15000 x g for a duration within the 15–30 min range at 4C, and treat the final supernatant as the nuclear fraction.

Use only the provided action-pool functions, keep all calls assigned to variables, use keyword arguments only, and maintain a single dependency chain from the initial cell sample through both fractions.

available_inputs.name: hela_cell_sample
available_inputs.description: Harvested HeLa cells from two wells combined in a 1.5 mL tube after PBS washes.

available_inputs.name: buffer_a_mix
available_inputs.description: Pre-cooled Buffer A supplemented with protease inhibitor cocktail and DTT.

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer_mix
available_inputs.description: Pre-cooled wash buffer supplemented with protease inhibitor cocktail and DTT.

available_inputs.name: buffer_b_mix
available_inputs.description: Pre-cooled Buffer B supplemented with protease inhibitor cocktail and DTT.

gold_program:
pelleted_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=hela_cell_sample, speed_xg=120, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
cyto_buffer_added = mix_sample(sample=pelleted_cells, method='pipetting', duration_s=15, speed='gentle')
cyto_extraction = incubate_sample(sample=cyto_buffer_added, temperature_c=0, duration_min=30, condition='static on ice')
cytoplasmic_fraction = centrifuge_sample(sample=cyto_extraction, speed_xg=15000, duration_min=6, temperature_c=4)
wash_cycle_1 = mix_sample(sample=cytoplasmic_fraction, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
wash_pellet_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_cycle_1, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
wash_cycle_2 = mix_sample(sample=wash_pellet_1, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
wash_pellet_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_cycle_2, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
nuclear_buffer_added = mix_sample(sample=wash_pellet_2, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
nuclear_extraction = incubate_sample(sample=nuclear_buffer_added, temperature_c=0, duration_min=30, condition='static on ice')
nuclear_vortexed = vortex_mix(sample=nuclear_extraction, duration_s=15, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=4)
nuclear_fraction = centrifuge_sample(sample=nuclear_vortexed, speed_xg=15000, duration_min=15, temperature_c=4)

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Block cells with 1% BSA for 30 min at 25°C.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Dilute the PARP1 antibody in QuickBlock Primary antibody diluent for immunostaining (Beyotime, Shanghai, China) at 1:100 and add to the dish, then incubate for 12 h at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Wash cells 3 times with 1×PBS, and incubate with 1 mL of secondary antibody (goat anti-Rabbit Alex Fluor594 antibody) (Beyotime, Shanghai, China) (diluted 1:1,000 in 1% BSA) at 25°C for 1 h in the dark. Note: Light should be avoided during the incubation of secondary antibody to prevent fluorescence quenching.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Incubate cells with 200 μL DAPI Staining Solution (Beyotime, Shanghai, China) at 25°C for 5–10 min in the dark. Note: DAPI incubation time should not be too long to avoid high fluorescent background.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: After 3 washes with 1×PBS, add 1 mL of antifade mounting medium. Collect images using a Leica TCS SP8 confocal laser microscopy system (Leica Microsystems, Buffalo Grove, IL) with 63× oil immersion. Note: It is best to observe dish with antifade mounting medium immediately. If not immediately, it is recommended to store the sample at 4°C in dark for no more than 1 week.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Determine PARP1 localization in cells from different dishes. The photos of ten different microscopic fields were taken. The DAPI signal corresponds to the nucleus. The localization of PARP can be analyzed by ImageJ software (1.8.0.172) according to signal segmentation and further data analysis can be carried out using the GraphPad Prism 8 software. Data were representative of three independent experiments with similar results can be found in Figure 1 A. Note: Avoid dish drying when washing with PBS or adding reagents in all the steps to prevent failure in experiment or collection of misleading information.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Detection of PARP1 localization by cytoplasmic and nuclear extraction. Seed HeLa cells in 6-well-plates (about 1 × 10 6 cells/well), and culture for 24 h to reach 70%–80% confluency. Collect cells from two wells as one sample for cytoplasmic and nuclear fraction. Note: In our experience, to ensure sample quantity, cells from at least two wells were needed for one group. Alternatives: If you want to improve the sample quantity, you can choose one 10 cm dish cells as a sample for cytoplasmic and nuclear extraction.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Collect cells and wash twice with 1×PBS in the 1.5 mL reaction tube;

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Centrifuge for 5 min at 120 × g and remove the supernatant completely;

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Add 40 μL Protease inhibitor cocktail (1 tablet in 2 mL PBS), 1 μL 1 M DTT to every 1 mL Buffer A (stocks can be kept at −20°C for 1–3 months) before use. Add 150–200 μL Buffer A supplemented with protease inhibitor cocktail and DTT to 20 μL cell pellet, place the tube on ice for 30 min, and centrifuge at 15,000 × g for 6 min. The supernatant is cytoplasmic extract, which is transferred to a pre-cooled 1.5 mL reaction tube. Note: Buffer A is best to be prepared freshly and pre-cooled before use. The nucleoplasm must be separated cleanly, and the residual liquid should be aspirated with a pipette.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Wash the pellets 5 times with 500 μL wash buffer (10 mM HEPES (pH 7.9), 2 mM MgCl 2 , 20 mM KCl, 100 μM EDTA (pH 8.0)) (stocks can be kept at −20°C for 1–3 months) supplemented with protease inhibitor cocktail and DTT. Avoid pipetting the pellet, centrifuge at 1,000 × g for 5 min.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the localization of PARP1 after virus infection
source_steps.description: Add Protease inhibitor cocktail 40 μL (1 tablet in 2 mL PBS) and 1 M DTT 1 μL for every 1 mL Buffer B (stocks can be kept at −20°C for 1–3 months) before use, add 50–80 μL Buffer B to the pellet, vortex the tube at high speed for 10–15 s every 10 min for a total of 30 min, and place it on ice during the period. Centrifuge the mixture at 15,000 × g for 15–30 min and collect the supernatant as the nuclear extract. Note: Buffer B is best prepared freshly and pre-cooled before adding.

background: This experiment profiles transcriptome-wide RNA interactions of small molecules in human cells. The current portion starts from Trizol LS lysate and covers phase separation plus condensed on-column cleanup to leave purified total RNA after DNase treatment and wash steps.
goal: Isolate the RNA-containing phase from the lysate and complete a shortened but protocol-faithful cleanup sequence that includes ethanol conditioning, DNase digestion, and final wash removal.
constraints: Vortex the lysate for 15 s, add 1200 uL chloroform, vortex again for 15 s at 20 C, then incubate 5 min at 20 C before centrifugation.
Use phase-separation centrifugation at 2500 x g for 15 min at 4 C, and only the upper aqueous phase may be transferred forward into a fresh vessel.
The transferred aqueous phase must be mixed with an equal volume of 96% to 100% ethanol before cleanup.
Include DNase treatment with 80 uL digestion buffer for 15 min at 20 C and use 10000 x g spins at 20 C for cleanup, with the last spin lasting 2 min rather than 0.5 min.
Represent the repeated post-digestion cleanup buffers in a compact but valid way while preserving one RNA Prep Buffer addition and RNA Wash Buffer usage more than once.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keyword arguments, and a single dependency chain of 8 to 12 top-level calls.

available_inputs.name: trizol_lysate
available_inputs.description: Cell lysate prepared in Trizol LS reagent.

available_inputs.name: chloroform
available_inputs.description: Chloroform for organic phase separation.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_100
available_inputs.description: 96% to 100% ethanol used prior to RNA binding cleanup.

available_inputs.name: rna_wash_buffer
available_inputs.description: RNA Wash Buffer from the RNA purification kit.

available_inputs.name: dna_digestion_buffer
available_inputs.description: DNA Digestion Buffer containing DNase I.

available_inputs.name: rna_prep_buffer
available_inputs.description: RNA Prep Buffer used during column cleanup.

gold_program:
step1 = vortex_mix(sample=trizol_lysate, duration_s=15, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=20)
step2 = add_reagent(sample=step1, reagent=chloroform, volume_ul=1200, mixing="vortex")
step3 = incubate_sample(sample=step2, temperature_c=20, duration_min=5, condition="static")
step4 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step3, speed_xg=2500, duration_min=15, temperature_c=4)
step5 = aliquot_sample(sample=step4, destination="aqueous_phase_tube", volume_ul=1000, aliquot_count=1)
step6 = add_reagent(sample=step5, reagent=ethanol_100, volume_ul=1000, mixing="vortex")
step7 = add_reagent(sample=step6, reagent=rna_wash_buffer, volume_ul=400, mixing="none")
step8 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step7, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=20)
step9 = add_reagent(sample=step8, reagent=dna_digestion_buffer, volume_ul=80, mixing="none")
step10 = incubate_sample(sample=step9, temperature_c=20, duration_min=15, condition="static")
step11 = add_reagent(sample=step10, reagent=rna_prep_buffer, volume_ul=400, mixing="none")
step12 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step11, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=2, temperature_c=20)
protocol_title: Reactivity-based RNA profiling for analyzing transcriptome interactions of small molecules in human cells

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Vortex for 15 s to homogenize the samples. CRITICAL: Trizol LS reagent is toxic when in contact with skin and if swallowed. Trizol LS reagent should be handled in a chemical fume hood. Be sure to wear a lab coat, disposable gloves, and safety googles when working with Trizol LS reagent. If in contact with skin, wash immediately with soap and water.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Add 1.2 mL of chloroform and vortex for 15 s at 20°C.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Incubate the sample at 20°C for 5 min.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 2,500 × g for 15 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Transfer the aqueous layer on the top to a new 15 mL conical vial. Note: The top aqueous layer should be collected carefully using a pipette. Avoid making contact or disturbing the organic layer. It’s preferred to tilt the vial to maximize the recovery of the aqueous layer during the collection.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Add 1× volume of 96%–100% of ethanol and vortex to mix thoroughly.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Extraction of total cellular RNA. Transfer the mixture to a Zymo-Spin V-E Column (binding capacity: 1 mg of RNA) from Zymo Quick-RNA Midiprep kit (Zymo, Cat#R1056) with reservoir on a vacuum manifold. Note: The vacuum manifold should be attached to a vacuum pump that is ideally capable of producing at least 400 mm Hg pressure. This step can also be performed on a centrifuge as an alternative, but it is not recommended as it requires extensive rounds of centrifugation. Steps 8b-f are performed with a microcentrifuge at 20°C.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Digest genomic DNAs. Add 400 μL of RNA Wash Buffer to the Zymo-Spin V-E Column. Centrifuge at 10,000 × g for 30 s at 20°C.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Add 80 μL of DNA Digestion Buffer containing 5 μL of DNase I (1 U/μL) and incubate for 15 min at 20°C. Centrifuge at 10,000 × g for 30 s at 20°C.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Add 400 μL RNA Prep Buffer to the column and centrifuge. Discard the flow-through.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Add 400 μL RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge. Discard the flow-through.

source_steps.stage: Isolation of total cellular RNAs and quality control
source_steps.description: Add 400 μL RNA Wash Buffer and centrifuge for 2 min for complete removal of the wash buffer.

background: This experiment enriches long ribosome-protected RNA fragments generated by collided ribosomes and carries them into enzymatic end-processing before downstream library preparation. At this stage, gel-extracted ribosome footprints have been recovered and must be precipitated, washed, dried, resuspended, thermally denatured, dephosphorylated, and linker-ligated under specific temperature-controlled conditions in tubes suitable for centrifugation and thermal cycling.
goal: Process the gel-extracted ribosome footprint RNA through precipitation cleanup and the initial enzymatic handling stage so that the sample is ready for post-ligation purification.
constraints: Include an ambient-temperature hold for the extracted material lasting between 12 and 20 hours at approximately room temperature, using a mixed or rotating condition.
Perform a cold clarification spin after filtration-related recovery at 10000 x g for 1 minute at 4 C.
Model RNA precipitation with a 1 hour hold at -20 C before a high-speed cold spin at 20000 x g for 30 minutes at 4 C.
Include an ethanol wash followed by a second centrifugation at 20000 x g for 3 minutes at 4 C.
Dry the washed RNA pellet briefly for 2 to 5 minutes before resuspension.
After resuspension in a small-volume aqueous buffer, denature the RNA at 95 C for 2 minutes and then chill it on ice for 3 minutes.
Represent dephosphorylation as a 37 C incubation for 1 hour and linker ligation as a 22 C incubation for 3 hours on the same sample lineage.
Use only provided action-pool functions and preserve dependency between cleanup, denaturation, dephosphorylation, and ligation steps.

available_inputs.name: gel_extracted_rna
available_inputs.description: Gel-extracted ribosome footprint RNA in extraction buffer after fragment recovery.

available_inputs.name: spinx_filter_tube
available_inputs.description: Spin-X filter assembly used to clear broken gel debris from the extraction mixture.

available_inputs.name: rna_pcr_tube
available_inputs.description: 0.2 mL PCR tube containing the resuspended RNA for thermal cycler steps.

available_inputs.name: thermal_cycler_plate
available_inputs.description: Plate or tube position identifier used to run thermal cycler incubations.

gold_program:
room_temp_extract = incubate_sample(sample=gel_extracted_rna, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=720.0, condition='rotating')
clarified_extract = centrifuge_sample(sample=room_temp_extract, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=4.0)
precipitation_chilled = cool_sample(sample=clarified_extract, temperature_c=-20.0, duration_min=60.0, device='freezer')
rna_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=precipitation_chilled, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=30.0, temperature_c=4.0)
washed_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=rna_pellet, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=4.0)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=washed_pellet, method='vacuum dry', temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=3.0)
denatured_rna = heat_sample(sample=dried_pellet, temperature_c=95.0, duration_min=2.0, device='thermal cycler')
chilled_rna = cool_sample(sample=denatured_rna, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=3.0, device='ice')
dephosphorylated_rna = incubate_sample(sample=chilled_rna, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=60.0, condition='thermal cycler incubation')
linker_ligated_rna = incubate_sample(sample=dephosphorylated_rna, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=180.0, condition='thermal cycler incubation')
protocol_title: Protocol for Disome Profiling to Survey Ribosome Collision in Humans and Zebrafish

source_steps.description: Leave the sample for 12–20 h at 23°C–26°C on a rotary mixer (Nissin).

Place a 0.22-μm filter (accompanied by Spin-X centrifuge tube) into a 1.5-mL tube and transfer RNA gel extraction buffer and broken gel with wide-bore 200-μL tips. Centrifuge at 10,000 × g at 4°C for 1 min. Ensure that no buffer remains on the filter.
Add 3 μL of GlycoBlue and 500 μL of isopropanol, mix well by vortexing, incubate in a −20°C freezer for 1 h, and then precipitate RNA at 20,000 × g at 4°C for 30 min.
Discard supernatant thoroughly by pipetting. Add 1 mL of 70% ethanol to rinse and centrifuge at 20,000 × g at 4°C for 3 min.
Discard the supernatant thoroughly by pipetting and dry up the pellet by centrifugal evaporation for 2–5 min.
Resuspend the precipitated RNA in 7 μL of 10 mM Tris pH 7.5 (or RNase-free water) and transfer to a 0.2-mL PCR tube.
Denature RNA at 95°C for 2 min in a thermal cycler (with the lid at 105°C) and then place on ice for 3 min.
Prepare the dephosphorylation reaction and incubate at 37°C for 1 h in a thermal cycler (with the lid at 57°C).

Prepare the linker ligation reaction in the same tube by adding components as described below. Incubate at 22°C for 3 h in a thermal cycler (with the lid at 45°C). Use a preadenylated linker (one of #1-#8) for each sample for pooling.

Add 30 μL of RNase-free water to the reaction and purify linker-ligated RNA by the Oligo Clean & Concentrator Kit according to the manufacturer's instructions. Elute the linker into 7 μL of RNase-free water.
Add 7 μL of 2× RNA Loading Buffer (FUJIFILM Wako Pure Chemical).

Gel-purify the linker-ligated RNA as described in steps 42–54. Add the following free linker control in the gel-purification step. See Figure 5 for a representative result. A Representative Gel Image for Linker-Ligated Footprints The areas indicated by dashed white lines are gel-excised and used for downstream processes.

background: An insulator-seq cloning workflow in Drosophila has reached the amplification and cleanup stage for a pooled reporter library. The reaction mix has already been prepared and divided into multiple PCR tubes on ice. The next work involves thermocycler amplification under a heated lid, followed by column-based purification with repeated loading and washing spins, removal of residual ethanol, DNA elution, and optional cold storage of the purified product.
goal: Amplify the split PCR library tubes and complete purification to obtain a clean pooled DNA product ready for quantification or short-term storage.
constraints: The PCR material is split across 20 tubes and should be treated as a pooled thermocycler batch rather than a single unsplit reaction.

The thermal cycler run must use a heated lid at 110 C and reflect a program containing an initial 98 C denaturation, 8-10 amplification cycles with 64 C annealing and 72 C extension, then a post-amplification denaturation and gradual cool-down to a 10 C hold.
The chilled PCR tubes should remain cold before loading, and the run should begin only after the instrument has reached the high-temperature start condition associated with the protocol.
After amplification, cleanup should include repeated high-speed spins at 12000 x g for 1 min during column loading and wash handling.
Column washing must use Buffer PE in 3 wash repetitions before a longer spin to remove residual ethanol.
Residual ethanol removal requires an additional 5 min centrifugation at 12000 x g followed by an open-column evaporation period of 10 min at about room temperature, 20-25 C.
DNA elution should total 30 uL using 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.5, implemented as two 15 uL elutions rather than a single larger one.
A valid completion state is purified pooled PCR product, optionally stored at -20 C.

available_inputs.name: split_pcr_tubes
available_inputs.description: Twenty PCR tubes containing the aliquoted insulator-seq master mix kept on ice.

available_inputs.name: thermal_cycler
available_inputs.description: Thermal cycler used for amplification with programmable lid heating.

available_inputs.name: minelute_column
available_inputs.description: PCR purification column for binding, washing, and eluting the pooled PCR product.

available_inputs.name: pooled_pcr_product
available_inputs.description: Amplified PCR material pooled after thermocycling and prepared for column purification.

available_inputs.name: clean_lobind_tube
available_inputs.description: Clean 1.5 mL DNA LoBind tube used to collect the final eluate.

gold_program:
cold_tubes = cool_sample(sample=split_pcr_tubes, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5, device="ice")
cycler_open = open_thermal_cycler_lid(instrument=thermal_cycler, latch_state="unlocked", plate=cold_tubes)
cycler_run = run_thermal_cycler(plate=cold_tubes, program_name="insulator_seq_library_pcr_98C_45s_8to10cycles_64C_30s_72C_15s_95C_15s_cooldown_hold10C", cycles=10, lid_temperature_c=110)
cycler_closed = close_thermal_cycler_lid(instrument=thermal_cycler, latch_state="locked", plate=cold_tubes)
load_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=pooled_pcr_product, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
wash_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=load_spin, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
wash_spin_repeat = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_spin, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=25)
ethanol_removal_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_spin_repeat, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=25)
open_column_dry = dry_sample(sample=ethanol_removal_spin, method="air dry", temperature_c=25, duration_min=10)
first_elution = incubate_sample(sample=open_column_dry, temperature_c=25, duration_min=1, condition="static with 15 uL elution buffer in clean_lobind_tube")
second_elution = incubate_sample(sample=first_elution, temperature_c=25, duration_min=1, condition="static with second 15 uL elution buffer in clean_lobind_tube")
stored_product = store_sample(sample=second_elution, temperature_c=-20, duration_h=24, container=clean_lobind_tube)
protocol_title: Protocol for detecting genomic insulators in Drosophila using insulator-seq, a massively parallel reporter assay

source_steps.description: Mix by pipetting up and down, centrifuge briefly.
Split the master mix into 20 tubes.

Start the PCR program on a thermocycler with a lid heated to 110°C, and transfer tubes from ice into the thermocyler when the block reaches 98°C: Steps Temperature Time Cycles Initial Denaturation 98°C 45 s 1 Denaturation 98°C 15 s 8–10 cycles Annealing 64°C 30 s Extension 72°C 15 s Final extension 72°C 1 min 1 Denature 95°C 15 s 1 Cool down −0.5°C 15 s 140 cycles Hold 10°C Forever Note: We recommend optimizing the number of cycles (8–10 cycles), amount of template and volume of master mix, such that the lowest number of cycles is used to generate a sufficient amount of product while maintaining a 2–5-fold amplification relative to the input. Note: Emulsion PCR may lead to better results, as discussed in the “ limitations ” section.
Purify the PCR product with a MinElute PCR Purification Kit (QIAGEN) or equivalent. Pool the PCR reactions into one tube.
Add 5 volumes of PB buffer without the pH indicator to 1 volume of PCR reactions.
Add 10 μL of 3 M sodium acetate, pH 5.0.
Apply 750 μL sample to the MinElute column. Centrifuge 12000 g for 1 min, discard supernatant and repeat until all the sample has gone through the column.
To wash the column, add 750 μL Buffer PE, centrifuge 12000 g for 1 min and discard supernatant. Wash the column 3 times.
To remove residual ethanol, centrifuge the column 12000 g for 5 min.
Place the column over a clean 1.5 mL DNA LoBind tube and let ethanol evaporate by incubating an open column 10 min at 20°C–25°C.

To elute DNA, add 15 μL of elution buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.5) to the column. Let the column stand for 1 min, then centrifuge 12000 g for 1 min. Repeat the elution twice, so that the total volume of elution buffer added to the column is 30 μL.

Measure the concentration of eluted DNA by using Qubit dsDNA HS assay kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific) or equivalent. Expect 1 μg total DNA yield from 500 μL PCR reaction. Pause point: Purified PCR product can be stored at −20°C.

background: This item covers the worm dissociation workflow for C. elegans neuron isolation just before pronase digestion. Worms have already been harvested from plates into a 50 mL conical tube in M9, and the remaining work is to clean away bacteria, concentrate the worms into a microcentrifuge-format sample, expose them briefly to SDS-DTT at room temperature, then recover and wash the pellet in egg buffer so it is ready for the next dissociation stage.
goal: Convert the harvested worm suspension into a washed post-SDS-DTT pellet that is prepared for the upcoming pronase dissociation step.
constraints: Use the collected worm suspension as the starting material, include the initial 1000 x g spin for 2.5 min, and include two repeated low-speed bacterial separation spins at 150 x g for 2.5 min each with explicit cold handling.

Include a microcentrifuge-scale concentration step after cleanup using a 1 min spin in the 500-1000 x g range, then perform SDS-DTT incubation at 22 C for a duration within the 2-6 min window under a rocking or nutating-style condition.

After SDS-DTT treatment, represent quench and pellet recovery conditions consistent with bringing the sample to 1.5 mL in egg buffer before centrifugation, then use repeated 16000 x g spins for 0.5 min at 22 C to model the egg buffer wash sequence, ending with a washed pellet rather than a pronase-treated sample.
Use only provided action-pool functions with keyword arguments, keep 6-12 top-level calls, and make at least two action names recur.

available_inputs.name: worm_suspension
available_inputs.description: Collected worms in M9 in a 50 mL conical tube after washing animals from culture plates.

available_inputs.name: m9_cold
available_inputs.description: Ice-cold M9 used during bacterial separation washes.

available_inputs.name: microfuge_pellet
available_inputs.description: Worm sample transferred to a 1.7 mL microcentrifuge tube and adjusted to working volume.

available_inputs.name: sds_dtt_sample
available_inputs.description: Microcentrifuge tube sample after addition of SDS-DTT at twice the pellet volume.

available_inputs.name: egg_buffer_sample
available_inputs.description: Sample after SDS-DTT treatment has been quenched by bringing volume to 1.5 mL with egg buffer.

gold_program:
spin1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=worm_suspension, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=2.5, temperature_c=22)
cold1 = cool_sample(sample=spin1, temperature_c=0, duration_min=1.0, device=m9_cold)
wash_spin1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=cold1, speed_xg=150, duration_min=2.5, temperature_c=4)
cold2 = cool_sample(sample=wash_spin1, temperature_c=0, duration_min=1.0, device=m9_cold)
wash_spin2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=cold2, speed_xg=150, duration_min=2.5, temperature_c=4)
concentrated_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=microfuge_pellet, speed_xg=800, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22)
sds_incubation = incubate_sample(sample=sds_dtt_sample, temperature_c=22, duration_min=4.0, condition="rocking")
recovery_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=egg_buffer_sample, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=22)
wash1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=recovery_spin, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=22)
wash2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash1, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=22)
ready_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash2, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=22)

source_steps.stage: Dissociating worms into single cell suspensions
source_steps.description: Collect worms by washing plates with 10 mL of M9 per plate and transfer solution to a 50 mL conical tube.

source_steps.stage: Dissociating worms into single cell suspensions
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 1000 × g (2500 rpm on Thermo CL-2 centrifuge) for 2.5 min. Discard supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Dissociating worms into single cell suspensions
source_steps.description: Separate worms from bacteria by resuspending pellet in 50 mL of ice-cold M9 and centrifuging at 150 × g (1000 rpm on Thermo CL-2 centrifuge) for 2.5 min. Remove supernatant, and repeat.

source_steps.stage: Dissociating worms into single cell suspensions
source_steps.description: Transfer the pellet from the 50 mL tube to a 1.7 mL microcentrifuge tube. Add M9 if necessary to bring the volume up to 1.5 mL.

source_steps.stage: Dissociating worms into single cell suspensions
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 500–1000 × g for 1 min and discard supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Dissociating worms into single cell suspensions
source_steps.description: Permeabilize worms by treatment with SDS-DTT solution. Add SDS-DTT at 2× the volume of the worm pellet (e.g., 500 μL of freshly thawed SDS-DTT for 250 μL of pelleted worms).

source_steps.stage: Dissociating worms into single cell suspensions
source_steps.description: Incubate for 2–6 min at room temperature (22°C) on the nutator ( Figure 2 A). Note: A two-minute incubation with SDS-DTT is typically sufficient for dissociating most neurons types from early stage larvae. Longer incubation times (4–6 min) are usually needed for older larval and adult animals ( Figure 2 ). Longer incubation times release more cells from the mid-body, but may also be detrimental to the health of head/tail cells.

source_steps.stage: Dissociating worms into single cell suspensions
source_steps.description: Add egg buffer to 1.5 mL, close lid and invert the tube to stop the SDS-DTT treatment.

source_steps.stage: Dissociating worms into single cell suspensions
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 16,000 × g at room temperature (22°C) for 30 s. Remove supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Dissociating worms into single cell suspensions
source_steps.description: Wash the pellet 5× with 1 mL of egg buffer, centrifuging at 16,000 × g for 30 s. On the last wash, transfer the pellet to a new tube.

source_steps.stage: Dissociating worms into single cell suspensions
source_steps.description: Incubate with pronase (≤20 min) to dissociate cells. Prepare a 15 mg/mL solution of pronase in egg buffer. Prepare enough for 2× the original worm pellet volume for each sample. CRITICAL: Ensure that pronase is completely dissolved before proceeding.

source_steps.stage: Dissociating worms into single cell suspensions
source_steps.description: Add a volume of freshly prepared 15 mg/mL pronase that is 2× the size of the pellet. Incubate for 20–25 min at room temperature (22°C) on a rocking nutator. CRITICAL: Incubation in pronase alone will not dissociate animals. Mechanical disruption by trituration is necessary as described below.

background: This Day 3 segment of the wastewater SARS-CoV-2 RNA workflow starts from the recovered aqueous phase after extraction. The RNA in this fraction is precipitated with isopropanol, collected by refrigerated centrifugation, washed twice with cold 75% ethanol without disturbing the pellet, and then briefly air-dried on ice before later elution.
goal: Cleanly recover an RNA pellet from the aqueous wastewater extract by carrying out precipitation, cold pelleting, two ethanol washes, and a short final drying period.
constraints: Add 600 uL room-temperature isopropanol to the aqueous phase so the precipitation uses a 1:1 isopropanol:aqueous ratio, then mix gently by inversion.
Include a 15 minute incubation at room temperature after precipitation setup.
Use centrifugation at 12000 x g and 4 C, with one 10 minute pelleting spin and two later 3 minute wash spins.
Perform exactly two washes with 1 mL freshly made ice-cold 75% ethanol, and do not use vigorous mixing during these wash additions.
Record a pellet-location or visibility QC note after the first pelleting spin so downstream handling reflects pellet preservation.
Finish with a short air-dry on ice for 5-10 minutes, avoiding overdrying.

available_inputs.name: aqueous_phase_tube
available_inputs.description: Tube containing the aqueous phase from wastewater RNA extraction

available_inputs.name: isopropanol_rt
available_inputs.description: Room-temperature isopropanol used for RNA precipitation

available_inputs.name: ethanol75_cold
available_inputs.description: Freshly prepared ice-cold 75% ethanol for RNA pellet washing

gold_program:
precipitation_mix = mix_sample(sample=aqueous_phase_tube, method="inversion after adding 600 uL isopropanol_rt for 1:1 precipitation", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
precipitation_hold = incubate_sample(sample=precipitation_mix, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=15.0, condition="static")
first_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=precipitation_hold, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
pellet_note = record_measurement(sample=first_pellet, metric="pellet_qc", unit="note", note="Pellet expected on tube side facing outside of rotor; preserve during supernatant removal")
first_wash_added = cool_sample(sample=first_pellet, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=0.5, device="1 mL ethanol75_cold added gently without mixing")
first_wash_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=first_wash_added, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=4.0)
second_wash_added = cool_sample(sample=first_wash_pellet, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=0.5, device="fresh 1 mL ethanol75_cold added gently without mixing")
second_wash_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=second_wash_added, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=4.0)
final_dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=second_wash_pellet, method="air dry on ice", temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=7.0)

source_steps.stage: Day 3: Wastewater viral concentration and RNA extraction
source_steps.description: Add 600 μL of room temperature isopropanol into the aqueous phase (isopropanol:aqueous phase = 1:1 ratio), and invert the tube gently 10 times to mix.

source_steps.stage: Day 3: Wastewater viral concentration and RNA extraction
source_steps.description: Incubate at room temperature for 15 min.

source_steps.stage: Day 3: Wastewater viral concentration and RNA extraction
source_steps.description: Mark the side of the tube you expect the pellet to form and make sure to label the tube to help distinguish from the water balance if processing an odd number of tubes. CRITICAL: Make sure to mark on the side of the tube, rather than at the actual location of the pellet, to not inhibit your view of the pellet. Note: The pellet should be localized on the side of the tube facing the outside of the rotor.

source_steps.stage: Day 3: Wastewater viral concentration and RNA extraction
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 10 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Day 3: Wastewater viral concentration and RNA extraction
source_steps.description: Carefully remove the tubes from the centrifuge. An opaque pellet may be able to be seen on the bottom of the tube facing the outside of the rotor. Note: It is usually easier to see the pellet once all the supernatant is removed. Note: In some instances, it is very difficult to visualize the pellet. If you cannot see the pellet, trust the mark you made on the tube prior to centrifuging.

source_steps.stage: Day 3: Wastewater viral concentration and RNA extraction
source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant. Note: Tilt the tube away from the pellet and slowly draw up the supernatant using a p1000 until there is only a little supernatant left. Then switch to a p200 and touch the tip to the bottom of the tube on the opposite side of the pellet to remove the remaining liquid. Be very careful not to disturb the pellet, as this is your RNA that will be quantified!

source_steps.stage: Day 3: Wastewater viral concentration and RNA extraction
source_steps.description: RNA Wash and Elution. Add 1 mL of ice cold, 75% ethanol (100% ethanol:nuclease-free water = 3:1) to wash the pellet. Note: To make 75% ethanol add 250 μL DEPC treated water and 750 μL of 200 proof ethanol. Note: Do not invert or pipette up and down to avoid disrupting the pellet. Simply place the pipette tip on the side of the tube (not near the pellet) and add the 75% ethanol slowly.

source_steps.stage: Day 3: Wastewater viral concentration and RNA extraction
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 3 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Day 3: Wastewater viral concentration and RNA extraction
source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant. Note: Tilt the tube away from the pellet and slowly draw up the supernatant using a p1000 until there is only a little supernatant left. Then switch to a p200 and touch the tip to the bottom of the tube on the opposite side of the pellet to remove the remaining liquid. Be very careful not to disturb the pellet, as this is your RNA that will be quantified! Note: After this step, you will be adding more ethanol, so you don’t need to be overly aggressive with removing all the liquid in this step and potentially risk disrupting the pellet and losing RNA.

source_steps.stage: Day 3: Wastewater viral concentration and RNA extraction
source_steps.description: Repeat the wash once again with 1 mL of freshly made, ice-cold 75% ethanol. CRITICAL: Do not invert or pipette up and down to avoid disrupting the pellet. Simply place the pipette tip on the side of the tube (not near the pellet) and add the 75% ethanol slowly.

source_steps.stage: Day 3: Wastewater viral concentration and RNA extraction
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 3 min at 4°C. Discard the supernatant. Note: Tilt the tube away from the pellet and slowly draw up the supernatant using a p1000 until there is only a little supernatant left. Then switch to a p200 and touch the tip to the bottom of the tube on the opposite side of the pellet to remove the remaining liquid. Be very careful not to disturb the pellet, as this is your RNA that will be quantified! Note: After this step, the pellet will air dry. In order to not have the sample air dry too long, you must remove as much liquid as possible.

source_steps.stage: Day 3: Wastewater viral concentration and RNA extraction
source_steps.description: Let the RNA pellet air dry on ice for 5–10 min (do not over-dry the pellet).

background: This experiment tracks SARS-CoV-2 RNA in wastewater over time by concentrating viral material, extracting RNA, and preparing it for downstream RT-qPCR with PMMoV-based normalization. The current stage is the late RNA extraction workflow after precipitation, where the pellet must be washed under cold conditions, briefly dried, and brought back into water before setting aside assay aliquots and documenting expected assay volumes.
goal: Process the centrifuged wastewater RNA pellet through the cold ethanol wash and recovery stage, then prepare the recovered RNA for downstream SARS-CoV-2 N1 and PMMoV RT-qPCR use while preserving pellet integrity.
constraints: Begin from a wastewater RNA pellet sample that requires cold centrifugation-based cleanup.
Use centrifugation at 12000 x g and 4 C, with one longer spin of 10 min already associated with pellet recovery and two short wash spins of 3 min each during cleanup.
Maintain cold handling during the wash sequence; ethanol wash conditions correspond to ice-cold 75% ethanol and the pellet should remain compatible with handling on ice.
The pellet must undergo two ethanol wash rounds before drying and resuspension.
Avoid aggressive mixing during ethanol wash handling because the pellet can be difficult to see and must not be disrupted.
After the final wash, remove residual liquid as completely as possible before drying so the pellet does not remain wet too long.
Dry only briefly by air on ice for 5 to 10 min; do not over-dry.
Resuspend the recovered pellet in exactly 40 uL DEPC-treated water.
Record downstream aliquot planning for two assays using the recovered RNA: 18 uL for SARS-CoV-2 N1 triplicate runs and 9 uL for PMMoV triplicate runs.
Preserve a dependency chain in which the final documented RNA state arises from the same pellet that was washed and dried.

available_inputs.name: rna_pellet_tube
available_inputs.description: Wastewater RNA pellet in a 50 mL centrifuge tube after precipitation and prior recovery cleanup.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_wash_1
available_inputs.description: First freshly prepared ice-cold 75% ethanol wash reagent.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_wash_2
available_inputs.description: Second freshly prepared ice-cold 75% ethanol wash reagent.

available_inputs.name: depc_water
available_inputs.description: DEPC-treated nuclease-free water for final RNA resuspension.

gold_program:
pellet_recovered = centrifuge_sample(sample=rna_pellet_tube, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
wash1_added = mix_sample(sample=pellet_recovered, method=ethanol_wash_1, duration_s=5, speed="slow side-wall addition")
wash1_spun = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash1_added, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=3, temperature_c=4)
wash2_added = mix_sample(sample=wash1_spun, method=ethanol_wash_2, duration_s=5, speed="slow side-wall addition")
wash2_spun = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash2_added, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=3, temperature_c=4)
pellet_chilled = cool_sample(sample=wash2_spun, temperature_c=0, duration_min=1, device="ice")
pellet_dried = dry_sample(sample=pellet_chilled, method="air dry on ice", temperature_c=0, duration_min=7)
rna_resuspended = mix_sample(sample=pellet_dried, method=depc_water, duration_s=10, speed="gentle resuspension to 40 uL final volume")
n1_aliquot_record = record_measurement(sample=rna_resuspended, metric="SARS-CoV-2 N1 aliquot volume", unit="uL", note="18 uL reserved for triplicate RT-qPCR with pipetting overage")
pmmov_aliquot_record = record_measurement(sample=rna_resuspended, metric="PMMoV aliquot volume", unit="uL", note="9 uL reserved for triplicate RT-qPCR with pipetting overage")
protocol_title: Protocol to longitudinally quantify SARS-CoV-2 RNA in wastewater using RT-qPCR and pepper mild mottle virus normalization

background: Human ILC2s have already been magnetically enriched from peripheral blood and are now at the local enrichment-to-expansion stage. The task focuses on the post-magnetic cleanup wash, short-term culture setup in a 96-well round-bottom plate, monitoring through the expansion window, and preparation of a day-10 aliquot for downstream purity staining.
goal: Process the enriched human ILC2 sample through washing, seeding for expansion, interval culture monitoring, and preparation of a representative day-10 aliquot for antibody staining setup.
constraints: Use the enriched ILC2 suspension as the starting material for this segment.
Include a PBS wash performed by centrifugation at 650 x g for 5 min at 21 C before culture setup.
Culture the enriched cells in a 96-well round-bottom plate under expansion conditions corresponding to 37 C and 8% CO2.
The seeded culture should reflect the stated protocol density context of 2 x 10^5 cells in 200 uL per well, even if the action pool cannot encode volume or cell counting directly.
Represent culture monitoring across the 10-day expansion window using repeated incubation-style actions rather than a single direct jump.
At day 10, work from a representative aliquot intended for purity checking in a FACS tube.
Before staining setup, wash the aliquot with cold PBS using centrifugation at 650 x g for 5 min at 21 C.
Prepare the aliquot for staining conditions compatible with an antibody mix that includes LIVE/DEAD Fixable Blue, lineage cocktail 1-FITC, and CRTH2-PE; protect the prepared sample from light.
Use only provided action-pool functions and keep the program at protocol level without adding unsupported transfer mechanics.
Reuse at least two action families multiple times with a dependency chain carried forward from earlier outputs.

available_inputs.name: enriched_ilc2_tube
available_inputs.description: Tube containing magnet-enriched human ILC2s after the second magnetic separation.

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer_pbs
available_inputs.description: PBS available for washing enriched cells and the day-10 aliquot.

available_inputs.name: culture_plate_96rb
available_inputs.description: 96-well round-bottom plate prepared for ILC2 expansion culture.

available_inputs.name: facs_aliquot_tube
available_inputs.description: FACS tube intended to hold the representative day-10 cell aliquot.

available_inputs.name: staining_ready_sample
available_inputs.description: Day-10 aliquot after antibody components have been added in staining buffer.

gold_program:
washed_ilc2_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=enriched_ilc2_tube, speed_xg=650, duration_min=5, temperature_c=21)
seeded_ilc2_plate = incubate_sample(sample=culture_plate_96rb, temperature_c=37, duration_min=1, condition="humidified 8% CO2 static")
expanded_ilc2_plate_day2 = incubate_sample(sample=seeded_ilc2_plate, temperature_c=37, duration_min=2880, condition="humidified 8% CO2 static")
expanded_ilc2_plate_day4 = incubate_sample(sample=expanded_ilc2_plate_day2, temperature_c=37, duration_min=2880, condition="humidified 8% CO2 static")
expanded_ilc2_plate_day10 = incubate_sample(sample=expanded_ilc2_plate_day4, temperature_c=37, duration_min=8640, condition="humidified 8% CO2 static")
cooled_facs_aliquot = cool_sample(sample=facs_aliquot_tube, temperature_c=4, duration_min=1, device="ice")
washed_day10_aliquot = centrifuge_sample(sample=cooled_facs_aliquot, speed_xg=650, duration_min=5, temperature_c=21)
cooled_postwash_aliquot = cool_sample(sample=washed_day10_aliquot, temperature_c=4, duration_min=1, device="ice")

source_steps.description: Add Magnetic Particles from EasySep Human ILC2 Enrichment Kit to cells: 25 μL per 1 mL of sample and incubate for 1 min at 21°C.
Add EasySep Buffer to the sample and gently mix.
Place the 14 mL tube without lid into “The Big Easy” magnet and incubate for 5 min at 21°C to allow labelled non-ILC2s to adhere to the walls of the tube.
Transfer the medium containing enriched ILC2s to a new 14 mL round-bottom tube by inverting the magnet and tube in a single fluid movement.

Put the new tube without lid in the magnet and incubate for 5 min at 21°C to allow labelled non-ILC2s to adhere to the walls of the tube for the second time. This step is needed to further enrich the ILC2s.
Transfer the medium containing enriched ILC2s in a new 14 mL round-bottom tube by inverting the magnet and tube in a single fluid movement.
Add 10 mL of PBS and centrifugate at 650 g for 5 min at 21°C to wash ILC2.

Seed the enriched ILC2s in a 96-well round-bottom plate at a density of 2 × 10 5 cells/200 μL/well in CTS NK-Xpander basal medium with the cytokines IL-2, IL-7, and IL-1β (50 ng/ml each) in the incubator at 37°C and 8% CO 2 .

source_steps.stage: Expansion of human ILC2s following enrichment
source_steps.description: Check the cells and their confluence every 1–2 days.

source_steps.stage: Expansion of human ILC2s following enrichment
source_steps.description: At day 10, harvest a representative cell aliquot to check the purity of the cell population. Transfer 30 μL of cells into a FACS tube.

source_steps.stage: Expansion of human ILC2s following enrichment
source_steps.description: Wash the cells with 1 mL of cold PBS by centrifugation at 650 g for 5 min at 21°C and discard the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Expansion of human ILC2s following enrichment
source_steps.description: Prepare an antibody mix with the LIVE/DEAD Fixable Blue Dead Cell Stain Kit (diluted 1:100), anti-lineage cocktail 1 -FITC (diluted 1:10) and anti-CRTH2-PE (diluted 1:50) antibody in staining buffer (consider 20 μL per sample, always prepare at least 10% excess to account for pipetting errors).

background: This experiment isolates fluorescently labeled C. elegans neurons and is now in the RNA cleanup stage after binding material to a spin column. The immediate work focuses on column-based purification, including buffer washes, a final elution, optional handling for DNA digestion setup, and cold storage of the recovered RNA for downstream quality analysis and sequencing.
goal: Process the bound RNA on the purification column through the remaining wash and elution operations, then place the recovered RNA into frozen storage for later analysis.
constraints: Use the spin column sample as the starting material and maintain a single dependency chain through wash and elution handling.
All centrifugation steps in this segment use 16000 x g and 30 s or 2 min durations, which normalize to 0.5 min and 2.0 min.
Use room-temperature handling for centrifugation and incubation steps unless a colder storage condition is explicitly required.
The wash sequence includes one prep-buffer spin, one 700 uL wash-buffer spin, and one 400 uL wash-buffer spin with the longer 2 min centrifugation used to clear residual wash buffer.
After the final wash-clearing spin, the material is considered moved into a fresh RNase-free tube context before elution.
Elution is performed with DNase/RNase-free water applied directly to the column matrix; use the standard 15 uL condition rather than the lower-volume concentration option.
The eluate should be preserved immediately by freezing at -80 C after recovery.
Do not execute the optional DNase I digestion branch as part of the main solution.

available_inputs.name: rna_spin_column
available_inputs.description: Zymo spin column containing bound RNA after earlier purification steps

available_inputs.name: rna_prep_buffer
available_inputs.description: RNA prep buffer used for the first cleanup wash

available_inputs.name: rna_wash_buffer_700
available_inputs.description: RNA wash buffer aliquot corresponding to the 700 uL wash condition

available_inputs.name: rna_wash_buffer_400
available_inputs.description: RNA wash buffer aliquot corresponding to the 400 uL wash condition

available_inputs.name: rnase_free_tube
available_inputs.description: Fresh RNase-free microcentrifuge tube for the post-wash column context

available_inputs.name: dnase_rnase_free_water
available_inputs.description: Nuclease-free water used to elute RNA from the column

available_inputs.name: freezer_box
available_inputs.description: Frozen storage location for purified RNA

gold_program:
prep_buffer_applied = mix_sample(sample=rna_spin_column, method='pipetting', duration_s=5, speed='gentle')
prep_buffer_spun = centrifuge_sample(sample=prep_buffer_applied, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=22)
wash700_applied = mix_sample(sample=prep_buffer_spun, method='pipetting', duration_s=5, speed='gentle')
wash700_spun = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash700_applied, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=22)
wash400_applied = mix_sample(sample=wash700_spun, method='pipetting', duration_s=5, speed='gentle')
wash400_spun = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash400_applied, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=22)
column_in_new_tube = mix_sample(sample=wash400_spun, method='pipetting', duration_s=5, speed='gentle')
elution_water_applied = mix_sample(sample=column_in_new_tube, method='pipetting', duration_s=5, speed='gentle')
rna_eluate = centrifuge_sample(sample=elution_water_applied, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=22)
frozen_rna = store_sample(sample=rna_eluate, temperature_c=-80, duration_h=24.0, container=freezer_box)

source_steps.description: Add 400 μL of RNA Prep Buffer to the column and centrifuge (16,000 × g ) for 30 s. Discard flow-through.
Add 700 μL of RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge (16,000 × g ) for 30 s. Discard flow-through.
Add 400 μL of RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge (16,000 × g ) for 2 min to ensure complete removal of wash buffer. Transfer the column to a new RNase-free microcentrifuge tube.
Add 15 μL of DNase/RNase-Free water directly to the column matrix and centrifuge for 30 s to elute bound RNA. As little as 6 μL could be added for more highly concentrated RNA.
Use eluted RNA immediately or store at −80°C. Aliquot 2 μL for Bioanalysis (Agilent 2100 Bionanalyzer Pico Chip) and freeze the remainder at −80°C.

Optional DNase I treatment to remove DNA. Note: We typically do not treat with DNase I since it reduces RNA yield and residual DNA does not interfere with RNA-seq library construction in our hands. Add 400 μL of RNA Wash Buffer to the Zymo-Spin IC Column.
Centrifuge at 16,000 × g (full-speed) for 30 s. Discard flow-through.
Prepare DNase I reaction mix in a separate RNase-free tube for each sample.
Add 35 μL of DNA Digestion Buffer and 5 μL of DNase I for each sample.
Mix well by gentle inversion.
Add 40 μL of reaction mix directly to the column matrix.
Incubate at room temperature (22°C) for 15 min then go to step 25.

background: Adult human skin has already been split into epidermal and dermal fractions for parallel primary cell isolation. The current local stage focuses on the dermis-derived endothelial fraction: endothelial growth medium must be warmed, an Attachment Factor-coated flask must be readied and equilibrated, the extruded endothelial suspension in a 50 mL tube must be recovered by a cold gentle spin, and the recovered cells must be placed into primary culture. Epidermal strips may be held cold only for a short same-day delay.
goal: Recover the dermal endothelial cell fraction and establish it in an equilibrated coated flask for overnight primary culture while preserving a short cold hold state for the epidermal strips.
constraints: Warm ECGM to 37 C before using it for flask equilibration or pellet resuspension.
Attachment Factor coating on the T75 flask requires 30 min at 37 C, with no rinse afterward, followed by addition of 15 mL ECGM and at least 30 min equilibration at 37 C.
The dermis-derived endothelial suspension in the 50 mL conical must be centrifuged at about 250 x g for 7 min at 4 C.
Treat the post-spin pellet as fragile and resuspend it gently in 2 mL ECGM per T75 flask before inoculation.
After inoculation, incubate the endothelial culture overnight for about 16 h at 37 C under a humidified 5% CO2 condition.
Include a temporary 4 C storage state for the epidermal strips for same-day processing rather than overnight.
Use repeated action families and keep later steps dependent on outputs from earlier ones.

available_inputs.name: ecgm_media
available_inputs.description: Endothelial cell growth medium prepared for HDMEC culture

available_inputs.name: attachment_factor_t75
available_inputs.description: Uncoated T75 flask intended for endothelial culture coating

available_inputs.name: extruded_hdmec_suspension_50ml
available_inputs.description: Dermis-derived endothelial cell suspension collected in a 50 mL conical tube

available_inputs.name: epidermal_strips_hbs
available_inputs.description: Epidermal strips held in HBS pending same-day keratinocyte isolation

gold_program:
warmed_ecgm_media = heat_sample(sample=ecgm_media, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=20.0, device='water bath')
coated_t75 = incubate_sample(sample=attachment_factor_t75, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=30.0, condition='static sealed with Attachment Factor')
equilibrated_t75 = incubate_sample(sample=coated_t75, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=30.0, condition='static with 15 mL warmed ECGM')
held_epidermis = store_sample(sample=epidermal_strips_hbs, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=3.0, container='refrigerator dish')
rinsed_hdmec_suspension = mix_sample(sample=extruded_hdmec_suspension_50ml, method='pipetting with ECGM rinse combined in tube', duration_s=20.0, speed='gentle')
spun_hdmec_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=rinsed_hdmec_suspension, speed_xg=250, duration_min=7.0, temperature_c=4.0)
resuspended_hdmec_cells = mix_sample(sample=spun_hdmec_pellet, method='pipetting in 2 mL warmed ECGM', duration_s=15.0, speed='gentle')
inoculated_t75 = mix_sample(sample=equilibrated_t75, method='add 2 mL HDMEC suspension', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
overnight_hdmec_culture = incubate_sample(sample=inoculated_t75, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=960.0, condition='humidified 5% CO2 static')
protocol_title: Isolation and feeder-free primary culture of four cell types from a single human skin sample
