source_steps.description: Prepare FGM (for fibroblasts), “regular” calcium (200 μM) KGM (for keratinocytes), and ECGM (for endothelial cells). Warm to 37°C in water bath before use, protecting from light. Note: KGM-LC (“low calcium,” 0.06 mM CaCl 2 ) is used for feeding primary keratinocytes prior to passage (P0), but KGM with “regular” calcium (0.2 mM) is used during the initial isolation procedure (culture day 0) and the first overnight incubation after inoculation of flasks. After passaging (P1 and beyond), KGM with “regular” calcium (0.2 mM) will be used.

Prepare collagen-coated flasks for keratinocytes: Coat T225 flasks (∼1 per ∼10 cm 2 of skin) with collagen I using the Collagen I-Cell Culture Surface Coating Kit according to manufacturer’s instructions with minor modifications. Dilute the 100× Collagen I Solvent provided with the kit 1:100 with sterile Milli-Q water and store at 4°C. To coat one T225 flask (225 cm 2 ), dilute collagen I solution (1 mg/mL) 1:10 in 1× Collagen I Solvent and add 11.25 mL per flask (5 μg/cm 2 ). Incubate for a minimum of 1 h at 37°C. Aspirate the collagen I solution and rinse each flask 3 times with 10 mL HBS. To use immediately, add 45 mL KGM to flask and pre-equilibrate in incubator at 37°C for at least 30 min. If not used immediately, air dry the flask in the hood and store at 4°C. Note: For best results, equilibrate all flasks with media in a 37°C, 5% CO2 incubator for ≥30 min before inoculation of cells. We recommend this during the primary procedure and for all later passages.
Prepare T150 flasks for fibroblast inoculation (1 per ∼10 cm 2 of skin). Add 10 mL FGM to each flask and equilibrate in incubator ≥30 min before use.

Prepare Attachment Factor-coated flask(s) for HDMECs according to manufacturer’s instructions. Note that Attachment Factor is a 0.1% gelatin solution; a sterile solution of 0.1% gelatin in water can be used instead of the pre-made product. Coat T75 flask(s) (1 per ∼20 cm 2 of skin) with 3–4 mL Attachment Factor: rock vigorously to completely cover growth surface and incubate in sealed flask for 30 min at 37°C or at least 2 h at room temperature (20°C–22°C). Aspirate liquid (do not rinse) and store flask tightly capped in hood at room temperature (20°C–22°C) for up to 24 h. Before use, add 15 mL ECGM per flask and equilibrate in incubator for ≥30 min before inoculating cells.
Prepare 100 mL KGM + 10% FBS (trypsin/EDTA “stop” solution) and store on ice until ready to use.

Separate epidermis from dermis: Remove tissue strips from Dispase and place in 100 mm dish containing 10 mL HBS. Prepare two additional 100 mm dishes with 20 mL HBS each; label for “epidermis” and “dermis”.

Separate dermis from epidermis. To do this, grasp the dermis with fine tipped forceps, grasp the epidermis with blunt forceps, and peel off epidermis ( troubleshooting 2 ; Figure 2 C). Place epidermal strips in a fresh dish (labeled “epidermis”) with 20 mL HBS ( Figures 2 C and 2D).

Place dish with epidermal strips in a plastic zippered bag to avoid spillage, and store in refrigerator or cold room (4°C) until ready to process for keratinocyte isolation. The epidermal strips can be stored for several hours, while the dermal strips are processed for isolation of HDMEC and fibroblasts, but do not store overnight. Keratinocytes should be isolated the same day to preserve cell viability.

Isolation of HDMEC from dermis: Mechanical force will be used to extrude HDMEC from vessels in the dermis. To do this, you will compress the tissue down the length of each dermal strip by squeezing with forceps, using pressure to force the endothelial cells out from vessels (imagine flattening a tube of toothpaste from the bottom up to get out the last bits of toothpaste). Grasp each dermal strip at one end with forceps, and use blunt forceps to squeeze the dermal strip near that end. Then, run the blunt forceps down the length of the strip, squeezing gently, to extrude the endothelial cells ( Figure 2 E and Methods video S1 ). Repeat ten times for each strip, then transfer strip to fresh dish with 20 mL HBS (labeled “dermis”) for subsequent isolation of fibroblasts. Note: This step must be done carefully because too much force will result in contamination of the HDMEC with fibroblasts, but too little force will result in low yield of endothelial cells. If large amounts of fibrous material are present after the extrusion procedure, this may indicate too much force was used. If this is observed, use less pressure for remaining strips ( troubleshooting 4 ).

After scraping and moving all dermal strips to a new dish with fresh HBS, transfer the extruded cell suspension to a sterile 50 mL conical centrifuge tube. Rinse the dish used for extruding HDMEC several times with small amounts of ECGM, and add to conical tube.

Centrifuge for 7 min at 1000 rpm (∼250× g ), 4°C, without brake. Note: Although spinning without the brake may add a small amount of time to each centrifugation step, we strongly recommend this for primary cells. Using the brake at the end of the spin can disrupt or dislodge the pellet, making it more difficult to remove the supernatant without disturbing the cells, and may cause mechanical damage to fragile primary cells.

Carefully aspirate or pipet off the supernatant. The cell pellet may be difficult to see, and/or may be loose; use care not to aspirate the cell pellet. Resuspend cell pellet in 2 mL ECGM per T75 flask. Inoculate prepared T75 flask(s) (coated with Attachment Factor and equilibrated with medium at 37°C; Day 2, step 4h) with 2 mL of the cell suspension per flask and incubate overnight (∼16 h) at 37°C, 5% CO 2 . Note: For isolation of HDMEC from skin pieces smaller than 10 cm 2 , T25 flask(s) may be used instead of T75 flasks.

background: A recombinant coronavirus spike protein expressed in ExpiCHO supernatant is being captured on Ni-NTA agarose before column elution. At this stage, the resin must be cleaned of storage solution, equilibrated through repeated wash spins, then combined with clarified expression supernatant supplemented with salt and imidazole and allowed to bind under cold mixing conditions.
goal: Prepare Ni-NTA resin for spike capture and generate the bead-bound ExpiCHO supernatant ready for loading onto the gravity column.
constraints: Use benchtop microcentrifugation for the resin cleanup spins at 4000 x g for 1 minute at room temperature.
Perform three total wash-buffer centrifuge cycles on the resin before combining it with the expression supernatant.
Model the wash cycles as repeated dependency-linked operations rather than restarting from the original resin each time.
After the wash sequence, combine the washed resin with the ExpiCHO supernatant and supplement the mixture to 150 mM NaCl and 10 mM imidazole as part of the prepared binding sample.
Include a cold binding incubation at 4 C for 40 minutes with a mixing or rotating condition.
Keep the output focused on the bead-containing binding mixture; do not include gravity-column equilibration or elution steps.
Use only provided action-pool functions and keyword arguments.
Include at least two action families that recur multiple times, with one repeated family differing only by the input sample carried forward.

gold_program:
resin_spin_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=ni_nta_resin, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=22)
resin_wash_mix_1 = mix_sample(sample=resin_spin_1, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
resin_spin_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=resin_wash_mix_1, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=22)
resin_wash_mix_2 = mix_sample(sample=resin_spin_2, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
resin_spin_3 = centrifuge_sample(sample=resin_wash_mix_2, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=22)
resin_wash_mix_3 = mix_sample(sample=resin_spin_3, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
resin_spin_4 = centrifuge_sample(sample=resin_wash_mix_3, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=22)
binding_mix = mix_sample(sample=nacl_imidazole_supplemented_supernatant, method="pipetting", duration_s=15, speed="moderate")
bead_supernatant_binding_sample = mix_sample(sample=binding_mix, method="pipetting", duration_s=15, speed="moderate")
cold_bound_beads = incubate_sample(sample=bead_supernatant_binding_sample, temperature_c=4, duration_min=40, condition="rotating")

source_steps.description: Shake on a shaker at 200 rpm for 1 hr at 4°C (this incubation time can be increased to overnight).
Remove the primary antibody solution. Add enough PBST to cover the membrane, allow to wash on a shaker at 200 rpm for 10 min, and remove the PBST. Repeat two more times.
After removing the PBST from the final wash, add the secondary antibody diluted to 1/20,000 with PBST.
Shake on a shaker at 200 rpm for 1 hr at 4°C.
Remove the secondary antibody solution and wash the membrane again as described in step 70.
After washing, the western blot is ready to be visualized.

Add 1 ml Ni‐NTA agarose resin (500 µl Ni beads) to a microcentrifuge tube and centrifuge for 1 min in a benchtop centrifuge at 4000 × g , room temperature, using a Pico 21 microcentrifuge with 24 × 1.5/2.0 Pico 21 microcentrifuge with 24 × 1.5/2.0 ml rotorml rotor. Once the beads have been separated from the storage solution, aspirate the solution.
Add 1 ml wash buffer, centrifuge again as in step 75, and decant wash buffer. Repeat twice more and then add the beads to the ExpiCHO expression supernatant from step 54.

Add 150 mM NaCl and 10 mM imidazole to the ExpiCHO supernatant and rotate tube for 40 min at 4°C (this can be increased to 2 hr if needed). If using 5 M NaCl and imidazole stocks, use 15 µl of NaCl and 10 µl imidazole per milliliter of supernatant instead. CAUTION : Imidazole is toxic if inhaled, swallowed, or in contact with skin. If making imidazole solution from powder stock, the bottle should only be opened in a fume hood with reagent‐impermeable gloves. For further information, see Sigma‐Aldrich Safety Data Sheet.
Equilibrate the gravity flow column by adding 3 ml wash buffer and allowing it to flow through.
Add the sample supernatant containing Ni‐NTA beads to the column. Collect all fractions, including the flowthrough, in case protein flows off the column prior to elution.
Stop the column with the provided stopper, add 500 µl elution buffer, and incubate for 3 min.

background: PBMCs from healthy donor blood have already been recovered from the density-gradient interface, and the workflow is now focused on the local ILC2 enrichment and startup culture stage. The remaining work is to carry the PBMC fraction through a wash, short room-temperature enrichment reagent exposures, two magnetic depletion rounds, a final wash, and then place the enriched cells into cytokine-containing culture conditions.
goal: Process the recovered PBMC fraction through the magnetic enrichment stage and leave the enriched human ILC2 population in expansion culture conditions.
constraints: Begin with the PBMC interface fraction and maintain a single forward sample lineage through the procedure.
Include one PBS-equivalent wash before enrichment and one wash after the second magnetic round, each modeled as centrifugation at 650 x g for 5 min at 21 C.
Represent enrichment handling at 21 C with separate incubations that include a 10 min exposure and a 1 min exposure before magnetic depletion.
Model two magnetic enrichment rounds at 21 C, each lasting 5 min, with explicit magnet opening and closing actions associated with the rounds.
Use only the provided action-pool functions and keyword arguments.
Keep the abstraction at device/incubation level rather than manual transfer or pipetting details.
End by placing the resulting enriched cells into culture conditions corresponding to 37 C and humidified 8% CO2.
Use a long dependency chain so later steps consume previously generated variables, and reuse at least two action families multiple times.

available_inputs.description: PBMC fraction collected from the density-gradient interface in a 50 mL tube

available_inputs.name: culture_plate
available_inputs.description: 96-well round-bottom plate prepared with CTS NK-Xpander basal medium and IL-2, IL-7, and IL-1beta

gold_program:
wash1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=pbmc_ring, speed_xg=650, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=21.0)
reagent_incubation1 = incubate_sample(sample=wash1, temperature_c=21.0, duration_min=10.0, condition="with enrichment cocktail")
reagent_incubation2 = incubate_sample(sample=reagent_incubation1, temperature_c=21.0, duration_min=1.0, condition="with magnetic particles")
magnet_open1 = open_device(device=big_easy_magnet, target="slot", mode="load")
mag_round1 = incubate_sample(sample=reagent_incubation2, temperature_c=21.0, duration_min=5.0, condition="in Big Easy magnet")
magnet_close1 = close_device(device=big_easy_magnet, target="slot", mode="loaded")
magnet_open2 = open_device(device=big_easy_magnet, target="slot", mode="reload")
mag_round2 = incubate_sample(sample=mag_round1, temperature_c=21.0, duration_min=5.0, condition="second enrichment in Big Easy magnet")
magnet_close2 = close_device(device=big_easy_magnet, target="slot", mode="loaded")
wash2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=mag_round2, speed_xg=650, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=21.0)
culture_start = incubate_sample(sample=wash2, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=1.0, condition="humidified 8% CO2 in cytokine medium")

source_steps.description: Collect the PBMC ring to a 50 mL tube.
Wash the PBMC with 30 mL of PBS by centrifugation at 650 g for 5 min.
Collect the PBMC to a 14 mL round-bottom tube and resuspend 1 × 10 8 cells/mL in EasySep Buffer.
Add Enrichment Cocktail from EasySep Human ILC2 Enrichment Kit to cells: 25 μL per 1 mL of sample and incubate for 10 min at 21°C.
Add Magnetic Particles from EasySep Human ILC2 Enrichment Kit to cells: 25 μL per 1 mL of sample and incubate for 1 min at 21°C.
Add EasySep Buffer to the sample and gently mix.
Place the 14 mL tube without lid into “The Big Easy” magnet and incubate for 5 min at 21°C to allow labelled non-ILC2s to adhere to the walls of the tube.
Transfer the medium containing enriched ILC2s to a new 14 mL round-bottom tube by inverting the magnet and tube in a single fluid movement.

Put the new tube without lid in the magnet and incubate for 5 min at 21°C to allow labelled non-ILC2s to adhere to the walls of the tube for the second time. This step is needed to further enrich the ILC2s.
Transfer the medium containing enriched ILC2s in a new 14 mL round-bottom tube by inverting the magnet and tube in a single fluid movement.
Add 10 mL of PBS and centrifugate at 650 g for 5 min at 21°C to wash ILC2.

Seed the enriched ILC2s in a 96-well round-bottom plate at a density of 2 × 10 5 cells/200 μL/well in CTS NK-Xpander basal medium with the cytokines IL-2, IL-7, and IL-1β (50 ng/ml each) in the incubator at 37°C and 8% CO 2 .

background: Mouse epiblast stem cell spheroids grown in Matrigel are being processed at the passaging stage. The current material has already been exposed to matrix-dissolving and dissociation reagents, and the remaining work focuses on the cold spin and wash cycle needed to recover small cell clumps suitable for replating.
goal: Process the dissociating 3D EpiSC suspension through the required recovery, wash, and pellet-loosening stage so that a replating-ready cell suspension is obtained for subsequent mixing with cold Matrigel.
constraints: Include the initial 37 C incubation for 20 min after Matrigel breakup, and a second 37 C incubation for 4 min after TrypLE exposure.
Use centrifugation at 300 x g for 4 min at 4 C for each required spin in this segment.
Represent repeated pipette resuspension events with a mixing action, and preserve the idea that the pellet is washed after enzymatic dissociation before final recovery.
The workflow should include three cold centrifugation events separated by reagent-driven mixing or incubation states.
After the final spin, the pellet must be loosened and treated as the replating-ready material rather than jumping directly to plating medium addition.
Do not include downstream Matrigel plating, well setup, freezing medium preparation, or storage.
Maintain a single dependency chain where each new state comes from the previous processed sample.

step2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step1, speed_xg=300, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
step3 = mix_sample(sample=tryple_cell_mix, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="moderate")
step4 = incubate_sample(sample=step3, temperature_c=37, duration_min=4, condition="static")
step5 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step4, speed_xg=300, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
step6 = mix_sample(sample=wash_buffer_cell_mix, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="moderate")
step7 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step6, speed_xg=300, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
step8 = mix_sample(sample=step7, method="pipetting", duration_s=5, speed="gentle")

source_steps.description: Add 1 mL of dispase for every 2 drops and pipette up and down 10 times to break the Matrigel. Incubate the plate at 37°C for 20 min.
Collect the cell suspension in a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube and centrifuge it at 300 × g , 4°C for 4 min.
Carefully remove the supernatant, add 1 mL of TrypLE, pipette it up and down 10 times and incubate at 37°C for 4 min.
Centrifuge the cells at 300 × g , 4°C for 4 min.
After centrifugation, carefully remove the supernatant, add 1 mL of wash buffer and pipette up and down 10 times to wash the pellet containing small clumps of cells.
Centrifuge the cells at 300 × g , 4°C for 4 min.
Carefully remove the supernatant and loosen up the pellet by rubbing the Eppendorf tube against the grid of the tissue culture hood. With a pipette, check the final volume of the dissolved pellet.
Transfer to a new tube the volume needed for plating (based on the splitting ratio and the number of drops required).

Add the volume of 100% ice-cold Matrigel needed for the number of drops required using 25 μL per drop. For example, if you require 4 drops, add 100 μL of Matrigel, pipette up and down avoiding bubbles, and plate 25 μL per drop (maximum 2 drops per well in a 24-well plate). See Figure 2 A.
Place the plate in the incubator for 10 min to allow the Matrigel to solidify and add 600 μL of 3D FAXN medium per well.

Freezing down 3D EpiSCs. Note: Before starting, cool down a microcentrifuge to 4°C. To dissociate the 3D EpiSCs, follow the steps described in the section “ passaging 3D EpiSCs ”, from steps 16 to 22.

After washing the pellet with wash buffer and centrifugating it, remove the supernatant and resuspend the cells in the freezing medium containing 500 μL of 3D FAXN medium, 400 μL of FBS and 100 μL of DMSO. Add 10 μM of Rock inhibitor in the freezing medium.

background: A rhodamine-labeled tubulin mixture has been prepared on ice to begin double-cycle GMPCPP microtubule polymerization. The next stage is to clear aggregates, drive the first polymerization, collect and wash the polymerized microtubules, then depolymerize them in cold buffer and start the second polymerization.
goal: Process the prepared tubulin mixture through the first polymerization and recovery cycle, then generate the chilled depolymerized tubulin state and initiate the second GMPCPP-driven polymerization incubation.
constraints: Keep the starting tubulin mixture cold for 5 min before aggregate removal.
Remove aggregates by centrifugation at 279000 x g for 10 min at 4 C.
After clarification, include a cold GMPCPP addition stage equivalent to 0.5 mM final from a 10 mM stock for 5 min on ice before the first warm polymerization.
The first polymerization incubation must be performed at 37 C for 60 min.
Collect polymerized microtubules by centrifugation at 20000 x g for 10 min at 25 C.
The pellet must be washed twice with warm 1x BRB80 before depolymerization handling.
Depolymerization uses cold 1x BRB80 with the tubulin resuspended to about 2 mg/mL, reflecting roughly half-volume recovery from the first cycle.
During depolymerization, maintain the sample on ice for 30 min and include intermittent pipette-style mixing.
Before the second polymerization, include another 5 min cold GMPCPP exposure corresponding to 0.5 mM final.
The final state should be a sample incubating in the second polymerization at 37 C for 120 min.

available_inputs.name: tubulin_mix
available_inputs.description: Tubulin mixture prepared from rhodamine-labeled and unlabeled tubulin in 1x BRB80 on ice

available_inputs.name: gmpcpp_stock
available_inputs.description: 10 mM GMPCPP stock used to trigger polymerization

available_inputs.name: warm_brb80
available_inputs.description: Warm 1x BRB80 buffer for washing the microtubule pellet

available_inputs.name: cold_brb80
available_inputs.description: Cold 1x BRB80 buffer for resuspension and depolymerization

gold_program:
cooled_tubulin = cool_sample(sample=tubulin_mix, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5, device="ice bath")
clarified_tubulin = centrifuge_sample(sample=cooled_tubulin, speed_xg=279000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
primed_first_cycle = cool_sample(sample=clarified_tubulin, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5, device="ice bath")
first_polymerized = incubate_sample(sample=primed_first_cycle, temperature_c=37, duration_min=60, condition="static")
first_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=first_polymerized, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=25)
washed_once = mix_sample(sample=first_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
washed_twice = mix_sample(sample=washed_once, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
depolymerizing_mt = cool_sample(sample=washed_twice, temperature_c=0, duration_min=30, device="ice bath")
mixed_depolymerized = mix_sample(sample=depolymerizing_mt, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
primed_second_cycle = cool_sample(sample=mixed_depolymerized, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5, device="ice bath")
second_polymerizing = incubate_sample(sample=primed_second_cycle, temperature_c=37, duration_min=120, condition="static")

source_steps.description: Snap-freeze and store in −80 ° C . Note: Protein is active for about 1 week after the purification when stored in −80 ° C . Note: Ideal product is about 900 μL of 4 μM protein without concentrating the protein, while about 300 μL of 7 μM with concentrating the protein in the best elution fractions using this protocol is achieved.

Rhodamine-labeled double-cycle GMPCPP MTs. Perform the first cycle polymerization. On ice, mix 2 μL of 10 mg/mL rhodamine-labeled tubulin, 18 μL of 10 mg/mL unlabeled tubulin, and 70.5 μL of 1× BRB80 buffer (20 μM final concentration of total tubulin with 11% labeling).
Incubate the tubulin mix on ice for 5 min.
Ultracentrifuge the tubulin mix at 279,000 × g for 10 min at 4 ° C to remove tubulin aggregates.
Add 5 μL of 10 mM GMPCPP (0.5 mM final concentration) for 5 min on ice.
Polymerize MTs by incubating at 37 ° C for 1 h.
Pellet the MTs by centrifuging at 20,000 × g for 10 min at 25 ° C .
Discard the supernatant and gently wash the pellet with warm 1× BRB80 twice.

Perform MT depolymerization. Resuspend the MT pellet in cold 1× BRB80 such that the final tubulin concentration is ∼2 mg/mL. Note: The usual recovery is ∼50% of tubulins from the first cycle. Therefore, resuspend the pellet in a half of the original volume of the tubulin mix.
Incubate the tubulin mix on ice for 30 min with periodically mixing every 10 min by pipette.
Perform the second cycle polymerization. Add 2.5 μL of 10 mM GMPCPP (0.5 mM final concentration) to the tubulin mix for 5 min on ice.
Incubate at 37 ° C for 2 h.

background: This item covers the post-PCR handling of SARS-CoV-2 tiled amplicons. The sample has already completed amplification in separate Pool A and Pool B reactions and a brief spin. The next stage is to complete bead-based cleanup of the combined amplicons, recover purified product, account for the dilution-based quantification checkpoint, and run the short end-prep temperature treatment used before barcode addition.
goal: Carry a single sample from combined post-PCR amplicons through magnetic bead cleanup, elution, and the subsequent end-prep thermal treatment using only the available device-level actions.
constraints: Begin with Pool A and Pool B products that are already amplified and spun, and treat them as one sample workflow for cleanup.
The cleanup context must include 50 uL AMPure XP beads, a 10 minute room-temperature binding period, and a 5 minute magnetic separation period.
Model two 80% ethanol washes explicitly, then remove residual ethanol with a distinct drying step before elution.
Represent elution in 15 uL nuclease-free water, include the 1:10 dilution quantification checkpoint, and continue into end-prep rather than storage.
The end-prep context is a 15 uL reaction using about 200 ng input and must include sequential 20 C for 5 minutes and 65 C for 5 minutes incubations.

available_inputs.name: pool_a_pcr
available_inputs.description: PCR amplicon product from primer Pool A for one sample

available_inputs.name: pool_b_pcr
available_inputs.description: PCR amplicon product from primer Pool B for the same sample

available_inputs.name: combined_plate_well
available_inputs.description: Well containing the merged Pool A and Pool B amplicons after setup with beads

available_inputs.name: magnetic_plate
available_inputs.description: Plate or plate position used during magnetic bead separation

available_inputs.name: endprep_plate
available_inputs.description: Plate or tube containing the prepared end-prep reaction

gold_program:
binding_incubation = incubate_sample(sample=combined_plate_well, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=10.0, condition="static with 50 uL AMPure XP beads")
magnetic_capture = incubate_sample(sample=magnetic_plate, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=5.0, condition="magnetic separation")
ethanol_wash_1 = incubate_sample(sample=magnetic_capture, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=1.0, condition="80% ethanol wash")
ethanol_wash_2 = incubate_sample(sample=ethanol_wash_1, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=1.0, condition="80% ethanol wash")
dried_beads = dry_sample(sample=ethanol_wash_2, method="air dry", temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=5.0)
eluted_amplicon = incubate_sample(sample=dried_beads, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=1.0, condition="elute in 15 uL nuclease-free water")
qubit_checkpoint = incubate_sample(sample=eluted_amplicon, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=1.0, condition="1:10 dilution for Qubit HS dsDNA assay")
endprep_mixed = incubate_sample(sample=endprep_plate, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=1.0, condition="15 uL end-prep reaction with approximately 200 ng input")
endprep_20c = heat_sample(sample=endprep_mixed, temperature_c=20.0, duration_min=5.0, device="thermal cycler")
endprep_65c = heat_sample(sample=endprep_20c, temperature_c=65.0, duration_min=5.0, device="thermal cycler")

source_steps.description: PCR tiling of SARS-CoV-2 virus. We amplify the SARS-CoV-2 genome from the double-stranded cDNA using ARTIC V3 primers specific for SARS-CoV-2. Note: Divide these primers into two pools: Pool A and Pool B, and dilute to a concentration of 10 nM before being utilized in the subsequent step. Prepare the reaction mixture according to the following instructions. PCR reaction master mix Reagent Volume (pool A) Volume (pool B) Storage Purified double stranded cDNA (∼100 ng) x μl x μl −20°C Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix 12.5 μL 12.5 μL −20°C V2 Primer pool at 10 μM (A or B) 3.7 μL 3.7 μL −20°C V3 Primer pool at 10 μM (A or B) 0.45 μL 0.38 μL −20°C Nuclease-free water 8.35-x μl 8.42-x μl RT Total 25 μL 25 μL -

Gently mix the reaction plate by pipetting and incubate in a thermal cycler as per the program below. PCR cycling conditions Steps Temperature Time Cycles Initial Denaturation 98°C 30 s 1 Denaturation 98°C 15 s 35 cycles Annealing and Extension 65°C 5 min Hold 4°C forever
Briefly spin the plate after PCR reaction and perform the following steps: Centrifugation: Centrifuge the plate once before proceeding to the purification step.
Combining Pools: Combine the reactions from Pool A and Pool B into a new deep 96-well plate, with one well designated for each sample. Then, add 50 μL of resuspended AMPure XP beads to each well.
Incubation: Incubate the plate at room temperature for 10 min.
Magnetic Separation: Subsequently, transfer the plate to a magnetic stand and wait for 5 min. Once the beads have completely moved to the side of the magnet, carefully discard the supernatant.
Ethanol Wash: Wash the pellet with 200 μL of 80% ethanol twice to remove any impurities. Afterwards, remove the residual ethanol by spinning the plate, and allow it to air dry.
Elution: Elute the amplified product using 15 μL of nuclease-free water.
Dilution and Quantification: After diluting the eluted product in 1:10 ratio, quantify using the Qubit High Sensitivity dsDNA assay kit.

End-prep. In order to add the barcodes to the amplicons, short PolyA should be added to the end of the amplicons. Prepare the following reaction mix: Reagent Volume Storage cDNA (∼200 ng) x μL −20°C Nuclease-free water (12.5 - x) μL −20°C Ultra II End-prep reaction buffer 1.75 μL −20°C Ultra II End-prep enzyme mix 0.75 μL −20°C Total 15 μL -
Gently mix the reaction by pipetting up and down 8–10 times and spin down the plate.
Incubate at 20°C for 5 min and 65°C for 5 min in a thermal cycler.

background: This protocol surveys ribosome collision by preparing ribosome-protected RNA fragments for downstream library construction. At this stage, gel-extracted ribosome footprints have been recovered and must be precipitated, washed, dried, resuspended, and then processed through heat denaturation and two thermal-cycler enzymatic incubations before cleanup and interim storage for later gel purification.
goal: Process the recovered ribosome footprint RNA from post-gel extraction through precipitation, ethanol wash, drying, resuspension, denaturation, dephosphorylation, linker ligation, cleanup, and hold the purified linker-ligated RNA in eluted form for the next size-selection stage.
constraints: Begin by recovering the RNA-containing solution from the Spin-X filter by centrifugation at 10000 x g for 1 min at 4 C, ensuring the clarified sample is the one carried forward.

Precipitation must include a -20 C hold for 1 h followed by centrifugation at 20000 x g for 30 min at 4 C, then a 70% ethanol wash with a second centrifugation at 20000 x g for 3 min at 4 C before pellet drying.
Dry the RNA pellet only briefly, within the 2-5 min range, before resuspending it in 7 uL of 10 mM Tris pH 7.5 or RNase-free water in a PCR-compatible tube.
Thermal processing must include denaturation at 95 C for 2 min with the lid at 105 C, then chilling on ice for 3 min before any enzymatic incubation.
Use a thermal cycler for the enzymatic reactions: dephosphorylation at 37 C for 1 h with lid at 57 C, followed in the same tube by linker ligation at 22 C for 3 h with lid at 45 C.
After ligation, the sample should be diluted and column-purified, ending with linker-ligated RNA eluted into 7 uL RNase-free water and held for the next gel-purification stage.

gold_program:
clarified_rna = centrifuge_sample(sample=gel_extraction_mix, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=4)
precipitation_hold = cool_sample(sample=clarified_rna, temperature_c=-20, duration_min=60, device='freezer')
pelleted_rna = centrifuge_sample(sample=precipitation_hold, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=30, temperature_c=4)
washed_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=pelleted_rna, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=3, temperature_c=4)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=washed_pellet, method='vacuum dry', temperature_c=25, duration_min=3)
resuspended_rna = incubate_sample(sample=dried_pellet, temperature_c=25, duration_min=5, condition='resuspended in 7 uL 10 mM Tris pH 7.5 in PCR tube')
denatured_rna = run_thermal_cycler(plate=pcr_tube, program_name='RNA denaturation 95C 2min', cycles=1, lid_temperature_c=105)
chilled_rna = cool_sample(sample=denatured_rna, temperature_c=0, duration_min=3, device='ice')
dephosphorylated_rna = run_thermal_cycler(plate=pcr_tube, program_name='dephosphorylation 37C 1h', cycles=1, lid_temperature_c=57)
ligated_rna = run_thermal_cycler(plate=pcr_tube, program_name='linker ligation 22C 3h', cycles=1, lid_temperature_c=45)
eluted_linker_ligated_rna = store_sample(sample=ligated_rna, temperature_c=4, duration_h=1, container='cleanup column eluate in 7 uL RNase-free water')

source_steps.description: Place a 0.22-μm filter (accompanied by Spin-X centrifuge tube) into a 1.5-mL tube and transfer RNA gel extraction buffer and broken gel with wide-bore 200-μL tips. Centrifuge at 10,000 × g at 4°C for 1 min. Ensure that no buffer remains on the filter.
Add 3 μL of GlycoBlue and 500 μL of isopropanol, mix well by vortexing, incubate in a −20°C freezer for 1 h, and then precipitate RNA at 20,000 × g at 4°C for 30 min.
Discard supernatant thoroughly by pipetting. Add 1 mL of 70% ethanol to rinse and centrifuge at 20,000 × g at 4°C for 3 min.
Discard the supernatant thoroughly by pipetting and dry up the pellet by centrifugal evaporation for 2–5 min.
Resuspend the precipitated RNA in 7 μL of 10 mM Tris pH 7.5 (or RNase-free water) and transfer to a 0.2-mL PCR tube.
Denature RNA at 95°C for 2 min in a thermal cycler (with the lid at 105°C) and then place on ice for 3 min.
Prepare the dephosphorylation reaction and incubate at 37°C for 1 h in a thermal cycler (with the lid at 57°C).

Prepare the linker ligation reaction in the same tube by adding components as described below. Incubate at 22°C for 3 h in a thermal cycler (with the lid at 45°C). Use a preadenylated linker (one of #1-#8) for each sample for pooling.

Add 30 μL of RNase-free water to the reaction and purify linker-ligated RNA by the Oligo Clean & Concentrator Kit according to the manufacturer's instructions. Elute the linker into 7 μL of RNase-free water.
Add 7 μL of 2× RNA Loading Buffer (FUJIFILM Wako Pure Chemical).

Gel-purify the linker-ligated RNA as described in steps 42–54. Add the following free linker control in the gel-purification step. See Figure 5 for a representative result. A Representative Gel Image for Linker-Ligated Footprints The areas indicated by dashed white lines are gel-excised and used for downstream processes.
Resuspend the linker-ligated RNA with 10 mM Tris pH 7.5. Pool samples, scaling up to 26 μL by 10 mM Tris pH 7.5.

background: PCR bands from a mini-gene splicing assay have already been excised and broken up in microcentrifuge tubes. The next local stage is to recover DNA from the disrupted gel, carry out blunt-end ligation into the cloning vector, transform competent bacteria, allow recovery, prepare blue-white selection plating, and begin screening candidate colonies.
goal: Advance the gel-derived PCR products through recovery, cloning, transformation, bacterial recovery, plate incubation, and initial colony-screening preparation so that candidate insert-containing clones can be identified for downstream analysis.
constraints: Clarify the crushed gel material by centrifugation at 10000 x g for 10 min under cold conditions normalized to 4°C before using the recovered material for cloning.
The ligation mixture should receive a short room-temperature ligation incubation at 25°C for 5 min and then be chilled on ice before transformation handling.
Competent cells must remain on ice for a period within 20–30 min before heat shock; normalize this hold to 25 min, then apply 42°C heat shock for 45 s followed immediately by 2.5 min on ice.
After transformation, use antibiotic-free recovery medium and incubate at 37°C with shaking for 60 min before plating-related incubation steps.

The ampicillin plate supplemented with IPTG and X-gal needs a 37°C absorption period of 30 min before colony growth incubation, and colony growth should be represented by a 37°C incubation within the 12–16 h window.

A white colony suspension in 10 μL sterile water must be mixed before colony PCR representation, and all operations in the program must use only the provided action-pool functions with later steps consuming earlier outputs.

gold_program:
clarified_gel = centrifuge_sample(sample=crushed_gel_pcr_tube, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
ligated_mix = incubate_sample(sample=ligation_reaction_tube, temperature_c=25, duration_min=5, condition='static')
iced_ligation = cool_sample(sample=ligated_mix, temperature_c=0, duration_min=2, device='ice bath')
pretransform_cells = cool_sample(sample=competent_cells_tube, temperature_c=0, duration_min=25, device='ice bath')
heat_shocked_cells = heat_sample(sample=pretransform_cells, temperature_c=42, duration_min=0.75, device='water bath')
postshock_cells = cool_sample(sample=heat_shocked_cells, temperature_c=0, duration_min=2.5, device='ice bath')
outgrown_cells = incubate_sample(sample=recovered_bacteria_tube, temperature_c=37, duration_min=60, condition='shaking')
prepared_plate = incubate_sample(sample=amp_iptg_xgal_plate, temperature_c=37, duration_min=30, condition='static')
colonies_plate = incubate_sample(sample=prepared_plate, temperature_c=37, duration_min=14, condition='static')
mixed_colony = vortex_mix(sample=white_colony_in_water, duration_s=5, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=25)
colony_pcr_product = run_thermal_cycler(plate=mixed_colony, program_name='colony_pcr_with_M13_primers', cycles=30, lid_temperature_c=105)
gel_ready_pcr = store_sample(sample=colony_pcr_product, temperature_c=25, duration_h=0.5, container='PCR tube')

source_steps.description: Collect all the PCR electrophoresis bands into separate 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes respectively. Use a 1 mL pipette tip to crush the gel, making the pieces as small as possible.
Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 10 min at 2°C–8°C. Transfer the supernatant to a new 1.5 mL centrifuge tube.

Ligate all the PCR products to pEASY -Blunt Cloning Vector respectively, strictly adhering to the manufacturer’s protocol: https://www.transgenbiotech.com/pdf/246 . The steps are summarized below. Prepare the reaction system at 20°C–25°C ( Table 11 ).

Mix gently and incubate at 25°C for 5 min. Place the centrifuge tube on ice afterward. Note: Optimum reaction time varies with fragment length: 0.1–1 kb, 5 min; 1–2 kb, 10 min; 2–3 kb, 15 min; >3 kb, 20 min.
Transformation. Add the ligation product to 50 μL Trans1-T1 competent cells. Flick to mix, then place on ice for 20–30 min.
Heat shock in a 42°C water bath for 45 s, then immediately cool on ice for 2–3 min.
Add 250 μL of SOC or LB medium (without antibiotics) and shake at 37°C for 1 h (220 rpm).
Prepare IPTG and X-gal mix: Take 8 μL of 500 mM IPTG and 40 μL of 20 mg/mL X-gal, mix, and apply evenly to the LB agar plate containing ampicillin. Incubate at 37°C for 30 min.

After absorption, take 200 μL of bacterial solution and evenly apply it on the plate, incubating 12–16 h at 37°C. Note: For more clones, centrifuge at 1500 x g for 1 min, discard some supernatant, retain 100–150 μL, and plate all the suspended bacteria. Note: The Trans1-T1 competent cells are from a commercial kit (see key resources table for catalog information).
Identify positive clones. Select white monoclonal clones and add 10 μL of sterile water. Vortex to mix.

Take 1 μL of the mixture and add it to a 25 μL PCR system, using M13 Forward and Reverse Primers for identification ( Tables 12 and 13 ). Note: The 2× EasyPfu PCR SuperMix is from a commercial kit (see key resources table for catalog information).

Electrophorese the PCR amplification product on a 1.5% agarose gel at 120 V for 25 min. Use a gel imaging system for visualization. Note: If the vector self-ligates, the product length will be 200 bp; if ligation is successful, the product length will be greater than 200 bp.

background: An insulator-seq cloning workflow is at the amplification and cleanup stage. Split PCR reactions for the reporter library are on ice and must be thermocycled, then the pooled amplicon must undergo MinElute-style purification, ethanol removal, elution, and storage.
goal: Process the split PCR library through thermal cycling and downstream column cleanup so that purified amplified DNA is recovered and stored for later use.
constraints: Treat the material as a 20-tube PCR set kept cold before cycling; use a thermal cycler program with a 110�b0C lid and an optimized 9-cycle amplification within the allowed 8-10 cycle range.
Cleanup must include repeated centrifugation at 12000 x g, specifically three 1 min wash-associated spins followed by one 5 min spin to clear residual ethanol.
Before elution, allow the purified column/sample to dry for 10 min at about room temperature (20-25�b0C).
Model the elution as two room-temperature 1 min static incubations associated with the two elution rounds totaling 30 �b5L.
Store the final purified PCR product at -20�b0C after cleanup.

gold_program:
cold_reactions = cool_sample(sample=split_pcr_tubes, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=5.0, device='ice')
sealed_reactions = seal_plate(plate=cold_reactions, seal_type='cap_strip', pressure='firm', duration_s=5.0)
thermocycled_library = run_thermal_cycler(plate=sealed_reactions, program_name='insulator_seq_library_pcr', cycles=9, lid_temperature_c=110.0)
load_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=minelute_column, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
wash_spin_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_column, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
wash_spin_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_spin_1, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
wash_spin_3 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_spin_2, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
dry_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_spin_3, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=25.0)
air_dried_column = dry_sample(sample=dry_spin, method='air_dry', temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=10.0)
elution_wait_1 = incubate_sample(sample=elution_column, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=1.0, condition='static')
elution_wait_2 = incubate_sample(sample=elution_wait_1, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=1.0, condition='static')
stored_purified_library = store_sample(sample=elution_wait_2, temperature_c=-20.0, duration_h=24.0, container='DNA_LoBind_tube')

source_steps.description: Split the master mix into 20 tubes.

Start the PCR program on a thermocycler with a lid heated to 110°C, and transfer tubes from ice into the thermocyler when the block reaches 98°C: Steps Temperature Time Cycles Initial Denaturation 98°C 45 s 1 Denaturation 98°C 15 s 8–10 cycles Annealing 64°C 30 s Extension 72°C 15 s Final extension 72°C 1 min 1 Denature 95°C 15 s 1 Cool down −0.5°C 15 s 140 cycles Hold 10°C Forever Note: We recommend optimizing the number of cycles (8–10 cycles), amount of template and volume of master mix, such that the lowest number of cycles is used to generate a sufficient amount of product while maintaining a 2–5-fold amplification relative to the input. Note: Emulsion PCR may lead to better results, as discussed in the “ limitations ” section.
Purify the PCR product with a MinElute PCR Purification Kit (QIAGEN) or equivalent. Pool the PCR reactions into one tube.
Add 5 volumes of PB buffer without the pH indicator to 1 volume of PCR reactions.
Add 10 μL of 3 M sodium acetate, pH 5.0.
Apply 750 μL sample to the MinElute column. Centrifuge 12000 g for 1 min, discard supernatant and repeat until all the sample has gone through the column.
To wash the column, add 750 μL Buffer PE, centrifuge 12000 g for 1 min and discard supernatant. Wash the column 3 times.
To remove residual ethanol, centrifuge the column 12000 g for 5 min.
Place the column over a clean 1.5 mL DNA LoBind tube and let ethanol evaporate by incubating an open column 10 min at 20°C–25°C.

To elute DNA, add 15 μL of elution buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.5) to the column. Let the column stand for 1 min, then centrifuge 12000 g for 1 min. Repeat the elution twice, so that the total volume of elution buffer added to the column is 30 μL.

Measure the concentration of eluted DNA by using Qubit dsDNA HS assay kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific) or equivalent. Expect 1 μg total DNA yield from 500 μL PCR reaction. Pause point: Purified PCR product can be stored at −20°C.

Inoculate insulator-seq barcode vector-carrying bacteria (Addgene 221458) into 50 mL of LB with selective antibiotic (34 μg/mL chloramphenicol) and incubate at 37°C 16–18 h with vigorous shaking in a bacterial incubator.

background: A purified RhoA preparation has already been obtained in the GDP-bound state and is being converted into a nonhydrolyzable or thiophosphate nucleotide-bound form using CIP-agarose-assisted exchange at cold temperature. After exchange, the beads must be cleared, the released protein recovered with a bead wash, and the resulting sample finished for downstream storage with concentration and documentation steps.
goal: Process the post-exchange RhoA mixture to recover the exchanged protein from CIP-agarose, combine the recovered liquid fractions, reconcentrate the protein, quantify it, and place aliquots into frozen storage as the final GMPPNP- or GTPγS-bound preparation.
constraints: The exchange reaction has already occurred on CIP-agarose beads at 4 degrees Celsius for 12-16 hours and should be treated as the starting material.
Bead clearing must use centrifugation at 4 degrees Celsius for 2 minutes at maximum speed, represented as 6000 x g in the action pool.
Recover the exchanged protein as a supernatant fraction and also recover a separate 0.5 mL wash from the beads using 20 mM Tris pH 7.5 before combining liquid material.
Use cold handling for the recovered exchanged protein and keep downstream concentration at 4 degrees Celsius.
The reconcentration target is about 1 mL using the same centrifugal concentration condition represented by 6000 x g at 4 degrees Celsius.
Include a Bradford-based concentration measurement and a separate recorded metric entry derived from that readout.
The final material must be aliquoted conceptually and stored at -80 degrees Celsius after freezing; storage should reflect the nucleotide-exchanged state rather than the original GDP-bound state.
Use only available action-pool functions; no chromatography operation should be added because that device is not in the pool.

gold_program:
spin1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=rhoA_exchange_mix, speed_xg=6000, duration_min=2, temperature_c=4)
supernatant1 = collect_fraction(sample=spin1, phase='supernatant', destination='new_tube', volume_ul=1500.0)
wash_incubation = incubate_sample(sample=spin1, temperature_c=4, duration_min=1.0, condition='static in 0.5 mL 20 mM Tris pH 7.5 wash')
spin2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_incubation, speed_xg=6000, duration_min=2, temperature_c=4)
wash_fraction = collect_fraction(sample=spin2, phase='supernatant', destination='same_tube_as_supernatant', volume_ul=500.0)
concentrated_rhoA = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_fraction, speed_xg=6000, duration_min=20.0, temperature_c=4)
cooled_rhoA = cool_sample(sample=concentrated_rhoA, temperature_c=4, duration_min=5.0, device='cold block')
bradford_read = measure_signal(sample=bradford_reagent_mix, assay='Bradford', wavelength_nm=595, replicate_n=1)
conc_record = record_measurement(sample=bradford_read, metric='protein_concentration', unit='mg/mL', note='RhoA nucleotide-exchanged sample concentrated to approximately 1 mL')
frozen_aliquots = cool_sample(sample=aliquot_tubes, temperature_c=-80, duration_min=10.0, device='freezer')
stored_final = store_sample(sample=frozen_aliquots, temperature_c=-80, duration_h=24.0, container='aliquot_tubes')

source_steps.description: Wash the CIP-agarose beads with 20 mM Tris pH 7.5 for 3 times, then add RhoA-GDP sample, 10 molar fold GMPPNP or GTPγS, bring the final volume to 1.5 mL using Nucleotide exchange buffer. Rotate at 4°C for 12–16 h.

source_steps.stage: Expression and purification of RhoA FL , RhoA WT , RhoA G14V , RhoA T37A , and RhoA F30L
source_steps.description: Spin down the CIP-agarose beads by desktop centrifuge at 4°C for 2 min at maximum speed, transfer the supernatant to a new tube. Wash the beads with 0.5 mL 20 mM Tris pH 7.5, spin down, combine the wash with the supernatant. Inject the sample onto HiLoad 16/600 Superdex 200 pg column pre-equilibrated with 2 CV Gel filtration buffer and run at 1 mL/min. RhoA elutes at ∼90 mL on this column. The CIP-agarose beads can be reused after being washed with 1 mL 20 mM Tris pH 7.5 for three times, and then stored in 1 mL CIP-agarose storage buffer at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Expression and purification of RhoA FL , RhoA WT , RhoA G14V , RhoA T37A , and RhoA F30L
source_steps.description: Pool protein-containing fractions and concentrate the protein to ∼ 1 mL using Amicon Ultra-15 Centrifugal Filter Unit (10 KDa MWCO) at a speed of 6,000 × g at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Expression and purification of RhoA FL , RhoA WT , RhoA G14V , RhoA T37A , and RhoA F30L
source_steps.description: Measure the concentration using Bradford reagent. Aliquot the sample, freeze the tubes in liquid nitrogen, then store the tubes at −80°C. The resulted RhoA protein is in the GMPPNP- or GTPγS-bound state.
Express GST-Rhotekin-RBD using steps 3–7 as above.
Purify GST-Rhotekin-RBD using steps 8–13 as above.
Elute the protein from the GSTrap column using 5 CV GST elution buffer.
Concentrate the elution to 2 mL final volume using Amicon Ultra-15 Centrifugal Filter Unit (30 KDa MWCO) at a speed of 6,000 × g at 4°C.

background: Purified RhoA in the GDP-loaded form has already been obtained and quantified. The next local stage is the nucleotide-exchange and cleanup segment that uses CIP-agarose, followed by recovery of the exchanged protein and cold storage of the active-state preparation.
goal: Process the GDP-bound RhoA sample through the nucleotide-exchange stage with CIP-agarose under cold conditions, recover the exchanged protein-containing supernatant and wash, and preserve the resulting activated preparation for later use.
constraints: Keep all bead handling and centrifugation steps at 4 degrees Celsius.
The exchange reaction must represent RhoA-GDP combined with CIP-agarose beads plus a 10-fold molar excess of either GMPPNP or GTPγS in nucleotide exchange buffer, with a final volume of 1.5 mL.
The exchange incubation must last within the protocol window of 12 to 16 hours under rotation-equivalent mixing at 4 degrees Celsius.
After exchange, pellet the CIP-agarose beads by desktop centrifugation for 2 minutes at maximum speed; normalize maximum speed to 16000 x g.
Recover the post-spin supernatant into a fresh tube and also recover a bead wash performed with 0.5 mL of 20 mM Tris pH 7.5.
The wash fraction must be combined conceptually with the first recovered supernatant as the exchanged protein output.
The resulting preparation should be documented as nucleotide-bound active-state RhoA rather than GDP-bound RhoA.
Finish by storing the exchanged sample frozen at minus 80 degrees Celsius.

available_inputs.description: Pre-equilibrated CIP-agarose bead slurry for nucleotide exchange.

available_inputs.name: nucleotide_analog
available_inputs.description: Exchange nucleotide reagent, either GMPPNP or GTPγS, prepared at 10-fold molar excess relative to RhoA.

available_inputs.name: nucleotide_exchange_mix
available_inputs.description: Reaction mixture containing RhoA-GDP, CIP-agarose beads, nucleotide analog, and nucleotide exchange buffer adjusted to 1.5 mL final volume.

available_inputs.name: tris_wash_buffer
available_inputs.description: 20 mM Tris pH 7.5 used for bead washing after exchange.

gold_program:
wash1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=cip_agarose_beads, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=2, temperature_c=4)
wash2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash1, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=2, temperature_c=4)
wash3 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash2, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=2, temperature_c=4)
exchange_incubation = set_thermal_mixer(sample=nucleotide_exchange_mix, temperature_c=4, speed_rpm=20, duration_min=720)
post_exchange_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=exchange_incubation, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=2, temperature_c=4)
exchange_supernatant = collect_fraction(sample=post_exchange_spin, phase="supernatant", destination="new_tube", volume_ul=1500)
wash_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=post_exchange_spin, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=2, temperature_c=4)
wash_fraction = collect_fraction(sample=wash_spin, phase="wash_supernatant", destination="same_tube", volume_ul=500)
active_state_record = record_measurement(sample=wash_fraction, metric="state", unit="label", note="RhoA in GMPPNP- or GTPγS-bound state after CIP-agarose exchange and Tris wash recovery")
frozen_active_rhoa = store_sample(sample=active_state_record, temperature_c=-80, duration_h=24, container="aliquot_tubes")

source_steps.stage: Expression and purification of RhoA FL , RhoA WT , RhoA G14V , RhoA T37A , and RhoA F30L
source_steps.description: Pool protein-containing fractions and concentrate the protein to ∼1 mL using Amicon Ultra-15 Centrifugal Filter Unit (10 KDa MWCO) at a speed of 6,000 × g at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Expression and purification of RhoA FL , RhoA WT , RhoA G14V , RhoA T37A , and RhoA F30L
source_steps.description: Measure the concentration using Bradford reagent. Aliquot the sample, freeze the tubes in liquid nitrogen, then store the tubes at −80°C. The RhoA protein purified in this step is in the GDP-bound state.

source_steps.stage: Expression and purification of RhoA FL , RhoA WT , RhoA G14V , RhoA T37A , and RhoA F30L
source_steps.description: Wash the CIP-agarose beads with 20 mM Tris pH 7.5 for 3 times, then add RhoA-GDP sample, 10 molar fold GMPPNP or GTPγS, bring the final volume to 1.5 mL using Nucleotide exchange buffer. Rotate at 4°C for 12–16 h.

source_steps.stage: Expression and purification of RhoA FL , RhoA WT , RhoA G14V , RhoA T37A , and RhoA F30L
source_steps.description: Spin down the CIP-agarose beads by desktop centrifuge at 4°C for 2 min at maximum speed, transfer the supernatant to a new tube. Wash the beads with 0.5 mL 20 mM Tris pH 7.5, spin down, combine the wash with the supernatant. Inject the sample onto HiLoad 16/600 Superdex 200 pg column pre-equilibrated with 2 CV Gel filtration buffer and run at 1 mL/min. RhoA elutes at ∼90 mL on this column. The CIP-agarose beads can be reused after being washed with 1 mL 20 mM Tris pH 7.5 for three times, and then stored in 1 mL CIP-agarose storage buffer at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Expression and purification of RhoA FL , RhoA WT , RhoA G14V , RhoA T37A , and RhoA F30L
source_steps.description: Pool protein-containing fractions and concentrate the protein to ∼ 1 mL using Amicon Ultra-15 Centrifugal Filter Unit (10 KDa MWCO) at a speed of 6,000 × g at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Expression and purification of RhoA FL , RhoA WT , RhoA G14V , RhoA T37A , and RhoA F30L
source_steps.description: Measure the concentration using Bradford reagent. Aliquot the sample, freeze the tubes in liquid nitrogen, then store the tubes at −80°C. The resulted RhoA protein is in the GMPPNP- or GTPγS-bound state.
Express GST-Rhotekin-RBD using steps 3–7 as above.
Purify GST-Rhotekin-RBD using steps 8–13 as above.
Elute the protein from the GSTrap column using 5 CV GST elution buffer.
Concentrate the elution to 2 mL final volume using Amicon Ultra-15 Centrifugal Filter Unit (30 KDa MWCO) at a speed of 6,000 × g at 4°C.
Inject the concentrated sample onto HiLoad 16/600 Superdex 200 pg column pre-equilibrated with 2 CV Gel filtration buffer and run at 1 mL/min. GST-Rhotekin-RBD elutes at ∼60 mL on this column.
Pool protein-containing fractions and concentrate the protein to ∼1 mL using Amicon Ultra-15 Centrifugal Filter Unit (30 KDa MWCO) at a speed of 6,000 × g at 4°C.

background: Human embryonic stem cell samples have already been quenched and extracted for metabolite analysis, and the workflow is now at the stage where clarified extracts or lipid fractions must be processed under cold conditions before LC-MS. This segment emphasizes keeping material chilled, separating phases or debris by high-speed cold centrifugation, and minimizing storage or contamination risks during preparation.
goal: Process the extracted hESC sample through the cold clarification stage and prepare the LC-MS-ready metabolite fraction while preserving a short optional storage branch and documenting the recommended handling condition.
constraints: Keep extraction-derived tubes on ice or otherwise at 4C-equivalent cold conditions during handling after collection.
A vortex mixing step is required before the clarifying spin, and the clarified sample must then be subjected to centrifugation at 18000 x g for 5 min at 4C.
The post-spin supernatant represents the extracted metabolite fraction and should remain cold before aliquoting.
An LC-MS aliquot of 200 �b5L is prepared from the clarified extract; represent this as the downstream sample-ready state rather than inventing transfer mechanics.
Include the optional short-term preservation window at -80C for up to 24 h for the extracted sample branch, while also recording that immediate analysis is preferred to reduce metabolite loss.
Use only action-pool operations; do not add pipetting, scraping, or phase-transfer primitives not present in the pool.
The program should reflect dependency from crude extract to vortexed extract to centrifuged extract to chilled clarified extract and final LC-MS-ready handling.
At least two action families should recur, with one recurrence differing mainly by temperature or duration values.

available_inputs.name: tube1_extract
available_inputs.description: Cold crude intracellular metabolite extract collected from one hESC well in a pre-chilled 1.5 mL tube.

available_inputs.name: tube2_supernatant
available_inputs.description: New labeled 1.5 mL tube intended to hold the clarified metabolite supernatant after centrifugation.

available_inputs.name: sample_vial
available_inputs.description: LC-MS sample vial that will receive the final extract aliquot.

gold_program:
cold_extract = cool_sample(sample=tube1_extract, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=2.0, device="ice")
vortexed_extract = vortex_mix(sample=cold_extract, duration_s=10.0, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=4.0)
clarified_extract = centrifuge_sample(sample=vortexed_extract, speed_xg=18000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
cold_supernatant = cool_sample(sample=clarified_extract, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=2.0, device="ice")
stored_branch = store_sample(sample=cold_supernatant, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_h=24.0, container="1.5 mL tube")
ready_extract = vortex_mix(sample=cold_supernatant, duration_s=5.0, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=4.0)
aliquot_ready = cool_sample(sample=ready_extract, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=1.0, device="ice")
lcms_record = record_measurement(sample=aliquot_ready, metric="LC-MS aliquot volume", unit="uL", note="Prepare 200 uL extract in sample vial for immediate analysis when possible")
storage_record = record_measurement(sample=stored_branch, metric="storage recommendation", unit="h", note="24 h at -80 C is allowable, but immediate LC-MS analysis is preferred to minimize metabolite loss")
protocol_title: Protocol for intracellular and extracellular metabolite detection in human embryonic stem cells

source_steps.description: Aspirate the saline and add 500 μL/well cold cell extraction solution (kept in −80°C). Scrape off cells by cell scraper and transfer the suspension into a labeled, pre-chilled 1.5-mL Eppendorf tube (Tube 1). Put tubes on ice. CRITICAL: To ensure a correct volume, use a new pipette tip to add the cold cell extraction solution into each well.
Wash the well with 100 μL cell extraction solution and combine it into Tube 1. Pause point: Sample in Tube 1 can be stored in −80°C fridge for 24 h (see Note after step 12d).

Vortex each sample. Centrifuge at 18,000 × g for 5 min at 4°C. Transfer the supernatant into a new 1.5-mL tube (Tube 2). Put tubes on ice. Note: The pellet could be preserved to measure protein concentration as described below (step 14). Note: After step 12d, Tube 2 could be preserved at −80°C for 24 h. This 24 h preservation was evaluated in Figure 2 . Peak intensities of most metabolites were comparable with or without 24 h-storage. Because of the risk of losing metabolites caused by storage, we recommend immediate LC-MS analysis after extraction. Long term storage of extracted metabolites, even in −80°C refrigerator, should be avoided.
Vortex Tube 2, put tube on ice, and then transfer 200 μL extract into a sample vial for LC-MS analysis.

Method 2 (for lipid determination): CRITICAL: To avoid plastic contamination, glass vials are recommended. Polypropylene pipette tip is conditionally resistant to chloroform for a minimized contact time. A fresh tip must be used each time chloroform is dispensed. Put Milli-Q water and labeled glass vials on ice. Make sure methanol and chloroform containing 1 μg/mL heptadecanoate and 1 μg/mL coprostan-3-ol (see Materials and equipment) are pre-chilled at −80°C. Note: Heptadecanoate is used as internal control for fatty acids. coprostan-3-ol is used as internal control for cholesterol.
Rinse cells with 1 mL/well ice-cold 0.9% saline twice.
Aspirate the saline. Add 250 μL/well −80°C methanol and then add 100 μL/well ice-cold Milli-Q water.
Scrape off cells and transfer the suspension into glass vials. Put vials on ice.
Add 250 μL/vial −80°C chloroform containing 1 μg/mL heptadecanoate and 1 μg/mL coprostan-3-ol. Vortex and then centrifuge at 18,000 × g for 5 min at 4°C.

Remove the upper hydrophilic phase. Protein pellet at the interface is carefully pushed aside on the wall of the vial. A fresh tip is used to reach the bottom of the vial to collect the hydrophobic phase. Stop collecting before reaching the interface.
Transfer the hydrophobic phase into a new glass vial. Evaporate under nitrogen gas till dry. Re-dissolve the residue in 100 μL 2:1 chloroform/methanol (v/v) for LC-MS analysis.
Determine protein mass (optional) Decant supernatant and add 200 μL methanol to the protein pellet. Vigorously vortex and then centrifuge at 18,000 × g for 5 min at 4°C.

background: Active control protein has already been aliquoted and frozen, and the workflow has moved to preparing rhodamine-labeled GMPCPP microtubules for downstream activity assays. At this stage, a chilled tubulin mixture must be clarified, polymerized once, recovered and washed as a pellet, then depolymerized in cold buffer and prepared for a second polymerization setup.
goal: Carry the tubulin preparation from the initial cold mixture through aggregate removal, first-cycle polymerization, pellet recovery and washing, cold depolymerization with intermittent mixing, and final cold nucleotide loading for the second cycle.
constraints: Start from the provided tubulin mixture on ice and include an initial 5 min cold hold followed by clarification at 279000 x g for 10 min at 4 C.
Use GMPCPP-loading cold holds of 5 min before each polymerization stage, and include a 37 C incubation for 60 min for the first polymerization.
Recover polymerized microtubules by centrifugation at 20000 x g for 10 min at 25 C, then represent two warm BRB80 washes before moving on.
Model depolymerization as resuspension in cold BRB80 to about 2 mg/mL, followed by a 30 min ice incubation with repeated pipette mixing reflected in the action sequence.
The program must be a single dependency chain of 6-12 top-level calls using only provided action functions, with at least two action names reused and all calls written with keyword arguments.

available_inputs.name: tubulin_mix
available_inputs.description: Preassembled cold tubulin mixture in 1x BRB80 containing rhodamine-labeled and unlabeled tubulin.

available_inputs.name: gmpcpp_stock
available_inputs.description: GMPCPP stock solution used to charge the tubulin mixture before each polymerization cycle.

available_inputs.name: warm_brb80
available_inputs.description: Warm 1x BRB80 buffer used to wash the microtubule pellet after the first polymerization.

available_inputs.name: cold_brb80
available_inputs.description: Cold 1x BRB80 buffer used to resuspend the pellet for depolymerization.

gold_program:
chilled_mix = cool_sample(sample=tubulin_mix, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5, device="ice bath")
clarified_mix = centrifuge_sample(sample=chilled_mix, speed_xg=279000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
first_gmpcpp_load = cool_sample(sample=clarified_mix, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5, device="ice bath")
first_polymerized_mts = incubate_sample(sample=first_gmpcpp_load, temperature_c=37, duration_min=60, condition="static")
mt_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=first_polymerized_mts, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=25)
first_wash = mix_sample(sample=mt_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
second_wash = mix_sample(sample=first_wash, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
resuspended_pellet = mix_sample(sample=second_wash, method="pipetting", duration_s=20, speed="gentle")
depolymerized_mix = cool_sample(sample=resuspended_pellet, temperature_c=0, duration_min=30, device="ice bath")
remixed_depolymerized = mix_sample(sample=depolymerized_mix, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
second_gmpcpp_load = cool_sample(sample=remixed_depolymerized, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5, device="ice bath")
protocol_title: Protocol for purifying biologically active microtubule-severing protein UNC-45A from E.coli using GST-affinity and spin columns

source_steps.description: Make appropriately sized, single use aliquots.

Snap-freeze and store in −80 ° C . Note: Protein is active for about 1 week after the purification when stored in −80 ° C . Note: Ideal product is about 900 μL of 4 μM protein without concentrating the protein, while about 300 μL of 7 μM with concentrating the protein in the best elution fractions using this protocol is achieved.

Rhodamine-labeled double-cycle GMPCPP MTs. Perform the first cycle polymerization. On ice, mix 2 μL of 10 mg/mL rhodamine-labeled tubulin, 18 μL of 10 mg/mL unlabeled tubulin, and 70.5 μL of 1× BRB80 buffer (20 μM final concentration of total tubulin with 11% labeling).
Incubate the tubulin mix on ice for 5 min.
Ultracentrifuge the tubulin mix at 279,000 × g for 10 min at 4 ° C to remove tubulin aggregates.
Add 5 μL of 10 mM GMPCPP (0.5 mM final concentration) for 5 min on ice.
Polymerize MTs by incubating at 37 ° C for 1 h.
Pellet the MTs by centrifuging at 20,000 × g for 10 min at 25 ° C .
Discard the supernatant and gently wash the pellet with warm 1× BRB80 twice.

Perform MT depolymerization. Resuspend the MT pellet in cold 1× BRB80 such that the final tubulin concentration is ∼2 mg/mL. Note: The usual recovery is ∼50% of tubulins from the first cycle. Therefore, resuspend the pellet in a half of the original volume of the tubulin mix.
Incubate the tubulin mix on ice for 30 min with periodically mixing every 10 min by pipette.
Perform the second cycle polymerization. Add 2.5 μL of 10 mM GMPCPP (0.5 mM final concentration) to the tubulin mix for 5 min on ice.

background: Nascent proteins in Xenopus tadpole brain lysates have already been captured on NeutrAvidin beads after BONCAT tagging. The current stage is the bead-based enrichment cleanup and elution preparation before downstream western blot analysis, including overnight cold binding, sequential wash steps, an SDS-containing stringency wash, and final resuspension in Laemmli buffer.
goal: Process the NeutrAvidin bead-bound protein sample through the required incubation and wash sequence, ending with the beads fully mixed in sample buffer and ready for later elution analysis.
constraints: The bead-bound sample must undergo a cold incubation at 4 degrees Celsius for 14-16 hours under a rotating or equivalent nonstatic mixing condition before the day-3 wash sequence.
Use centrifugation-based bead pelleting rather than magnetic separation.
Initial pelleting and the early PBS wash steps use 1700 x g for 2 minutes, while the last PBS wash uses 4000 x g for 2 minutes.
Include one wash corresponding to 1× PBS and one higher-stringency wash corresponding to 1× PBS with 1% SDS.
The SDS-containing wash must include a 10 minute room-temperature exposure at 25 degrees Celsius with rotational or equivalent mixing before the following spin.
After the stringency wash, perform additional PBS washes so that the beads are cleaned before buffer addition.
The final state must represent beads with supernatant removed and resuspended in 30 microliters of 2× Laemmli sample buffer.
End with a gentle manual-equivalent mixing action to reflect tapping the tube repeatedly rather than vigorous vortexing.

available_inputs.description: Protein sample combined with NeutrAvidin beads after distribution and volume adjustment to 1 mL in PBS.

available_inputs.name: pbs_wash
available_inputs.description: Wash solution corresponding to 1× PBS.

available_inputs.name: pbs_sds_wash
available_inputs.description: Stringency wash corresponding to 1× PBS containing 1% SDS.

available_inputs.name: laemmli_buffer_2x
available_inputs.description: Final resuspension buffer, 2× Laemmli sample buffer, 30 μL intended per tube.

gold_program:
overnight_bound = incubate_sample(sample=bead_bound_sample, temperature_c=4, duration_min=900, condition='rotating')
pellet_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=overnight_bound, speed_xg=1700, duration_min=2, temperature_c=4)
pbs_wash_1 = mix_sample(sample=pellet_1, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
pellet_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=pbs_wash_1, speed_xg=1700, duration_min=2, temperature_c=4)
sds_wash_mix = incubate_sample(sample=pellet_2, temperature_c=25, duration_min=10, condition='rotating')
pellet_3 = centrifuge_sample(sample=sds_wash_mix, speed_xg=1700, duration_min=2, temperature_c=25)
pbs_wash_2 = mix_sample(sample=pellet_3, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
pellet_4 = centrifuge_sample(sample=pbs_wash_2, speed_xg=1700, duration_min=2, temperature_c=25)
pbs_wash_3 = mix_sample(sample=pellet_4, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
pellet_5 = centrifuge_sample(sample=pbs_wash_3, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=2, temperature_c=25)
laemmli_resuspended = mix_sample(sample=pellet_5, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
final_ready_sample = shake_container(sample=laemmli_resuspended, amplitude_mm=5.0, repeat_n=50, duration_s=25)

source_steps.description: Add NeutrAvidin beads to samples: Distribute resuspended beads into the tubes of the protein samples (54) as evenly as possible. Use a cut P200 tip to transfer the beads.
Add the bead suspension to all tubes in multiple rounds, adding 50–100 μL each time.
Lightly vortex the bead suspension in between each round.
Bring the total volume of the solution in each tube to 1 mL with 1× PBS.
Seal the tube with parafilm. Set the tubes up for head-over-head rotation in the cold room (4°C) for 14–16 h incubation.

source_steps.stage: Elution of proteins from NeutrAvidin beads and western blotting—Day 3
source_steps.description: Lightly vortex and spin down the samples at 1700 × g for 2 min and remove the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Elution of proteins from NeutrAvidin beads and western blotting—Day 3
source_steps.description: Wash the pellet with 1 mL of 1× PBS, lightly vortex and spin down at 1700 × g for 2 min to remove the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Elution of proteins from NeutrAvidin beads and western blotting—Day 3
source_steps.description: Wash pellet with 1 mL of 1× PBS containing 1% SDS to remove any weakly bound proteins; seal the tube with parafilm and set on head-to-toe rotation at 25°C for 10 min, then spin down at 1700 × g for 2 min to remove the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Elution of proteins from NeutrAvidin beads and western blotting—Day 3
source_steps.description: Wash the pellet with 1 mL of 1× PBS; lightly vortex and spin down at 1700 × g for 2 min to remove the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Elution of proteins from NeutrAvidin beads and western blotting—Day 3
source_steps.description: Do one more wash with 1 mL of 1× PBS – lightly vortex and spin down at 4000 × g for 2 min.

source_steps.stage: Elution of proteins from NeutrAvidin beads and western blotting—Day 3
source_steps.description: Carefully pipette out all the supernatant – when the beads appear as dry add 30 μL of 2× Laemmli sample buffer.

source_steps.stage: Elution of proteins from NeutrAvidin beads and western blotting—Day 3
source_steps.description: Gently tap each tube about 50 times to mix the beads with the sample buffer.

background: KG-1a leukemia cells have already been placed into IMDM in a 15 mL tube and need to be expanded before later avidity analysis. At this stage, the cells are recovered by low-speed centrifugation, resuspended into fresh complete medium for a first expansion period, then harvested again after growth for further expansion. In parallel, a z-Movi chip is prepared through rinse and alkaline cleaning, drying, Concanavalin A coating, and final hydration in serum-free medium.
goal: Advance the KG-1a culture through the required expansion sequence and prepare a z-Movi chip that is cleaned, coated, dried, and hydrated for subsequent seeding.
constraints: Start from the KG-1a suspension already in a 15 mL centrifuge tube and the unprepared z-Movi chip.
Both KG-1a harvest spins must use 126 x g for 5 min at 25 C, with fresh complete-medium resuspension after each pellet is formed.
Include a 48 h cultivation interval between the first resuspension and the later high-density cell harvest.
Chip cleaning must include a short water treatment, a 1 h exposure to 1 M NaOH, and then two separate short ddH2O rinses.
The chip must be dried at 37 C after cleaning and again after a 1 h Concanavalin A coating step.
Before preparing the Concanavalin A stock, briefly centrifuge the reagent tube; then represent dissolution in 1 mL ddH2O to a final 5 mg/mL stock using a supported mixing action.
Finish with hydration of the chip using 100 uL serum-free IMDM at 37 C for 1 h before later seeding.

available_inputs.description: 15 mL centrifuge tube containing KG-1a cell suspension in IMDM

available_inputs.name: chip
available_inputs.description: z-Movi chip requiring cleaning and coating

available_inputs.name: conA_tube
available_inputs.description: Tube of Concanavalin A reagent to be spun down and dissolved

available_inputs.name: serum_free_imdm_chip
available_inputs.description: Chip loaded with 100 uL serum-free IMDM for hydration

kg1a_t75_growth = incubate_sample(sample=kg1a_resuspended_1, temperature_c=25, duration_min=2880, condition='static')
kg1a_pellet_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=kg1a_t75_growth, speed_xg=126, duration_min=5, temperature_c=25)
kg1a_resuspended_2 = mix_sample(sample=kg1a_pellet_2, method='pipetting', duration_s=30, speed='gentle')
chip_cleaned = incubate_sample(sample=chip, temperature_c=25, duration_min=69, condition='static')
chip_dried_1 = dry_sample(sample=chip_cleaned, method='oven dry', temperature_c=37, duration_min=30)
conA_stock = mix_sample(sample=conA_tube, method='pipetting', duration_s=30, speed='thorough')
chip_coated = incubate_sample(sample=chip_dried_1, temperature_c=25, duration_min=60, condition='static')

chip_hydrated = incubate_sample(sample=serum_free_imdm_chip, temperature_c=37, duration_min=60, condition='static')

source_steps.description: Add 5 mL of IMDM medium to a 15 mL centrifuge tube. Using a pipette, transfer the cell suspension into the centrifuge tube and gently mix the cells.
Centrifuge at 126 × g for 5 min at 25°C, discard the supernatant.
Add fresh 20 mL complete medium (IMDM + 10% FBS + 1% Penicillin Streptomycin Amphotericin) to resuspend the cell pellet and transfer the cell suspension to a T75 culture flask.
After 48 h of cultivation, when cell density is high, transfer the cell suspension to a 50 mL centrifuge tube, centrifuge at 126 × g for 5 min at 25°C, and discard the supernatant.
Add fresh 50 mL complete medium to resuspend the cell pellet and transfer the cell suspension to a T300 culture flask.
Clean the chip according to the following steps. Add 100 μL ddH 2 O to the chip, rinse off after 3 min.
Add 100 μL 1 M NaOH to the chip, rinse off after 1 h.
Add 100 μL ddH 2 O to the chip, rinse off after 3 min, repeat once.
After cleaning, place the chips in a 37°C dry incubator until dry.
The chip must be pre-coated with Concanavalin A (5 mg/mL) for 1 h and dehydrated in a 37°C dry incubator. Before opening the tube of Concanavalin A, centrifuge briefly to pellet the dye.

Prepare work stock solution Concanavalin A. Add 1 mL of ddH 2 O to the tube of Concanavalin A and mix thoroughly with a pipette until the dye is completely dissolved to a final concentration of 5 mg/mL.
Hydrate the chips with 100 μL serum-free IMDM culture medium at 37°C for 1 h before in-chip seeding.

background: This item covers the local post-PCR cleanup segment of SARS-CoV-2 tiled amplicon sequencing. After amplification is complete, the reactions are briefly spun, the two primer-pool products are treated as a combined bead-cleanup sample, incubated at room temperature, held for magnetic bead capture, washed twice with 80% ethanol, cleared of residual ethanol, eluted in water, and kept ready for the next end-prep stage.
goal: Carry out the post-amplification purification workflow on the tiled SARS-CoV-2 PCR products so the cleaned amplicon output is obtained and preserved for downstream end-prep setup.
constraints: Start from a completed PCR plate and include an initial brief centrifugation before purification handling.
Use the cleaned output of each step as the input to the next step to maintain one continuous dependency chain through cleanup, drying, elution, and holding.
Capture the bead-binding incubation as 10 minutes at room temperature and the magnetic separation wait as a distinct 5 minute static hold.
Perform two repeated ethanol-wash actions for the same sample context before a second centrifugation that represents clearing residual ethanol.
Include an air-dry step before elution, and model the elution as recovery in 15 uL-equivalent nuclease-free water.
Stay within the provided action pool and use at least two action families more than once.

available_inputs.description: Plate containing completed SARS-CoV-2 tiled PCR reactions before post-PCR cleanup.

available_inputs.name: combined_pool_plate
available_inputs.description: Combined Pool A and Pool B amplicon plate with AMPure XP beads added.

available_inputs.name: cleanup_plate
available_inputs.description: Working plate used during magnetic cleanup and ethanol wash processing.

available_inputs.name: elution_tube
