gold_program:
step1_spun_pcr = centrifuge_sample(sample=pcr_plate, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=25)
step2_bound_beads = incubate_sample(sample=combined_pool_plate, temperature_c=25, duration_min=10, condition='static bead binding after pool combination')
step3_magnet_wait = incubate_sample(sample=step2_bound_beads, temperature_c=25, duration_min=5, condition='static on magnetic stand')
step4_ethanol_wash1 = incubate_sample(sample=step3_magnet_wait, temperature_c=25, duration_min=0.5, condition='80 percent ethanol wash 200 uL')
step5_ethanol_wash2 = incubate_sample(sample=step4_ethanol_wash1, temperature_c=25, duration_min=0.5, condition='80 percent ethanol wash 200 uL repeat')
step6_postwash_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=step5_ethanol_wash2, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=25)
step7_air_dry = dry_sample(sample=step6_postwash_spin, method='air dry', temperature_c=25, duration_min=5)
step8_elute = incubate_sample(sample=step7_air_dry, temperature_c=25, duration_min=1, condition='elute in 15 uL nuclease-free water')
step9_hold_cool = cool_sample(sample=step8_elute, temperature_c=4, duration_min=1, device='bench cooler')
step10_store_ready = store_sample(sample=step9_hold_cool, temperature_c=4, duration_h=1, container=elution_tube)

source_steps.description: Concentration Measurement: Measure the concentration of the purified cDNA product using a NanoDrop or similar instrument.

PCR tiling of SARS-CoV-2 virus. We amplify the SARS-CoV-2 genome from the double-stranded cDNA using ARTIC V3 primers specific for SARS-CoV-2. Note: Divide these primers into two pools: Pool A and Pool B, and dilute to a concentration of 10 nM before being utilized in the subsequent step. Prepare the reaction mixture according to the following instructions. PCR reaction master mix Reagent Volume (pool A) Volume (pool B) Storage Purified double stranded cDNA (∼100 ng) x μl x μl −20°C Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix 12.5 μL 12.5 μL −20°C V2 Primer pool at 10 μM (A or B) 3.7 μL 3.7 μL −20°C V3 Primer pool at 10 μM (A or B) 0.45 μL 0.38 μL −20°C Nuclease-free water 8.35-x μl 8.42-x μl RT Total 25 μL 25 μL -

Gently mix the reaction plate by pipetting and incubate in a thermal cycler as per the program below. PCR cycling conditions Steps Temperature Time Cycles Initial Denaturation 98°C 30 s 1 Denaturation 98°C 15 s 35 cycles Annealing and Extension 65°C 5 min Hold 4°C forever
Briefly spin the plate after PCR reaction and perform the following steps: Centrifugation: Centrifuge the plate once before proceeding to the purification step.
Combining Pools: Combine the reactions from Pool A and Pool B into a new deep 96-well plate, with one well designated for each sample. Then, add 50 μL of resuspended AMPure XP beads to each well.
Incubation: Incubate the plate at room temperature for 10 min.
Magnetic Separation: Subsequently, transfer the plate to a magnetic stand and wait for 5 min. Once the beads have completely moved to the side of the magnet, carefully discard the supernatant.
Ethanol Wash: Wash the pellet with 200 μL of 80% ethanol twice to remove any impurities. Afterwards, remove the residual ethanol by spinning the plate, and allow it to air dry.
Elution: Elute the amplified product using 15 μL of nuclease-free water.
Dilution and Quantification: After diluting the eluted product in 1:10 ratio, quantify using the Qubit High Sensitivity dsDNA assay kit.

End-prep. In order to add the barcodes to the amplicons, short PolyA should be added to the end of the amplicons. Prepare the following reaction mix: Reagent Volume Storage cDNA (∼200 ng) x μL −20°C Nuclease-free water (12.5 - x) μL −20°C Ultra II End-prep reaction buffer 1.75 μL −20°C Ultra II End-prep enzyme mix 0.75 μL −20°C Total 15 μL -
Gently mix the reaction by pipetting up and down 8–10 times and spin down the plate.

background: This protocol segment handles two linked affinity-purification branches during in vitro analysis of OXCT1-mediated LACTB succinylation. One branch starts from a clarified sample that is captured on GST magnetic beads, then washed, eluted with glutathione, and concentrated or desalted. The other branch starts from bacterial cells expressing His-tagged LACTB, which are pelleted, resuspended in cold imidazole buffer with DTT, clarified, captured on His magnetic beads, and washed before later elution.
goal: Complete the local purification stage so that GST-associated material is recovered into an ultrafiltration-compatible eluate and His-tagged LACTB is prepared on washed magnetic beads ready for subsequent elution.
constraints: Use only cold centrifugation and cold bead incubation conditions at 4 degrees Celsius for this stage.
Apply a 16000 x g spin for 10 minutes to clarify lysates and a 3000 x g spin for 10 minutes for bacterial harvest and the concentration or desalting step.
Use 100 uL magnetic beads for each affinity capture and incubate for 20 minutes with rolling.
The GST branch must include three cooled binding-buffer washes before glutathione elution with 200 uL at pH 8.0.
The His branch must begin from 50 mL bacterial cells resuspended in 20 mL of 50 mM imidazole buffer containing 1 mM DTT.
The His branch must include exactly two washes with 50 mM imidazole buffer before stopping.
Outputs from each branch must feed the next operation in sequence without introducing unrelated restart material.

available_inputs.description: Sample entering the GST affinity purification segment.

available_inputs.name: binding_buffer
available_inputs.description: Cooled binding buffer used for GST wash steps.

available_inputs.name: glutathione_elution_buffer
available_inputs.description: Glutathione elution buffer for GST-tagged protein recovery.

available_inputs.name: his_bacterial_cells
available_inputs.description: Fifty milliliters of bacterial cells containing His-tagged LACTB.

available_inputs.name: imidazole_buffer_dtt
available_inputs.description: Cold 50 mM imidazole buffer containing 1 mM DTT for resuspension.

available_inputs.name: gst_beads
available_inputs.description: GST fusion protein purified magnetic beads.

available_inputs.name: his_beads
available_inputs.description: His fusion protein purified magnetic beads.

gold_program:
gst_clarified = centrifuge_sample(sample=gst_lysate, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
gst_supernatant = collect_fraction(sample=gst_clarified, phase='supernatant', destination='pre_cooled_15ml_tube', volume_ul=1000)
gst_bead_mix = add_reagent(sample=gst_supernatant, reagent='GST fusion protein purified magnetic beads', volume_ul=100, mixing='mix gently')
gst_bound = incubate_sample(sample=gst_bead_mix, temperature_c=4, duration_min=20, condition='rolling')
gst_beads_retained = magnetic_separate(sample=gst_bound, rack='Magnetic Frame', duration_min=2, collect_phase='beads')
gst_desalted = centrifuge_sample(sample=gst_beads_retained, speed_xg=3000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
his_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=his_bacterial_cells, speed_xg=3000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
his_resuspended = add_reagent(sample=his_pellet, reagent='50 mM Imidazole buffer containing 1 mM DTT', volume_ul=20000, mixing='resuspend')
his_clarified = centrifuge_sample(sample=his_resuspended, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
his_supernatant = collect_fraction(sample=his_clarified, phase='supernatant', destination='pre_cooled_15ml_tube', volume_ul=1000)
his_bead_mix = add_reagent(sample=his_supernatant, reagent='His fusion protein purified magnetic beads', volume_ul=100, mixing='mix gently')
his_bound_washed = magnetic_separate(sample=his_bead_mix, rack='Magnetic Frame', duration_min=2, collect_phase='beads')

source_steps.description: Centrifuge for 10 min at 4°C, 16,000 × g , and transfer the supernatant to a new pre-cooled 15 mL reaction tube.
Add 100 μL GST fusion protein purified magnetic beads (BeaverBio, China) and incubate with the Rolling incubator for 20 min at 4°C.
Discard the supernatant and wash magnetic beads three times with the cooled Binding buffer with the help of a Magnetic Frame.
Elute protein with 200 μL Glutathione elution buffer (20 mM Glutathione, pH 8.0) and collect in an ultrafiltration tube (Amicon Ultra-15, Millipore).
Centrifuge for concentration and desalination at 4°C, 3,000 × g. Note: Protein recovery can be improved by prolonging elution time or increasing elution number.

Purify His-tagged LACTB. Representative data from three independent experiments with similar results can be found in Figure 2 B. Collect 50 mL bacterial cells containing His-tagged LACTB and centrifuge for 10 min at 4°C, 3,000 × g.
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the bacterial pellet with the cooled 20 mL 50 mM Imidazole buffer containing 1 mM DTT.
Break cells in an ice bath with an ultrasonic homogenizer with a 6 mm probe (SCIENTZ, China) at 20% power for 20 min, work for 1 s, and stop for 2 s.
Centrifuge for 10 min at 4°C, 16,000 × g , and transfer the supernatant to a new pre-cooled 15 mL reaction tube.
Add 100 μL His fusion protein purified magnetic beads (BeaverBio, China) and incubate with the Rolling incubator for 20 min at 4°C.
Discard the supernatant and wash magnetic beads twice with 50 mM Imidazole buffer.
Elute protein with a gradient concentration of Imidazole buffer and collect eluted buffer to an ultrafiltration tube (Amicon Ultra-15, Millipore).

background: Cord blood mononuclear cells have already been labeled with NK-cell magnetic beads and are at the MACS enrichment stage. The task is to keep the sample cold, recover the unlabeled NK-cell fraction through repeated column wash handling into a 15 mL tube, then pellet the enriched cells and place them into cytokine-supplemented NK culture medium for short pre-activation.
goal: Carry out the NK-cell enrichment and immediate post-isolation preparation so that unlabeled NK cells are recovered, pelleted, resuspended in fresh cytokine-containing medium, and incubated for pre-activation.
constraints: Keep the bead-labeled sample at 4 degrees Celsius for 20 minutes before the magnetic enrichment stage.
Include MACS buffer handling equivalent to 150 microliters per 1e7 cells and repeated 1 mL wash-style additions under cold conditions.
Recover the unlabeled fraction into a 15 mL centrifuge tube, then centrifuge the isolated NK cells at 450 x g for 8 minutes at 4 degrees Celsius before resuspension.
Use fresh NK culture medium containing 10 ng/mL rhIL-12 and 50 ng/mL rhIL-15, and incubate the resuspended cells for 18 hours.
Use only the provided action-pool functions, assign every call to a variable, and maintain a dependency chain across 6-12 top-level steps.

available_inputs.name: bead_labeled_cbmcs
available_inputs.description: Cord blood mononuclear cell suspension already mixed with NK Cell MicroBead Cocktail.

available_inputs.name: macs_buffer
available_inputs.description: Cold MACS buffer for dilution, prewash, and repeated column washes.

available_inputs.name: macs_separator
available_inputs.description: Suitable MACS magnetic separator used for enrichment.

available_inputs.name: nk_collection_tube_15ml
available_inputs.description: 15 mL centrifuge tube for collecting the unlabeled NK-cell fraction.

available_inputs.name: nk_culture_medium_cytokines
available_inputs.description: Fresh NK-cell culture medium supplemented with 10 ng/mL rhIL-12 and 50 ng/mL rhIL-15.

gold_program:
cooled_bead_labeled_cbmcs = cool_sample(sample=bead_labeled_cbmcs, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=20.0, device='refrigerator')
diluted_cbmcs = mix_sample(sample=cooled_bead_labeled_cbmcs, method='pipetting', duration_s=15.0, speed='gentle with 150 uL MACS buffer per 1e7 cells')
prewashed_column_state = mix_sample(sample=macs_buffer, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='cold prewash equivalent to 1 mL into 15 mL collection tube')
initial_unlabeled_fraction = magnetic_separate(sample=diluted_cbmcs, rack=macs_separator, duration_min=1.0, collect_phase='unlabeled cells into 15 mL centrifuge tube')
wash_state_1 = mix_sample(sample=initial_unlabeled_fraction, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='cold wash equivalent to 1 mL MACS buffer')
wash_state_2 = mix_sample(sample=wash_state_1, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='cold wash equivalent to 1 mL MACS buffer')
wash_state_3 = mix_sample(sample=wash_state_2, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='cold wash equivalent to 1 mL MACS buffer')
enriched_nk_fraction = magnetic_separate(sample=wash_state_3, rack=macs_separator, duration_min=1.0, collect_phase='combined unlabeled effluent in 15 mL centrifuge tube')
pelleted_nk_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=enriched_nk_fraction, speed_xg=450, duration_min=8.0, temperature_c=4.0)
resuspended_nk_cells = mix_sample(sample=pelleted_nk_cells, method='pipetting', duration_s=20.0, speed='gentle resuspension in fresh NK culture medium with 10 ng/mL rhIL-12 and 50 ng/mL rhIL-15')
preactivated_nk_cells = incubate_sample(sample=resuspended_nk_cells, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=1080.0, condition='static')

source_steps.description: Add 20 μL of NK Cell MicroBead Cocktail per 1 × 10 7 cells, mix thoroughly, and incubate at 4°C for 20 min.
Add 150 μL MACS buffer per 1 × 10 7 cells, followed by subsequent magnetic cell separation.
Choose separation columns of the appropriate size based on the cell counts. Place the column in the magnetic field of a suitable MACS Separator.
Position 15 mL centrifuge tube beneath the column to collect unlabeled NK cells.
Prewash the column with MACS buffer to remove the bubbles (MS column: 1 mL MACS buffer; LS column: 3 mL MACS buffer).
Add the cell suspension to the column, 500 μL at a time. Allow the liquid in the column to completely drain after each addition.
Wash the column with the 1 mL MACS buffer, repeat 3 times. Collect the unlabeled cells that pass through and combine them with the effluent from step 20.
Obtain and culture NK cells. Troubleshooting . Centrifuge the isolated NK cells and discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the pellet in fresh NK cell culture medium. Add 10 ng/mL recombinant human (rh) interleukin (IL)-12 (rhIL-12) and 50 ng/mL rhIL-15 to pre-activate NK cells for 18 h.

Assess the purity of isolated NK cells by staining CD3 and CD56 with fluorescently labeled antibodies for flow cytometry analysis. Separately add preisolated 2 × 10 5 CBMCs and 1 × 10 5 isolated NK cells into two different 1.5 mL micro-centrifuge tubes. Note: Please keep the sample on ice during the following steps. Note: The isolated NK cells used in this step do not require in vitro culture with cytokines activation, but using cytokines-activated isolated NK cells is also allowed.
Add 1 mL of 1 × PBS to wash the cells. Centrifuge at 4°C and 450 × g for 8 min, then discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the cell pellet in 100 μL 1 × PBS. Add 1 μL anti-CD45 FITC, anti-CD3 PerCP/Cy5.5 and 1 μL anti-CD56 PE antibodies to stain the cells and vortex to mix.

background: A mini-gene splicing assay has produced distinct PCR bands corresponding to different splice products. At this stage, the bands have been excised and crushed in microcentrifuge tubes, and the workflow now focuses on recovering DNA from the gel debris, carrying out blunt-end ligation, transforming competent cells, and handling the post-transformation recovery and plating-related incubations before clone screening.
goal: Process the crushed gel band sample through recovery, ligation setup handling, bacterial transformation, recovery, and plate-preparation-associated incubation/cooling steps so the transformed cells are ready for downstream clone identification.
constraints: Begin from a crushed gel band sample in a 1.5 mL centrifuge tube and include a refrigerated centrifugation at 10000 x g for 10 min with the temperature kept within the 2-8 C range.
After ligation components are prepared, perform gentle mixing at room temperature-like conditions before incubation.
The ligation reaction requires a short incubation at 25 C for 5 min, followed by immediate chilling on ice.
Competent cells must receive the ligation product, be mixed without harsh treatment, and remain on ice for 20-30 min before heat shock.
Heat shock must be performed at 42 C for 45 s in a water bath-equivalent device, then followed by rapid cooling on ice for 2-3 min.
After heat shock, cells must recover in antibiotic-free SOC or LB at 37 C for 1 h with shaking-like conditions.
The IPTG/X-gal-treated ampicillin plate requires a separate 37 C incubation for 30 min before transformed bacteria are applied.
Maintain a dependency chain in which each later transformed-cell handling step uses the output from the preceding transformation-related step rather than restarting from raw cells.

gel_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=crushed_gel_band, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
ligation_mixed = mix_sample(sample=ligation_reaction, method='gentle pipetting', duration_s=5, speed='gentle')
ligation_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=ligation_mixed, temperature_c=25, duration_min=5, condition='static')
ligation_chilled = cool_sample(sample=ligation_incubated, temperature_c=0, duration_min=2, device='ice bath')
cells_mixed = mix_sample(sample=competent_cells_mix, method='flicking', duration_s=5, speed='gentle')
cells_on_ice = cool_sample(sample=cells_mixed, temperature_c=0, duration_min=25, device='ice bath')
heat_shocked_cells = heat_sample(sample=cells_on_ice, temperature_c=42, duration_min=0.75, device='water bath')
post_shock_cooled = cool_sample(sample=heat_shocked_cells, temperature_c=0, duration_min=2.5, device='ice bath')
recovered_cells = incubate_sample(sample=recovery_culture, temperature_c=37, duration_min=60, condition='shaking')
prepared_plate = incubate_sample(sample=iptg_xgal_amp_plate, temperature_c=37, duration_min=30, condition='static')

source_steps.description: Use a gel imaging system for visualization ( Figure 3 ).
Collect all the PCR electrophoresis bands into separate 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes respectively. Use a 1 mL pipette tip to crush the gel, making the pieces as small as possible.
Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 10 min at 2°C–8°C. Transfer the supernatant to a new 1.5 mL centrifuge tube.

Ligate all the PCR products to pEASY -Blunt Cloning Vector respectively, strictly adhering to the manufacturer’s protocol: https://www.transgenbiotech.com/pdf/246 . The steps are summarized below. Prepare the reaction system at 20°C–25°C ( Table 11 ).

Mix gently and incubate at 25°C for 5 min. Place the centrifuge tube on ice afterward. Note: Optimum reaction time varies with fragment length: 0.1–1 kb, 5 min; 1–2 kb, 10 min; 2–3 kb, 15 min; >3 kb, 20 min.
Transformation. Add the ligation product to 50 μL Trans1-T1 competent cells. Flick to mix, then place on ice for 20–30 min.
Heat shock in a 42°C water bath for 45 s, then immediately cool on ice for 2–3 min.
Add 250 μL of SOC or LB medium (without antibiotics) and shake at 37°C for 1 h (220 rpm).
Prepare IPTG and X-gal mix: Take 8 μL of 500 mM IPTG and 40 μL of 20 mg/mL X-gal, mix, and apply evenly to the LB agar plate containing ampicillin. Incubate at 37°C for 30 min.

After absorption, take 200 μL of bacterial solution and evenly apply it on the plate, incubating 12–16 h at 37°C. Note: For more clones, centrifuge at 1500 x g for 1 min, discard some supernatant, retain 100–150 μL, and plate all the suspended bacteria. Note: The Trans1-T1 competent cells are from a commercial kit (see key resources table for catalog information).
Identify positive clones. Select white monoclonal clones and add 10 μL of sterile water. Vortex to mix.

Take 1 μL of the mixture and add it to a 25 μL PCR system, using M13 Forward and Reverse Primers for identification ( Tables 12 and 13 ). Note: The 2× EasyPfu PCR SuperMix is from a commercial kit (see key resources table for catalog information).

background: A DNA amplification product has already been cleaned up and quantified after fragmentation. The current stage is to recover DNA by cold precipitation, wash and re-dissolve the pellet, then denature the recovered DNA and carry out fluorescent labeling under light-protected room-temperature conditions before the final cleanup.
goal: Process the purified amplification product through pellet recovery and wash, re-solubilize the DNA, then complete denaturation and fluorescent labeling to generate material ready for the final purification step.
constraints: Include a precipitation hold at -20 C for at least 1 h after the sample has been mixed with added reagents.
Use two cold centrifugation steps during precipitation handling at 16000 x g and 4 C, with durations of 12 min and 5 min, and place the ethanol wash between them.
Dry the pellet before reconstitution, then dissolve it in 6 uL water with a 37 C incubation for 15 min.
Denature the DNA at 95 C for 3 min and immediately chill on ice before proceeding to labeling.
Before the labeling incubation, include a brief spin; then incubate the labeling reaction at room temperature in the 18-24 C range, protected from light, for at least 1 h.
Keep the program focused on this local protocol segment and use only provided action-pool functions with keyword arguments, preserving a single dependency chain from recovered pellet to labeled DNA.

gold_program:
cold_precipitated = incubate_sample(sample=precipitated_mix, temperature_c=-20.0, duration_min=60.0, condition='static')
first_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=cold_precipitated, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=12.0, temperature_c=4.0)
washed_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=ethanol_wash_tube, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=washed_pellet, method='air dry', temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=10.0)
dissolved_pellet = incubate_sample(sample=resuspended_pellet, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=15.0, condition='static')
denatured_dna = heat_sample(sample=dissolved_pellet, temperature_c=95.0, duration_min=3.0, device='heat block')
iced_dna = cool_sample(sample=denatured_dna, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=2.0, device='ice bath')
spun_labeling_reaction = centrifuge_sample(sample=labeling_reaction, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=0.2, temperature_c=22.0)
labeled_dna = incubate_sample(sample=spun_labeling_reaction, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=60.0, condition='protected from light')
final_labeled_prep = store_sample(sample=labeled_dna, temperature_c=22.0, duration_h=0.5, container='cleanup eluate tube')
protocol_title: Generation of Inexpensive, Highly Labeled Probes for Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization (FISH)

source_steps.stage: Sonication and Purification of the Amplification Products
source_steps.description: Purify the reaction using a PCR clean up kit or SPRI beads (1.8x Ampure) according to the manufacturer’s instructions. Elute in 80 ul MilliQ water. Estimate the DNA concentration using Nanodrop. (Under ideal conditions, the reaction would yield a total of 1.5 to 2.4 ug of DNA)
Add the following to the reaction:
Vortex and spin briefly and incubate the reaction at −20°C for at least 1 h.
Spin the tube at 16000 g for 12 min at 4°C.
Remove supernatant and wash pellet with 500 ul of 70% Ethanol.
Spin again at 16000 g for 5 min at 4°C.
Air dry the pellet.
Add 6 ul of nuclease free water to the tube and incubate at 37°C for 15 min. Pipette gently to dissolve the pellet.

source_steps.stage: Labeling the DNA Fragments with Fluorescent Dye
source_steps.description: Denature the DNA by incubating the tube at 95°C for 3 min and immediately cool on ice.

source_steps.stage: Labeling the DNA Fragments with Fluorescent Dye
source_steps.description: Spin briefly and add in the following order:

source_steps.stage: Labeling the DNA Fragments with Fluorescent Dye
source_steps.description: Vortex for 15 s, spin briefly and incubate at 18-24°C in the dark for at least one h.

source_steps.stage: Purification and Precipitation of the Labeled DNA
source_steps.description: Add 340ul of MilliQ water to the reaction and purify using the PCR purification kit or SPRI beads (1.8x Ampure) according to the manufacturer’s instructions. Elute in 20 ul of MilliQ water.

background: A single-cell suspension from mouse lung metastasis tissue has already been prepared and counted in MACS buffer. The current stage is the cold MACS cleanup and endothelial pre-enrichment segment: unwanted immune and epithelial cells are depleted first, the unlabeled fraction is retained, and that recovered fraction is then labeled for endothelial selection. The workflow is dominated by repeated low-speed spins, cold incubations, wash steps, and a magnetic separation performed after filtration.
goal: Carry the counted lung cell suspension through bead-based depletion of CD45 and EpCAM positive cells, recover and pellet the negative fraction, then label and wash that fraction with CD31 beads so it is ready for the next magnetic enrichment stage.
constraints: Start from the counted suspension and include an initial centrifugation at 250 x g for 5 min before the first bead incubation.
Use cold bead incubations of 15 min at 4 C for both the depletion-labeling stage and the later CD31-labeling stage.
Represent the depletion labeling as using 80 uL MACS buffer with 10 uL CD45 beads and 10 uL CD326 beads, and represent the later enrichment labeling as using 90 uL MACS buffer with 10 uL CD31 beads.
Each labeling stage must be followed by a wash corresponding to 3 mL MACS buffer and a centrifugation at 250 x g for 5 min at 4 C.

Before magnetic depletion, the sample should be treated as resuspended in 0.5 mL MACS buffer, passed through a 40 um strainer, and the unlabeled flow-through into a 15 mL conical tube must be the retained fraction.
Keep the program within the provided action pool, use a dependency chain across the whole segment, and reuse multiple action families such as centrifugation and mixing more than once.

available_inputs.description: MACS buffer for resuspension and washes

available_inputs.description: CD45 and CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads for depletion labeling

gold_program:
pelleted_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=counted_cell_suspension, speed_xg=250, duration_min=5, temperature_c=22)
depletion_labeled = mix_sample(sample=pelleted_cells, method="pipetting", duration_s=20, speed="gentle")
depletion_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=depletion_labeled, temperature_c=4, duration_min=15, condition="static")
depletion_wash_mixed = mix_sample(sample=depletion_incubated, method="pipetting", duration_s=20, speed="gentle")
depletion_wash_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=depletion_wash_mixed, speed_xg=250, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
column_input = filter_sample(sample=depletion_wash_pellet, filter_type="cell_strainer", pore_size_um=40, destination="15ml_conical_tube")
negative_fraction = magnetic_separate(sample=column_input, rack=ms_column_setup, duration_min=3, collect_phase="flow_through")
negative_fraction_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=negative_fraction, speed_xg=250, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
cd31_labeled = mix_sample(sample=negative_fraction_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=20, speed="gentle")
cd31_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=cd31_labeled, temperature_c=4, duration_min=15, condition="static")
cd31_wash_mixed = mix_sample(sample=cd31_incubated, method="pipetting", duration_s=20, speed="gentle")
cd31_wash_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=cd31_wash_mixed, speed_xg=250, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Resuspend the cell pellet in 5 mL MACS buffer, pipet up and down with a pipette P1000, count the cells.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 250 g for 5 min and aspirate the supernatant. Proceed with the pellet to step b-i.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Depletion of immune and epithelial cells using CD45 and CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads. (Timing: 45 min) Add 80 μL MACS buffer, 10 μL CD45 MicroBeads and 10 μL CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads to the cell pellet. Note: We suggest to resuspend the cells in 80 μL of MACS buffer as indicated above. Adapt the amount of MicroBeads according to the cell number and follow the manufacturer's instructions, e.g. 10 μL of the beads for up to 1 x 10 7 cells; for larger numbers of cells, scale up the amount accordingly ( https://www.miltenyibiotec.com/NL-en/products/cd45-microbeads-mouse.html?countryRedirected=1#gref ; https://www.miltenyibiotec.com/US-en/products/cd326-epcam-microbeads-mouse.html#gref ).

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Mix and incubate for 15 min at 4°C. Note: Process the samples quickly to avoid non-specific binding of MicroBeads at 20°C–22°C.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Wash the cells by adding 3 mL of MACS buffer and centrifuge at 250 g for 5 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Aspirate the supernatant completely and resuspend the pellet in 0.5 mL of MACS buffer.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Place an MS column in the magnetic field of the suitable MACS Separator and rinse with 0.5 mL MACS buffer.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Apply the cell suspension from step iv onto the column through a 40 μm cell strainer (blue) and collect unlabeled cells that flow through in 15 mL conical tube (this is the fraction that contains ECs).

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Perform three washing steps with 0.5 mL MACS buffer each.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the flow-through (CD45 & CD326 (EpCAM) negative fraction) at 250 g for 5 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Enrichment of ECs using CD31 murine MicroBeads. (Timing: 45 min) Add 90 μL MACS buffer and 10 μL CD31 MicroBeads to the cell pellet. Mix and incubate for 15 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Wash the cells by adding 3 mL of MACS buffer and centrifuge at 250 g for 5 min at 4°C.

background: A transformed DH10Bac colony containing the ACACA construct has already been grown overnight in selective LB. The active task is the bacmid purification portion: collect cells from the overnight culture, generate a cleared lysate during mini-prep processing, precipitate bacmid DNA with cold isopropanol, perform repeated cold ethanol washes, remove residual ethanol, and resuspend the final pellet for immediate downstream use.
goal: Recover purified bacmid DNA from the overnight DH10Bac culture through the centrifugation, precipitation, wash, drying, and resuspension stages.
constraints: Start from an overnight 4 mL selective culture from a single transformed colony and pellet it at 1600 x g for 10 min at room temperature.

Use a high-speed clarification spin of 17805 x g for 2 min after the P1/P2/P3 mini-prep stage, then precipitate with 800 uL isopropanol for 90 min at -20 C before spinning at 17805 x g for 15 min at 4 C.
Wash the pellet twice with 750 uL of 75% ethanol, each followed by centrifugation at 17805 x g for 5 min at 4 C, and ensure the pellet is fully dried before dissolving it in 30 uL ddH2O.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keep outputs chained from prior steps, and include repeated use of at least two action families.

available_inputs.name: overnight_culture
available_inputs.description: Single-colony DH10Bac overnight culture in 4 mL LB with kanamycin, gentamicin, and tetracycline

available_inputs.name: prep_supernatant
available_inputs.description: Cleared mini-prep lysate supernatant after P1, P2, and P3 treatment

available_inputs.name: isopropanol_800ul
available_inputs.description: 800 uL isopropanol for bacmid precipitation

available_inputs.name: ethanol75
available_inputs.description: 75% ethanol used for pellet washing

available_inputs.name: ddh2o_30ul
available_inputs.description: 30 uL nuclease-free water for final bacmid dissolution

gold_program:
cell_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=overnight_culture, speed_xg=1600, duration_min=10, temperature_c=25)
clarified_prep = centrifuge_sample(sample=prep_supernatant, speed_xg=17805, duration_min=2, temperature_c=25)
precipitation_mix = mix_sample(sample=clarified_prep, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="moderate with 800 uL isopropanol")
chilled_mix = cool_sample(sample=precipitation_mix, temperature_c=-20, duration_min=90, device="freezer")
bacmid_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=chilled_mix, speed_xg=17805, duration_min=15, temperature_c=4)
ethanol_wash_1 = mix_sample(sample=bacmid_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="moderate with 750 uL 75% ethanol")
washed_pellet_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=ethanol_wash_1, speed_xg=17805, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
ethanol_wash_2 = mix_sample(sample=washed_pellet_1, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="moderate with 750 uL 75% ethanol")
washed_pellet_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=ethanol_wash_2, speed_xg=17805, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=washed_pellet_2, method="air dry", temperature_c=25, duration_min=10)
final_bacmid = mix_sample(sample=dried_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=15, speed="gentle in 30 uL ddH2O")

source_steps.stage: A. Transformation of DH10Bac competent cells (to obtain the bacmids to produce the baculovirus)
source_steps.description: Recombine fragments and vectors in a 1:1 molar ratio. Add 4 μL of the linearized vector and 1 μL of the target fragment to 5 μL of 2× cloning mix and place at 50 °C for 15 min. After ligation, incubate the mixture at 4 °C. Then, add the mixture to 100 μL of DH5α competent cells, incubate on ice for 30 min, and heat-shock at 42 °C for 60 s. Further incubate the mixture on ice for 2 min. Add 200 μL of antibiotic-free LB medium and incubate at 37 °C at 220 rpm for 45 min. Then, coat the cells on an ampicillin-resistant plate and incubate at 37 °C overnight.

source_steps.stage: A. Transformation of DH10Bac competent cells (to obtain the bacmids to produce the baculovirus)
source_steps.description: Mix 1 μL (10 ng) of pBMCL1-ACACA plasmid ( Figure 1A ) with 100 μL of DH10Bac competent cells in a microcentrifuge tube. Incubate on ice for 30 min. Heat-shock the mixture at 42 °C for 45 s, then incubate on ice for 2.5 min. Add 900 μL of LB medium (without antibiotics) and incubate at 37 °C in a shaking incubator for 5 h.

source_steps.stage: A. ACACA recombinant plasmid map. B. Illustration for virus passaging. C. Diagram for purifying ACACA protein. D. SDS-PAGE for ACACA purification.
source_steps.description: Plate 100 μL of the transformation mixture onto a blue and white spot screening plate containing kanamycin (50 μg/mL), gentamicin (7 μg/mL), tetracycline (10 μg/mL), X-Gal (100 μg/mL), and IPTG (40 μg/mL).

source_steps.stage: A. ACACA recombinant plasmid map. B. Illustration for virus passaging. C. Diagram for purifying ACACA protein. D. SDS-PAGE for ACACA purification.
source_steps.description: Incubate the plate at 37 °C for 48 h.

source_steps.stage: A. ACACA recombinant plasmid map. B. Illustration for virus passaging. C. Diagram for purifying ACACA protein. D. SDS-PAGE for ACACA purification.
source_steps.description: Select white colonies and streak for isolation of single transformed colonies. Incubate at 37 °C for 12 h.

source_steps.stage: A. ACACA recombinant plasmid map. B. Illustration for virus passaging. C. Diagram for purifying ACACA protein. D. SDS-PAGE for ACACA purification.
source_steps.description: Inoculate a single transformed colony into 4 mL of LB medium containing 50 μg/mL kanamycin, 7 μg/mL gentamicin, and 10 μg/mL tetracycline. Incubate overnight at 37 °C with shaking at 220 rpm.

source_steps.stage: A. ACACA recombinant plasmid map. B. Illustration for virus passaging. C. Diagram for purifying ACACA protein. D. SDS-PAGE for ACACA purification.
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the overnight culture at 1,600× g for 10 min at room temperature. Discard the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: A. ACACA recombinant plasmid map. B. Illustration for virus passaging. C. Diagram for purifying ACACA protein. D. SDS-PAGE for ACACA purification.
source_steps.description: Purify the bacmid using P1, P2, and P3 solution from a Plasmid Mini Prep kit and centrifuge at 17,805× g for 2 min.

source_steps.stage: A. ACACA recombinant plasmid map. B. Illustration for virus passaging. C. Diagram for purifying ACACA protein. D. SDS-PAGE for ACACA purification.
source_steps.description: Mix the supernatant with 800 μL of isopropanol for 1.5 h at -20 °C and centrifuge at 17,805× g for 15 min at 4 °C.

source_steps.stage: A. ACACA recombinant plasmid map. B. Illustration for virus passaging. C. Diagram for purifying ACACA protein. D. SDS-PAGE for ACACA purification.
source_steps.description: Wash the pellet with 750 μL of 75% ethanol at 17,805× g for 5 min at 4 °C. Repeat twice. Dry and allow the ethanol to evaporate completely in a hood.

source_steps.stage: A. ACACA recombinant plasmid map. B. Illustration for virus passaging. C. Diagram for purifying ACACA protein. D. SDS-PAGE for ACACA purification.
source_steps.description: Dissolve the bacmids in 30 μL of ddH 2 O for immediate use.

source_steps.stage: B. Transfection of ACACA-pBMCL1 bacmid into Sf9 Cells (to obtain baculoviruses to infect HEK293 cells)
source_steps.description: Seed Sf9 cells (0.8 × 10 6 ) in a 6-well plate and incubate at room temperature for 1 h to allow the cells to adhere.

background: A recombinant bacmid preparation for full-length human HTT production has progressed to the DNA isolation stage after bacterial growth and an initial harvest. The remaining work is to complete alkaline lysis cleanup, precipitate the bacmid DNA, wash the pellet, dry it, and gently resuspend it for downstream analysis.
goal: Complete the local bacmid purification segment starting from a harvested bacterial pellet and ending with bacmid DNA dissolved in TE buffer.
constraints: Start from the harvested cell pellet and maintain a single continuous sample lineage through lysis, neutralization, clarification, precipitation, wash, drying, and final dissolution.
Represent gentle inversion-style mixing during buffer addition and precipitation handling; do not use vortexing in this DNA isolation segment.
Include a 5 min room-temperature incubation after lysis, using a temperature within the 20-24 C range.
Include two separate 10 min ice incubations: one after neutralization and one after isopropanol-based DNA precipitation setup.
Use high-speed centrifugation at 17000 x g twice for 20 min, with one spin at 4 C and the later spin at room temperature.
Include a 70% ethanol wash and then a final centrifugation at 17000 x g for 5 min at room temperature.
Represent pellet drying as air drying before the final TE resuspension.
End with gentle resuspension in TE buffer rather than pipette-based mixing.

available_inputs.name: cell_pellet
available_inputs.description: Harvested bacterial pellet from the bacmid culture.

available_inputs.name: sol2_lysis
available_inputs.description: Second isolation buffer used for alkaline lysis.

available_inputs.name: sol3_neutralization
available_inputs.description: Third isolation buffer used to neutralize the lysate.

available_inputs.name: isopropanol_100
available_inputs.description: Absolute isopropanol for DNA precipitation.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_70
available_inputs.description: 70% ethanol for washing the DNA pellet.

available_inputs.name: te_buffer
available_inputs.description: TE buffer for dissolving the dried bacmid DNA pellet.

gold_program:
lysed_sample = mix_sample(sample=cell_pellet, method="inversion", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
rt_lysed_sample = incubate_sample(sample=lysed_sample, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=5.0, condition="static")
neutralized_sample = mix_sample(sample=rt_lysed_sample, method="inversion", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
iced_neutralized_sample = cool_sample(sample=neutralized_sample, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=10.0, device="ice bath")
clarified_sample = centrifuge_sample(sample=iced_neutralized_sample, speed_xg=17000, duration_min=20.0, temperature_c=4.0)
precipitation_mix = mix_sample(sample=clarified_sample, method="inversion", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
iced_precipitation_mix = cool_sample(sample=precipitation_mix, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=10.0, device="ice bath")
dna_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=iced_precipitation_mix, speed_xg=17000, duration_min=20.0, temperature_c=22.0)
washed_pellet = mix_sample(sample=dna_pellet, method="inversion", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
recovered_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_pellet, speed_xg=17000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=22.0)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=recovered_pellet, method="air dry", temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=10.0)
resuspended_bacmid_dna = mix_sample(sample=dried_pellet, method="tapping", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
protocol_title: Purification of full-length recombinant human huntingtin proteins with allelic series of polyglutamine lengths

source_steps.description: Incubate for 2 days at 37°C in a shaking incubator.
Harvest the cells by centrifugation at 3,000 × g for 20 min at 4°C.
Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 300 μL of Sol I buffer.
Add 300 μL of Sol II buffer and mix gently by inverting. Incubate the sample at room temperature (RT; 20°C–24°C) for 5 min.
Add 300 μL of Sol III buffer to the mixture and gently mix by inverting. Incubate the sample on ice for 10 min.
Centrifuge the sample at 17,000 × g for 20 min at 4°C.
Transfer 750 μL of the supernatant to 800 μL of 100% isopropanol and mix gently the sample by inverting. Incubate the sample on ice for 10 min.
Centrifuge the incubated sample at 17,000 × g for 20 min at RT.
Decant the supernatant and add 1 mL of 70% ethanol on the top of the white pellet. Wash the pellet by inverting the tube.
Centrifuge the sample at 17,000 × g for 5 min at RT.

After centrifugation, remove the supernatant with a pipet without disturbing the pellet. Dry the white DNA pellet with airdry while the tubes are upside-down on a Kimwipe until the white pellet becomes transparent.
Dissolve the transparent DNA pellet by adding 40 μL of TE buffer. Resuspend the DNA pellet gently by tapping. Do not use a pipette.

background: Primary human liver endothelial cell isolation has reached the depletion stage after pooled pellets were washed and transferred into a 15 mL tube. The next operations enrich endothelial cells by antibody labeling, a room-temperature wash, cooling, bead-based depletion on ice, and two sequential magnetic separations to recover the endothelial-cell-enriched supernatant.
goal: Complete the local enrichment segment so that contaminating biliary epithelial and immune cells are depleted and the endothelial-cell-enriched fraction is retained in a fresh sterile 15 mL tube.
constraints: Include two wash centrifugations at 850 x g for 5 minutes each, performed at 21.0-23.0 C.
Represent a 30 minute EpCAM-associated incubation at 37.0 C with agitation, followed later by a 30 minute bead-binding incubation on ice with agitation.
Switch from room-temperature PBS handling to ice-cold PBS before magnetic bead depletion, and preserve the on-ice state through bead binding.
Model two consecutive magnetic separation periods of 2 minutes each, with the second acting on material transferred from the first separation.
The final output must correspond to endothelial-cell-enriched supernatant placed into a new sterile 15 mL tube.
Use only provided action-pool functions, assign every call to a variable, and keep the program between 6 and 12 top-level steps.

available_inputs.name: combined_pellet_15ml
available_inputs.description: Combined liver cell pellet in a 15 mL centrifuge tube at the start of the depletion workflow.

available_inputs.name: epcam_antibody_mix
available_inputs.description: Sample state after the pellet has been resuspended in PBS and combined with EpCAM antibody.

available_inputs.name: bead_depletion_mix
available_inputs.description: Sample state after the washed pellet has been cooled and combined with Goat Anti-Mouse IgG and CD45 Dynabeads.

available_inputs.name: first_mag_supernatant
available_inputs.description: Supernatant transferred from the first magnetic separation into a second 15 mL tube.

gold_program:
step1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=combined_pellet_15ml, speed_xg=850, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=22.0)
step2 = incubate_sample(sample=epcam_antibody_mix, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=30.0, condition='shaking')
step3 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step2, speed_xg=850, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=22.0)
step4 = cool_sample(sample=step3, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=1.0, device='ice')
step5 = incubate_sample(sample=bead_depletion_mix, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=30.0, condition='agitation_on_ice')
step6 = incubate_sample(sample=step5, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=2.0, condition='magnetic_separation')
step7 = incubate_sample(sample=first_mag_supernatant, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=2.0, condition='magnetic_separation')
step8 = store_sample(sample=step7, temperature_c=22.0, duration_h=0.5, container='new_sterile_15ml_tube')
protocol_title: Protocol to study monocyte transmigration across primary human liver endothelial cells under physiological shear flow conditions in vitro

source_steps.description: Remove supernatant and combine 2 pellets into 1 universal container (2 universals in total; Figure 1 Civ). Make volume up to 25 mL with sterile PBS and centrifuge at 850 × g for 5 min.
Tip off supernatant and combine pellet into a 15 mL centrifuge tube. Make the volume up to 14 mL with sterile PBS and centrifuge at 850 × g for 5 min at 21°C–23°C.
Depleting biliary epithelial cells (BEC) ( Figure 1 Di) and immune cells ( Figure 1 Dii). Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 500 μL of sterile PBS.

Add 50 μL EpCAM antibody and incubate at 37°C for 30 min in an orbital shaker to provide constant agitation. Note: During this 30 min incubation period, put 50 mL of sterile PBS on ice in preparation for the following steps.
Make the volume up to ∼10 mL with sterile room temperature (21°C–23°C) PBS, mix well and centrifuge at 850 × g for 5 min at 21°C–23°C.
Tip off supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 500 μL ice-cold sterile PBS.
Add 10 μL Goat Anti-Mouse IgG Dynabeads and 50 μL CD45 Dynabeads (ensuring that the beads are thoroughly resuspended prior to addition).
Mix well and incubate for 30 min on ice with constant agitation. Note: An orbital shaker or benchtop rocker can be used for this purpose.
Make up the volume to ∼6 mL with ice-cold PBS and mix well.
Loosen lid of centrifuge tube and place in DynaMag-15 Separation Magnet for 2 min.
With the 15 mL centrifuge tube in the magnet pipette off the supernatant and transfer to a second 15 mL centrifuge tube (do not discard).
Place the second tube back in the magnet for 2 min and pipette off supernatant enriched for endothelial cells into a new sterile 15 mL centrifuge tube.

background: A lentiviral transfection setup for an ecDHFR-DD fusion construct is already underway in HEK-293T producer cells. The immediate work is to finish forming the transfection complexes, expose the cells to the reagent, allow virus production over two collection windows, pool the supernatants in a 15 mL conical tube, and clarify the pooled material by filtration.
goal: Produce a valid action-only plan for the late transfection and virus-harvest segment, ending with filtered lentiviral supernatant ready for downstream single-use storage.
constraints: Keep the short complex-formation incubations at room temperature within 20-23 C, including a 2 min hold after the first 10 s vortex and a 5-10 min hold after the combined reagent is vortexed.
Use gentle mixing for the cell-exposure step, then include a 6 h post-transfection incubation before media replacement and an overnight incubation of 14-18 h.
Represent the first viral harvest as material stored at 4 C in a 15 mL conical tube until the second harvest is available, and pool the 24 h and 48 h harvests before clarification.
The terminal processing step must be filtration through a 0.45 um PES membrane into single-use screw-top storage tubes.
Use only provided action-pool functions, assign every call to a variable, and keep all operations at the protocol abstraction level without adding unsupported transfer mechanics.

available_inputs.name: dna_mix_a
available_inputs.description: Tube containing the DNA, P3000, and Opti-MEM portion of the lentiviral transfection mix.

available_inputs.name: l3000_mix_b
available_inputs.description: Tube containing the L3000 and Opti-MEM portion of the transfection mix.

available_inputs.name: transfection_reagent_combined
available_inputs.description: Single tube holding the merged contents of the two transfection-mix parts after manual combination.

available_inputs.name: hek293t_cells
available_inputs.description: HEK-293T producer cells seeded the previous day in a well containing culture media.

available_inputs.name: harvest_24h_in_conical
available_inputs.description: First lentiviral supernatant harvest collected around 24 h post-transfection into a 15 mL conical tube.

available_inputs.name: pooled_harvest_48h_in_conical
available_inputs.description: Same 15 mL conical tube after the second 48 h harvest has been added to the first harvest.

gold_program:
vortexed_dna_mix_a = vortex_mix(sample=dna_mix_a, duration_s=10.0, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=22.0)
rested_dna_mix_a = incubate_sample(sample=vortexed_dna_mix_a, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=2.0, condition="static")
precombined_reagent = mix_sample(sample=transfection_reagent_combined, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
vortexed_transfection_reagent = vortex_mix(sample=precombined_reagent, duration_s=10.0, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=22.0)
formed_transfection_reagent = incubate_sample(sample=vortexed_transfection_reagent, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=7.0, condition="static")
transfected_cells = mix_sample(sample=hek293t_cells, method="rocking", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
post_transfection_cells = incubate_sample(sample=transfected_cells, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=360.0, condition="humidified")
overnight_production_cells = incubate_sample(sample=post_transfection_cells, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=960.0, condition="humidified")
chilled_first_harvest = store_sample(sample=harvest_24h_in_conical, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=24.0, container="15 mL conical tube")
pooled_harvest = mix_sample(sample=pooled_harvest_48h_in_conical, method="pipetting", duration_s=5.0, speed="gentle")
filtered_lentivirus = filter_sample(sample=pooled_harvest, filter_type="PES", pore_size_um=0.45, destination="single-use screw-top tubes")

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: For each reaction, combine 125 μL of Opti-MEM medium with 5 μL of P3000 reagent, 1.43 μg of viral packaging DNA (psPAX2 plasmid), and 476 ng of viral envelope DNA (VSV-G is recommended due to its broad tropism, this is included in the pMD2.G plasmid). Finally, add 595 ng of the pLenti plasmid DNA containing the ecDHFR-DD fusion gene (pLenti CMV Puro plasmid, for example, as the vector backbone). Vortex for 10 seconds and incubate at room temperature (20°C–23°C) for 2 min.

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: For each reaction, in another tube combine 125 μL of Opti-MEM medium with 7.5 μL of L3000 reagent.

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: Combine the volumes from steps 5 and 6, vortex for 10 seconds, and incubate at room temperature (20°C–23°C) for 5–10 min. During this time, the transfection complex forms. This is your transfection reagent.

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: Gently pipette the 250 μL of transfection reagent into the existing media in the well containing the HEK-293T cells which were seeded the day before. Rock the plate gently to mix.

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: 6 h post transfection, aspirate the existing media and add 2 mL of fresh media. Continue incubation overnight (14–18 h).

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: 24 h post transfection, the media in the well will contain virus. Collect this media while utilizing the proper safety equipment and technique into a 15 mL conical tube. Store this media at 4°C and add 2 mL of fresh, warm media to the well.

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: 48 h post transfection, collect the media once again and add to the 15 mL conical tube described in step #10. Bleach and discard the 6-well plate.

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: Filter the collected media through a 0.45 μm PES filter and aliquot into single use screw top tubes. We recommend using up to 500 μL per vial. The lentivirus can be used immediately or frozen at −80°C for later use. This is an appropriate pause point, if necessary.

source_steps.stage: Generate a Stable Cell Line Expressing the ecDHFR-DD-POI
source_steps.description: Plate target cells (e.g., ARPE-19) to be infected with lentivirus at a density of 1 × 10 5 cells/well of a 12 well (or scale accordingly). Allow to attach overnight (14–18 h).

source_steps.stage: Generate a Stable Cell Line Expressing the ecDHFR-DD-POI
source_steps.description: Dilute polybrene into full DMEM media to reach a final concentration of 1–10 μg/mL. Make enough media to cover cells in their appropriate dish. For ARPE-19 cells, we use 1 μg/mL polybrene.

source_steps.stage: Generate a Stable Cell Line Expressing the ecDHFR-DD-POI
source_steps.description: Thaw a single lentivirus aliquot at room temperature (20°C–23°C). For each well to be infected, transfer 50–100 μL into the appropriate amount of polybrene-containing media.

source_steps.stage: Generate a Stable Cell Line Expressing the ecDHFR-DD-POI
source_steps.description: Remove old media on cells, add polybrene/lentivirus containing media on top, incubate for 24 h. Alternatively, for suspension cells, spinoculation can be used.

background: A lentiviral preparation has already been produced from transfected HEK293T cultures and filtered. The current stage is the concentration and cleanup of viral particles by cold ultracentrifugation, followed by gentle resuspension and storage. This segment emphasizes maintaining low temperature, performing two high-speed spins with a DPBS wash in between, and ending with short-term cold equilibration before aliquoting for long-term freezer storage.
goal: Process the filtered lentiviral supernatant through the concentration, wash, resuspension, and storage stage so that the final product is an aliquoted virus stock ready for frozen storage.
constraints: Use cold handling conditions for the concentration workflow, with centrifugation performed at 49000 x g and 4 degrees Celsius.
The viral concentrate requires two separate high-speed centrifugation rounds, each lasting 2 hours.
A wash with cold DPBS must occur between the two centrifugation rounds, and the washed material should be mixed gently rather than aggressively.
After the final spin, retain approximately 100 uL DPBS for resuspension of the viral pellet.
The resuspended virus should be held at 4 degrees Celsius for 24 hours before final aliquot storage.
Final storage must reflect small aliquots corresponding to 10 uL portions in 1.5 mL tubes and be at minus 80 degrees Celsius.
Use only the provided action-pool functions and keep the workflow at the sample-processing level rather than describing manual handling mechanics.

available_inputs.name: filtered_virus_supernatant
available_inputs.description: 0.22 um-filtered lentiviral supernatant ready for ultracentrifugal concentration

available_inputs.name: cold_dpbs
available_inputs.description: Pre-chilled DPBS used for washing and final resuspension

available_inputs.name: ultra_tube
available_inputs.description: Ultracentrifuge tube containing the viral preparation

available_inputs.name: aliquot_tubes_1_5ml
available_inputs.description: Set of sterile 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes for final 10 uL aliquots

gold_program:
spin1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=filtered_virus_supernatant, speed_xg=49000, duration_min=120.0, temperature_c=4.0)
cool1 = cool_sample(sample=spin1, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=5.0, device="ice bath")
wash_mix = mix_sample(sample=cold_dpbs, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
washed_pellet = mix_sample(sample=spin1, method="pipetting", duration_s=20.0, speed="gentle")
spin2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_pellet, speed_xg=49000, duration_min=120.0, temperature_c=4.0)
resuspend_mix = mix_sample(sample=spin2, method="pipetting", duration_s=20.0, speed="gentle")
equilibration = store_sample(sample=resuspend_mix, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=24.0, container="ultra_tube")
aliquot_cool = cool_sample(sample=equilibration, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_min=10.0, device="freezer")
final_stock = store_sample(sample=aliquot_cool, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_h=168.0, container="aliquot_tubes_1_5ml")

source_steps.description: Check for the confluence of HEK293T cells (recommend is 80%) through the microscope and replace the culture medium with 10 mL fresh culture medium 1 h before transfection.

Prepare the mixture of helper plasmids and target plasmid with PEI ( Figure 1 ). Note: Dilute total DNA (target plasmid and helper plasmid) and PEI (1 mg/mL) in 1:2.5 ratio (1 μg of DNA: 2.5 μL of PEI) Take one sterile 50 mL Eppendorf (EP) tube, add 5 mL of Opti-MEM I Reduced Serum Media, and add the plasmids as follows: Vector DNA 150 μg VSVG 40 μg pMDLg 100 μg pRSV-Rev 55 μg Mix this mixture by pipette (approximately 10 times) using a 5 mL disposable pipette. Label the tube “DNA” and incubate for 5 min at 25°C. Note: When you first use those plasmids, check your plasmid with restriction enzymes or sequence to make sure you have right plasmid (e.g.: cutting the VSV-G plasmid with restriction endonucleases HindIII and EcoRI produces three fragments of 5.4 kb, 1.6 kb and 0.58 kb in length; cutting the pMDLg plasmid with restriction endonuclease EcoRI produces three fragments of 4.3 kb, 4.1 kb and 0.4 kb in length; and cutting the pRSV-Rev plasmid with restriction endonuclease EcoRI produces two fragments of 4.1 kb and 0.3 kb in length). The concentration of plasmid also needs to be confirmed to ensure the amount added (e.g.: the original concentration of VSV-G is 800 ng/μL (measured by NanoDrop instrument), therefore the volume of VSV-G added is 50 μL (40∗1000/800 = 50 μL)).

Take another sterile 50 mL EP tube, add 5 mL of Opti-MEM I Reduced Serum Media, add 862.5 μL PEI (1 mg/mL), mix by pipette (approximately 10 times) using a 5 mL disposable pipette. Label the tube “PEI” and incubate for 5 min at 25°C. Note: PEI, a non-viral cationic polymer, can be used to transfect plasmid DNA or siRNA into suspension or adherent cells due to its low toxicity, low cost, and low immunogenicity. 4 Alternatives: You can use Lipofectamine 3000 transfection reagent to replace PEI. 5
Add PEI diluent to plasmid diluent 50 mL EP tube (DNA tube), gently mix by pipette (approximately 10 times) using a 10 mL disposable pipette. Incubate for 20 min at 25°C.
Take out 5 dishes of HEK293T cell from incubator, add 2 mL the mixture of DNA-PEI drop by drop to each dish, gently mix, mark dishes, and put them back to incubator.
Remove media by aspiration after 4–6 h transfection, add 13 mL fresh culture media (see materials and equipment ) to each dish.
Harvest released viral particles by collecting viral supernatant at 40, 64, and 88 h in a T75 flask, stored at 4°C.

Filter virus through a 0.22 μm vacuum bottle filter and centrifuge at 49000 g, 4°C for 2 h. Discard the supernatant by aspiration ( Figure 2 ). Preparation for lentivirus (LVs) harvest by ultracentrifugation (A) Main components before LVs harvest by ultracentrifugation. (B) The ultracentrifuge is used for LVs harvest. (C) The proper SW32Ti swinging-bucket rotor and the matched six tubes were well installed. (D) Choose the right rotor and laboratory consumables. (E) Set up the correct parameters in order: 20,000 rpm, 2 h, and 4°C.
Wash virus once with cold DPBS and then perform the final centrifugation at 49000 g, 4°C for 2 h.
Leave approximately 100 μL of cold DPBS in the tube to resuspend the viruses’ particles, and then stored at 4°C for 24 h.
Aliquot the viruses at 10 μL per 1.5 mL tube, store at −80°C.
Measure virus titer by infecting HEK293T cells with serially diluted virus preparations. Plate HEK293T cells in 6-well plate (5 × 10ˆ4 cells/per well) a day before viral titer infection.

background: A lentiviral packaging workflow in HEK293T cultures has already progressed through transfection and supernatant collection. The current stage is downstream concentration and handling of viral particles: the pooled supernatant has been clarified by filtration, and the virus now needs to be pelleted, washed, gently resuspended in a small DPBS volume, briefly held cold to complete resuspension, and then portioned for frozen storage.
goal: Process the filtered lentiviral preparation through the concentration, wash, resuspension, short cold hold, and aliquot storage stage so that a usable frozen virus stock is obtained.
constraints: Use ultracentrifugation conditions corresponding to 49000 x g at 4 degrees C for 2 h, and perform this high-speed spin twice with a DPBS wash in between.
Maintain cold handling during the post-spin wash and resuspension stage; the wash buffer is cold DPBS.
After the first high-speed pellet, remove supernatant before proceeding to the wash.
Resuspend the final pellet in approximately 100 uL cold DPBS rather than a large volume.
Allow the resuspended virus to remain at 4 degrees C for 24 h before final aliquot storage.
Final storage must represent 10 uL aliquots in 1.5 mL tubes at minus 80 degrees C.
Capture the wash/resuspension using available generic mixing actions rather than adding unsupported liquid-transfer primitives.
Keep the workflow focused on the harvest/concentration segment and do not jump to titer measurement or cell infection.

available_inputs.name: filtered_viral_supernatant
available_inputs.description: 0.22 um-filtered lentiviral supernatant pooled from producer-cell harvests

available_inputs.name: cold_dpbs
available_inputs.description: Pre-chilled DPBS used for washing and final resuspension of viral pellets

available_inputs.name: ultracentrifuge_tubes
available_inputs.description: Compatible centrifuge tubes containing the filtered virus preparation

available_inputs.name: aliquot_tubes_1_5ml
available_inputs.description: Sterile 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes for final 10 uL aliquots

gold_program:
pelleted_virus_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=filtered_viral_supernatant, speed_xg=49000, duration_min=120.0, temperature_c=4.0)
supernatant_removed_pellet = record_measurement(sample=pelleted_virus_1, metric="post_spin_state", unit="state", note="supernatant aspirated after first ultracentrifugation")
washed_pellet = mix_sample(sample=cold_dpbs, method="pipetting", duration_s=20.0, speed="gentle")
virus_in_wash = mix_sample(sample=supernatant_removed_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=20.0, speed="gentle")
pelleted_virus_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=virus_in_wash, speed_xg=49000, duration_min=120.0, temperature_c=4.0)
final_resuspension = mix_sample(sample=pelleted_virus_2, method="pipetting", duration_s=30.0, speed="gentle")
cold_held_virus = store_sample(sample=final_resuspension, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=24.0, container="ultracentrifuge_tubes")
aliquot_preparation = record_measurement(sample=cold_held_virus, metric="aliquot_volume", unit="uL", note="prepare 10 uL aliquots in 1.5 mL tubes from virus resuspended in approximately 100 uL cold DPBS")
frozen_aliquots = store_sample(sample=aliquot_preparation, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_h=24.0, container="aliquot_tubes_1_5ml")

source_steps.description: Prepare the mixture of helper plasmids and target plasmid with PEI ( Figure 1 ). Note: Dilute total DNA (target plasmid and helper plasmid) and PEI (1 mg/mL) in 1:2.5 ratio (1 μg of DNA: 2.5 μL of PEI) Take one sterile 50 mL Eppendorf (EP) tube, add 5 mL of Opti-MEM I Reduced Serum Media, and add the plasmids as follows: Vector DNA 150 μg VSVG 40 μg pMDLg 100 μg pRSV-Rev 55 μg Mix this mixture by pipette (approximately 10 times) using a 5 mL disposable pipette. Label the tube “DNA” and incubate for 5 min at 25°C. Note: When you first use those plasmids, check your plasmid with restriction enzymes or sequence to make sure you have right plasmid (e.g.: cutting the VSV-G plasmid with restriction endonucleases HindIII and EcoRI produces three fragments of 5.4 kb, 1.6 kb and 0.58 kb in length; cutting the pMDLg plasmid with restriction endonuclease EcoRI produces three fragments of 4.3 kb, 4.1 kb and 0.4 kb in length; and cutting the pRSV-Rev plasmid with restriction endonuclease EcoRI produces two fragments of 4.1 kb and 0.3 kb in length). The concentration of plasmid also needs to be confirmed to ensure the amount added (e.g.: the original concentration of VSV-G is 800 ng/μL (measured by NanoDrop instrument), therefore the volume of VSV-G added is 50 μL (40∗1000/800 = 50 μL)).

Take another sterile 50 mL EP tube, add 5 mL of Opti-MEM I Reduced Serum Media, add 862.5 μL PEI (1 mg/mL), mix by pipette (approximately 10 times) using a 5 mL disposable pipette. Label the tube “PEI” and incubate for 5 min at 25°C. Note: PEI, a non-viral cationic polymer, can be used to transfect plasmid DNA or siRNA into suspension or adherent cells due to its low toxicity, low cost, and low immunogenicity. 4 Alternatives: You can use Lipofectamine 3000 transfection reagent to replace PEI. 5
Add PEI diluent to plasmid diluent 50 mL EP tube (DNA tube), gently mix by pipette (approximately 10 times) using a 10 mL disposable pipette. Incubate for 20 min at 25°C.
Take out 5 dishes of HEK293T cell from incubator, add 2 mL the mixture of DNA-PEI drop by drop to each dish, gently mix, mark dishes, and put them back to incubator.
Remove media by aspiration after 4–6 h transfection, add 13 mL fresh culture media (see materials and equipment ) to each dish.
Harvest released viral particles by collecting viral supernatant at 40, 64, and 88 h in a T75 flask, stored at 4°C.

Filter virus through a 0.22 μm vacuum bottle filter and centrifuge at 49000 g, 4°C for 2 h. Discard the supernatant by aspiration ( Figure 2 ). Preparation for lentivirus (LVs) harvest by ultracentrifugation (A) Main components before LVs harvest by ultracentrifugation. (B) The ultracentrifuge is used for LVs harvest. (C) The proper SW32Ti swinging-bucket rotor and the matched six tubes were well installed. (D) Choose the right rotor and laboratory consumables. (E) Set up the correct parameters in order: 20,000 rpm, 2 h, and 4°C.
Wash virus once with cold DPBS and then perform the final centrifugation at 49000 g, 4°C for 2 h.
Leave approximately 100 μL of cold DPBS in the tube to resuspend the viruses’ particles, and then stored at 4°C for 24 h.
Aliquot the viruses at 10 μL per 1.5 mL tube, store at −80°C.
Measure virus titer by infecting HEK293T cells with serially diluted virus preparations. Plate HEK293T cells in 6-well plate (5 × 10ˆ4 cells/per well) a day before viral titer infection.
Prepare viruses of different titers by serial dilution (1:1, 1:10, 1:100…1:10ˆ6).

background: This experiment enriches newly synthesized biotin-tagged proteins from Xenopus tadpole brain lysates using NeutrAvidin beads before western blot analysis. At this stage, the beads have already been prewashed and resuspended in PBS, added across the protein samples, and the mixtures have been adjusted to the required binding volume. The remaining work is to complete the cold binding incubation and perform the post-binding wash sequence, including the more stringent SDS-containing wash and the final higher-speed cleanup spin.
goal: Process the bead-bound protein samples through overnight binding and the subsequent wash series so they are ready for downstream elution and western blotting.
constraints: Maintain the bead-protein binding incubation at 4 C for 14-16 h under a rotating or equivalent mixing condition suitable for head-over-head incubation.
After the overnight binding, use a light resuspension before each pellet-forming spin and remove supernatant between washes.
Use PBS as the standard wash buffer for multiple washes, but include one distinct wash with PBS containing 1% SDS.
Most wash spins in this segment are performed at about 1700 x g for 2 min, while the final cleanup spin is stronger at 4000 x g for 2 min.
The SDS-containing wash is performed at 25 C for 10 min with rotation-like mixing before the following spin.
Later actions should consume the output of earlier wash or spin steps rather than restarting from the raw sample.
Keep the workflow at the tube/sample processing level using only mixing, incubation, and centrifugation abstractions.

gold_program:
bound_overnight = incubate_sample(sample=bead_sample_mix, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=900.0, condition="head-over-head rotation")
resuspended_post_bind = vortex_mix(sample=bound_overnight, duration_s=3.0, speed_rpm=800, temperature_c=4.0)
pelleted_post_bind = centrifuge_sample(sample=resuspended_post_bind, speed_xg=1700, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=4.0)
pbs_wash_1 = vortex_mix(sample=pelleted_post_bind, duration_s=3.0, speed_rpm=800, temperature_c=4.0)
pbs_spin_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=pbs_wash_1, speed_xg=1700, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=4.0)
sds_wash_mix = mix_sample(sample=pbs_spin_1, method="rocking", duration_s=10.0, speed="light")
sds_wash_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=sds_wash_mix, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=10.0, condition="rotation")
sds_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=sds_wash_incubated, speed_xg=1700, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=25.0)
pbs_wash_2 = vortex_mix(sample=sds_spin, duration_s=3.0, speed_rpm=800, temperature_c=25.0)
pbs_spin_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=pbs_wash_2, speed_xg=1700, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=25.0)
final_pbs_wash = vortex_mix(sample=pbs_spin_2, duration_s=3.0, speed_rpm=800, temperature_c=25.0)
washed_bead_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=final_pbs_wash, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=25.0)

source_steps.description: Wash the beads with 500 μL of 1× PBS. Vortex lightly and spin down at 1699 × g for 1 min. Repeat three more times.
Resuspend the beads in 1 mL of 1× PBS.
Add NeutrAvidin beads to samples: Distribute resuspended beads into the tubes of the protein samples (54) as evenly as possible. Use a cut P200 tip to transfer the beads.
Add the bead suspension to all tubes in multiple rounds, adding 50–100 μL each time.
Lightly vortex the bead suspension in between each round.
Bring the total volume of the solution in each tube to 1 mL with 1× PBS.
Seal the tube with parafilm. Set the tubes up for head-over-head rotation in the cold room (4°C) for 14–16 h incubation.

source_steps.stage: Elution of proteins from NeutrAvidin beads and western blotting—Day 3
source_steps.description: Lightly vortex and spin down the samples at 1700 × g for 2 min and remove the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Elution of proteins from NeutrAvidin beads and western blotting—Day 3
source_steps.description: Wash the pellet with 1 mL of 1× PBS, lightly vortex and spin down at 1700 × g for 2 min to remove the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Elution of proteins from NeutrAvidin beads and western blotting—Day 3
source_steps.description: Wash pellet with 1 mL of 1× PBS containing 1% SDS to remove any weakly bound proteins; seal the tube with parafilm and set on head-to-toe rotation at 25°C for 10 min, then spin down at 1700 × g for 2 min to remove the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Elution of proteins from NeutrAvidin beads and western blotting—Day 3
source_steps.description: Wash the pellet with 1 mL of 1× PBS; lightly vortex and spin down at 1700 × g for 2 min to remove the supernatant.

source_steps.description: Do one more wash with 1 mL of 1× PBS – lightly vortex and spin down at 4000 × g for 2 min.

background: A clarified chemokine receptor preparation is being processed on Rho 1D4 affinity resin during the cold on-bead purification stage. The immediate task is to complete the main centrifuge-based wash cycle around receptor capture and then move the resin into small low-bind tubes for a final cleanup wash before later nanodisc reconstitution.
goal: Clean and repartition the receptor-bound 1D4 resin so it is ready for subsequent reconstitution steps while keeping the bound material on the resin throughout the cold wash workflow.
constraints: Keep all centrifugation steps at 4 C and 300 x g in this local stage.
Use two equilibration washes with Regular buffer at 5000 uL before receptor addition.
After receptor capture, pellet the resin and remove the liquid phase while preserving the pellet.
Include one larger Regular buffer wash of 20000 uL on the receptor-bound resin before splitting into smaller tubes.
Transfer or split the resin into two 2 mL Protein LoBind tubes at about 250 uL resin per tube.
After transfer to the small tubes, perform one additional Regular buffer wash of 1000 uL and then remove the supernatant.
Stay within the purification segment only; do not add lipids, MSP1D1, or Bio-beads.
Use a dependency chain where later cleanup acts on outputs from earlier wash and centrifuge operations.

available_inputs.name: resin_slurry
available_inputs.description: Rho 1D4 antibody resin slurry prepared for equilibration.

available_inputs.name: regular_buffer
available_inputs.description: Regular buffer for resin equilibration and washes.

available_inputs.name: solubilized_receptor
available_inputs.description: Solubilized receptor sample ready to bind the washed affinity resin.

available_inputs.name: lobind_tubes
available_inputs.description: Two 2 mL Protein LoBind tubes for the final small-volume resin handling.

gold_program:
eq_wash_1 = wash_sample(sample=resin_slurry, wash_buffer=regular_buffer, volume_ul=5000, repeat_index=1)
eq_spin_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=eq_wash_1, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
eq_pellet_1 = discard_supernatant(sample=eq_spin_1, volume_ul=5000, method="decanting", retain_pellet=True)
eq_wash_2 = wash_pellet(pellet=eq_pellet_1, wash_buffer=regular_buffer, volume_ul=5000, repeat_index=2)
captured_resin = add_reagent(sample=eq_wash_2, reagent=solubilized_receptor, volume_ul=1000, mixing="gentle rotation at 4C for 2 h")
capture_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=captured_resin, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
post_capture_pellet = discard_supernatant(sample=capture_spin, volume_ul=1000, method="decanting", retain_pellet=True)
large_wash = wash_pellet(pellet=post_capture_pellet, wash_buffer=regular_buffer, volume_ul=20000, repeat_index=1)
large_wash_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=large_wash, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
washed_resin = discard_supernatant(sample=large_wash_spin, volume_ul=20000, method="decanting", retain_pellet=True)
split_resin = aliquot_sample(sample=washed_resin, destination=lobind_tubes, volume_ul=250, aliquot_count=2)
final_cleanup = wash_pellet(pellet=split_resin, wash_buffer=regular_buffer, volume_ul=1000, repeat_index=1)
protocol_title: On-bead purification and nanodisc reconstitution of human chemokine receptor complexes for structural and biophysical studies
