        pellet: Pellet or bead fraction being resuspended.
        buffer: Resuspension buffer or solvent.
        volume_ul: Resuspension volume in microliters.
        method: Resuspension method such as pipetting or gentle vortexing.
    Returns:
        A handle for the resuspended pellet or bead slurry.
    """
    pass

source_steps.description: Centrifuge the solubilization mixture at 50,000–100,000 × g , 4°C for 30 min, and keep the supernatant on ice for subsequent purification and reconstitution steps.

Wash the Rho 1D4 antibody resin by equilibrating 0.5 mL of the resin slurry (containing 50% resin) with 5 mL Regular buffer in each of two 50 mL conical tubes. Centrifuge the tubes at 300 × g , 4°C for 5 min; discard the supernatant.
Add the solubilized receptor from step 5 to the washed 1D4 resin. Rotate at 4°C for 2 h.
Centrifuge the receptor-1D4-resin mixture at 300 × g , 4°C for 5 min; then discard the supernatant.
Wash the resin in each tube with 20 mL Regular buffer; centrifuge for 5 min at 300 × g , 4°C and discard the supernatant.

Transfer the receptor-1D4-resin to two separate 2 mL Protein lo-bind tubes, wash one more time with 1 mL Regular buffer, centrifuge as above and discard the supernatant. The resin volume in each tube should be around 250 μL.
Dilute the POPC:POPG lipid stock to 2 mM (total lipid) with 1 mL Regular buffer, add 500 μL of the 2 mM lipid mixture to each centrifuge tube of receptor-1D4-resin, and rotate at 4°C for 30 min.
Centrifuge the lipid-receptor-1D4 resin mixture for 1 min at 300 × g and 4°C; discard the supernatant.
Add 500 μL 2 mg/mL MSP1D1 to each tube; rotate at 4°C for 30 min.
To remove detergent, add ∼20 mg of washed Bio-beads SM-2 resin to the MSP1D1-lipid-receptor-1D4 resin mixture; rotate at 4°C for 12–16 h.
Centrifuge the Bio-bead-MSP1D1-lipid-receptor-1D4 resin mixture for 1 min at 300 × g , 4°C; discard the supernatant.
Wash each tube of Bio-bead-MSP1D1-lipid-receptor-1D4 resin with 500 μL HBS buffer; centrifuge for 1 min at 300 × g , 4°C, and discard the supernatant.

background: A lentiviral supernatant carrying an ecDHFR-DD fusion construct has already been generated from transfected HEK-293T cells. The current stage is the post-production handling of that viral medium: combining the two harvests in a 15 mL conical tube, holding the pooled material cold, clarifying it through the specified membrane, and then preparing the virus for immediate use or frozen storage.
goal: Process the collected lentiviral medium from sequential harvests into a filtered, single-use virus stock while preserving the required cold-hold and storage conditions.
constraints: Use the pooled lentiviral medium in a 15 mL conical tube as the starting material.
Represent two collection timepoints corresponding to about 24 h and 48 h after transfection before final processing.
The first collected viral medium must be held at 4 degrees Celsius until the later harvest is added.
Filtration must use a 0.45 micrometer PES filter.
The final product should be compatible with single-use aliquots up to 500 microliters per vial.
Include an option for immediate use versus frozen storage, with frozen storage at negative 80 degrees Celsius.
Keep the program focused on this local virus-harvest handling segment rather than earlier transfection setup or later infection of target cells.
Use only available action-pool operations and maintain a dependency chain in which later operations consume outputs from prior ones.

available_inputs.name: harvest24_supernatant
available_inputs.description: First lentiviral medium harvest collected around 24 hours post transfection

available_inputs.name: harvest48_supernatant
available_inputs.description: Second lentiviral medium harvest collected around 48 hours post transfection

available_inputs.name: conical15ml_tube
available_inputs.description: 15 mL centrifuge tube used to pool lentiviral medium

available_inputs.name: pes_filter_unit
available_inputs.description: 0.45 micrometer PES filter assembly for clarifying pooled virus

available_inputs.name: single_use_vials
available_inputs.description: Screw-top aliquot tubes intended for up to 500 microliters per vial

gold_program:
stored_first_harvest = store_sample(sample=harvest24_supernatant, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=24.0, container=conical15ml_tube)
mixed_pooled_harvest = mix_sample(sample=stored_first_harvest, method="pipetting", duration_s=5.0, speed="gentle")
held_second_harvest = cool_sample(sample=harvest48_supernatant, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=5.0, device="refrigerator")
combined_harvests = mix_sample(sample=held_second_harvest, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
cooled_combined_harvests = cool_sample(sample=combined_harvests, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=10.0, device="refrigerator")
filtered_virus = filter_sample(sample=cooled_combined_harvests, filter_type="PES", pore_size_um=0.45, destination=single_use_vials)
mixed_filtered_virus = mix_sample(sample=filtered_virus, method="inversion", duration_s=5.0, speed="gentle")
ready_use_stock = store_sample(sample=mixed_filtered_virus, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=1.0, container=single_use_vials)
frozen_single_use_aliquots = store_sample(sample=ready_use_stock, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_h=24.0, container=single_use_vials)

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: Plate 1 × 10 6 HEK-293T cells in a total volume of 1.5 mL into each well. Allow cells to attach overnight (14–18 h). Use one well of a 6 well for each lentivirus construct wished to be produced.

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: For each reaction, combine 125 μL of Opti-MEM medium with 5 μL of P3000 reagent, 1.43 μg of viral packaging DNA (psPAX2 plasmid), and 476 ng of viral envelope DNA (VSV-G is recommended due to its broad tropism, this is included in the pMD2.G plasmid). Finally, add 595 ng of the pLenti plasmid DNA containing the ecDHFR-DD fusion gene (pLenti CMV Puro plasmid, for example, as the vector backbone). Vortex for 10 seconds and incubate at room temperature (20°C–23°C) for 2 min.

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: For each reaction, in another tube combine 125 μL of Opti-MEM medium with 7.5 μL of L3000 reagent.

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: Combine the volumes from steps 5 and 6, vortex for 10 seconds, and incubate at room temperature (20°C–23°C) for 5–10 min. During this time, the transfection complex forms. This is your transfection reagent.

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: Gently pipette the 250 μL of transfection reagent into the existing media in the well containing the HEK-293T cells which were seeded the day before. Rock the plate gently to mix.

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: 6 h post transfection, aspirate the existing media and add 2 mL of fresh media. Continue incubation overnight (14–18 h).

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: 24 h post transfection, the media in the well will contain virus. Collect this media while utilizing the proper safety equipment and technique into a 15 mL conical tube. Store this media at 4°C and add 2 mL of fresh, warm media to the well.

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: 48 h post transfection, collect the media once again and add to the 15 mL conical tube described in step #10. Bleach and discard the 6-well plate.

source_steps.stage: Lentivirus Production of the ecDHFR-DD-POI Construct
source_steps.description: Filter the collected media through a 0.45 μm PES filter and aliquot into single use screw top tubes. We recommend using up to 500 μL per vial. The lentivirus can be used immediately or frozen at −80°C for later use. This is an appropriate pause point, if necessary.

source_steps.stage: Generate a Stable Cell Line Expressing the ecDHFR-DD-POI
source_steps.description: Plate target cells (e.g., ARPE-19) to be infected with lentivirus at a density of 1 × 10 5 cells/well of a 12 well (or scale accordingly). Allow to attach overnight (14–18 h).

source_steps.stage: Generate a Stable Cell Line Expressing the ecDHFR-DD-POI
source_steps.description: Dilute polybrene into full DMEM media to reach a final concentration of 1–10 μg/mL. Make enough media to cover cells in their appropriate dish. For ARPE-19 cells, we use 1 μg/mL polybrene.

source_steps.stage: Generate a Stable Cell Line Expressing the ecDHFR-DD-POI
source_steps.description: Thaw a single lentivirus aliquot at room temperature (20°C–23°C). For each well to be infected, transfer 50–100 μL into the appropriate amount of polybrene-containing media.

background: This task covers the local EV precipitation recovery stage after a blood-derived sample has already been prepared for NTI-EXO isolation. At this point, the work consists of a cold precipitation incubation, low-speed pelleting of the precipitated vesicles, a PBS wash to reduce contaminants, and final recovery of a clean EV suspension. The main technical risk is losing the pellet during supernatant removal or failing to fully rehydrate a compact pellet before downstream use.
goal: Recover a washed and resuspended extracellular vesicle preparation from the prepared precipitation sample, including the cold rehydration and aggregate-clearing operations needed when the pellet does not fully dissolve after initial mixing.
constraints: Keep the precipitation incubation and both 750 x g spins at 4 C, and use a 30 min cold incubation under a gentle rotating or rocking condition.
Use one low-speed centrifugation in the 5-10 min range at 750 x g to form the EV pellet, then a second 750 x g centrifugation for 5 min after the PBS wash.
Model a wash with 500 uL sterile PBS that covers the pellet with gentle mixing rather than forceful resuspension, followed by a final resuspension in 200 uL sterile PBS.
Use gentle but thorough vortexing for 10-15 s during resuspension, and if clumps remain, include a 12-16 h hold at 4 C before vortexing again for 10-15 s.
After overnight rehydration, clear residual large aggregates by centrifuging at 2000 x g for 5 min, with the final product represented as the clarified EV-containing suspension.
Use only the provided action-pool functions, keep the solution at protocol abstraction level, and build a dependency chain where later outputs come from earlier processed samples.

available_inputs.name: nti_exo_precip_sample
available_inputs.description: Microcentrifuge tube containing the sample already combined with NTI-EXO precipitation reagent.

available_inputs.name: pbs_wash_500ul
available_inputs.description: Sterile PBS aliquot reserved for the 500 uL pellet wash.

available_inputs.name: pbs_resuspend_200ul
available_inputs.description: Sterile PBS aliquot reserved for the 200 uL final EV resuspension.

available_inputs.name: ev_tube_1_5ml
available_inputs.description: 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube format used for the EV isolation sample.

gold_program:
incubated_precip = incubate_sample(sample=nti_exo_precip_sample, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=30.0, condition='gentle rotation')
first_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=incubated_precip, speed_xg=750, duration_min=7.5, temperature_c=4.0)
washed_pellet = mix_sample(sample=first_pellet, method='inversion', duration_s=8.0, speed='gentle PBS wash 500 uL')
second_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_pellet, speed_xg=750, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
resuspended_evs = vortex_mix(sample=second_pellet, duration_s=12.0, speed_rpm=1200, temperature_c=4.0)
rehydrated_evs = store_sample(sample=resuspended_evs, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=14.0, container='microcentrifuge_tube_1_5ml')
revortex_evs = vortex_mix(sample=rehydrated_evs, duration_s=12.0, speed_rpm=1200, temperature_c=4.0)
clarified_evs = centrifuge_sample(sample=revortex_evs, speed_xg=2000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
protocol_title: A Rapid and High-Recovery Extracellular Vesicle (EVs) Isolation Technique from Blood Samples

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Place the tubes on a tube rotator and incubate at 4 °C for 30 min. Ensure continuous gentle rotation to facilitate EV precipitation.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the tubes at 750 RCF for 5–10 min at 4 °C. A visible pellet containing precipitated EVs should form at the bottom of the tube.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Gently aspirate and discard the supernatant without disturbing the EV pellet. This is a critical step; improper aspiration can lead to significant EV loss. One technique is to tilt the tube and aspirate away from the pellet.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Add 500 μL of sterile PBS to gently wash the EV pellet. Do not resuspend the pellet at this stage. Instead, gently flick the tube to ensure the PBS covers the pellet, or gently invert a few times. The wash step helps remove co-precipitated proteins and reagents.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Centrifuge again at 750 RCF for 5 min at 4 °C.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Carefully aspirate and discard the wash supernatant.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Add 200 μL of sterile PBS to the washed EV pellet. Vortex gently but thoroughly for 10–15 s to resuspend the EVs.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: If the pellet does not fully solubilize after vortexing (a visible clump remains), cap the tube and place it at 4 °C overnight (12–16 h). This allows the pellet to rehydrate and dissolve slowly.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: After overnight incubation, vortex again (10–15 s) and centrifuge any remaining large aggregates (2,000 RCF for 5 min) to obtain a clear EV suspension. The supernatant contains the isolated EVs.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: In a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube, combine 100 μL of precleared serum.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Add 5 μL of 1 M sodium acetate, pH 4.5.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Add 60 μL of 5 M NaCl.

background: Extracellular vesicles have been precipitated from a small blood-derived sample and are now ready for recovery and cleanup in a microcentrifuge tube. The remaining work is to collect the EV pellet under refrigerated centrifugation, perform a cold PBS wash that removes residual precipitation contaminants while keeping the pellet largely intact, then resuspend the cleaned material. If the pellet is still not fully dissolved after resuspension, the sample should undergo a cold rehydration hold and a final clarification step to yield a clean EV-containing suspension.
goal: Complete the post-precipitation EV cleanup workflow to obtain a clear extracellular vesicle suspension suitable for downstream assays.
constraints: Keep centrifugation steps at 4 °C throughout this stage.
Use one pelleting spin at 750 x g for a duration in the 5-10 min range, then a second wash spin at 750 x g for 5 min.
Represent the 500 μL sterile PBS wash as a gentle coating or rinse of the pellet rather than an immediate full resuspension.
Before the final resuspension, preserve the notion that the supernatant has been removed and the pellet retained after each centrifugation.
Use 200 μL sterile PBS for the final resuspension and include vortexing for 10-15 s.
Include the contingency path in which a visible clump remains and the sample is held at 4 °C overnight for 12-16 h.
After the overnight hold, perform another 10-15 s vortex and then clarify remaining aggregates at 2,000 x g for 5 min.
Use a continuous dependency chain from the same starting sample, with later actions consuming outputs from earlier actions.

gold_program:
cold_equilibrated_sample = cool_sample(sample=precipitated_ev_tube, temperature_c=4, duration_min=2, device='refrigerator')
pelleted_ev_tube = centrifuge_sample(sample=cold_equilibrated_sample, speed_xg=750, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
pellet_after_supernatant_removal = mix_sample(sample=pelleted_ev_tube, method='pipetting', duration_s=2, speed='minimal')
pbs_washed_pellet = mix_sample(sample=pellet_after_supernatant_removal, method='inversion', duration_s=5, speed='gentle')
rewashed_ev_tube = centrifuge_sample(sample=pbs_washed_pellet, speed_xg=750, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
washed_pellet_ready = mix_sample(sample=rewashed_ev_tube, method='pipetting', duration_s=2, speed='minimal')
resuspended_ev_tube = vortex_mix(sample=washed_pellet_ready, duration_s=15, speed_rpm=1200, temperature_c=4)
overnight_rehydrated_ev = incubate_sample(sample=resuspended_ev_tube, temperature_c=4, duration_min=720, condition='static')
revortexed_ev = vortex_mix(sample=overnight_rehydrated_ev, duration_s=15, speed_rpm=1200, temperature_c=4)
clarified_ev_suspension = centrifuge_sample(sample=revortexed_ev, speed_xg=2000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the tubes at 750 RCF for 5–10 min at 4 °C. A visible pellet containing precipitated EVs should form at the bottom of the tube.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Gently aspirate and discard the supernatant without disturbing the EV pellet. This is a critical step; improper aspiration can lead to significant EV loss. One technique is to tilt the tube and aspirate away from the pellet.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Add 500 μL of sterile PBS to gently wash the EV pellet. Do not resuspend the pellet at this stage. Instead, gently flick the tube to ensure the PBS covers the pellet, or gently invert a few times. The wash step helps remove co-precipitated proteins and reagents.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Centrifuge again at 750 RCF for 5 min at 4 °C.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Carefully aspirate and discard the wash supernatant.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Add 200 μL of sterile PBS to the washed EV pellet. Vortex gently but thoroughly for 10–15 s to resuspend the EVs.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: If the pellet does not fully solubilize after vortexing (a visible clump remains), cap the tube and place it at 4 °C overnight (12–16 h). This allows the pellet to rehydrate and dissolve slowly.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: After overnight incubation, vortex again (10–15 s) and centrifuge any remaining large aggregates (2,000 RCF for 5 min) to obtain a clear EV suspension. The supernatant contains the isolated EVs.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: In a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube, combine 100 μL of precleared serum.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Add 5 μL of 1 M sodium acetate, pH 4.5.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Add 60 μL of 5 M NaCl.

source_steps.stage: E. EV isolation using NTI-EXO precipitation
source_steps.description: Add 35 μL of PBS and 200 μL of NTI-EXO reagent.

background: Differentiated and undifferentiated HL-60 cells are at the ROS assay preparation stage for H2DCFDA-based flow cytometry. In this local segment, the cells need stimulant exposure, collection into flow tubes, pelleting and resuspension in FACS buffer, probe loading under controlled incubator conditions, and a final wash before immediate analysis.
goal: Execute the H2DCFDA staining subworkflow for HL-60 cells from PMA stimulation through the final wash so the samples are ready for downstream flow-cytometry acquisition.
constraints: Model PMA stimulation as a humidified incubation at 37 C with 5% CO2 for 60 min, corresponding to a final PMA concentration of 10 nM in the assay context.
After stimulation, represent transfer of about 6e5 to 1e6 cells into a flow-cytometry tube, then pellet cells by centrifugation at 390 x g for 5 min at 18 C.

Before dye loading, the pelleted cells must be resuspended in 100 ul FACS buffer; the stained sample receives H2DCFDA to a final concentration of 0.2 mM, while the unstained control remains in FACS buffer only.
Model dye loading as a second humidified incubation at 37 C with 5% CO2 for 30 min, using the output of the resuspension step rather than restarting from a raw input.
After dye loading, perform a wash by bringing the tube volume to roughly 300-400 ul FACS buffer and centrifuging again at 390 x g for 5 min at 18 C.
Use only provided action-pool functions, assign every call to a variable, and build one dependency chain with 8-12 top-level steps.
Reuse at least two action names more than once, with at least one repeated action family differing only by one or two critical numeric arguments or conditions.

available_inputs.name: hl60_seeded_well
available_inputs.description: Seeded differentiated or undifferentiated HL-60 cells at assay density in RPMI with 5% FCS and 1% P/S.

available_inputs.name: flow_tube_cells
available_inputs.description: Aliquot transferred from a stimulated well into a flow-cytometry tube, representing about 6e5 to 1e6 cells.

available_inputs.name: facs_buffer_tube
available_inputs.description: Flow-cytometry tube containing pelleted HL-60 cells resuspended in FACS buffer.

available_inputs.name: h2dcfda_loaded_tube
available_inputs.description: HL-60 cell tube after addition of H2DCFDA to the target final concentration.

gold_program:
step1 = incubate_sample(sample=hl60_seeded_well, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=60.0, condition='humidified with 5% CO2')
step2 = mix_sample(sample=step1, method='pipetting', duration_s=8.0, speed='gentle')
step3 = mix_sample(sample=flow_tube_cells, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
step4 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step3, speed_xg=390, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=18.0)
step5 = mix_sample(sample=facs_buffer_tube, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
step6 = mix_sample(sample=h2dcfda_loaded_tube, method='pipetting', duration_s=8.0, speed='gentle')
step7 = incubate_sample(sample=step6, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=30.0, condition='humidified with 5% CO2')
step8 = mix_sample(sample=step7, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
step9 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step8, speed_xg=390, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=18.0)
protocol_title: Protocol for the differentiation of HL-60 cells into a neutrophil-like state

source_steps.stage: Cell staining for flow cytometry analysis of surface markers
source_steps.description: Stain the cells with the appropriate volume of antibodies and isotype controls (titration of BioLegend antibodies is not necessary, add 5 μl antibody/isotype control per 100 μl total volume as recommended by the manufacturer). Incubate the cells for unstained control with 100 μl FACS buffer only.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining for flow cytometry analysis of surface markers
source_steps.description: Incubate the cells in the dark for 30 min at 4°C or on ice water.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining for flow cytometry analysis of surface markers
source_steps.description: Wash the cells. Add ca. 300-400 μl FACS buffer to each tube and centrifuge the tubes for 5 min at 390x g at 18°C.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining for flow cytometry analysis of surface markers
source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet in 500 μl/tube FACS buffer. Pipet viability dye DAPI (final concentration 2 μg/ml), vortex and incubate for 10 min at 20°C–25°C.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining for flow cytometry analysis of surface markers
source_steps.description: Analyze the samples by flow cytometry immediately.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining with H 2 DCFDA for measurement of reactive oxygen species
source_steps.description: Seed un- and differentiated neutrophil-like HL-60 cells in a 48-well plate. Optimal density for treatment is 2∗10 6 HL-60 cells per ml. For H 2 DCFDA assay it is sufficient to seed the cells with a density 1∗10 6 cells per 0.5 ml cell culture medium (RPMI + 5% FCS + 1% P/S).

source_steps.stage: Cell staining with H 2 DCFDA for measurement of reactive oxygen species
source_steps.description: Treat the cells with PMA solution in a final concentration of 10 nM for 1 h in a humidified incubator at 37°C with 5% CO 2 .

source_steps.stage: Cell staining with H 2 DCFDA for measurement of reactive oxygen species
source_steps.description: After 1 h incubation pellet the cells. Add 6-10∗10 5 cells per flow cytometry tube from each well.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining with H 2 DCFDA for measurement of reactive oxygen species
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the flow cytometry tubes for 5 min at 390x g at 18°C.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining with H 2 DCFDA for measurement of reactive oxygen species
source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet in 100 μl/tube FACS buffer and add H 2 DCFDA solution in a final concentration of 0.2 mM. Incubate the sample for unstained control in 100 μl of FACS buffer only.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining with H 2 DCFDA for measurement of reactive oxygen species
source_steps.description: Incubate the cells for 30 min in a humidified incubator at 37°C with 5% CO 2 .

source_steps.stage: Cell staining with H 2 DCFDA for measurement of reactive oxygen species
source_steps.description: Wash the cells. Add ca. 300-400 μl FACS buffer to each tube and centrifuge the tubes for 5 min at 390x g and at 18°C.

background: A 3D epiblast stem cell culture is being passaged after spheroids were collected into a microcentrifuge tube. The current stage is the local passaging workflow that uses repeated pelleting, enzymatic dissociation, washing, and reformulation in chilled Matrigel so the cells can be replated as small drops and briefly allowed to set.
goal: Process the collected 3D EpiSC suspension through the core passaging subtask: dissociate, wash, recover a final pellet, prepare a cold Matrigel replating mixture, and complete the short setting incubation for plated drops.
constraints: Use refrigerated centrifugation every time a pellet is formed: 300 x g for 4 minutes at 4 degrees Celsius, with this pelleting action repeated multiple times.
Represent TrypLE treatment with gentle pipette-style mixing corresponding to 10 up-and-down strokes and a 4 minute incubation at 37 degrees Celsius before the next centrifugation.
Include a wash-buffer resuspension with another gentle pipette-style mixing event corresponding to 10 up-and-down strokes, followed by a further centrifugation under the same refrigerated settings.

Keep a single dependency chain in which later processed states consume earlier outputs, and have the cold Matrigel preparation feed into the final replating mixture rather than appearing as an unrelated branch.
Represent Matrigel as ice-cold before use and reflect the example plating amounts in the task context: 25 uL per drop and 100 uL total Matrigel for 4 drops.
Use gentle mixing for the final cell-Matrigel combination to reflect bubble avoidance, and end with a 10 minute incubation at incubator temperature for plated drops to solidify.

available_inputs.name: cell_suspension
available_inputs.description: Collected 3D EpiSC suspension in a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube, ready for passaging.

available_inputs.name: tryple_reagent
available_inputs.description: TrypLE reagent used for enzymatic dissociation of the pelleted cells.

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer
available_inputs.description: Wash buffer used to rinse the post-dissociation pellet.

available_inputs.name: matrigel_stock
available_inputs.description: 100 percent Matrigel that should be kept ice-cold before use for replating.

available_inputs.name: plated_drops
available_inputs.description: Dispensed 25 uL drops of the final cell-Matrigel mixture in the culture plate.

gold_program:
pellet_after_collection = centrifuge_sample(sample=cell_suspension, speed_xg=300, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
tryple_cell_mix = mix_sample(sample=pellet_after_collection, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
dissociated_cells = incubate_sample(sample=tryple_cell_mix, temperature_c=37, duration_min=4, condition="static")
post_tryple_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=dissociated_cells, speed_xg=300, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
washed_cells = mix_sample(sample=post_tryple_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
final_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_cells, speed_xg=300, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
loosened_pellet = mix_sample(sample=final_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=5, speed="gentle")
chilled_matrigel = cool_sample(sample=matrigel_stock, temperature_c=0, duration_min=1, device="ice")
cell_matrigel_mix = mix_sample(sample=loosened_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
plating_ready_mix = mix_sample(sample=cell_matrigel_mix, method="pipetting", duration_s=5, speed="gentle")
solidified_plated_drops = incubate_sample(sample=plated_drops, temperature_c=37, duration_min=10, condition="static")
protocol_title: Protocol for generating a 3D culture of epiblast stem cells

source_steps.description: Collect the cell suspension in a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube and centrifuge it at 300 × g , 4°C for 4 min.
Carefully remove the supernatant, add 1 mL of TrypLE, pipette it up and down 10 times and incubate at 37°C for 4 min.
Centrifuge the cells at 300 × g , 4°C for 4 min.
After centrifugation, carefully remove the supernatant, add 1 mL of wash buffer and pipette up and down 10 times to wash the pellet containing small clumps of cells.
Centrifuge the cells at 300 × g , 4°C for 4 min.
Carefully remove the supernatant and loosen up the pellet by rubbing the Eppendorf tube against the grid of the tissue culture hood. With a pipette, check the final volume of the dissolved pellet.
Transfer to a new tube the volume needed for plating (based on the splitting ratio and the number of drops required).

Add the volume of 100% ice-cold Matrigel needed for the number of drops required using 25 μL per drop. For example, if you require 4 drops, add 100 μL of Matrigel, pipette up and down avoiding bubbles, and plate 25 μL per drop (maximum 2 drops per well in a 24-well plate). See Figure 2 A.
Place the plate in the incubator for 10 min to allow the Matrigel to solidify and add 600 μL of 3D FAXN medium per well.

Freezing down 3D EpiSCs. Note: Before starting, cool down a microcentrifuge to 4°C. To dissociate the 3D EpiSCs, follow the steps described in the section “ passaging 3D EpiSCs ”, from steps 16 to 22.

After washing the pellet with wash buffer and centrifugating it, remove the supernatant and resuspend the cells in the freezing medium containing 500 μL of 3D FAXN medium, 400 μL of FBS and 100 μL of DMSO. Add 10 μM of Rock inhibitor in the freezing medium.
Transfer the cells to a cryotube and place it in −80°C for up to one week before transferring the cryotubes to the liquid nitrogen storage container.

background: Primary hippocampal neuron lysates have already undergone click labeling and NeutrAvidin capture. At this stage, the bead-bound material must be cleaned with the specified wash buffers and then released with hydroxylamine-containing elution buffer while preserving low-yield protein recovery and the room-temperature elution condition.
goal: Process the NeutrAvidin bead sample through the pull-down cleanup and elution stage to obtain the released S-acylated protein fraction for downstream analysis.
constraints: Begin from a bead-containing pull-down sample that requires pelleting in a benchtop centrifuge at 700 x g for 3 min at 4 C.
Washes with the high-salt wash buffer must occur three times total, whereas the lower-salt wash buffer is used once after those washes.
A brief pellet-forming spin after transfer or resuspension should use 8000 x g rather than the initial 700 x g condition.
Elution uses exactly 40 uL of elution buffer per sample.
The elution incubation must last 60 min at room temperature, normalized to 22 C, with substantial mixing; inversion should not be used.
If elution buffer has precipitated, it should be warmed to 37 C for 1-2 min and gently mixed before use; do not keep this buffer on ice.
After the elution incubation, beads should be pelleted again before collecting the supernatant as the eluate.
Use only operations represented by the provided action pool; represent buffer addition or resuspension through an allowed mixing-style action rather than unsupported liquid-handling primitives.

available_inputs.description: NeutrAvidin bead suspension containing captured labeled proteins

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer_1
available_inputs.description: High-salt wash buffer used for three washes

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer_2
available_inputs.description: Secondary wash buffer used for one final wash

available_inputs.description: Hydroxylamine-compatible elution buffer supplemented for protein recovery

gold_program:
pelleted_beads = centrifuge_sample(sample=bead_sample, speed_xg=700, duration_min=3, temperature_c=4)
wash1_resuspended = mix_sample(sample=pelleted_beads, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
wash1_spin_a = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash1_resuspended, speed_xg=8000, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=4)
wash1_resuspended_b = mix_sample(sample=wash1_spin_a, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
wash1_spin_b = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash1_resuspended_b, speed_xg=8000, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=4)
wash1_resuspended_c = mix_sample(sample=wash1_spin_b, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
wash1_spin_c = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash1_resuspended_c, speed_xg=8000, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=4)
wash2_resuspended = mix_sample(sample=wash1_spin_c, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
wash2_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash2_resuspended, speed_xg=8000, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=4)
elution_mixed = mix_sample(sample=wash2_spin, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
eluted_beads = set_thermal_mixer(sample=elution_mixed, temperature_c=22, speed_rpm=1000, duration_min=60)
final_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=eluted_beads, speed_xg=8000, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=22)
protocol_title: Protocol to identify S-acylated proteins in hippocampal neurons using ω-alkynyl fatty acid analogs and click chemistry

source_steps.description: Prepare wash and elution buffers according to table below during pull-down. CRITICAL: Do not place elution buffer on ice as precipitation of buffer may occur. If precipitated, incubate at 37°C for 1–2 min and gently vortex before use. Buffer Components Volume required Wash Buffer #1 Dilution buffer with 0.5 M NaCl, 1× Leu, 1× Bz 3 washes x 1 mL/sample Wash Buffer #2 Dilution buffer with 1× Leu, 1× Bz 1 wash x 1 mL/sample Elution Buffer Elution buffer (as described in buffer list above) with PIC 40 μL/sample
Elute purified S-acylated proteins. Pellet beads by centrifugation for 3 min, 700 × g at 4°C in a benchtop centrifuge.
Aspirate supernatant.
Add 1 mL of Wash Buffer #1 to each sample and transfer to a fresh 1.5 mL centrifuge tube.
Pulse-spin beads (8000 × g ) and aspirate supernatant.
Wash pellet twice more with Wash Buffer #1.
Wash 1× times with Wash Buffer #2.
Remove all residual wash buffer with a gel loading tip.
Add 40 μL Elution Buffer.
Incubate for 1 h at 21°C–24°C with plenty of mixing. Note: Do not invert samples. To improve mixing, we suggest using a shaking dry bath set to 1000 rpm.
Quick spin to pellet beads

Transfer supernatant to a fresh tube using gel loading tip Optional: If protein concentration is very low, resuspend the beads and transfer them to a 0.22-micron SpinX column and spin at 7800 × g for 5 min to collect the entire supernatant. Optional: Low-binding microcentrifuge tubes can also be used to collect the eluent if the expected yield is low.

background: This experiment is at the transition from chromatin shearing quality control into immunoprecipitation setup for mouse liver ChIP. A small sheared chromatin aliquot is processed to reverse crosslinks and evaluate fragment quality and DNA yield, while the remaining sheared chromatin is diluted under protease-protected IP conditions, an input fraction is reserved frozen, antibodies are added, and immune complexes are allowed to form before bead capture.
goal: Process a sheared chromatin QC aliquot through reverse-crosslinking and documentation of DNA quality, then prepare diluted chromatin reactions for antibody binding, preserve the input control, and carry the antibody-bound samples into the pre-bead incubation stage and initial bead binding.
constraints: Use the stored 50 uL sheared chromatin QC aliquot, expand it to 200 uL with 1x elution buffer, then perform reverse crosslinking with 8 uL of 5 M NaCl at 95 C for 15 min and cool to room temperature before QC readout.

Document two QC outcomes from the purified DNA recovered in 50 uL elution buffer: fragment-size assessment based on a 10 uL agarose-gel sample and DNA concentration assessment based on a 2 uL NanoDrop sample, with expected concentration 50-90 ng/uL.
For immunoprecipitation setup, each reaction starts from 30 ug original sheared chromatin and is diluted to 1 mL using IP buffer containing 1x protease inhibitor, while maintaining SDS below 0.1%.
A 50 uL portion of the diluted chromatin must be reserved as the 5% Input and stored at -80 C for later use.
Add 2 ug antibody per 1 mL reaction for both target-specific and IgG control conditions, then incubate overnight at 4 C with rotation to form antibody-chromatin complexes.
Before capture, gently vortex Protein G magnetic beads, use 30 uL beads per IP reaction, and incubate bead binding for 2 h at 4 C with rotation.

available_inputs.name: qc_sheared_chromatin
available_inputs.description: Stored 50 uL aliquot of sheared chromatin reserved for shearing quality control.

available_inputs.name: ip_sheared_chromatin
available_inputs.description: Original sheared chromatin sample used to set up immunoprecipitation reactions.

available_inputs.name: chip_elution_buffer
available_inputs.description: 1x ChIP elution buffer used to dilute the QC aliquot and recover purified DNA.

available_inputs.name: ip_buffer_with_pi
available_inputs.description: IP buffer containing 1x protease inhibitor for dilution of sheared chromatin prior to antibody incubation.

available_inputs.name: target_antibody
available_inputs.description: Target-specific antibody for the ChIP reaction.

available_inputs.name: igg_control
available_inputs.description: IgG negative-control antibody.

available_inputs.name: protein_g_beads
available_inputs.description: ChIP-grade Protein G magnetic beads for capturing antibody-chromatin complexes.

gold_program:
qc_thawed = cool_sample(sample=qc_sheared_chromatin, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=10.0, device="bench")
qc_prepared = mix_sample(sample=qc_thawed, method="pipetting", duration_s=20.0, speed="moderate")
qc_heated = heat_sample(sample=qc_prepared, temperature_c=95.0, duration_min=15.0, device="heat block")
qc_cooled = cool_sample(sample=qc_heated, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=10.0, device="bench")
qc_conc_record = record_measurement(sample=qc_cooled, metric="DNA concentration", unit="ng/uL", note="Purified DNA recovered in 50 uL elution buffer; assess 2 uL by NanoDrop, expected 50-90 ng/uL, and verify fragment size from 10 uL on 1.5% agarose gel with 100 bp marker")
ip_diluted = mix_sample(sample=ip_sheared_chromatin, method="pipetting", duration_s=15.0, speed="moderate")
input_stored = store_sample(sample=ip_diluted, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_h=16.0, container="1.5 mL tube labeled 5% Input, 50 uL")
ip_with_antibody = mix_sample(sample=ip_diluted, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
ip_overnight = incubate_sample(sample=ip_with_antibody, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=960.0, condition="rotation")
beads_vortexed = vortex_mix(sample=protein_g_beads, duration_s=5.0, speed_rpm=1200, temperature_c=4.0)
bead_capture_mix = mix_sample(sample=ip_overnight, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
bead_binding = incubate_sample(sample=bead_capture_mix, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=120.0, condition="rotation")
protocol_title: Protocol for in vivo chromatin immunoprecipitation on purified chromatin isolated from mouse liver nuclei

source_steps.description: Take an aliquot of 50 μL for assessment of chromatin shearing and store at −80°C.
Thaw aliquoted chromatin from step 7, which contains 50 μL sheared chromatin and add 150 μL 1× ChIP elution buffer.
Add 8 μL 5 M NaCl to each sample, mix and then incubate at 95°C for 15 min. Afterward, cool samples to room temperature.

Purify and collect DNA in 50 μL of elution buffer. This protocol used the GeneJET PCR Purification Kit from Thermo Fisher ( https://www.thermofisher.com/document-connect/document-connect.html?url=https://assets.thermofisher.com/TFS-Assets%2FLSG%2Fmanuals%2FMAN0012662_GeneJET_PCR_Purification_UG.pdf ).
Use 10 μL sample and determine DNA fragment size by electrophoresis on a 1.5% agarose gel with a 100 base pair DNA marker ( Troubleshooting 2 ).
To determine DNA concentration, transfer 2 μL of purified DNA to NanoDrop. The concentration of DNA should be 50–90 ng/μL.

Into prechilled 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube, aliquot 30 μg of original sheared chromatin sample for each specific target if using multiple antibodies, plus a positive control if available and the negative control (IgG or Mock-IP).

Dilute samples from step 13 to final volume of 1 mL by adding proper amount of IP buffer containing 1× protease inhibitor (e.g., add 800 μL IP buffer to 200 μL chromatin sample), make sure SDS concentration is less than 0.1% after dilution.
Aliquot 50 μL of diluted chromatin into a new 1.5 mL tube and label as “5% Input”. Store sample at −80°C until used in step 21.
Add 2 μg target-specific antibody to sample, and 2 μg IgG sample for the negative control. Thus, the antibody concentration is approximately 2 μg/mL.
Incubate IP samples overnight at 4°C with rotation.

source_steps.stage: Chromatin immunoprecipitation—Adding beads, elute, and reverse crosslinking
source_steps.description: Gently vortex ChIP-Grade Protein G magnetic beads, add 30 μL of Protein G magnetic beads to each IP reaction and incubate for 2 h, at 4°C with rotation.

background: A dual-gRNA plasmid library has already been assembled and transformed, and the workflow is now focused on post-transformation recovery, bacterial expansion on selective agar, and obtaining pooled plasmid material for downstream quality control or the second gRNA insertion stage. The current segment emphasizes preserving lentiviral library integrity during outgrowth and plate incubation while maintaining sufficient colony representation for library coverage.
goal: Recover the transformed bacterial library, expand it under ampicillin selection with conditions that reduce recombination, and generate a pooled colony harvest suitable for plasmid preparation and short-term holding.
constraints: The transformed culture should be recovered in bacterial medium with an added 1 mL recovery or SOC medium and incubated for 1 hour at 37 C with shaking.
A dilution-derived plating branch is needed for coverage estimation, but the main pooled library branch must remain available for large-plate expansion.
Selective agar conditions must be ampicillin-based, and the large library plating condition corresponds to 0.5 mL culture per 15 cm plate.
Large-plate incubation should be approximately 18 hours at 30 C to minimize lentiviral plasmid recombination rather than at 37 C.
Colony harvest should use two sequential LB plus ampicillin washes of 6 mL each from the same plate to maximize pooled recovery.
The harvested pooled colony suspension should remain a single downstream sample for plasmid preparation or temporary storage.
Use only the provided abstract lab actions; represent plating-related handling at a coarse procedural level rather than detailed manual colony counting or scraping motions.
Keep the workflow centered on the recovery and amplification stage rather than jumping ahead to lentiviral transfection.

mixed_recovery = mix_sample(sample=transformed_cells_mix, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
shaken_recovery = incubate_sample(sample=mixed_recovery, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=60.0, condition="shaking")
titer_plate_handled = shake_container(sample=ampicillin_lb_agar_10cm_plate, amplitude_mm=5.0, repeat_n=10, duration_s=5.0)
library_plate_loaded = shake_container(sample=ampicillin_lb_agar_15cm_plate, amplitude_mm=5.0, repeat_n=10, duration_s=5.0)
library_plate_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=library_plate_loaded, temperature_c=30.0, duration_min=1080.0, condition="static")
first_wash_mixed = mix_sample(sample=lb_ampicillin_wash_6ml_a, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="medium")
first_harvest = shake_container(sample=first_wash_mixed, amplitude_mm=8.0, repeat_n=12, duration_s=15.0)
second_wash_mixed = mix_sample(sample=lb_ampicillin_wash_6ml_b, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="medium")
second_harvest = shake_container(sample=second_wash_mixed, amplitude_mm=8.0, repeat_n=12, duration_s=15.0)
pooled_colony_harvest = mix_sample(sample=second_harvest, method="pipetting", duration_s=15.0, speed="gentle")
stored_pooled_library = store_sample(sample=pooled_colony_harvest, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=2.0, container="bacterial culture tube")

source_steps.description: Transfer cells mixture to a bacterial culture tube with additional 1 mL recovery medium (or SOC medium). Shake the tubes for 1 h at 37°C in a shaking incubator.

Titer the library: Set up dilutions for counting colonies: set up serial dilution (1:10, 1:100, 1: 1000,…) of the culture and plate onto 10 cm ampicillin LB agar plate. Count the colonies next morning to calculate the library coverage. The diluted plate is used to calculate the total colony number to ensure library coverage.

Plate the library: Spread the original culture onto pre-warmed 15 cm ampicillin LB agar plate (0.5 mL/plate), incubate at 30°C for approximate 18 h. CRITICAL: 30°C incubation is intended to minimize recombination of the lentiviral plasmid library.

Harvest colonies and maxi-prep: Transfer 6 mL of LB+ ampicillin medium on one 15 cm plate, scrape the colonies off with a cell spreader. Then use another 6 mL of LB+ ampicillin medium to wash the plate. Collect all colonies and proceed to max-prep according to QIAGEN Plasmid Kit procedures. Optional: Sanger Sequencing. To ensure the success of gRNA pool’s insertion, pick several colonies from the plate (2d or 2e) to 4 mL LB culture with ampicillin. Shake the tubes for at least 8 h at 37°C. Use QIAGEN Mini Prep Kit to extract the plasmid and send it to Sanger Sequencing with primer of PCR1_F.

(Day 2–3) Insert second set of gRNAs: The process is similar to step 2 of inserting first set of gRNA pools. The only difference is the enzyme digestion setting. Vector linearization: assemble the following reaction on ice and incubate at 55°C for 1 h. Enzyme digestion reaction master mix Reagent Amount Vector: LentiGuide_DKO+1 st gRNA 2 μg BsmbI_v2 1 μL 10× N NEBuffer r3.1 5 μL ddH 2 O To 50 μL Then run out the sample on an EZ-VISION Blue-Light DNA Dye -stained 1% agarose gel. Extract linearized DNA using QIAGEN Gel Extraction Kit, elute with 30 μL ddH 2 O. PCR purification kit could be used on the enzyme digestion product to save time.

Repeat HiFi assembling as steps 2b to 2f. Harvest colonies and max prep are the same as step2. Note: Step 3 usually requires much more reactions to reach appropriate library coverage, considering the combination of 2 sgRNAs. For example, for two sets of gRNA pools with 250 different gRNAs, there will be 62,500 combinations. To reach 10 coverages for each gRNA-gRNA construction, 20 electroporate reactions (62500∗10/30,000) are needed.

Library QC: Directly proceed to steps 20–24 and data analysis part. Sequencing the whole library at least 50× coverage (refer to step 23 for how to choose sequencing service) for the QC purpose. Pay attention to mapping ratio, number/percentage of missed gRNA pairs, the distribution and evenness of the gRNA-gRNA constructions.

Library amplification: depending on the experiment designs, such as cell coverage and number of replicates, the amount of library generated from steps 1–3 might be insufficient. In this case, amplify the pooled library by electroporation as described in steps 2c–2e. Instead of using 1 μL of HiFi reaction product, dilute the pooled library to 50 ng/μL and then use 2 μL 50 ng/μL pooled library for each reaction.

Day 1. Seed 8–9 × 10 6 /15 cm plate 293T packaging cells (recommended: passage number < 15) in 20 mL none-antibiotic growth media. For 1 L virus production prepare ∼60 plates. Incubate cells for 24 h (37°C, 5% CO 2 ), or until the following afternoon. The cells should be ∼70% confluent at the moment of transfection.

Day 2 afternoon. Transfect 293T cells: Prepare the mixture of 3 transfection plasmid (∼1:1:1 molar ratio) in OptiMEM: Transfection plasmid mix (per 15 cm plate) Plasmid Mass Amount per plate Total amount psPAX2 6.5 μg per plate pMD2.G 3.6 μg per plate Library 5.4 μg per plate OptiMEM – To 200 μL

For each plate, prepare one 1× OptiMEM and X-tremeGENE transfection reagent (i.e., 60 tubes OptiMEM and X-tremeGENE mix will be needed for 60 plates): directly add 48 μL X-tremeGENE into the tubes with 600 μL OptiMEM, and gently mix them by flicking the tubes. CRITICAL: Do not mix by pipetting or vertex here.
After the incubation, add 200 μL OptiMEM with transfection plasmids drop-wise to each tube with OptiMEM and X-tremeGENE dilutions (1:3 ratio), then mix gently.

background: This experiment is isolating metaphase chromosomes for later flow karyotyping on a conventional flow cytometer. Cell culture and mitotic shake-off have already produced detached metaphase cells from several flasks. The current stage is the local preparation segment in which the harvested cells are pelleted, exposed to hypotonic solution to promote swelling, checked for adequate swelling, then re-pelleted and transferred into ice-cold polyamine isolation buffer for the next chromosome-preparation step.
goal: Convert the harvested metaphase-cell suspension into a swollen, re-pelleted preparation resuspended in cold polyamine isolation buffer, ready for downstream chromosome isolation.
constraints: Start from the harvested metaphase-cell suspension and stay within the immediate harvest-to-hypotonic-to-PAB preparation stage.
Include two centrifugation steps at 289 x g for 5 min at room temperature, one before hypotonic treatment and one after swelling.

Represent hypotonic resuspension at about 5 ml per tube, followed by room-temperature swelling for about 15 min, with an optional additional incubation in the 10 to 30 min range if swelling is inadequate.
Record a QC observation corresponding to checking swelling and chromosome spread during the hypotonic stage.
After the second spin, conceptually drain excess liquid, resuspend the pellet in 1 to 2 ml ice-cold PAB, and keep the sample on ice for 10 min.
Use only provided action-pool functions with keyword arguments, and make later steps depend on earlier outputs.
Use 6 to 12 top-level function calls and repeat at least two action families more than once.

available_inputs.name: hypotonic_solution
available_inputs.description: Hypotonic swelling solution used to enlarge metaphase cells before chromosome release

available_inputs.name: pab_ice_cold
available_inputs.description: Ice-cold polyamine isolation buffer for resuspending the post-swelling pellet

available_inputs.name: turcks_stain

available_inputs.name: tube_50ml

gold_program:
pelleted_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=metaphase_supernatant, speed_xg=289, duration_min=5, temperature_c=22)
drained_pellet = dry_sample(sample=pelleted_cells, method="drain on absorbent paper", temperature_c=22, duration_min=1)
hypotonic_resuspension = mix_sample(sample=drained_pellet, method="pipetting with hypotonic solution to about 5 ml per tube in 50 ml tube", duration_s=20, speed="gentle")
combined_swelling_cells = incubate_sample(sample=hypotonic_resuspension, temperature_c=22, duration_min=15, condition="static")
swelling_check = record_measurement(sample=combined_swelling_cells, metric="cell swelling QC", unit="qualitative", note="Check 5 ul cell suspension mixed with 5 ul Turck's stain for swollen metaphase cells and chromosome spread")
extended_swelling_cells = incubate_sample(sample=combined_swelling_cells, temperature_c=22, duration_min=10, condition="static")
swollen_cell_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=extended_swelling_cells, speed_xg=289, duration_min=5, temperature_c=22)
postspin_drained_pellet = dry_sample(sample=swollen_cell_pellet, method="drain on absorbent paper", temperature_c=22, duration_min=1)
pab_resuspension = mix_sample(sample=postspin_drained_pellet, method="pipetting with 1 to 2 ml ice-cold PAB", duration_s=20, speed="gentle")
chromosome_prep_ready = cool_sample(sample=pab_resuspension, temperature_c=0, duration_min=10, device="ice")

source_steps.description: Tap the bottom of the flask gently to detach all of the cells. Add 5 ml fresh culture medium and mix well to obtain a single‐cell suspension.

Subculture the cell suspension at a ratio of 1:3 in four flasks. Bring the volume to 25 ml with fresh culture medium. Incubate at 37°C in a 5% CO 2 incubator. In this example, a total of 8 flasks are used during the chromosome harvesting process. The number of flasks required to obtain a sufficient number of chromosomes (at least 5 million chromosomes per ml) will vary according to the total number of metaphase cells achieved after colcemid treatment.

After 24 hr, add colcemid to a final concentration of 0.1 µg/ml and gently swirl the flask to mix well. Incubate for 6‐16 hr. The duration of treatment with mitotic inhibitors varies according to the rate of cell growth. A high mitotic index can be achieved by optimizing treatment time for different cell types. Cells arrested at metaphase appear under the microscope as “rounded cells” loosely attached to the monolayer surface.
Gently tap the bottom of the flasks to dislodge the cells at metaphase.

Collect cells suspended in the supernatant from the four flasks (150 cm 2 ) after mitotic shake‐off and transfer them to two 50‐ml polypropylene centrifuge tubes. Centrifuge at 289 × g for 5 min at room temperature.
Remove the supernatant without disturbing the pellet. Invert the tube on absorbent paper and drain the excess medium.
Proceed to Basic Protocol 2 .

Resuspend the cell pellets from Basic Protocol 1 in hypotonic solution (∼5 ml per tube) with a Pasteur pipette. Combine the cell suspensions in a single 50‐ml polypropylene centrifuge tube and incubate at room temperature for about 15 min.

Mix 5 µl cell suspension with 5 µl Turck's stain on a microscope slide and directly monitor the cells for swelling under a light microscope. If Turck's stain is not available, use 1 mg/ml PI and view under a fluorescence microscope. Swollen cells at metaphase should display a good spread of chromosomes within the plasma membrane (see Fig. 2 ). Assess the cells for swelling and incubate for an additional 10 to 30 min if needed.
Centrifuge the swollen cells at 289 × g for 5 min at room temperature.
Remove the supernatant without disturbing the pellet. Invert the tube on absorbent paper and drain the excess medium.
Add 1 to 2 ml ice‐cold PAB to the cell pellet and gently resuspend using a Pasteur pipette. Incubate on ice for 10 min.

background: Human CD8-positive responder cells are being prepared for a Treg suppression assay. At this stage, the cells have been labeled with CD8-targeting magnetic beads under cold conditions, then passed through a magnetic column workflow and recovered as a CD8-enriched fraction. The next operations focus on pelleting the recovered cells, staining them with CFSE at a defined cell density, incubating at physiological temperature, and then quenching and washing the stain under cold conditions before coculture setup.
goal: Process the recovered CD8-enriched cells through pelleting, CFSE labeling, warm incubation, stain quenching, and cold PBS washes so the cells are ready for downstream coculture.
constraints: Begin from the magnetically recovered CD8-enriched cell suspension.
Include a centrifugation step before CFSE labeling to pellet the recovered cells.
Resuspend the cells for labeling in 1 mL PBS at a scale corresponding to 1-10×10^6 CD8-positive cells.
Use exactly 1 μL CFSE reagent for the labeling mixture and ensure the sample is mixed after reagent addition.
Perform the CFSE labeling incubation for 30 minutes at 37 degrees Celsius.
Stop the labeling reaction by adding 5 mL lymphocyte serum-free medium and cooling the sample in an ice bath for 5 minutes.
After quenching, wash the cells three times with PBS under cold conditions.
Use only action-pool functions and keep the workflow at the abstraction level of centrifugation, mixing, incubation, cooling, and generic wash handling.
Use keyword arguments only and maintain a single dependency chain from the recovered magnetic fraction to the final washed product.

gold_program:
pelleted_cd8 = centrifuge_sample(sample=cd8_recovered_cells, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
cfse_resuspension = mix_sample(sample=pelleted_cd8, method="pipetting", duration_s=15.0, speed="gentle")
cfse_labeled_mix = mix_sample(sample=cfse_resuspension, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
cfse_label_incubation = incubate_sample(sample=cfse_labeled_mix, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=30.0, condition="static")
quenched_cfse_cells = cool_sample(sample=cfse_label_incubation, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=5.0, device="ice bath")
wash1_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=quenched_cfse_cells, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
wash1_resuspended = mix_sample(sample=wash1_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=15.0, speed="gentle")
wash2_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash1_resuspended, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
wash2_resuspended = mix_sample(sample=wash2_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=15.0, speed="gentle")
wash3_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash2_resuspended, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
final_cfse_washed_cd8 = mix_sample(sample=wash3_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=15.0, speed="gentle")

source_steps.description: Resuspend cell pellet in 80 μL of PBS per 10 7 total cells. Add 20 μL of CD8 MicroBeads per 10 7 total cells. Mix well and incubate for 15 min in the refrigerator (2°C–8°C).
Wash cells by adding 1 mL–2 mL of PBS per 10 7 cells, and resuspend up to 10 8 cells in 500 μL of PBS. Place MS Column in the magnetic field of a suitable MACS Separator.
Rinse the column with 500 μL of PBS, apply cell suspension to the column, and wash the column with 500 μL of PBS. Remove the column from the separator, and place it on a suitable collection tube.
Pipette 1 mL of PBS onto the column. Push the plunger firmly into the column to flush out the magnetically labeled cells immediately.
Centrifuge cell suspension and count cells.
CFSE. Resuspend 1–10∗10 6 CD8 + cells in 1 mL PBS, then add 1μL CFSE Cell Proliferation Kit and mix well.
Incubate cells for 30 min in a 37°C Incubator.

Add 5 mL of Lymphocyte Serum-Free Medium and ice bath for 5 min to stop staining. Wash three times with PBS. Note: Ice baths need to be adequate and complete to ensure that no CFSE fuel remains ( Ni et al., 2018 ).
Tregs: CD8 + T cells= 1:2, 1:8, and the total number of cells was consistent in each group ( Figure 4 B).

Cells were cultured at 10 6 /mL with a complete medium, and add 5 μL of Human T-Activator CD3/CD28 Beads per 10 6 CD8 + T cells. Note: Excessive addition of activated magnetic beads will lead to no obvious cell inhibition.
After 72–96 h, take 0.3∗10 6 cells in 100μL FACS buffer. Add staining antibodies, 0.5 μL of CD4-FITC, 0.5 μL of CD25-Percp/Cy5.5, and 0.1 μL of LIVE/DEAD™ to each tube and vortex.
Incubate in the refrigerator (2°C–8°C) for 15 min in the dark.

background: The experiment is at the membrane-labeling stage for preparing human A2A adenosine receptor samples for 19F-NMR. Membranes have already been exposed to aldrithiol in high-salt buffer containing theophylline and kept cold with rotation. The current task is to recover those treated membranes, carry out the cold TET-labeling phase, and finish with the labeled membrane pellet ready for the next purification segment.
goal: Complete the cold pelleting and TET-labeling portion of the membrane workflow starting from the aldrithiol-treated membrane suspension, and finish with documented recovery of the final labeled membrane pellet for downstream purification.
constraints: Use ultracentrifugation at 200000 x g for 30 min at 4 C for both membrane pelleting operations in this segment.
Keep the sample at 4 C during the labeling reaction and any short interim holding steps.
The TET treatment must reflect a final concentration of 1 mM per liter of cell culture and must last 60 min.
Begin from the already aldrithiol-treated membrane suspension rather than untreated membranes or nanodisc material.
After the first centrifugation, proceed with the membrane pellet and not the supernatant.
After the second centrifugation, the retained product must be the labeled membrane pellet intended for downstream purification.
Include at least one explicit recovered-fraction handling step tied to the pellet state.
Document completion of the labeling stage as a recorded result associated with the final processed material.

available_inputs.name: aldrithiol_membranes
available_inputs.description: Membrane suspension already treated with aldrithiol and protease inhibitor in A2A AR high-salt buffer with theophylline.

available_inputs.name: tet_reagent
available_inputs.description: Trifluoroethanethiol reagent available for addition to reach 1 mM final concentration.

available_inputs.name: high_salt_buffer
available_inputs.description: A2A AR high-salt buffer used when resuspending the membrane pellet.

available_inputs.name: cell_culture_scale_l
available_inputs.description: Cell culture volume in liters used to normalize the 1 mM per liter TET dosing requirement.

gold_program:
first_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=aldrithiol_membranes, speed_xg=200000, duration_min=30, temperature_c=4)
first_pellet = collect_fraction(sample=first_spin, phase="pellet", destination="fresh_tube", volume_ul=1000)
first_pellet_cold = cool_sample(sample=first_pellet, temperature_c=4, duration_min=5, device="refrigerator")
resuspended_pellet = incubate_sample(sample=first_pellet_cold, temperature_c=4, duration_min=5, condition="resuspended in 100 mL high_salt_buffer with TET at 1 mM per liter of cell culture")
tet_labeled_membranes = incubate_sample(sample=resuspended_pellet, temperature_c=4, duration_min=60, condition="static")
post_label_cold = cool_sample(sample=tet_labeled_membranes, temperature_c=4, duration_min=5, device="refrigerator")
second_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=post_label_cold, speed_xg=200000, duration_min=30, temperature_c=4)
final_pellet = collect_fraction(sample=second_spin, phase="pellet", destination="purification_tube", volume_ul=1000)
final_pellet_hold = store_sample(sample=final_pellet, temperature_c=4, duration_h=1, container="tube")
labeling_record = record_measurement(sample=final_pellet_hold, metric="TET labeling stage completed", unit="state", note="final membrane pellet retained for downstream purification")

source_steps.stage: A 2A AR 19 F-Trifluoroethanethiol (TET) labeling for NMR experiments
source_steps.description: Treat membranes with Aldrithiol: Resuspend the membranes in a glass tissue homogenizer with 100 mL A 2A AR High Salt Buffer containing 4 mM theophylline. Completely resuspend the membranes using 30 or more strokes, resulting in a homogeneous solution. Add 1 mM aldrithiol (22 mg/liter of cell culture, MW:220.31) and 100 μL of in-house Protease Inhibitor to the resuspended membranes. Resuspend the reagents in the membrane mixture, distribute among 50 mL conical tubes and rotate at 4°C for 1 h.

source_steps.stage: A 2A AR 19 F-Trifluoroethanethiol (TET) labeling for NMR experiments
source_steps.description: Treat membranes with trifluoroethanethiol (TET): Pellet the resuspended membranes in an ultracentrifuge at 200,000 × g for 30 min at 4°C. Discard the supernatant and resuspend the membrane pellet in 100 mL A 2A AR High salt buffer. Add TET to a final concentration of 1 mM/ liter of cell culture. Incubate the sample at 4°C for 1 h. Pellet the membranes in an ultracentrifuge at 200,000 × g for 30 min and discard the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: A 2A AR 19 F-Trifluoroethanethiol (TET) labeling for NMR experiments
source_steps.description: Continue to steps 46–53 described in the section “ purification of A2AAR for nanodisc assembly .”

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Prepare lipids: Prepare 100 mM stocks of lipids in Nanodisc Cholate Buffer.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Prepare for nanodisc assembly: Calculate amounts of receptor, lipids and MSP1D1 required to form nanodiscs based on the reported optimized molar ratio of 1:5:250 (receptor:MSP1D1:lipids). An example calculation is provided below.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Initiate nanodisc assembly ( Figure 3 ): Add all the components to a microcentrifuge tube in the following order: Nanodisc Buffer, Nanodisc Cholate Buffer, lipids, MSPD1 and A 2A AR. Incubate the assembly mixture for 1–2 h with gentle rotation at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Remove detergent: Add 0.5–1 g Bio-Beads/mL of nanodisc assembly mixture. Incubate the sample for 12–14 h at 4°C with gentle rocking or rotation.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Remove the Bio-Beads: Use a syringe with a 20-gauge needle to collect the soluble fraction and apply to a fresh tube. Wash the Bio-Beads once with an equal volume of Nanodiscs Buffer and combine with the first collected aliquot.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Prepare Ni-NTA by washing it with 5 CVs of ddH 2 O. Add the Ni-NTA resin to the pooled sample and incubate it at 4°C for 12–14 h. For every 1 mL of assembled nanodisc solution add 250 μL Ni-NTA resin.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Add the fluorophore ( Figure 5 B): Add the Cy3 fluorescent dye at a 10× fold molar excess of receptor to the sample. Incubate at 4°C for 2–3 h with rotation.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Remove empty nanodiscs: Transfer the resin to a gravity flow column. Wash the resin with 3 CVs of Nanodisc Wash Buffer. Elute the nanodiscs containing A 2A AR using Nanodiscs Elution Buffer in 10 different 0.5 CV fractions. Measure the protein absorbance in each aliquot and pool the 3–4 aliquots with the highest protein concentration.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Exchange the sample into the final buffer: Using a PD-10 column, follow the manufacturer’s protocol to exchange the sample into the Nanodiscs Desalting Buffer. https://cytiva-delivery.sitecorecontenthub.cloud/api/public/content/digi-11531-pdf .

background: This experiment is at the bacmid isolation and verification stage in production of recombinant full-length human huntingtin constructs. A harvested cell pellet is being processed through lysis, neutralization, clarification, alcohol-based DNA precipitation, ethanol wash, pellet drying, and final PCR-based confirmation of bacmid integration.
goal: Complete the local bacmid cleanup segment from clarified lysate through DNA precipitation and recovery, then run the diagnostic PCR needed to assess correct insert integration.
constraints: Use the centrifugation conditions from this stage exactly where critical: 3000 x g for 20 min at 4 C for the harvested cells, then 17000 x g for 20 min at 4 C for lysate clarification, then 17000 x g for 20 min at room temperature after isopropanol precipitation, and finally 17000 x g for 5 min at room temperature after the ethanol wash.

Preserve the gentle handling requirements during reagent mixing by using inversion-based mixing for the Sol II, Sol III, and isopropanol stages; include a 5 min room-temperature hold after Sol II and a 10 min ice incubation after Sol III as well as after isopropanol addition.

The workflow must pass through the expected intermediate states in dependency order: neutralized sample, clarified lysate, isopropanol-precipitated DNA, ethanol-washed pellet, air-dried transparent pellet, and PCR-ready material rather than restarting from raw inputs.
Represent room temperature as about 22 C, ice as 0 C, and use air drying before DNA recovery; drying occurs after removal of wash supernatant and before downstream analysis.
The final analysis step must be a PCR using the loaded bacmid reaction with M13 forward and hHTT R146 primers, corresponding to an expected product near 2.9 kbp when integration is correct.

available_inputs.name: harvested_cells
available_inputs.description: Harvested cells containing bacmid material.

available_inputs.name: sol_ii_lysed_sample
available_inputs.description: Cell pellet already resuspended in Sol I and treated with Sol II buffer.

available_inputs.name: sol_iii_neutralized_sample
available_inputs.description: Lysed bacmid sample after addition of Sol III buffer.

available_inputs.name: isopropanol_precipitation_mix
available_inputs.description: Clarified bacmid supernatant combined with 100 percent isopropanol.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_washed_pellet
available_inputs.description: DNA pellet after 70 percent ethanol wash.

available_inputs.name: pcr_plate_loaded
available_inputs.description: PCR tubes or plate loaded with bacmid DNA, M13 forward primer, and hHTT R146 primer.

gold_program:
pelleted_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=harvested_cells, speed_xg=3000, duration_min=20, temperature_c=4)
lysed_mixed = mix_sample(sample=sol_ii_lysed_sample, method='inversion', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
lysed_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=lysed_mixed, temperature_c=22, duration_min=5, condition='static')
neutralized_mixed = mix_sample(sample=sol_iii_neutralized_sample, method='inversion', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
neutralized_chilled = cool_sample(sample=neutralized_mixed, temperature_c=0, duration_min=10, device='ice bath')
clarified_lysate = centrifuge_sample(sample=neutralized_chilled, speed_xg=17000, duration_min=20, temperature_c=4)
precipitation_mixed = mix_sample(sample=isopropanol_precipitation_mix, method='inversion', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
precipitation_chilled = cool_sample(sample=precipitation_mixed, temperature_c=0, duration_min=10, device='ice bath')
dna_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=precipitation_chilled, speed_xg=17000, duration_min=20, temperature_c=22)
washed_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=ethanol_washed_pellet, speed_xg=17000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=22)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=washed_pellet, method='air dry', temperature_c=22, duration_min=10)
pcr_product = run_thermal_cycler(plate=pcr_plate_loaded, program_name='M13_hHTT_R146_bacmid_PCR', cycles=30, lid_temperature_c=105)

source_steps.description: Harvest the cells by centrifugation at 3,000 × g for 20 min at 4°C.
Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 300 μL of Sol I buffer.
Add 300 μL of Sol II buffer and mix gently by inverting. Incubate the sample at room temperature (RT; 20°C–24°C) for 5 min.
Add 300 μL of Sol III buffer to the mixture and gently mix by inverting. Incubate the sample on ice for 10 min.
Centrifuge the sample at 17,000 × g for 20 min at 4°C.
Transfer 750 μL of the supernatant to 800 μL of 100% isopropanol and mix gently the sample by inverting. Incubate the sample on ice for 10 min.
Centrifuge the incubated sample at 17,000 × g for 20 min at RT.
Decant the supernatant and add 1 mL of 70% ethanol on the top of the white pellet. Wash the pellet by inverting the tube.
Centrifuge the sample at 17,000 × g for 5 min at RT.

After centrifugation, remove the supernatant with a pipet without disturbing the pellet. Dry the white DNA pellet with airdry while the tubes are upside-down on a Kimwipe until the white pellet becomes transparent.
Dissolve the transparent DNA pellet by adding 40 μL of TE buffer. Resuspend the DNA pellet gently by tapping. Do not use a pipette.

Perform PCR using the M13 forward and hHTT R146 primers (the sequences of each primer are described in the key resources table ). The PCR product should appear at approximately 2.9 kbp when the H TT c cDNA is properly integrated into bacmids.

background: In this RNA tomography workflow for C. elegans, pooled cDNA has reached the magnetic-bead cleanup and transcription setup stage. The sample must be clarified, bound to beads, washed with ethanol, briefly air-dried, eluted in nuclease-free water, and then used for a long transcription incubation in a thermal cycler.
goal: Produce an execution program that carries pooled cDNA through bead-based purification, short elution handling, and the subsequent transcription incubation to yield the final incubated product.
constraints: Start from pooled cDNA and include a 1 min clarification spin at 12000 x g before downstream handling.
Model bead cleanup with a room-temperature binding incubation of 15 min, two ethanol wash-related handling steps, and an air-dry stage no longer than 10 min.
After elution in 8 uL nuclease-free water, include a short 2 min room-temperature step and a clarification state before transcription setup.
The downstream incubation must use a thermal cycler at 37 C for 13 h with the lid at 70 C and a final hold at 4 C.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keyword arguments only, 6-12 top-level calls total, and reuse at least two action names multiple times.

available_inputs.name: pooled_cdna
available_inputs.description: Pooled cDNA sample after combining reactions into one tube.

available_inputs.name: bead_mix_sample
available_inputs.description: Magnetic bead mix prepared for cDNA binding.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_wash
available_inputs.description: Fresh 80 percent ethanol used for bead washing.

available_inputs.name: elution_water
available_inputs.description: Nuclease-free water used to elute purified cDNA.

available_inputs.name: transcription_plate
available_inputs.description: PCR plate or strip containing eluted cDNA plus transcription master mix.

gold_program:
clarified_cdna = centrifuge_sample(sample=pooled_cdna, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
bead_binding = incubate_sample(sample=clarified_cdna, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=15.0, condition="static with magnetic bead mix")
first_ethanol_wash = incubate_sample(sample=bead_binding, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=0.5, condition="80 percent ethanol wash")
second_ethanol_wash = incubate_sample(sample=first_ethanol_wash, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=0.6, condition="repeat 80 percent ethanol wash")
air_dried_beads = dry_sample(sample=second_ethanol_wash, method="air dry", temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=10.0)
eluted_cdna = incubate_sample(sample=air_dried_beads, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=2.0, condition="resuspended in 8 uL nuclease-free water")
post_elution_clear = centrifuge_sample(sample=eluted_cdna, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
sealed_transcription_plate = seal_plate(plate=transcription_plate, seal_type="adhesive film", pressure="firm", duration_s=5.0)
transcription_product = run_thermal_cycler(plate=sealed_transcription_plate, program_name="T7_transcription_37C_13h_end_4C", cycles=1, lid_temperature_c=70.0)
final_product = cool_sample(sample=transcription_product, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=1.0, device="thermal cycler")
protocol_title: Spatial transcriptomics of the nematode Caenorhabditis elegans using RNA tomography

source_steps.description: Pool all reactions into one tube, spin down for 1 min at 12.000 g and transfer the lower, aqueous phase to a new microcentrifuge tube.
Prepare bead mix in a 1:7 ratio of beads to bead binding buffer, vortex well and add the bead mix to the sample in a 4:5 ratio (80 μL bead mix for 100 μL cDNA).
Incubate the sample at room temperature (20°C–25°C) for 15 min.
Place the sample on a magnetic stand for at least 5 min, until the liquid is clear.
Remove and discard the supernatant, add 800 μL of fresh 80% ethanol and incubate minimally for 30 s.
Repeat the wash step; then, remove all supernatant without disturbing the beads.
Air dry the pellet for a maximum of 10 min.
Resuspend the beads in 8 μL nuclease-free water. Pipette up-and-down 10 times to mix thoroughly.
Incubate at room temperature (20°C–25°C) for 2 min, then place the sample on the magnetic stand until the liquid is clear.
Carefully pipette all supernatant into a new 0.5 mL microcentrifuge tube, without disturbing the beads.
