background: Fixed and sheared mouse liver chromatin has already been generated. At this point, the work centers on checking a thawed chromatin aliquot for acceptable DNA recovery and shearing quality, then setting up cold antibody-binding reactions from the original sheared chromatin and proceeding through bead capture until the samples are ready for magnetic separation.
goal: Carry out the local workflow that verifies sheared chromatin quality and prepares chromatin immunoprecipitation reactions through antibody incubation, bead binding, and magnetic clearing.
constraints: Use a QC aliquot corresponding to 50 uL sheared chromatin, combine it with 150 uL 1x ChIP elution buffer, then add 8 uL of 5 M NaCl before the reverse-crosslinking heat treatment.

The QC heating condition must be 95 C for 15 min, followed by cooling to room temperature; document that purified DNA is recovered in 50 uL elution buffer and that QC includes a 10 uL agarose-gel check with a 1.5% gel and 100 bp marker plus a 2 uL NanoDrop concentration check.
The QC concentration note should reflect the expected acceptable range of 50-90 ng/uL.

For each immunoprecipitation target, start from 30 ug original sheared chromatin in a prechilled 1.5 mL tube and dilute with IP buffer containing 1x protease inhibitor to a final volume of 1 mL while ensuring SDS is below 0.1% after dilution.
Reserve 50 uL of the diluted chromatin as a 5% input and store it at -80 C for later use.
Add 2 ug target-specific antibody to each target reaction and 2 ug IgG to the negative control, then incubate the IP reactions overnight at 4 C with rotation.

Before capture, gently vortex the ChIP-grade Protein G magnetic beads; use 30 uL beads per IP reaction and incubate for 2 h at 4 C with rotation, then place tubes on a magnetic stand and allow 1 min for clearing.

available_inputs.name: sheared_chromatin_qc
available_inputs.description: Aliquoted sheared chromatin tube containing 50 uL material reserved for quality control.

available_inputs.name: sheared_chromatin_ip
available_inputs.description: Original sheared chromatin sample used to prepare immunoprecipitation reactions.

available_inputs.name: chip_elution_buffer
available_inputs.description: 1x ChIP elution buffer used to dilute the QC aliquot.

available_inputs.name: nacl_5M
available_inputs.description: 5 M sodium chloride added during reverse-crosslinking for QC.

available_inputs.name: ip_buffer_pi
available_inputs.description: IP buffer containing 1x protease inhibitor for chromatin dilution.

available_inputs.name: target_antibody
available_inputs.description: Target-specific antibody for chromatin immunoprecipitation.

available_inputs.name: igg_control
available_inputs.description: Negative control IgG antibody.

available_inputs.name: protein_g_beads
available_inputs.description: ChIP-grade Protein G magnetic beads for capture of antibody-bound chromatin.

gold_program:
qc_diluted = mix_sample(sample=sheared_chromatin_qc, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="medium")
qc_salted = mix_sample(sample=qc_diluted, method="pipetting", duration_s=8.0, speed="medium")
qc_heated = heat_sample(sample=qc_salted, temperature_c=95.0, duration_min=15.0, device="heat block")
qc_cooled = cool_sample(sample=qc_heated, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=10.0, device="bench")
qc_conc = record_measurement(sample=qc_cooled, metric="DNA concentration", unit="ng/uL", note="Purified DNA recovered in 50 uL elution buffer; assess QC by 10 uL on 1.5% agarose gel with 100 bp marker and 2 uL NanoDrop; expected range 50-90 ng/uL")
ip_diluted = mix_sample(sample=sheared_chromatin_ip, method="pipetting", duration_s=15.0, speed="medium")
input_stored = store_sample(sample=ip_diluted, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_h=24.0, container="1.5 mL tube labeled 5% Input, 50 uL")
ip_overnight = incubate_sample(sample=ip_diluted, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=960.0, condition="rotation after addition of 2 ug target antibody or 2 ug IgG control")
beads_vortexed = vortex_mix(sample=protein_g_beads, duration_s=5.0, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=4.0)
ip_with_beads = incubate_sample(sample=ip_overnight, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=120.0, condition="rotation with 30 uL Protein G magnetic beads per reaction")
magnet_cleared = incubate_sample(sample=ip_with_beads, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=1.0, condition="magnetic stand until solution clears")
protocol_title: Protocol for in vivo chromatin immunoprecipitation on purified chromatin isolated from mouse liver nuclei

source_steps.description: Thaw aliquoted chromatin from step 7, which contains 50 μL sheared chromatin and add 150 μL 1× ChIP elution buffer.
Add 8 μL 5 M NaCl to each sample, mix and then incubate at 95°C for 15 min. Afterward, cool samples to room temperature.

Purify and collect DNA in 50 μL of elution buffer. This protocol used the GeneJET PCR Purification Kit from Thermo Fisher ( https://www.thermofisher.com/document-connect/document-connect.html?url=https://assets.thermofisher.com/TFS-Assets%2FLSG%2Fmanuals%2FMAN0012662_GeneJET_PCR_Purification_UG.pdf ).
Use 10 μL sample and determine DNA fragment size by electrophoresis on a 1.5% agarose gel with a 100 base pair DNA marker ( Troubleshooting 2 ).
To determine DNA concentration, transfer 2 μL of purified DNA to NanoDrop. The concentration of DNA should be 50–90 ng/μL.

Into prechilled 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube, aliquot 30 μg of original sheared chromatin sample for each specific target if using multiple antibodies, plus a positive control if available and the negative control (IgG or Mock-IP).

Dilute samples from step 13 to final volume of 1 mL by adding proper amount of IP buffer containing 1× protease inhibitor (e.g., add 800 μL IP buffer to 200 μL chromatin sample), make sure SDS concentration is less than 0.1% after dilution.
Aliquot 50 μL of diluted chromatin into a new 1.5 mL tube and label as “5% Input”. Store sample at −80°C until used in step 21.
Add 2 μg target-specific antibody to sample, and 2 μg IgG sample for the negative control. Thus, the antibody concentration is approximately 2 μg/mL.
Incubate IP samples overnight at 4°C with rotation.

source_steps.stage: Chromatin immunoprecipitation—Adding beads, elute, and reverse crosslinking
source_steps.description: Gently vortex ChIP-Grade Protein G magnetic beads, add 30 μL of Protein G magnetic beads to each IP reaction and incubate for 2 h, at 4°C with rotation.

source_steps.description: Place the tubes in a magnetic stand, wait 1 min for solution to clear.

background: This item covers a cold-stage purification segment within the in vitro LACTB succinylation workflow. A GST-tagged bacterial lysate must be clarified, captured on magnetic affinity beads, washed, eluted, and concentrated for downstream analysis, while a separate His-tagged LACTB bacterial culture is converted into a pellet and resuspended in imidazole buffer for later purification.
goal: Use the provided materials to obtain an enriched GST-fusion eluate suitable for concentration/desalting and to prepare the His-tagged LACTB cell pellet in the correct cold resuspension buffer, while preserving the required low-temperature handling conditions.
constraints: Maintain 4 degrees Celsius for the specified centrifugation, bead incubation, and concentration operations, and use a pre-cooled 15 mL destination when collecting the clarified GST lysate supernatant.

Clarify lysates with a 16000 x g spin for 10 minutes, then recover the soluble fraction before bead capture; separately, recover the His-tagged LACTB cells from 50 mL culture by centrifugation at 3000 x g for 10 minutes at 4 degrees Celsius.

For GST affinity capture, add exactly 100 uL magnetic GST beads, incubate for 20 minutes with rolling or equivalent mixing at 4 degrees Celsius, then use magnetic separation while retaining the bead phase.

Wash the GST beads three times with cooled binding buffer before elution, then elute with exactly 200 uL glutathione elution buffer at pH 8.0 into an ultrafiltration-compatible destination and concentrate/desalt by centrifugation at 3000 x g and 4 degrees Celsius.

Resuspend the His-tagged LACTB pellet using 20 mL of cooled 50 mM imidazole buffer containing 1 mM DTT, and do not add unsupported sonication or manual discard actions not represented in the action pool.

available_inputs.name: gst_lysate
available_inputs.description: Sonicated bacterial lysate containing GST-tagged fusion protein, kept cold and ready for clarification.

available_inputs.name: binding_buffer_cold
available_inputs.description: Pre-cooled binding buffer used for GST magnetic bead washing.

available_inputs.name: gst_beads
available_inputs.description: GST fusion protein purified magnetic beads.

available_inputs.name: glutathione_elution_buffer
available_inputs.description: Glutathione elution buffer containing 20 mM glutathione at pH 8.0.

available_inputs.name: his_bacterial_cells_50ml
available_inputs.description: Fifty milliliters of bacterial cells containing His-tagged LACTB.

available_inputs.name: imidazole_buffer_dtt_cold
available_inputs.description: Pre-cooled 50 mM imidazole buffer containing 1 mM DTT.

gold_program:
gst_clarified = centrifuge_sample(sample=gst_lysate, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
gst_supernatant = collect_fraction(sample=gst_clarified, phase="supernatant", destination="pre_cooled_15ml_tube", volume_ul=1000)
gst_with_beads = add_reagent(sample=gst_supernatant, reagent=gst_beads, volume_ul=100, mixing="gentle mix")
gst_bound = incubate_sample(sample=gst_with_beads, temperature_c=4, duration_min=20, condition="rolling")
gst_sep1 = magnetic_separate(sample=gst_bound, rack="magnetic_frame", duration_min=2, collect_phase="beads")
gst_wash1 = add_reagent(sample=gst_sep1, reagent=binding_buffer_cold, volume_ul=3000, mixing="wash three times")
gst_eluted = add_reagent(sample=gst_wash1, reagent=glutathione_elution_buffer, volume_ul=200, mixing="gentle mix")
gst_eluate = collect_fraction(sample=gst_eluted, phase="eluate", destination="amicon_ultra_15", volume_ul=200)
gst_concentrated = centrifuge_sample(sample=gst_eluate, speed_xg=3000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
his_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=his_bacterial_cells_50ml, speed_xg=3000, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
his_resuspended = add_reagent(sample=his_pellet, reagent=imidazole_buffer_dtt_cold, volume_ul=20000, mixing="resuspend")
protocol_title: Protocol for identifying OXCT1-mediated LACTB succinylation sites in vitro

source_steps.description: Sonicate bacterial lysate in the ice bath with an ultrasonic homogenizer with a 6 mm probe (SCIENTZ, China) at 20% power for 10 min, work for 1 s, and stop for 2 s.
Centrifuge for 10 min at 4°C, 16,000 × g , and transfer the supernatant to a new pre-cooled 15 mL reaction tube.
Add 100 μL GST fusion protein purified magnetic beads (BeaverBio, China) and incubate with the Rolling incubator for 20 min at 4°C.
Discard the supernatant and wash magnetic beads three times with the cooled Binding buffer with the help of a Magnetic Frame.
Elute protein with 200 μL Glutathione elution buffer (20 mM Glutathione, pH 8.0) and collect in an ultrafiltration tube (Amicon Ultra-15, Millipore).
Centrifuge for concentration and desalination at 4°C, 3,000 × g. Note: Protein recovery can be improved by prolonging elution time or increasing elution number.

Purify His-tagged LACTB. Representative data from three independent experiments with similar results can be found in Figure 2 B. Collect 50 mL bacterial cells containing His-tagged LACTB and centrifuge for 10 min at 4°C, 3,000 × g.
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the bacterial pellet with the cooled 20 mL 50 mM Imidazole buffer containing 1 mM DTT.
Break cells in an ice bath with an ultrasonic homogenizer with a 6 mm probe (SCIENTZ, China) at 20% power for 20 min, work for 1 s, and stop for 2 s.
Centrifuge for 10 min at 4°C, 16,000 × g , and transfer the supernatant to a new pre-cooled 15 mL reaction tube.
Add 100 μL His fusion protein purified magnetic beads (BeaverBio, China) and incubate with the Rolling incubator for 20 min at 4°C.
Discard the supernatant and wash magnetic beads twice with 50 mM Imidazole buffer.

background: Primary human liver cell isolates are in the endothelial-enrichment phase after an earlier pellet collection. The current segment uses antibody-based depletion of unwanted biliary epithelial and immune cells, followed by chilled magnetic bead handling and a final recovery spin to obtain an endothelial-enriched pellet suitable for downstream culture or assay setup.
goal: Carry out the local depletion and enrichment workflow from the current liver cell pellet to the final endothelial-enriched pellet, preserving the required warm and cold handling conditions and the magnetic clarification stage.
constraints: Start from a pellet in a 15 mL tube, with an initial PBS wash/spin at 850 x g for 5 min around room temperature (21-23 C), then a resuspension state equivalent to 500 uL PBS before antibody incubation.
Include a 30 min incubation at 37 C with constant agitation after EpCAM antibody labeling, and also represent preparation of 50 mL sterile PBS chilled on ice for later cold steps.
After the warm incubation, include a PBS dilution to about 10 mL and a second centrifugation at 850 x g for 5 min at room temperature, followed by conversion to an ice-cold resuspension state.
Model bead-based depletion as an on-ice 30 min constant-agitation incubation after bead addition, then a chilled dilution to about 6 mL before magnetic separation.

Represent two successive 2 min magnetic clarification periods, with the first occurring in a 15 mL tube with a loosened lid and the second acting on the recovered supernatant to produce an endothelial-enriched supernatant.
Finish by adjusting the endothelial-enriched fraction to 14 mL with ice-cold PBS and centrifuging at 850 x g for 5 min at 4 C.

available_inputs.description: 15 mL centrifuge tube containing the combined liver cell pellet after the preceding isolation stage.

available_inputs.name: epcam_labeled_cells
available_inputs.description: Cell suspension after EpCAM antibody has been added to the resuspended pellet.

available_inputs.name: ice_pbs_50ml
available_inputs.description: Fifty milliliters of sterile PBS reserved for chilling on ice for later use.

available_inputs.name: bead_labeled_cells
available_inputs.description: Cell suspension after addition of goat anti-mouse IgG Dynabeads and CD45 Dynabeads.

gold_program:
step1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=cell_pellet_tube, speed_xg=850, duration_min=5, temperature_c=22)
step2 = cool_sample(sample=ice_pbs_50ml, temperature_c=0, duration_min=30, device="ice")
step3 = incubate_sample(sample=epcam_labeled_cells, temperature_c=37, duration_min=30, condition="constant agitation")
step4 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step3, speed_xg=850, duration_min=5, temperature_c=22)
step5 = cool_sample(sample=step4, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5, device="ice")
step6 = incubate_sample(sample=bead_labeled_cells, temperature_c=0, duration_min=30, condition="constant agitation on ice")
step7 = cool_sample(sample=step6, temperature_c=0, duration_min=2, device="DynaMag-15 separation magnet with loosened lid")
step8 = cool_sample(sample=step7, temperature_c=0, duration_min=2, device="DynaMag-15 separation magnet")
step9 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step8, speed_xg=850, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)

source_steps.description: Tip off supernatant and combine pellet into a 15 mL centrifuge tube. Make the volume up to 14 mL with sterile PBS and centrifuge at 850 × g for 5 min at 21°C–23°C.
Depleting biliary epithelial cells (BEC) ( Figure 1 Di) and immune cells ( Figure 1 Dii). Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 500 μL of sterile PBS.

Add 50 μL EpCAM antibody and incubate at 37°C for 30 min in an orbital shaker to provide constant agitation. Note: During this 30 min incubation period, put 50 mL of sterile PBS on ice in preparation for the following steps.
Make the volume up to ∼10 mL with sterile room temperature (21°C–23°C) PBS, mix well and centrifuge at 850 × g for 5 min at 21°C–23°C.
Tip off supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 500 μL ice-cold sterile PBS.
Add 10 μL Goat Anti-Mouse IgG Dynabeads and 50 μL CD45 Dynabeads (ensuring that the beads are thoroughly resuspended prior to addition).
Mix well and incubate for 30 min on ice with constant agitation. Note: An orbital shaker or benchtop rocker can be used for this purpose.
Make up the volume to ∼6 mL with ice-cold PBS and mix well.
Loosen lid of centrifuge tube and place in DynaMag-15 Separation Magnet for 2 min.
With the 15 mL centrifuge tube in the magnet pipette off the supernatant and transfer to a second 15 mL centrifuge tube (do not discard).
Place the second tube back in the magnet for 2 min and pipette off supernatant enriched for endothelial cells into a new sterile 15 mL centrifuge tube.
Make the volume of endothelial-enriched supernatant up to 14 mL with ice-cold PBS and centrifuge 850 × g for 5 min at 4°C.

background: Metaphase cells from adherent culture have already been collected by mitotic shake-off into 50-ml tubes and pelleted. The next local stage is to process these pellets into a swollen cell suspension suitable for chromosome preparation by resuspending in hypotonic solution, allowing swelling at room temperature, checking morphology, extending the swelling period if needed, and then re-pelleting the swollen cells.
goal: Prepare the harvested metaphase cell pellet as a properly swollen hypotonic cell suspension and finish with a room-temperature centrifugation step that yields a pellet ready for the next chromosome isolation stage.
constraints: Start from two harvested cell pellets in 50-ml centrifuge tubes produced from the metaphase harvest stage.
Each pellet should be resuspended in hypotonic solution at about 5 ml per tube before pooling.
The two resuspended pellets must be combined into a single 50-ml tube for swelling.
Swelling must occur at room temperature, represented as 25 C, for an initial period of about 15 min.
A morphology/QC assessment must be recorded after the initial swelling period, noting whether metaphase cells appear sufficiently swollen with chromosome spread.
If additional swelling is needed, include a second room-temperature incubation using a duration within the 10 to 30 min range.
Finish by centrifuging the swollen cells at 289 x g for 5 min at room temperature.
Use only the provided action-pool functions and keep the program at protocol level rather than low-level handling details.

available_inputs.name: pellet_tube_a
available_inputs.description: First 50-ml tube containing harvested metaphase cell pellet.

available_inputs.name: pellet_tube_b
available_inputs.description: Second 50-ml tube containing harvested metaphase cell pellet.

available_inputs.name: hypotonic_solution
available_inputs.description: Hypotonic swelling solution used to resuspend the metaphase cell pellets.

available_inputs.name: combined_swelling_tube
available_inputs.description: Single 50-ml polypropylene centrifuge tube used after pooling the resuspended cells.

gold_program:
resuspended_a = mix_sample(sample=pellet_tube_a, method='pipetting with ~5 ml hypotonic_solution', duration_s=20.0, speed='gentle')
resuspended_b = mix_sample(sample=pellet_tube_b, method='pipetting with ~5 ml hypotonic_solution', duration_s=20.0, speed='gentle')
pooled_cells = mix_sample(sample=combined_swelling_tube, method='combine resuspended_a and resuspended_b by pipetting', duration_s=15.0, speed='gentle')
initial_swelling = incubate_sample(sample=pooled_cells, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=15.0, condition='static')
qc_observation_1 = record_measurement(sample=initial_swelling, metric='cell_swelling_qc', unit='qualitative', note='Assess swollen metaphase cells for adequate chromosome spread within plasma membrane after initial hypotonic treatment')
extended_swelling = incubate_sample(sample=initial_swelling, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=20.0, condition='static')
qc_observation_2 = record_measurement(sample=extended_swelling, metric='cell_swelling_qc', unit='qualitative', note='Additional swelling performed because more incubation may be needed within the 10-30 min range')
final_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=extended_swelling, speed_xg=289, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=25.0)
protocol_title: Chromosome Analysis and Sorting Using Conventional Flow Cytometers

source_steps.description: Briefly rinse the cell layer with 5 ml sterile 1× PBS and then discard the buffer using a sterile 10‐ml pipette.
Add 3 ml TrypLE Express and ensure it covers the entire surface of the flask. Incubate at 37°C for 3‐5 min.
Tap the bottom of the flask gently to detach all of the cells. Add 5 ml fresh culture medium and mix well to obtain a single‐cell suspension.

Subculture the cell suspension at a ratio of 1:3 in four flasks. Bring the volume to 25 ml with fresh culture medium. Incubate at 37°C in a 5% CO 2 incubator. In this example, a total of 8 flasks are used during the chromosome harvesting process. The number of flasks required to obtain a sufficient number of chromosomes (at least 5 million chromosomes per ml) will vary according to the total number of metaphase cells achieved after colcemid treatment.

After 24 hr, add colcemid to a final concentration of 0.1 µg/ml and gently swirl the flask to mix well. Incubate for 6‐16 hr. The duration of treatment with mitotic inhibitors varies according to the rate of cell growth. A high mitotic index can be achieved by optimizing treatment time for different cell types. Cells arrested at metaphase appear under the microscope as “rounded cells” loosely attached to the monolayer surface.
Gently tap the bottom of the flasks to dislodge the cells at metaphase.

Collect cells suspended in the supernatant from the four flasks (150 cm 2 ) after mitotic shake‐off and transfer them to two 50‐ml polypropylene centrifuge tubes. Centrifuge at 289 × g for 5 min at room temperature.
Remove the supernatant without disturbing the pellet. Invert the tube on absorbent paper and drain the excess medium.
Proceed to Basic Protocol 2 .

Resuspend the cell pellets from Basic Protocol 1 in hypotonic solution (∼5 ml per tube) with a Pasteur pipette. Combine the cell suspensions in a single 50‐ml polypropylene centrifuge tube and incubate at room temperature for about 15 min.

Mix 5 µl cell suspension with 5 µl Turck's stain on a microscope slide and directly monitor the cells for swelling under a light microscope. If Turck's stain is not available, use 1 mg/ml PI and view under a fluorescence microscope. Swollen cells at metaphase should display a good spread of chromosomes within the plasma membrane (see Fig. 2 ). Assess the cells for swelling and incubate for an additional 10 to 30 min if needed.
Centrifuge the swollen cells at 289 × g for 5 min at room temperature.

background: Human natural regulatory T cells have already been washed and reduced to a small residual volume before intracellular Foxp3 staining. At this stage, the cells must undergo fixation/permeabilization, cold protected incubation, repeated permeabilization-buffer wash cycles with centrifugation, intracellular antibody staining, and final resuspension in FACS buffer for flow-cytometry analysis.
goal: Complete the intracellular Foxp3 staining preparation of the nTreg sample so it is ready for flow cytometry after fixation, permeabilization washes, staining, and final buffer resuspension.
constraints: Start from an nTreg tube that has already been washed and left with about 100 uL residual volume.
Use 500 uL fixation/permeabilization solution, then pulse vortex and incubate at refrigerator temperature in the dark for a duration within 30-60 min.
Include two consecutive wash cycles with 500 uL 1x Permeabilization PBS, each followed by centrifugation, and keep the cells in about 500 uL permeabilization buffer before staining.
Add Foxp3-647 at 0.5 uL per tube, pulse vortex, and incubate for 30 min at 2-8 C protected from light.
After staining, perform one more 500 uL permeabilization-buffer wash with centrifugation and finish in 500 uL FACS buffer.
Use only provided action-pool functions and maintain a dependency chain where later operations consume prior outputs.

available_inputs.description: nTreg cell suspension after FACS buffer wash, with about 100 uL residual volume remaining

available_inputs.name: fixperm_working_solution
available_inputs.description: 1x Fixation/Permeabilization working solution used at 500 uL per tube

available_inputs.name: permeabilization_pbs
available_inputs.description: 1x Permeabilization PBS for wash and resuspension steps

available_inputs.name: foxp3_647
available_inputs.description: Foxp3-647 intracellular staining antibody used at 0.5 uL per tube

gold_program:
fixperm_mixed = vortex_mix(sample=washed_ntreg_tube, duration_s=2.0, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=22.0)
fixperm_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=fixperm_mixed, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=45.0, condition='protected from light')
wash1_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=fixperm_incubated, speed_xg=500, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
wash1_resuspended = mix_sample(sample=wash1_pellet, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
wash2_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash1_resuspended, speed_xg=500, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
pre_stain_resuspended = mix_sample(sample=wash2_pellet, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
foxp3_mixed = vortex_mix(sample=pre_stain_resuspended, duration_s=2.0, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=22.0)
foxp3_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=foxp3_mixed, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=30.0, condition='protected from light')
post_stain_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=foxp3_incubated, speed_xg=500, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
final_ready_sample = mix_sample(sample=post_stain_pellet, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
protocol_title: Protocol for in vitro isolation, induction, expansion, and determination of human natural regulatory T cells and induced regulatory T cells

source_steps.stage: Culture of natural human regulatory T cells (nTregs)
source_steps.description: Wash cells with 500 μL FACS buffer, abandon the supernatant and retain about 100 μL residual volume.

source_steps.stage: Culture of natural human regulatory T cells (nTregs)
source_steps.description: Add 500 μL 1× Fixation / Permeabilization Concentrate working solution to each tube and pulse vortex. Incubate for 30–60 min in the dark in the refrigerator (2°C–8°C).

source_steps.stage: Culture of natural human regulatory T cells (nTregs)
source_steps.description: Centrifuge cell suspension and abandon the supernatant. Add 500 μL of 1× Permeabilization PBS to each tube and discard the supernatant after centrifugation. Repeat once.

source_steps.stage: Culture of natural human regulatory T cells (nTregs)
source_steps.description: Resuspend pellet in remaining volume of 1× Permeabilization PBS, typically 500 μL.

source_steps.stage: Culture of natural human regulatory T cells (nTregs)
source_steps.description: Add staining antibodies, 0.5 μL of Foxp3-647 to each tube, pulse vortex and incubate for 30 min in the dark in the refrigerator (2°C–8°C).

source_steps.stage: Culture of natural human regulatory T cells (nTregs)
source_steps.description: Add 500 μL of 1× Permeabilization PBS to each tube and discard the supernatant after centrifugation.

source_steps.stage: Culture of natural human regulatory T cells (nTregs)
source_steps.description: The stained cells were resuspended in 500 μL FACS buffer and analyzed by flow cytometry ( Figure 2 B).

source_steps.stage: Isolation and culture of human in vitro -induced Tregs (iTregs)
source_steps.description: Isolation of CD4 + CD45RA + T cells. Determine the cell number of PBMCs, which can be obtained from peripheral blood and apheresis products.

source_steps.stage: Isolation and culture of human in vitro -induced Tregs (iTregs)
source_steps.description: For each 10⁷ total cells, suspend the cell pellet in 40 μL of PBS. To this, add 10 μL of Naive CD4 + T Cell Biotin-Antibody Cocktail II and incubate for 5 min in the refrigerator (2°C–8°C).

source_steps.stage: Isolation and culture of human in vitro -induced Tregs (iTregs)
source_steps.description: Mix well and incubate for another 10 min in the refrigerator (2°C–8°C) with 20 μL of Naive CD4 + T Cell MicroBeads Cocktail II and 30 μL of PBS per 10 7 cells.

source_steps.stage: Isolation and culture of human in vitro -induced Tregs (iTregs)
source_steps.description: Proceed to magnetic separation.

source_steps.stage: Isolation and culture of human in vitro -induced Tregs (iTregs)
source_steps.description: Place LS Column in the magnetic field of a suitable MACS Separator. After rinsing the LS Column with 3 mL of PBS, apply cell suspension to the column. Take unlabeled cells that pass through the column and wash it with 2×1 mL of PBS ( Figure 3 A). Isolation and culture of human in vitro -induced Tregs (iTregs) (A) Naïve CD4 + T cells were sorted from PBMCs by MACS, then cultured with TGF-B, IL-2, and atRA to generate iTregs. (B) The purity of naïve CD4 + T cells was identified by flow cytometry after sorting. (C) Representative plots of CD4 + Foxp3 + iTregs by flow cytometry on day 0, 3, 5, 7. (D) The expansion fold of iTregs (The first day set to 1).

background: A dissociated mouse lung metastasis cell suspension has already been prepared and pelleted. At this stage, the sample undergoes magnetic-bead cleanup to remove immune and epithelial cells, followed by recovery of the negative fraction and preparation for endothelial-cell enrichment. The work involves cold incubations, repeated MACS buffer washes, centrifugation, column-based magnetic handling, and a final resuspension for the next separation step.
goal: Convert the existing lung cell pellet into a washed, CD31-labeled cell fraction in MACS buffer that is ready for downstream endothelial enrichment processing.
constraints: Begin from the provided lung cell pellet and keep the bead-labeling incubations and centrifugation steps at 4 C where specified.
The depletion stage must include labeling with 80 uL MACS buffer, 10 uL CD45 MicroBeads, and 10 uL CD326 EpCAM MicroBeads, followed by a 15 min cold incubation.
After depletion labeling, represent a 3 mL MACS buffer wash and centrifuge at 250 x g for 5 min, then resuspend the pellet in 0.5 mL MACS buffer before column handling.

Model recovery of the unlabeled flow-through by passing the sample through a 40 um cell strainer into a 15 mL conical context and include three 0.5 mL MACS buffer column washes before spinning the negative fraction.
The recovered CD45/CD326-negative fraction must be centrifuged at 250 x g for 5 min at 4 C before the endothelial labeling stage.
The endothelial labeling stage must use 90 uL MACS buffer and 10 uL CD31 MicroBeads with a 15 min incubation at 4 C.
After CD31 labeling, perform another 3 mL MACS buffer wash with a 250 x g, 5 min, 4 C centrifugation and leave the final pellet resuspended in 0.5 mL MACS buffer.

available_inputs.name: lung_cell_pellet
available_inputs.description: Pelleted single-cell preparation from metastatic mouse lung tissue after a prior centrifugation step.

available_inputs.name: macs_buffer
available_inputs.description: Cold MACS buffer used for labeling, washing, and resuspension.

available_inputs.name: cd45_cd326_beads
available_inputs.description: Combined depletion reagents corresponding to CD45 and CD326 EpCAM MicroBeads.

available_inputs.name: cd31_beads
available_inputs.description: CD31 murine MicroBeads for endothelial-cell enrichment.

available_inputs.name: ms_column_setup
available_inputs.description: MS column in a compatible magnetic separator for MACS processing.

available_inputs.name: cell_strainer_40um
available_inputs.description: Forty-micrometer cell strainer used during column application.

gold_program:
depletion_mix = mix_sample(sample=lung_cell_pellet, method='pipetting with 80 uL MACS buffer + 10 uL CD45 beads + 10 uL CD326 EpCAM beads', duration_s=20.0, speed='gentle')
cold_depletion = incubate_sample(sample=depletion_mix, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=15.0, condition='static')
washed_depletion = mix_sample(sample=cold_depletion, method='add 3 mL MACS buffer by pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
spun_depletion = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_depletion, speed_xg=250, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
column_loaded = filter_sample(sample=spun_depletion, filter_type='40 um cell strainer into 15 mL conical through MS column after resuspension in 0.5 mL MACS buffer', pore_size_um=40.0, destination='15 mL conical flow-through collection')
negative_fraction = magnetic_separate(sample=column_loaded, rack='MS column magnetic separator with three 0.5 mL MACS buffer washes', duration_min=3.0, collect_phase='flow-through unlabeled fraction')
spun_negative = centrifuge_sample(sample=negative_fraction, speed_xg=250, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
cd31_label_mix = mix_sample(sample=spun_negative, method='resuspend pellet in 90 uL MACS buffer + 10 uL CD31 beads by pipetting', duration_s=20.0, speed='gentle')
cd31_labeled = incubate_sample(sample=cd31_label_mix, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=15.0, condition='static')
washed_cd31 = mix_sample(sample=cd31_labeled, method='add 3 mL MACS buffer by pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
spun_cd31 = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_cd31, speed_xg=250, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
final_resuspended_pellet = mix_sample(sample=spun_cd31, method='resuspend pellet in 0.5 mL MACS buffer by pipetting', duration_s=20.0, speed='gentle')
protocol_title: Generation of vessel co-option lung metastases mouse models for single-cell isolation of metastases-derived cells and endothelial cells

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 250 g for 5 min and aspirate the supernatant. Proceed with the pellet to step b-i.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Depletion of immune and epithelial cells using CD45 and CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads. (Timing: 45 min) Add 80 μL MACS buffer, 10 μL CD45 MicroBeads and 10 μL CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads to the cell pellet. Note: We suggest to resuspend the cells in 80 μL of MACS buffer as indicated above. Adapt the amount of MicroBeads according to the cell number and follow the manufacturer's instructions, e.g. 10 μL of the beads for up to 1 x 10 7 cells; for larger numbers of cells, scale up the amount accordingly ( https://www.miltenyibiotec.com/NL-en/products/cd45-microbeads-mouse.html?countryRedirected=1#gref ; https://www.miltenyibiotec.com/US-en/products/cd326-epcam-microbeads-mouse.html#gref ).

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Mix and incubate for 15 min at 4°C. Note: Process the samples quickly to avoid non-specific binding of MicroBeads at 20°C–22°C.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Wash the cells by adding 3 mL of MACS buffer and centrifuge at 250 g for 5 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Aspirate the supernatant completely and resuspend the pellet in 0.5 mL of MACS buffer.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Place an MS column in the magnetic field of the suitable MACS Separator and rinse with 0.5 mL MACS buffer.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Apply the cell suspension from step iv onto the column through a 40 μm cell strainer (blue) and collect unlabeled cells that flow through in 15 mL conical tube (this is the fraction that contains ECs).

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Perform three washing steps with 0.5 mL MACS buffer each.

source_steps.stage: Metastases-derived cell and endothelial cell single-cell isolation from lung tissues from RENCA and 4T1 mouse model with sunitinib treatment
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the flow-through (CD45 & CD326 (EpCAM) negative fraction) at 250 g for 5 min at 4°C.

source_steps.description: Enrichment of ECs using CD31 murine MicroBeads. (Timing: 45 min) Add 90 μL MACS buffer and 10 μL CD31 MicroBeads to the cell pellet. Mix and incubate for 15 min at 4°C.

source_steps.description: Aspirate the supernatant completely and resuspend the pellet in 0.5 mL of MACS buffer.

background: Human fibroblasts have completed L-AHA incorporation and must now be converted from adherent cultures into fixed, permeabilized, fluorescently click-labeled suspensions suitable for later flow-cytometry analysis. The current segment combines harvest, wash, fixation, permeabilization, click labeling, cleanup washes, and final chilled holding.
goal: Produce a concise executable plan that takes the post-labeling adherent cells through harvest and chemical processing to a final protected suspension ready for cytometry.
constraints: Start from cells immediately after L-AHA exposure and include a prewarmed DPBS rinse before detachment; use 250 uL of 0.25% trypsin-EDTA at 37 C for about 2 to 3 min, then resuspend with 750 uL prewarmed methionine-free medium.
Represent the initial recovery and wash using 300 x g for 5 min at 4 C, including a cold DPBS wash before fixation.

Fix in 200 uL of 4% paraformaldehyde for 15 min at room temperature and then process at 800 x g for 10 min at 4 C; permeabilize in 200 uL of 0.5% Triton X-100 buffer for 15 min at room temperature before the click step.
Run the click reaction in 200 uL for 30 min at room temperature with protection from light.
After click labeling, capture the required cleanup as two washes with 500 uL of 1% BSA in DPBS using 800 x g for 10 min at 4 C, then end in 500 uL of 1% FBS in DPBS on ice in the dark.

available_inputs.name: dpbs_prewarmed
available_inputs.description: Prewarmed 1x DPBS for brief rinse

available_inputs.name: trypsin_edta_025
available_inputs.description: Prewarmed 0.25% trypsin-EDTA

available_inputs.name: met_free_medium

gold_program:
step1 = add_reagent(sample=cells_laha_labeled, reagent='prewarmed 1x DPBS, 1500 uL; then 0.25% trypsin-EDTA, 250 uL', volume_ul=1750.0, mixing='brief rinse then cover cell layer')
step2 = incubate_sample(sample=step1, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=3.0, condition='static')
step3 = add_reagent(sample=step2, reagent=met_free_medium, volume_ul=750.0, mixing='pipette gently to resuspend cells')
step4 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step3, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step5 = add_reagent(sample=step4, reagent='cold 1x DPBS 1000 uL; then 4% paraformaldehyde 200 uL', volume_ul=1200.0, mixing='wash pellet then resuspend for fixation')
step6 = incubate_sample(sample=step5, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=15.0, condition='static')
step7 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step6, speed_xg=800, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step8 = add_reagent(sample=step7, reagent='0.5% Triton X-100 permeabilization buffer 200 uL; then click reaction solution 200 uL', volume_ul=400.0, mixing='resuspend for permeabilization and later click labeling')
step9 = incubate_sample(sample=step8, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=45.0, condition='protected from light')
step10 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step9, speed_xg=800, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step11 = add_reagent(sample=step10, reagent='1% BSA in 1x DPBS wash twice, 500 uL each; then 1% FBS in 1x DPBS 500 uL', volume_ul=1500.0, mixing='wash and final resuspension')
step12 = cool_sample(sample=step11, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=5.0, device='ice bath')
protocol_title: A flow-cytometry-based assessment of global protein synthesis in human senescent cells

source_steps.description: Label cells with 1 mL 100 μM L-AHA for 30 min at 37°C. Treatment with CHX (100 μg/mL) that interferes with the translocation step in protein synthesis can be used as a negative control ( Schneider-Poetsch et al., 2010 ). In addition, prepare cells that are not labeled with L-AHA (ddH 2 O treated, unstained control) as an additional negative control for assessing a non-specific signal from unconjugated AFDye 405 alkyne.
Discard the L-AHA culture medium and briefly rinse cells with 1–2 mL prewarmed 1 × DPBS per well to remove all traces of serum.
Add 250 μL prewarmed trypsin-EDTA (0.25%) to cells and hold at 37°C for 2–3 min until cell layer is dispersed.
Add 750 μL prewarmed methionine-free medium to cells and gently pipette 10 to 15 times to resuspend cells.
Collect cell suspension in U-bottom FACS tubes and centrifuge at 300 × g , 4°C for 5 min. Discard supernatants.
Wash cells with cold 1 × DPBS: resuspend cells in 1 mL cold 1 × DBPS per tube; centrifuge at 300 × g , 4°C for 5 min; and discard supernatants.

Fix cells with paraformaldehyde: resuspend cells in 200 μL of 4% paraformaldehyde (w/v in DPBS) per tube; incubate cells for 15 min at room temperature (20°C–25°C); centrifuge at 800 × g , 4°C for 10 min; and gently discard supernatants by pipetting.

Permeabilize cells with the permeabilization buffer: resuspend cells in 200 μL of permeabilization buffer (1 × Triton X-100 [0.5%]) per tube; incubate cells for 15 min at room temperature (20°C–25°C); centrifuge at 800 × g , 4°C for 10 min; and gently discard supernatants by pipetting.
Prepare the L-AHA click reaction cocktail as described in materials and equipment .

Add 200 μL L-AHA click reaction solution per tube. Resuspend the cell pellet and incubate for 30 min at room temperature (20°C–25°C). Make sure to protect the sample from light (e.g., wrap sample tubes with aluminum foil when not in use) during the following steps.

Wash cells twice with 1% BSA in 1 × DPBS: resuspend cells in with 500 μL 1% BSA in 1 × DPBS per tube; centrifuge at 800 × g , 4°C for 10 min; gently discard supernatants by pipetting; and repeat this washing step once more.
Resuspend cells in 500 μL 1% FBS in 1 × DBPS. Place all tubes on ice in the dark.

background: A pooled dual-gRNA lentiviral library has already been cloned with the first guide set and plated after transformation. You are now working at the late library-construction stage where bacterial colonies are expanded, plasmid library material is recovered, the intermediate construct is prepared for insertion of the second guide set, and the resulting digestion product is conditioned for downstream assembly and quality control.
goal: Process the plated library and first-guide intermediate so that you obtain recovered pooled plasmid material, optional colony plasmids suitable for sequence confirmation handling, and a linearized first-guide vector prepared after the second-guide insertion digestion workflow.
constraints: Use the plated library culture on a 15 cm ampicillin LB agar plate with a 30°C incubation for about 18 h to reduce recombination risk.
Colony recovery should reflect two sequential LB plus ampicillin washes of the same plate, each using 6 mL medium, before pooled plasmid preparation handling.
If colony verification material is handled, represent growth in 4 mL LB plus ampicillin with shaking for at least 8 h at 37°C before storage or downstream use.

For second-guide insertion preparation, the first-guide vector digestion mixture corresponds to 2 μg vector in a 50 μL reaction with BsmBI_v2 and 10× NEBuffer r3.1, assembled cold and incubated at 55°C for 1 h.
The digestion workflow should preserve the idea that the sample is first kept cold before heating, then conditioned for downstream cleanup and eluted/prepared in water-equivalent final material.
Do not introduce operations outside the provided action pool; keep the procedure at incubation, cooling, heating, mixing, shaking, and storage abstraction.
Later steps should consume outputs generated from earlier ones rather than restarting from untouched inputs.
Include multiple repeated action families, with at least one family differing mainly by a critical numeric setting.

available_inputs.name: library_plate
available_inputs.description: 15 cm ampicillin LB agar plate containing transformed pooled library colonies

available_inputs.name: lb_amp_wash1
available_inputs.description: First 6 mL aliquot of LB plus ampicillin used to recover colonies from the plate

available_inputs.name: lb_amp_wash2
available_inputs.description: Second 6 mL aliquot of LB plus ampicillin used to wash the same plate

available_inputs.name: picked_colonies_tubes
available_inputs.description: Several individual colony cultures in 4 mL LB plus ampicillin for optional verification

available_inputs.name: first_grna_vector_digest_mix
available_inputs.description: Ice-assembled 50 μL digestion reaction containing LentiGuide_DKO plus first gRNA insert and digestion reagents

gold_program:
step1 = incubate_sample(sample=library_plate, temperature_c=30.0, duration_min=1080.0, condition='static')
step2 = stir_with_rod(container=step1, duration_s=20.0, speed_desc='gentle scrape in 6 mL LB plus ampicillin', temperature_c=25.0)
step3 = mix_sample(sample=lb_amp_wash1, method='pipetting', duration_s=15.0, speed='medium')
step4 = stir_with_rod(container=step1, duration_s=20.0, speed_desc='gentle wash in second 6 mL LB plus ampicillin', temperature_c=25.0)
step5 = mix_sample(sample=lb_amp_wash2, method='pipetting', duration_s=15.0, speed='medium')
step6 = shake_container(sample=picked_colonies_tubes, amplitude_mm=8.0, repeat_n=1, duration_s=28800.0)
step7 = incubate_sample(sample=step6, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=480.0, condition='shaking')
step8 = cool_sample(sample=first_grna_vector_digest_mix, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=5.0, device='ice bath')
step9 = heat_sample(sample=step8, temperature_c=55.0, duration_min=60.0, device='heat block')
step10 = mix_sample(sample=step9, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
step11 = store_sample(sample=step10, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=1.0, container='clean tube for downstream cleanup and elution')
protocol_title: Generation of dual-gRNA library for combinatorial CRISPR screening of synthetic lethal gene pairs

source_steps.description: Plate the library: Spread the original culture onto pre-warmed 15 cm ampicillin LB agar plate (0.5 mL/plate), incubate at 30°C for approximate 18 h. CRITICAL: 30°C incubation is intended to minimize recombination of the lentiviral plasmid library.

Harvest colonies and maxi-prep: Transfer 6 mL of LB+ ampicillin medium on one 15 cm plate, scrape the colonies off with a cell spreader. Then use another 6 mL of LB+ ampicillin medium to wash the plate. Collect all colonies and proceed to max-prep according to QIAGEN Plasmid Kit procedures. Optional: Sanger Sequencing. To ensure the success of gRNA pool’s insertion, pick several colonies from the plate (2d or 2e) to 4 mL LB culture with ampicillin. Shake the tubes for at least 8 h at 37°C. Use QIAGEN Mini Prep Kit to extract the plasmid and send it to Sanger Sequencing with primer of PCR1_F.

(Day 2–3) Insert second set of gRNAs: The process is similar to step 2 of inserting first set of gRNA pools. The only difference is the enzyme digestion setting. Vector linearization: assemble the following reaction on ice and incubate at 55°C for 1 h. Enzyme digestion reaction master mix Reagent Amount Vector: LentiGuide_DKO+1 st gRNA 2 μg BsmbI_v2 1 μL 10× N NEBuffer r3.1 5 μL ddH 2 O To 50 μL Then run out the sample on an EZ-VISION Blue-Light DNA Dye -stained 1% agarose gel. Extract linearized DNA using QIAGEN Gel Extraction Kit, elute with 30 μL ddH 2 O. PCR purification kit could be used on the enzyme digestion product to save time.

Repeat HiFi assembling as steps 2b to 2f. Harvest colonies and max prep are the same as step2. Note: Step 3 usually requires much more reactions to reach appropriate library coverage, considering the combination of 2 sgRNAs. For example, for two sets of gRNA pools with 250 different gRNAs, there will be 62,500 combinations. To reach 10 coverages for each gRNA-gRNA construction, 20 electroporate reactions (62500∗10/30,000) are needed.

Library QC: Directly proceed to steps 20–24 and data analysis part. Sequencing the whole library at least 50× coverage (refer to step 23 for how to choose sequencing service) for the QC purpose. Pay attention to mapping ratio, number/percentage of missed gRNA pairs, the distribution and evenness of the gRNA-gRNA constructions.

Library amplification: depending on the experiment designs, such as cell coverage and number of replicates, the amount of library generated from steps 1–3 might be insufficient. In this case, amplify the pooled library by electroporation as described in steps 2c–2e. Instead of using 1 μL of HiFi reaction product, dilute the pooled library to 50 ng/μL and then use 2 μL 50 ng/μL pooled library for each reaction.

Day 1. Seed 8–9 × 10 6 /15 cm plate 293T packaging cells (recommended: passage number < 15) in 20 mL none-antibiotic growth media. For 1 L virus production prepare ∼60 plates. Incubate cells for 24 h (37°C, 5% CO 2 ), or until the following afternoon. The cells should be ∼70% confluent at the moment of transfection.

Day 2 afternoon. Transfect 293T cells: Prepare the mixture of 3 transfection plasmid (∼1:1:1 molar ratio) in OptiMEM: Transfection plasmid mix (per 15 cm plate) Plasmid Mass Amount per plate Total amount psPAX2 6.5 μg per plate pMD2.G 3.6 μg per plate Library 5.4 μg per plate OptiMEM – To 200 μL

For each plate, prepare one 1× OptiMEM and X-tremeGENE transfection reagent (i.e., 60 tubes OptiMEM and X-tremeGENE mix will be needed for 60 plates): directly add 48 μL X-tremeGENE into the tubes with 600 μL OptiMEM, and gently mix them by flicking the tubes. CRITICAL: Do not mix by pipetting or vertex here.
After the incubation, add 200 μL OptiMEM with transfection plasmids drop-wise to each tube with OptiMEM and X-tremeGENE dilutions (1:3 ratio), then mix gently.
Carefully transfer the transfection mix to 293T packaging cells by drop-wise adding in circular motion.
Incubate cells for 18 h (37°C, 5% CO 2 ).

background: The experiment is in the CEL-Seq2 library-preparation stage for single-embryo RNA-seq. Primer stocks and ERCC spike-in material are being organized so that barcoded source plates can be prepared for downstream reverse transcription setup. At this point, the key needs are to maintain cold handling, generate the required ERCC dilution state for the spike-in/dNTP master mix, briefly mix and collect thawed reagents, and then preserve the prepared materials at the specified freezer conditions.
goal: Prepare and preserve the ERCC spike-in reagent states and the Spike-In-dNTPs master mix needed for barcoded source plate setup, while keeping the materials cold and ensuring the final mix is ready for distribution into source plates.
constraints: Undiluted primers must be stored at -80 C, while 10 uM primer aliquots must be stored at -20 C.
The ERCC spike-in must reach a final 1:100000 dilution through an intermediate 1:1000 dilution state before being used in the master mix.
When ERCC is first handled, create two aliquots of 2 uL from the undiluted stock to reduce freeze-thaw exposure.
Prepare three 200 uL aliquots of the 1:1000 ERCC dilution for storage; leftover diluted material is not retained.
Store undiluted ERCC material and retained ERCC aliquots at -80 C.
The Spike-In-dNTPs master mix total volume is 3600 uL and is composed of 2520 uL nuclease-free water, 720 uL of 1:100000 ERCC dilution, and 360 uL dNTP mix.
Reagents for the master mix are thawed on ice, then briefly mixed and briefly spun at no more than 2000 x g before and after master-mix assembly.
The completed master mix must be kept on ice before use in source plates.

stored_undiluted_primers = store_sample(sample=undiluted_primers, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_h=24.0, container='freezer_box')

iced_spikein_dntps_master_mix = cool_sample(sample=centrifuged_master_mix, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=5.0, device='ice_bucket')

source_steps.description: Store undiluted primers at −80°C, and 10 μM aliquots at −20°C.

Prepare working plates for RNA-seq. Prepare a serial dilution of ERCC RNA Spike-In Mix in nuclease-free water to reach a 1:100,000 final dilution. For example, add 1 μL ERCC RNA Spike-In Mix in 999 μL nuclease-free water to get a 1:1,000 dilution. Then, add 10 μL diluted ERCC RNA Spike-In (1:1,000) to 990 μL nuclease-free water to get a 1:100,000 dilution. To avoid unnecessary freeze-thaw cycles, prepare two 2 μL aliquots of the undiluted ERCC RNA Spike-In mix the first time you use it.
Make three 200 μL aliquots of 1:1,000 diluted mix for storage and discard the rest. Note the expiration date for the original reagent on the lid of the tube.
Store undiluted reagent and aliquots at −80°C. Any remaining diluted mix can be discarded.
Prepare 3600 μL Spike-In-dNTPs master mix. Thaw reagents on ice, vortex for 5 s, and spin them for 5 s at ≤2000 × g .
Prepare Spike-In-dNTPs master as described in the table below.
Vortex for 5 s and spin it for 5 s at ≤2000 × g .
Place master mix on ice. Spike-In-dNTPs master mix Reagent Amount Nuclease-free water 2520 μL ERCC RNA Spike-In Mix (1:100,000) 720 μL dNTP Mix (10 mM/each) 360 μL Total 3600 μL
Transfer Spike-In-dNTPs master mix into source plates. Pour the 3.6 mL Spike-In-dNTPs master mix into a multi-channel reagent reservoir.
Pipette 15 μL Spike-In-dNTPs master mix into each well of two new 96-well I.DOT source plates, using a multi-channel pipette.
Keep samples on ice during transfer. Use a clean empty 96-well plate as a holder for the source plates to avoid direct contact of the pore with the ice.

Transfer 3 μL of each 1 μM CEL-Seq2 RT primer into a different well of the two 96-well I.DOT source plates containing the Spike-In-dNTP mix. The resulting two 96-well plates are your barcoded-primer source plate #1 and #2 for the next steps. Pipette primers using a multichannel pipette. Change tips every time.

background: A pooled sgRNA library targeting lysine codons has already been amplified and purified. At this stage, the workflow focuses on processing the Golden Gate assembly mixture in the thermal cycler and then preparing the assembled plasmid library for downstream cloning continuity by cooling or holding the product under the correct conditions.
goal: Execute the thermal-cycling portion of the Golden Gate assembly for the sgRNA library plasmid cloning stage and leave the assembled product in an appropriate post-run state for subsequent purification.
constraints: Use the Golden Gate assembly reaction as the starting material and keep the workflow centered on the thermal cycler stage of cloning.
The programmed temperature scheme must capture alternating digestion and ligation phases with 20 cycles, using 37°C for 5 min and 16°C for 5 min, followed by a final 37°C incubation for 5 min.
The reaction vessel should be in a sealed state before thermocycling.
The thermal cycler lid must be explicitly opened before loading and closed before the run.
A post-run cold hold or equivalent cooled state at 4°C should be established for the assembled product.
Use only provided action-pool functions and keyword arguments.
Create a dependency chain in which later steps consume outputs from earlier steps rather than restarting from raw inputs.
Include repeated action families, with at least one family varying by only one or two numeric settings.

available_inputs.description: Prepared Golden Gate assembly reaction containing vector, purified sgRNA PCR inserts, enzymes, buffer, ATP, DTT, and water.

available_inputs.description: Plate or tube strip holding the Golden Gate reactions for thermal cycling.

available_inputs.name: thermal_cycler
available_inputs.description: Thermal cycler used to run the Golden Gate assembly program.

available_inputs.name: cold_block
available_inputs.description: Cooling device or chilled location used to maintain the reaction at 4°C after cycling.

gold_program:
sealed_gg_plate = seal_plate(plate=gg_plate, seal_type='adhesive film', pressure='firm', duration_s=5.0)
opened_tc = open_thermal_cycler_lid(instrument=thermal_cycler, latch_state='unlocked', plate=sealed_gg_plate)
cooled_loaded_plate = cool_sample(sample=sealed_gg_plate, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=2.0, device=cold_block)
closed_tc = close_thermal_cycler_lid(instrument=thermal_cycler, latch_state='locked', plate=cooled_loaded_plate)
thermocycled_assembly = run_thermal_cycler(plate=cooled_loaded_plate, program_name='Golden_Gate_37C_16C_20x_final_37C', cycles=20, lid_temperature_c=105.0)
postrun_cool = cool_sample(sample=thermocycled_assembly, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=5.0, device=thermal_cycler)
opened_tc_after_run = open_thermal_cycler_lid(instrument=thermal_cycler, latch_state='unlocked', plate=postrun_cool)
held_product = store_sample(sample=postrun_cool, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=1.0, container='thermal_cycler_hold')

source_steps.description: Amplify the sgRNA oligos under the following conditions: PCR reaction master mix Reagent Amount DNA template 1,000-fold coverage of the library in 2.5 μL NEBNext Ultra II Q5 Master Mix 25 μL Forward primer 5 μL Reverse primer 5 μL ddH 2 O 12.5 μL Total volume 50 μL PCR cycling conditions Steps Temperature Time Cycles Initial Denaturation 98°C 30 s 1 Denaturation 98°C 10 s 20 cycles Annealing 69°C 20 s Extension 72°C 10 s Final extension 72°C 2 min 1 Hold 4°C forever

Purify the PCR products using the DNA Clean & Concentrator-5 according to the manufacturer’s protocol ( https://files.zymoresearch.com/protocols/_d4003t_d4003_d4004_d4013_d4014_dna_clean_concentrator_-5.pdf ), and adjust the concentration to 10 ng/μL.

Ligate the purified fragments into pCG_2.0_SV40_Puro_iBAR vectors using Golden Gate assembly ( Figure 3 B). Prepare the Golden Gate reaction mix and perform the reaction as follows: Golden Gate assembly reaction master mix Reagent Amount 10 × reactions Vector (100 ng/μL) 2 μL 20 μL Purified sgRNA oligo PCR products (10 ng/μL) 4 μL 40 μL DTT (50 mM) 0.4 μL 4 μL ATP (10 mM) 2 μL 20 μL BsmBI 1.5 μL 15 μL T4 DNA ligase 0.5 μL 5 μL Tango buffer 2 μL 20 μL ddH 2 O 7.6 μL 76 μL Total volume 20 μL 200 μL Golden Gate assembly conditions Steps Temperature Time Cycles Digestion 37°C 5 min 20 cycles Ligation 16°C 5 min Final incubation 37°C 5 min 1 Hold 4°C forever
Purify the Golden Gate assembly products using the DNA Clean & Concentrator-5 and adjust the concentration to 100 ng/μL.

Transform E. coli with the purified Golden Gate products by electroporation ( Figure 3 C). Note: Use the following formula to calculate the number of reactions required: N u m b e r o f r e a c t i o n s = s g R N A c o u n t × P l a s m i d l i b r a r y c o v e r a g e T r a n s f o r m a t i o n e f f i c i e n c y Where the transformation efficiency is the number of transformants produced per reaction and should be determined beforehand. For the E. coli HST08 premium electro-cells used in this protocol, the transformation efficiency is 2 × 10 6 . Each reaction consists of 50 μL of electro-cells and 1 μL (100 ng) of purified Golden Gate products, recovered in 1 mL of SOC medium (included with the E. coli HST08 premium electro-cells kit). In this protocol, the plasmid library coverage is set to 300-fold, and the number of reactions is calculated to be approximately 43 (45–50 reactions are needed for potential loss during subesequent steps). Note: Pre-chill the cuvettes and SOC medium on ice before starting. Thaw the E. coli HST08 premium electro-cells on ice just before use.
For each reaction, add 1 μL of the purified Golden Gate products to 50 μL of the thawed cell suspension.
Transfer the mixture to a chilled electroporation cuvette and incubate on ice for 2 min. Note: Avoid introducing bubbles during the transfer.

Set up the Gene Pulser Xcell Microbial System for electroporation with the following conditions: Electroporation condition Parameter Value Voltage 1.5 kV Capacitance 25 μF Resistance 200 Ω Cuvette 1 mm
Place the cuvette in the ShockPod, close the chamber lid, and apply the pulse.
Immediately add 1 mL of prechilled SOC medium to the pulsed cuvette and gently mix by pipetting. CRITICAL: The short time between pulsing and adding SOC medium is crucial for optimal cell recovery.
Transfer the cell suspension to a Falcon 14 mL Round Bottom High Clarity PP Test Tube and incubate at 37°C with shaking at 225 rpm for 1 h.

Mix all cell suspensions and incubate in liquid LB medium at 37°C with shaking at 225 rpm for 15–16 h. Note: Before mixing, take several tubes and spread a diluted cell suspension (e.g., 1 μL in 200 μL of liquid LB) onto an LB agar plate to confirm the transformation efficiency.

background: A chemokine receptor bound to 1D4 affinity resin is at the nanodisc reconstitution stage. In this segment, the resin is washed and divided into two low-bind tubes, then exposed sequentially to lipid mixture, MSP1D1 scaffold protein, and Bio-Beads for detergent removal under cold rotating conditions. The receptor is then released from the resin with 1D4 peptide and the eluate is collected into a 15 mL tube for refrigerated holding.
goal: Execute the local on-bead reconstitution and recovery segment so that the receptor is assembled into nanodiscs on resin, detergent is removed, and the final eluate is pooled into a conical tube for short-term storage.
constraints: Use only cold conditions specified for this segment: all spins and incubations are at 4 C.
The first wash uses 20 mL Regular buffer followed by centrifugation at 300 x g for 5 min.
After washing, split the material into 2 aliquots in 2 mL Protein Lo-bind tubes, targeting about 250 uL resin per tube.
A subsequent wash uses 1 mL Regular buffer and the same centrifuge temperature and force, but only 1 min.
Each aliquot then receives 500 uL of diluted POPC:POPG lipid mixture at 2 mM total lipid and is rotated for 30 min.
After lipid loading and a low-speed clarification spin, each aliquot receives 500 uL MSP1D1 at 2 mg/mL and is rotated for 30 min.
Detergent removal is performed by adding about 20 mg washed Bio-Beads SM-2 resin and rotating for 12-16 h before another 1 min spin.
Use HBS buffer in the final cold wash/elution stage and elute with 500 uL of 200 uM 1D4 peptide in HBS buffer for 1 h.
Collect the supernatant into a 15 mL conical tube and keep the recovered nanodisc-solubilized receptor at 4 C.
Represent the paired 2-tube handling as one combined sample state rather than separate unsupported hardware manipulations.

step1 = add_reagent(sample=receptor_resin_tubes, reagent=regular_buffer, volume_ul=20000.0, mixing="resuspend")
step2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step1, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step3 = aliquot_sample(sample=step2, destination="2 mL Protein Lo-bind tubes", volume_ul=250.0, aliquot_count=2)
step4 = add_reagent(sample=step3, reagent=regular_buffer, volume_ul=1000.0, mixing="resuspend")
step5 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step4, speed_xg=300, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step6 = add_reagent(sample=step5, reagent=lipid_mix_2mM, volume_ul=500.0, mixing="gentle mix")
step7 = incubate_sample(sample=step6, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=30.0, condition="rotating")
step8 = add_reagent(sample=step7, reagent=msp1d1_2mgml, volume_ul=500.0, mixing="gentle mix")
step9 = incubate_sample(sample=step8, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=30.0, condition="rotating")
step10 = add_reagent(sample=step9, reagent=biobeads_sm2, volume_ul=20.0, mixing="gentle mix")
step11 = incubate_sample(sample=step10, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=720.0, condition="rotating")
step12 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step11, speed_xg=300, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step13 = collect_fraction(sample=step12, phase="supernatant", destination="15 mL conical tube", volume_ul=1000.0)
protocol_title: On-bead purification and nanodisc reconstitution of human chemokine receptor complexes for structural and biophysical studies

source_steps.description: Wash the resin in each tube with 20 mL Regular buffer; centrifuge for 5 min at 300 × g , 4°C and discard the supernatant.

Transfer the receptor-1D4-resin to two separate 2 mL Protein lo-bind tubes, wash one more time with 1 mL Regular buffer, centrifuge as above and discard the supernatant. The resin volume in each tube should be around 250 μL.
Dilute the POPC:POPG lipid stock to 2 mM (total lipid) with 1 mL Regular buffer, add 500 μL of the 2 mM lipid mixture to each centrifuge tube of receptor-1D4-resin, and rotate at 4°C for 30 min.
Centrifuge the lipid-receptor-1D4 resin mixture for 1 min at 300 × g and 4°C; discard the supernatant.
Add 500 μL 2 mg/mL MSP1D1 to each tube; rotate at 4°C for 30 min.
To remove detergent, add ∼20 mg of washed Bio-beads SM-2 resin to the MSP1D1-lipid-receptor-1D4 resin mixture; rotate at 4°C for 12–16 h.
Centrifuge the Bio-bead-MSP1D1-lipid-receptor-1D4 resin mixture for 1 min at 300 × g , 4°C; discard the supernatant.
Wash each tube of Bio-bead-MSP1D1-lipid-receptor-1D4 resin with 500 μL HBS buffer; centrifuge for 1 min at 300 × g , 4°C, and discard the supernatant.
Repeat step 16 twice.
To elute the nanodisc-solubilized receptor from the 1D4 resin, add 500 μL of 200 μM 1D4 peptide solubilized in HBS buffer to each tube and rotate at 4°C for 1 h.
To separate the nanodisc-solubilized receptor from the beads and the resin, centrifuge for 1 min at 300 × g , 4°C, and collect the supernatant from each 2 mL centrifuge tube in a 15 mL conical tube.

Repeat step 18–19 twice and combine all eluants in a single 15 mL conical tube for a total of approximately 3 mL of nanodisc-solubilized receptor. Store at 4°C for future use. The length of time the sample can be stored without loss of integrity is dependent on the receptor systems but in general it can be stored for at least a few days to a week.

background: Human fibroblasts that completed L-AHA incorporation are entering the harvest and click-labeling portion of a flow-cytometry workflow for measuring global protein synthesis. The sample must be converted from adherent cells into a washed, fixed, permeabilized, click-reacted suspension that is finally held cold and protected from light for later acquisition.
goal: Transform the L-AHA-treated adherent fibroblast sample into a final chilled suspension in FACS tubes after harvest, fixation, permeabilization, click labeling, and post-label cleanup.
constraints: Use 250 uL of prewarmed 0.25% trypsin-EDTA with a short 37 C incubation of about 3 min, then resuspend with 750 uL methionine-free medium before tube transfer.
Include an initial pellet at 300 x g for 5 min at 4 C and one cold DPBS wash with 1000 uL under the same centrifugation conditions.

Fix in 200 uL of 4% paraformaldehyde for 15 min at room temperature and then centrifuge at 800 x g for 10 min at 4 C; permeabilize in 200 uL Triton X-100 buffer for 15 min at room temperature before another 800 x g, 10 min, 4 C spin.
Expose the permeabilized pellet to 200 uL click reaction solution for 30 min at room temperature with light protection maintained thereafter.
After click labeling, perform the post-reaction cleanup using 1% BSA in DPBS and finish in 500 uL 1% FBS in DPBS on ice in the dark.
Keep a single dependency chain, reuse at least two action families multiple times, and do not introduce operations outside the provided action pool.

step1 = add_reagent(sample=cells_well, reagent=trypsin_edta_025, volume_ul=250.0, mixing='cover cell layer')
step2 = incubate_sample(sample=step1, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=3.0, condition='static')
step3 = add_reagent(sample=step2, reagent=met_free_medium, volume_ul=750.0, mixing='pipette to resuspend')
step4 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step3, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step5 = add_reagent(sample=step4, reagent=dpbs_cold, volume_ul=1000.0, mixing='resuspend pellet')
step6 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step5, speed_xg=300, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step7 = add_reagent(sample=step6, reagent=pfa_4pct, volume_ul=200.0, mixing='resuspend pellet')
step8 = incubate_sample(sample=step7, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=15.0, condition='static')
step9 = add_reagent(sample=step8, reagent=perm_buffer, volume_ul=200.0, mixing='resuspend pellet after fixation pellet recovery')
step10 = incubate_sample(sample=step9, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=15.0, condition='static')
step11 = add_reagent(sample=step10, reagent=click_solution, volume_ul=200.0, mixing='resuspend pellet for click reaction')
step12 = incubate_sample(sample=step11, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=30.0, condition='protected from light')
protocol_title: A flow-cytometry-based assessment of global protein synthesis in human senescent cells

source_steps.description: Discard the L-AHA culture medium and briefly rinse cells with 1–2 mL prewarmed 1 × DPBS per well to remove all traces of serum.
Add 250 μL prewarmed trypsin-EDTA (0.25%) to cells and hold at 37°C for 2–3 min until cell layer is dispersed.
Add 750 μL prewarmed methionine-free medium to cells and gently pipette 10 to 15 times to resuspend cells.
Collect cell suspension in U-bottom FACS tubes and centrifuge at 300 × g , 4°C for 5 min. Discard supernatants.
Wash cells with cold 1 × DPBS: resuspend cells in 1 mL cold 1 × DBPS per tube; centrifuge at 300 × g , 4°C for 5 min; and discard supernatants.

Fix cells with paraformaldehyde: resuspend cells in 200 μL of 4% paraformaldehyde (w/v in DPBS) per tube; incubate cells for 15 min at room temperature (20°C–25°C); centrifuge at 800 × g , 4°C for 10 min; and gently discard supernatants by pipetting.

Permeabilize cells with the permeabilization buffer: resuspend cells in 200 μL of permeabilization buffer (1 × Triton X-100 [0.5%]) per tube; incubate cells for 15 min at room temperature (20°C–25°C); centrifuge at 800 × g , 4°C for 10 min; and gently discard supernatants by pipetting.
Prepare the L-AHA click reaction cocktail as described in materials and equipment .

Add 200 μL L-AHA click reaction solution per tube. Resuspend the cell pellet and incubate for 30 min at room temperature (20°C–25°C). Make sure to protect the sample from light (e.g., wrap sample tubes with aluminum foil when not in use) during the following steps.

Wash cells twice with 1% BSA in 1 × DPBS: resuspend cells in with 500 μL 1% BSA in 1 × DPBS per tube; centrifuge at 800 × g , 4°C for 10 min; gently discard supernatants by pipetting; and repeat this washing step once more.
Resuspend cells in 500 μL 1% FBS in 1 × DBPS. Place all tubes on ice in the dark.

Using the control unstained samples, draw the gate by the forward (FSC-A) and side scatter profiling (SSC-A) that defines the main live cell population, excluding cellular debris and dead cells with the Attune Nxt Software (v3.1.2) ( Figure 3 ).

background: This item covers the bead-based cleanup and split of multiplexed PCR1 material during library preparation on the Rhapsody workflow. A mixed PCR1 product is combined with SPRISelect beads so that the targeted mRNA fraction remains bead-associated while the AbSeq and sample tag fraction stays in the supernatant. The task focuses on separating these fractions and carrying out the wash, dry, resuspension, brief room-temperature incubation, and quick spin needed to recover the mRNA-targeted PCR1 products while keeping the non-mRNA supernatant available.
goal: Process the PCR1 cleanup segment to isolate the bead-bound targeted mRNA PCR1 material and preserve the supernatant fraction for later handling, ending with the eluted mRNA-targeted product ready after the brief post-elution spin.
constraints: Use the initial PCR1 product tube and SPRISelect beads as the starting materials.
The bead addition corresponds to 140 uL beads with 200 uL PCR1 product, followed by pipette-style mixing equivalent to 10 mixes.
Include a room-temperature incubation for 5 min after bead mixing; room temperature should be represented within the 15-25 C range.
Perform magnetic separation for 5 min and retain the supernatant fraction in a new 1.5 mL tube for temporary room-temperature holding while the mRNA fraction is purified.
The mRNA-targeted fraction is the bead-bound phase after magnetic separation and must remain associated with beads through the ethanol washes.
Wash the bead-bound mRNA fraction twice with fresh 80% ethanol, using 500 uL each wash and a 30 s contact time while on the magnet.
After washes, remove residual liquid, then air-dry the beads at room temperature for 5 min without overheating.
Elute the bead pellet in 30 uL elution buffer by vigorous pipette mixing until uniformly dispersed.
After elution, incubate at room temperature for 2 min and then perform a brief centrifugation.
Use only action-pool functions and keep the workflow at protocol level rather than explicit liquid-transfer robotics.

gold_program:
mixed_cleanup = mix_sample(sample=pcr1_products, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="vigorous")
bead_binding = incubate_sample(sample=mixed_cleanup, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=5.0, condition="static")
magnet_split = magnetic_separate(sample=bead_binding, rack="magnetic rack", duration_min=5.0, collect_phase="supernatant")
stored_supernatant = store_sample(sample=magnet_split, temperature_c=25.0, duration_h=1.0, container=new_tube)
ethanol_wash_1 = incubate_sample(sample=magnet_split, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=0.5, condition="80% ethanol wash on magnet")
ethanol_wash_2 = incubate_sample(sample=ethanol_wash_1, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=0.5, condition="80% ethanol wash on magnet")
air_dried_beads = dry_sample(sample=ethanol_wash_2, method="air dry", temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=5.0)
eluted_beads = mix_sample(sample=air_dried_beads, method="pipetting", duration_s=20.0, speed="vigorous")
post_elution_incubation = incubate_sample(sample=eluted_beads, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=2.0, condition="static")
final_mrna_pcr1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=post_elution_incubation, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=25.0)

background: Primary hippocampal neurons have already been metabolically labeled with an alkynyl fatty acid and harvested into ice-cold EDTA-free modified RIPA buffer containing protease inhibitors and Palmostatin B in 1.5 mL tubes. The immediate task is the pre-click lysate handling segment: resume any paused samples, keep them cold, allow detergent extraction with gentle rotation at 4 C, then clarify the lysate by a cold high-speed centrifugation and retain the cleared supernatant for the next chemistry step.
goal: Prepare harvested neuronal lysate for click chemistry by carrying out the cold equilibration and clarification segment so that a cleared supernatant is ready for downstream use.
constraints: Only use lysate compatible with click chemistry, meaning no EDTA or EGTA-containing harvest buffer.
A pause point is allowed at -20 C, and any stored lysate must be thawed on ice before further processing.
Maintain cold handling at 4 C for the extraction period and centrifugation workflow.
Include a 30 min mixed incubation corresponding to tube rotation at 12 rpm at 4 C.
Clarify by centrifuging at 17000 x g for 10 min at 4 C and carry forward the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.
Keep the plan within this local lysate-preparation segment and use outputs from earlier actions as inputs to later ones.

available_inputs.description: Harvested primary hippocampal neuron lysate in EDTA-free modified RIPA buffer with PIC and Palmostatin B.

available_inputs.name: ice_bath
available_inputs.description: Ice-based cooling setup for thawing and keeping samples cold.

available_inputs.name: cold_storage
available_inputs.description: Freezer location for optional -20 C pause-point storage.

gold_program:
stored_lysate = store_sample(sample=neuronal_lysate, temperature_c=-20.0, duration_h=1.0, container=cold_storage)
thawed_lysate = cool_sample(sample=stored_lysate, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=10.0, device=ice_bath)
chilled_lysate = cool_sample(sample=thawed_lysate, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=5.0, device=ice_bath)
pre_rotation_mix = mix_sample(sample=chilled_lysate, method="inversion", duration_s=5.0, speed="gentle")
rotated_lysate = incubate_sample(sample=pre_rotation_mix, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=30.0, condition="rotating at 12 rpm")
post_rotation_hold = cool_sample(sample=rotated_lysate, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=2.0, device=ice_bath)
pre_spin_mix = mix_sample(sample=post_rotation_hold, method="inversion", duration_s=5.0, speed="gentle")
clarified_lysate = centrifuge_sample(sample=pre_spin_mix, speed_xg=17000, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
supernatant_ready = cool_sample(sample=clarified_lysate, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=1.0, device=ice_bath)
