source_steps.description: Add an equal volume of 120 mM KOH to the fatty acid and gently tap vial to mix, do not pipette. Note: The minimum recommended volume for saponification is 4 μL, i.e., 2 μL of fatty acid plus 2 μL of KOH.

Saponify fatty acids by incubating at 70°C for 30 s –3 min. Submerge the vials in water in a heat block with 25 mm slots or in a water bath. Note: Saponification time varies depending on fatty-acid length and volume. Check after 30 s by gently tapping the vial to mix. 16-carbon chain fatty acids take approximately 1.25 min to saponify, while 18-carbon chain fatty acids take approximately 3 min. Fatty acids will change from an opaque white color to clear and colorless when saponified. CRITICAL: Proceed to addition of FAF-BSA as soon as the fatty acid-KOH mix turns clear. If it is incubated at 70°C for too long, the DMSO will evaporate, and the fatty acid will precipitate. If this occurs, it cannot be re-saponified, prepare fresh fatty acid-KOH mix.

Add FAF-BSA without letting saponified fatty acids cool down. Note: Some precipitation of BSA at this point due to heat will occur, the precipitate should go back into solution during the binding step. For immortal cells use 1% final FAF-BSA concentration, i.e., for every 2 μL of fatty acid and 2 μL of KOH add 100 μL of FAF-BSA for 2 mL culture volume, scaled up or down according to desired culture plate/well size.

For neuronal cultures use 0.5% final FAF-BSA concentration, i.e., for every 2 μL of fatty acid and 2 μL of KOH add 50 μL of FAF-BSA for 2 mL culture volume, scaled up or down according to desired culture plate/well size.

Incubate for 15 min at 37°C to bind the fatty acid to BSA. Note: 15 min is the minimum time. As this solution is stable for extended periods of time, it is acceptable if it remains at 37°C for longer (up to an hour).

Label cells. Add 100 μL (immortal) or 50 μL (neuronal cultures) of saponified fatty acid/FAF-BSA mix for every 2 mL media to cells dropwise across the surface of the plate. Note: To increase uptake of fatty acids when labeling immortalized cells, prior to adding the fatty acids, wash the cells with 1× PBS once, and replace regular media with delipidated media for 30–60 min. Delipidated media contains 5% charcoal stripped serum (instead of 10% regular serum), which allows for the depletion of fatty acids from the cells without inducing autophagy and cell death while conserving the expensive charcoal stripped serum. In addition, at this point the plate/well volume can be reduced to further conserve the delipidated media, i.e., for a 10-cm plate use 4 mL of delipidated media or for a 60-mm plate use 2 mL delipidated media. Note: Delipidated media should not be used for primary neuronal cultures that are cultured in Neurobasal media with B27 as a full media change can stress neurons. In addition, Neurobasal media with B27 does not contain saturated fatty acids so no delipidated media is required. CRITICAL: Adding fatty acids drop-wise reduces excessive toxicity/cells stress and reduced lipid solubility caused by areas of high fatty acid concentration and ensures even distribution across the entire well/plate.

Incubate 1–16 h. Note: Labeling time depends on the experimental set-up and the protein(s) of interest. We recommend first testing a 4–7 h. labeling period. A protein with a short S-acylation half-life such as Ras can be labeled in as little as 5 min to 1 h, while proteins that are S-acylated more stably and slowly can be labeled for 8–12 h. CRITICAL: To avoid fatty acid β-oxidation, we do not recommend labeling cells for more than 16 h. 8 , 9

Harvest cells. Following incubation, aspirate the media, wash cells with 1× PBS (immortal cultures) or 1× Recording Buffer + 1 mM MgCl2 (neuronal cultures; 25 mM HEPES pH7.4, 120 mM NaCl, 5 mM KCl, 2 mM CaCl 2 , 30 mM Glucose). Note: The buffer used to wash the cells can be any buffer that is commonly used for the given cell type.

Lyse cells in an appropriate volume of ice-cold EDTA-free modified RIPA Buffer, supplemented with PIC and Palmostatin B and collect into 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes on ice. Note: The volume of lysis buffer will depend on the seeding density and cell type, i.e., for neurons we use 300–500 μL per well of a 6-well plate (seeded at a density of 30,000 cells/cm 2 ) or 500–750 μL per 60 mm dish of HEK293T cells that are 90% confluent. Note: Palmostatin B is a potent inhibitor of acyl-protein thioesterases 1 and 2 and it also targets other serine hydrolases. 16 , 17 We recommend adding it to prevent potential de-acylation due to residual activity of de-S-acylating enzymes in the lysis buffer. Alternatives: We recommend testing lysis buffers with different detergents to optimize solubilization of the protein of interest. We have successfully used 2% SDS lysis buffer (2% SDS, 50 mM HEPES or Tris pH 7.4) and 1% β-D-maltoside in PBS. Indeed, we were able to successfully detect S-acylation using 2% SDS buffer for a protein that was previously not detectable using the RIPA buffer. CRITICAL: Do not harvest in buffers that contain copper chelators, such as EDTA or EGTA, that will interfere with the click reaction. Pause point: Samples can be stored at −20°C until ready to proceed.
Prepare lysates. If frozen, thaw cell lysates on ice.
Rotate cell lysates for ½ h at 4°C at 12 rpm on a tube revolver.
Spin down lysates at 17,000 × g for 10 min at 4°C and carefully remove the supernatant taking care not to disturb the pellet.

background: This protocol segment focuses on extracting circulating small RNAs from clarified human plasma using a spin-column workflow. The plasma is combined with lysis buffer and ethanol to create binding conditions, then transferred onto a column, washed repeatedly, dried by an empty spin, and exposed to a small-volume elution buffer before a staged recovery spin and a second brief contact with the column.
goal: Carry out the extraction-stage handling from clarified plasma through column cleanup and first elution cycle, ending with the eluate placed back on the column for a short room-temperature incubation.
constraints: Begin with 200 uL of centrifuged plasma.
Use 600 uL of lysis buffer A plus beta-mercaptoethanol and 800 uL of 96-100% ethanol, with 10 s vortex mixing after each addition.
Capture the repeated 650 uL column-loading behavior in a compressed but still valid action sequence tied to a 3300 x g spin for 2 min at room temperature.
Represent three total washes using Wash Solution A, each equivalent to 400 uL and associated with 3300 x g centrifugation for 0.5 min at room temperature.
Include an empty-column spin at 13000 x g for 2 min before elution.
Use 20 uL of Elution Solution A followed by a 2 min static room-temperature incubation.
Model the staged elution centrifugation as 1 min at 400 x g followed by 2 min at 5800 x g.
After elution, place the recovered volume back onto the same column and incubate it for 2 min at room temperature.
Use only provided action-pool functions and keep later steps dependent on earlier outputs.

available_inputs.description: Centrifuged human plasma sample for RNA extraction

available_inputs.description: Lysis Buffer A supplemented with beta-mercaptoethanol

available_inputs.name: ethanol_100
available_inputs.description: 96-100% ethanol

available_inputs.name: spin_column
available_inputs.description: Micro Spin column assembly

available_inputs.name: wash_solution_a
available_inputs.description: Wash Solution A for column washing

available_inputs.description: Elution Solution A for RNA recovery

gold_program:
step1 = aliquot_sample(sample=plasma_sample, destination=spin_column, volume_ul=200.0, aliquot_count=1)
step2 = add_reagent(sample=step1, reagent=lysis_buffer_bme, volume_ul=600.0, mixing="none")
step3 = vortex_mix(sample=step2, duration_s=10.0, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=22.0)
step4 = add_reagent(sample=step3, reagent=ethanol_100, volume_ul=800.0, mixing="none")
step5 = vortex_mix(sample=step4, duration_s=10.0, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=22.0)
step6 = aliquot_sample(sample=step5, destination=spin_column, volume_ul=650.0, aliquot_count=3)
step7 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step6, speed_xg=3300, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=22.0)
step8 = add_reagent(sample=step7, reagent=wash_solution_a, volume_ul=1200.0, mixing="none")
step9 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step8, speed_xg=3300, duration_min=1.5, temperature_c=22.0)
step10 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step9, speed_xg=13000, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=22.0)
step11 = add_reagent(sample=step10, reagent=elution_solution_a, volume_ul=20.0, mixing="none")
step12 = incubate_sample(sample=step11, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=2.0, condition="static")
step13 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step12, speed_xg=5800, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=22.0)

source_steps.description: Transfer 200 μL of centrifuged plasma sample into a new 2.0 mL tube (sterile, nuclease-free, pyrogen-free).
Add a volume of 600 μL of Lysis Buffer A + β-Mercaptoethanol. Mix well by vortexing for 10 s.
Add 800 μL of 96–100% ethanol. Mix well by vortexing for 10 s.

Transfer 650 μL of the resulting mixture into a Micro Spin column (included in Norgen kit). Centrifuge 3,300× g for 2 min. Discard the flow-through and reassemble the spin column with its collection tube.
Repeat this step twice more, until the whole mixture has been transferred to the Micro Spin column.
Add 400 μL of Wash Solution A to the column and centrifuge for 30 s at 3,300× g ; discard the flow-through and reassemble the spin column with its collection tube.
Repeat this step twice more, for a total of three washes.
Spin the column, empty, for 2 min at 13,000× g . Discard the collection tube.
Transfer the spin column to a fresh 1.5 mL elution tube.
Elute by adding 20 μL of Elution Solution A to the column and let stand at room temperature for 2 min.
Centrifuge for 1 min at 400× g , followed by 2 min at 5,800× g .
Transfer the eluted volume back to the column and let stand at room temperature for 2 min.

background: This protocol stage prepares the ATAC-seq fraction after earlier transposition and before downstream cleanup. The DNA is being recovered on a MinElute column, washed to remove contaminants and ethanol, eluted in a small volume, and then amplified in a thermocycler with indexed primers and high-fidelity master mix to generate an ATAC library from limited-input material.
goal: Complete the column-based recovery of ATAC DNA through elution and then run the indexed PCR amplification program for the purified ATAC library.
constraints: Use the MinElute-bound ATAC DNA sample as the starting material for spin-column processing.
Perform two room-temperature centrifugation steps at 17900 x g for 1 minute during purification, with the later spin representing the extra drying spin after wash removal.
Include a brief 1 minute room-temperature wait after adding the elution buffer to the membrane before the elution spin.
The elution uses 11 uL Buffer EB and should produce the purified ATAC DNA that feeds directly into PCR setup.
The PCR reaction corresponds to a 50 uL amplification using purified ATAC DNA together with universal i5 primer, index i7 primer, and NEBNext HiFi 2x master mix.
Before thermocycling, the PCR vessel must be closed or sealed appropriately for a thermal cycler run.
Run the predefined ATAC PCR program on a thermal cycler; cycle count is input-dependent and should remain a variable rather than a fixed literal.
Keep the task within purification and PCR amplification only; do not continue into bead cleanup.

available_inputs.name: buffer_pe_washed_column

available_inputs.name: elution_ready_column
available_inputs.description: The washed and dried MinElute column transferred to a clean 1.5 mL tube and ready for Buffer EB elution.

available_inputs.name: pcr_tube

gold_program:
spin_bound_column = centrifuge_sample(sample=atac_minelute_column, speed_xg=17900, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
wash_incubation = incubate_sample(sample=buffer_pe_washed_column, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=1.0, condition="static")
spin_washed_column = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_incubation, speed_xg=17900, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
dry_spin_column = centrifuge_sample(sample=spin_washed_column, speed_xg=17900, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
elution_wait = incubate_sample(sample=elution_ready_column, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=1.0, condition="static")
eluted_atac_dna = centrifuge_sample(sample=elution_wait, speed_xg=17900, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
sealed_pcr_tube = seal_plate(plate=pcr_tube, seal_type="cap", pressure="firm", duration_s=2.0)
cycler_open = open_thermal_cycler_lid(instrument="thermal_cycler", latch_state="unlocked", plate=thermocycler_plate)
cycler_closed = close_thermal_cycler_lid(instrument="thermal_cycler", latch_state="locked", plate=sealed_pcr_tube)
atac_pcr_product = run_thermal_cycler(plate=cycler_closed, program_name="ATAC PCR", cycles=atac_pcr_cycles, lid_temperature_c=105.0)

source_steps.description: Purify ATAC-DNA from step 12 using Qiagen MinElute PCR Purification Kit. Mix and apply the sample to the MinElute column.
Centrifuge for 1 min at 17,900 × g at room temperature in a microcentrifuge.
Discard flow-through and place the MinElute column back into the same tube.
Add 750 μL Buffer PE to the MinElute column and centrifuge at 17,900 × g for 1 min. Note: Add ethanol (96%–100%) to Buffer PE before use (see bottle label for volume).

Discard flow-through and place the MinElute column back in the same tube. Centrifuge the column for an additional 1 min at maximum speed. CRITICAL: Residual ethanol from Buffer PE will not be completely removed unless the flowthrough is discarded before this additional centrifugation.
Place the MinElute column in a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

Add 11 μL Buffer EB (10 mM Tris·Cl, pH 8.5) to the center of the membrane to elute DNA. Let the column stand for 1 min, and then centrifuge for 1 min. CRITICAL: Ensure that the elution buffer is dispensed directly onto the center of the membrane for complete elution of bound DNA.
Repeat step 42 g once. Pause point: If you are stopping, store the purified ATAC-DNA at −20°C.

Amplify the purified ATAC-DNA with NEBNext® High-Fidelity 2× PCR Master Mix and ATAC-seq PCR primers ( Buenrostro et al., 2015 ). Add 20 μL DNA, 2.5 μL universal i5 primer (10 μM), 2.5 μL index i7 primer (10 μM), and 25 μL NEBNext HiFi 2× PCR Master Mix into a PCR tube. Note: If multiple samples are processed at the same time, make sure that each sample uses a different index i7 primer ( Buenrostro et al., 2015 ). If ATAC-seq and mRNA-seq libraries will be sequenced together, please follow Illumina Index Adapters Pooling Guide to select the indexes compatible with those used in step 38 for mRNA-seq libraries.

Mix, quick spin, then place the PCR tube in the preprogrammed thermocycler and run the “ATAC PCR” program. Note: The PCR cycles needed is dependent on the input amount. The optimal PCR cycles can be determined by qPCR amplification after initial 5 cycles according to Omni-ATAC protocol ( Corces et al., 2017 ).

background: Human A2A adenosine receptor membranes have already been resuspended and are entering the 19F-TET labeling stage for NMR sample preparation. The immediate work involves cold ultracentrifugation to pellet membranes, a low-temperature incubation with trifluoroethanethiol in high-salt buffer, and a second cold ultracentrifugation before the material is passed onward for downstream purification.
goal: Process the resuspended membrane preparation through the TET labeling substage so that a membrane pellet is recovered after labeling and is ready for subsequent purification.
constraints: Use ultracentrifugation at 200000 x g for both membrane pelleting operations.
Each centrifugation must run for 30 minutes at 4 C.
The labeling incubation must be performed at 4 C for 60 minutes.
The incubation state must correspond to membranes resuspended in high-salt buffer containing TET at a final amount equivalent to 1 mM per liter of cell culture.
A membrane pellet must be recovered after the first spin before labeling conditions are applied.
A second membrane pellet must be recovered after the TET incubation, representing the material advanced to the next purification stage.
Use only action-pool operations and keep the plan at protocol level rather than detailing manual liquid handling.

available_inputs.name: resuspended_membranes
available_inputs.description: Membrane preparation already resuspended prior to the TET labeling stage.

available_inputs.name: high_salt_buffered_membranes
available_inputs.description: Membranes resuspended in A2A AR high-salt buffer after the first pelleting step.

available_inputs.name: tet_labeled_membranes
available_inputs.description: High-salt membrane suspension supplemented with trifluoroethanethiol for 19F labeling.

gold_program:
spin1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=resuspended_membranes, speed_xg=200000, duration_min=30, temperature_c=4)
pellet1 = collect_fraction(sample=spin1, phase='pellet', destination='ultracentrifuge_tube', volume_ul=1000)
supernatant1 = collect_fraction(sample=spin1, phase='supernatant', destination='waste', volume_ul=1000)
label_ready = cool_sample(sample=high_salt_buffered_membranes, temperature_c=4, duration_min=5, device='cold_room')
labeling_incubation = incubate_sample(sample=tet_labeled_membranes, temperature_c=4, duration_min=60, condition='static')
spin2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=labeling_incubation, speed_xg=200000, duration_min=30, temperature_c=4)
final_pellet = collect_fraction(sample=spin2, phase='pellet', destination='purification_input_tube', volume_ul=1000)
final_supernatant = collect_fraction(sample=spin2, phase='supernatant', destination='waste', volume_ul=1000)
stored_pellet = store_sample(sample=final_pellet, temperature_c=4, duration_h=1, container='purification_input_tube')

source_steps.stage: A 2A AR 19 F-Trifluoroethanethiol (TET) labeling for NMR experiments
source_steps.description: Treat membranes with trifluoroethanethiol (TET): Pellet the resuspended membranes in an ultracentrifuge at 200,000 × g for 30 min at 4°C. Discard the supernatant and resuspend the membrane pellet in 100 mL A 2A AR High salt buffer. Add TET to a final concentration of 1 mM/ liter of cell culture. Incubate the sample at 4°C for 1 h. Pellet the membranes in an ultracentrifuge at 200,000 × g for 30 min and discard the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: A 2A AR 19 F-Trifluoroethanethiol (TET) labeling for NMR experiments
source_steps.description: Continue to steps 46–53 described in the section “ purification of A2AAR for nanodisc assembly .”

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Prepare lipids: Prepare 100 mM stocks of lipids in Nanodisc Cholate Buffer.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Prepare for nanodisc assembly: Calculate amounts of receptor, lipids and MSP1D1 required to form nanodiscs based on the reported optimized molar ratio of 1:5:250 (receptor:MSP1D1:lipids). An example calculation is provided below.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Initiate nanodisc assembly ( Figure 3 ): Add all the components to a microcentrifuge tube in the following order: Nanodisc Buffer, Nanodisc Cholate Buffer, lipids, MSPD1 and A 2A AR. Incubate the assembly mixture for 1–2 h with gentle rotation at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Remove detergent: Add 0.5–1 g Bio-Beads/mL of nanodisc assembly mixture. Incubate the sample for 12–14 h at 4°C with gentle rocking or rotation.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Remove the Bio-Beads: Use a syringe with a 20-gauge needle to collect the soluble fraction and apply to a fresh tube. Wash the Bio-Beads once with an equal volume of Nanodiscs Buffer and combine with the first collected aliquot.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Prepare Ni-NTA by washing it with 5 CVs of ddH 2 O. Add the Ni-NTA resin to the pooled sample and incubate it at 4°C for 12–14 h. For every 1 mL of assembled nanodisc solution add 250 μL Ni-NTA resin.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Add the fluorophore ( Figure 5 B): Add the Cy3 fluorescent dye at a 10× fold molar excess of receptor to the sample. Incubate at 4°C for 2–3 h with rotation.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Remove empty nanodiscs: Transfer the resin to a gravity flow column. Wash the resin with 3 CVs of Nanodisc Wash Buffer. Elute the nanodiscs containing A 2A AR using Nanodiscs Elution Buffer in 10 different 0.5 CV fractions. Measure the protein absorbance in each aliquot and pool the 3–4 aliquots with the highest protein concentration.

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Exchange the sample into the final buffer: Using a PD-10 column, follow the manufacturer’s protocol to exchange the sample into the Nanodiscs Desalting Buffer. https://cytiva-delivery.sitecorecontenthub.cloud/api/public/content/digi-11531-pdf .

source_steps.stage: Nanodisc assembly and Cy3 labeling for single-molecule fluorescence
source_steps.description: Characterize the sample: Use Nanofilm SEC-250, 5 μm, 250 Å, 7.8 × 300 mm (Sepax Technologies) analytical size exclusion chromatography to check the monodispersity and homogeneity of samples ( Figure 6 ) from step 12.

background: Drug-exposed Plasmodium falciparum red blood cell cultures have completed the initial treatment period and now need extensive post-treatment cleanup followed by parasite pellet preparation. The current stage is to remove residual compound through repeated media exchange and shaking, then isolate parasites by PBS washing, cold saponin lysis of erythrocytes, cold centrifugation, and final PBS cleanup until the supernatant is no longer visibly red.
goal: Process the treated malaria culture through the washout and parasite pellet preparation stage to obtain a cleaned parasite suspension in PBS suitable for downstream proteasome analysis.
constraints: Begin from a treated cell culture that has already undergone drug addition, and include the remaining 6 h incubation under incubator conditions before washout handling.

Model repeated washout behavior with dependent outputs using room-temperature centrifugation at 2521 x g, including both a 3 min wash spin and a 5 min PBS wash spin, with resuspension in fresh medium or cold PBS represented by a mixing action.
Include a shaking incubation during washout at 55 rpm for 10 min, and reuse at least two action families multiple times across the workflow.
After washout, cool the sample on ice and perform erythrocyte lysis using cold 10% saponin with a 10 min ice incubation that includes vigorous mixing behavior.
Use a cold centrifugation step at 4863 x g for 10 min at 4 C after saponin treatment, then continue PBS cleanup so the final state represents parasites resuspended in 1.2 mL PBS.

available_inputs.description: Plasmodium falciparum culture in red blood cells after addition of 100X drug solution

available_inputs.name: complete_media
available_inputs.description: Fresh complete malaria culture medium for washout steps

available_inputs.name: cold_pbs
available_inputs.description: Pre-chilled PBS used for cell and parasite washing

available_inputs.name: cold_saponin
available_inputs.description: Pre-chilled 10% saponin solution for erythrocyte lysis

available_inputs.name: ice_bath
available_inputs.description: Ice cooling setup for chilled handling and lysis incubation

gold_program:
step1 = incubate_sample(sample=treated_culture, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=360.0, condition='static incubator')
step2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step1, speed_xg=2521, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=25.0)
step3 = mix_sample(sample=step2, method='inversion resuspension in fresh complete media', duration_s=20.0, speed='gentle')
step4 = incubate_sample(sample=step3, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=10.0, condition='shaking 55 rpm')
step5 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step4, speed_xg=2521, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=25.0)
step6 = mix_sample(sample=step5, method='resuspension in cold PBS', duration_s=20.0, speed='vigorous')
step7 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step6, speed_xg=2521, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=25.0)
step8 = cool_sample(sample=step7, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=5.0, device=ice_bath)
step9 = mix_sample(sample=step8, method='resuspension in cold saponin', duration_s=20.0, speed='vigorous')
step10 = incubate_sample(sample=step9, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=10.0, condition='on ice with intermittent vigorous mixing')
step11 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step10, speed_xg=4863, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step12 = mix_sample(sample=step11, method='resuspension in 1.2 mL cold PBS', duration_s=20.0, speed='gentle')

source_steps.description: Add above 50 μL of 100X drug solution to 5 mL cell culture in a 75 cm 2 flask, swirl the flask several times to mix well, then keep in incubator for 6 h.

source_steps.stage: Thorough washing of red blood cells
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the cell culture, remove the supernatant, and resuspend the pelleted red cells in fresh complete media, then shake in the incubator. Transfer the cell culture to 15 mL centrifuge tube, cap the tube.

source_steps.stage: Thorough washing of red blood cells
source_steps.description: Centrifuge (2,521 × g , 3 min, r.t.) and then carefully remove the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Thorough washing of red blood cells
source_steps.description: Refill the centrifuge tube with fresh complete media (12 mL), and cap the tube, then resuspend the cells well by inverting several times.

source_steps.stage: Thorough washing of red blood cells
source_steps.description: Transfer the resuspended cell culture to a new 25 cm 2 flask carefully, invert and shake the flask gently, and place back in the incubator on a rotating shaker.

source_steps.stage: Thorough washing of red blood cells
source_steps.description: Set shaking speed at 55 rpm and shake for 10 min.

source_steps.stage: Thorough washing of red blood cells
source_steps.description: Repeat steps 2a∼2e additional times.

Following the final wash, place red blood cell pellets on ice, washed with PBS and then lyse with 10% saponin to obtain parasite pellets. Following step 2a and 2b, resuspend pelleted cells in cold PBS (10 mL), and pellet cells again by centrifugation (2521 × g , 5 min, r.t.).
Aspirate off the supernatant.
Resuspend pelleted erythrocytes in 5 pellet volumes of cold saponin solution and incubate on ice for 10 min, with vigorous mixing every 3 min to lyse the erythrocytes.
Set centrifuge to 4°C, and centrifuge the resuspended cells (4,863 × g , 10 min).
Carefully aspirate off the supernatant and wash the parasites 4 times with cold PBS until supernatant is free of red color. Aspirate off supernatant and resuspend parasite pellet in 1.2 mL PBS.

background: A recombinant coronavirus spike protein produced in ExpiCHO supernatant is being captured on Ni-NTA agarose before loading onto a gravity-flow column. The resin must first be pelleted and washed multiple times in wash buffer, then combined with the expression supernatant adjusted with salt and imidazole and incubated cold to allow binding.
goal: Prepare washed Ni-NTA resin, combine it with the conditioned ExpiCHO supernatant, and complete the cold binding incubation so the sample is ready for downstream column handling.
constraints: Use benchtop centrifugation for the resin at 4000 x g for 1 minute at room temperature.
Start from 1 ml Ni-NTA agarose resin in a microcentrifuge tube and perform three total wash-buffer rinse cycles on the resin before binding.
The repeated resin wash cycles should preserve the pelleted-bead state between washes rather than restarting from raw resin each time.
Condition the expression supernatant to final concentrations of 150 mM NaCl and 10 mM imidazole before or during resin combination.
The resin-supernatant binding incubation must be carried out at 4 C for 40 minutes with a rotating or equivalent mixing condition.
Keep the program focused on the purification segment and stop once the bound resin-supernatant mixture is ready for later gravity-column loading.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keyword arguments only, and maintain a dependent chain where later steps consume earlier outputs.

available_inputs.description: ExpiCHO expression supernatant containing recombinant spike protein

available_inputs.description: Expression supernatant adjusted to 150 mM NaCl and 10 mM imidazole

resin_pellet_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=resin_wash_1, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
resin_wash_2 = mix_sample(sample=resin_pellet_2, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='medium')
resin_pellet_3 = centrifuge_sample(sample=resin_wash_2, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
resin_wash_3 = mix_sample(sample=resin_pellet_3, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='medium')
resin_pellet_4 = centrifuge_sample(sample=resin_wash_3, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
resin_plus_conditioned_supernatant = mix_sample(sample=nacl_imidazole_conditioned_supernatant, method='pipetting', duration_s=15.0, speed='medium')
binding_mix = mix_sample(sample=resin_plus_conditioned_supernatant, method='rocking', duration_s=20.0, speed='gentle')
bound_resin_supernatant = incubate_sample(sample=binding_mix, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=40.0, condition='rotating')

source_steps.description: After removing the blocking buffer, add the primary antibody diluted 1/5,000 with PBST.
Shake on a shaker at 200 rpm for 1 hr at 4°C (this incubation time can be increased to overnight).
Remove the primary antibody solution. Add enough PBST to cover the membrane, allow to wash on a shaker at 200 rpm for 10 min, and remove the PBST. Repeat two more times.
After removing the PBST from the final wash, add the secondary antibody diluted to 1/20,000 with PBST.
Shake on a shaker at 200 rpm for 1 hr at 4°C.
Remove the secondary antibody solution and wash the membrane again as described in step 70.
After washing, the western blot is ready to be visualized.

Add 1 ml Ni‐NTA agarose resin (500 µl Ni beads) to a microcentrifuge tube and centrifuge for 1 min in a benchtop centrifuge at 4000 × g , room temperature, using a Pico 21 microcentrifuge with 24 × 1.5/2.0 Pico 21 microcentrifuge with 24 × 1.5/2.0 ml rotorml rotor. Once the beads have been separated from the storage solution, aspirate the solution.
Add 1 ml wash buffer, centrifuge again as in step 75, and decant wash buffer. Repeat twice more and then add the beads to the ExpiCHO expression supernatant from step 54.

Add 150 mM NaCl and 10 mM imidazole to the ExpiCHO supernatant and rotate tube for 40 min at 4°C (this can be increased to 2 hr if needed). If using 5 M NaCl and imidazole stocks, use 15 µl of NaCl and 10 µl imidazole per milliliter of supernatant instead. CAUTION : Imidazole is toxic if inhaled, swallowed, or in contact with skin. If making imidazole solution from powder stock, the bottle should only be opened in a fume hood with reagent‐impermeable gloves. For further information, see Sigma‐Aldrich Safety Data Sheet.
Equilibrate the gravity flow column by adding 3 ml wash buffer and allowing it to flow through.
Add the sample supernatant containing Ni‐NTA beads to the column. Collect all fractions, including the flowthrough, in case protein flows off the column prior to elution.

background: In this spatial transcriptomics workflow for C. elegans, washed bead-bound cDNA has reached the point where it must be dried, eluted into water, separated from the magnetic beads, and advanced into T7 in vitro transcription. After the long transcription incubation, residual CEL-Seq2 primers are removed by EXO-SAP treatment before downstream processing.
goal: Finish the local cleanup-to-transcription segment starting from the washed bead pellet and ending after primer-removal incubation.
constraints: Begin with a bead pellet that has already completed ethanol washing and supernatant removal.
Air-dry the pellet for at most 10 min, then elute in 8 uL nuclease-free water and allow a 2 min room-temperature incubation before magnetic clarification.
Keep bead carryover out of the recovered eluate; only the clear supernatant should proceed into the next vessel and reaction.
The transcription setup corresponds to 8 uL eluate plus 12 uL T7 master mix, followed by a 37 C incubation lasting 13 h with a 70 C heated lid and a terminal 4 C hold.
After transcription, add 7.5 uL EXO-SAP and perform a second 37 C incubation for 15 min.
Use only provided action-pool functions, assign every call to a variable, and ensure at least two distinct action names recur.

available_inputs.description: cDNA-bound magnetic bead pellet after the second ethanol wash with liquid removed

available_inputs.name: elution_tube
available_inputs.description: fresh 0.5 mL microcentrifuge tube for recovered clarified eluate

available_inputs.name: ivt_plate
available_inputs.description: PCR-compatible plate or strip used for transcription and subsequent incubation

available_inputs.name: thermal_cycler
available_inputs.description: thermal cycler capable of 37 C incubation, 70 C lid heating, and 4 C hold

gold_program:
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=washed_bead_pellet, method="air dry", temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=10.0)
eluted_beads = incubate_sample(sample=dried_pellet, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=2.0, condition="resuspended in 8 uL nuclease-free water and mixed thoroughly")
clarified_eluate = incubate_sample(sample=eluted_beads, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=5.0, condition="on magnetic stand until liquid is clear; recover supernatant only into fresh tube")
ivt_loaded = incubate_sample(sample=clarified_eluate, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=1.0, condition="combine 8 uL eluate with 12 uL T7 transcription master mix in PCR vessel")
sealed_ivt_plate = seal_plate(plate=ivt_plate, seal_type="adhesive film", pressure="firm", duration_s=5.0)
resealed_ivt_plate = seal_plate(plate=ivt_plate, seal_type="adhesive film", pressure="firm", duration_s=3.0)
open_lid_state = open_thermal_cycler_lid(instrument=thermal_cycler, latch_state="unlocked", plate=ivt_plate)
closed_lid_state = close_thermal_cycler_lid(instrument=thermal_cycler, latch_state="locked", plate=ivt_plate)
transcribed_product = run_thermal_cycler(plate=ivt_plate, program_name="37C_13h_then_4C_hold", cycles=1, lid_temperature_c=70.0)
exo_sap_added = incubate_sample(sample=transcribed_product, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=1.0, condition="add 7.5 uL EXO-SAP")
primer_removed_product = incubate_sample(sample=exo_sap_added, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=15.0, condition="static")

source_steps.description: Prepare bead mix in a 1:7 ratio of beads to bead binding buffer, vortex well and add the bead mix to the sample in a 4:5 ratio (80 μL bead mix for 100 μL cDNA).
Incubate the sample at room temperature (20°C–25°C) for 15 min.
Place the sample on a magnetic stand for at least 5 min, until the liquid is clear.
Remove and discard the supernatant, add 800 μL of fresh 80% ethanol and incubate minimally for 30 s.
Repeat the wash step; then, remove all supernatant without disturbing the beads.
Air dry the pellet for a maximum of 10 min.
Resuspend the beads in 8 μL nuclease-free water. Pipette up-and-down 10 times to mix thoroughly.
Incubate at room temperature (20°C–25°C) for 2 min, then place the sample on the magnetic stand until the liquid is clear.
Carefully pipette all supernatant into a new 0.5 mL microcentrifuge tube, without disturbing the beads.

source_steps.stage: Continuation of both Protocol A & B

source_steps.description: Incubate the sample in a thermal cycler at 37°C for 13 h, with the lid set at 70°C. Set the cycler to go to 4°C at the end of the incubation period.

source_steps.description: Add 7.5 μL of EXO-SAP enzyme and incubate at 37°C for 15 min, to remove the CEL-Seq2 primers.

background: In the CEL-seq2 setup phase for single-embryo RNA-seq, the operator is preparing diluted primer aliquots and ERCC spike-in working stocks while keeping reagents cold and minimizing freeze-thaw exposure. At this point, undiluted primer stocks have been thawed on ice, diluted primer tubes and aliquots are being generated, and the ERCC spike-in mix is being diluted for future plate preparation and storage.
goal: Process the thawed primer and ERCC reagents through the required brief spins, cold handling, and storage-ready dilution stages so that diluted primer aliquots and ERCC dilution stocks are ready for later RNA-seq plate setup.
constraints: Maintain cold handling by returning thawed primers to ice-level cooling after each brief spin, represented as 0 C cooling steps.
Use brief centrifugation for primer-containing tubes at no more than 2000 x g and normalize the 5 s spin to about 0.083 min.
Model both the initial diluted primer preparation state and the later aliquoted diluted primer state; these are distinct intermediate materials.
Undiluted primer stocks must end in long-term storage at -80 C, while 10 uM diluted primer aliquots must end in storage at -20 C.
Represent ERCC serial dilution as a two-stage dependency chain that passes through an intermediate 1:1000 state before the final 1:100000 state.
Include storage of the undiluted ERCC reagent at -80 C and separate storage of the 1:1000 ERCC aliquots at -80 C.
Use only action-pool operations; do not add manual liquid-transfer functions even though the protocol involves dilution and aliquoting.
The program should reflect repeated use of the same action families across related materials rather than unrelated protocol jumps.

available_inputs.description: Thawed 100 uM CEL-seq2 primer stock tubes on ice.

available_inputs.name: diluted_primer_tubes
available_inputs.description: Freshly prepared 10 uM diluted primer tubes before aliquot handling.

available_inputs.name: primer_aliquot_tubes
available_inputs.description: Aliquoted 10 uM primer tubes generated from the diluted primer preparation.

available_inputs.name: ercc_stock_mix
available_inputs.description: Undiluted ERCC RNA Spike-In Mix reagent.

available_inputs.name: ercc_1to1000_mix
available_inputs.description: Intermediate ERCC dilution at 1:1000.

available_inputs.name: ercc_1to100000_mix
available_inputs.description: Final ERCC dilution at 1:100000 for working plate preparation.

gold_program:
step1 = cool_sample(sample=primer_stock_tubes, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5.0, device="ice")
step2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step1, speed_xg=2000, duration_min=0.083, temperature_c=4)
step3 = cool_sample(sample=step2, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5.0, device="ice")
step4 = centrifuge_sample(sample=diluted_primer_tubes, speed_xg=2000, duration_min=0.083, temperature_c=4)
step5 = cool_sample(sample=step4, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5.0, device="ice")
step6 = centrifuge_sample(sample=primer_aliquot_tubes, speed_xg=2000, duration_min=0.083, temperature_c=4)
step7 = cool_sample(sample=step6, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5.0, device="ice")
step8 = store_sample(sample=step3, temperature_c=-80, duration_h=24.0, container="primer_stock_box")
step9 = store_sample(sample=step7, temperature_c=-20, duration_h=24.0, container="primer_aliquot_box")
step10 = store_sample(sample=ercc_stock_mix, temperature_c=-80, duration_h=24.0, container="ercc_stock_aliquots")
step11 = store_sample(sample=ercc_1to1000_mix, temperature_c=-80, duration_h=24.0, container="ercc_1to1000_aliquots")
step12 = cool_sample(sample=ercc_1to100000_mix, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5.0, device="ice")

source_steps.description: Dilute the CEL-seq2 library RT primer, RPI, and RPI1 primers to 10 μM and aliquot to avoid repeated freeze-thaw cycles. Thaw the 100 μM stock primers on ice.
Spin tubes for 5 s at ≤2000 × g and place them back on ice.

As a starting point, make 4 sets of 50 μL diluted primers using 1.5 mL low-binding tubes (DNA LoBind tubes). Using filter tips, transfer 20 μL of each 100 μM primer solution to new 1.5 tubes containing 180 μL nuclease-free water to reach 10 μM concentration.
Mix by pipetting the entire volume up and down 5 times.
Spin tubes for 5 s at ≤2000 × g and place them on ice.
Transfer 50 μL of each diluted primer to 3 other new 1.5 mL tubes.
Spin tubes for 5 s at ≤2000 × g and place them on ice.
Store undiluted primers at −80°C, and 10 μM aliquots at −20°C.

Prepare working plates for RNA-seq. Prepare a serial dilution of ERCC RNA Spike-In Mix in nuclease-free water to reach a 1:100,000 final dilution. For example, add 1 μL ERCC RNA Spike-In Mix in 999 μL nuclease-free water to get a 1:1,000 dilution. Then, add 10 μL diluted ERCC RNA Spike-In (1:1,000) to 990 μL nuclease-free water to get a 1:100,000 dilution. To avoid unnecessary freeze-thaw cycles, prepare two 2 μL aliquots of the undiluted ERCC RNA Spike-In mix the first time you use it.
Make three 200 μL aliquots of 1:1,000 diluted mix for storage and discard the rest. Note the expiration date for the original reagent on the lid of the tube.
Store undiluted reagent and aliquots at −80°C. Any remaining diluted mix can be discarded.
Prepare 3600 μL Spike-In-dNTPs master mix. Thaw reagents on ice, vortex for 5 s, and spin them for 5 s at ≤2000 × g .

background: Differentiated HL-60 cells are at the flow-cytometry preparation stage for surface marker validation. The cells need to be blocked to reduce Fc-mediated nonspecific binding, stained with antibody or control reagents, washed, and then prepared with DAPI so the final suspension is suitable for immediate cytometry acquisition.
goal: Complete the local surface-marker staining preparation for differentiated HL-60 cells so the sample is ready for flow cytometry with viability dye included.
constraints: Use a workflow that starts from 0.5–1×10^6 cells in a flow cytometry tube and includes two pelleting spins, each at 390 x g for 5 min at 18°C.
Represent Fc blocking as resuspension in 100 μl per tube of Fc receptor blocking solution diluted 1:20 in FACS buffer, followed by a 10 min room-temperature incubation in the 20–25°C range.
Represent antibody or isotype staining in a 100 μl total volume, with 5 μl reagent per tube when applicable, then protect the sample from light for 30 min at 4°C or an equivalent chilled condition.
Include a post-stain wash using about 300–400 μl FACS buffer before the second centrifugation, then resuspend the pellet in 500 μl FACS buffer.
Add DAPI to a final concentration of 2 μg/ml after the final resuspension, vortex the sample, and incubate for 10 min at 20–25°C.
Stay at the action-pool abstraction level: use only the provided functions, keyword arguments only, assign every call to a variable, and do not add explicit pipetting or analysis operations.
Create a single dependency chain of 6–12 top-level steps, with at least two action names reused and later steps consuming outputs from earlier ones.

available_inputs.name: hl60_cells_tube
available_inputs.description: Flow cytometry tube containing 0.5–1×10^6 differentiated HL-60 cells.

available_inputs.name: fc_block_solution
available_inputs.description: Fc receptor blocking solution pre-diluted 1:20 in FACS buffer.

available_inputs.name: antibody_stain_tube
available_inputs.description: Tube after antibody or isotype control has been added in a 100 μl staining volume.

available_inputs.name: facs_buffer
available_inputs.description: FACS buffer used for wash and final resuspension.

available_inputs.name: dapi_tube
available_inputs.description: Tube after DAPI has been added to reach a final concentration of 2 μg/ml.

gold_program:
pelleted_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=hl60_cells_tube, speed_xg=390, duration_min=5, temperature_c=18)
blocked_cells = mix_sample(sample=pelleted_cells, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
fc_incubated_cells = incubate_sample(sample=blocked_cells, temperature_c=22, duration_min=10, condition="static")
stained_cells = mix_sample(sample=antibody_stain_tube, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
protected_stained_cells = incubate_sample(sample=stained_cells, temperature_c=4, duration_min=30, condition="protected from light")
washed_cells = mix_sample(sample=protected_stained_cells, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
repelleted_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_cells, speed_xg=390, duration_min=5, temperature_c=18)
resuspended_cells = mix_sample(sample=repelleted_cells, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
dapi_vortexed_cells = vortex_mix(sample=dapi_tube, duration_s=5, speed_rpm=2000, temperature_c=22)
ready_for_flow = incubate_sample(sample=dapi_vortexed_cells, temperature_c=22, duration_min=10, condition="static")
protocol_title: Protocol for the differentiation of HL-60 cells into a neutrophil-like state

source_steps.stage: Cell staining with propidium iodide for cell cycle/apoptosis analysis
source_steps.description: Pellet the cells. Centrifuge the flow cytometry tubes for 5 min at 390x g at 18°C.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining with propidium iodide for cell cycle/apoptosis analysis
source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet in 300 μl Nicoletti buffer and mix gently.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining with propidium iodide for cell cycle/apoptosis analysis
source_steps.description: Incubate the cells in the dark for 1–4 h at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining with propidium iodide for cell cycle/apoptosis analysis
source_steps.description: Afterwards analyze the samples by flow cytometry immediately without wash steps.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining for flow cytometry analysis of surface markers
source_steps.description: Add 0.5–1∗10 6 cells per flow cytometry tube.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining for flow cytometry analysis of surface markers
source_steps.description: Pellet the cells. Centrifuge the flow cytometry tubes for 5 min at 390x g at 18°C.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining for flow cytometry analysis of surface markers
source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet in 100 μl/tube of diluted Fc receptor blocking solution (1:20 in FACS buffer) for blocking Fc binding sites.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining for flow cytometry analysis of surface markers
source_steps.description: Incubate the cells for 10 min at 20°C–25°C.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining for flow cytometry analysis of surface markers
source_steps.description: Stain the cells with the appropriate volume of antibodies and isotype controls (titration of BioLegend antibodies is not necessary, add 5 μl antibody/isotype control per 100 μl total volume as recommended by the manufacturer). Incubate the cells for unstained control with 100 μl FACS buffer only.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining for flow cytometry analysis of surface markers
source_steps.description: Incubate the cells in the dark for 30 min at 4°C or on ice water.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining for flow cytometry analysis of surface markers
source_steps.description: Wash the cells. Add ca. 300-400 μl FACS buffer to each tube and centrifuge the tubes for 5 min at 390x g at 18°C.

source_steps.stage: Cell staining for flow cytometry analysis of surface markers
source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet in 500 μl/tube FACS buffer. Pipet viability dye DAPI (final concentration 2 μg/ml), vortex and incubate for 10 min at 20°C–25°C.

background: Dissected Drosophila ovarioles have already settled in a tube and excess liquid has been removed. The remaining task is a local immunofluorescence-preparation segment for autophagy analysis: label acidic compartments with LysoTracker, fix and permeabilize the tissue, carry out antibody and nuclear-stain treatments under the required temperature and light conditions, then finish with final PBS handling and mounting medium.
goal: Prepare the settled ovarioles into a mounted fluorescence sample suitable for imaging after LysoTracker staining and downstream immunofluorescence treatment.
constraints: Use 200 uL LysoTracker working stock at 1:100 for 5 min with light protection, then include one PBS wash before fixation.

Fix with 200 uL 4% paraformaldehyde for 10 min at room temperature with rocking-like agitation, and include PBST permeabilization using 400 uL PBS plus 0.3% Triton with 10 min room-temperature rocking.

Include a primary-antibody incubation in PBST plus 0.5% BSA using 200-400 uL total volume and an overnight 4 C incubation, followed by a secondary-antibody incubation in PBST plus 0.5% BSA for 2 h at room temperature; protect from light from the secondary step onward.
After antibody handling, include a 10 min RNAse A treatment, a 10 min DAPI or Draq5 stain at 1:1000 in 100 uL PBST, a final 400 uL PBS wash with a 5 min incubation, and end with 30 uL Vectashield.

gold_program:
step1 = add_reagent(sample=ovarioles_tube, reagent=lysotracker_working_stock, volume_ul=200.0, mixing="gentle")
step2 = incubate_sample(sample=step1, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=5.0, condition="protected from light")
step3 = add_reagent(sample=step2, reagent="1x PBS", volume_ul=400.0, mixing="wash")
step4 = add_reagent(sample=step3, reagent="4% paraformaldehyde", volume_ul=200.0, mixing="gentle")
step5 = incubate_sample(sample=step4, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=10.0, condition="rocking")
step6 = add_reagent(sample=step5, reagent=primary_antibody_mix, volume_ul=300.0, mixing="gentle")
step7 = incubate_sample(sample=step6, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=960.0, condition="rocking")
step8 = add_reagent(sample=step7, reagent=secondary_antibody_mix, volume_ul=300.0, mixing="gentle")
step9 = incubate_sample(sample=step8, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=120.0, condition="rocking, protected from light")
step10 = add_reagent(sample=step9, reagent=rnase_a_solution, volume_ul=100.0, mixing="gentle")
step11 = incubate_sample(sample=step10, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=10.0, condition="protected from light")
step12 = add_reagent(sample=step11, reagent=vectashield, volume_ul=30.0, mixing="pipette once")

source_steps.description: Tap the tube gently to nudge the ovarioles to settle down and let the tube rest on a rack for 2–3 min. Once the ovarioles settle to the bottom (hold the tube up to the light to see if there are any still floating in the liquid), remove as much liquid as possible.
Add 200 μL of the Lysotracker working stock (1:100) per tube of ovarioles for 5 min and wrap the tube in foil. Wash with 1× PBS once and proceed to the next step.
Fix the ovarioles by adding 200 μL of 4% paraformaldehyde. Rock for 10 min at room temperature.

Wash thrice in 400 μL of PBS + 0.3% Triton (PBST) to permeabilize the tissue and rock for 10 min in between washes at room temperature. Be sure to let the tissue settle to the bottom of the tube after each wash to give the germaria time to settle to the bottom of the tube.

Make the working stock of your primary antibody of interest in PBST+0.5% BSA. For example: Fas III (at a concentration of 1:100) (Developmental Studies Hybridoma Bank, Cat.#7G10), is useful for staining the follicular epithelium ( Zhang and Kalderon, 2000 )). Let the ovaries settle, remove all the liquid and add 200–400 μL of primary antibody working stock per tube and rock overnight at 4°C.
Next day, wash thrice with PBST for 10 min each.

Let the ovaries settle, remove the PBST, then add working stock of secondary antibody (diluted in PBST+0.5% BSA) and rock the samples for 2 h at room temperature. Protect your samples from light from this point onward by covering the tubes with foil.
Incubate for 10 min in RNAse A to digest the RNA.
Add Draq5 or DAPI (1:1000) in PBST 100 μL, incubate and rock for 10 min.
Add 400 μL of PBS and incubate 5 min, rock and wash twice with PBS.
Take your samples off the rocker, let them settle, and carefully remove as much liquid as possible.
Add 30 μL Vectashield/sample, pipette up-down once to mix the Vectashield and gently pipette the contents of the tube on a slide and mount with 22 × 22 #1.5 coverslip.

background: This workflow segment follows palladium barcoding in a high-throughput mass cytometry staining protocol for clinical samples. The immediate task is to complete wash handling on fixed barcoded cells, prepare and cold-hold the intracellular antibody master mix after clarification, then stain the cells and perform the post-stain cleanup spins.
goal: Advance one barcoded sample batch through intracellular staining setup and cleanup, including cold processing of the antibody master mix and the required wash-associated centrifugation steps.
constraints: Include a 20 min incubation at 23°C for the barcoded sample before the first wash-associated spin.
Represent the cell-sample wash handling with repeated centrifugation at 935 x g for 3 min, and leave the pellet as dry as possible before intracellular staining.
Clarify the intracellular antibody master mix by microcentrifugation at 14000 x g for 5 min at 4°C, then document restoration to the original volume with 1× Perm/Wash and keep it at 4°C until use.
Model addition of 100 μL intracellular master mix to the barcode batch, followed by a 30 min incubation on ice.
Use only provided action-pool functions with keyword arguments, and maintain a chained program where later outputs depend on earlier ones while reusing at least two action names multiple times.

available_inputs.name: barcode_batch
available_inputs.description: Barcoded and fixed cell sample batch ready for intracellular staining.

available_inputs.name: intracellular_master_mix
available_inputs.description: Prepared intracellular antibody master mix before spin-filter clarification.

available_inputs.name: perm_wash_buffer
available_inputs.description: 1× Perm/Wash buffer used for wash context and post-filter volume restoration.

available_inputs.name: cyfacs_buffer
available_inputs.description: CyFACS buffer used for the first post-stain wash.

available_inputs.name: pbs_buffer
available_inputs.description: PBS used for the final resuspension and wash.

gold_program:
incubated_barcode_batch = incubate_sample(sample=barcode_batch, temperature_c=23.0, duration_min=20.0, condition="static")
first_perm_wash_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=incubated_barcode_batch, speed_xg=935, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=23.0)
second_perm_wash_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=first_perm_wash_pellet, speed_xg=935, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=23.0)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=second_perm_wash_pellet, method="air dry", temperature_c=23.0, duration_min=1.0)
filtered_master_mix = centrifuge_sample(sample=intracellular_master_mix, speed_xg=14000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
restored_master_mix = record_measurement(sample=filtered_master_mix, metric="volume_restoration", unit="state", note="Returned to original volume with 1x Perm/Wash")
cold_master_mix = store_sample(sample=filtered_master_mix, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=0.5, container="microcentrifuge_tube_1_5ml")
stained_barcode_batch = mix_sample(sample=dried_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="100 uL intracellular master mix")
ice_incubated_batch = incubate_sample(sample=stained_barcode_batch, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=30.0, condition="on ice")
post_cyfacs_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=ice_incubated_batch, speed_xg=935, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=23.0)
final_pbs_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=post_cyfacs_pellet, speed_xg=935, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=23.0)
protocol_title: High-Throughput Mass Cytometry Staining for Immunophenotyping Clinical Samples

source_steps.description: Add 10 mL 1× Perm Wash to each sample and centrifuge (935 x g , 3 min)

source_steps.stage: Palladium Mass-Tag Cell Barcoding Strategy and Fixing Samples
source_steps.description: Wash 1× with 10 mL of 1× Perm Wash

source_steps.stage: Palladium Mass-Tag Cell Barcoding Strategy and Fixing Samples
source_steps.description: Decant and use a pipette to aspirate after the last Perm Wash to leave pellet as dry as possible
Prepare intracellular antibody master mixes using 1× Perm/Wash (see Tables 3 and 4 )
Filter intracellular antibody master mixes with a 0.1 μM spinfilter in a microcentrifuge: 14,000 × g , 5 min, 4°C
Measure the master mix volume after filtering and adjust to the original volume with 1× Perm/Wash
Keep at 4°C until ready to add to samples
Add 100 μL of intracellular master mix to each barcode batch
Incubate for 30 min on ice
Add 10 mL of CyFACS and centrifuge (935 × g , 3 min)
Decant supernatants, resuspend pellets with 10 mL PBS and centrifuge (935 × g , 3 min)

background: This protocol stage starts with a low-input whole-cell aliquot for paired chromatin-accessibility and transcriptome profiling. The cells undergo ATAC transposition under controlled temperature and shaking, the reaction is then quenched, and parallel reagent conditioning is performed for direct poly(A)+ RNA capture using oligo(dT) magnetic beads in binding buffer.
goal: Carry the sample through whole-cell ATAC tagmentation and prepare washed oligo(dT) beads so the material is ready for the next direct mRNA isolation stage.
constraints: Include a cold centrifugation of the 1e4-cell sample at 500 x g for 5 min at 4 C before transposition, and use the spun material as the input to the transposition branch.
Resuspend the cell pellet in a 10 uL-scale ATAC transposition mixture with pipetting-style mixing, then run transposition at 37 C for 30 min on a thermal mixer at 1000 RPM.
After transposition, perform an immediate brief spin and then quench using the EDTA-LiCl stop solution at the 1 uL amount appropriate for a 10 uL reaction scale.
For the mRNA-capture reagents, keep 10 mM Tris-HCl cold at about 4 C, while bringing the other buffer into room-temperature working condition and dissolving any precipitation before use.
Fully resuspend Dynabeads Oligo(dT)25 by vortexing for more than 30 s, then prepare a 20 uL bead aliquot in 1 mL lysis/binding buffer.
Use magnetic separation for 1 min on the bead preparation and retain the bead phase for downstream use rather than the supernatant.

available_inputs.name: cell_suspension
available_inputs.description: Whole-cell suspension containing enough material to provide an aliquot of 1 × 10^4 cells.

available_inputs.name: atac_transposition_mix
available_inputs.description: ATAC transposition mixture used for the 10 uL whole-cell tagmentation reaction.

available_inputs.name: edta_licl_mix
available_inputs.description: EDTA-LiCl stop solution for quenching the transposition reaction.

available_inputs.name: dynabeads_odt25
available_inputs.description: Dynabeads Oligo(dT)25 suspension for direct mRNA capture preparation.

available_inputs.name: lysis_binding_buffer
available_inputs.description: Lysis/Binding Buffer from the mRNA purification kit.

available_inputs.name: tris_hcl_10mM
available_inputs.description: 10 mM Tris-HCl that should be maintained cold before use.

gold_program:
cells_mixed = mix_sample(sample=cell_suspension, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
cells_pelleted = centrifuge_sample(sample=cells_mixed, speed_xg=500, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
atac_resuspended = mix_sample(sample=atac_transposition_mix, method='pipetting', duration_s=12, speed='gentle')
atac_tagmented = set_thermal_mixer(sample=atac_resuspended, temperature_c=37, speed_rpm=1000, duration_min=30)
atac_quick_spun = centrifuge_sample(sample=atac_tagmented, speed_xg=500, duration_min=0.2, temperature_c=4)
atac_stopped = mix_sample(sample=atac_quick_spun, method='pipetting', duration_s=5, speed='gentle')
tris_chilled = cool_sample(sample=tris_hcl_10mM, temperature_c=4, duration_min=5, device='ice')
buffer_warmed = heat_sample(sample=lysis_binding_buffer, temperature_c=25, duration_min=2, device='bench')
beads_resuspended = vortex_mix(sample=dynabeads_odt25, duration_s=35, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=25)
beads_in_buffer = mix_sample(sample=beads_resuspended, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='moderate')
beads_captured = magnetic_separate(sample=beads_in_buffer, rack='magnetic_rack', duration_min=1, collect_phase='beads')
protocol_title: Low-input ATAC&mRNA-seq protocol for simultaneous profiling of chromatin accessibility and gene expression

source_steps.description: Gently mix the cell suspension and pipette a volume corresponding to 1×10 4 cells into a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube.
Centrifuge the tube containing 1×10 4 cells at 500 × g for 5 min at 4°C in a fixed angle centrifuge.
Remove all the supernatant carefully using two pipetting steps (aspirate down to 100 ul with a P1000 pipette then remove final 100 ul with a P200 pipette).
Resuspend the cell pellet in 10 μL ATAC transposition mixture by pipetting up and down 6 times.
Incubate the transposition reaction at 37°C for 30 min in an Eppendorf Thermomixer with shaking at 1000 RPM.

Immediately upon completion of the incubation, quick spin the sample tube, then add 1 μL of EDTA-LiCl mixture (0.1 volume of the ATAC transposition mixture used in step 4) to stop the transposition reaction.

source_steps.stage: Direct mRNA isolation from the tagmented cells
source_steps.description: Prepare reagents Prepare buffers from Dynabeads™ mRNA DIRECT™ Micro Purification Kit Bring all buffers except the 10 mM Tris-HCl to room temperature before use. Store the 10 mM Tris-HCl on ice or at 2°C–8°C before use.

source_steps.stage: Direct mRNA isolation from the tagmented cells
source_steps.description: Confirm that the Lysis/Binding Buffer has not precipitated. If any precipitation is observed, warm to room temperature and shake to dissolve.

source_steps.stage: Direct mRNA isolation from the tagmented cells
source_steps.description: Wash Dynabeads® Oligo (dT)25 before use Resuspend Dynabeads® Oligo (dT)25 by vortex for more than 30 s. Note: Keep the Dynabeads® Oligo (dT)25 in liquid suspension during handling and storage to avoid reduced performance. If the Dynabeads® Oligo (dT)25 becomes dried out, resuspend the beads in the buffer they are supplied in by placing the vial on a roller or equivalent overnight at 2°C–8°C to restore their complete functionality.

source_steps.stage: Direct mRNA isolation from the tagmented cells
source_steps.description: Pipette 20 μL Dynabeads® Oligo (dT)25 into a 1.5 mL RNase-free Eppendorf tube. Note: Use 20 μL Dynabeads® per sample with 1 × 10 4 cells. For multiple samples, include 5–10% excess volume for pipetting losses.

source_steps.stage: Direct mRNA isolation from the tagmented cells
source_steps.description: Add 1 mL of Lysis/Binding Buffer and mix.

source_steps.stage: Direct mRNA isolation from the tagmented cells
source_steps.description: Place the tube on a magnet for 1 min, then remove and discard the supernatant.

background: A His-tagged protein complex from living E. coli has already been captured on Ni-NTA agarose during the purification and detection phase of a crosslinking experiment. The immediate task is to keep the bound material cold during binding and wash handling, then recover the proteins under denaturing conditions so the eluate is ready for downstream gel-based detection.
goal: Complete the local purification segment for the resin-bound sample, including cold incubation, pellet recovery, two washes, and denaturing elution so that the recovered protein solution is prepared for later analytical separation.
constraints: Maintain the resin-binding incubation at 4 C for 12-16 h with gentle rotation approximated as 10 rpm.
Use repeated pellet recovery spins at 9100 x g for 20 s, normalized as 0.33 min, with cold temperature for the wash-phase centrifugations.
Include exactly two separate wash-resuspension states before elution, corresponding to a Triton Buffer wash followed by a 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.1 wash.
After the wash sequence, perform denaturing elution associated with 80 uL SDS sample buffer and then heat the sample to 98 C for 5 min.
Finish with a final clarification spin at 9100 x g for 20 s at room temperature, approximated as 22 C, and keep the whole procedure as one dependency chain from resin sample to eluate.

available_inputs.name: resin_sample
available_inputs.description: Tube containing the protein sample after addition of 30 uL Ni-NTA agarose suspended in 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.1

available_inputs.name: triton_buffer
available_inputs.description: Triton wash buffer used for the first wash of the Ni-NTA pellet

available_inputs.name: tris_buffer
available_inputs.description: 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.1 used for the second wash of the Ni-NTA pellet

available_inputs.name: sds_sample_buffer
available_inputs.description: Denaturing SDS sample buffer used to elute His-tagged proteins from the resin

gold_program:
bound_resin = set_thermal_mixer(sample=resin_sample, temperature_c=4, speed_rpm=10, duration_min=720)
first_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=bound_resin, speed_xg=9100, duration_min=0.33, temperature_c=4)
triton_wash = vortex_mix(sample=first_pellet, duration_s=5, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=4)
triton_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=triton_wash, speed_xg=9100, duration_min=0.33, temperature_c=4)
tris_wash = vortex_mix(sample=triton_pellet, duration_s=5, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=4)
washed_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=tris_wash, speed_xg=9100, duration_min=0.33, temperature_c=4)
elution_mix = mix_sample(sample=washed_pellet, method="tapping", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
heated_eluate = heat_sample(sample=elution_mix, temperature_c=98, duration_min=5, device="heat_block")
clarified_eluate = centrifuge_sample(sample=heated_eluate, speed_xg=9100, duration_min=0.33, temperature_c=22)
ready_protein = store_sample(sample=clarified_eluate, temperature_c=22, duration_h=0.5, container="tube")
protocol_title: Analyzing protein intermediate interactions in living E. coli cells using site-specific photo-crosslinking combined with chemical crosslinking

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Add 30 μL of Ni-NTA Agarose (QIAGEN) suspended in 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8.1).

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Gently mix the samples overnight (12–16 h) at 4°C with vertical rotation of tubes at approximately 10 rpm.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the tubes (9,100 × g for 20 s at 4°C), discard the supernatants.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Wash the pellets by adding 1,000 μL of Triton Buffer and vortexing.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: After centrifugation (9,100 × g for 20 s at 4°C), discard the supernatants.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Wash the pellets again by adding 1,000 μL of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8.1) and vortexing.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the tube (9,100 × g for 20 s at 4°C), and completely discard the supernatants.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Add 80 μL of SDS Sample Buffer and mix well by tapping to elute the proteins (the His 10 -tagged proteins are eluted by the SDS sample buffer as 2-mercaptoethanol reduces Ni in this buffer).

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Heat the sample at 98°C for 5 min.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the tube (9,100 × g for 20 s at room temperature (20°C–25°C)) and collect the supernatant containing eluted proteins.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Separate the purified proteins by 7.5% Laemmli SDS-PAGE using a standard protocol.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Transfer the separated proteins on to PVDF (0.45 μm) membrane using a standard semi-dry blotting procedure.

background: Dissected Drosophila ovarioles have already been collected into a labeled microcentrifuge tube in Grace's medium. The next stage is the early immunofluorescence workflow used to visualize autophagy-related LysoTracker signal and prepare the tissue for antibody staining, including dye incubation, fixation, permeabilization washes, and overnight primary antibody incubation.
goal: Process the collected ovarioles through LysoTracker staining and fixation, then carry out the required permeabilization washes and place the sample into primary antibody incubation under the appropriate temperature and mixing conditions.
constraints: Begin from a tube of settled ovarioles after dissection; preserve the sample as a single dependency chain rather than restarting from a fresh input.
Include an initial settling period of 2 to 3 minutes before removing liquid from the dissection medium.
LysoTracker treatment must use the protected-from-light condition for 5 minutes at room temperature-equivalent temperature.
A PBS wash must occur after LysoTracker exposure and before fixation.
Fixation must use a 4% paraformaldehyde sample state for 10 minutes at room temperature with rocking-style mixing.
Permeabilization requires three PBST washes, each 10 minutes, with settling between washes so the tissue can return to the bottom of the tube.
Primary antibody incubation must use PBST plus 0.5% BSA working stock conditions and proceed overnight at 4 degrees Celsius with rocking-style mixing.
Use only provided action-pool functions, with keyword arguments only.
The program must contain 6 to 12 top-level calls and reuse at least two action names multiple times.
Use at least three protocol-grounded numeric literals, including values such as 2, 3, 4, 5, 10, or 200.

available_inputs.name: ovarioles_tube
available_inputs.description: Microcentrifuge tube containing dissected Drosophila ovarioles in Grace's medium.

available_inputs.name: lysotracker_working_stock
available_inputs.description: LysoTracker working solution prepared at the appropriate staining dilution.

available_inputs.name: pbs_buffer
available_inputs.description: 1x PBS used for wash steps.

available_inputs.name: pfa_4pct
available_inputs.description: 4% paraformaldehyde fixation solution.

available_inputs.name: pbst_buffer
available_inputs.description: PBS with 0.3% Triton used for permeabilization washes.

available_inputs.name: primary_antibody_working_stock
available_inputs.description: Primary antibody diluted in PBST plus 0.5% BSA.

gold_program:
step1 = incubate_sample(sample=ovarioles_tube, temperature_c=25, duration_min=3, condition='static settling')
step2 = incubate_sample(sample=step1, temperature_c=25, duration_min=5, condition='protected from light')
step3 = mix_sample(sample=step2, method='rocking wash in PBS', duration_s=30, speed='gentle')
step4 = mix_sample(sample=step3, method='rocking fixation in 4% paraformaldehyde', duration_s=600, speed='gentle')
step5 = incubate_sample(sample=step4, temperature_c=25, duration_min=10, condition='static settling')
step6 = mix_sample(sample=step5, method='rocking wash in PBST', duration_s=600, speed='gentle')
step7 = incubate_sample(sample=step6, temperature_c=25, duration_min=10, condition='static settling')
step8 = mix_sample(sample=step7, method='rocking wash in PBST', duration_s=600, speed='gentle')
step9 = incubate_sample(sample=step8, temperature_c=25, duration_min=10, condition='static settling')
step10 = mix_sample(sample=step9, method='rocking wash in PBST', duration_s=600, speed='gentle')
step11 = incubate_sample(sample=step10, temperature_c=4, duration_min=960, condition='rocking with primary antibody in PBST plus 0.5% BSA')
protocol_title: Protocol for evaluating autophagy using LysoTracker staining in the epithelial follicle stem cells of the Drosophila ovary

source_steps.description: Repeat until you have plenty of ovarioles sitting in the Grace’s Insect Medium.

Once finished, cut the end off a 200 μL tip, collect the ovarioles very carefully. To do this, fill up the cut-off tip with ~50 μL Graces before pipetting the ovarioles and pipette a small amount at a time to avoid sticking of sample to the walls of the tube and loss of material to the tip. Collect all the ovarioles in this manner into a labeled Microcentrifuge tube.

Tap the tube gently to nudge the ovarioles to settle down and let the tube rest on a rack for 2–3 min. Once the ovarioles settle to the bottom (hold the tube up to the light to see if there are any still floating in the liquid), remove as much liquid as possible.
Add 200 μL of the Lysotracker working stock (1:100) per tube of ovarioles for 5 min and wrap the tube in foil. Wash with 1× PBS once and proceed to the next step.
Fix the ovarioles by adding 200 μL of 4% paraformaldehyde. Rock for 10 min at room temperature.

Wash thrice in 400 μL of PBS + 0.3% Triton (PBST) to permeabilize the tissue and rock for 10 min in between washes at room temperature. Be sure to let the tissue settle to the bottom of the tube after each wash to give the germaria time to settle to the bottom of the tube.

Make the working stock of your primary antibody of interest in PBST+0.5% BSA. For example: Fas III (at a concentration of 1:100) (Developmental Studies Hybridoma Bank, Cat.#7G10), is useful for staining the follicular epithelium ( Zhang and Kalderon, 2000 )). Let the ovaries settle, remove all the liquid and add 200–400 μL of primary antibody working stock per tube and rock overnight at 4°C.
Next day, wash thrice with PBST for 10 min each.

Let the ovaries settle, remove the PBST, then add working stock of secondary antibody (diluted in PBST+0.5% BSA) and rock the samples for 2 h at room temperature. Protect your samples from light from this point onward by covering the tubes with foil.
Incubate for 10 min in RNAse A to digest the RNA.
Add Draq5 or DAPI (1:1000) in PBST 100 μL, incubate and rock for 10 min.
Add 400 μL of PBS and incubate 5 min, rock and wash twice with PBS.

background: This item covers the late RNA extraction stage for circulating small non-coding RNA analysis from human plasma. A lysed plasma sample has already been combined with ethanol and now must be processed through a spin column using repeated loading, washing, an empty-column drying spin, and a two-pass elution to maximize RNA recovery.
goal: Produce the final RNA eluate from the prepared extraction mixture by carrying out the remaining column purification operations through final recovery.
constraints: Start from a prepared RNA-plus-ethanol extraction mixture and a fresh micro spin column.
Use 650 uL loading portions on the same column and account for three total loading passes derived from the same mixture.
Each loading centrifugation uses 3300 x g for 2 minutes at room temperature.
Apply Wash Solution A in 400 uL portions for three total wash cycles on the same column.
Each wash centrifugation uses 3300 x g for 0.5 minutes.
After washing, perform an empty-column drying spin at 13000 x g for 2 minutes.
Elute with 20 uL of Elution Solution A and include a 2 minute room-temperature static hold before spinning.
Each elution round must include sequential centrifugations of 400 x g for 1 minute and 5800 x g for 2 minutes.
The first eluate must be passed through the same column a second time before the final recovery spin sequence.
Use only provided action-pool functions, with keyword arguments only, and maintain a single dependency chain.

available_inputs.name: rna_ethanol_mixture
available_inputs.description: Prepared extraction mixture containing RNA lysate and ethanol, ready for column loading.

available_inputs.name: micro_spin_column
available_inputs.description: Micro spin column used for binding, washing, drying, and elution.

available_inputs.name: wash_solution_a
available_inputs.description: Column wash buffer used during the wash phase.

available_inputs.name: elution_solution_a
available_inputs.description: Elution buffer used to recover RNA from the column.

gold_program:
loaded_column = aliquot_sample(sample=rna_ethanol_mixture, destination=micro_spin_column, volume_ul=650.0, aliquot_count=3)
bound_column = centrifuge_sample(sample=loaded_column, speed_xg=3300, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=22.0)
washed_column_1 = add_reagent(sample=bound_column, reagent=wash_solution_a, volume_ul=400.0, mixing="none")
washed_spin_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_column_1, speed_xg=3300, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=22.0)
washed_column_2 = add_reagent(sample=washed_spin_1, reagent=wash_solution_a, volume_ul=400.0, mixing="none")
washed_spin_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_column_2, speed_xg=3300, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=22.0)
washed_column_3 = add_reagent(sample=washed_spin_2, reagent=wash_solution_a, volume_ul=400.0, mixing="none")
washed_spin_3 = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_column_3, speed_xg=3300, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=22.0)
dry_column = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_spin_3, speed_xg=13000, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=22.0)
elution_loaded = add_reagent(sample=dry_column, reagent=elution_solution_a, volume_ul=20.0, mixing="none")
elution_rest_1 = incubate_sample(sample=elution_loaded, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=2.0, condition="static")
elution_round_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=elution_rest_1, speed_xg=400, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
final_eluate = centrifuge_sample(sample=elution_round_1, speed_xg=5800, duration_min=2.0, temperature_c=22.0)
protocol_title: Protocol to analyze circulating small non-coding RNAs by high-throughput RNA sequencing from human plasma samples
