source_steps.description: Add a volume of 600 μL of Lysis Buffer A + β-Mercaptoethanol. Mix well by vortexing for 10 s.
Add 800 μL of 96–100% ethanol. Mix well by vortexing for 10 s.

Transfer 650 μL of the resulting mixture into a Micro Spin column (included in Norgen kit). Centrifuge 3,300× g for 2 min. Discard the flow-through and reassemble the spin column with its collection tube.
Repeat this step twice more, until the whole mixture has been transferred to the Micro Spin column.
Add 400 μL of Wash Solution A to the column and centrifuge for 30 s at 3,300× g ; discard the flow-through and reassemble the spin column with its collection tube.
Repeat this step twice more, for a total of three washes.
Spin the column, empty, for 2 min at 13,000× g . Discard the collection tube.
Transfer the spin column to a fresh 1.5 mL elution tube.
Elute by adding 20 μL of Elution Solution A to the column and let stand at room temperature for 2 min.
Centrifuge for 1 min at 400× g , followed by 2 min at 5,800× g .
Transfer the eluted volume back to the column and let stand at room temperature for 2 min.
Centrifuge for 1 min at 400× g , followed by 2 min at 5,800× g .

background: Malaria parasite cultures in red blood cells have already been drug-treated and mostly washed. This planning item covers the last wash handling and the transition into parasite pellet preparation using cold PBS, saponin lysis, and refrigerated centrifugation to isolate cleaned parasite material.
goal: Complete the end of the red blood cell wash workflow and generate a washed parasite pellet collected after saponin lysis and cold PBS cleanup.
constraints: Include a room-temperature wash spin at 2521 x g for 3 min, followed by resuspension in fresh complete media and a 10 min shaking period reflecting 55 rpm.
After the last wash, move into parasite pellet preparation with a cold PBS wash and a second centrifugation at 2521 x g for 5 min at room temperature.
Model erythrocyte lysis using cold 10% saponin on ice for 10 min, with mixing during this cold incubation.
Use refrigerated centrifugation at 4863 x g for 10 min after saponin lysis, then represent repeated cold PBS washes until the red color is cleared.
End with the parasite pellet resuspended in 1.2 mL PBS and collected by another 4863 x g centrifugation for 10 min at 4 C.

available_inputs.name: washed_rbc_culture
available_inputs.description: Previously treated infected red blood cell culture ready for the final wash cycle.

available_inputs.name: complete_media
available_inputs.description: Fresh complete medium used during the final red blood cell wash.

available_inputs.name: cold_pbs
available_inputs.description: Cold PBS used for pre-lysis and post-lysis washing.

available_inputs.name: saponin_solution
available_inputs.description: Cold 10% saponin solution for lysing erythrocytes.

gold_program:
wash_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_rbc_culture, speed_xg=2521, duration_min=3, temperature_c=25)
media_resuspend = mix_sample(sample=wash_spin, method='inversion in fresh complete media', duration_s=20, speed='gentle')
shaken_culture = incubate_sample(sample=media_resuspend, temperature_c=25, duration_min=10, condition='shaking 55 rpm')
iced_culture = cool_sample(sample=shaken_culture, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5, device='ice bath')
pbs_resuspend = mix_sample(sample=iced_culture, method='inversion in cold PBS', duration_s=20, speed='gentle')
pbs_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=pbs_resuspend, speed_xg=2521, duration_min=5, temperature_c=25)
saponin_lysis = incubate_sample(sample=pbs_spin, temperature_c=0, duration_min=10, condition='cold 10% saponin on ice with vigorous mixing every 3 min')
post_lysis_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=saponin_lysis, speed_xg=4863, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
washed_parasites = mix_sample(sample=post_lysis_spin, method='resuspension in cold PBS with repeated washes until supernatant is not red, final volume 1.2 mL PBS', duration_s=30, speed='gentle')
final_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_parasites, speed_xg=4863, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
protocol_title: Protocol for analysis of intracellular conversion of artezomib molecules into new proteasome inhibitors in Plasmodium falciparum parasites

source_steps.stage: Thorough washing of red blood cells
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the cell culture, remove the supernatant, and resuspend the pelleted red cells in fresh complete media, then shake in the incubator. Transfer the cell culture to 15 mL centrifuge tube, cap the tube.

source_steps.stage: Thorough washing of red blood cells
source_steps.description: Centrifuge (2,521 × g , 3 min, r.t.) and then carefully remove the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Thorough washing of red blood cells
source_steps.description: Refill the centrifuge tube with fresh complete media (12 mL), and cap the tube, then resuspend the cells well by inverting several times.

source_steps.stage: Thorough washing of red blood cells
source_steps.description: Transfer the resuspended cell culture to a new 25 cm 2 flask carefully, invert and shake the flask gently, and place back in the incubator on a rotating shaker.

source_steps.stage: Thorough washing of red blood cells
source_steps.description: Set shaking speed at 55 rpm and shake for 10 min.

source_steps.stage: Thorough washing of red blood cells
source_steps.description: Repeat steps 2a∼2e additional times.

Following the final wash, place red blood cell pellets on ice, washed with PBS and then lyse with 10% saponin to obtain parasite pellets. Following step 2a and 2b, resuspend pelleted cells in cold PBS (10 mL), and pellet cells again by centrifugation (2521 × g , 5 min, r.t.).
Aspirate off the supernatant.
Resuspend pelleted erythrocytes in 5 pellet volumes of cold saponin solution and incubate on ice for 10 min, with vigorous mixing every 3 min to lyse the erythrocytes.
Set centrifuge to 4°C, and centrifuge the resuspended cells (4,863 × g , 10 min).
Carefully aspirate off the supernatant and wash the parasites 4 times with cold PBS until supernatant is free of red color. Aspirate off supernatant and resuspend parasite pellet in 1.2 mL PBS.
Centrifuge the resuspended pellet (4,863 × g , 4°C, 10 min).

background: A Salmonella promoter-reporter strain is being prepared for downstream anti-virulence screening. At this stage, a mid-log culture is made electrocompetent by cold pelleting and repeated water washes, then mixed with a reporter plasmid, recovered in LB, and taken into selective overnight growth.
goal: Generate the local transformation workflow that prepares electrocompetent Salmonella from the provided mid-log culture and carries the transformed cells through recovery and selective outgrowth.
constraints: Start from a mid-log culture already split across two conical tubes, and keep the competency-preparation stages cold.
Use centrifugation at 3600 x g for 10 min at 4 C for each pellet during the wash sequence, with two 10 mL ice-cold water washes before glycerol resuspension.
After the final wash, resuspend pellets in about 250 uL sterile 10% glycerol and create 50 uL aliquots in 1.5 mL tubes kept on ice.
Add about 3 to 4 uL plasmid DNA to one competent-cell aliquot, preserve the 2.5 kV electroporation setting in the abstracted program representation, then recover immediately in about 1 mL LB.
Include a 37 C recovery incubation for about 60 min with shaking 250 rpm, then represent selective overnight growth in LB plus ampicillin using a dependent downstream incubation.

available_inputs.name: midlog_split_culture
available_inputs.description: Mid-log S. Typhimurium culture already divided into two 50 mL conical tubes.

available_inputs.name: ice_cold_ddh2o
available_inputs.description: Sterile ice-cold double-distilled water for electrocompetent cell washes.

available_inputs.name: glycerol_10pct
available_inputs.description: Sterile ice-cold 10 percent v/v glycerol.

available_inputs.name: pgen_psseA_luxcdabe
available_inputs.description: Reporter plasmid DNA for transformation.

available_inputs.name: lb_medium
available_inputs.description: LB medium for post-pulse recovery and outgrowth.

available_inputs.name: ampicillin
available_inputs.description: Ampicillin for selective culture after transformation.

gold_program:
pelleted_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=midlog_split_culture, speed_xg=3600, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
washed_once = add_reagent(sample=pelleted_cells, reagent=ice_cold_ddh2o, volume_ul=10000, mixing='vigorous resuspension')
repelleted_once = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_once, speed_xg=3600, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
washed_twice = add_reagent(sample=repelleted_once, reagent=ice_cold_ddh2o, volume_ul=10000, mixing='vigorous resuspension')
repelleted_twice = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_twice, speed_xg=3600, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
glycerol_resuspended = add_reagent(sample=repelleted_twice, reagent=glycerol_10pct, volume_ul=250, mixing='resuspend on ice')
competent_aliquot = aliquot_sample(sample=glycerol_resuspended, destination='cold_1_5ml_microcentrifuge_tube', volume_ul=50, aliquot_count=1)
plasmid_cell_mix = add_reagent(sample=competent_aliquot, reagent=pgen_psseA_luxcdabe, volume_ul=4, mixing='brief on-ice mixing')
electroporated_cells = run_thermal_cycler(plate=plasmid_cell_mix, program_name='electroporation_2_5kV', cycles=1, lid_temperature_c=4)
recovery_mix = add_reagent(sample=electroporated_cells, reagent=lb_medium, volume_ul=1000, mixing='immediate recovery')
recovered_cells = incubate_sample(sample=recovery_mix, temperature_c=37, duration_min=60, condition='shaking 250 rpm')
selective_outgrowth = incubate_sample(sample=recovered_cells, temperature_c=37, duration_min=1080, condition='shaking 250 rpm in LB plus ampicillin')

source_steps.description: Grow a 5 mL antibiotic-free LB culture of wild-type S. Typhimurium by incubating at 37°C for ∼18 h with shaking at 250 rpm.
Dilute 1 mL of overnight culture into 50 mL of LB, and incubate the culture at 37°C with shaking at 250 rpm until an OD of 0.5–0.6 (mid-log phase, ∼2.5 h).

Transfer the cells to two 50 mL Falcon conical tubes (∼25 mL culture per tube). Pellet by centrifugation for 10 min at 3,600 g in a pre-chilled (4°C) centrifuge. Remove promptly and discard supernatant.
Wash the cells by vigorously resuspending in 10 mL ice-cold sterile ddH 2 O. Pellet by centrifugation for 10 min at 3,600 g in a pre-chilled (4°C) centrifuge. Remove promptly and discard supernatant.
Repeat step d once.
Resuspend each pellet in ∼250 μL ice-cold, sterile 10% (v/v) glycerol. Divide into 50 μL aliquots in cold 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes, kept on ice.

If proceeding directly from step 13, immediately add ∼3–4 μL of the pGEN-P sseA-luxCDABE plasmid to one tube of electrocompetent cells and incubate on ice briefly (∼3 min). If using previously made, frozen electrocompetent cells, first thaw on ice for ∼5 min.
Carefully transfer the competent cell/plasmid mixture into a pre-chilled electroporation cuvette and perform the electroporation using a Gene Pulser Xcell™ Electroporation System at 2.5 kV.
After the pulse, immediately transfer the mixture out of the cuvette and into a sterile 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube containing ∼1 mL LB.
Incubate the LB mixture for ∼1 h at 37°C with shaking at 250 rpm.
Plate 100 μL of the transformed cells onto an LB agar plate supplemented with 200 μg/mL ampicillin. Incubate the plate at 37°C for ∼18 h.
Pick a single colony of the pGEN-P sseA-luxCDABE- harboring S. Typhimurium strain and inoculate 5 mL of LB with 100 μg/mL ampicillin. Incubate the culture at 37°C for ∼18 h with shaking at 250 rpm.

background: This planning item covers the fixation-to-intracellular-stain transition in a high-throughput mass cytometry workflow. Barcoded cell samples are fixed, washed twice in permeabilization buffer with pellet drying after the last wash, while an intracellular antibody master mix is clarified by cold spin filtration, volume-corrected, kept cold, and then used for intracellular staining before a final wash spin.
goal: Carry the barcoded sample through the fixation, wash, pellet-drying, intracellular stain preparation, cold staining incubation, and final post-stain centrifugation steps using the available abstract actions.
constraints: Include a 20 min incubation at 23°C for the fixed sample before downstream washing.
Use two sample centrifugation steps at 935 x g for 3 min as the wash-associated spins, followed by a drying step that leaves the pellet as dry as possible.
Process the intracellular antibody master mix with a 14000 x g spin for 5 min at 4°C, then document restoration of the original volume and keep the mix at 4°C until use.
After the sample is combined with intracellular master mix, include a 30 min incubation on ice or equivalent 0-4°C cooling condition before the final CyFACS-associated spin.
Use only provided action-pool functions, assign every call to a variable, and maintain one continuous dependency chain for the sample while reusing at least two action names multiple times.

available_inputs.name: cytofix_cytoperm_sample
available_inputs.description: Barcoded sample after addition of BD Cytofix/Cytoperm, ready for fixation incubation.

available_inputs.name: intracellular_master_mix
available_inputs.description: Intracellular antibody master mix prepared in 1x Perm/Wash before spin filtration.

available_inputs.name: permwash_sample
available_inputs.description: Sample state after addition of 1x Perm Wash for wash handling.

available_inputs.name: cyfacs_sample
available_inputs.description: Sample state after CyFACS addition for the final wash step.

gold_program:
fixed_incubated_sample = incubate_sample(sample=cytofix_cytoperm_sample, temperature_c=23.0, duration_min=20.0, condition='static')
first_wash_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=fixed_incubated_sample, speed_xg=935, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=23.0)
second_wash_mix = mix_sample(sample=first_wash_spin, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='moderate')
second_wash_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=second_wash_mix, speed_xg=935, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=23.0)
dry_pellet = dry_sample(sample=second_wash_spin, method='air dry', temperature_c=23.0, duration_min=1.0)
filtered_master_mix = centrifuge_sample(sample=intracellular_master_mix, speed_xg=14000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
restored_master_mix = record_measurement(sample=filtered_master_mix, metric='volume_adjusted_to_original', unit='qualitative', note='Adjusted to original volume with 1x Perm/Wash after filtration')
cold_master_mix = store_sample(sample=filtered_master_mix, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=0.5, container='microcentrifuge_tube_1_5ml')
stained_sample = mix_sample(sample=dry_pellet, method='pipetting', duration_s=15.0, speed='gentle')
cold_stain_incubation = cool_sample(sample=stained_sample, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=30.0, device='ice bath')
final_post_stain_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=cold_stain_incubation, speed_xg=935, duration_min=3.0, temperature_c=23.0)
protocol_title: High-Throughput Mass Cytometry Staining for Immunophenotyping Clinical Samples

source_steps.description: Decant and use a pipette to aspirate after the last Perm Wash to leave pellet as dry as possible
Prepare intracellular antibody master mixes using 1× Perm/Wash (see Tables 3 and 4 )
Filter intracellular antibody master mixes with a 0.1 μM spinfilter in a microcentrifuge: 14,000 × g , 5 min, 4°C
Measure the master mix volume after filtering and adjust to the original volume with 1× Perm/Wash

background: A rabies virus glycoprotein amplicon is being cloned into a VSV-based expression plasmid. At this stage, the gel-excised DNA fragment has been solubilized and is ready for column purification, quantification, and setup of downstream restriction digestion and heat inactivation before later gel-based confirmation.
goal: Process the dissolved gel sample through purification, recover and quantify the DNA, then carry out the digestion-stage temperature program needed to prepare the insert/vector material for subsequent analytical gel verification.
constraints: Use a silica-column style purification flow in which the solubilized gel mixture is spun at 12000 x g for 1 min, followed by a 700 uL wash and an additional 12000 x g spin for 1 min.
After the wash, include a further 1 min centrifugation at 12000 x g to remove residual wash buffer before elution.
Elute the purified DNA into a clean 1.5 mL tube using 30-50 uL elution buffer, allow a 5 min room-temperature hold at 22-25 C, then centrifuge at 12000 x g for 2 min.
Record a DNA concentration readout after purification using a spectrophotometric-style measurement abstraction.
The digestion-stage incubation must be represented as 37 C for about 18 h, normalized as 1080 min.
Heat inactivation after digestion must be performed at 80 C for 20 min.
Use only provided action-pool functions and keep the workflow as a single dependency chain where later operations consume earlier outputs.
Include repeated action families, with at least two action names used more than once and at least one repeated family differing mainly by duration or another small numeric change.

available_inputs.name: solubilized_gel_sample
available_inputs.description: Gel slice dissolved in binding buffer and ready to be loaded onto the purification column.

available_inputs.name: genejet_column_loaded
available_inputs.description: Purification column containing the transferred solubilized gel mixture.

available_inputs.name: wash_buffer_column
available_inputs.description: Same purification column after addition of 700 uL wash buffer.

available_inputs.name: eluted_dna
available_inputs.description: Purified DNA eluate recovered from the column into a clean microcentrifuge tube.

available_inputs.name: digestion_reaction_mix
available_inputs.description: Prepared restriction digestion reaction containing vector or RV-G amplicon in a PCR tube.

gold_program:
column_spin_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=genejet_column_loaded, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=22)
washed_column_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_buffer_column, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=22)
dry_column_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_column_spin, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=22)
elution_hold = incubate_sample(sample=dry_column_spin, temperature_c=25, duration_min=5, condition='static')
elution_recovered = centrifuge_sample(sample=elution_hold, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=2, temperature_c=22)
dna_signal = measure_signal(sample=elution_recovered, assay='nanodrop_dna', wavelength_nm=260, replicate_n=1)
dna_record = record_measurement(sample=dna_signal, metric='DNA concentration', unit='ng/uL', note='purified eluate after GeneJET column')
digestion_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=digestion_reaction_mix, temperature_c=37, duration_min=1080, condition='static')
digestion_inactivated = heat_sample(sample=digestion_incubated, temperature_c=80, duration_min=20, device='thermal_cycler')

background: Purified BRPF1 PZP has already been isolated and the next local-stage task is to prepare a clean NMR sample from a thawed 15N-labeled stock. This involves removing any precipitate from freeze-thaw, assessing concentration, adjusting the sample into the required Tris/NaCl/DTT buffer range, and supplementing with D2O before analysis.
goal: Generate a clarified BRPF1 PZP NMR sample in the specified final buffer with 10% D2O and record concentration and final sample QC.
constraints: Start from a thawed 15N-labeled BRPF1 PZP stock kept cold, and clear debris by centrifugation at 15000 x g and 4 C before using the sample further.
Only the post-spin supernatant is suitable for downstream preparation and concentration assessment.
Check protein concentration with a NanoDrop-style UV readout and record the result; the diluted sample should fall within 0.1-0.2 mM.
The final NMR buffer must be 50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 150 mM NaCl, and 5 mM DTT, using Buffer C and Buffer D to reach the final salt condition.
Prepare the final NMR mixture as 198 uL protein solution plus 22 uL D2O for 10% D2O, and mix gently by pipetting.
Document a final QC note confirming acceptable clarified sample quality and correct final NMR composition.

available_inputs.name: pzp_thawed_stock
available_inputs.description: Gently thawed 15N-labeled BRPF1 PZP protein stock on ice

available_inputs.name: purification_buffer_c
available_inputs.description: Buffer C containing 50 mM Tris pH 7.5 and 5 mM DTT

available_inputs.name: purification_buffer_d
available_inputs.description: Buffer D containing 50 mM Tris pH 7.5, 150 mM NaCl, and 5 mM DTT

available_inputs.name: d2o_stock
available_inputs.description: D2O for final NMR sample supplementation

available_inputs.name: nmr_tube
available_inputs.description: Tube for the finished NMR sample

gold_program:
clarified_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=pzp_thawed_stock, speed_xg=15000, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
clarified_supernatant = collect_fraction(sample=clarified_spin, phase="supernatant", destination="clarified_pzp_tube", volume_ul=400.0)
uv_readout = measure_signal(sample=clarified_supernatant, assay="A280_protein_concentration", wavelength_nm=280, replicate_n=1)
conc_record = record_measurement(sample=uv_readout, metric="protein_concentration", unit="mM", note="Clarified supernatant collected after freeze-thaw debris removal")
diluted_with_c = add_reagent(sample=clarified_supernatant, reagent=purification_buffer_c, volume_ul=60.0, mixing="pipette")
diluted_with_d = add_reagent(sample=diluted_with_c, reagent=purification_buffer_d, volume_ul=138.0, mixing="pipette")
post_dilution_mix = mix_sample(sample=diluted_with_d, method="pipetting", duration_s=8.0, speed="gentle")
nmr_protein_aliquot = aliquot_sample(sample=post_dilution_mix, destination=nmr_tube, volume_ul=198.0, aliquot_count=1)
nmr_with_d2o = add_reagent(sample=nmr_protein_aliquot, reagent=d2o_stock, volume_ul=22.0, mixing="pipette")
final_nmr_mix = mix_sample(sample=nmr_with_d2o, method="pipetting", duration_s=8.0, speed="gentle")
final_qc = record_measurement(sample=final_nmr_mix, metric="final_sample_qc", unit="percent", note="Prepared in 50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 150 mM NaCl, 5 mM DTT with 10 percent D2O and target 0.1-0.2 mM protein")

source_steps.description: In the tube containing the beads, bring the volume up to 15–20 mL with PZP Purification Buffer B. To cleave the GST-tag, add TEV enzyme and follow protocol from the manufacture or use home purified enzyme (14 mg/mL, 30 μL) ( Liu et al., 2020a ) and gently rock on a platform shaker set to level 2-3, (Microplate Titer Plate Shaker, LAB-LINE INSTRUMENTS, Inc.) for 15–20 h at 4°C.

Verify cleavage and purification steps by SDS PAGE. The 10–15 well 15% SDS polyacrylamide gels can either be poured (5% stacking, 15% resolving) in a 1.5 mm mini gel cassette (Thermo Fisher Sci) or purchased (gradient 4%–15%). http://www.bio-rad.com/webroot/web/pdf/lsr/literature/Bulletin_6201.pdf
Prepare gel and 4× SDS loading buffer (200 mM Tris-HCl (pH 6.8), 400 mM DTT, 8% (w/v) SDS, 0.4% (w/v) bromophenol blue, and 40% (v/v) glycerol).
Heat samples at 90°C for 10 min to denature proteins, spin down for 5–10 s at 5,000 × g in an Eppendorf Microcentrifuge 5424R (Eppendorf) and then load on gel.
Run until the dye front reaches the bottom of the gel.

After desired cleavage is obtained (visually inspect that 75%–90% of cleavage has occurred), elute the protein contained in the supernatant. Using a 10,000 Da MWCO centrifugal filter (Millipore) concentrate the protein to 2 mL at 3,000 × g in an Eppendorf 5810 R Centrifuge with an A-4-62 rotor (Eppendorf).

To further purify the BRPF1 PZP protein, inject concentrated protein into a HiPrep™ 16/60 Sephacryl® S-100 HR (Cytiva) equilibrated with Purification Buffer B. Run column at 0.2 mL/minute in 4°C. Protein elutes at approx. 66 mL on this column.

Run SDS PAGE as in step 16 to verify and select pure fractions. Pool fractions and concentrate to 20–40 mg/mL using a 10,000 Da MWCO centrifugal filter (Millipore) at 3,000 × g in an Eppendorf 5810 R Centrifuge with an A-4-62 rotor (Eppendorf).
BRPF1 PZP proteins can be used immediately or flash frozen in liquid nitrogen and stored at −80°C.

Gently thaw purified 15 N-labeled BRPF1 PZP on ice. Centrifuge at 15,000 × g in an Eppendorf Microcentrifuge 5424R (Eppendorf) and separate supernatant from any debris or precipitation that has formed during freeze thaw process.

Check the protein concentration (NanoDrop 2000c, Thermo Fisher Sci). The first time the protein concentration is checked it should be calculated using Bradford solution and a standard curve using BSA at known concentrations. With the standard curve, it is possible to determine the unknown concentration of the BRPF1 PZP protein. The calculated protein concentration by Bradford solution can be compared to that found using the extinction coefficient (extinction coefficient determined from the protein sequence). If using the extinction coefficient is an accurate method to determine BRPF1 PZP concentration, future protein concentration calculations can be completed using this method.

Dilute protein to 0.1–0.2 mM using Purification Buffer C (50 mM Tris pH 7.5, and 5 mM DTT) and Purification Buffer D (50 mM Tris pH 7.5, 150 mM NaCl, and 5 mM DTT), bringing the salt concentration of the buffer to 150 mM NaCl. Final buffer for NMR sample is 50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 150 mM NaCl, 5 mM DTT. Combine 198 μL sample with 22 μL of D 2 O (final concentration of 10%), mix gently up and down with a pipette.

background: This experiment analyzes transient protein interactions in living E. coli by combining photo-crosslinking and chemical crosslinking, followed by purification and detection of tagged crosslinked products. At this stage, the affinity-captured material has already been bound and requires low-temperature cleanup washes, denaturing elution, and recovery of the eluted protein fraction before downstream gel analysis and blotting.
goal: Process the bound protein sample through the wash and elution stage so that a clean supernatant containing denatured, eluted proteins is obtained for subsequent electrophoretic analysis.
constraints: Maintain the sample at 4°C during the overnight gentle mixing period and during the cold pellet-forming spins used in the wash sequence.
Use an overnight incubation of 12-16 h with gentle agitation approximating 10 rpm; a thermal mixer may be used to represent controlled slow mixing.
Include two separate wash/resuspension events before elution: one corresponding to Triton buffer and one corresponding to 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.1.
Each wash cycle should include a brief centrifugation at 9100 x g for about 20 s at 4°C before moving on.
After the final cold spin, the supernatant must be completely removed before adding the denaturing elution buffer.
Elute with 80 uL SDS sample buffer and then heat at 98°C for 5 min to release the His-tagged proteins.
Recover the final eluate by a short centrifugation at 9100 x g for about 20 s at room temperature, using the post-heat sample as input.
Do not include SDS-PAGE, membrane transfer, or blocking steps; stop once the eluted protein-containing supernatant has been obtained.

available_inputs.name: bound_sample
available_inputs.description: Affinity-purified tube containing bead- or pellet-associated crosslinked proteins ready for wash and elution.

available_inputs.name: triton_buffer
available_inputs.description: Triton-containing wash buffer used for the first cleanup wash.

available_inputs.name: tris_buffer
available_inputs.description: 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.1 used for the second wash.

available_inputs.name: sds_sample_buffer
available_inputs.description: Denaturing SDS sample buffer used to elute bound His-tagged proteins.

gold_program:
overnight_mixed = set_thermal_mixer(sample=bound_sample, temperature_c=4.0, speed_rpm=10, duration_min=720.0)
cold_spin_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=overnight_mixed, speed_xg=9100, duration_min=0.333, temperature_c=4.0)
triton_resuspended = vortex_mix(sample=cold_spin_1, duration_s=5.0, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=4.0)
cold_spin_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=triton_resuspended, speed_xg=9100, duration_min=0.333, temperature_c=4.0)
tris_resuspended = vortex_mix(sample=cold_spin_2, duration_s=5.0, speed_rpm=2500, temperature_c=4.0)
cold_spin_3 = centrifuge_sample(sample=tris_resuspended, speed_xg=9100, duration_min=0.333, temperature_c=4.0)
elution_mixed = shake_container(sample=cold_spin_3, amplitude_mm=5.0, repeat_n=4, duration_s=8.0)
heated_eluate = heat_sample(sample=elution_mixed, temperature_c=98.0, duration_min=5.0, device="heat_block")
final_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=heated_eluate, speed_xg=9100, duration_min=0.333, temperature_c=22.0)
protocol_title: Analyzing protein intermediate interactions in living E. coli cells using site-specific photo-crosslinking combined with chemical crosslinking

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Gently mix the samples overnight (12–16 h) at 4°C with vertical rotation of tubes at approximately 10 rpm.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the tubes (9,100 × g for 20 s at 4°C), discard the supernatants.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Wash the pellets by adding 1,000 μL of Triton Buffer and vortexing.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: After centrifugation (9,100 × g for 20 s at 4°C), discard the supernatants.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Wash the pellets again by adding 1,000 μL of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8.1) and vortexing.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the tube (9,100 × g for 20 s at 4°C), and completely discard the supernatants.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Add 80 μL of SDS Sample Buffer and mix well by tapping to elute the proteins (the His 10 -tagged proteins are eluted by the SDS sample buffer as 2-mercaptoethanol reduces Ni in this buffer).

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Heat the sample at 98°C for 5 min.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the tube (9,100 × g for 20 s at room temperature (20°C–25°C)) and collect the supernatant containing eluted proteins.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Separate the purified proteins by 7.5% Laemmli SDS-PAGE using a standard protocol.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Transfer the separated proteins on to PVDF (0.45 μm) membrane using a standard semi-dry blotting procedure.

source_steps.stage: Purification and detection of crosslinked products
source_steps.description: Incubate the PVDF membrane in the BLOTTO solution for 30 min at 42°C for blocking.

background: A fixed-cell CRISPR screening population has completed selection and is now in the intracellular staining stage before flow sorting. Because fixation and permeabilization promote aggregation, the preparation relies on gentle resuspension, repeated wash spins, protected-light fluorophore handling, and final cold holding in EDTA/BSA sorting buffer so the sample remains suitable for next-day FACS.
goal: Process the fixed-cell suspension through permeabilization/blocking, primary and secondary antibody staining, wash cycles, and final overnight cold preparation for sorting.
constraints: Keep the workflow as one continuous sample-prep chain in a 50 mL tube context, ending with a sort-ready sample stored overnight rather than performing the sort itself.
Use centrifugation conditions of 685 xg for 5 min throughout this segment; room-temperature handling may be represented as 22.0°C, and the final hold is at 4.0°C.
Permeabilization/blocking must use 5 mL of DPBS with 0.1% Triton X-100 and 10% FBS, followed by a 30 min room-temperature incubation with agitation.

Primary staining must use Mab2C1 at 1 ug/mL with a sample volume of 1125 uL and a 45 min room-temperature agitated incubation; secondary staining must use DyLight 633 at 1:1000 with 1125 uL and a 30 min protected-from-light incubation.
Model wash behavior using repeated additions of 5 mL permeabilization/blocking buffer and repeated centrifugation steps, with at least two action families recurring multiple times.
The final resuspension must use DPBS containing 2 mM EDTA and 2% BSA, and the prepared sample should be stored in the dark at 4°C overnight in a 50 mL tube.
Use only the provided action-pool functions, keyword arguments only, 6-12 top-level calls, and assign every call to a variable.

gold_program:
step1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=fixed_cell_suspension, speed_xg=685, duration_min=5, temperature_c=22.0)
step2 = add_reagent(sample=step1, reagent=perm_block_buffer, volume_ul=5000, mixing="gentle resuspension")
step3 = incubate_sample(sample=step2, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=30, condition="shaking")
step4 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step3, speed_xg=685, duration_min=5, temperature_c=22.0)
step5 = add_reagent(sample=step4, reagent=primary_antibody_solution, volume_ul=1125, mixing="gentle resuspension")
step6 = incubate_sample(sample=step5, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=45, condition="shaking")
step7 = add_reagent(sample=step6, reagent=perm_block_buffer, volume_ul=5000, mixing="gentle resuspension")
step8 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step7, speed_xg=685, duration_min=5, temperature_c=22.0)
step9 = add_reagent(sample=step8, reagent=secondary_antibody_solution, volume_ul=1125, mixing="gentle resuspension")
step10 = incubate_sample(sample=step9, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=30, condition="shaking, protected from light")
step11 = add_reagent(sample=step10, reagent=sorting_buffer, volume_ul=5000, mixing="gentle resuspension")
step12 = store_sample(sample=step11, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=16.0, container="50 mL tube, protected from light")

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Transfer the cell suspension to 5 mL low-binding protein tubes.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Incubate at 4°C without rotation for staining the next day.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Day 16: Large-scale permeabilization and intracellular polymer staining for FACS analysis. CRITICAL: Permeabilization and fixation increase cell stickiness and the formation of cell clumps (so-called doublets), reducing sorting efficiency. To mitigate this, include EDTA and BSA in the sorting buffer, maintain cell concentrations between 1–10 × 10⁶, use a 1 mL pipette for pipetting in all steps and check cells under a microscope with a coverslip. Note: To shorten the protocol, combine permeabilization and blocking into a single step using a permeabilization/blocking buffer (DPBS containing 0.1% Triton X-100 and 10% FBS). Note: Use 5 mL low-binding protein tubes for staining, sorting and pellet visualization to reduce cell loss during washes. Centrifuge cells at 685 ×g, 5 min at 20°C–25°C.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Aspirate the supernatant and resuspend the cells in 5 mL of permeabilization/blocking buffer. Gently pipette and incubate with orbital rotation for 30 min at 20°C–25°C.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 685 ×g for 5 min and discard the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Resuspend the cells in permeabilization/blocking buffer containing the primary antibody (Mab2C1; 1 μg/mL). For controls, use 350 μL of 1 μg/mL antibody solution; for sorting samples, use 1,125 μL of 1 μg/mL antibody solution. Incubate for 45 min with orbital rotation at 20°C–25°C. Note: For FACS staining, typically use 1–10 μg of antibody per million cells. For example, use 1–10 μg of primary antibody in a total volume of 100 μL to 1 mL. Mab2C1 is used at 1ug/mL; but other antibodies may require titration.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Wash the cells 3 times with 5 mL of permeabilization/blocking buffer by centrifugation at 685 ×g for 5 min. Resuspend the pellet gently with a 1 mL pipette after each wash.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Prepare secondary DyLight 633 Ab (1:1,000) in permeabilization/blocking buffer. Use 350 μL for controls and 1,125 μL for samples, both at the same concentration. Incubate for 30 min with orbital rotation at 20°C–25°C in the dark. Note: Secondary antibodies for FACS staining are generally diluted from 1:100 to 1:1,000. Optimize the dilution through titration for the best signal and minimal background.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Wash cells 3 times with 5 mL of permeabilization/blocking buffer by centrifugation at 685 ×g for 5 min. Resuspend the pellet carefully after each wash.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Resuspend the cells in DPBS containing 2 mM EDTA and 2% BSA. Keep them in the dark at 4°C with gentle agitation.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: For sorting purposes, concentrated samples are preferable. Reduce the cell suspension volume by half by gentle centrifugation at 685 ×g for 5 min at 20°C–25°C and resuspend. Keep in the dark at 4°C with gentle agitation overnight until the next step.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Day 17: FACS Sorting of CRISPR-mutagenized fixed cells. This protocol describes FACS using a Beckman Coulter MoFlo cell sorter, typically operated in a flow cytometry core facility. Parameters may need to be optimized by the investigator, especially if a different flow cytometer is used. Keep the following numbers in mind for the sorting day: Sorting capacity: Typically, 3 × 10 7 cells/h.

background: A fixed-cell pooled CRISPR screen has reached the intracellular staining stage just before FACS enrichment. The cells have been fixed and now need permeabilization/blocking, antibody staining, repeated wash spins, and transfer into an EDTA/BSA sorting buffer suitable for overnight cold holding before sorting. The protocol emphasizes minimizing clumping and maintaining conditions compatible with downstream enrichment of the brightest population.
goal: Prepare a fixed-cell sorting sample through permeabilization/blocking, primary and secondary antibody staining, wash cycles, and final resuspension in sorting buffer for protected cold storage prior to FACS.
constraints: Use the fixed-cell sample as the starting material and maintain a continuous dependency chain through the staining workflow.
Permeabilization/blocking uses DPBS with 0.1% Triton X-100 and 10% FBS, with a 5000 uL resuspension followed by 30 min incubation at room temperature with agitation.
Wash spins during this segment use 685 xg for 5 min at 20.0-25.0 C; normalize temperature as 23.0 C if needed.
Primary antibody is Mab2C1 at 1 ug/mL and the sorting-scale staining volume is 1125 uL, incubated 45 min at room temperature with agitation.
After primary staining, perform three wash additions with permeabilization/blocking buffer, represented at protocol level without modeling supernatant aspiration.
Secondary antibody is DyLight 633 at 1:1000 in permeabilization/blocking buffer, using 1125 uL and incubated 30 min at room temperature protected from light.
After secondary staining, perform another three-wash sequence conceptually, but keep the total program within 6-12 action calls by abstracting repetitive washes where necessary.
Final resuspension must be in DPBS containing 2 mM EDTA and 2% BSA, then held cold and protected from light overnight before sorting.
Use only available action-pool functions and keyword arguments.
The program should include repeated action families, especially add_reagent and centrifuge_sample or incubate_sample.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Incubate at 4°C without rotation for staining the next day.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Day 16: Large-scale permeabilization and intracellular polymer staining for FACS analysis. CRITICAL: Permeabilization and fixation increase cell stickiness and the formation of cell clumps (so-called doublets), reducing sorting efficiency. To mitigate this, include EDTA and BSA in the sorting buffer, maintain cell concentrations between 1–10 × 10⁶, use a 1 mL pipette for pipetting in all steps and check cells under a microscope with a coverslip. Note: To shorten the protocol, combine permeabilization and blocking into a single step using a permeabilization/blocking buffer (DPBS containing 0.1% Triton X-100 and 10% FBS). Note: Use 5 mL low-binding protein tubes for staining, sorting and pellet visualization to reduce cell loss during washes. Centrifuge cells at 685 ×g, 5 min at 20°C–25°C.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Aspirate the supernatant and resuspend the cells in 5 mL of permeabilization/blocking buffer. Gently pipette and incubate with orbital rotation for 30 min at 20°C–25°C.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 685 ×g for 5 min and discard the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Resuspend the cells in permeabilization/blocking buffer containing the primary antibody (Mab2C1; 1 μg/mL). For controls, use 350 μL of 1 μg/mL antibody solution; for sorting samples, use 1,125 μL of 1 μg/mL antibody solution. Incubate for 45 min with orbital rotation at 20°C–25°C. Note: For FACS staining, typically use 1–10 μg of antibody per million cells. For example, use 1–10 μg of primary antibody in a total volume of 100 μL to 1 mL. Mab2C1 is used at 1ug/mL; but other antibodies may require titration.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Wash the cells 3 times with 5 mL of permeabilization/blocking buffer by centrifugation at 685 ×g for 5 min. Resuspend the pellet gently with a 1 mL pipette after each wash.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Prepare secondary DyLight 633 Ab (1:1,000) in permeabilization/blocking buffer. Use 350 μL for controls and 1,125 μL for samples, both at the same concentration. Incubate for 30 min with orbital rotation at 20°C–25°C in the dark. Note: Secondary antibodies for FACS staining are generally diluted from 1:100 to 1:1,000. Optimize the dilution through titration for the best signal and minimal background.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Wash cells 3 times with 5 mL of permeabilization/blocking buffer by centrifugation at 685 ×g for 5 min. Resuspend the pellet carefully after each wash.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Resuspend the cells in DPBS containing 2 mM EDTA and 2% BSA. Keep them in the dark at 4°C with gentle agitation.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: For sorting purposes, concentrated samples are preferable. Reduce the cell suspension volume by half by gentle centrifugation at 685 ×g for 5 min at 20°C–25°C and resuspend. Keep in the dark at 4°C with gentle agitation overnight until the next step.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Day 17: FACS Sorting of CRISPR-mutagenized fixed cells. This protocol describes FACS using a Beckman Coulter MoFlo cell sorter, typically operated in a flow cytometry core facility. Parameters may need to be optimized by the investigator, especially if a different flow cytometer is used. Keep the following numbers in mind for the sorting day: Sorting capacity: Typically, 3 × 10 7 cells/h.

source_steps.stage: Genome-wide screen: Infection full size library and selection
source_steps.description: Initial sorting amount: For the first enrichment round, collect around 2% of the total sorted cells with the highest staining signal to enrich for the strongest phenotypes.

background: Fungal cells have already been grown on SD-agar plates and are ready for extracellular vesicle recovery. At this stage, cells are collected into filtered PBS, kept as a gentle suspension, and the vesicle-containing material is enriched by sequential low-speed clearing, clarification, membrane filtration, ultracentrifugation, careful pellet resuspension, and frozen aliquot storage.
goal: Generate the local workflow to recover extracellular vesicles from plate-grown fungal cells after incubation, producing a resuspended vesicle preparation suitable for aliquoting and frozen storage.
constraints: Begin from incubated SD-agar plates and preserve the 24 h growth period at 30 C under static conditions.
Use gentle resuspension by pipetting rather than vortexing when handling collected cells or the final vesicle pellet.
Model the clarification with two refrigerated centrifugation steps: 5000 x g for 15 min at 4 C and 15000 x g for 15 min at 4 C.
After clarification, include filtration through a 0.45 um membrane into an ultracentrifugation-compatible destination.
Include an ultracentrifugation step at 100000 x g for 60 min at 4 C.
The final pellet should be resuspended in filtered PBS with a small-volume gentle mix corresponding to up to 300 uL equivalent handling.
Store the recovered vesicles as aliquots at -80 C.
Use only action-pool operations and keep the workflow as a single dependency chain from plates to stored EV aliquots.

available_inputs.name: sd_agar_plates
available_inputs.description: Solid medium plates containing fungal growth prepared for EV isolation.

available_inputs.name: filtered_pbs
available_inputs.description: Sterile 0.22 um-filtered PBS 1x used for cell collection and EV pellet resuspension.

available_inputs.name: polypropylene_bottle
available_inputs.description: Sterile centrifugation bottle holding the harvested cells in PBS.

available_inputs.name: ultracentrifuge_tube
available_inputs.description: Tube compatible with the chosen ultracentrifuge rotor for EV pelleting.

gold_program:
incubated_plates = incubate_sample(sample=sd_agar_plates, temperature_c=30.0, duration_min=1440.0, condition='static')
collected_cells = mix_sample(sample=polypropylene_bottle, method='pipetting', duration_s=30.0, speed='gentle')
first_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=collected_cells, speed_xg=5000, duration_min=15.0, temperature_c=4.0)
clarified_supernatant = mix_sample(sample=first_pellet, method='pipetting', duration_s=20.0, speed='careful transfer')
second_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=clarified_supernatant, speed_xg=15000, duration_min=15.0, temperature_c=4.0)
filtered_supernatant = filter_sample(sample=second_pellet, filter_type='sterile syringe filter', pore_size_um=0.45, destination=ultracentrifuge_tube)
ev_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=filtered_supernatant, speed_xg=100000, duration_min=60.0, temperature_c=4.0)
resuspended_evs = mix_sample(sample=ev_pellet, method='pipetting', duration_s=45.0, speed='gentle')
stored_ev_aliquots = store_sample(sample=resuspended_evs, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_h=24.0, container='sterile aliquot tubes')
protocol_title: Protocol for separation of fungal extracellular vesicles using ultracentrifugation from solid medium cultures

source_steps.description: Incubate the plates for 24 h at 30°C. CRITICAL: Let SD-agar plates dry completely before spreading out the cells. Note: Typically, 6 SD-agar plates provide adequate EVs for executing the subsequent procedures; nevertheless, this quantity should be adjusted based on the fungus or the strain under consideration as well as the posterior analyses. For proceeding with the iodixanol gradient (Step 2), we usually use 12 SD-agar plates.

Collect the cells and obtain the EVs. Collect the cells with a loop in a sterile Polypropylene Bottle for centrifugation containing 10 mL of 0.22 μm-filtered PBS 1x. Note: Collect a maximum of 10 plates in 10 mL of 0.22 μm-filtered PBS 1x. If you plan to collect more plates, divide them into 2 tubes.
Suspend the cells by gently pipetting (do not vortex the cells). With the pipette, determine the total volume of cell suspension.
Collect an aliquot of the cells and determine the concentration of the cells. Optional: The recovered cells can be fixed with 4% paraformaldehyde to be counted later on.
Centrifuge the tubes at 5,000 × g for 15 min at 4°C.
Carefully pipet the supernatant into a new sterile Polypropylene Bottle for centrifugation without touching the pellet of cells.
Centrifuge the tubes at 15,000 × g for 15 min at 4°C.

Filter the remaining supernatant in a sterile 0.45 μm filter with a syringe in a tube specific for ultracentrifugation. Note: In the laboratory, we use either the SW41 Ti rotor or the fixed-angle rotor type 70 Ti. For the SW41 Ti rotor we use the Ultra-clear tube and for the rotor 70 Ti we use Polycarbonate tubes. To reuse the tubes, it is important to clean them according to the manufacturer’s guide . Note: If tubes are meant for reuse, cleaning and sterilization processes are crucial. The cleaning procedure involves manual washing with Extran detergent (20%), followed by extensive rinsing with milliQ-water to eliminate any lingering detergent residue. Subsequently, the tubes are left to air dry. Prior to usage, the tubes are rinsed with 70% ethanol and allowed to air dry under a biosafety cabinet. Right before adding the sample, three additional washes are conducted using 0.22 μm-filtered PBS. Tubes that have been used should always be inspected for signs of any deformation or cracking.

Centrifuge at 100,000 × g for 1 h at 4°C, rotor acceleration and deceleration on maximum. CRITICAL: It is essential to accurately balance the tubes for the ultracentrifugation. An unbalanced rotor may cause equipment damage and personal injury.

Discard the supernatant by inverting the tube. The EV pellet must be in the bottom of the tube or at the edge of the tube, for swing or fixed-angled rotor, respectively. Invert the tube in an absorbent paper for some seconds to remove the remaining supernatant. CRITICAL: Work as fast as possible to prevent the EV pellet from dissociating and drying.
Suspend the EV pellet in up to 300 μL of 0.22 μm-filtered PBS 1x by gently pipetting 10–15 L times and rinsing the edges of the tube.

Aliquot the EVs in several sterile tubes and store them at −80°C. Note: It is important to store the EVs in multiple aliquots at −80°C to prevent the need for repetitive freezing and thawing of the samples.

background: This item covers the cleanup stage of multiplexed library preparation on the Rhapsody platform after PCR1 amplification. The mRNA-targeted PCR1 material is being purified on SPRI beads while the supernatant is kept at room temperature for later AbSeq and Sample Tag cleanup. The task focuses on ethanol washing of bead-bound mRNA products, drying, elution, a brief clarification spin, magnetic recovery of the eluate, and then initiating bead binding for the combined AbSeq/Sample Tag fraction.
goal: Process the bead-bound mRNA-targeted PCR1 sample through wash, dry, elution, and magnetic clarification to obtain purified eluate in a fresh LoBind tube, while maintaining the saved supernatant at room temperature and then beginning cleanup of the combined AbSeq/Sample Tag PCR1 material by adding SPRI beads and mixing.
constraints: Keep the saved supernatant at room temperature within the 15-25 C range during the mRNA purification workup.
The bead-bound mRNA PCR1 material requires two washes with fresh 80% ethanol while remaining associated with the magnetic separation workflow.
After the wash phase, remove residual liquid and air-dry the beads for 5 minutes at room temperature before elution.
Elute the mRNA-targeted PCR1 products in 30 uL Elution Buffer and mix vigorously by pipetting until the beads are uniformly dispersed.
Following elution, incubate the sample at room temperature for 2 minutes and then perform a brief centrifugation before magnetic clarification.
Use magnetic separation again after elution until the solution is clear; the recovered phase should correspond to the eluate transferred to a fresh 1.5 mL LoBind tube.
Initiate purification of the combined AbSeq/Sample Tag PCR1 material by combining 400 uL PCR1 product with 100 uL SPRISelect beads and mixing thoroughly.
Use only the provided action-pool functions and keep the program at procedural lab-operation level rather than liquid-handling micro-steps.

available_inputs.name: saved_supernatant
available_inputs.description: Supernatant retained from the earlier SPRI cleanup and held for later AbSeq/Sample Tag purification.

available_inputs.name: mrna_bead_pellet
available_inputs.description: Tube containing SPRISelect beads bound with mRNA-targeted PCR1 products.

available_inputs.name: elution_buffer_30ul
available_inputs.description: Thirty microliters of Elution Buffer for releasing purified mRNA-targeted PCR1 products from beads.

available_inputs.name: abseq_sampletag_pcr1_400ul
available_inputs.description: Combined AbSeq and Sample Tag PCR1 product volume reserved for the next purification.

available_inputs.name: spri_beads_100ul
available_inputs.description: One hundred microliters of SPRISelect beads to add to the AbSeq/Sample Tag PCR1 products.

available_inputs.name: lobind_tube
available_inputs.description: Fresh 1.5 mL LoBind tube intended to receive the clarified mRNA eluate.

gold_program:
stored_supernatant = store_sample(sample=saved_supernatant, temperature_c=22.0, duration_h=1.0, container="room_temperature_tube")
mrna_wash1 = magnetic_separate(sample=mrna_bead_pellet, rack="magnetic_rack", duration_min=0.5, collect_phase="beads")
mrna_wash2 = magnetic_separate(sample=mrna_wash1, rack="magnetic_rack", duration_min=0.5, collect_phase="beads")
mrna_dried = dry_sample(sample=mrna_wash2, method="air dry", temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=5.0)
mrna_resuspended = mix_sample(sample=mrna_dried, method="pipetting", duration_s=20.0, speed="vigorous")
mrna_eluted = incubate_sample(sample=mrna_resuspended, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=2.0, condition="static")
mrna_spun = centrifuge_sample(sample=mrna_eluted, speed_xg=500, duration_min=0.1, temperature_c=22.0)
mrna_cleared = magnetic_separate(sample=mrna_spun, rack="magnetic_rack", duration_min=0.5, collect_phase="supernatant")
purified_mrna_in_lobind = store_sample(sample=mrna_cleared, temperature_c=22.0, duration_h=0.1, container=lobind_tube)
abseq_bead_binding_input = mix_sample(sample=abseq_sampletag_pcr1_400ul, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="moderate")
abseq_with_beads = mix_sample(sample=abseq_bead_binding_input, method="pipetting", duration_s=20.0, speed="moderate")

background: This experiment is at the fractionation stage for measuring cGAS localization after DNA stimulation. A whole-cell aliquot has already been reserved, and the remaining cells must be gently extracted to separate cytoplasmic material from nuclei while keeping nuclei intact for later nuclear extraction.
goal: Generate cytoplasmic and nuclear fractions from the remaining stimulated cells, including detergent-assisted lysis, low-speed separation, repeated nuclear washing, and final extraction of the washed nuclei in buffer B.
constraints: Start from the remaining two-thirds cell sample, not the saved whole-cell fraction, and keep samples cold except for the brief room-temperature incubation.
Use an initial centrifugation of 200 x g for 5 min at 4 C, then resuspend in about 250 uL extraction buffer A as a single-cell suspension before incubating 2 min at 25 C and 10 min on ice.
After adding NP40 to reach 1% final concentration, perform separation at 500 x g for 3 min at 4 C; mixing must stay gentle to avoid nuclear envelope breakage.
Wash the nuclear pellet three times with 300 uL extraction buffer A containing 1% NP40, with each wash followed by centrifugation at 500 x g for 3 min at 4 C.
Finish by resuspending the washed nuclei in 80 uL extraction buffer B and vortexing vigorously for 20 s.

available_inputs.name: remaining_cells
available_inputs.description: Two-thirds portion of the PBS-resuspended stimulated cell sample reserved for cytoplasmic and nuclear fractionation.

available_inputs.name: extraction_buffer_A
available_inputs.description: Cold extraction buffer A used for initial lysis and NP40-containing nuclear washes.

available_inputs.name: np40_stock
available_inputs.description: 10% NP40 stock used to obtain 1% final NP40 during fractionation.

available_inputs.name: extraction_buffer_B
available_inputs.description: Extraction buffer B used for final nuclear extraction.

gold_program:
cell_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=remaining_cells, speed_xg=200, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
resuspended_in_A = mix_sample(sample=cell_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=20, speed="gentle")
room_temp_extract = incubate_sample(sample=resuspended_in_A, temperature_c=25, duration_min=2, condition="static")
iced_extract = cool_sample(sample=room_temp_extract, temperature_c=4, duration_min=10, device="ice")
np40_lysis = mix_sample(sample=iced_extract, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
first_split = centrifuge_sample(sample=np40_lysis, speed_xg=500, duration_min=3, temperature_c=4)
wash1 = mix_sample(sample=first_split, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
spin1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash1, speed_xg=500, duration_min=3, temperature_c=4)
wash2 = mix_sample(sample=spin1, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
spin2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash2, speed_xg=500, duration_min=3, temperature_c=4)
wash3 = mix_sample(sample=spin2, method="pipetting", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
final_nuclear_extract = vortex_mix(sample=wash3, duration_s=20, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=4)
protocol_title: Protocol for nuclear export signal characterization of cGAS in mammalian cells

source_steps.stage: Detection of the subcellular localization of cGAS in exogenous DNA stimulation
source_steps.description: Resuspend the cell pellet with 1 mL cold sterile PBS, transfer one-third of the volume (about 330 μL) to a new 1.5 mL tube, and save this as samples of whole cells. CRITICAL: Single cell suspension is critical to ensure data quality in the next steps.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the subcellular localization of cGAS in exogenous DNA stimulation
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the whole cell sample (WCE) at 200 × g for 5 min at 4°C. Discard the supernatant. Add 80 μL ice-cold RIPA buffer to lyse the cells for subsequent immunoblotting (C+N).

source_steps.stage: Detection of the subcellular localization of cGAS in exogenous DNA stimulation
source_steps.description: Pellet the rest two-third cells at 200 × g for 5 min at 4°C, discard the supernatant, and keep the tube on ice.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the subcellular localization of cGAS in exogenous DNA stimulation
source_steps.description: Add 5 pellet volume (about 250 μL) extraction buffer A to resuspend the cell pellet to single-cell solution with 1 mL tips and confirmed by microscopy. CRITICAL: A sufficient volume (3–10 pellet volume) of extraction buffer A is important for isolation. Too small (less than 3 pellet volume) a volume is insufficient to break cytomembranes. Too much (more than 10 pellet volume) a volume will dilute the cytoplasmic protein concentration.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the subcellular localization of cGAS in exogenous DNA stimulation
source_steps.description: Keep the lysed cell samples at 25°C for 2 min, then transfer them to ice rapidly for another 10 min incubation.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the subcellular localization of cGAS in exogenous DNA stimulation
source_steps.description: Add 10% NP40 (about 25 μL) to cell suspension to obtain a final concentration of 1% NP40, and mix gently with 200 μL tips. CRITICAL: Make sure to gently mix the cells to avoid breaking the nuclear envelope. CRITICAL: Keep a consistent number of mixing aspirations between samples.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the subcellular localization of cGAS in exogenous DNA stimulation
source_steps.description: Perform a low-speed centrifugation for 3 min at 500 × g at 4°C to separate the cytoplasmic and nuclear components.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the subcellular localization of cGAS in exogenous DNA stimulation
source_steps.description: Save 30% supernatant carefully to a new 1.5 mL tube as the cytoplasmic component (C), discard the supernatant and keep the pellet on ice.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the subcellular localization of cGAS in exogenous DNA stimulation
source_steps.description: The pellet contains low-purity nuclei. Wash the pellet with 300 μL extraction buffer A which contains 1% NP40 at 500 × g for 3 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the subcellular localization of cGAS in exogenous DNA stimulation
source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant, gently resuspend the pellet with another 300 μL extraction buffer A containing 1% NP40.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the subcellular localization of cGAS in exogenous DNA stimulation
source_steps.description: Repeat step m and n 3 times.

source_steps.stage: Detection of the subcellular localization of cGAS in exogenous DNA stimulation
source_steps.description: Resuspend the nuclei pellet with 80 μL extraction buffer B and vortex the sample vigorously for 20 s.

background: A DNA-FISH probe preparation workflow is in progress after amplification, and the material is now in the precipitation, dye-labeling, and cleanup phase. The immediate work involves recovering precipitated DNA, redissolving it, thermally denaturing the fragments, allowing fluorescent labeling under light-protected conditions, then performing a final purification before checking probe yield and fluorochrome signal.
goal: Process the partially prepared DNA sample through pellet recovery, resuspension, denaturation, fluorescent labeling incubation, final cleanup, and QC measurement to obtain a labeled probe ready for downstream use.
constraints: Include a cold precipitation hold at -20 C for at least 60 min before the first high-speed spin.
Use two centrifugation steps at 16000 x g and 4 C, with durations of 12 min and 5 min respectively, separated by an ethanol wash state.
The pellet must be dried before resuspension, and resuspension must use nuclease-free water with a 37 C incubation lasting 15 min.
Denaturation must occur at 95 C for 3 min and be followed by immediate cooling on ice before subsequent labeling-state handling.
A brief spin is required after the ice-cooling stage and before the protected labeling incubation.
The labeling incubation must be carried out at 18-24 C in the dark for at least 60 min.
Later operations should consume the output of earlier ones rather than restarting from the original sample.
End with a fluorochrome-related absorbance readout and a recorded concentration or QC observation.

available_inputs.description: DNA reaction mixture entering the precipitation and labeling segment

available_inputs.name: ethanol70_wash
available_inputs.description: Prepared 70% ethanol used for pellet washing

available_inputs.name: nuclease_free_water
available_inputs.description: Water used to resuspend and elute DNA

available_inputs.name: labeled_dna_cleanup
available_inputs.description: Labeled DNA sample after cleanup and elution to be measured

gold_program:
precipitated_hold = incubate_sample(sample=dna_reaction, temperature_c=-20.0, duration_min=60.0, condition='static')
first_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=precipitated_hold, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=12.0, temperature_c=4.0)
washed_pellet = mix_sample(sample=first_pellet, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle with 500 ul 70% ethanol wash')
second_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_pellet, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=second_pellet, method='air dry', temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=10.0)
resuspended_dna = incubate_sample(sample=dried_pellet, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=15.0, condition='static in 6 ul nuclease-free water')
denatured_dna = heat_sample(sample=resuspended_dna, temperature_c=95.0, duration_min=3.0, device='heat block')
cooled_dna = cool_sample(sample=denatured_dna, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=1.0, device='ice bath')
post_cool_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=cooled_dna, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=0.2, temperature_c=4.0)
labeled_probe = incubate_sample(sample=post_cool_spin, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=60.0, condition='protected from light')
fluor_readout = measure_signal(sample=labeled_dna_cleanup, assay='nanodrop microarray fluorochrome absorbance', wavelength_nm=550, replicate_n=1)
qc_record = record_measurement(sample=fluor_readout, metric='probe concentration', unit='ng/ul', note='final labeled DNA after cleanup and elution in 20 ul water')
protocol_title: Generation of Inexpensive, Highly Labeled Probes for Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization (FISH)

source_steps.description: Add the following to the reaction:
Vortex and spin briefly and incubate the reaction at −20°C for at least 1 h.
Spin the tube at 16000 g for 12 min at 4°C.
Remove supernatant and wash pellet with 500 ul of 70% Ethanol.
Spin again at 16000 g for 5 min at 4°C.
Air dry the pellet.
Add 6 ul of nuclease free water to the tube and incubate at 37°C for 15 min. Pipette gently to dissolve the pellet.

source_steps.description: Denature the DNA by incubating the tube at 95°C for 3 min and immediately cool on ice.

source_steps.stage: Labeling the DNA Fragments with Fluorescent Dye
source_steps.description: Spin briefly and add in the following order:

source_steps.stage: Labeling the DNA Fragments with Fluorescent Dye
source_steps.description: Vortex for 15 s, spin briefly and incubate at 18-24°C in the dark for at least one h.

source_steps.stage: Purification and Precipitation of the Labeled DNA
source_steps.description: Add 340ul of MilliQ water to the reaction and purify using the PCR purification kit or SPRI beads (1.8x Ampure) according to the manufacturer’s instructions. Elute in 20 ul of MilliQ water.

source_steps.stage: Purification and Precipitation of the Labeled DNA
source_steps.description: Check the probe concentration and absorption of the fluorochrome at the specific wavelength using the ‘microarray’ function of Nanodrop ( Figure 3 ).

background: Antibody-functionalized beads have already been washed and are now in the capture phase for DMNB-caged proteins. This segment covers bead blocking, room-temperature binding of the DMNB-protein to form immunocapture complexes, recovery and repeated cleanup of the bead-bound material by centrifugation, consolidation into a pooled suspension for the analytical branch, and initial handling of one pooled aliquot before later light-triggered release.
goal: Assemble and clean bead-bound immunocapture complexes, then prepare one pooled aliquot for the downstream photorelease-analysis branch without losing the captured protein.
constraints: Use a 1 h incubation at 20-22 C for blocking in PBS with 1% BSA and a separate 1 h incubation at the same temperature range for capture after thorough mixing.
Reflect the source quantities by encoding a total DMNB-protein preparation of 1.3 mL and a 200 uL portion per bead tube; one pooled branch should end as a 200 uL immunocapture suspension.
After binding, collect the complexes by centrifugation and model exactly three successive washes in PBS with 2 mM DTT, retaining the bead-bound complexes after each wash.
For the branch entering release preparation, pellet one 200 uL pooled aliquot, resuspend it in 200 uL PBS with 10 mM L-Met, then generate a 65 uL minus-UV aliquot kept at 4 C.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keyword arguments only, assign every call to a variable, and ensure at least two action families recur.

available_inputs.description: Prepared DMNB-protein sample solution for capture, total preparation 1.3 mL.

available_inputs.description: PBS containing 2 mM DTT for post-capture washing and resuspension.

available_inputs.description: PBS containing 10 mM L-Met for the initial release-preparation branch.

gold_program:
blocked_beads = incubate_sample(sample=rinsed_beads, temperature_c=21, duration_min=60, condition='orbital rotation in PBS/1% BSA')
bound_complexes = incubate_sample(sample=blocked_beads, temperature_c=21, duration_min=60, condition='orbital rotation with 200 uL DMNB-protein per tube from 1.3 mL preparation')
pelleted_complexes = centrifuge_sample(sample=bound_complexes, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=21)
wash1 = mix_sample(sample=pelleted_complexes, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle in PBS/2 mM DTT')
wash1_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash1, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=21)
wash2 = mix_sample(sample=wash1_pellet, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle in PBS/2 mM DTT')
wash2_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash2, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=21)
wash3 = mix_sample(sample=wash2_pellet, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle in PBS/2 mM DTT and combine three tubes to 200 uL')
release_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash3, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=21)
lmet_resuspended = vortex_mix(sample=release_pellet, duration_s=5, speed_rpm=1800, temperature_c=21)
minus_uv_cooled = cool_sample(sample=lmet_resuspended, temperature_c=4, duration_min=1, device='refrigerator')

source_steps.description: Add 200 μL of PBS to the beads and rinse as above (steps 2 and 3).
Add 300 μL of PBS/1% BSA to the beads, incubate in orbital rotator for 1 h at 20°C–22°C.
Collect the beads by centrifugation and rinse two times as above (steps 2 and 3).
Make 1.3 mL of a DMNB-protein solution. If working with bioconjugted EGFP D117C-DMNB, make solution in PBS at 25 μg/mL.
If working with cell lysate containing biosynthetic SUMOstar I106C-DMNB, dilute the lysate 1:10 in PBS with added cOmplete™, EDTA-free Protease Inhibitor Cocktail.
Add 200 μL of the DMNB-protein solution to each tube containing the beads. Mix thoroughly by vortex mixing and incubate with orbital rotation at 20°C–22°C for 1 h. Troubleshooting 5
Collect the beads by centrifugation. At his point, the IC complexes are fully assembled.
Wash the IC complexes three times with 300 μL PBS/2 mM DTT as above (steps 2 and 3).

Use 200 μL of PBS/2 mM DTT to combine and resuspend the IC complexes from each 3 tubes into one aliquot, finally resulting in 2 tubes, each containing 200 μL of IC suspension. One aliquot is required to make samples for HPLC/ESI-MS analysis (steps 17–23), while the other aliquot will be used to make samples for SDS-PAGE analysis (steps 24–28).

source_steps.stage: Light-controlled photochemical release of uncaged proteins
source_steps.description: Collect the IC complexes from one of the 200 μL preparation (step 16) by centrifuging. Remove and discard the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Light-controlled photochemical release of uncaged proteins
source_steps.description: Add 200 μL of PBS/10 mM L-Met buffer, resuspend very well by pipetting and vortex mixing.

source_steps.stage: Light-controlled photochemical release of uncaged proteins
source_steps.description: Make two 65 μL aliquots: -UV. Transfer 65 μL of IC suspension to a new tube. Keep at 4°C.

background: This planning item focuses on the detergent titration stage used before Drs2-Cdc50 reconstitution. Three parallel reactions with different starting CHAPS conditions are equalized with reconstitution buffer, gently mixed at room temperature, read by absorbance after transfer to a 96-well plate, then returned to their original material for a second detergent challenge and a final absorbance readout. The important feature is maintaining the identity of each titration condition across both measurement rounds.
goal: Complete the two-round CHAPS titration assay for three parallel conditions, including initial buffer completion, equilibration, plate-based absorbance measurement, restoration to source material, second CHAPS addition, and final absorbance measurement.
constraints: Represent the three titration conditions as separate samples corresponding to initial CHAPS additions of 0, 1, and 2 uL.
Complete the reactions with reconstitution buffer using 41.7 uL, 40.7 uL, and 39.7 uL respectively.
Use room temperature approximated as 25.0 C and gentle mixing approximated as 30 rpm for each 25.0 min equilibration.
Use a 100.0 uL transfer from each condition to the 96-well plate before each absorbance read.
Measure absorbance at 550 nm for both readouts and treat the plate read as containing three technical entries.
After the first plate read, return each sampled aliquot to the corresponding source condition before adding more CHAPS.
Apply a second CHAPS addition of 3.0 uL of 0.5 M CHAPS to each condition before the second equilibration.
Keep later actions dependent on the outputs of the earlier actions for the same condition.

available_inputs.name: tube_chaps_0
available_inputs.description: Reaction tube for the condition that started with 0 uL CHAPS.

available_inputs.name: tube_chaps_1
available_inputs.description: Reaction tube for the condition that started with 1 uL CHAPS.

available_inputs.name: tube_chaps_2
available_inputs.description: Reaction tube for the condition that started with 2 uL CHAPS.

available_inputs.name: reconstitution_buffer
available_inputs.description: Buffer used to normalize the reaction volumes.

available_inputs.name: chaps_0_5M
available_inputs.description: 0.5 M CHAPS stock used for the second detergent addition.

available_inputs.name: plate_96well
available_inputs.description: 96-well plate used for absorbance measurements.

gold_program:
prep0 = add_reagent(sample=tube_chaps_0, reagent=reconstitution_buffer, volume_ul=41.7, mixing="pipette mix")
prep1 = add_reagent(sample=tube_chaps_1, reagent=reconstitution_buffer, volume_ul=40.7, mixing="pipette mix")
prep2 = add_reagent(sample=tube_chaps_2, reagent=reconstitution_buffer, volume_ul=39.7, mixing="pipette mix")
inc0 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=prep0, temperature_c=25.0, speed_rpm=30, duration_min=25.0)
inc1 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=prep1, temperature_c=25.0, speed_rpm=30, duration_min=25.0)
inc2 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=prep2, temperature_c=25.0, speed_rpm=30, duration_min=25.0)
plate_r1 = aliquot_sample(sample=inc0, destination=plate_96well, volume_ul=100.0, aliquot_count=3)
read1 = measure_signal(sample=plate_96well, assay="absorbance", wavelength_nm=550, replicate_n=3)
second_chaps = add_reagent(sample=inc0, reagent=chaps_0_5M, volume_ul=3.0, mixing="pipette mix")
inc_r2 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=second_chaps, temperature_c=25.0, speed_rpm=30, duration_min=25.0)
plate_r2 = aliquot_sample(sample=inc_r2, destination=plate_96well, volume_ul=100.0, aliquot_count=3)
read2 = measure_signal(sample=plate_96well, assay="absorbance", wavelength_nm=550, replicate_n=3)
