source_steps.description: Add 0, 1, and 2 µL of a 0.5 M CHAPS solution (see Recipe 2), and 41.7, 40.7 and 39.7 µL of reconstitution buffer (see Recipe 1), respectively, and incubate with end-over-end rotation at 30 rpm and room temperature for 25 min.
Pipette 100 µL from each reaction tube into a well of a 96-well plate. Measure the absorbance at 550 nm in the plate reader.
Pipette the 100 µL from each well back into their original tubes and add 3 µL of a 0.5 M CHAPS solution (see Recipe 2) to each tube. Incubate with end-over-end rotation at 30 rpm for 25 min.

Pipette 100 µL from each reaction tube into a well of a 96-well plate. Measure the absorbance at 550 nm in the microplate reader. The CHAPS concentration used for the reconstitution should correspond to a concentration between the R half and R sol , indicated by the blue area in Figure 2 . For this lipid composition and concentration, 13 mM CHAPS is required.
In a 1.5 mL reaction tube and to a final volume of 250 µL, add:
Add 10 µL of a 1 µg/µL Drs2-Cdc50 stock in elution buffer containing 0.05% DDM (final concentration 0.04 µg/µL) and incubate at room temperature with end-over-end rotation at 30 rpm for 5 min.
It is advised to additionally prepare a mock sample with 10 µL of protein elution buffer instead of protein stock solution.
Detailed information regarding the protein-to-lipid ratio can be found under General note 2.
Put a clamp on one side of a piece of pre-rinsed dialysis tubing, carefully pipette the sample into the tubing, and seal it with a second clamp.
Add the tubing to a 1 L glass cylinder filled with 900 mL of reconstitution buffer (see Recipe 1) and incubate while stirring at 4 °C for 3–4 h (see Figure 3 ).
Discard the buffer and fill up the glass cylinder with 900 mL of fresh reconstitution buffer (see Recipe 1). Incubate while stirring at 4 °C overnight.
Discard the buffer and fill up the glass cylinder with 900 mL of fresh reconstitution buffer (see Recipe 1). Incubate while stirring at 4 °C for 4 h.

background: A gel-extracted RV-G DNA fragment is being purified for insertion into the pVSV-dG-GFP-2.6 vector. The current segment covers silica-column cleanup of the dissolved gel sample, recovery and quantification of purified DNA, and the temperature-controlled restriction digest stage that precedes ligation.
goal: Obtain purified RV-G DNA from the dissolved gel sample, document its concentration, and complete the digestion and heat-inactivation handling needed before ligation setup.
constraints: Start from a gel solution already dissolved after heating at 60 C, then perform column-style cleanup with a wash using 700 uL wash buffer.
Use repeated centrifugation at 12000 x g, including 1 min spins during cleanup and a final 2 min spin after elution.
Represent elution as 30-50 uL warmed elution buffer incubated for 5 min near room temperature before DNA recovery into a clean 1.5 mL tube.
Include a NanoDrop-like DNA concentration measurement and a separate record of that concentration result.
Model the digestion stage in PCR-tube format with incubation at 37 C for 18 h followed by heat inactivation at 80 C for 20 min.
Use only the provided action-pool functions, and make later steps consume outputs generated earlier in the program.

available_inputs.name: solubilized_gel_dna
available_inputs.description: RV-G gel slice dissolved in binding buffer and ready for purification.

available_inputs.name: genejet_column_loaded_sample
available_inputs.description: GeneJET column containing the applied dissolved gel sample.

available_inputs.name: clean_microcentrifuge_tube
available_inputs.description: Fresh 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube intended to receive eluate.

available_inputs.name: warmed_elution_buffer
available_inputs.description: Pre-warmed elution buffer for DNA recovery from the purification column.

available_inputs.name: restriction_digest_mix
available_inputs.description: Restriction digestion reaction prepared with purified DNA in a 0.2 mL PCR tube.

gold_program:
purification_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=genejet_column_loaded_sample, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=22)
wash_hold = incubate_sample(sample=purification_spin, temperature_c=22, duration_min=1, condition="column with 700 uL wash buffer")
wash_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_hold, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=22)
column_dry_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_spin, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=22)
elution_hold = incubate_sample(sample=column_dry_spin, temperature_c=25, duration_min=5, condition="30-50 uL warmed elution buffer into clean 1.5 mL tube")
eluted_dna = centrifuge_sample(sample=elution_hold, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=2, temperature_c=22)
dna_measurement = measure_signal(sample=eluted_dna, assay="nanodrop_dna_concentration", wavelength_nm=260, replicate_n=1)
dna_record = record_measurement(sample=dna_measurement, metric="DNA concentration", unit="ng/uL", note="purified RV-G eluate")
digested_dna = incubate_sample(sample=restriction_digest_mix, temperature_c=37, duration_min=1080, condition="static")
heat_inactivated_digest = heat_sample(sample=digested_dna, temperature_c=80, duration_min=20, device="thermal cycler")

background: A fungal extracellular vesicle preparation has already been separated on an iodixanol density gradient and now needs downstream recovery of the gradient fractions, cleanup by ultracentrifugation, and preservation for later analysis. The current stage focuses on harvesting equal-volume fractions, diluting them in filtered PBS, re-pelleting vesicle material, removing residual liquid, resuspending the pellets in a small final volume, and freezing the recovered material.
goal: Generate the procedure to recover extracellular vesicle material from the completed iodixanol gradient and prepare the cleaned fraction samples for frozen storage.
constraints: Start from a gradient sample that has already finished the enrichment ultracentrifugation stage.
Recover 12 fractions of 1 mL each from the top toward the bottom into fresh tubes.
Add 9 mL of 0.22 μm-filtered PBS to each fraction before the cleanup ultracentrifugation.
Use a cleanup spin at 100000 x g for 60 min at 4°C after dilution.
Include removal of remaining supernatant before final resuspension, represented by a brief drying or draining operation.
Resuspend the recovered pellet material in 100 μL PBS with active mixing, then transfer the processed fractions to sterile tubes for storage at -80°C.

available_inputs.name: iodixanol_gradient
available_inputs.description: Completed iodixanol density gradient containing extracellular vesicle material after the enrichment ultracentrifugation.

available_inputs.name: pbs_022um
available_inputs.description: Sterile 1x PBS filtered through a 0.22 μm membrane.

available_inputs.name: fraction_tubes
available_inputs.description: Fresh ultracentrifuge-compatible tubes for collecting gradient fractions.

available_inputs.name: storage_tubes
available_inputs.description: Sterile tubes intended for final frozen storage of recovered fractions.

gold_program:
spin_complete = centrifuge_sample(sample=iodixanol_gradient, speed_xg=100000, duration_min=1080, temperature_c=4)
fractions = collect_fraction(sample=spin_complete, phase="F1-F12 top_to_bottom", destination=fraction_tubes, volume_ul=1000)
diluted_fractions = add_reagent(sample=fractions, reagent=pbs_022um, volume_ul=9000, mixing="invert to dilute each fraction")
washed_pellets = centrifuge_sample(sample=diluted_fractions, speed_xg=100000, duration_min=60, temperature_c=4)
drained_fractions = dry_sample(sample=washed_pellets, method="air dry after inversion on absorbent paper", temperature_c=4, duration_min=1)
resuspended_fractions = add_reagent(sample=drained_fractions, reagent=pbs_022um, volume_ul=100, mixing="pipette several times and rinse tube edges")
storage_aliquots = aliquot_sample(sample=resuspended_fractions, destination=storage_tubes, volume_ul=100, aliquot_count=12)
frozen_fractions = store_sample(sample=storage_aliquots, temperature_c=-80, duration_h=24, container=storage_tubes)

background: A mammalian suspension culture expressing the SARS-CoV-2 spike ectodomain has completed the initial transfection period and now needs medium supplementation followed by harvest. The task is to prepare the media under the specified temperature conditions, support the remaining expression phase, and clarify the culture to recover spike-containing supernatant for purification.
goal: Carry out the medium-conditioning, extended expression, and harvest clarification stage to produce filtered supernatant ready for downstream spike protein purification.
constraints: FreeStyle 293F medium must be warmed to 37 C for about 45 min before it is used, and Hyclone SFM4HEK293 medium must equilibrate at room temperature around 22 C for about 45 min.

The culture is supplemented in two additions: 80 mL of Hyclone medium first, then enough FreeStyle 293F medium to bring the total volume to 1 L; each addition requires gentle mixing rather than harsh agitation.
After supplementation, the culture must continue incubation for 5 days at 37 C under shaking conditions consistent with 120 rpm and 9% CO2.
For harvest, the final 1 L culture is represented as two 500 mL portions that are each centrifuged at 4000 x g for 30 min at 22 C.
The retained material after centrifugation is the clarified supernatant, and each clarified portion must be passed through a 0.20 um complete filtration unit into a clean collection vessel.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keep all calls assigned to variables, and preserve dependencies so later steps consume outputs or stage-specific inputs from earlier work.

available_inputs.name: cell_culture_800ml
available_inputs.description: Transiently transfected 800 mL mammalian suspension culture after the initial expression period.

available_inputs.name: freestyle_293f_medium
available_inputs.description: FreeStyle 293F medium reserved for expanding the culture volume.

available_inputs.name: hyclone_sfm4hek293_medium
available_inputs.description: Hyclone SFM4HEK293 medium used during the enhancer addition stage.

available_inputs.name: culture_half_a
available_inputs.description: First 500 mL portion of the 1 L culture prepared for harvest centrifugation.

available_inputs.name: culture_half_b
available_inputs.description: Second 500 mL portion of the 1 L culture prepared for harvest centrifugation.

gold_program:
heated_freestyle = heat_sample(sample=freestyle_293f_medium, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=45.0, device='water bath')
incubated_hyclone = incubate_sample(sample=hyclone_sfm4hek293_medium, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=45.0, condition='static')
mixed_after_hyclone = mix_sample(sample=cell_culture_800ml, method='rocking', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
mixed_to_one_liter = mix_sample(sample=mixed_after_hyclone, method='rocking', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
expressed_culture = incubate_sample(sample=mixed_to_one_liter, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=7200.0, condition='shaking 120 rpm, 9% CO2')
spun_half_a = centrifuge_sample(sample=culture_half_a, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=30.0, temperature_c=22.0)
spun_half_b = centrifuge_sample(sample=culture_half_b, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=30.0, temperature_c=22.0)
filtered_supernatant_a = filter_sample(sample=spun_half_a, filter_type='complete filtration unit', pore_size_um=0.2, destination='clean collection vessel')
filtered_supernatant_b = filter_sample(sample=spun_half_b, filter_type='complete filtration unit', pore_size_um=0.2, destination='clean collection vessel')

source_steps.description: Prepare DNA/transfection reagent complex: dilute 2.25 mL of Turbo 293 transfection reagent into 37.5 mL of Opti-MEM I medium in a 125 mL flask, gently swirl to mix. Incubate at room temperature (20°C–23°C) for 5 min.
Meanwhile, dilute 750 μg of spike DNA into 37.5 mL of Opit-MEM I Medium in the funnel of a sterile filter top, attached to a 50 mL tube.

Add diluted DNA to Turbo 293 complex by pouring gently down the side of the flask wall, mix gently by swirling and incubate at room temperature (20°C–23°C) for 15 min to allow the formation of DNA-Turbo 293 complex.

Add DNA-Turbo 293 complex, made in step 4, to the 800 mL of cell culture made in step 1, by tilting the cell culture flask at approximately 45 degrees and gently pour the DNA-Turbo 293 complex into the cell culture flask, gently swirl to mix. Leave the flask at room temperature (20°C–23°C) to incubate for 15 min.
Return the flask to incubate at 37°C, 9% CO 2 , 120 rpm, for 16–18 h.
Warm FreeStyle 293F Medium in 37°C water bath until warm, approximately 45 min to 1 h.
Place Hyclone SFM4HEK293 medium at room temperature (20°C–23°C), for approximately 45 min to 1 h.
Add 80 mL Hyclone Medium to cell culture flask, swirl gently to mix.
Add Freestyle 293F medium to cell culture flask until volume reaches 1 L, swirl gently to mix.
Return to incubator at 37°C, 9% CO 2 , 120 rpm, for 5 more days.

source_steps.description: Divide 1 L cell culture into two 500 mL conical tubes and centrifuge at 4,000 × g for 30 min at 22°C.

background: Immune cells isolated from DSS-colitic mice are at the intracellular phospho-Smad2/3 staining stage for flow cytometry. The remaining work is to carry the cells through viability staining, fixation, methanol permeabilization, intracellular antibody labeling, and final preparation under the required temperature and light conditions for later acquisition.
goal: Process the cell sample through intracellular phospho-Smad2/3 staining and leave it ready for storage before flow-cytometer acquisition.
constraints: Use pelleting spins of 420 x g for 5 min during the workflow, starting from cells already in a 96-well round-bottom format.
Include 100 uL viability dye with a 10 min incubation at 37 C, followed by a PBS wash before fixation.
Fix with 100 uL of 2% PFA in PBS for 10 min at 37 C, then permeabilize with 100 uL ice-cold methanol for 60 min at 4 C in the dark.
Use two 200 uL FACS buffer washes after methanol treatment before intracellular antibody staining.

Perform intracellular staining in 100 uL FACS buffer containing 2 uL phospho-Smad2/3 antibody for 60 min at room temperature, then wash with 100 uL and 200 uL FACS buffer and finish in 200 uL FACS buffer.
Maintain one continuous sample lineage and end with storage at 4 C until acquisition.

available_inputs.description: Cell suspension already transferred into a 96-well round-bottom plate for intracellular staining.

available_inputs.name: zombie_aqua_1x
available_inputs.description: 1x fixable LIVE/DEAD Zombie Aqua staining solution.

available_inputs.name: pbs_1x
available_inputs.description: 1x PBS for washing.

available_inputs.name: pfa_2pct
available_inputs.description: 2% paraformaldehyde in PBS for fixation.

available_inputs.name: methanol_cold
available_inputs.description: Ice-cold methanol for permeabilization.

gold_program:
step1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=cells_96well, speed_xg=420, duration_min=5, temperature_c=25)
step2 = add_reagent(sample=step1, reagent=zombie_aqua_1x, volume_ul=100, mixing="resuspend")
step3 = incubate_sample(sample=step2, temperature_c=37, duration_min=10, condition="static")
step4 = wash_sample(sample=step3, wash_buffer=pbs_1x, volume_ul=100, repeat_index=1)
step5 = add_reagent(sample=step4, reagent=pfa_2pct, volume_ul=100, mixing="resuspend")
step6 = incubate_sample(sample=step5, temperature_c=37, duration_min=10, condition="static")
step7 = add_reagent(sample=step6, reagent=methanol_cold, volume_ul=100, mixing="resuspend")
step8 = incubate_sample(sample=step7, temperature_c=4, duration_min=60, condition="protected from light")
step9 = wash_sample(sample=step8, wash_buffer=facs_buffer, volume_ul=200, repeat_index=1)
step10 = add_reagent(sample=step9, reagent=phospho_smad23_antibody, volume_ul=2, mixing="mix")
step11 = incubate_sample(sample=step10, temperature_c=25, duration_min=60, condition="static")
step12 = store_sample(sample=step11, temperature_c=4, duration_h=4, container="flow_cytometry_compatible_tube")

source_steps.description: Transfer cells to a 96-well round bottom plate to allow for effective pelleting. Centrifuge for 5 min at 420 × g . Discard supernatant.

Perform live/dead staining by resuspending the cells in 100 μL of 1× fixable LIVE/DEAD stain (Zombie Aqua Fixable Viability Dye) prepared by diluting 1 μL dye in 1000 μL 1× PBS. Incubate for 10 min at 37°C.
Wash cells by adding 100 μL of PBS. Centrifuge for 5 min at 420 × g . Discard supernatant.
Fix cells by resuspending in 100 μL 2% paraformaldehyde (PFA) in PBS. Incubate at 37°C for 10 min. Centrifuge for 5 min at 420 × g . Discard supernatant.
Resuspend cells in 100 μL of ice-cold methanol. Incubate at 4°C for 60 min in the dark.
Wash cells by adding 200 μL FACS buffer. Centrifuge for 5 min at 420 × g . Discard supernatant.
Wash cells again by adding 200 μL FACS buffer. Centrifuge for 5 min at 420 × g . Discard supernatant.
Perform intracellular staining by resuspending cells in 100 μL FACS buffer containing 2 μL of phospho-Smad2/3 antibody. Incubate for 60 min at RT.
Wash cells by adding 100 μL FACS buffer. Centrifuge for 5 min at 420 × g . Discard supernatant.
Wash cells again by adding 200 μL FACS buffer. Centrifuge for 5 min at 420 × g . Discard supernatant.
Resuspend cells in 200 μL FACS Buffer. Transfer to tubes compatible with your flow cytometer’s sample injection port.

Store at 4°C until samples are acquired on a flow cytometer. See Figure 2 for representative flow cytometry data. Flow cytometric analysis of phospho-Smad2/3 in DCs and CD4 + T cells TGF−β induces Smad2/3 phosphorylation and deletion of Smad7 enhances TGF-β-induced phospho-Smad2/3. (A) Representative FACS histograms (left) and median fluorescence intensities (MFI)s (right) of phospho- Smad2/3 in bone marrow-derived DCs (BMDCs) stimulated with or without TGF-β (2 ng/mL), for 1 h (n = 6). Adapted from reference. 1 (B) Representative FACS histograms (left) and MFIs (right) of phospho- Smad2/3 in naive CD4 + T cells from Smad7 fl/fl and T cell specific-Smad7 −/− mice stimulated with or without TGF-β (2.5 ng/mL), for 1 h (n = 4). Data representative of ≥3 independent experiments. Means ± SEMs. ∗∗∗p < 0.001 by unpaired t test. Adapted from reference. 1

background: A primary lung patient-derived xenograft tumor sample has already undergone initial dissociation and is now being advanced toward a viable adherent culture for later CFSE labeling. The current stage involves recovering dissociated cells, cleaning the suspension, allowing viable cells to attach in culture, and then harvesting those attached cells into a washed pellet for the next staining phase.
goal: Process the dissociated lung PDX material through recovery, short-term attachment culture, and harvest so that a washed viable cell pellet is prepared for downstream staining.
constraints: Include a 37 C incubation for 30 min of the dissociation tube before the second dissociation run, and represent that second run only with available device/button actions.

Use centrifugation at 300 x g for 1 min near 22 C immediately after the second dissociation round, and later use 300 x g for 7 min near 22 C for both the filtered suspension and the post-detachment wash workflow.
Maintain a single dependency chain from the initial C tube through recovery into short-term culture, then include a 37 C incubation for attached cells in complete medium for 12-24 h before harvest.

Model the two PBS washes of the attached culture as repeated allowed actions on the same sample lineage, then include a 37 C, 5 min detachment incubation before transferring into the tube-based harvest path.
Use only provided action-pool functions, assign every call to a variable, keep 6-12 top-level steps, and reuse at least two action families more than once.

pelleted_filtered_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=filtered_cell_suspension, speed_xg=300, duration_min=7.0, temperature_c=22.0)
attached_primary_cells = incubate_sample(sample=pelleted_filtered_cells, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=720.0, condition='5% CO2 static complete medium')
pbs_wash_1 = incubate_sample(sample=attached_primary_cells, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=1.0, condition='PBS wash')
pbs_wash_2 = incubate_sample(sample=pbs_wash_1, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=1.0, condition='PBS wash')
detached_cells = incubate_sample(sample=pbs_wash_2, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=5.0, condition='2x TE detachment in CO2 incubator')
pelleted_detached_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=detached_cell_tube, speed_xg=300, duration_min=7.0, temperature_c=22.0)
washed_final_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=pelleted_detached_cells, speed_xg=300, duration_min=7.0, temperature_c=22.0)

source_steps.description: Detach the C tube and centrifuge at 300 × g for 1 min at room temperate (RT, 22 ± 2°C) to collect the dissociated cells.

source_steps.description: Resuspend the dissociated cell pellet with 10 mL of culture media (complete RPMI 1640) and transfer the cell suspension into a cell strainer (70 μm) placed on a 50 mL conical tube. Add 10 mL of culture media (complete RPMI 1640) to wash cells through the strainer.

source_steps.stage: Collection of lung PDX tumor from immunodeficient mouse and establishment of a primary cell line
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the filtered cell suspension at 300 × g for 7 min. Remove the supernatant completely and resuspend the cell pellet in 5 mL of cell culture media (complete RPMI 1640).

source_steps.description: Add 100 μL of 0.4% trypan blue solution to 100 μL cell suspension.

source_steps.stage: Collection of lung PDX tumor from immunodeficient mouse and establishment of a primary cell line
source_steps.description: Seed 0.5–1 × 10 7 viable cells per a 90 mm culture dish with 10 mL complete culture media. Incubate cells at 37°C in a 5% CO 2 incubator for -12–24 h.
Remove unattached dead cells and floating murine-immune cells with 5 mL of PBS (for a 90 mm culture dish) twice.
Add 1 mL of 2× TE solution to detach the viable cells from the culture dish. Incubate the cells in 37°C CO 2 incubator for 5 min.
Add complete culture media of two times the volume of 2× TE solution. Transfer the detached cells to a 15 mL conical tube and centrifuge at 300 × g for 7 min at RT.
Remove the supernatant and resuspend and wash the cell pellet with 5 mL PBS. Centrifuge at 300 × g for 7 min at RT.

background: Activated mouse, human, or Jurkat T cells have already been prepared and counted for the staining phase of a spectral flow cytometry experiment. At this stage, the workflow centers on washing the aliquoted cells, performing surface-marker staining, then carrying out fixation, permeabilization, and intranuclear Nrf2 staining under the required temperature and light-protection conditions.
goal: Process the prepared T-cell staining tubes through the remaining local staining segment so the samples are ready for downstream flow cytometry acquisition of intranuclear Nrf2.
constraints: Start from counted staining tubes containing about 0.5-0.6 million cells each, and maintain a single continuous dependency chain through wash, stain, fixation/permeabilization, and intranuclear staining states.
Include repeated centrifugation-based wash handling at 500 x g for 4 min at 4 C before and after the surface staining stage, with later steps consuming the washed output.
Represent the surface staining phase as brief mixing followed by incubation at 4 C for 20-25 min under light-protected conditions, using the prepared antibody cocktail in a 100 uL staining volume.

Represent fixation/permeabilization as brief resuspension or vortexing followed by incubation with 1X reagent in a 100 uL per-tube volume, using either the overnight 4 C option or the 1 h room-temperature option within 22-25 C while protected from light.
After fixation, include handling with 1X permeabilization buffer and a centrifugation step at 500 x g for 4 min at 4 C before intranuclear staining.
Model the Nrf2 staining as mixing with antibody diluted 1:50 in 1X permeabilization buffer and incubating for 1 h at 22-25 C in the dark.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keyword arguments only, 6-12 top-level assigned calls, and reuse at least two action names multiple times.

available_inputs.name: counted_tcell_tubes
available_inputs.description: Counted mouse, human, or Jurkat T-cell aliquots in 5 mL round-bottom polystyrene tubes for staining.

available_inputs.name: surface_antibody_cocktail
available_inputs.description: Prepared surface-marker antibody mix for the relevant cell type.

available_inputs.name: fixperm_1x
available_inputs.description: Freshly prepared 1X fixation/permeabilization solution.

available_inputs.name: permeabilization_buffer_1x
available_inputs.description: Prepared 1X permeabilization buffer.

available_inputs.name: nrf2_antibody_diluted
available_inputs.description: Nrf2 antibody diluted 1:50 in 1X permeabilization buffer.

gold_program:
wash1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=counted_tcell_tubes, speed_xg=500, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
wash2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash1, speed_xg=500, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
surface_stain_mix = vortex_mix(sample=wash2, duration_s=3, speed_rpm=1200, temperature_c=4)
surface_stained = incubate_sample(sample=surface_stain_mix, temperature_c=4, duration_min=25, condition='protected from light')
post_surface_wash1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=surface_stained, speed_xg=500, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
post_surface_wash2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=post_surface_wash1, speed_xg=500, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
fixperm_mixed = vortex_mix(sample=post_surface_wash2, duration_s=3, speed_rpm=1200, temperature_c=22)
fixed_cells = incubate_sample(sample=fixperm_mixed, temperature_c=22, duration_min=60, condition='protected from light')
perm_washed = centrifuge_sample(sample=fixed_cells, speed_xg=500, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
nrf2_mixed = mix_sample(sample=perm_washed, method='pipetting', duration_s=5, speed='gentle')
nrf2_stained = incubate_sample(sample=nrf2_mixed, temperature_c=22, duration_min=60, condition='protected from light')

source_steps.stage: Surface and intranuclear staining of activated T cells
source_steps.description: Count the cells and transfer 0.5–0.6 million cells (mouse, human, or Jurkat T cells) into a 5 mL round bottom polystyrene tube for staining and acquisition of flow cytometry data.

source_steps.stage: Surface and intranuclear staining of activated T cells
source_steps.description: Wash the cells twice with 2 mL FACS buffer, centrifuge at 500 x g for 4 min at 4°C and carefully discard the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Surface and intranuclear staining of activated T cells
source_steps.description: Prepare the surface antibody cocktail as listed in Tables 1 , 2 , and 3 for the respective cell type.

source_steps.stage: Surface and intranuclear staining of activated T cells
source_steps.description: Add 100 μL of the antibody mix to each tube containing the cell pellet and vortex briefly to mix.

source_steps.stage: Surface and intranuclear staining of activated T cells
source_steps.description: Incubate at 4°C for 20–25 min in the dark, protected from light.

source_steps.stage: Surface and intranuclear staining of activated T cells
source_steps.description: Wash the cells twice with 2 mL FACS buffer as in step 26.

source_steps.stage: Surface and intranuclear staining of activated T cells
source_steps.description: Prepare the 1X Fixation/Permeabilization solution by diluting the 4X concentrate with the supplied Fixation/Permeabilization diluent from the kit as per manufacturer’s instructions. (For e.g., for 4 tubes, mix 100 μL concentrate with 300 μL diluent; use 100 μL per tube).

source_steps.stage: Surface and intranuclear staining of activated T cells
source_steps.description: Briefly vortex the cell pellet and add 100 μL of 1X Fixation/Permeabilization solution to each tube.

source_steps.stage: Surface and intranuclear staining of activated T cells
source_steps.description: Incubate the samples at 4°C for 16 h (overnight) or 1 h at 22°C–25°C protected from light.

source_steps.stage: Surface and intranuclear staining of activated T cells
source_steps.description: Prepare 1X permeabilization buffer by diluting the supplied 10X stock in autoclaved pure water.

source_steps.stage: Surface and intranuclear staining of activated T cells
source_steps.description: Add 1 mL of 1X permeabilization buffer per tube of the fixed cells, centrifuge at 500 x g for 4 min at 4°C and carefully discard the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Surface and intranuclear staining of activated T cells
source_steps.description: Add 50 μL of Nrf2 antibody diluted 1:50 in 1X permeabilization buffer, mix and incubate for 1h at 22°C–25°C in the dark.

background: Activated T cells are ready for the staining phase used before spectral flow analysis of nuclear Nrf2. The remaining work is a local sequence of cold washes, surface-marker staining, fixation/permeabilization, intranuclear antibody incubation, and final cleanup washes while maintaining light protection where appropriate.
goal: Carry the activated T-cell pellet through the staining workflow so that it ends as a washed, fixed, permeabilized, Nrf2-stained cell preparation suitable for downstream flow acquisition.
constraints: Use repeated centrifugation washes at 500 x g for 4 min at 4 C during the wash portions of this segment.
Surface staining requires brief mixing after adding 100 uL antibody cocktail, followed by a 20-25 min incubation at 4 C protected from light.
Apply 100 uL of 1X fixation/permeabilization solution per tube and use a valid incubation choice of either 16 h at 4 C or 60 min at about 22-25 C, with light protection.
Before Nrf2 staining, fixed cells must be washed in 1X permeabilization buffer and re-pelleted; Nrf2 staining uses 50 uL antibody at 1:50 dilution for 60 min at 22-25 C in the dark.
Finish with one wash in 1X permeabilization buffer and one wash in FACS buffer, preserving a single dependency chain from the starting pellet to the final stained cells.

available_inputs.name: surface_antibody_mix
available_inputs.description: Prepared surface antibody cocktail for the relevant T-cell sample

available_inputs.name: fixperm_1x
available_inputs.description: 1X fixation/permeabilization reagent prepared from the kit concentrate and diluent

available_inputs.name: perm_buffer_1x
available_inputs.description: 1X permeabilization buffer prepared from the supplied stock

available_inputs.name: nrf2_antibody_diluted
available_inputs.description: Nrf2 antibody diluted 1:50 in 1X permeabilization buffer

wash1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=activated_tcell_pellet, speed_xg=500, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
wash2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash1, speed_xg=500, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
surface_mix = vortex_mix(sample=wash2, duration_s=3, speed_rpm=2000, temperature_c=4)
surface_stained = incubate_sample(sample=surface_mix, temperature_c=4, duration_min=25, condition='protected from light')
post_surface_wash1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=surface_stained, speed_xg=500, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
post_surface_wash2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=post_surface_wash1, speed_xg=500, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
fixed_prep = vortex_mix(sample=post_surface_wash2, duration_s=3, speed_rpm=2000, temperature_c=4)
fixed_cells = incubate_sample(sample=fixed_prep, temperature_c=25, duration_min=60, condition='protected from light')
perm_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=fixed_cells, speed_xg=500, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)
nrf2_mixed = mix_sample(sample=perm_pellet, method='pipetting', duration_s=5, speed='gentle')
nrf2_stained = incubate_sample(sample=nrf2_mixed, temperature_c=25, duration_min=60, condition='protected from light')
post_nrf2_wash = centrifuge_sample(sample=nrf2_stained, speed_xg=500, duration_min=4, temperature_c=4)

background: A Salmonella promoter-reporter strain is being prepared for downstream screening. The culture has already been grown to mid-log phase in LB, and the next stage is to convert the cells into electrocompetent material by chilling, pelleting, washing, and resuspending them in glycerol before making small cold aliquots suitable for immediate plasmid addition or short-term handling on ice.
goal: Process the mid-log culture into washed, electrocompetent cell aliquots in cold 1.5 mL tubes, preserving the low-temperature conditions required for later electroporation.
constraints: Begin from a 50 mL mid-log LB culture and split it into two equal portions of about 25 mL each before competency preparation.
Use refrigerated centrifugation at 3600 x g for 10 min at 4 C for each pellet-forming spin.
Perform two ice-cold sterile water washes, each using 10 mL ddH2O with vigorous resuspension before the next spin.
Keep the material cold throughout the competency workflow; chilled equipment or on-ice handling should be reflected by explicit cooling steps where appropriate.
After the final wash, each pellet should be resuspended in about 250 uL sterile 10 percent glycerol.
Finish by creating 50 uL aliquots in cold 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes.
Do not include electroporation, recovery in LB, plating, or glycerol stock storage.
Use only the provided action-pool functions and keep the program at the sample-processing level.

available_inputs.name: midlog_culture
available_inputs.description: 50 mL Salmonella Typhimurium culture in LB at OD 0.5-0.6

available_inputs.name: ice_cold_ddh2o
available_inputs.description: Sterile ice-cold ddH2O for washing electrocompetent cells

available_inputs.name: glycerol_10pct
available_inputs.description: Sterile ice-cold 10% (v/v) glycerol

available_inputs.name: falcon_tubes
available_inputs.description: Two 50 mL conical tubes for splitting the culture

available_inputs.name: eppendorf_tubes
available_inputs.description: Cold 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes for final aliquots

gold_program:
split_culture = aliquot_sample(sample=midlog_culture, destination=falcon_tubes, volume_ul=25000.0, aliquot_count=2)
chilled_split_culture = cool_sample(sample=split_culture, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=5.0, device="ice")
first_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=chilled_split_culture, speed_xg=3600, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
first_wash = add_reagent(sample=first_pellet, reagent=ice_cold_ddh2o, volume_ul=10000.0, mixing="vigorous resuspension")
second_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=first_wash, speed_xg=3600, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
second_wash = add_reagent(sample=second_pellet, reagent=ice_cold_ddh2o, volume_ul=10000.0, mixing="vigorous resuspension")
third_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=second_wash, speed_xg=3600, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
glycerol_resuspension = add_reagent(sample=third_pellet, reagent=glycerol_10pct, volume_ul=250.0, mixing="resuspend pellet")
cold_competent_cells = cool_sample(sample=glycerol_resuspension, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=3.0, device="ice")
competent_aliquots = aliquot_sample(sample=cold_competent_cells, destination=eppendorf_tubes, volume_ul=50.0, aliquot_count=10)
protocol_title: High-Throughput Chemical Screening for Inhibitors of Salmonella Pathogenicity Island 2

source_steps.description: Dilute 1 mL of overnight culture into 50 mL of LB, and incubate the culture at 37°C with shaking at 250 rpm until an OD of 0.5–0.6 (mid-log phase, ∼2.5 h).

Transfer the cells to two 50 mL Falcon conical tubes (∼25 mL culture per tube). Pellet by centrifugation for 10 min at 3,600 g in a pre-chilled (4°C) centrifuge. Remove promptly and discard supernatant.
Wash the cells by vigorously resuspending in 10 mL ice-cold sterile ddH 2 O. Pellet by centrifugation for 10 min at 3,600 g in a pre-chilled (4°C) centrifuge. Remove promptly and discard supernatant.
Repeat step d once.
Resuspend each pellet in ∼250 μL ice-cold, sterile 10% (v/v) glycerol. Divide into 50 μL aliquots in cold 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes, kept on ice.

If proceeding directly from step 13, immediately add ∼3–4 μL of the pGEN-P sseA-luxCDABE plasmid to one tube of electrocompetent cells and incubate on ice briefly (∼3 min). If using previously made, frozen electrocompetent cells, first thaw on ice for ∼5 min.
Carefully transfer the competent cell/plasmid mixture into a pre-chilled electroporation cuvette and perform the electroporation using a Gene Pulser Xcell™ Electroporation System at 2.5 kV.
After the pulse, immediately transfer the mixture out of the cuvette and into a sterile 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube containing ∼1 mL LB.
Incubate the LB mixture for ∼1 h at 37°C with shaking at 250 rpm.
Plate 100 μL of the transformed cells onto an LB agar plate supplemented with 200 μg/mL ampicillin. Incubate the plate at 37°C for ∼18 h.
Pick a single colony of the pGEN-P sseA-luxCDABE- harboring S. Typhimurium strain and inoculate 5 mL of LB with 100 μg/mL ampicillin. Incubate the culture at 37°C for ∼18 h with shaking at 250 rpm.

Prepare a glycerol stock of the pGEN-P sseA-luxCDABE- harboring S. Typhimurium strain with ∼1 mL of the overnight culture and ∼500 μL of 50% (v/v) sterile glycerol. Store at −80°C for long-term usage.

background: This experiment is at the detergent titration stage used to choose a CHAPS level suitable for later Drs2-Cdc50 reconstitution into liposomes. Three LUV-containing reactions are prepared with different initial detergent amounts in reconstitution buffer, incubated under gentle rotation, and compared by absorbance at 550 nm. After a second detergent increase, the samples are measured again so the operator can identify the detergent range appropriate for reconstitution.
goal: Set up and process the three CHAPS titration reactions through both incubation and absorbance-readout phases, then document the detergent concentration selected for subsequent reconstitution.
constraints: Prepare exactly three reactions from the same LUV stock, each starting with 83.3 uL of premade LUV solution in a 1.5 mL tube.
Use initial CHAPS additions of 0 uL, 1 uL, and 2 uL from a 0.5 M stock, paired with 41.7 uL, 40.7 uL, and 39.7 uL reconstitution buffer so each pre-read reaction totals 125 uL.

After initial setup, incubate the reactions for 25 min at room temperature with end-over-end style mixing represented as 30 rpm agitation, then transfer 100 uL from each reaction to a 96-well plate and measure absorbance at 550 nm.
Return the measured 100 uL portions to their original tubes before adding a uniform second CHAPS increment of 3 uL of 0.5 M stock to each reaction.

After the second 25 min incubation under the same 30 rpm room-temperature condition, measure 100 uL from each reaction again at 550 nm and record that 13 mM CHAPS is the chosen reconstitution concentration for this lipid composition.

available_inputs.name: luv_solution
available_inputs.description: Premade large unilamellar vesicle solution used for detergent titration.

available_inputs.name: chaps_0_5M
available_inputs.description: 0.5 M CHAPS stock solution.

available_inputs.name: reconstitution_buffer
available_inputs.description: Reconstitution buffer used to adjust assay volume.

available_inputs.name: tube_set
available_inputs.description: Three labeled 1.5 mL reaction tubes for the titration conditions.

available_inputs.name: plate96
available_inputs.description: 96-well plate used for absorbance measurements.

gold_program:
tubes_with_luv = aliquot_sample(sample=luv_solution, destination=tube_set, volume_ul=83.3, aliquot_count=3)
setup_buffer = add_reagent(sample=tubes_with_luv, reagent=reconstitution_buffer, volume_ul=41.7, mixing="tube 1 buffer setup; adjust companion tubes with 40.7 uL and 39.7 uL buffer")
setup_chaps = add_reagent(sample=setup_buffer, reagent=chaps_0_5M, volume_ul=1.0, mixing="tube 2 receives 1 uL CHAPS and tube 3 receives 2 uL CHAPS; tube 1 receives no CHAPS")
first_incubation = set_thermal_mixer(sample=setup_chaps, temperature_c=25.0, speed_rpm=30, duration_min=25.0)
first_plate = aliquot_sample(sample=first_incubation, destination=plate96, volume_ul=100.0, aliquot_count=3)
first_read = measure_signal(sample=first_plate, assay="absorbance", wavelength_nm=550, replicate_n=3)
returned_samples = transfer_liquid(source=plate96, destination=tube_set, volume_ul=100.0, tool="pipette")
second_chaps = add_reagent(sample=returned_samples, reagent=chaps_0_5M, volume_ul=3.0, mixing="pipette mix each tube after addition")
second_incubation = set_thermal_mixer(sample=second_chaps, temperature_c=25.0, speed_rpm=30, duration_min=25.0)
second_plate = aliquot_sample(sample=second_incubation, destination=plate96, volume_ul=100.0, aliquot_count=3)
second_read = measure_signal(sample=second_plate, assay="absorbance", wavelength_nm=550, replicate_n=3)
selected_condition = record_measurement(sample=second_read, metric="selected_CHAPS_concentration", unit="mM", note="Choose a condition between R half and R sol; for this lipid composition, use 13 mM CHAPS for reconstitution.")

source_steps.description: To each of three 1.5 mL reaction tubes, add 83.3 µL of the premade LUV solution.

Add 0, 1, and 2 µL of a 0.5 M CHAPS solution (see Recipe 2), and 41.7, 40.7 and 39.7 µL of reconstitution buffer (see Recipe 1), respectively, and incubate with end-over-end rotation at 30 rpm and room temperature for 25 min.
Pipette 100 µL from each reaction tube into a well of a 96-well plate. Measure the absorbance at 550 nm in the plate reader.
Pipette the 100 µL from each well back into their original tubes and add 3 µL of a 0.5 M CHAPS solution (see Recipe 2) to each tube. Incubate with end-over-end rotation at 30 rpm for 25 min.

Pipette 100 µL from each reaction tube into a well of a 96-well plate. Measure the absorbance at 550 nm in the microplate reader. The CHAPS concentration used for the reconstitution should correspond to a concentration between the R half and R sol , indicated by the blue area in Figure 2 . For this lipid composition and concentration, 13 mM CHAPS is required.
In a 1.5 mL reaction tube and to a final volume of 250 µL, add:
Add 10 µL of a 1 µg/µL Drs2-Cdc50 stock in elution buffer containing 0.05% DDM (final concentration 0.04 µg/µL) and incubate at room temperature with end-over-end rotation at 30 rpm for 5 min.
It is advised to additionally prepare a mock sample with 10 µL of protein elution buffer instead of protein stock solution.
Detailed information regarding the protein-to-lipid ratio can be found under General note 2.
Put a clamp on one side of a piece of pre-rinsed dialysis tubing, carefully pipette the sample into the tubing, and seal it with a second clamp.
Add the tubing to a 1 L glass cylinder filled with 900 mL of reconstitution buffer (see Recipe 1) and incubate while stirring at 4 °C for 3–4 h (see Figure 3 ).
Discard the buffer and fill up the glass cylinder with 900 mL of fresh reconstitution buffer (see Recipe 1). Incubate while stirring at 4 °C overnight.

background: This protocol stage processes barcoded, restriction-digested DNA through adaptor ligation and pooled column purification so the combined reduced-representation library is ready for downstream methylation-focused enrichment. The task starts from ligation-ready DNA in a PCR plate and follows the material through room-temperature mixing, ligation incubation, pooling for silica-column cleanup, wash steps, and EB elution.
goal: Produce the action sequence that finishes ligation and performs pooled silica-column purification through recovery of the eluted DNA library.
constraints: Begin from a PCR plate containing digested DNA with adapters and ligation mix, using room-temperature handling near 21-22 C for brief mixing and spin-down preparation.
The ligation stage must include 120 min at 22 C followed by 30 min at 65 C before cleanup.
After ligation, represent the material as a single pooled sample prepared for silica binding with PB-type buffer at a 5:1 buffer-to-sample ratio before loading to the column.
Model column purification with filtration and centrifugation at 10000 x g for 1 min at about 22 C, including a PE wash and an additional spin to clear residual wash buffer.
Elute with 30 uL EB, allow 1 min at room temperature, then recover the eluate by a final 10000 x g spin for 1 min.
Later actions must consume outputs from earlier actions, and only provided action-pool functions with keyword arguments may be used.

available_inputs.name: ligation_reaction_plate
available_inputs.description: PCR plate containing digested DNA samples with adapters and ligation master mix.

available_inputs.name: pooled_ligation_tube
available_inputs.description: Combined ligation reactions collected into a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

available_inputs.name: column_loaded_pool
available_inputs.description: DNA pool after addition of PB binding buffer and ready for silica-column processing.

available_inputs.name: wash_ready_column
available_inputs.description: Silica column format used for PE wash and EB elution.

gold_program:
mixed_ligation_plate = vortex_mix(sample=ligation_reaction_plate, duration_s=10, speed_rpm=800, temperature_c=22)
spun_ligation_plate = centrifuge_sample(sample=mixed_ligation_plate, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=22)
ligated_plate = incubate_sample(sample=spun_ligation_plate, temperature_c=22, duration_min=120, condition="static")
heat_finished_plate = heat_sample(sample=ligated_plate, temperature_c=65, duration_min=30, device="heating block")
mixed_pool = vortex_mix(sample=pooled_ligation_tube, duration_s=10, speed_rpm=1200, temperature_c=22)
filtered_pool = filter_sample(sample=column_loaded_pool, filter_type="silica column", pore_size_um=0.0, destination="collection tube")
loaded_column = centrifuge_sample(sample=filtered_pool, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=22)
washed_column = filter_sample(sample=loaded_column, filter_type="PE wash on silica column", pore_size_um=0.0, destination="collection tube")
dry_column = centrifuge_sample(sample=washed_column, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=22)
elution_wait = incubate_sample(sample=dry_column, temperature_c=22, duration_min=1, condition="static")
eluted_library = centrifuge_sample(sample=elution_wait, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=22)

source_steps.description: After mixing each DNA sample with the digestion reagents, immediately place the vials in a heating block. The recommended incubation time is 3 h for non-human DNA and 16 h for human DNA at 37°C, followed by 20 min at 85°C.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: In a PCR plate, mix 3 μL of adapters with 8 μL of each PstI digested DNA samples (∼100 ng of DNA). Vortex the plate at the lowest speed (while the master mix is prepared for the next step) and spin down at around 21°C–22°C.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: Prepare a Ligation Master mix according to the following table, considering a larger number of samples to account for pipetting errors. Vortex the plate and spin down for 30 s at around 21°C–22°C.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: Incubate the ligation reaction immediately in a heat block for 2 h at 22°C followed by 30 min at 65°C.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: Spin down and collect the whole content (30 μL) of each reaction in the PCR plate to make one pool in a 1.5- or 2-mL tube. The purification is then immediately performed using the Qiagen QIAquick PCR purification kit, as described in Box 1 . PCR purification using Qiagen QIAquick kit Calculate the final pool volume.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: Transfer the pool to one 15 mL falcon tube, then add 5 volumes of PB buffer and mix well using vortex.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: Place the DNA pool in the provided silica filter column and spin. The pool should be divided considering a maximum volume of 750 μL per column and maximum filtering capacity of 1.5 mL per column.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification

background: A pigeon brain has been post-fixed and kept cold in PBS in a 50 mL tube. The current task is the solvent-based preprocessing segment immediately before bleaching: warm the sample from cold buffer, drive dehydration through selected methanol/PBS compositions, move into a DCM-containing solvent condition, return to methanol with repeated rinses, then chill the sample and introduce the bleaching solution.
goal: Process the stored whole-brain sample through dehydration, DCM transition, methanol rinsing, and pre-bleach cooling so that it ends in chilled 5% hydrogen peroxide in methanol.
constraints: Keep the sample in a 50 mL tube context and model reagent exchanges with 50000 uL additions, except use 55000 uL for the 66% DCM/methanol step to reflect filling the tube completely.
Begin from cold 1x PBS and include a warming/agitation step represented as 25 C, 20 rpm, for 60 min before the methanol series starts.
Use methanol/PBS conditions of 10%, 20%, and 80%; do not include a 90% methanol/PBS step.

Represent the 10% methanol/PBS and 66% DCM/methanol incubations as 960 min shaking incubations at room temperature, and represent the 20%, 80%, and final chilled bleaching handoff in the same dependency chain.
Before DCM, include two 100% methanol washes of 720 min each; after DCM, include three 100% methanol rinses of 60 min each.
After the last methanol rinse, cool to 4 C for 240 min and then add 5% H2O2 in methanol at 50000 uL.
Use only provided action-pool functions and avoid centrifugation, thermal cycling, and drying.

available_inputs.description: Whole pigeon brain stored in a 50 mL Falcon tube after post-fixation.

available_inputs.description: 1x PBS pre-cooled to 4 C.

available_inputs.name: meoh_10_pbs
available_inputs.description: 10% methanol in 1x PBS.

available_inputs.name: meoh_20_pbs
available_inputs.description: 20% methanol in 1x PBS.

available_inputs.name: meoh_80_pbs
available_inputs.description: 80% methanol in 1x PBS.

available_inputs.name: meoh_100
available_inputs.description: 100% methanol.

available_inputs.name: dcm_66_meoh
available_inputs.description: 66% dichloromethane in methanol.

available_inputs.name: h2o2_5_meoh_cold
available_inputs.description: 5% hydrogen peroxide in methanol, pre-cooled before use.

gold_program:
step1 = set_thermal_mixer(sample=brain_sample, temperature_c=25, speed_rpm=20, duration_min=60)
step2 = incubate_sample(sample=step1, temperature_c=25, duration_min=960, condition="shaking in 50000 uL 10% methanol in PBS")
step3 = incubate_sample(sample=step2, temperature_c=25, duration_min=60, condition="shaking in 50000 uL 20% methanol in PBS")
step4 = incubate_sample(sample=step3, temperature_c=25, duration_min=60, condition="shaking in 50000 uL 80% methanol in PBS")
step5 = incubate_sample(sample=step4, temperature_c=25, duration_min=720, condition="shaking in first 50000 uL wash of 100% methanol")
step6 = incubate_sample(sample=step5, temperature_c=25, duration_min=720, condition="shaking in second 50000 uL wash of 100% methanol")
step7 = add_reagent(sample=step6, reagent=dcm_66_meoh, volume_ul=55000, mixing="gentle inversion")
step8 = incubate_sample(sample=step7, temperature_c=25, duration_min=960, condition="shaking")
step9 = incubate_sample(sample=step8, temperature_c=25, duration_min=180, condition="three sequential 60 min rinses in 50000 uL 100% methanol")
step10 = cool_sample(sample=step9, temperature_c=4, duration_min=240, device="refrigerator")
step11 = add_reagent(sample=step10, reagent=h2o2_5_meoh_cold, volume_ul=50000, mixing="gentle inversion")

source_steps.stage: Sensory stimulation, perfusion, and brain isolation—Day 0
source_steps.description: Leave approximately 1 cm of the spinal cord attached to facilitate sample handling and transfer.

source_steps.stage: Sensory stimulation, perfusion, and brain isolation—Day 0
source_steps.description: Transfer each brain to a 50 mL Falcon tube and post-fix in 25 mL of 4% PFA at 4°C for 20 h without rotation.

source_steps.stage: Sensory stimulation, perfusion, and brain isolation—Day 0
source_steps.description: Remove PFA the next day and add 50 mL of 1x PBS cooled to 4°C. Store brains at 4°C until the experiment is complete.
Dehydrate brains in 10% increments of methanol/1× PBS. Transfer 50 mL of fresh 1× PBS cooled to 4°C and shake tubes gently (20 rpm) at room temperature until they are warmed to 20°C–25°C.
Remove 1× PBS and add 50 mL of 10% methanol/1× PBS to each tube. Shake overnight.
The next morning, remove 10% methanol/1× PBS and add 20% methanol/1× PBS.

Repeat in 10% increments until 80% methanol/1× PBS is reached. Note: The 90% methanol/1× PBS step is skipped because we have observed precipitate formation while preparing this solution. To avoid tissue damage, handle tubes containing dehydrated samples with care during solution exchange.
Wash two times in 100% methanol over a day at room temperature.
Remove 100% methanol and add 66% Dichloromethane (DCM)/methanol. Fill tubes to the top (>50 mL) to prevent oxidation and shake overnight.
The next day, remove 66% DCM/methanol and rinse with 100% methanol thrice, 1 h each at room temperature.

During the third and final methanol wash, put the brains onto a shaker at 4°C until the samples are cooled down (∼4 h). CRITICAL: Handle Dichloromethane (DCM) in a chemical fume hood and dispose according to local safety regulations. DCM can easily erode common plastic labware and requires careful handling. Fill tubes completely to prevent oxidation. Store secured in a chemical cabinet.
Bleaching. Remove methanol and add cooled 5% H 2 O 2 /methanol to each tube.

background: Sorted immune cells have already been prepared for intracellular phospho-Smad2/3 analysis by flow cytometry. At this stage, the sample must be fixed, methanol-permeabilized, washed in FACS buffer, stained with phospho-Smad2/3 antibody, washed again, and left as a cold flow-ready suspension.
goal: Prepare the existing cell sample through intracellular phospho-Smad2/3 staining and leave it ready for later flow-cytometry acquisition.
constraints: Start from a cell sample that still requires fixation, permeabilization, intracellular antibody staining, and final resuspension.
Use 100 uL 2% PFA in PBS followed by 10 min at 37 C, then pellet at 420 x g for 5 min before methanol treatment.
Permeabilize with 100 uL ice-cold methanol for 60 min at 4 C in the dark, then perform two FACS-buffer washes associated with 420 x g spins for 5 min each.
The intracellular staining mixture must total 100 uL and contain 2 uL phospho-Smad2/3 antibody, with 60 min incubation at room temperature.
After staining, complete two further FACS-buffer washes while preserving the distinct 100 uL and 200 uL wash volumes before the final 200 uL resuspension.
Finish by transferring the 200 uL final suspension into a flow-cytometer-compatible tube and storing it at 4 C until acquisition.

available_inputs.name: methanol_ice_cold
available_inputs.description: Ice-cold methanol for permeabilization.

step1 = add_reagent(sample=cells, reagent=pfa_pbs_2pct, volume_ul=100.0, mixing='resuspend')
step2 = incubate_sample(sample=step1, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=10.0, condition='static')
step3 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step2, speed_xg=420, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=25.0)
step4 = add_reagent(sample=step3, reagent=methanol_ice_cold, volume_ul=100.0, mixing='resuspend')
step5 = incubate_sample(sample=step4, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=60.0, condition='protected from light')
step6 = add_reagent(sample=step5, reagent='FACS buffer with phospho-Smad2/3 antibody', volume_ul=500.0, mixing='resuspend')
step7 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step6, speed_xg=420, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step8 = add_reagent(sample=step7, reagent='100 uL total intracellular stain containing 2 uL phospho-Smad2/3 antibody in FACS buffer', volume_ul=100.0, mixing='resuspend')
step9 = incubate_sample(sample=step8, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=60.0, condition='static')
step10 = add_reagent(sample=step9, reagent='FACS buffer for sequential 100 uL wash, 200 uL wash, and final 200 uL resuspension', volume_ul=500.0, mixing='resuspend')
step11 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step10, speed_xg=420, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step12 = store_sample(sample=step11, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=4.0, container=flow_tube)

source_steps.description: Fix cells by resuspending in 100 μL 2% paraformaldehyde (PFA) in PBS. Incubate at 37°C for 10 min. Centrifuge for 5 min at 420 × g . Discard supernatant.
Resuspend cells in 100 μL of ice-cold methanol. Incubate at 4°C for 60 min in the dark.
Wash cells by adding 200 μL FACS buffer. Centrifuge for 5 min at 420 × g . Discard supernatant.
Wash cells again by adding 200 μL FACS buffer. Centrifuge for 5 min at 420 × g . Discard supernatant.
Perform intracellular staining by resuspending cells in 100 μL FACS buffer containing 2 μL of phospho-Smad2/3 antibody. Incubate for 60 min at RT.
Wash cells by adding 100 μL FACS buffer. Centrifuge for 5 min at 420 × g . Discard supernatant.
Wash cells again by adding 200 μL FACS buffer. Centrifuge for 5 min at 420 × g . Discard supernatant.
Resuspend cells in 200 μL FACS Buffer. Transfer to tubes compatible with your flow cytometer’s sample injection port.

Store at 4°C until samples are acquired on a flow cytometer. See Figure 2 for representative flow cytometry data. Flow cytometric analysis of phospho-Smad2/3 in DCs and CD4 + T cells TGF−β induces Smad2/3 phosphorylation and deletion of Smad7 enhances TGF-β-induced phospho-Smad2/3. (A) Representative FACS histograms (left) and median fluorescence intensities (MFI)s (right) of phospho- Smad2/3 in bone marrow-derived DCs (BMDCs) stimulated with or without TGF-β (2 ng/mL), for 1 h (n = 6). Adapted from reference. 1 (B) Representative FACS histograms (left) and MFIs (right) of phospho- Smad2/3 in naive CD4 + T cells from Smad7 fl/fl and T cell specific-Smad7 −/− mice stimulated with or without TGF-β (2.5 ng/mL), for 1 h (n = 4). Data representative of ≥3 independent experiments. Means ± SEMs. ∗∗∗p < 0.001 by unpaired t test. Adapted from reference. 1

background: Human lung epithelial cell lysates containing UV-crosslinked vtRNA-protein complexes have already been hybridized with biotinylated antisense probes. The current segment covers cooling the hybridized material, capturing probe-bound complexes on streptavidin magnetic beads, saving diagnostic fractions, washing the beads repeatedly in hybridization buffer, and carrying out Benzonase-assisted elution in a thermomixer.
goal: Complete the local capture-and-elution stage for the hybridized vtRNA pulldown sample so that bead-bound complexes are isolated, key intermediate fractions are preserved, and the washed beads are subjected to nuclease elution under the required mixing conditions.
constraints: Allow the hybridized lysate to cool to room temperature for about 60 minutes before bead capture, and clear the prepared magnetic beads on a DynaMag-15-style rack before combining them with lysate.

Preserve an input-with-probe fraction from the cooled sample before bead capture, corresponding to 20 uL, then perform bead binding for 60 minutes at room temperature with 1100 rpm intermittent mixing.

After capture, retain an unbound supernatant fraction following magnetic separation, then perform six successive washes with 1x hybridization buffer using repeated magnetic handling rather than a single collapsed wash.
During the final wash stage preserve a pulldown aliquot representing 30 uL for western blot and 5 uL for RNA isolation, then remove remaining supernatant from the bead phase before elution.
For elution, resuspend the washed beads in 100 uL Benzonase elution buffer with 0.5 uL Benzonase (125 U total) and incubate at 37 C for 120 minutes with 1100 rpm mixing in a thermomixer.

available_inputs.name: hybridized_lysate
available_inputs.description: Probe-hybridized cell lysate containing UV-crosslinked vtRNA-protein complexes.

available_inputs.name: prepared_beads
available_inputs.description: Streptavidin-coated magnetic beads already prepared for one pulldown.

available_inputs.name: hybridization_buffer
available_inputs.description: 1x hybridization buffer used for bead washing.

available_inputs.name: benzonase_elution_buffer
available_inputs.description: Elution buffer used to resuspend washed beads before Benzonase treatment.

gold_program:
cooled_lysate = cool_sample(sample=hybridized_lysate, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=60.0, device='thermocycler_without_heat_block')
cleared_beads = magnetic_separate(sample=prepared_beads, rack='DynaMag-15', duration_min=1.0, collect_phase='beads')
input_probe_fraction = store_sample(sample=cooled_lysate, temperature_c=25.0, duration_h=1.0, container='20uL_input_with_probe')
bead_capture_mix = mix_sample(sample=cooled_lysate, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
bound_on_beads = set_thermal_mixer(sample=bead_capture_mix, temperature_c=25.0, speed_rpm=1100, duration_min=60.0)
unbound_fraction = magnetic_separate(sample=bound_on_beads, rack='DynaMag-15', duration_min=1.0, collect_phase='supernatant')
washed_beads = incubate_sample(sample=unbound_fraction, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=6.0, condition='six_washes_with_1x_hybridization_buffer')
pulldown_fraction = store_sample(sample=washed_beads, temperature_c=25.0, duration_h=1.0, container='30uL_WB_plus_5uL_RNA_pulldown')
beads_after_final_clear = magnetic_separate(sample=washed_beads, rack='DynaMag-15', duration_min=1.0, collect_phase='beads')
elution_resuspension = mix_sample(sample=beads_after_final_clear, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
benzonase_elution = set_thermal_mixer(sample=elution_resuspension, temperature_c=37.0, speed_rpm=1100, duration_min=120.0)
protocol_title: Protocol to uncover the protein interactome of small non-coding vault RNAs through RNA antisense purification coupled to mass spectrometry

source_steps.stage: Hybridization, capture of the hybridized RNA, and protein elution
source_steps.description: Add the probe to the lysate and incubate for 2 hours at 67 °C with intermittent mixing at 1100 rpm (30s on, 30s off).

source_steps.stage: Hybridization, capture of the hybridized RNA, and protein elution
source_steps.description: Remove the heat block from the thermocycler to allow the samples to slowly cool down to RT (approximately 1 hour). In the meantime, take the streptavidin-coated beads prepared during the “Preparation of beads to capture the probe” step and divide 200 μl of beads into a new 15ml DNA LoBind tube. Note: Prepare one tube for each pulldown.

source_steps.stage: Hybridization, capture of the hybridized RNA, and protein elution
source_steps.description: Remove the supernatant from the beads through separation with the DynaMag™-15 prior to the addition of the lysate to the beads.

source_steps.stage: Hybridization, capture of the hybridized RNA, and protein elution
source_steps.description: After the 1 hour cooldown period (and prior to addition to the beads), remove a 20 μl aliquot from each individual sample (resulting in 12 samples in total), serving as ‘input with probe sample’ for downstream qPCR analysis.

source_steps.stage: Hybridization, capture of the hybridized RNA, and protein elution
source_steps.description: Subsequently, add the lysates to the beads and mix through gentle pipetting.

source_steps.stage: Hybridization, capture of the hybridized RNA, and protein elution
source_steps.description: Incubate for 1 hour at RT with intermittent mixing at 1100 rpm in a thermomixer (30s on, 30s off).

source_steps.stage: Hybridization, capture of the hybridized RNA, and protein elution
source_steps.description: Separate the beads with the DynaMag™-15 and take an aliquot of “unbound sample” from the supernatant for each lysate, as described in step 4 (45 μl for Western blot and 20 μl for RNA isolation). Discard the leftover supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Hybridization, capture of the hybridized RNA, and protein elution
source_steps.description: Wash the beads 6 times with 0.5 mL 1x Hybridization buffer using the DynaMag™-15 magnet.

source_steps.stage: Hybridization, capture of the hybridized RNA, and protein elution
source_steps.description: During the last wash step, take an aliquot serving as “pulldown sample” from the bead suspension (30 μl for Western blot, 5 μl for RNA isolation), as described in step 4.

source_steps.stage: Hybridization, capture of the hybridized RNA, and protein elution
source_steps.description: Next, remove the supernatant from the remaining beads using the magnet and discard the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: Hybridization, capture of the hybridized RNA, and protein elution
source_steps.description: Resuspend the beads in 100 μl Benzonase Elution buffer and add 0.5 μl of Benzonase (125 U) to each tube.

source_steps.stage: Hybridization, capture of the hybridized RNA, and protein elution
source_steps.description: Incubate for 2 hours at 37 °C with intermittent mixing at 1100 rpm on a thermomixer (30s on, 30s off).

background: Patient-derived tumor organoids have already been recovered from Matrigel cultures and divided into a counting fraction and an infection fraction. The immediate task is to clean both fractions, process the counting tube with enzymatic dissociation, keep the infection tube cold in basal medium, and finish with matched final resuspensions suitable for downstream infection calculations.
goal: Complete local preparation of the two harvested organoid fractions so the counting sample and infection sample are both cleaned, differentially processed, and resuspended for subsequent assay setup.
constraints: Keep the counting and infection fractions separate throughout because they require different dissociation conditions after the initial cleanup spin.
Use an initial cold centrifugation for each tube at 100 x g for 5 min at 4 C, and later a second cold centrifugation at the same settings after dilution.
The counting fraction must receive 1000 uL TrypLE Select followed by a 37 C incubation for 5 min before its dilution to 13 mL total.
The infection fraction must be resuspended in 2000 uL basal medium, held cold on ice rather than warmed, and then diluted to 10 mL total.
Finish by resuspending the post-spin pellets from both fractions in 1000 uL basal medium, using only provided action-pool functions and a continuous dependency chain.

available_inputs.name: tube1_organoids
available_inputs.description: Harvested organoid suspension designated for cell counting.

available_inputs.name: tube2_organoids
available_inputs.description: Harvested organoid suspension designated for infection.

available_inputs.name: basal_medium
available_inputs.description: Basal medium used for resuspension and dilution.

available_inputs.name: tryple_select
available_inputs.description: TrypLE Select for enzymatic dissociation of the counting fraction.

available_inputs.name: ice_bath
available_inputs.description: Cold holding device for the infection fraction.

gold_program:
tube1_spin1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=tube1_organoids, speed_xg=100, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
tube2_spin1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=tube2_organoids, speed_xg=100, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
tube1_tryple = add_reagent(sample=tube1_spin1, reagent=tryple_select, volume_ul=1000, mixing="pipette")
tube1_dissociated = incubate_sample(sample=tube1_tryple, temperature_c=37, duration_min=5, condition="static")
tube2_resuspended = add_reagent(sample=tube2_spin1, reagent=basal_medium, volume_ul=2000, mixing="pipette")
tube2_cold = cool_sample(sample=tube2_resuspended, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5, device=ice_bath)
tube1_diluted = add_reagent(sample=tube1_dissociated, reagent=basal_medium, volume_ul=12000, mixing="invert")
tube2_diluted = add_reagent(sample=tube2_cold, reagent=basal_medium, volume_ul=8000, mixing="invert")
tube1_spin2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=tube1_diluted, speed_xg=100, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
tube2_spin2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=tube2_diluted, speed_xg=100, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
tube1_final = add_reagent(sample=tube1_spin2, reagent=basal_medium, volume_ul=1000, mixing="pipette")
tube2_final = add_reagent(sample=tube2_spin2, reagent=basal_medium, volume_ul=1000, mixing="pipette")
protocol_title: Preclinical testing of oncolytic adenovirus sensitivity in patient-derived tumor organoids

source_steps.description: Harvest organoids. Tube 1: 2 wells for cell count Tubes 2-X: 2–3 wells per tube for organoid infection Remove organoid growth medium from the wells without touching the Matrigel dome.

Add 500 μL of basal medium from the corresponding tube to each well to disaggregate the Matrigel dome with the help of a P1000 pipette, and collect the content into the tube. Note: Either F12+++ or F12+++B basal media can be used for this step. BSA present in F12+++B helps prevent attachment of organoids to the walls of the tube, which can lead to loss of cells during the process.
Repeat step 5b 2–3 times until removing most of Matrigel remainders inside the wells.
Centrifuge at 100 g 5 min at 4°C.

Remove supernatant carefully, trying to remove as much Matrigel as possible but without touching the pellet. Note: If organoids are not cleanly pelleted under a Matrigel layer, remove medium leaving the Matrigel and add 10 mL of fresh basal medium to repeat centrifugation in step 5d at higher speed (400 g , 5 min, 4°C).

Disaggregate organoids. Note: Organoids that will be used for cell count (Tube 1) are dissociated to single cells using TrypLE, while those used for infection with OAd (Tube 2) are mechanically disaggregated. See Figure 4 in Driehuis et al. (2020) for examples of how organoids look like before and after disaggregation. Resuspend the pellet of organoids in Tube 1 (cell count) in 1 mL TrypLE Select by pipetting up and down 10–15 times with a P1000 pipette to facilitate disaggregation.
Incubate Tube 1 for 5 min at 37°C.

During the incubation step, disaggregate organoids in Tube 2 (infection) mechanically. For that, resuspend the pellet in 2 mL of basal medium and use a pre-narrowed, pre-wet (with F12+++B) sterile glass Pasteur pipette to pipette the volume up and down 8–10 times and keep the tube on ice. Try not to create bubbles when pipetting. Note: For preparation of pre-narrowed glass Pasteur pipettes, after lighting a Bunsen burner, hold the pipette horizontally with both hands and roll it 3–4 times while keeping the tip in the flame. Make sure that the tip is equally narrowed by all sides, and that it has been reshaped to a quarter of its original diameter. Narrowed tips should be sterilized by autoclaving before being used. For pictures on how pre-narrowed pipettes look like, refer to Driehuis et al. (2020) .
Check the suspension in Tube 1 under a microscope. If big organoids are still visible, pipette up and down a few times and incubate 5 min more at 37°C.
Add cold basal medium up to 13 mL to Tube 1, and up to 10 mL to Tube 2.

Centrifuge at 100 g 5 min at 4°C. Like before, repeat the centrifugation step at a higher speed if organoids are not nicely pelleted. Note: During this time, thaw ready-to-use virus aliquots (stored at −80°C) on ice, and label one 1.5-mL tube for each experimental condition. E.g., for infection of 1 PDO with 2 doses of 2 different OAds (+ non-infected control), prepare 5 tubes.

Remove the supernatant of both tubes carefully and resuspend each pellet in 1 mL of basal medium. Note: If more than 3 wells were harvested for infection (more than 1 tube for infection), this is the moment to pool all organoids together in a single tube, maintaining a proportion of 2 wells/mL. It is very important to maintain the same concentration of organoids in Tube 1 and Tube 2 to have a comparable cell number that can be used for calculations of the volume needed for infection and seeding.

background: Isolated zebrafish embryo nuclei have already been collected and are entering the FESEM preparation stage. The sample is resuspended in PBS with glycerol, deposited by a cold low-speed centrifugation step, and then stabilized on a silicon chip with aldehyde fixation before later osmium treatment and dehydration.
goal: Complete the local FESEM preparation segment so the nuclei sample is gently resuspended, cold-deposited onto the chip setup, fixed on the silicon chip, and transferred through the immediate post-fixation washes needed for the next processing stage.
constraints: Begin from a nuclei pellet and use a gentle resuspension in PBS containing 10% glycerol before centrifugation.
Use a cold deposition spin at 800 x g for 10 min at 4 C.
Represent the chip-handling phase with an initial PBS wash, then fixation in 2% PFA plus 2% GA for 30 min at room temperature under protected-from-light conditions.
After fixation, perform three washes in 0.1 M cacodylate buffer, each for 2 min.
Keep the sample in liquid throughout this segment and do not add drying or downstream osmium/dehydration operations.
Use 6-12 top-level calls, assign every call to a variable, and repeat at least two distinct action names.

available_inputs.name: nuclei_pellet
available_inputs.description: Pelleted isolated nuclei obtained from the preceding collection step.

available_inputs.name: pbs_glycerol
available_inputs.description: PBS containing 10% glycerol used to resuspend and dilute nuclei for chip deposition.

available_inputs.name: silicon_chip_sample
available_inputs.description: Silicon chip carrying deposited nuclei after the low-speed centrifugation setup.

available_inputs.name: pbs_wash
available_inputs.description: PBS used for the initial chip wash before aldehyde fixation.

available_inputs.name: fixative_pfa_ga
available_inputs.description: Aldehyde fixative containing 2% paraformaldehyde and 2% glutaraldehyde.

available_inputs.name: cacodylate_buffer
available_inputs.description: 0.1 M cacodylate buffer prepared for post-fixation washing.

gold_program:
resuspended_nuclei = mix_sample(sample=nuclei_pellet, method='pipetting', duration_s=15, speed='gentle')
diluted_nuclei = mix_sample(sample=resuspended_nuclei, method='pipetting', duration_s=15, speed='gentle')
chilled_nuclei = cool_sample(sample=diluted_nuclei, temperature_c=4, duration_min=5, device='cold block')
deposited_chip = centrifuge_sample(sample=chilled_nuclei, speed_xg=800, duration_min=10, temperature_c=4)
chip_in_pbs = incubate_sample(sample=silicon_chip_sample, temperature_c=25, duration_min=2, condition='static in PBS')
fixed_chip = incubate_sample(sample=chip_in_pbs, temperature_c=25, duration_min=30, condition='protected from light in 2% PFA + 2% GA')
prewash_chip = incubate_sample(sample=fixed_chip, temperature_c=25, duration_min=2, condition='static in 0.1 M cacodylate buffer')
wash1_chip = incubate_sample(sample=prewash_chip, temperature_c=25, duration_min=2, condition='static in 0.1 M cacodylate buffer')
wash2_chip = incubate_sample(sample=wash1_chip, temperature_c=25, duration_min=2, condition='static in 0.1 M cacodylate buffer')
protocol_title: Protocol for imaging nuclear pore complexes on isolated nuclei of zebrafish early embryos by field emission scanning electron microscopy
