source_steps.stage: Microscopy, nuclei concentration, and reverse cross-linking
source_steps.description: Turn on the microscope, select the laser for DAPI or PI, and set the objective first to 20× and then to 40× for an overview of the nuclei. Once the nuclei are spotted, use the 60× objective to assess nuclear integrity in more detail.

source_steps.stage: Microscopy, nuclei concentration, and reverse cross-linking
source_steps.description: Add 200 μL of Lysis Buffer to the remaining re-suspended nuclei from step 21. The final volume will be ∼300 μL. Incubate on a spinning wheel 10 min at 4°C. Keep as nuclear fraction.

source_steps.stage: Microscopy, nuclei concentration, and reverse cross-linking
source_steps.description: In parallel, during centrifugation time (step 20), process total fraction from step 6. Grind the sample in liquid Nitrogen with a mortar and pestle while adding 1 mL of Lysis Buffer and filter through a 35 μm mesh. Afterwards, incubate it on a spinning wheel for 10 min at 4°C. Take ∼300 μL of the total fraction for sonication.

source_steps.stage: Microscopy, nuclei concentration, and reverse cross-linking
source_steps.description: Sonicate total and nuclear fractions using a Bioruptor. Use the following settings: Power: H position (High), Sonication cycle: 30 s ON, 30 s OFF, Total sonication time: five cycles on a refrigerated bath. The main purpose of the sonication step is not only to shear DNA but also ensure all nuclei are lysed after the Lysis Buffer incubation.

source_steps.stage: Microscopy, nuclei concentration, and reverse cross-linking
source_steps.description: Add 12 μL of 5 M NaCl, to a final concentration of ∼200 mM, and 40 μg of proteinase K. First, incubate for 15 min at 50°C to allow digestion of cross-linked polypeptides by proteinase K, and then for 30 min at 65°C to reverse the formaldehyde cross-linking.

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Add 300 μL of RotiAqua P/C/I (Phenol acid/Chloroform/Isoamilic) for RNA extraction.

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Vortex strongly for 15 s, mix well.

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 15 min at 4°C

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Transfer ∼250 μL of aqueous phase (upper phase) into a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube. Add 1 μL of glycogen , 30 μL of NaAc 3 M pH 5.2 and 750 μL Ethanol absolute. Mix by inverting and incubate at −80°C for at least 30 min or at −20°C for at least 8 h (recommended).

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 30 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant. The RNA should be seen as a white pellet.

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Wash the pellet with ∼500 μL of 80% EtOH. Centrifuge at maximum speed for 5 min at 4°C.

background: Patient-derived tumor organoids are being prepared for downstream oncolytic adenovirus infection testing. At this stage, harvested organoids have been separated into one tube intended for cell counting and another intended for infection, and the workflow focuses on medium exchange, pelleting, dissociation, incubation, cooling, and preparation of virus aliquots under cold conditions.
goal: Process the organoid harvest so that the cell-count fraction is enzymatically dissociated and washed, the infection fraction is mechanically resuspended and chilled, and frozen virus aliquots are thawed on ice for later condition setup.
constraints: Use one tube for the cell-count branch and one tube for the infection branch; both branches require an initial pelleting spin at 100 x g for 5 min at 4 C.

For the cell-count branch, resuspend the pellet in 1000 uL TrypLE Select and incubate at 37 C for 5 min under static conditions; if dissociation is incomplete, the same sample may need another 5 min at 37 C.
For the infection branch, resuspend the pellet in 2000 uL basal medium and keep it chilled on ice during handling.
Before the second pelleting step, adjust the cell-count branch to 13 mL total volume and the infection branch to 10 mL total volume using cold basal medium.
Both branches require a second spin at 100 x g for 5 min at 4 C, with an allowable fallback cleanup spin at 400 x g for 5 min at 4 C if pelleting is poor.
Virus aliquots originate from -80 C storage and should be thawed on ice rather than warmed.

available_inputs.name: tube1_pellet
available_inputs.description: Harvested organoid pellet reserved for cell counting

available_inputs.name: tube2_pellet
available_inputs.description: Harvested organoid pellet reserved for infection setup

available_inputs.name: basal_medium_cold
available_inputs.description: Cold basal medium used for washes and volume adjustment

available_inputs.name: tryple_select
available_inputs.description: TrypLE Select reagent for enzymatic dissociation

available_inputs.name: virus_aliquots_frozen
available_inputs.description: Ready-to-use virus aliquots stored at minus 80 degrees Celsius

gold_program:
tube1_spin1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=tube1_pellet, speed_xg=100, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
tube2_spin1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=tube2_pellet, speed_xg=100, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
tube1_tryple = add_reagent(sample=tube1_spin1, reagent=tryple_select, volume_ul=1000, mixing="pipette mix")
tube1_dissociated = incubate_sample(sample=tube1_tryple, temperature_c=37, duration_min=5, condition="static")
tube2_resuspended = add_reagent(sample=tube2_spin1, reagent=basal_medium_cold, volume_ul=2000, mixing="pipette mix")
tube2_chilled = cool_sample(sample=tube2_resuspended, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5, device="ice")
tube1_brought_to_volume = add_reagent(sample=tube1_dissociated, reagent=basal_medium_cold, volume_ul=12000, mixing="gentle inversion")
tube2_brought_to_volume = add_reagent(sample=tube2_chilled, reagent=basal_medium_cold, volume_ul=8000, mixing="gentle inversion")
tube1_spin2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=tube1_brought_to_volume, speed_xg=100, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
tube2_spin2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=tube2_brought_to_volume, speed_xg=100, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
virus_aliquots_thawed = cool_sample(sample=virus_aliquots_frozen, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5, device="ice")

source_steps.description: Take an aliquot of basal medium to prepare OAd dilutions. This can be kept at room temperature (20°C–25°C) inside the cell culture hood during organoid processing until use.
Harvest organoids. Tube 1: 2 wells for cell count Tubes 2-X: 2–3 wells per tube for organoid infection Remove organoid growth medium from the wells without touching the Matrigel dome.

Add 500 μL of basal medium from the corresponding tube to each well to disaggregate the Matrigel dome with the help of a P1000 pipette, and collect the content into the tube. Note: Either F12+++ or F12+++B basal media can be used for this step. BSA present in F12+++B helps prevent attachment of organoids to the walls of the tube, which can lead to loss of cells during the process.
Repeat step 5b 2–3 times until removing most of Matrigel remainders inside the wells.
Centrifuge at 100 g 5 min at 4°C.

Remove supernatant carefully, trying to remove as much Matrigel as possible but without touching the pellet. Note: If organoids are not cleanly pelleted under a Matrigel layer, remove medium leaving the Matrigel and add 10 mL of fresh basal medium to repeat centrifugation in step 5d at higher speed (400 g , 5 min, 4°C).

Disaggregate organoids. Note: Organoids that will be used for cell count (Tube 1) are dissociated to single cells using TrypLE, while those used for infection with OAd (Tube 2) are mechanically disaggregated. See Figure 4 in Driehuis et al. (2020) for examples of how organoids look like before and after disaggregation. Resuspend the pellet of organoids in Tube 1 (cell count) in 1 mL TrypLE Select by pipetting up and down 10–15 times with a P1000 pipette to facilitate disaggregation.
Incubate Tube 1 for 5 min at 37°C.

During the incubation step, disaggregate organoids in Tube 2 (infection) mechanically. For that, resuspend the pellet in 2 mL of basal medium and use a pre-narrowed, pre-wet (with F12+++B) sterile glass Pasteur pipette to pipette the volume up and down 8–10 times and keep the tube on ice. Try not to create bubbles when pipetting. Note: For preparation of pre-narrowed glass Pasteur pipettes, after lighting a Bunsen burner, hold the pipette horizontally with both hands and roll it 3–4 times while keeping the tip in the flame. Make sure that the tip is equally narrowed by all sides, and that it has been reshaped to a quarter of its original diameter. Narrowed tips should be sterilized by autoclaving before being used. For pictures on how pre-narrowed pipettes look like, refer to Driehuis et al. (2020) .
Check the suspension in Tube 1 under a microscope. If big organoids are still visible, pipette up and down a few times and incubate 5 min more at 37°C.
Add cold basal medium up to 13 mL to Tube 1, and up to 10 mL to Tube 2.

Centrifuge at 100 g 5 min at 4°C. Like before, repeat the centrifugation step at a higher speed if organoids are not nicely pelleted. Note: During this time, thaw ready-to-use virus aliquots (stored at −80°C) on ice, and label one 1.5-mL tube for each experimental condition. E.g., for infection of 1 PDO with 2 doses of 2 different OAds (+ non-infected control), prepare 5 tubes.

background: High-molecular-weight genomic DNA from potato cyst nematodes has already undergone organic extraction, and the recovered aqueous material is now in a downstream cleanup stage. The next work focuses on a final chloroform/isoamyl alcohol extraction of the pooled aqueous phase, followed by ethanol precipitation, cold centrifugation, two ethanol washes, pellet drying, and cold resuspension to recover purified DNA suitable for later long-read sequencing library preparation.
goal: Process the extracted aqueous DNA through the remaining cleanup and precipitation stage to obtain a dried DNA pellet resuspended in elution buffer and held cold for extended recovery.
constraints: Begin from the pooled aqueous phase in tube_2 after addition of chloroform/isoamyl alcohol at approximately equal volume to the sample, typically about 360 uL total organic solvent, and include a vigorous extraction mix of about 1 min before phase separation.

Use a high-speed centrifugation step for 5 min to separate the final chloroform/isoamyl alcohol extraction, then represent the recovered aqueous phase as entering precipitation with ammonium acetate adjusted to 0.75 M, glycogen carrier, and 2.5x volume of 100% ethanol.
Model the precipitation with a 20 min centrifugation at 4 C, followed by two distinct 80% ethanol wash cycles in which each wash is paired with a 15 min centrifugation at 4 C.
After the second wash, include removal of residual ethanol by air drying for about 30 min before resuspension.
Finish with the DNA pellet resuspended in elution buffer and stored at 4 C overnight, normalized as 12 h.
Use only the provided action-pool functions, keep the abstraction at tube-processing level, and make later steps consume outputs from earlier steps rather than restarting from raw inputs.

available_inputs.name: tube_2
available_inputs.description: Microcentrifuge tube containing the pooled aqueous DNA phase after prior extraction steps.

available_inputs.name: chloroform_isoamyl_alcohol
available_inputs.description: Organic solvent mixture used for the final back-extraction, added to tube_2 at roughly equal volume.

available_inputs.name: nh4oac
available_inputs.description: Ammonium acetate stock used to bring the DNA solution to a final concentration of 0.75 M during precipitation.

available_inputs.name: glycogen
available_inputs.description: Carrier glycogen added during genomic DNA precipitation.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_100
available_inputs.description: Absolute ethanol used at 2.5x sample volume for DNA precipitation.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_80
available_inputs.description: Fresh 80% ethanol used to wash the DNA pellet.

available_inputs.name: elution_buffer
available_inputs.description: Buffer used to resuspend the dried DNA pellet.

gold_program:
mixed_extraction = vortex_mix(sample=tube_2, duration_s=60.0, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=22.0)
separated_extraction = centrifuge_sample(sample=mixed_extraction, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=22.0)
precipitation_mix = mix_sample(sample=separated_extraction, method="inversion", duration_s=20.0, speed="moderate")
precipitated_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=precipitation_mix, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=20.0, temperature_c=4.0)
first_wash = shake_container(sample=precipitated_pellet, amplitude_mm=5.0, repeat_n=6, duration_s=10.0)
first_wash_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=first_wash, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=15.0, temperature_c=4.0)
second_wash = shake_container(sample=first_wash_pellet, amplitude_mm=5.0, repeat_n=6, duration_s=10.0)
second_wash_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=second_wash, speed_xg=16000, duration_min=15.0, temperature_c=4.0)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=second_wash_pellet, method="air dry", temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=30.0)
resuspended_dna = mix_sample(sample=dried_pellet, method="pipetting", duration_s=15.0, speed="gentle")
stored_resuspension = store_sample(sample=resuspended_dna, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=12.0, container="microcentrifuge_tube_1_5ml")
protocol_title: Protocol for Cas9-targeted long-read sequencing in Globodera pallida and Globodera rostochiensis

source_steps.stage: Total high molecular weight (HMW) DNA extraction from J2s
source_steps.description: Remove ∼180 μL of the top aqueous solution from tube 1 while avoiding any of the phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol phase. Add it to the previously collected aqueous solution in tube 2.

source_steps.stage: Total high molecular weight (HMW) DNA extraction from J2s
source_steps.description: Third extraction/ back extraction: Add equal volumes of the chloroform/isoamyl alcohol solution (usually ∼360 μL) to tube 2.

source_steps.stage: Total high molecular weight (HMW) DNA extraction from J2s
source_steps.description: Either vortex tube 2 for 1 min or rotate on a HulaMixer sample mixer for 15 min.

source_steps.stage: Total high molecular weight (HMW) DNA extraction from J2s
source_steps.description: Spin tube 2 at high speed for 5 min.

source_steps.stage: Total high molecular weight (HMW) DNA extraction from J2s
source_steps.description: Remove as much of the top aqueous solution as possible while avoiding the chloroform/isoamyl alcohol solution and place it into a new tube, tube 3.

source_steps.stage: Total high molecular weight (HMW) DNA extraction from J2s
source_steps.description: Genomic DNA precipitation: Add NH 4 OAc to tube 3 to a final concentration of 0.75 M. Add 1 μL of glycogen (Thermo Fisher Scientific, 20 mg/ml). Mix the solution well. Add 2.5X volume of 100% ethanol and mix well.

source_steps.stage: Total high molecular weight (HMW) DNA extraction from J2s
source_steps.description: Spin for 20 min in a 4°C centrifuge at top speed. Decant the supernatant carefully without disturbing the pellet.

source_steps.stage: Total high molecular weight (HMW) DNA extraction from J2s
source_steps.description: Wash the pellet by adding 300 μL of 80% ethanol (prepared fresh) and flicking the tube.

source_steps.stage: Total high molecular weight (HMW) DNA extraction from J2s
source_steps.description: Spin for 15 min in a 4°C centrifuge at top speed.

source_steps.stage: Total high molecular weight (HMW) DNA extraction from J2s
source_steps.description: Decant the supernatant and repeat step 12.c-12.d to wash the pellet a second time.

source_steps.stage: Total high molecular weight (HMW) DNA extraction from J2s
source_steps.description: Decant the supernatant and use a P20 pipette to carefully remove excess 80% ethanol. Allow the pellet to air dry for ∼30 min.

source_steps.stage: Total high molecular weight (HMW) DNA extraction from J2s
source_steps.description: Resuspend the pellet in 25–30 μL of elution buffer. Allow the pellet to resuspend overnight at 4°C.

background: This experiment processes reduced-representation DNA libraries for the GBS-MeDIP workflow after restriction digestion and adapter ligation. The current stage is the immediate post-ligation cleanup, where the ligated reactions are pooled and purified on silica columns under room-temperature conditions to recover a clean DNA pool for downstream analysis.
goal: Complete the post-ligation pool cleanup so that the ligated DNA is incubated appropriately, purified on a silica column, washed thoroughly, and eluted into a final clean pool.
constraints: The ligation material must undergo 2 h at 22°C followed by 30 min at 65°C before purification.
Before column loading, the pooled ligation reaction must be combined with 5 volumes of PB buffer and mixed well; room-temperature handling is about 21–22°C.
Silica-column purification must include a bind/load stage followed by centrifugation at 10000 x g for 1 min at about 22°C.
The wash stage uses 750 μL PE buffer and requires two centrifugation events to ensure the wash buffer is fully removed.
Elution uses 30 μL EB buffer added to the column center, and later steps must consume the outputs of earlier incubation and purification states rather than restarting from raw inputs.

available_inputs.name: ligation_reaction_pool
available_inputs.description: Combined ligation reaction material ready for incubation and purification.

available_inputs.name: pb_buffer
available_inputs.description: Binding buffer used at five volumes relative to the pooled ligation reaction.

available_inputs.name: silica_column
available_inputs.description: Silica filter column used for purification of the pooled DNA library.

available_inputs.name: pe_buffer
available_inputs.description: Wash buffer for the silica-column cleanup.

available_inputs.name: eb_buffer
available_inputs.description: Elution buffer used to recover purified DNA from the column.

gold_program:
ligated_22 = incubate_sample(sample=ligation_reaction_pool, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=120.0, condition="static")
ligated_65 = heat_sample(sample=ligated_22, temperature_c=65.0, duration_min=30.0, device="heat block")
pool_with_pb = vortex_mix(sample=ligated_65, duration_s=10.0, speed_rpm=1200, temperature_c=22.0)
column_load_ready = mix_sample(sample=pool_with_pb, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle with 5 volumes PB buffer")
column_bound = filter_sample(sample=column_load_ready, filter_type="silica column", pore_size_um=0.0, destination=silica_column)
post_bind_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=column_bound, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
pe_wash_added = mix_sample(sample=post_bind_spin, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle with 750 uL PE buffer")
pe_wash_spin1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=pe_wash_added, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
pe_wash_spin2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=pe_wash_spin1, speed_xg=10000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
elution_ready = mix_sample(sample=pe_wash_spin2, method="pipetting", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle with 30 uL EB buffer")
purified_pool = filter_sample(sample=elution_ready, filter_type="silica column", pore_size_um=0.0, destination="purified_dna_pool")
protocol_title: GBS-MeDIP: A protocol for parallel identification of genetic and epigenetic variation in the same reduced fraction of genomes across individuals

source_steps.description: Perform DNA denaturation by incubating the samples for 10 min at 95°C. Transfer the DNA samples to ice and maintain for 5 min.
Prepare a digestion mix according to the indications below for each sample.

After mixing each DNA sample with the digestion reagents, immediately place the vials in a heating block. The recommended incubation time is 3 h for non-human DNA and 16 h for human DNA at 37°C, followed by 20 min at 85°C.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: In a PCR plate, mix 3 μL of adapters with 8 μL of each PstI digested DNA samples (∼100 ng of DNA). Vortex the plate at the lowest speed (while the master mix is prepared for the next step) and spin down at around 21°C–22°C.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: Prepare a Ligation Master mix according to the following table, considering a larger number of samples to account for pipetting errors. Vortex the plate and spin down for 30 s at around 21°C–22°C.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: Incubate the ligation reaction immediately in a heat block for 2 h at 22°C followed by 30 min at 65°C.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: Spin down and collect the whole content (30 μL) of each reaction in the PCR plate to make one pool in a 1.5- or 2-mL tube. The purification is then immediately performed using the Qiagen QIAquick PCR purification kit, as described in Box 1 . PCR purification using Qiagen QIAquick kit Calculate the final pool volume.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: Transfer the pool to one 15 mL falcon tube, then add 5 volumes of PB buffer and mix well using vortex.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: Place the DNA pool in the provided silica filter column and spin. The pool should be divided considering a maximum volume of 750 μL per column and maximum filtering capacity of 1.5 mL per column.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: Centrifuge for 1 min at 10,000× g at around 21°C–22°C.

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: Add 750 μL of PE buffer in the column (centrifuge, discard the flow through, centrifuge again and discard the flow through again to ensure that the PE buffer is completely out of the column).

source_steps.stage: Adaptor ligation, sample pooling, and purification
source_steps.description: Add 30 μL of EB buffer to the center of each column

background: Pigeon brains have already been isolated and placed into 50 mL tubes for the early whole-brain iDISCO+-style preprocessing workflow before immunolabeling. The current stage spans post-fixation storage and the beginning of methanol-based dehydration, including the room-temperature equilibration from cold PBS, graded methanol exposure, and the DCM/methanol transition followed by methanol rinses and cooling.
goal: Process each brain sample from post-fixation storage through the early dehydration and delipidation segment so that it ends chilled in methanol after the DCM-containing step and subsequent rinses.
constraints: Use a 50 mL Falcon tube context for sample handling and preserve the notion that the sample remains in tube-based storage/incubation throughout.
Include an initial cold post-fix hold at 4 C for 20 h in 4% PFA, followed by storage in cold 1x PBS at 4 C before dehydration begins.
Before graded dehydration, account for warming from 4 C PBS to room temperature under gentle shaking approximated as 20 rpm.
Represent graded methanol treatment with 10% increments and explicitly include at least 10%, 20%, and 80% methanol/PBS conditions while omitting any 90% methanol step.
Use overnight timing for the 10% methanol step and for the 66% DCM/methanol step.
Capture two washes in 100% methanol over one day before the DCM-containing incubation.
After the 66% DCM/methanol incubation, include three methanol rinses of 1 h each at room temperature, with the final methanol stage followed by cooling to about 4 C for about 4 h.
Maintain a dependency chain where later incubations consume outputs from earlier ones rather than restarting from the raw sample.

available_inputs.name: brain_sample
available_inputs.description: Freshly isolated pigeon brain with approximately 1 cm spinal cord retained, already transferred to a 50 mL Falcon tube.

available_inputs.name: falcon_50ml_tube
available_inputs.description: Tube vessel used for post-fixation, storage, dehydration, and solvent exchanges.

available_inputs.name: pfa_4pct
available_inputs.description: 4% paraformaldehyde solution for post-fixation.

available_inputs.name: pbs_1x_cold
available_inputs.description: Cold 1x PBS used for storage and pre-dehydration equilibration.

available_inputs.name: methanol_series
available_inputs.description: Methanol solutions prepared in 1x PBS at graded concentrations and as 100% methanol.

available_inputs.name: dcm_methanol_66pct
available_inputs.description: 66% dichloromethane in methanol solution for the pre-staining solvent transition.

gold_program:
postfix_brain = store_sample(sample=brain_sample, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=20.0, container=falcon_50ml_tube)
pbs_storage = store_sample(sample=postfix_brain, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=24.0, container=falcon_50ml_tube)
warmed_in_pbs = set_thermal_mixer(sample=pbs_storage, temperature_c=22.0, speed_rpm=20, duration_min=120.0)
methanol_10 = incubate_sample(sample=warmed_in_pbs, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=960.0, condition="shaking in 10% methanol in PBS")
methanol_20_to_80 = incubate_sample(sample=methanol_10, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=480.0, condition="graded dehydration through 20%, 30%, 40%, 50%, 60%, 70%, and 80% methanol in PBS")
methanol_100_washes = incubate_sample(sample=methanol_20_to_80, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=720.0, condition="two washes in 100% methanol over one day")
dcm_transition = incubate_sample(sample=methanol_100_washes, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=960.0, condition="shaking in 66% DCM in methanol with tube filled to top")
methanol_rinse_1 = incubate_sample(sample=dcm_transition, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=60.0, condition="100% methanol rinse 1 of 3")
methanol_rinse_2 = incubate_sample(sample=methanol_rinse_1, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=60.0, condition="100% methanol rinse 2 of 3")
methanol_rinse_3 = incubate_sample(sample=methanol_rinse_2, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=60.0, condition="100% methanol rinse 3 of 3")
cooled_final = cool_sample(sample=methanol_rinse_3, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=240.0, device="refrigerated shaker")
protocol_title: Protocol for whole-brain immunolabeling, clearing, and light-sheet imaging of C-FOS in the pigeon

source_steps.stage: Sensory stimulation, perfusion, and brain isolation—Day 0
source_steps.description: Open the cisterna magna over the brain stem to avoid formation of air bubbles during final clearing steps.

source_steps.stage: Sensory stimulation, perfusion, and brain isolation—Day 0
source_steps.description: Leave approximately 1 cm of the spinal cord attached to facilitate sample handling and transfer.

source_steps.stage: Sensory stimulation, perfusion, and brain isolation—Day 0
source_steps.description: Transfer each brain to a 50 mL Falcon tube and post-fix in 25 mL of 4% PFA at 4°C for 20 h without rotation.

source_steps.stage: Sensory stimulation, perfusion, and brain isolation—Day 0
source_steps.description: Remove PFA the next day and add 50 mL of 1x PBS cooled to 4°C. Store brains at 4°C until the experiment is complete.
Dehydrate brains in 10% increments of methanol/1× PBS. Transfer 50 mL of fresh 1× PBS cooled to 4°C and shake tubes gently (20 rpm) at room temperature until they are warmed to 20°C–25°C.
Remove 1× PBS and add 50 mL of 10% methanol/1× PBS to each tube. Shake overnight.
The next morning, remove 10% methanol/1× PBS and add 20% methanol/1× PBS.

Repeat in 10% increments until 80% methanol/1× PBS is reached. Note: The 90% methanol/1× PBS step is skipped because we have observed precipitate formation while preparing this solution. To avoid tissue damage, handle tubes containing dehydrated samples with care during solution exchange.
Wash two times in 100% methanol over a day at room temperature.
Remove 100% methanol and add 66% Dichloromethane (DCM)/methanol. Fill tubes to the top (>50 mL) to prevent oxidation and shake overnight.
The next day, remove 66% DCM/methanol and rinse with 100% methanol thrice, 1 h each at room temperature.

During the third and final methanol wash, put the brains onto a shaker at 4°C until the samples are cooled down (∼4 h). CRITICAL: Handle Dichloromethane (DCM) in a chemical fume hood and dispose according to local safety regulations. DCM can easily erode common plastic labware and requires careful handling. Fill tubes completely to prevent oxidation. Store secured in a chemical cabinet.

background: This experiment profiles m6A in single cells by preparing a viable, low-clump suspension for FACS and then carrying the sorted 384-well plate into the first SMART-seq2 preheating step. The relevant stage includes viability staining, DNase treatment, a wash and filtration of cells, followed by immediate plate spin, chilling, and brief heating before downstream cDNA synthesis.
goal: Process the induced cells into a filtered, sorter-ready suspension and then handle the sorted plate through the immediate post-sort spin, chilling, and initial heating steps required for the start of single-cell cDNA synthesis.
constraints: Maintain cell-pelleting spins at 1000 x g for 3 min at 23 C to protect viability, and use the same centrifugation family again for the post-DNase pellet.
Model both resuspension stages as gentle pipetting-style mixing rather than vortexing: first after pelleting in 200 uL flow buffer and later after washing in 1 mL flow buffer.
Include two dark incubations at 23 C with different durations: 5 min for viability staining and 15 min for DNase treatment, with gentle mixing associated with the DNase addition.
After the wash resuspension, pass the cells through a 40 um nylon filter into a sorter-compatible destination.
For the sorted plate, include a brief 1000 x g spin for 1 min, chilling on dry ice, then an ice-based cooling stage before a 2000 x g, 5 min, 4 C spin.
End with a 72 C heating step for 3 min using a thermocycler-compatible device context.

available_inputs.name: cell_pellet_tube
available_inputs.description: Polypropylene tube containing 2 million induced cells before preparation for sorting.

available_inputs.name: flow_buffer_cells
available_inputs.description: Flow buffer used for cell resuspension and wash steps.

available_inputs.name: viability_dye_mix
available_inputs.description: Near-IR LIVE/DEAD viability dye for staining cells before sorting.

available_inputs.name: dnaseI_mix
available_inputs.description: DNase I reagent used to reduce DNA-mediated clumping.

available_inputs.name: nylon_filter_40um
available_inputs.description: 40 micrometer nylon filter for producing a single-cell suspension.

available_inputs.name: sorted_384_plate
available_inputs.description: 384-well plate containing one sorted viable cell per well.

gold_program:
pelleted_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=cell_pellet_tube, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=3, temperature_c=23)
resuspended_cells = mix_sample(sample=pelleted_cells, method="gentle pipetting in 200 uL flow buffer with wide-bore tip", duration_s=20, speed="gentle")
stained_cells = incubate_sample(sample=resuspended_cells, temperature_c=23, duration_min=5, condition="protected from light")
dnase_mixed_cells = mix_sample(sample=stained_cells, method="gentle pipetting after DNase I addition with wide-bore tip", duration_s=15, speed="gentle")
dnase_treated_cells = incubate_sample(sample=dnase_mixed_cells, temperature_c=23, duration_min=15, condition="protected from light")
washed_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=dnase_treated_cells, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=3, temperature_c=23)
resuspended_wash = mix_sample(sample=washed_cells, method="gentle pipetting in 1 mL flow buffer with wide-bore tip", duration_s=20, speed="gentle")
filtered_cells = filter_sample(sample=resuspended_wash, filter_type="nylon", pore_size_um=40.0, destination="sorter tube")
spun_plate = centrifuge_sample(sample=sorted_384_plate, speed_xg=1000, duration_min=1, temperature_c=23)
iced_plate = cool_sample(sample=spun_plate, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5, device="ice")
prepped_plate = centrifuge_sample(sample=iced_plate, speed_xg=2000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
ready_plate = heat_sample(sample=prepped_plate, temperature_c=72, duration_min=3, device="thermocycler")
protocol_title: Detection of m 6 A in single cultured cells using scDART-seq

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Add 2 million cells to a 1.5 mL tube and pellet by centrifuging at 1,000 × g for 3 min at 23°C. CRITICAL: It is important not to centrifuge cells above 1,000 × g to maintain high cell viability. Centrifugation at lower speeds may improve viability but lead to greater cell loss. Use polypropylene tubes during these steps.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Remove as much supernatant as possible without disturbing the pellet.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Resuspend pellet in 200 μL of flow buffer. Gently dissociate the pellet using a wide-bore P1000 pipette tip with pipette set to 150 μL. CRITICAL: Gently resuspending cell pellets using wide-bore pipette tips is recommended, as vigorous dissociation can damage cells, increase cellular debris, and decrease viability. Avoid creating large amounts of bubbles. Note: Instead of wide-bore tips, it is possible to cut the bottom of standard P1000 pipette tips using sterilized scissors or a razor blade to expand the bore size.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Add 2 μL of near-IR LIVE/DEAD viability dye and incubate in the dark for 5 min at 23°C.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Add 2 μL (4 units) of DNaseI. Gently mix by pipetting with a wide-bore P1000 pipette tip. Incubate for 15 min at 23°C in the dark.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Pellet the cells by centrifugation at 1,000 × g for 3 min.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Remove as much supernatant as possible without disturbing the pellet.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Add 1 mL of flow buffer and gently dissociate the pellet with a wide-bore P1000 pipette tip.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Filter cells using 40 μm nylon filter.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Use a FACS Aria II to sort 1 viable cell per well of each plate. Immediately after a plate is finished sorting, cover with a foil adhesive plate cover, spin plate at 1,000 × g for 1 min in a swinging bucket centrifuge, and immediately place on dry ice.
Thaw one of the 384-well plates with sorted single cells on ice. Centrifuge at 2,000 × g for 5 min at 4°C.
Incubate plate in a thermocycler set to 72°C for 3 min.

background: Embryonic cortical tissue has been collected for establishing a 3D Matrigel culture of differentiating neurons. At this stage, the cortex pieces are in a microtube with cold L15 medium and need to be enzymatically dissociated, pelleted, resuspended into a homogeneous cell suspension, then chilled and embedded before short culture setup.
goal: Process the cortical tissue through digestion, quenching, low-speed pelleting, chilled resuspension, Matrigel handling, and final culture incubation so that a Matrigel-embedded neuronal aggregate is ready for the initial 3-day culture period.
constraints: Begin from cortex tissue already in ice-cold L15 medium in a microtube, and include a brief sedimenting spin before enzymatic digestion.
Represent digestion with 0.05% Trypsin-EDTA at 37 C for 25 min, with mixing during the digestion period, and quench the reaction with room-temperature FBS before removing supernatant.
Model mechanical dissociation as pipetting to homogeneity, then perform a gentle centrifugation at 80 x g for 5 min before chilled L15 resuspension.
Preserve cold conditions for the post-pellet L15 handling and Matrigel handling, including a 1 min ice adherence period before gelation.
Include Matrigel gelation for 25 min at 37 C and finish with the initial culture phase in prewarmed DMEM for 3 days at 37 C under humidified 5% CO2-like conditions.

gold_program:
brief_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=cortical_tissue_tube, speed_xg=2500, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=4)
trypsin_digest = incubate_sample(sample=brief_spin, temperature_c=37, duration_min=25, condition='static')
digest_mix = mix_sample(sample=trypsin_digest, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='gentle')
quench_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=digest_mix, speed_xg=2500, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=22)
dissociated_cells = mix_sample(sample=quench_spin, method='pipetting', duration_s=30, speed='moderate')
gentle_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=dissociated_cells, speed_xg=80, duration_min=5, temperature_c=22)
chilled_resuspension = cool_sample(sample=gentle_pellet, temperature_c=0, duration_min=1, device='ice bath')
resuspended_pellet = mix_sample(sample=chilled_resuspension, method='pipetting', duration_s=15, speed='gentle')
matrigel_chilled = cool_sample(sample=matrigel_mix, temperature_c=0, duration_min=1, device='ice bath')
ice_adhesion = incubate_sample(sample=resuspended_pellet, temperature_c=0, duration_min=1, condition='on ice with chilled Matrigel')
gelated_embed = incubate_sample(sample=ice_adhesion, temperature_c=37, duration_min=25, condition='humidified 5% CO2')
initial_culture = incubate_sample(sample=gelated_embed, temperature_c=37, duration_min=72, condition='humidified 5% CO2 with prewarmed DMEM')

source_steps.stage: A. Primary culture of differentiating cortical neurons in Matrigel
source_steps.description: Transfer the cortices from four embryos to a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube, mince the tissue into two or three pieces with forceps, and add 200 μL of ice-cold L15 medium to allow the tissue to sediment to the bottom of the tube in the following step.

source_steps.stage: A. Primary culture of differentiating cortical neurons in Matrigel
source_steps.description: Briefly spin down the tissue and remove the L15 medium from the tube. Add 200 μL of ice-cold 0.05% Trypsin-EDTA (pre-dilute 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA solution with L15) and incubate the tissue for 25 min at 37 °C. Tap the tube every 10 min to mix the tissue well.

source_steps.stage: A. Primary culture of differentiating cortical neurons in Matrigel
source_steps.description: Add 200 μL of room temperature (RT) FBS to the tissue and tap the tube. Spin down the tissue with a benchtop centrifuge for 30 s (max ca. 2,500× g ). Remove the supernatant.

source_steps.stage: A. Primary culture of differentiating cortical neurons in Matrigel
source_steps.description: Add 200 μL of dissociation buffer to the tissue and titrate the suspension five times with a P1000 and 10 times with a P200 pipette until the suspension is homogeneous.

source_steps.stage: A. Primary culture of differentiating cortical neurons in Matrigel
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the tube at 80× g for 5 min. Remove the supernatant from the tube.

source_steps.stage: A. Primary culture of differentiating cortical neurons in Matrigel
source_steps.description: Carefully add 500 μL of ice-cold L15 medium to the tube. Flush the medium using a P200 pipette to the adhering part of the cell pellet and carefully lift the pellet from the tube surface.

source_steps.stage: A. Primary culture of differentiating cortical neurons in Matrigel
source_steps.description: Transfer the cell pellet to a 35 mm dissection Petri dish with pre-chilled L15 medium. Prepare 0.5–1 mm 3 cell aggregate cubes from the cell pellet using an ophthalmic knife and ultrafine forceps.

source_steps.stage: A. Primary culture of differentiating cortical neurons in Matrigel
source_steps.description: Place a 5 μL 75% Matrigel/L15 drop on a glass-bottom dish and keep the dish on ice.

source_steps.stage: A. Primary culture of differentiating cortical neurons in Matrigel
source_steps.description: Prepare a fresh 35 mm tissue culture dish with a 10 μL 75%/L15 Matrigel drop. Transfer a cell aggregate (from step A10) to the Matrigel drop using a P20 pipette.

source_steps.stage: A. Primary culture of differentiating cortical neurons in Matrigel
source_steps.description: Suck the cell aggregate together with 10 μL of Matrigel and place it on a 5 μL Matrigel drop on the glass-bottom dish (step A11). Incubate the glass-bottom dish on ice for 1 min to let the pellet adhere to the bottom of the dish.

source_steps.stage: A. Primary culture of differentiating cortical neurons in Matrigel
source_steps.description: Incubate the glass-bottom dish for 25 min at 37 °C in the presence of 5% CO 2 for Matrigel gelation.

source_steps.stage: A. Primary culture of differentiating cortical neurons in Matrigel
source_steps.description: Carefully add 200 μL of prewarmed DMEM culture medium and culture the cell aggregates for 3 days at 37 °C in the presence of 5% CO 2 .

background: An oral tumor specimen has already been received for the dissociation portion of a workflow used before flow-cytometry analysis of stem-like oral cancer cell diversity. At this stage, the tissue must be cleaned, briefly treated to remove contaminating blood cells, kept chilled when indicated, and then enzymatically dissociated with periodic resuspension so released cells can be collected.
goal: Complete the local wash, lysis, chilling, digestion, and post-digestion recovery segment to produce a released-cell suspension and a separately washed residual pellet suitable for the next processing stage.
constraints: Start from a chilled tumor sample and preserve cold handling where indicated, including returning the material to chilled transport conditions before the dissociation phase.

Represent the initial cleaning and lysis as an antibiotic-containing PBS wash for 5 minutes with gentle rocking and an ACK treatment for 2 minutes at room temperature in the 24-27 C range, each followed by a 500 x g centrifugation for 10 s.
Model the post-lysis cleanup as two chilled PBS antibiotic washes overall, using the same brief centrifugation condition of 500 x g for 10 s.

Capture the dissociation stage as exposure to fresh dissociation medium in a 37 C water bath for 60 minutes, with repeated gentle pipette-style mixing events that abstract the every-15-minute resuspension requirement.
After digestion, perform another 500 x g spin for 10 s, then produce an output state corresponding to the collected released-cell suspension in a fresh tube.
Also include one additional wash of the remaining pellet in transport medium, followed by a final 500 x g, 10 s centrifugation.
Use only provided action-pool functions with keyword arguments, maintain a single dependency chain, and reuse at least two action families multiple times.

available_inputs.name: tumor_sample
available_inputs.description: Chilled oral tumor tissue specimen received for dissociation.

available_inputs.name: pbs_antibiotic
available_inputs.description: PBS containing 2x gentamicin and 2x antibiotic-antimycotic mix.

available_inputs.name: ack_buffer
available_inputs.description: ACK lysis buffer for brief room-temperature treatment.

available_inputs.name: transport_media
available_inputs.description: Fresh tissue transport medium used during chilled handling and pellet wash.

available_inputs.name: dissociation_media
available_inputs.description: Fresh tissue dissociation medium used for enzymatic digestion.

gold_program:
chilled_input = cool_sample(sample=tumor_sample, temperature_c=4, duration_min=5, device="ice")
pbs_wash = mix_sample(sample=chilled_input, method="rocking", duration_s=300, speed="gentle")
spin_after_pbs = centrifuge_sample(sample=pbs_wash, speed_xg=500, duration_min=0.17, temperature_c=4)
ack_lysis = incubate_sample(sample=spin_after_pbs, temperature_c=25, duration_min=2, condition="gentle rocking")
spin_after_ack = centrifuge_sample(sample=ack_lysis, speed_xg=500, duration_min=0.17, temperature_c=25)
post_lysis_cleanup = mix_sample(sample=spin_after_ack, method="rocking", duration_s=60, speed="gentle")
chilled_prewarm_state = cool_sample(sample=post_lysis_cleanup, temperature_c=4, duration_min=5, device="ice")
digestion = heat_sample(sample=chilled_prewarm_state, temperature_c=37, duration_min=60, device="water bath")
post_digest_mix = mix_sample(sample=digestion, method="pipetting", duration_s=60, speed="gentle")
spin_after_digest = centrifuge_sample(sample=post_digest_mix, speed_xg=500, duration_min=0.17, temperature_c=25)
tube_a_cells = store_sample(sample=spin_after_digest, temperature_c=4, duration_h=0.1, container="Tube A")
final_pellet_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=tube_a_cells, speed_xg=500, duration_min=0.17, temperature_c=4)

source_steps.description: Transport samples to the lab in freshly prepared tissue transport media (prepared fresh and did not store) under chilled condition using frozen gel packs.

Wash tumor sample pieces with PBS containing 2× Gentamicin, 2× antibiotic-antimycotic mix (Penicillin, Streptomycin, Amphotericin B - mix) with gentle rocking for five minutes followed by centrifugation at 500 × g for 10 s.
Wash the sample with ACK lysis buffer at room-temperature (24°C–27°C) for two minutes with gentle rocking, and centrifuge at 500 × g for 10 s.
Wash twice with chilled PBS containing 2× Gentamicin, 2× antibiotic-antimycotic mix. Centrifuge at 500 × g for 10 s.
Transfer in chilled tissue transport media. Keep it on ice.
Put a cell culture dish on ice and transfer the sample in the dish in 500 μL tissue transport media. Mince the sample into fine pieces, using a surgical blade.
Collect the samples and resuspend in 5 mL of tissue dissociation media (prepared fresh and did not store).
Transfer the tissue suspension in a 15 mL tube and put in a rack, placed in a 37°C water bath.
Digest the samples for 60 min with mixing by pipetting up and down by 2 mL serological pipette with gentle pressure against the bottom of the tube for 5 times in every 15 min.
Spin at 500 × g for 10 s.
Collect all the cells in suspension in a fresh tube (Tube A).
Wash the pellet once by adding 1 mL tissue transport media and centrifuging at 500 × g for 10 s.

background: HEK293F cells have already been transfected with a SARS-CoV-2 spike ectodomain expression construct and recovered overnight under expression conditions. The immediate work is to prepare the two media supplements at the proper temperatures, expand the culture to its final volume, continue expression for several days, and then harvest by clarifying the culture into conical tubes for downstream purification.
goal: Carry out the culture enhancement and harvest segment so the transfected mammalian culture is supplemented under the right temperature conditions, returned to expression, and finally clarified into harvest tubes ready for purification.
constraints: Use the overnight-transfected HEK293F culture as the starting material and keep later outputs dependent on that culture rather than restarting from raw inputs.
Prepare FreeStyle 293F medium by warming it to 37 C for about 45 min, and equilibrate Hyclone SFM4HEK293 medium at room temperature near 22 C for about 45 min before supplementation.

Model the two supplementation events as separate gentle-mixing updates to the same culture: first after adding 80 mL Hyclone medium, then after bringing the culture to a final volume of 1 L with FreeStyle 293F medium.
After supplementation, continue expression at 37 C for 5 days under a shaking condition.
Represent the harvest as two 500 mL split culture tubes that are each clarified by centrifugation at 4000 x g for 30 min at 22 C.
Use only provided action-pool functions, assign every call to a variable, and make at least two action names recur multiple times.
The program must have 6-12 top-level steps, include keyword arguments only, and preserve a long dependency chain from supplementation through clarification.

available_inputs.name: transfected_culture
available_inputs.description: HEK293F spike-expression culture after the initial 16-18 h post-transfection incubation.

available_inputs.name: freestyle_293f_medium
available_inputs.description: FreeStyle 293F medium to be warmed before addition to the culture.

available_inputs.name: hyclone_sfm4hek293_medium
available_inputs.description: Hyclone SFM4HEK293 medium to equilibrate at room temperature before supplementation.

available_inputs.name: culture_split_tube_a
available_inputs.description: First 500 mL conical tube containing half of the supplemented 1 L culture for harvest.

available_inputs.name: culture_split_tube_b
available_inputs.description: Second 500 mL conical tube containing the remaining half of the supplemented 1 L culture for harvest.

gold_program:
warmed_freestyle_293f_medium = heat_sample(sample=freestyle_293f_medium, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=45.0, device="water bath")
rt_hyclone_sfm4hek293_medium = incubate_sample(sample=hyclone_sfm4hek293_medium, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=45.0, condition="static")
post_hyclone_addition = mix_sample(sample=transfected_culture, method="swirl", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle after adding 80 mL room-temperature Hyclone medium")
post_freestyle_fill = mix_sample(sample=post_hyclone_addition, method="swirl", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle after filling with warmed FreeStyle medium to 1 L")
expression_culture = incubate_sample(sample=post_freestyle_fill, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=7200.0, condition="shaking")
preharvest_tube_a = mix_sample(sample=culture_split_tube_a, method="inversion", duration_s=5.0, speed="gentle")
preharvest_tube_b = mix_sample(sample=culture_split_tube_b, method="inversion", duration_s=5.0, speed="gentle")
clarified_tube_a = centrifuge_sample(sample=preharvest_tube_a, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=30.0, temperature_c=22.0)
clarified_tube_b = centrifuge_sample(sample=preharvest_tube_b, speed_xg=4000, duration_min=30.0, temperature_c=22.0)
protocol_title: Transient transfection and purification of SARS-CoV-2 spike protein from mammalian cells

source_steps.description: On the day of transfection prepare 800 mL of HEK293F cells at 2.0 × 10 6 cells/mL, with a viability of 95%–100% in a 2 L baffled bottom flask.

Prepare DNA/transfection reagent complex: dilute 2.25 mL of Turbo 293 transfection reagent into 37.5 mL of Opti-MEM I medium in a 125 mL flask, gently swirl to mix. Incubate at room temperature (20°C–23°C) for 5 min.
Meanwhile, dilute 750 μg of spike DNA into 37.5 mL of Opit-MEM I Medium in the funnel of a sterile filter top, attached to a 50 mL tube.

Add diluted DNA to Turbo 293 complex by pouring gently down the side of the flask wall, mix gently by swirling and incubate at room temperature (20°C–23°C) for 15 min to allow the formation of DNA-Turbo 293 complex.

Add DNA-Turbo 293 complex, made in step 4, to the 800 mL of cell culture made in step 1, by tilting the cell culture flask at approximately 45 degrees and gently pour the DNA-Turbo 293 complex into the cell culture flask, gently swirl to mix. Leave the flask at room temperature (20°C–23°C) to incubate for 15 min.
Return the flask to incubate at 37°C, 9% CO 2 , 120 rpm, for 16–18 h.
Warm FreeStyle 293F Medium in 37°C water bath until warm, approximately 45 min to 1 h.
Place Hyclone SFM4HEK293 medium at room temperature (20°C–23°C), for approximately 45 min to 1 h.
Add 80 mL Hyclone Medium to cell culture flask, swirl gently to mix.
Add Freestyle 293F medium to cell culture flask until volume reaches 1 L, swirl gently to mix.
Return to incubator at 37°C, 9% CO 2 , 120 rpm, for 5 more days.

source_steps.stage: Harvesting cell culture supernatant & preparation for purification
source_steps.description: Divide 1 L cell culture into two 500 mL conical tubes and centrifuge at 4,000 × g for 30 min at 22°C.

background: Single cells have already been sorted into a sealed 384-well plate and frozen immediately after collection. The current task is the initial handling for SMART-seq2 cDNA synthesis: recover the frozen plate under cold conditions, concentrate contents by plate centrifugation, apply the short denaturation-style heat treatment, return the plate to ice, and set up the reaction with the kit mastermix while preserving the cold workflow.
goal: Process one frozen sorted single-cell plate through the pre-cDNA-synthesis handling steps so it is cold, spun, heat-treated, recooled, and combined with the SMART-seq HT one-step mastermix in a state ready for downstream reaction setup.
constraints: Use one frozen 384-well sorted-cell plate as the main sample and keep a single dependency chain through the workflow.
Include an initial cold recovery stage on ice before the plate spin, then centrifuge the plate at 2000 x g for 5 min at 4 C.
After the cold spin, perform a thermocycler-associated heating step of 72 C for 3 min and then immediately cool the plate on ice for 2 min.
Represent addition of the SMART-seq HT one-step mastermix as gentle pipette-style mixing corresponding to 1 uL per well; do not use vortexing.
Keep the prepared plate cold after mastermix incorporation rather than holding it at room temperature.
Use only provided action-pool functions with keyword arguments, and include 8 to 12 top-level action calls total.

available_inputs.name: sorted_plate_frozen
available_inputs.description: 384-well plate containing single sorted cells that was sealed and frozen after sorting

available_inputs.name: ice_bucket
available_inputs.description: Ice-based cooling setup used for thawing and cold plate handling

available_inputs.name: plate_centrifuge_4c
available_inputs.description: Refrigerated centrifuge appropriate for plate spins at 4 C

available_inputs.name: thermocycler
available_inputs.description: Thermal cycler used for the short 72 C incubation

available_inputs.name: one_step_mastermix
available_inputs.description: SMART-seq HT one-step mastermix added at 1 uL per well

gold_program:
cold_recovered_plate = cool_sample(sample=sorted_plate_frozen, temperature_c=0, duration_min=5, device=ice_bucket)
pre_spin_hold = cool_sample(sample=cold_recovered_plate, temperature_c=0, duration_min=1, device=ice_bucket)
spun_plate = centrifuge_sample(sample=pre_spin_hold, speed_xg=2000, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
post_spin_cold_plate = cool_sample(sample=spun_plate, temperature_c=4, duration_min=1, device=ice_bucket)
heated_plate = heat_sample(sample=post_spin_cold_plate, temperature_c=72, duration_min=3, device=thermocycler)
recooled_plate = cool_sample(sample=heated_plate, temperature_c=0, duration_min=2, device=ice_bucket)
mastermix_premix_plate = mix_sample(sample=recooled_plate, method="pipetting with one_step_mastermix at 1 uL per well", duration_s=5, speed="gentle")
mastermix_complete_plate = mix_sample(sample=mastermix_premix_plate, method="pipetting with one_step_mastermix at 1 uL per well", duration_s=10, speed="gentle")
final_cold_plate = cool_sample(sample=mastermix_complete_plate, temperature_c=0, duration_min=1, device=ice_bucket)
protocol_title: Detection of m 6 A in single cultured cells using scDART-seq

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Resuspend pellet in 200 μL of flow buffer. Gently dissociate the pellet using a wide-bore P1000 pipette tip with pipette set to 150 μL. CRITICAL: Gently resuspending cell pellets using wide-bore pipette tips is recommended, as vigorous dissociation can damage cells, increase cellular debris, and decrease viability. Avoid creating large amounts of bubbles. Note: Instead of wide-bore tips, it is possible to cut the bottom of standard P1000 pipette tips using sterilized scissors or a razor blade to expand the bore size.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Add 2 μL of near-IR LIVE/DEAD viability dye and incubate in the dark for 5 min at 23°C.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Add 2 μL (4 units) of DNaseI. Gently mix by pipetting with a wide-bore P1000 pipette tip. Incubate for 15 min at 23°C in the dark.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Pellet the cells by centrifugation at 1,000 × g for 3 min.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Remove as much supernatant as possible without disturbing the pellet.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Add 1 mL of flow buffer and gently dissociate the pellet with a wide-bore P1000 pipette tip.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Filter cells using 40 μm nylon filter.

source_steps.stage: Transgene induction and sorting of single cells
source_steps.description: Use a FACS Aria II to sort 1 viable cell per well of each plate. Immediately after a plate is finished sorting, cover with a foil adhesive plate cover, spin plate at 1,000 × g for 1 min in a swinging bucket centrifuge, and immediately place on dry ice.
Thaw one of the 384-well plates with sorted single cells on ice. Centrifuge at 2,000 × g for 5 min at 4°C.
Incubate plate in a thermocycler set to 72°C for 3 min.
Immediately remove the plate from the thermocycler and incubate on ice for 2 min.
While on ice, add 1 μL of One-step mastermix (provided in SMART-seq HT Kit) and briefly mix by pipetting.

background: A dissociated lung patient-derived xenograft sample has completed mechanical tumor breakup and now needs local recovery and cleanup before CFSE labeling. The current segment focuses on collecting cells from the dissociation vessel, consolidating them by centrifugation, allowing viable cells to attach in short-term culture, then detaching, washing, and holding the cells in PBS so the suspension is ready for downstream staining.
goal: Prepare the dissociated lung PDX cells into a clean PBS suspension suitable for the next CFSE staining stage, while preserving the required recovery, attachment, detachment, and wash conditions from this part of the protocol.
constraints: Normalize room temperature centrifugation steps to 22.0 C and include the initial collection spin at 300 x g for 1.0 min after dissociation handling.
Include three additional centrifugation events at 300 x g for 7.0 min at RT that correspond to post-filtration recovery, post-detachment recovery, and PBS wash collection.
Represent the attachment culture as a 37.0 C humidified 5% CO2 static incubation lasting within the 12-24 h window.
Represent dish cleanup before detachment as two PBS washes, then model detachment as a 37.0 C incubation for 5.0 min after adding 2x TE and neutralization with complete medium at twice the TE volume.
Maintain a single dependency chain from the dissociation tube through final preparation rather than restarting from raw inputs.
End with cells resuspended in sterile PBS at 2e7 to 2e8 cells per mL for CFSE preparation, using only provided action-pool functions and keyword arguments.

available_inputs.name: c_tube_sample
available_inputs.description: Lung PDX dissociation C tube after the second gentleMACS tumor dissociation run.

available_inputs.name: complete_rpmi
available_inputs.description: Complete RPMI 1640 medium used for recovery, short culture, and detachment neutralization.

available_inputs.name: culture_dish_90mm
available_inputs.description: Ninety millimeter dish used for attachment culture of viable tumor cells.

available_inputs.name: pbs_buffer
available_inputs.description: Sterile PBS used for dish washes and final cell resuspension.

available_inputs.name: conical_15ml
available_inputs.description: Fifteen milliliter conical tube used during post-detachment and wash centrifugation.

gold_program:
collected_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=c_tube_sample, speed_xg=300, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=22.0)
resuspended_cells = store_sample(sample=collected_cells, temperature_c=22.0, duration_h=0.05, container='resuspended in 10 mL complete RPMI and passed through 70 um strainer with additional 10 mL complete RPMI wash into collection tube')
recovered_cells = centrifuge_sample(sample=resuspended_cells, speed_xg=300, duration_min=7.0, temperature_c=22.0)
attached_culture = incubate_sample(sample=recovered_cells, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=720.0, condition='humidified 5% CO2 static culture in 90 mm dish with 10 mL complete RPMI for cell attachment')
washed_attached_cells = store_sample(sample=attached_culture, temperature_c=22.0, duration_h=0.02, container='culture dish after two PBS washes removing unattached dead cells and floating murine-immune cells')
detached_cells = incubate_sample(sample=washed_attached_cells, temperature_c=37.0, duration_min=5.0, condition='2x TE detachment in culture dish')
neutralized_cells = store_sample(sample=detached_cells, temperature_c=22.0, duration_h=0.02, container='detached cells in 15 mL conical tube with complete culture media at twice the TE volume')
post_detachment_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=neutralized_cells, speed_xg=300, duration_min=7.0, temperature_c=22.0)
pbs_wash_state = store_sample(sample=post_detachment_pellet, temperature_c=22.0, duration_h=0.02, container='cell pellet resuspended and washed with 5 mL sterile PBS')
washed_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=pbs_wash_state, speed_xg=300, duration_min=7.0, temperature_c=22.0)
cfse_ready_cells = store_sample(sample=washed_pellet, temperature_c=22.0, duration_h=0.25, container='sterile PBS suspension at 2e7 to 2e8 cells per mL ready for CFSE staining')
protocol_title: Isolation of slow-cycling cancer cells from lung patient-derived xenograft using carboxyfluorescein-succinimidyl ester retention-mediated cell sorting

source_steps.stage: Collection of lung PDX tumor from immunodeficient mouse and establishment of a primary cell line
source_steps.description: Attach the C tube to the gentleMACS™ dissociator and run program h_tumor_02 (2 nd round).

source_steps.stage: Collection of lung PDX tumor from immunodeficient mouse and establishment of a primary cell line
source_steps.description: Detach the C tube and centrifuge at 300 × g for 1 min at room temperate (RT, 22 ± 2°C) to collect the dissociated cells.

source_steps.stage: Collection of lung PDX tumor from immunodeficient mouse and establishment of a primary cell line
source_steps.description: Resuspend the dissociated cell pellet with 10 mL of culture media (complete RPMI 1640) and transfer the cell suspension into a cell strainer (70 μm) placed on a 50 mL conical tube. Add 10 mL of culture media (complete RPMI 1640) to wash cells through the strainer.

source_steps.stage: Collection of lung PDX tumor from immunodeficient mouse and establishment of a primary cell line
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the filtered cell suspension at 300 × g for 7 min. Remove the supernatant completely and resuspend the cell pellet in 5 mL of cell culture media (complete RPMI 1640).

source_steps.stage: Collection of lung PDX tumor from immunodeficient mouse and establishment of a primary cell line
source_steps.description: Add 100 μL of 0.4% trypan blue solution to 100 μL cell suspension.

source_steps.stage: Collection of lung PDX tumor from immunodeficient mouse and establishment of a primary cell line
source_steps.description: Load 10 μL of cell suspension on a hemocytometer and count a viable (unstained) cell number.

source_steps.stage: Collection of lung PDX tumor from immunodeficient mouse and establishment of a primary cell line
source_steps.description: Seed 0.5–1 × 10 7 viable cells per a 90 mm culture dish with 10 mL complete culture media. Incubate cells at 37°C in a 5% CO 2 incubator for -12–24 h.
Remove unattached dead cells and floating murine-immune cells with 5 mL of PBS (for a 90 mm culture dish) twice.
Add 1 mL of 2× TE solution to detach the viable cells from the culture dish. Incubate the cells in 37°C CO 2 incubator for 5 min.
Add complete culture media of two times the volume of 2× TE solution. Transfer the detached cells to a 15 mL conical tube and centrifuge at 300 × g for 7 min at RT.
Remove the supernatant and resuspend and wash the cell pellet with 5 mL PBS. Centrifuge at 300 × g for 7 min at RT.
Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cells at 2–20 × 10 7 cells/mL in sterile PBS.

background: Cross-linked plant nuclear and total fractions have already been lysed and brought to the RNA recovery stage. The immediate task is to isolate RNA from the lysate by organic extraction, cold alcohol precipitation, pelleting, and repeated ethanol washing while maintaining cold centrifugation conditions.
goal: Recover a clean RNA pellet from the prepared lysate so it is ready for the next downstream handling step.
constraints: Start from a lysed fraction already suitable for phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol extraction, and perform vigorous mixing for 15 s before the first phase-separation spin.

Use refrigerated centrifugation for phase separation at 12000 x g for 15 min at 4 C, then proceed with precipitation conditions matching recovery of about 250 uL aqueous phase equivalent plus glycogen, 30 uL of 3 M sodium acetate pH 5.2, and 750 uL absolute ethanol.
Precipitate RNA under a valid cold condition of either -80 C for at least 30 min or -20 C for at least 8 h, then pellet using 12000 x g for 30 min at 4 C.
Wash the RNA pellet with 80 percent ethanol and centrifuge again for 5 min at 4 C; repeat the wash cycle once more on the same pellet-derived sample.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keep all calls assigned to variables with keyword arguments only, and maintain a dependency chain where later steps consume outputs from earlier ones.
Include 6-12 top-level action calls, reuse at least one action family more than once, and make at least two recurring action names differ only by one or two numeric parameters across repetitions.

available_inputs.name: lysed_fraction
available_inputs.description: Cross-link-reversed nuclear or total fraction lysate ready for RNA extraction.

available_inputs.name: rotiaqua_pci
available_inputs.description: Acid phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol extraction reagent to be added to the lysate.

available_inputs.name: sodium_acetate_3M
available_inputs.description: 3 M sodium acetate pH 5.2 used for RNA precipitation.

available_inputs.name: absolute_ethanol
available_inputs.description: Absolute ethanol used to precipitate RNA.

available_inputs.name: glycogen
available_inputs.description: Carrier glycogen added during RNA precipitation.

available_inputs.name: ethanol_80
available_inputs.description: 80 percent ethanol used for pellet washing.

gold_program:
extraction_mixed = vortex_mix(sample=lysed_fraction, duration_s=15.0, speed_rpm=3000, temperature_c=4.0)
phase_separated = centrifuge_sample(sample=extraction_mixed, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=15.0, temperature_c=4.0)
precipitation_mixed = mix_sample(sample=phase_separated, method="inversion", duration_s=10.0, speed="gentle")
precipitated_rna = cool_sample(sample=precipitation_mixed, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_min=30.0, device="freezer")
rna_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=precipitated_rna, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=30.0, temperature_c=4.0)
first_wash_mixed = mix_sample(sample=rna_pellet, method="inversion", duration_s=5.0, speed="gentle")
first_wash_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=first_wash_mixed, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
second_wash_mixed = mix_sample(sample=first_wash_pellet, method="inversion", duration_s=5.0, speed="gentle")
second_wash_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=second_wash_mixed, speed_xg=12000, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
washed_rna_pellet = store_sample(sample=second_wash_pellet, temperature_c=4.0, duration_h=0.5, container="1.5 mL tube")
protocol_title: Nuclear RNA purification by flow cytometry to study nuclear processes in plants

source_steps.stage: Microscopy, nuclei concentration, and reverse cross-linking
source_steps.description: Add 200 μL of Lysis Buffer to the remaining re-suspended nuclei from step 21. The final volume will be ∼300 μL. Incubate on a spinning wheel 10 min at 4°C. Keep as nuclear fraction.

source_steps.stage: Microscopy, nuclei concentration, and reverse cross-linking
source_steps.description: In parallel, during centrifugation time (step 20), process total fraction from step 6. Grind the sample in liquid Nitrogen with a mortar and pestle while adding 1 mL of Lysis Buffer and filter through a 35 μm mesh. Afterwards, incubate it on a spinning wheel for 10 min at 4°C. Take ∼300 μL of the total fraction for sonication.

source_steps.stage: Microscopy, nuclei concentration, and reverse cross-linking
source_steps.description: Sonicate total and nuclear fractions using a Bioruptor. Use the following settings: Power: H position (High), Sonication cycle: 30 s ON, 30 s OFF, Total sonication time: five cycles on a refrigerated bath. The main purpose of the sonication step is not only to shear DNA but also ensure all nuclei are lysed after the Lysis Buffer incubation.

source_steps.stage: Microscopy, nuclei concentration, and reverse cross-linking
source_steps.description: Add 12 μL of 5 M NaCl, to a final concentration of ∼200 mM, and 40 μg of proteinase K. First, incubate for 15 min at 50°C to allow digestion of cross-linked polypeptides by proteinase K, and then for 30 min at 65°C to reverse the formaldehyde cross-linking.

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Add 300 μL of RotiAqua P/C/I (Phenol acid/Chloroform/Isoamilic) for RNA extraction.

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Vortex strongly for 15 s, mix well.

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 15 min at 4°C

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Transfer ∼250 μL of aqueous phase (upper phase) into a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube. Add 1 μL of glycogen , 30 μL of NaAc 3 M pH 5.2 and 750 μL Ethanol absolute. Mix by inverting and incubate at −80°C for at least 30 min or at −20°C for at least 8 h (recommended).

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 30 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant. The RNA should be seen as a white pellet.

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Wash the pellet with ∼500 μL of 80% EtOH. Centrifuge at maximum speed for 5 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis: days 1 and 2
source_steps.description: Discard the supernatant. Repeat steps 33 and 34 once.

background: Myeloid cells have already been enriched from mouse brain tumor tissue by density gradient separation, and the cloudy interphase fraction containing the target cells has been collected into a fresh tube. The next stage is to wash away Percoll, concentrate the cells, label them with CD11b magnetic beads under cold conditions, and finish the post-label wash so the suspension is ready for magnetic-column enrichment.
goal: Process the recovered interphase cell fraction through the cold wash, concentration, bead-labeling, and final wash steps to obtain a bead-labeled myeloid cell suspension in cold MACS buffer suitable for the subsequent magnetic separation setup.
constraints: Begin from the collected Percoll interface fraction and keep the MACS-buffer workflow cold, using 4 degrees Celsius for the centrifuge steps.
The first cleanup after interface recovery is a wash that corresponds to dilution with ice-cold MACS buffer followed by centrifugation at 300 x g for 7 minutes.
After that wash, concentrate the cells by resuspending in 1 mL cold MACS buffer and performing a brief second spin at 300 x g for 1 minute.
Before labeling, the pellet should be in 90 uL MACS buffer per 10^7 total cells, and CD11b MicroBeads are applied at 10 uL per 90 uL cell suspension with a 15 minute incubation on ice.
Following bead labeling, carry out a wash in 1 mL MACS buffer with centrifugation at 300 x g for 10 minutes at 4 degrees Celsius, then finish in 500 uL cold MACS buffer.

Use only the provided action-pool functions; represent resuspension or gentle handling with the generic mixing action and preserve a single dependency chain from the interface fraction to the final labeled suspension.

available_inputs.name: interface_fraction
available_inputs.description: Collected cloudy Percoll interphase fraction containing myeloid cells in a 15 mL conical tube

available_inputs.name: cold_macs_buffer
available_inputs.description: Ice-cold MACS buffer used for washes and resuspensions

available_inputs.name: cd11b_microbeads
available_inputs.description: CD11b magnetic microbead reagent for labeling myeloid cells

gold_program:
mixed_wash1 = mix_sample(sample=interface_fraction, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
spun_wash1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=mixed_wash1, speed_xg=300, duration_min=7.0, temperature_c=4.0)
resuspended_1ml = mix_sample(sample=spun_wash1, method='pipetting', duration_s=8.0, speed='gentle')
spun_concentrated = centrifuge_sample(sample=resuspended_1ml, speed_xg=300, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=4.0)
resuspended_90ul = mix_sample(sample=spun_concentrated, method='pipetting', duration_s=8.0, speed='gentle')
bead_labeled_mix = mix_sample(sample=resuspended_90ul, method='pipetting', duration_s=8.0, speed='gentle')
bead_labeled_incubated = incubate_sample(sample=bead_labeled_mix, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=15.0, condition='static on ice')
post_label_wash_mix = mix_sample(sample=bead_labeled_incubated, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
post_label_spin = centrifuge_sample(sample=post_label_wash_mix, speed_xg=300, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
final_cd11b_cell_suspension = mix_sample(sample=post_label_spin, method='pipetting', duration_s=8.0, speed='gentle')
protocol_title: Isolation of myeloid cells from mouse brain tumors for single-cell RNA-seq analysis
