source_steps.stage: Percoll gradient-based isolation of myeloid cells
source_steps.description: Homogenize tissue as described above. Bring cell suspension volume up to 6 mL and add 4 mL of SIP to make 10 mL of 40% Percoll cell suspension. Layer 80% Percoll beneath the 40% Percoll layer using method described above. Spin at 325 × g for 30 min.

source_steps.stage: Percoll gradient-based isolation of myeloid cells
source_steps.description: Gently remove the tube from the centrifuge. A white, thin layer of myelin will be seen at the top of the tube. Additionally, a cloudy interface layer of several millimeters thick containing myeloid cells will be seen at the interface between the two Percoll layers.

source_steps.stage: Percoll gradient-based isolation of myeloid cells
source_steps.description: Aspirate and discard the top white layer that contains myelin fragments.

source_steps.stage: Percoll gradient-based isolation of myeloid cells
source_steps.description: Using a spinal needle attached to a 5 mL syringe, insert the needle into the gradient until the tip hits the interface layer and gently aspirate into the syringe the interface layer (∼2–3 mL depending on the number of cells that determines the thickness of the layer) and gently transfer to a fresh 15 mL conical tube.

source_steps.stage: Percoll gradient-based isolation of myeloid cells
source_steps.description: To wash cells and remove Percoll, add ice-cold 12 mL MACS buffer. Mix with gentle pipetting. Reset centrifuge deceleration back to maximum and temperature to 4°C. Spin cell suspension 300 × g for 7 min.

source_steps.stage: Percoll gradient-based isolation of myeloid cells
source_steps.description: Discard supernatant and resuspend cell pellet in 1 mL of cold MACs buffer and transfer to 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

source_steps.stage: Percoll gradient-based isolation of myeloid cells
source_steps.description: Spin at 300 × g speed for 1 min.

source_steps.stage: Percoll gradient-based isolation of myeloid cells
source_steps.description: Discard supernatant and resuspend cell pellet in 90 μL of MACs buffer (per 10 7 total cells).

source_steps.stage: Section 2: Further enrichment of myeloid cells using CD11b+ magnetic beads
source_steps.description: Incubate cell suspension with CD11b MicroBeads TM by adding 10 μL of microbeads to every 90 μL of cell suspension and incubate for 15 min on ice.

source_steps.stage: Section 2: Further enrichment of myeloid cells using CD11b+ magnetic beads
source_steps.description: To wash cells, resuspend in 1 mL of MACs buffer and spin at 300 × g for 10 min.

source_steps.stage: Section 2: Further enrichment of myeloid cells using CD11b+ magnetic beads
source_steps.description: Discard supernatant and resuspend in 500 μL of cold MACs buffer and mix with gentle pipetting.

source_steps.stage: Section 2: Further enrichment of myeloid cells using CD11b+ magnetic beads
source_steps.description: While cells are being prepared in step 14, set up an LS column in the MidiMACS separator mounted to a MACS Multistand (following manufacturer’s recommendation in protocol listed above) over a 15 mL conical tube to collect flow through and wash with 3 mL of cold MACS buffer.

background: Myeloid cells have already been enriched from mouse brain tumor tissue by a density gradient and are now at the stage where residual Percoll must be removed before CD11b magnetic-bead enrichment. The next operations focus on cold buffer washes, short low-speed centrifugation steps, bead labeling on ice, an additional wash, and magnetic column-based recovery of the labeled myeloid fraction.
goal: Process the post-gradient cell suspension through cleanup and CD11b bead enrichment to obtain the magnetically retained myeloid-cell fraction in cold MACS buffer, ready for downstream single-cell work.
constraints: Maintain cold handling throughout the cleanup and enrichment workflow; use 4 degrees Celsius for centrifugation and ice-cold or cold conditions for buffer-based manipulations.
Begin from a Percoll-containing cell suspension that requires dilution with 12 mL MACS buffer followed by a 300 x g spin for 7 minutes.
After the first wash, cells must be resuspended in 1 mL cold MACS buffer and subjected to another 300 x g centrifugation for 1 minute before the bead-labeling setup.
Before bead addition, normalize the suspension to 90 uL MACS buffer per 10^7 total cells.
Label cells using a 90 uL to 10 uL cell-suspension-to-CD11b-microbead ratio and incubate for 15 minutes on ice.
After bead labeling, perform a wash in 1 mL MACS buffer with centrifugation at 300 x g for 10 minutes, then resuspend in 500 uL cold MACS buffer.
Represent the column enrichment using the magnetic separation primitive, keeping the labeled fraction rather than the flow-through.
The final recovered fraction should reflect elution of magnetically retained cells into fresh cold MACS buffer after the wash and separation sequence.

available_inputs.name: percoll_cell_suspension
available_inputs.description: Cell suspension recovered after Percoll gradient isolation, still requiring buffer wash and cleanup.

available_inputs.name: macs_buffer
available_inputs.description: Cold MACS buffer used for washes and resuspension steps.

available_inputs.name: cd11b_microbeads
available_inputs.description: CD11b magnetic microbeads for labeling myeloid cells.

available_inputs.name: ls_column_setup
available_inputs.description: Prepared LS column in a magnetic separator for enrichment of labeled cells.

gold_program:
wash_mix_1 = mix_sample(sample=percoll_cell_suspension, method='pipetting', duration_s=15.0, speed='gentle')
wash_spin_1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_mix_1, speed_xg=300, duration_min=7.0, temperature_c=4.0)
resuspend_1 = mix_sample(sample=wash_spin_1, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
wash_spin_2 = centrifuge_sample(sample=resuspend_1, speed_xg=300, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=4.0)
label_prep = mix_sample(sample=wash_spin_2, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
bead_label = incubate_sample(sample=label_prep, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=15.0, condition='static on ice with CD11b microbeads at 90 uL cell suspension plus 10 uL beads per 10^7 cells')
wash_mix_2 = mix_sample(sample=bead_label, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
wash_spin_3 = centrifuge_sample(sample=wash_mix_2, speed_xg=300, duration_min=10.0, temperature_c=4.0)
resuspend_2 = mix_sample(sample=wash_spin_3, method='pipetting', duration_s=10.0, speed='gentle')
cd11b_positive_fraction = magnetic_separate(sample=resuspend_2, rack=ls_column_setup, duration_min=4.0, collect_phase='retained labeled cells')

background: This experiment profiles sequence preferences of DNA deaminases by preparing edited oligonucleotides for downstream sequencing. At this stage, a size-selected single-stranded DNA ligation product has already been excised from a gel, and the remaining work is to recover the ligated DNA from the gel slice, precipitate and dry the product, resuspend it, then carry out reverse-strand extension under thermal cycler conditions with a heated lid before generating a denatured extension product.
goal: Recover the ligated ssDNA from the gel fragment, concentrate it by precipitation, and produce the reverse-strand extension product under the specified temperature-controlled conditions.
constraints: Begin with the excised 47-nt ligated ssDNA gel band already collected in a 1.5 mL tube, and keep all later operations on the same sample lineage.
The recovery phase must include 400 uL extraction buffer, a 30 min flash-freeze equivalent on dry ice, then an overnight room-temperature thaw/extraction represented as 16 h with gentle mixing.
Include a brief clarification spin at 500 x g for 0.5 min before precipitation, then precipitate with 100% isopropanol at -20 C for 2 h.
Pellet the DNA using 20000 x g for 30 min at 4 C, then air-dry the pellet for 10 min before resuspension.
Resuspend the recovered ligated ssDNA in a small final volume consistent with 5 uL water before adding 20 uL extension reaction mix for the next stage.
Reverse-strand extension must be run on a thermal cycler with lid temperature set to 105 C, and the product must then receive 25 uL of 2X denaturing loading buffer.

available_inputs.name: excised_ligated_ssdna_band
available_inputs.description: Gel-excised 47-nt ssDNA ligation product in a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

available_inputs.name: dna_gel_extraction_buffer
available_inputs.description: DNA gel extraction buffer used to recover ligated ssDNA from the gel slice.

available_inputs.name: isopropanol_100
available_inputs.description: 100% isopropanol for precipitating the extracted ligated ssDNA.

available_inputs.name: nuclease_free_water
available_inputs.description: Water used to resuspend the dried DNA pellet.

available_inputs.name: extension_reaction_mix
available_inputs.description: Prepared extension reaction mix added before primer extension.

available_inputs.name: denaturing_loading_buffer_2x
available_inputs.description: 2X denaturing loading buffer added after thermal cycling.

gold_program:
frozen_gel_extract = cool_sample(sample=excised_ligated_ssdna_band, temperature_c=-78.0, duration_min=30.0, device="dry_ice")
thawed_gel_extract = incubate_sample(sample=frozen_gel_extract, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=960.0, condition="with_400uL_dna_gel_extraction_buffer_gentle_rotation")
clarified_extract = centrifuge_sample(sample=thawed_gel_extract, speed_xg=500, duration_min=0.5, temperature_c=22.0)
precipitating_extract = incubate_sample(sample=clarified_extract, temperature_c=-20.0, duration_min=120.0, condition="with_500uL_100pct_isopropanol")
pelleted_ssdna = centrifuge_sample(sample=precipitating_extract, speed_xg=20000, duration_min=30.0, temperature_c=4.0)
dried_pellet = dry_sample(sample=pelleted_ssdna, method="air_dry", temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=10.0)
resuspended_ssdna = incubate_sample(sample=dried_pellet, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=5.0, condition="resuspended_in_5uL_nuclease_free_water")
extension_setup = incubate_sample(sample=resuspended_ssdna, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=1.0, condition="add_20uL_extension_reaction_mix")
thermocycled_extension = run_thermal_cycler(plate=extension_setup, program_name="reverse_strand_extension", cycles=1, lid_temperature_c=105.0)
final_denatured_product = incubate_sample(sample=thermocycled_extension, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=1.0, condition="add_25uL_2X_denaturing_loading_buffer")
protocol_title: Oligo-seq protocol for mapping DNA motifs targeted by base editors

source_steps.stage: Ligation of an adaptor to the deaminated oligonucleotide
source_steps.description: Excise the ligated ssDNA (47-nt) band corresponding to the size of the target oligonucleotide (22 nt) ligated to the Oligo 3′ adaptor (25-nt), and collect it in a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

source_steps.stage: Ligation of an adaptor to the deaminated oligonucleotide
source_steps.description: Add 400 μL of DNA Gel Extraction Buffer (see materials and equipment setup) and flash-freeze the samples for 30 min using dry ice.

source_steps.stage: Ligation of an adaptor to the deaminated oligonucleotide
source_steps.description: Extract the ligated ssDNA by allowing the samples to thaw overnight at room temperature (20°C–24°C) while gently mixing on a platform rotator.

source_steps.stage: Ligation of an adaptor to the deaminated oligonucleotide
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the samples at 500 x g for 30 s and transfer the supernatants (400 μL) to new 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes.

source_steps.stage: Ligation of an adaptor to the deaminated oligonucleotide
source_steps.description: Optional: Add 1.5 μL of GlycoBlue coprecipitant (15 mg/mL). GlycoBlue coprecipitant is used only to visualize the DNA pellet during DNA precipitation, decreasing the risk of losing it. The GlycoBlue step can be omitted without compromising the quality of the results.

source_steps.stage: Ligation of an adaptor to the deaminated oligonucleotide
source_steps.description: Precipitate the extracted ligated ssDNA by adding 500 μL of isopropanol (100%), mix thoroughly, and incubate the samples for 2 h at −20°C.

source_steps.stage: Ligation of an adaptor to the deaminated oligonucleotide
source_steps.description: Pellet the ligated ssDNA by centrifugation at 20,000 x g for 30 min at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Ligation of an adaptor to the deaminated oligonucleotide
source_steps.description: Carefully discard the supernatant and air-dry the pellet for 10 min.

source_steps.stage: Ligation of an adaptor to the deaminated oligonucleotide
source_steps.description: Resuspend the pellet in 5 μL of nuclease-free H 2 O.
Add 20 μL of Extension Reaction Mix (see materials and equipment setup) to the 5 μL of ligated ssDNA obtained in step 22.
Perform the primer extension reaction as described in the table below in a thermocycler with the lid temperature set up at 105°C.
Add 25 μL of 2X Denaturing Loading Buffer (see materials and equipment setup) to each sample.

background: This protocol segment is in the Agrobacterium-mediated transformation stage for rice base editing. After bacterial recovery and selection, the workflow verifies transformants by colony-PCR, prepares sterilized immature seeds for callus induction, expands a fresh Agrobacterium culture, pellets the cells, and readies a low-density infection suspension before exposing the rice calli.
goal: Generate infection-ready rice calli and a properly prepared Agrobacterium suspension from the provided materials, including verification and preservation of the bacterial strain where appropriate.
constraints: Run colony-PCR on picked colonies using 25-30 cycles, and represent thermal-cycler handling only with available action-pool functions.
Include long-term storage of the Agrobacterium strain at -80 C as a pause point after verification material is available.

Surface sterilize the dehusked immature seeds under gentle shaking at 20 rpm for 45 min near room temperature, then perform a 10-day 28 C callus induction followed by an additional 3-5 day 28 C preinfection incubation.
Culture fresh Agrobacterium overnight for 8-12 h at 30 C with shaking at 120 rpm before pelleting, and include a centrifugation step at 5000 x g for 1 min.

Prepare the infection suspension so the final bacterial density is approximately OD600 0.15 within the effective 0.1-0.2 range, then expose calli to the suspension for 30-60 min at room temperature with gentle agitation.
Use 6-12 top-level action calls, reuse at least two action types more than once, and ensure later steps consume outputs from earlier ones.

available_inputs.name: fresh_agro_colony

gold_program:
sealed_colony_pcr_plate = seal_plate(plate=colony_pcr_plate, seal_type='PCR film', pressure='firm', duration_s=5.0)
closed_tc = close_thermal_cycler_lid(instrument='thermal cycler', latch_state='locked', plate=sealed_colony_pcr_plate)
colony_pcr_product = run_thermal_cycler(plate=sealed_colony_pcr_plate, program_name='colony_PCR', cycles=28, lid_temperature_c=105.0)
stored_agro_strain = store_sample(sample=agro_single_colonies, temperature_c=-80.0, duration_h=720.0, container='cryovial')
sterilized_seeds = set_thermal_mixer(sample=immature_rice_seeds, temperature_c=22.0, speed_rpm=20, duration_min=45.0)
induced_callus = incubate_sample(sample=sterilized_seeds, temperature_c=28.0, duration_min=14400.0, condition='light incubation for callus induction')
preinfection_callus = incubate_sample(sample=induced_callus, temperature_c=28.0, duration_min=5760.0, condition='fresh medium preinfection incubation')
overnight_agro_culture = set_thermal_mixer(sample=fresh_agro_colony, temperature_c=30.0, speed_rpm=120, duration_min=600.0)
pelleted_agro = centrifuge_sample(sample=agro_culture_tubes, speed_xg=5000, duration_min=1.0, temperature_c=25.0)
prepared_infection_suspension = incubate_sample(sample=pelleted_agro, temperature_c=25.0, duration_min=1.0, condition='resuspended in liquid MSD plus 200 uM AS to OD600 0.15')
infected_callus = incubate_sample(sample=preinfection_callus, temperature_c=22.0, duration_min=45.0, condition='soaked in Agrobacterium suspension with gentle shaking')

source_steps.stage: A. tumefaciens -mediated genetic transformation of rice
source_steps.description: Add 700 μL of LB medium to the tubes and shake them at 180 rpm for 3 h at 30°C.

source_steps.stage: A. tumefaciens -mediated genetic transformation of rice
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the tube at 5,000 g for 1 min to collect the bacteria. Discard 600 μL of the supernatant and resuspend the cells in the remaining liquid.

source_steps.stage: A. tumefaciens -mediated genetic transformation of rice
source_steps.description: Spread the resuspended cells on LB agar plate containing 50 mg/L Kan and 50 mg/L Rif. Incubate plates at 30°C for 2–3 d. Multiple visible single colonies should appear.

source_steps.stage: A. tumefaciens -mediated genetic transformation of rice
source_steps.description: Pick single colonies for colony-PCR (25–30 cycles) on a thermal cycler. Pause point: A. tumefaciens strains can be stored at −80°C for a long time.

source_steps.stage: A. tumefaciens -mediated genetic transformation of rice
source_steps.description: Callus induction. Select a 15-mL centrifuge tube and surface sterilize 30 dehusked immature rice seeds in 50% (v/v) sodium hypochlorite solution (10 mL Sodium hypochlorite, 10 mL ddH 2 O, and one drop of Tween-20) on a shaker at 20 rpm for 45 min at 20°C–25°C.

source_steps.stage: A. tumefaciens -mediated genetic transformation of rice
source_steps.description: Wash the seeds with ddH 2 O 3–5 times.

source_steps.stage: A. tumefaciens -mediated genetic transformation of rice
source_steps.description: Place seeds onto MSD plate (∼30 seeds/petri dish) and incubate for 10 days ( Figure 1 B) in a growth chamber (16-h light/8-h dark photoperiod at 28°C, or constant light at 28°C) for callus tissue induction.

source_steps.stage: A. tumefaciens -mediated genetic transformation of rice
source_steps.description: The embryos and seed coats are removed with forceps, and the calli are transferred to new MSD plates and incubated for 3–5 days before use for Agrobacterium -mediated transformation.

source_steps.stage: A. tumefaciens -mediated genetic transformation of rice
source_steps.description: Agrobacterium infection. Inoculate fresh bacterial colonies into a 125-mL conical flask containing 20 mL TY with 50 mg/L Rif, 50 mg/L Kan, and 200 μM AS.

source_steps.stage: A. tumefaciens -mediated genetic transformation of rice
source_steps.description: Grow overnight (8–12 h) at 120 rpm at 30°C until OD600 reaches 1.0–2.0.

source_steps.stage: A. tumefaciens -mediated genetic transformation of rice
source_steps.description: Transfer Agrobacterium cultures to 1.5-mL Eppendorf tubes and centrifuge at 5,000 g for 1 min to pellet the bacteria; resuspend the pellet in ∼20 mL of liquid MSD medium containing 200 μM AS for a final OD600 of ∼0.15. Note: The final OD600 needs to be in the range of 0.1–0.2 for effective transformation.

source_steps.stage: A. tumefaciens -mediated genetic transformation of rice
source_steps.description: Soak the calli in Agrobacterium suspension for 30–60 min in a Petri dish at room temperature (20°C–25°C) and shake it gently every 10 min.

background: A mouse tissue digest has already been mechanically dispersed and filtered to obtain a single-cell suspension for flow-cytometry analysis of myeloid populations. At this stage, the cells need to be pelleted, moved into staining conditions in FACS tubes, blocked, labeled with antibody mix under cold dark conditions, and washed so they are ready for the next resuspension step.
goal: Process the filtered tissue cell suspension through the cold staining workflow up to and including the post-antibody wash pellet, without performing the final branch-specific resuspension.
constraints: Keep all centrifugation and incubation steps in this segment at 4 degrees Celsius, and maintain the sample cold after the first resuspension.
Use 375 x g for 5 minutes for each centrifugation in this workflow, with at least three centrifugation steps represented.
Resuspend the first pellet in 2000 uL FACS buffer, then set up a labeled FACS tube with 1000 uL FACS buffer before transferring sample into staining format.

After transfer into the staining tube, perform Fc blocking with 70 uL blocking buffer for 15 minutes at 4 degrees Celsius, then add antibody mix and incubate for 30 minutes at 4 degrees Celsius protected from light.
Include a wash with 1000 uL FACS buffer after antibody staining and stop after the resulting pellet; do not include the optional lung/brain streptavidin path or the final DAPI step.
Use only provided action-pool functions, keep a single dependency chain, and reuse multiple action families.

available_inputs.name: filtered_tissue_suspension
available_inputs.description: Single-cell suspension obtained after tissue dissociation and passage through a 70 um strainer into a 50 mL tube.

available_inputs.name: pbs
available_inputs.description: PBS used for the processing wash associated with the filtered suspension.

available_inputs.name: facs_buffer
available_inputs.description: FACS buffer used for resuspension and wash steps.

available_inputs.name: fc_blocking_buffer
available_inputs.description: Fc receptor blocking buffer prepared for staining.

available_inputs.name: antibody_mix
available_inputs.description: Prepared antibody staining mixture for myeloid cell labeling.

available_inputs.name: facs_tube
available_inputs.description: Labeled flow-cytometry tube for one sample.

gold_program:
step1 = centrifuge_sample(sample=filtered_tissue_suspension, speed_xg=375, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step2 = add_reagent(sample=step1, reagent=facs_buffer, volume_ul=2000.0, mixing="resuspend")
step3 = cool_sample(sample=step2, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=5.0, device="ice")
step4 = add_reagent(sample=facs_tube, reagent=facs_buffer, volume_ul=1000.0, mixing="none")
step5 = aliquot_sample(sample=step3, destination=step4, volume_ul=1000.0, aliquot_count=1)
step6 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step5, speed_xg=375, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
step7 = add_reagent(sample=step6, reagent=fc_blocking_buffer, volume_ul=70.0, mixing="resuspend")
step8 = incubate_sample(sample=step7, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=15.0, condition="static")
step9 = add_reagent(sample=step8, reagent=antibody_mix, volume_ul=30.0, mixing="brief vortex")
step10 = incubate_sample(sample=step9, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=30.0, condition="protected from light")
step11 = add_reagent(sample=step10, reagent=facs_buffer, volume_ul=1000.0, mixing="wash and brief vortex")
step12 = centrifuge_sample(sample=step11, speed_xg=375, duration_min=5.0, temperature_c=4.0)
protocol_title: Analysis of Myeloid Cells in Mouse Tissues with Flow Cytometry

source_steps.description: Use a syringe with a 1.2 mm inner diameter needle to dissociate the digested tissue until no obvious debris are present.
Pass the suspension through a 70 μm cell strainer into 50-mL tubes and use 10 mL of PBS to pass through the strainer to wash it.
Centrifuge at 1350 rpm (375 × g ) for 5 min at 4°C, discard the supernatant and resuspend with 2 mL of FACs buffer. Keep on ice until staining.
Perform cell counting using Countess II.
Label FACs tubes for each sample, and add 1 mL of FACs buffer into each tube.
Transfer the tissue cell suspension containing ~2 million cells into FACs tubes, vortex briefly and centrifuge at 1350 rpm (375 × g ) for 5 min at 4°C.
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cells with 70 μL Fc blocking buffer (see Reagent setup), incubate at 4°C for 15 min.
Add indicated volume of the antibody mix (see Antibody mix preparation) into the suspension and vortex briefly, incubate at 4°C for 30 min in the dark by covering with aluminum foil.
Wash with 1 mL of FACs buffer, vortex briefly and centrifuge at 1350 rpm (375 × g ) for 5 min at 4°C.
For samples other than lung and brain, discard the supernatant and resuspend with 120 μL FACs buffer and 40 μL DAPI solution (final concentration 250 ng/mL).

For lung and brain sample, discard the supernatant and resuspend with 70 μL FACS buffer containing 0.25 μL PE-Cy7 conjugated streptavidin and vortex briefly, incubate at 4°C for 30 min in the dark by covering with aluminum foil.
Wash with 1 mL of FACs buffer, vortex briefly and centrifuge at 1350 rpm (375 × g ) for 5 min at 4°C.

background: A membrane vesicle preparation containing OpuA has been diluted into detergent-containing buffer on ice to extract the transporter from the membrane fraction. The next stage is to maintain the cold solubilization conditions, clarify the extract by ultracentrifugation, and then carry out the cold binding and wash/elution portion of Ni2+-Sepharose purification while preserving the intact OpuA complex.
goal: Process the detergent-solubilized OpuA sample through cold clarification and affinity purification so that separated imidazole elution fractions of the transporter are obtained.
constraints: Keep the solubilized membrane sample cold before clarification, using an ice-equivalent condition for 30 minutes total.
The clarification spin must be an ultracentrifugation at 336896 x g for 20 minutes at 4 C.
Protein-resin binding should be handled under cold static conditions to avoid agitation-related subunit dissociation.
Use two successive resin-settling incubations, each within the 10-20 minute range, after mixing the supernatant with Ni2+-Sepharose.
Represent the affinity cleanup with two wash operations using the same buffer family but a lower imidazole condition than elution.
Represent elution as consecutive fractions, including one smaller early fraction and later larger fractions under 200 mM imidazole conditions.
Later operations must consume outputs from earlier ones rather than restarting from the original raw sample.
Use only available action-pool functions and keep all function calls at top level with keyword arguments only.

available_inputs.name: cmv_mix
available_inputs.description: Cold detergent-solubilized OpuA-containing CMV mixture prepared in MLA-80 tubes at 3 mg/mL.

available_inputs.name: ni_resin_column
available_inputs.description: Disposable chromatography column containing 0.5 mL Ni2+-Sepharose resin equilibrated for OpuA binding.

available_inputs.name: bufferB_wash
available_inputs.description: Cold buffer B wash condition corresponding to 50 mM imidazole.

available_inputs.name: bufferB_elution
available_inputs.description: Cold buffer B elution condition corresponding to 200 mM imidazole.

gold_program:
solubilized_on_ice = incubate_sample(sample=cmv_mix, temperature_c=0.0, duration_min=30.0, condition="static on ice")
clarified_extract = centrifuge_sample(sample=solubilized_on_ice, speed_xg=336896, duration_min=20.0, temperature_c=4.0)
first_binding = incubate_sample(sample=clarified_extract, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=15.0, condition="static with Ni2+-Sepharose resin; no agitation")
second_binding = incubate_sample(sample=first_binding, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=15.0, condition="static after gentle remix with Ni2+-Sepharose resin; no agitation")
wash_1 = cool_sample(sample=second_binding, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=2.0, device=bufferB_wash)
wash_2 = cool_sample(sample=wash_1, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=2.0, device=bufferB_wash)
elution_fraction_1 = cool_sample(sample=wash_2, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=1.0, device=bufferB_elution)
elution_fraction_2 = cool_sample(sample=elution_fraction_1, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=1.0, device=bufferB_elution)
elution_fraction_3 = cool_sample(sample=elution_fraction_2, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=1.0, device=bufferB_elution)
elution_fraction_4 = cool_sample(sample=elution_fraction_3, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=1.0, device=bufferB_elution)
elution_fraction_5 = cool_sample(sample=elution_fraction_4, temperature_c=4.0, duration_min=1.0, device=bufferB_elution)
protocol_title: Protocol for sample preparation and single-molecule optical tweezers study of nanodisc-embedded ABC transporter OpuA

source_steps.stage: Purification and reconstitution of membrane protein OpuA in lipid nanodiscs
source_steps.description: Dilute the CMVs on ice in MLA-80 centrifuge tubes to 3 mg/mL in 6 mL. The final mixture should contain 50 mM KPi, pH 7.0–7.5, 20% (v/v) glycerol, 200 mM KCl, 1 mM DTT plus 0.5% (w/v) DDM. Note: An example with CMVs of 18 mg/mL can be found in Table 3 . Make sure that the CMVs are added last. Note: Take into account for the calculation that the CMVs are already in 50 mM KPi, pH 7.5, 20% (v/v) glycerol. 3 M KCl is used to compensate for the salt concentration.

source_steps.stage: Purification and reconstitution of membrane protein OpuA in lipid nanodiscs
source_steps.description: Incubate the mixture on ice for 30 min. Mix once by pipetting up and down after 15 min.

source_steps.stage: Purification and reconstitution of membrane protein OpuA in lipid nanodiscs
source_steps.description: Separate the solubilized proteins from the insolubilized fraction by ultracentrifugation at 336,896 g ( e.g. , with a Beckman MLA-80 rotor) for 20 min at 4 °C.

source_steps.stage: Purification and reconstitution of membrane protein OpuA in lipid nanodiscs
source_steps.description: Purification of the protein by Ni 2+ -Sepharose affinity chromatography: Equilibrate the Ni 2+ -Sepharose resin: Add 0.5 mL of Ni 2+ -Sepharose (column volume) to a 10 mL disposable chromatography column.

source_steps.stage: Purification and reconstitution of membrane protein OpuA in lipid nanodiscs
source_steps.description: Wash the resin with 12 column volumes of MQ.

source_steps.stage: Purification and reconstitution of membrane protein OpuA in lipid nanodiscs
source_steps.description: Equilibrate with 4 column volumes of 50 mM KPi pH 7.0, 200 mM KCl, 20% (v/v) glycerol, 1 mM DTT, 0.04% (w/v) DDM (buffer B) plus 10 mM imidazole. Note: When a fresh batch of Ni 2+ -Sepharose is used, the resin may turn slightly brown upon the addition of DTT. We have not observed any reduced binding of the His-tag to the Ni 2+ -Sepharose in the presence of DTT.

source_steps.stage: Purification and reconstitution of membrane protein OpuA in lipid nanodiscs
source_steps.description: Close the outlet of the column.

source_steps.stage: Purification and reconstitution of membrane protein OpuA in lipid nanodiscs
source_steps.description: Incubate the resin with the solubilized proteins: Add the supernatant, supplemented with 10 mM imidazole pH 7.5, to the resin and mix by gentle pipetting with a 1 mL pipette.

source_steps.stage: Purification and reconstitution of membrane protein OpuA in lipid nanodiscs
source_steps.description: Allow the Ni 2+ -Sepharose to sediment in 10 – 20 min.

source_steps.stage: Purification and reconstitution of membrane protein OpuA in lipid nanodiscs
source_steps.description: Mix the sample a second time and let it sediment again for 10 – 20 min. CRITICAL: Agitation during incubation with the resin is avoided, because this induces dissociation of OpuAA subunits from the full OpuA complex. Note that avoiding agitation does reduce the total protein yield.

source_steps.stage: Purification and reconstitution of membrane protein OpuA in lipid nanodiscs
source_steps.description: Drain the column and wash twice with 10 column volumes of buffer B plus 50 mM imidazole pH 7.5.

source_steps.stage: Purification and reconstitution of membrane protein OpuA in lipid nanodiscs
source_steps.description: Elute OpuA by adding consecutively one time 0.6 and four times 0.4 column volumes of buffer B plus 200 mM imidazole pH 7.5. Collect the five fractions separately. Note: Most protein is expected in the third and fourth fraction with a concentration around 3 – 5 mg/mL.

background: Murine splenocyte material is being processed during the naive CD8 T cell isolation and activation phase of a coculture workflow designed to study resident memory CD8 T cell differentiation with vaginal epithelial organoids. At this stage, red blood cells have just been lysed, the suspension must be cleaned up to improve enrichment quality, and the enriched naive CD8 T cells then need to be prepared for activation on a pre-coated plate while maintaining cold conditions where specified.
goal: Process the post-lysis cell suspension through cleanup and enrichment handling, then prepare the naive CD8 T cells and coated activation plate so the cells are ready to be plated in activation conditions.
constraints: The red blood cell lysis stage includes a brief room-temperature hold of 2 minutes at 22 degrees C after mixing in ACK buffer.
After lysis is stopped with harvest medium, the suspension must be passed through a 70 um filter to remove clumps before the enrichment-related spin.
Use a centrifugation condition of 584 x g for 5 minutes at 4 degrees C for the cleanup spin, and the same centrifugation condition again after enrichment handling.
A cold hold is required for the naive CD8 T cell pellet resuspended in base activation medium; keep it on ice at 0 degrees C until plating preparation proceeds.
The coated plate is maintained at 4 degrees C before rinsing, and should not be left dry for an extended period after DPBS handling.
The cell suspension should be well mixed by pipetting before final dispensing to activation wells.
Represent the manufacturer's enrichment interval as an incubation-like wait lasting within 1 to 1.5 hours.

available_inputs.name: rbc_pellet
available_inputs.description: Cell pellet requiring ACK red blood cell lysis.

available_inputs.name: post_lysis_suspension
available_inputs.description: Cell suspension after ACK buffer and harvest medium have been added.

available_inputs.name: enriched_cd8_suspension
available_inputs.description: Naive CD8 T cell fraction obtained after manufacturer-directed enrichment.

available_inputs.name: coated_plate
available_inputs.description: Pre-coated 24-well activation plate stored cold before use.

available_inputs.name: base_activation_media_cells
available_inputs.description: Naive CD8 T cells resuspended in base activation medium at 500000 cells per mL.

available_inputs.name: cytokine_master_mix
available_inputs.description: Activation cytokine mix prepared at double strength in base activation medium.

gold_program:
mixed_lysis = mix_sample(sample=rbc_pellet, method='swirling', duration_s=5, speed='gentle')
incubated_lysis = incubate_sample(sample=mixed_lysis, temperature_c=22, duration_min=2, condition='static')
filtered_cells = filter_sample(sample=post_lysis_suspension, filter_type='cell_strainer', pore_size_um=70.0, destination='same_tube')
cleanup_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=filtered_cells, speed_xg=584, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
enrichment_wait = incubate_sample(sample=cleanup_pellet, temperature_c=4, duration_min=75, condition='static')
enriched_pellet = centrifuge_sample(sample=enriched_cd8_suspension, speed_xg=584, duration_min=5, temperature_c=4)
chilled_cells = cool_sample(sample=base_activation_media_cells, temperature_c=0, duration_min=10, device='ice')
cold_plate = store_sample(sample=coated_plate, temperature_c=4, duration_h=0.1, container='refrigerator')
plate_rinse_hold = cool_sample(sample=cold_plate, temperature_c=4, duration_min=2, device='refrigerator')
mixed_cells = mix_sample(sample=chilled_cells, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='moderate')
ready_to_plate = mix_sample(sample=mixed_cells, method='pipetting', duration_s=10, speed='moderate')
protocol_title: Protocol for the coculture of murine vaginal epithelial organoids and T cells to induce resident memory CD8 T cell differentiation

source_steps.stage: Isolation, enrichment, and activation of naive CD8 T cells
source_steps.description: Perform RBC lysis with ACK RBC Lysis Buffer. Resuspend the pellet in 2 mL of sterile ACK RBC Lysis Buffer. Swirl the tube to mix.

source_steps.stage: Isolation, enrichment, and activation of naive CD8 T cells
source_steps.description: Incubate at 22°C for 2 minutes.

source_steps.stage: Isolation, enrichment, and activation of naive CD8 T cells
source_steps.description: After the incubation, neutralize the suspension by adding 2 mL of Harvest Media.

source_steps.stage: Isolation, enrichment, and activation of naive CD8 T cells
source_steps.description: Filter and centrifuge the resulting suspension. Draw up the suspension into a 5 mL pipette and filter it through a new 70 μm filter back into the same tube. CRITICAL: Removing clumps from the suspension is important for ideal T cell enrichment. If there are any remaining visible clumps in the suspension, it is best to remove them.

source_steps.stage: Isolation, enrichment, and activation of naive CD8 T cells
source_steps.description: Centrifuge the suspension at 584 x g for 5 minutes at 4°C. Dispose of the supernatant and rack the tube to loosen the pellet.

source_steps.stage: Isolation, enrichment, and activation of naive CD8 T cells
source_steps.description: Perform naive CD8 T cell enrichment following the manufacturer’s protocol (1–1.5h).

source_steps.stage: Isolation, enrichment, and activation of naive CD8 T cells
source_steps.description: Plate the naive CD8 T cells onto the pre-coated plate for activation. Note: The end product is ∼500,000 CD8 T cells in 2 mL of Complete Activation Media in each well of the coated plate. Centrifuge the enriched T cell suspension at 584 x g for 5 minutes at 4°C.

source_steps.stage: Isolation, enrichment, and activation of naive CD8 T cells
source_steps.description: Dispose of the supernatant. Resuspend the pellet of naive CD8 T cells in Base Activation Media for a concentration of 500,000 cells/mL. Leave this tube on ice until it is time to plate.

source_steps.stage: Isolation, enrichment, and activation of naive CD8 T cells
source_steps.description: In a separate tube, make the cytokine master mix. In an equal volume of Base Activation Media, add IL-2, IL-12, and beta-mercaptoethanol at double the final concentration specified in Complete Activation Media.

source_steps.stage: Isolation, enrichment, and activation of naive CD8 T cells
source_steps.description: Rinse the coated plate. Take out the coated plate from the 4°C fridge. Add an extra 1 mL of DPBS to each well and then aspirate the solution from all the wells using a vacuum aspirator.

source_steps.stage: Isolation, enrichment, and activation of naive CD8 T cells
source_steps.description: Add 1 mL of DPBS to each well. Note: Wait to aspirate this DPBS from the coated wells until just before you plan to plate the T cells, so that the coated wells are not left dry for a prolonged period.

source_steps.stage: Isolation, enrichment, and activation of naive CD8 T cells
source_steps.description: Plate the T cells with the cytokine master mix in the coated 24 well plate. Pipette up and down the entire volume of the T cell suspension multiple times to ensure the suspension is well-mixed.
